articles.headline: Chloroquine and pyronaridine-artesunate highly effective for P. vivax in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Efficacy of chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine and pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine against Plasmodium vivax malaria in adults and transmission to mosquitoes in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.
articles.details: Phase 2/3 randomized trial in Ethiopia with 176 adult P. vivax patients comparing chloroquine plus low-dose primaquine versus pyronaridine-artesunate plus low-dose primaquine. Day-28 ACPR was 98.7% for chloroquine (78/79) and 98.8% for pyronaridine-artesunate (82/83); day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine-artesunate was 96.4% (80/83). Both regimens well-tolerated with only mild adverse events and no serious adverse events.

articles.headline: Malaria determinants in African children under five identified
articles.title_original: Determinants of malaria prevalence among children under 5 years of age in 17 sub-Saharan African countries: insights from Malaria Indicator Surveys.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 51,457 children from 17 sub-Saharan African countries using Malaria Indicator Surveys (2016-2022). Risk factors included age 1-2 years (AOR 1.46), low household wealth (AOR 1.94), rural residence (AOR 2.69), and high community illiteracy (AOR 1.76). Protective factors were maternal primary education (AOR 0.84) and insecticide-treated bed nets (AOR 0.80). Study highlights need for expanded bed net distribution and health education.

articles.headline: Caretakers of malaria-affected children face substantial economic and emotional burden
articles.title_original: Perceptions and experiences of caretakers of children with neurologic deficits after severe malaria in Uganda.
articles.journal: Int J Qual Stud Health Well-being
articles.details: Qualitative study with 23 in-depth interviews and 4 focus groups of caretakers of children with neurologic deficits (motor, cognitive, behavioral, sensory, epilepsy) following severe malaria in Uganda. Caretakers reported mental/emotional distress, financial strain, community mistreatment, and limited access to specialized care. Study recommends health education campaigns targeting caretakers to address misconceptions.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine and pyronaridine-artesunate effective despite artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the effectiveness of artemether-lumefantrine and artesunate-pyronaridine each combined with single low-dose primaquine in adults with Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ethiopia: a phase 2/3, observer-masked, randomised, parallel-group trial.
articles.details: Phase 2/3 trial in Ethiopia with 199 P. falciparum patients, many carrying pfkelch13 R622I artemisinin-resistance markers and pfhrp2/3 gene deletions. Day-28 PCR-corrected ACPR was 96.8% for artemether-lumefantrine plus primaquine and 98.9% for pyronaridine-artesunate plus primaquine; day-42 ACPR for pyronaridine-artesunate was 96.5% (80/83). No early treatment failures; both regimens well-tolerated.
articles.notable_reason: Demonstrates continued artemisinin-based combination efficacy in pfkelch13 R622I parasites

articles.headline: Prognostic factors for mortality in malaria with acute kidney injury identified
articles.title_original: Prognostic factors in malaria patients with acute kidney injury: a systematic review and meta analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 16 studies (1,104 patients) evaluating predictors of mortality in malaria with acute kidney injury. Most strongly associated with mortality: ventilator requirement (OR 30.35), respiratory distress syndrome (OR 18.50), hypotension (OR 18.23), neurological involvement (OR 12.83), and disseminated intravascular coagulation (OR 12.00). Additional significant associations included oliguria, leukocytosis, hyponatremia, acidosis, hyperkalemia, and jaundice.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence higher in family replacement blood donors in Saudi Arabia
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted infections among blood donors in Saudi Arabia: demographic and malaria risk differences by donation purpose.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 49,590 blood donors in Saudi Arabia (10.6% family replacement, 89.4% voluntary). No significant differences between groups in HBV, HCV, HIV, or syphilis positivity. Malaria prevalence significantly higher in family replacement donors (5.3%) compared to voluntary donors (2.9%), particularly among non-Saudi donors. Findings highlight importance of continued malaria screening for transfusion safety.

articles.headline: Matrix-M-adjuvanted vaccines show favorable safety across multiple diseases
articles.title_original: Safety of Matrix-M-adjuvanted COVID-19, seasonal influenza, combination influenza-COVID-19, and malaria vaccines: a review of the evidence.
articles.details: Comprehensive safety review of 66 clinical trials and post-marketing studies of Matrix-M-adjuvanted vaccines involving 64,101 participants receiving 143,170 doses (55,939 COVID-19, 4,263 malaria). Vaccines evaluated included COVID-19, seasonal influenza, combination influenza-COVID-19, and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine. All vaccines well-tolerated across diverse populations with favorable reactogenicity profiles compared to licensed comparators.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets effective against resistant vectors but resistance emerging
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid nets for pyrethroid-resistant malaria vectors: efficacy, resistance risks, and policy implications.
articles.details: Systematic review of 31 studies (2010-2024) on chlorfenapyr-pyrethroid combination nets for pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles. Nets showed 1.8-fold increase in mosquito mortality (95% CI 1.5-2.1) compared to pyrethroid-only nets. Community trials reported 40-60% reductions in malaria incidence and entomological inoculation rates. Early chlorfenapyr resistance detected in Central Africa (RR 2.4, p=0.01). Integrated resistance management with rotational deployment recommended.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria carriers maintain artemisinin-resistant parasite reservoirs in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic malaria: a hidden reservoir for the transmission and circulation of artemisinin partial-resistant Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional study of 3,489 participants in Tanzania identifying 26.2% P. falciparum positivity by qPCR (15.8% submicroscopic by microscopy). Among 802 sequenced isolates, 3.0% from high-transmission areas carried pfk13 partial-resistance markers (R561H 2.0%, A675V 0.7%, Y493H 0.2%), with all low-transmission isolates wild-type. NFD haplotype present in ~48% of isolates. Higher artemether-lumefantrine use correlated with increased pfmdr1 mutations.
articles.notable_reason: Reveals asymptomatic carriers as significant reservoir for artemisinin-resistant parasites

articles.headline: Cold stress inhibits malaria parasite growth through erythrocyte invasion impairment
articles.title_original: Cold stress restricts malaria parasite growth by affecting erythrocyte invasion.
articles.authors: Odagawa T, Saiki E, Kanuka H
articles.details: In vitro and mouse model study demonstrating that cold stress (27-33°C in culture, 4°C in mice) suppresses P. falciparum growth in temperature-dependent manner. Cold stress decreased erythrocyte invasion efficiency with broad downregulation of invasion-associated genes including ama1 at schizont and ring stages. Cold stress synergistically enhanced suppression when combined with low-dose artesunate both in vitro and in vivo.

articles.headline: Stage-specific RNA editing machinery identified in malaria parasite development
articles.title_original: Genome-wide detection of conserved stage-specific RNA editing events for nuclear genes in the Plasmodium falciparum 3D7 malaria parasite.
articles.details: Genome-wide RNA editing analysis of synchronized P. falciparum 3D7 at developmental stages 16-40 hours identified conserved stage-specific editing events. G-to-A and C-to-T editing events predominated. Peak RNA editing occurred at 32-hour trophozoite stage, declining by 40-hour schizont stage. Pathways including RNA binding and catalytic activity downregulated at 40 hours. Findings demonstrate robust RNA editing machinery facilitating rapid base conversions.

articles.headline: Cryptic speciation and introgression revealed in avian malaria pathogen P. relictum
articles.title_original: Phylogenomic analyses reveal introgression and cryptic speciation in the globally distributed, vector-transmitted pathogen Plasmodium relictum.
articles.details: Phylogenomic analysis using sequence capture from 52 P. relictum infections across multiple continents identified two cryptic species with evidence of genetic introgression. One cryptic species (GRW4) devastated endemic Hawaiian avifauna approximately 100 years after introduction to the islands. Hawaiian GRW4 clade examined for positive selection but no evidence detected despite expected release from host selective pressures.

articles.headline: Indonesian medicinal plants show potent antimalarial activity in vitro
articles.title_original: Harnessing Indonesian natural products against neglected tropical diseases: Malaria.
articles.details: Review of 208 Indonesian medicinal plants with antimalarial potential. 99 crude extracts/fractions from 61 species tested against P. falciparum; 17 species underwent detailed phytochemical isolation yielding 146 compounds. Several compounds showed potent antiplasmodial activity: samaradine X (IC50=14 nM), norcaesalpinins E (90 nM), methyl 15-acetoxylansiolate (170 nM), and dehatrine (170 nM), compared to chloroquine (290 nM). Authors conclude Indonesian medicinal flora represent promising sources for new antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: Modern housing reduces malaria infection across African ecological zones
articles.title_original: Housing and malaria risk: Evidence across ecological and climatic gradients in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Pooled analysis of DHS and MIS data from 17 sub-Saharan African countries (2006–2022, n=206,723) using logistic regression to predict malaria RDT positivity. Modern housing associated with lower odds of infection (β=−0.543, p<0.001), controlling for socioeconomic status, WASH, net usage, and ecological-climatic factors. Protective effect persisted across diverse ecological contexts and climatic gradients. Housing quality emerges as durable, non-insecticidal correlate of reduced malaria vulnerability.

articles.headline: Reduced malaria model preserves dynamics with computational efficiency
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.
articles.details: Mathematical reduction technique applied to comprehensive malaria control model to derive simplified system preserving essential dynamics. Validation via Bayesian MCMC comparison, parameter identifiability, and sensitivity analyses showed reduced model exhibits similar transmission characteristics to full model while providing computational efficiency and flexibility. Reduced model valuable tool for evaluating and implementing malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Comment on malaria and Chagas surveillance in Brazil
articles.title_original: Comment on "Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024)".
articles.details: Commentary on comparative analysis of national surveillance data for malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil, 2007–2024.

articles.headline: Vietnamese P. vivax MSP-142 shows polymorphism; PvMSP-119 conserved
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of merozoite surface protein 1 C-terminal 42 kDa in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.
articles.details: Genetic polymorphism analysis of PvMSP-142 (vaccine candidate) from 88 Vietnamese P. vivax isolates in four endemic provinces. Polymorphisms resulted from nucleotide substitutions and meiotic recombination with balancing selection preserving gene diversity. Most polymorphic sites located in PvMSP-133; PvMSP-119 relatively well-conserved globally. Findings expand understanding of genetic structure and evolutionary dynamics for vaccine development.

articles.headline: Chloroquine-resistance markers decline in Gabon 19 years post-withdrawal
articles.title_original: Status of pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations in Plasmodium falciparum isolates in Gabon in 2019 after chloroquine withdrawal.
articles.details: Surveillance of pfcrt and pfmdr1 mutations in 70 P. falciparum isolates from Gabon in 2019. SNPs identified in 25.71% of pfcrt and 20% of pfmdr1. Wild-type haplotype CVMNK predominated (74.29%); mutant CVIET present at 17.13%, substantially lower than previously reported. For pfmdr1, wild-type NEYND observed in 80%. Results indicate persistence of mutant genotypes but declining frequencies over time, suggesting progressive restoration of chloroquine susceptibility.

articles.headline: Critical care management of malaria in resource-limited settings
articles.title_original: The Global Intensive Care Unit: Human Immunodeficiency Virus, Tuberculosis, and Malaria.
articles.authors: Riunga F, Wambua GM, Lee BW
articles.details: Review addressing burden of malaria as leading cause of critical care admissions in low- and middle-income countries where over 80% of global population resides. Discusses clinical presentation, diagnostic strategies, and management principles for malaria in critically ill patients, emphasizing early recognition and timely appropriate therapy to improve outcomes with high mortality.

articles.headline: Saturated anacardic acid reduces severe malaria severity via NF-κB
articles.title_original: Saturated anacardic acid shows promise as adjuvant therapy for preventing severe malaria via attenuation of inflammatory response by modulation of the NF-κB pathway.
articles.details: Preclinical study evaluating saturated anacardic acid (SAA) modulation of inflammatory mediators in microglia and monocytes exposed to Plasmodium. SAA increased survival in P. berghei ANKA-infected cerebral malaria-susceptible CBA mice. Co-incubation with Plasmodium and SAA decreased TNF-α, IL-8, MCP-1, ROS, NO, and COX-2 but increased IL-6 production. Authors conclude SAA modulates key inflammatory components of severe malaria pathogenesis and may represent promising adjunct therapy.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to urbanization, malaria, and avian microbiome
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to 'Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome' [Environ. Res. 297 (2026) 124073].
articles.details: Corrigendum notice for published article on interactions between urbanization, malaria infection, and avian cloacal microbiome.

articles.headline: Computational design of multi-epitope P. falciparum vaccine
articles.title_original: Designing a next-generation multi-stage vaccine for malaria prevention.
articles.authors: Arya H, Tripathi H, Bhatt TK
articles.details: Immunoinformatic design of three multi-epitope subunit vaccine constructs targeting five Plasmodium proteins, comprising adjuvants, CTL epitopes, HTL epitopes, B-cell epitopes, and linkers. Constructs were antigenic, non-allergenic, and non-toxic. Molecular docking showed construct 1 most stable binding to TLR-4; molecular dynamics simulations demonstrated stable interactions. In silico cloning indicated potential for E. coli expression. In vitro and in vivo studies necessary for efficacy validation.

articles.headline: P. vivax MSP-3 polymorphism identified in Pakistani field isolates
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein 3α and β field isolates.
articles.details: Genetic diversity analysis of PvMSP-3α and PvMSP-3β genes in 207 blood samples from malaria-suspected patients in Azad Jammu and Kashmir, Pakistan (March–August 2024). PCR detected P. vivax in 188/207 (90.82%) and P. falciparum in 19/207 (9.18%). PvMSP-3α showed three allelic variants: type A (66.33%), type B (11.22%), type C (13.27%); PvMSP-3β showed type A (76.67%) and type B (23.33%). Study demonstrates P. vivax genotypic diversity in region.

articles.headline: Black cumin-acha cookies suppress malaria parasitemia in diabetic mice
articles.title_original: Functional-Diet Potential of Black Cumin-Enriched Acha-Based Cookies on Plasmodium berghei-Infected Diabetic Mice.
articles.details: Study of black cumin-enriched acha-based cookies in type 2 diabetic mice infected with P. berghei NK65. Mice (n=8/group) induced with streptozotocin (35 mg/kg) then infected and fed fortified cookies for 14 days. Cookies reduced parasitemia and blood glucose levels, enhanced pancreatic enzyme inhibition and antioxidant enzyme activities (SOD, catalase, glutathione peroxidase). Authors conclude cookies could be promising nutraceutical therapy for T2D-malaria comorbidity.

articles.headline: Multi-antigen transmission-blocking vaccine induces robust antibody responses
articles.title_original: Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi.
articles.details: A multi-component transmission-blocking vaccine combining antigens APN-1, CelTOS, and cd-HAP with adjuvants CPG, MPL, and QS-21 was tested in BALB/c mice. The vaccine elicited robust IgG antibodies against all three antigens without antigenic interference and induced Th1-type cellular responses with increased IFN-γ and TNF production. Standard membrane feeding assays with P. falciparum NF54 gametocytes and Anopheles stephensi demonstrated substantial reduction in oocyst load.

articles.headline: Novel copper complex exhibits activity against P. falciparum in vitro
articles.title_original: Synthesis, Characterization, and Anti-Plasmodium falciparum Activity of a New Copper(II) Complex Containing 2-(Tert-Butoxy)-6-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)pyridine.
articles.details: A copper(II) coordination complex derived from a 2-(tert-butoxy)-6-(1H-imidazol-1-yl)pyridine ligand was synthesized and characterized. When tested in vitro against the P. falciparum 3D7 chloroquine-sensitive strain, the complex showed higher activity than the free ligand with low hemocytotoxicity (≤20% at concentrations >500 µg/mL). These findings suggest that metal coordination enhances antiparasitic activity.

articles.headline: Antimicrobial peptides show promise against malaria and tuberculosis
articles.title_original: Antimicrobial peptides in malaria and tuberculosis management: a systematic review of emerging evidence.
articles.details: A systematic review examined antimicrobial peptides (AMPs) against malaria and TB using APD3 database and PubMed (1965–June 2025). Anti-malarial AMPs include defensins and cecropins, while anti-TB AMPs are primarily bacteriocins, defensins, and cathelicidins. Despite varying toxicity levels, AMPs demonstrate promising efficacy through novel mechanisms, with recent advances in nanocarrier delivery, inhalable formulations for TB, and targeted erythrocyte delivery for malaria.

articles.headline: Seaweed-based nanoparticle study retracted
articles.title_original: Retraction notice to 'Seaweed Sargassum wightii mediated preparation of TiO(2) nanoparticles, larvicidal activity against malaria and filariasis vectors, and its effect on non-target organisms' [Environ. Res. 225 (2023) 115569].
articles.details: A study on seaweed (Sargassum wightii) mediated TiO2 nanoparticle preparation with reported larvicidal activity against malaria vectors was retracted from Environmental Research.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria at 5% in Yemen; household factors identified
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic malaria and its associated factors in the Al Zuhrah district, Al-Hodeidah Government, Yemen, 2022.
articles.authors: Hajjam MA, Al Amad MA, Nasher SS
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 422 participants in Al-Zuhra District, Yemen identified 5% asymptomatic malaria prevalence with P. falciparum accounting for 95% of infections. Education was protective (adjusted prevalence odds ratio 0.35, 95% CI 0.13–0.97), while absence of window screens (APOR 9.56, 95% CI 2.15–42.51) and proximity to stagnant water (APOR 4.93, 95% CI 1.50–16.23) were significant risk factors. Findings emphasize household-level vector control and environmental management.

articles.headline: Pf12 and Pf41 show high conservation and strong antibody recognition
articles.title_original: Conserved genetic and antigenic profiles of Pf12 and Pf41 in asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum carriers in Tanzania.
articles.details: Analysis of 172 P. falciparum isolates from asymptomatic Tanzanian individuals revealed very low genetic diversity in Pf12 (π = 0.00042 ± 0.00007) and Pf41 (π = 0.00061 ± 0.00004), with negative Tajima's D values indicating selective constraints. Both antigens elicited high IgG seropositivity (Pf12 97.7%, Pf41 98.3%) with strongly correlated antibody levels (ρ = 0.721, p < 0.001). These conserved merozoite antigens warrant further investigation for vaccine potential.

articles.headline: Host-parasite interactions in malaria: invasion, neutralization, and evasion
articles.title_original: Host-Pathogen Interactions in Malaria: Invasion, Neutralization, and Evasion.
articles.details: This review examines receptor-ligand interactions between Plasmodium parasites and the human host, focusing on clinically relevant antigens used for motility, cell traversal, and invasion. It reviews antibody-antigen interactions and parasite immune evasion mechanisms that inform ongoing vaccine and therapeutic development strategies targeting key parasite proteins.

articles.headline: ITN use gaps linked to geography and socioeconomic status in Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated nets utilization and determinants in Southeast Asia toward malaria elimination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: A meta-analysis of 20 studies (2003–2023, N=122,258 individuals) identified substantial gaps between insecticide-treated net (ITN) ownership and utilization in Southeast Asia. Geographic region was the strongest determinant of non-use, with higher odds in coastal versus delta areas (pooled OR 4.73, 95% CI 1.59–14.06), followed by socioeconomic status, with higher non-use among the poorest versus richest (pooled OR 1.70, 95% CI 1.01–2.86).

articles.headline: RIPR monoclonal antibodies show synergistic inhibition of parasite invasion
articles.title_original: Analysis of monoclonal antibodies against the malaria invasion complex protein RIPR reveals the structural basis for synergistic antibody protection.
articles.details: Analysis of 83 human IgG monoclonal antibodies against RIPR, a component of the PCRCR blood-stage invasion complex, revealed that while single mAbs had minimal neutralizing activity, pools targeting the RIPR-tail region achieved high-level synergistic inhibition. Structural studies showed mAbs targeting EGF-like domains 6–8 synergized to constrain RIPR-tail conformation and dissociate PTRAMP-CSS from RIPR. Addition to the R78C vaccine candidate enhanced malaria growth inhibition.

articles.headline: Cambodia's digital surveillance app enables rapid case reporting and response
articles.title_original: Digital Health Center App for Community and National Malaria Surveillance in Cambodia: Implementation Case Study.
articles.details: Cambodia's Android-based health center app, integrated with the Malaria Information System, demonstrates strong real-world performance supporting elimination efforts. Between January and July 2025, 69 cases were reported with 95.7% (66/69) notified and classified within 1 day; reactive case detection was completed within 3 days for all eligible cases. User satisfaction was high (96.3% satisfied), with 90.1% reporting minimal technical issues.

articles.headline: Ginsenoside Rg1 disrupts glucose metabolism and potentiates artemisinin
articles.title_original: Selective targeting of Plasmodium falciparum hexose transporter by phytochemical Ginsenoside Rg1 disrupts glucose metabolism and blocks development of parasite.
articles.details: Ginsenoside Rg1 (G-Rg1), identified via structure-based drug design as an inhibitor of Plasmodium falciparum hexose transporter (PfHT), suppressed growth of both artemisinin-sensitive and artemisinin-resistant parasites in vitro. G-Rg1 potentiated dihydroartemisinin activity in combination and ring survival assays. In P. berghei ANKA-infected mice, G-Rg1 alone and combined with artemisinin reduced parasite multiplication and increased mean survival time.

articles.headline: DHA-PPQ for malaria prevention does not affect ARV concentrations in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Effects of malaria intermittent preventive treatment with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine on antiretroviral drug concentrations in African pregnant women living with HIV.
articles.details: A study of 105 pregnant women with HIV in Mozambique and Gabon examined whether monthly dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) for malaria prevention affected antiretroviral (ARV) concentrations. Median maternal dolutegravir was 835.1 ng/mL (cord blood 1,204.2 ng/mL) and lamivudine 108.3 ng/mL (cord blood 186.5 ng/mL). No significant differences were found between DHA-PPQ and placebo arms, indicating DHA-PPQ is a safe option for intermittent preventive treatment in women living with HIV.

articles.headline: Iron homeostasis inhibition blocks mosquito reproduction and parasite transmission
articles.title_original: Targeting Iron Homeostasis Curtails Mosquito Fertility and Plasmodium Transmission Potential.
articles.details: Researchers characterized mosquito ferritin and tested functional knockdown in Anopheles stephensi females. Ferritin knockdown impaired reproductive tissue development. β-thujaplicin, a chelator, suppressed Plasmodium oocyst development, establishing iron homeostasis as a novel transmission-blocking target for future interventions.

articles.headline: IL-33 gene variants influence Plasmodium falciparum infection susceptibility
articles.title_original: Association of interleukin-33 gene polymorphisms with susceptibility to Plasmodium falciparum infection.
articles.details: Case-control study of 450 individuals from Saudi Arabia examined 13 IL-33 SNPs. rs10118795 CT increased malaria susceptibility (OR=1.584, p=0.022), while rs55726619 CT was protective (OR=0.042, p<0.001). Age-dependent effects were observed, with specific variants enriched in children aged 1-5 years compared to older patients.

articles.headline: Precipitation-malaria association shows spatial heterogeneity in temporal lag
articles.title_original: Exploratory analysis of the correlation between precipitation and storms and malaria cases from national surveillance in Mozambique.
articles.details: District-level surveillance data from Mozambique were linked to precipitation data across multiple regions using Poisson GLMMs. Nationally, increased malaria relative risk occurred at 8-week precipitation lag, but regional variation was significant: 6-8 weeks in the North, 10-12 weeks in the Central region, and 10-13 weeks in the South.

articles.headline: PROTAC-mediated p38-MAPK degradation reduces parasite burden
articles.title_original: Chemical degradation of Human p38-MAPK reveals it as a potential host target to combat parasitic infections by Leishmania donovani and Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: A PROTAC compound NR-7h was used to degrade human p38-MAPK in infected cells. Degradation reduced P. falciparum and L. donovani parasite load dose-dependently. For P. falciparum, impaired growth and merozoite invasion were observed; for L. donovani, NR-7h enhanced Amphotericin B efficacy.

articles.headline: Rapid ambient-temperature CRISPR-based Plasmodium detection from whole blood
articles.title_original: Extract-free one-pot ambient RPA-CRISPR detection of Plasmodium in whole blood.
articles.authors: Liao J, Su Y, Jiang F
articles.details: An extraction-free, one-pot RPA-CRISPR assay was developed for direct Plasmodium detection from whole blood at room temperature without specialized equipment. Clinical validation in 225 samples (116 positive, 109 negative) showed 93.1% sensitivity and 100% specificity for P. falciparum and P. vivax. Sensitivity varied by parasite density (97.8% high-density, 95.1% moderate, 82.8% low-density) with 40-minute assay time.

articles.headline: Novel lysyl-tRNA synthetase inhibitor shows multistage malaria activity
articles.title_original: Structure-Guided Optimization of Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Lysyl-tRNA Synthetase with Multistage Activity against Malaria Parasites.
articles.details: Structure-guided optimization of a KRS inhibitor yielded compound 30 with potent parasite growth inhibition and >1000-fold cellular selectivity. The compound showed oral efficacy in mouse malaria models, activity across Plasmodium species and drug-resistant strains, and long half-life supporting single-dose cure potential. However, compound 30 lacked oral bioavailability in humans.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 46% among symptomatic patients; education and ITN key
articles.title_original: Assessment of malaria prevalence and determinants among clinically suspected individuals attending Dejen Primary Hospital, East Gojjam Zone, northwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study (April-November 2024) of 385 symptomatic individuals in Ethiopia found 46.2% malaria prevalence with P. falciparum accounting for 56.1% of cases. Risk factors included lack of formal education (AOR=2.16), proximity to breeding sites (AOR=2.15), and non-use of ITNs (AOR=1.72).

articles.headline: Droplet digital PCR assay for Plasmodium vivax resistance marker monitoring
articles.title_original: Droplet digital PCR assays for Pvmdr1 and Pvcrt-o gene copy number variation determination in Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: A duplex ddPCR assay was developed for simultaneous quantification of pvmdr1 and pvcrt-o gene copy numbers using pvtubulin as internal reference, with quantification limits of ~21-22 copies/μL. Field samples from Thailand showed evolution in resistance marker prevalence: 16% of 2008 Tak samples had pvmdr1 amplification, while 2023-2025 Kanchanaburi samples showed no multiple copies.

articles.headline: SNP-rich markers outperform length-polymorphic markers for recrudescence detection
articles.title_original: Comparison of techniques and markers to distinguish Plasmodium falciparum recrudescence from new infection in Rwanda.
articles.details: Analysis of Rwanda Therapeutic Efficacy Study samples compared capillary electrophoresis (msp1, msp2, glurp, microsatellites) and amplicon deep sequencing (SNP-rich markers) for distinguishing P. falciparum recrudescence from new infection. SNP-rich markers provided most consistent results across different decision algorithms (WHO, 2/3, Bayesian).

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis identifies 12 million exposed to flood-driven malaria risk
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of malaria risk in flood-prone zones of Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Geospatial analysis integrated high-resolution flood maps (2000-2018) with malaria incidence data across 492 flood-prone zones in 38 Sub-Saharan African countries. Approximately 12 million individuals with P. falciparum infection were exposed to flooding, representing one-third of the flood-affected population. East and West Africa showed elevated relative risk in flood zones.

articles.headline: Novel pyrrolidinamide MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage P. falciparum
articles.title_original: The pyrrolidinamide antimalarial drug MMV367 rapidly clears blood-stage Plasmodium falciparum in healthy adults with experimental malaria.
articles.details: Volunteer infection study (12 healthy adults) of single oral MMV367 doses showed rapid parasite killing: for doses ≥20 mg, parasite clearance half-life 2.2-4.3 hours and viable parasite elimination 1.1-2.7 hours. Pharmacokinetic/pharmacodynamic modeling estimated MIC of 6.3 ng/ml. No in vitro resistance mutations emerged, and tolerability was good with no serious adverse events.

articles.headline: Mesenchymal stem cells attenuate experimental cerebral malaria via immune modulation
articles.title_original: Macrophage markers and gene signature profiling reveals mesenchymal stem cells mediated immune modulation in Plasmodium berghei ANKA infection.
articles.details: In Pb.ANKA-infected mice (experimental cerebral malaria), intravenous MSC administration reduced parasitemia, improved survival, preserved blood-brain barrier integrity, and reduced brain pathology. Flow cytometry and gene expression profiling revealed macrophage reprogramming toward balanced M1/M2 response with downregulation of pro-inflammatory markers (iNOS-2) and upregulation of anti-inflammatory genes.

articles.headline: IMC55 protein not required for Plasmodium falciparum replication
articles.title_original: The inner membrane complex protein, IMC55, is dispensable for intraerythrocytic development of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Researchers generated conditional mutants of IMC55 using inducible expression systems in two experimental platforms. No defect in asexual replication or replicative fitness was observed, indicating the protein is either functionally dispensable or its role is redundant with other cellular components.

articles.headline: HLQ-102 antimalarial targets cytochrome bc1 complex
articles.title_original: Revisiting 2-Substituted-4(1H)-Quinolones for Targeting the Plasmodium falciparum Cytochrome bc(1) Complex.
articles.details: HLQ-102, the 2-position isomer of the advanced-stage antimalarial ELQ-300, was synthesized using cost-effective procedures that avoid expensive catalysts. Cross-resistance patterns confirm HLQ-102 targets the Qi site of the Plasmodium falciparum cytochrome bc1 complex, similar to its predecessor.

articles.headline: Ghana community supports malaria mass drug administration strategy
articles.title_original: Community perspective on feasibility of malaria mass drug administration: a qualitative evidence from the Eastern Region of Ghana.
articles.details: Qualitative study (11 focus group discussions with 97 participants, 8 in-depth interviews) in Pokrom subdistrict examined community perceptions following a pilot mass drug administration (MDA). Participants reported notable reductions in malaria episodes and decreased healthcare costs. Mild transient adverse events (dizziness, abdominal pain, weakness) were reported. High willingness for future MDA rounds.

articles.headline: Mathematical modeling framework for malaria intervention tracking
articles.title_original: Dynamical and time-series approach to understanding compartmental stock and flow models: a case study in malaria intervention models.
articles.details: Researchers developed a compartmental stock-and-flow modeling approach with adapted expectation-maximization calibration for disparate sampling frequencies. The method was applied to insecticide-treated net (ITN) delivery, distribution, and household survey data from 44 countries over 24 years, demonstrating scalability across heterogeneous datasets.

articles.headline: pfmdr1 and K13 mutations drive artemisinin resistance in Uganda
articles.title_original: Polymorphism of pfmdr1 Gene Mutation Conferring Resistance to Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapy in Plasmodium falciparum in Patients at Gulu Regional Referral Hospital in Northern Uganda.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 353 febrile patients identified 214 with Plasmodium falciparum (60.6% prevalence). Genotyping revealed pfmdr1 SNPs at codons 1034-1042 (29.6%), 86-184 (28%), and 1246 (25.8%). Artemisinin resistance-conferring Kelch13 propeller mutations were present in 16.7%. Combined SNPs (86-184, 1034-1042, K13) occurred in 90.1% of resistant parasites; median parasite load varied significantly by genotype (p≤0.0001 to p=0.012).
articles.notable_reason: Major resistance breakthrough identifies K13/pfmdr1 combination driving ACT failure in Uganda

articles.headline: Ethnographic study reveals gender inequity in Ethiopian malaria treatment
articles.title_original: The social experience of malaria treatment-seeking: pathways, gender, and inequality in Jimma Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Gerbi T, Teshome D, Sudhakar M
articles.details: Ethnographic research in Jimma City (32 in-depth interviews, 8 key informant interviews, 2 focus group discussions) examined treatment pathways influenced by local explanatory models (buda, busa, sekera), gender roles, and structural barriers. Treatment typically progressed from home remedies and prayer to biomedical care, with women lacking decision-making authority despite recognizing illness first.

articles.headline: Mixed Plasmodium malaria with Libman-Sacks endocarditis and hemorrhagic stroke
articles.title_original: Libman sacks endocarditis with multivalvular involvement, hemorrhagic stroke, and concurrent mixed plasmodium malaria in systemic lupus erythematosus: a case report.
articles.details: Case report of 29-year-old female with systemic lupus erythematosus (SLE) non-compliant on immunosuppressants presenting with encephalopathy (Glasgow Coma Scale 7/15) and fever. Diagnosis: mixed P. vivax/P. falciparum malaria with multivalvular Libman-Sacks endocarditis (mitral and aortic valve masses with regurgitation) and hemorrhagic stroke (7 × 5.2 cm frontoparietal infarct). Treated with intravenous artesunate and methylprednisolone; patient died.

articles.headline: Malaria training improved provider knowledge but not treatment compliance in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria training on community healthcare providers' compliance with malaria treatment guidelines in Nigeria using propensity score matching.
articles.details: Quasi-experimental evaluation (February–December 2022) using propensity score matching assessed Community Pharmacists and Patent Medicine Vendors. Training significantly increased malaria knowledge (β=0.052, p=0.03) and acceptability of WHO test-treat-refer strategy (β=0.432, p=0.03), but had no effect on treatment guideline compliance or quality drug identification.

articles.headline: Impaired consciousness and coma predict ICU transfer in severe P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Clinical profiles and predictors of intensive care unit transfer among children hospitalized with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in a tertiary care hospital in Libreville, Gabon: a retrospective study.
articles.details: Retrospective study of 480 children with WHO-defined severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria (15.9% of 3,009 hospitalizations) with 8.5% ICU transfer rate (n=41) and 0.7% mortality. Impaired consciousness (adjusted odds ratio 14.86) and coma (aOR 53.3) were the strongest ICU predictors. Presence of >3 concurrent WHO severity criteria increased ICU risk (aOR 10.59). Neurological manifestations predominated in 64.2% of cases.

articles.headline: Sysmex XN-31 analyser shows excellent sensitivity for malaria screening
articles.title_original: Field diagnostic performance of the Sysmex XN31 automated hematology analyser for malaria screening among febrile children in a sentinel site for malaria surveillance site in Libreville, Gabon.
articles.details: Prospective diagnostic study compared Sysmex XN-31 automated hematology analyser with microscopy in 1,034 febrile children (<15 years). Overall sensitivity 98.4% (95% CI 96.9–99.9%), specificity 99.9% (95% CI 98.8–99.9%). Sensitivity was lower in children <5 years (96.9%) and at parasite densities <100/µL (90.7%, p=0.030). ROC AUC 0.991; optimal detection threshold 20 parasites/µL.
articles.notable_reason: Novel automated diagnostic with 98.4% sensitivity; practical paradigm shift for screening

articles.headline: IL-9 drives immunopathology in experimental cerebral malaria via MDSCs
articles.title_original: IL-9 Orchestrates MDSC Expansion and Inflammatory Programming to Amplify Immunopathology During Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.details: Experimental rodent study using Plasmodium berghei ANKA demonstrated IL-9 from T helper 9 (Th-9) cells drives myeloid-derived suppressor cell (MDSC) and macrophage pro-inflammatory phenotypes. Neutralizing IL-9 reduced immunopathology and increased host survival. MDSCs reciprocally expanded Th-9 cells via IL-1β, creating a positive feedback loop. IL-9 blockade proposed as adjunctive therapeutic strategy.
articles.notable_reason: First demonstration of IL-9-MDSC feedback in cerebral malaria pathology; novel therapeutic target

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum detected in Italy without recent endemic travel
articles.title_original: Cryptic severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Maremma (Tuscany, Italy), 2024: a case report from a historically receptive area.
articles.details: Case report of severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria (August 2024) in Italian patient with no endemic-country travel history, residing in Maremma coastal village (receptive area malaria-free since 1970). Patient presented with 10% parasitaemia and was treated with intravenous artesunate with clinical improvement. No transmission chain was identified, suggesting cryptic local acquisition.
articles.notable_reason: First P. falciparum case in Italy since 1970; suggests receptive zone reintroduction risk

articles.headline: Computational screening identifies DHODH inhibitors for antimalarial drug development
articles.title_original: Virtual screening and molecular dynamics analysis of Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH) inhibitors targeting pyrimidine biosynthesis pathway.
articles.details: Structure-based virtual screening and 500 ns molecular dynamics simulations identified three compounds targeting P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (DHODH), an essential enzyme for parasite pyrimidine biosynthesis. From 1,500 initial hits, compounds 17433008 and 17507474 showed the most favorable binding free energies (−86.46 and −85.91 kcal/mol respectively) with stable binding confirmed through simulation and are proposed as promising lead candidates for experimental validation.

articles.headline: Antigen location determines IgG glycosylation and parasite-specific protection
articles.title_original: Fc-fucosylation of Plasmodium falciparum-specific IgG varies with antigen and immunization.
articles.details: Study of naturally exposed children and vaccinated adults examined Fc-fucosylation of IgG responses to different P. falciparum antigens. IgG targeting host-expressed surface antigens (PfEMP1, PfGARP) were Fc-afucosylated, while IgG to parasite-stage antigens (merozoites, gametocytes, sporozoites) were fully Fc-fucosylated. Children with Fc-afucosylated PfGARP-specific IgG had reduced risk of parasite carriage independent of antibody levels, supporting context-dependent Fc glycosylation as an immunological mechanism linked to protective immunity.

articles.headline: WHO announces first malaria drug for infants and new diagnostic tests
articles.title_original: WHO Announces First Malaria Drug for Infants and New Rapid Diagnostic Tests.
articles.details: Without an abstract, specific details about the drug, its efficacy, or clinical trial data cannot be provided from the title alone.

articles.headline: Lizard malaria parasites conserve hemoglobin digestion genes despite cellular adaptation
articles.title_original: Hemoglobin Digestion Genes Are Conserved in Lizard-Infective Plasmodium Species With Different Host Cellular Niches.
articles.details: Transcriptome analysis of three Plasmodium species infecting Anolis lizards (P. floridense, P. azurophilum, P. leucocytica) revealed that all species transcribe canonical hemoglobin digestion pathway genes. Despite different host cellular niches (P. floridense and P. azurophilum infect RBCs; P. leucocytica infects WBCs) and hemozoin production differences, all parasites express the same digestive enzymes as mammalian Plasmodium, suggesting evolutionary conservation or potential additional cellular roles for these genes.

articles.headline: Copper-driven cell death activates fibrosis in murine malaria liver injury
articles.title_original: Copper overload drives hepatic stellate cell activation via cuproptosis to exacerbate acute liver injury in murine malaria.
articles.details: In P. berghei ANKA-infected C57BL/6 mice, hepatic copper accumulation during infection activated cuproptosis and hepatic stellate cell activation, exacerbating liver injury. Pharmacological copper elevation with disulfiram worsened parasitemia and liver parasite burden, increased cuproptosis markers (FDX1, DLAT, DLST), and enhanced hepatic stellate cell activation with increased α-SMA and collagen I expression. Copper chelation with tetrathiomolybdate reversed these pathological changes.

articles.headline: Review: organoids and organs-on-chips complement murine malaria research models
articles.title_original: Beyond the mouse: organoids, spheroids, and organs-on-chips as the (inevitable) future of malaria research?
articles.authors: Dormoi J, Amalvict R, Pradines B
articles.details: Narrative review examining the role of micropathological systems in addressing limitations of murine malaria models. Spheroids, organoids, and organ-on-chip platforms offer scalable three-dimensional alternatives that reproduce structural and functional features of human tissues (liver, brain, placenta, vasculature) and model sequestration, barrier dysfunction, and drug responses. These technologies complement rather than replace murine models by bridging gaps in understanding P. falciparum and P. vivax severe malaria pathophysiology.

articles.headline: TaqMan PCR assays detect simian malaria parasites in human infections
articles.title_original: Accurate and sensitive TaqMan real-time PCR assays to detect and quantify simian malaria parasites Plasmodium cynomolgi, Plasmodium inui and Plasmodium coatneyi.
articles.details: TaqMan real-time PCR assays targeting 18S rRNA genes were developed to detect and quantify P. inui, P. cynomolgi, and P. coatneyi with high specificity and limit of detection of approximately 5 parasites (10 template DNA copies). Assays showed >95% amplification efficiency, linear standard curves (10–10⁶ copies), and equivalence to nested PCR. Designed for routine malaria surveillance in Southeast Asia where zoonotic infections occur, with utility for detecting submicroscopic parasitemia and infections in mosquito samples.

articles.headline: SPR biosensor with AI predicts malaria parasitemia stages in real-time
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence enabled performance evaluation of an enhanced SPR biosensor for malaria diagnosis.
articles.details: Study presents a multilayer surface plasmon resonance biosensor design (N-FK51a+SiO2+Cu+HfO2+BP) optimized for malaria detection with maximum angular sensitivity of 541.42 deg/RIU, narrow FWHM of 1.98 deg, and quality factor of 272.21 RIU⁻¹. Artificial neural networks, ANFIS, and RBFNN models were trained to predict malaria parasitemia stages from sensor responses, demonstrating enhanced sensitivity and accuracy compared to previously published SPR sensors for ring-stage malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: China sustains malaria microscopist competency during elimination phase
articles.title_original: Development of human resources in malaria microscopy during and after the malaria elimination phase in China.
articles.details: Analysis of microscopist training and certification in China (2017–2024) through WHO External and National Competency Assessment courses. Among 64 WHO-ECAMM participants, 29 achieved Level 1 and 17 Level 2 certification across 29 of 31 provinces. Among 72 NCAMM participants, 10 achieved Level A (highest) and 33 Level B. Years of microscopy experience correlated moderately with competence (ρ = 0.4, P < 0.01); species identification scores improved significantly (Z = −7.3, P < 0.01). Proposed multilevel framework supports sustained diagnostic capacity.

articles.headline: First report of P. bubalis Type I in domestic Indian water buffaloes
articles.title_original: Clinical, hematobiochemical and phylogenetic studies of bubaline malaria in Indian water buffaloes (Bubalus bubalis).
articles.details: Molecular and clinical study of 56 water buffaloes identified P. bubalis infection in 8.93% (5/56) by PCR and 5.36% (3/56) by blood smear microscopy. Infected buffaloes showed clinical signs including fever, tachycardia, tachypnea, and pale conjunctival mucous membranes with hematobiochemical abnormalities (marked anemia, thrombocytopenia, hyperbilirubinemia, hypoalbuminemia). Phylogenetic sequence analysis confirmed P. bubalis Type I, the first report in domestic Indian water buffaloes. Human rapid diagnostic tests targeting pLDH antigen showed negative results for P. bubalis.

articles.headline: Extracellular vesicles mediate P. falciparum heat shock adaptation via ESCRT machinery
articles.title_original: Extracellular vesicles participate in proteostasis and heat shock adaptation in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study using PfVps60 knockout P. falciparum identified extracellular vesicles (EVs) as mediators of heat shock adaptation. 44.8% of EV-associated proteins overlapped with aggregation-prone proteins. Loss of PfVps60-dependent ESCRT machinery impaired export of heat shock chaperones (PfHsp70-x, PfHsp110) in EVs, reduced cytosolic PfHsp70-1 induction, and caused early parasite loss of viability after thermal stress. EVs from heat-stressed parasites partially rescued heat shock tolerance in PfVps60KO parasites dose-dependently, suggesting EV-mediated intercellular communication during febrile episodes.

articles.headline: Spatial repellent optimization improves malaria control impact in Western Kenya
articles.title_original: Optimizing the impact of a novel spatial repellent on malaria incidence in Western Kenya.
articles.details: Without an abstract, specific details about the spatial repellent formulation, study design, comparison groups, or quantitative impact on malaria incidence cannot be provided from the title alone.

articles.headline: IgG responses to PfHRP2 correlate with malaria severity
articles.title_original: Differential IgG responses to PfHRP2 across the malaria severity spectrum.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 240 samples assessed Pfhrp2 gene copy numbers and IgG responses in mild, severe, and cerebral malaria cases using qPCR and ELISA. While Pfhrp2 copy numbers were similar across severity groups, IgG responses to PfHRP2 were significantly higher in severe, cerebral, and severe non-cerebral malaria versus mild malaria (P<0.0001), with positive correlations to age and var P5 peptide (P<0.01). Host immune response to PfHRP2 appears more critical than gene copy variation in determining disease severity.

articles.headline: Wealth index, not net ownership, predicts malaria in Mozambican children
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria among children under five living in net-owning households in Mozambique from the 2022-3 Demographic and Health Survey.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 1,924 households from 2022-23 Mozambique DHS survey. Household net use showed no significant association with child malaria after controlling for wealth. Wealth showed dose-response: middle-income households 43% less likely (aOR=0.57; 95% CI 0.38-0.84; P<0.0048), highest-income 84% less likely (aOR=0.16; 95% CI 0.09-0.27; P<0.0001) compared to lowest-income. Nampula and Cabo Delgado regions showed highest malaria odds (aOR=18.97 and 14.70 respectively; P<0.0005).

articles.headline: Light microscopy misses 25% of malaria infections in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Diagnostic gaps in light microscopy for malaria case management and control in Dilla Zuria Health Facility, southern Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 474 cases at Dilla Zuria Health Facility comparing light microscopy to qPCR. Microscopy-qPCR discordance was 24.7% (95/384) for positive cases and 18.8% (17/90) for negative cases. Misdiagnosis rates: 21% for P. falciparum (48/228), 28.3% for P. vivax (32/113), and 34.8% for mixed infections (15/43). Notably, 18.8% of microscopy-negative cases were qPCR-positive, likely due to submicroscopic infections or technical errors. Enhanced training and quality assurance needed to optimize malaria case management.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to transmission-blocking vaccine efficacy study
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Multi-antigen transmission-blocking malaria vaccine elicits Th1-biased antibody responses and SMFA-confirmed oocyst reduction in Anopheles stephensi" [Vaccine 84 (2026) 128683].
articles.details: Corrigendum notice; no substantive content in title to summarize.

articles.headline: Peritrophin-48 essential for Plasmodium transmission in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Molecular characterization of midgut Peritrophin-44 and Peritrophin-48 and their role in Plasmodium development.
articles.authors: Dubey A, Chauhan VK, Chaitanya RK
articles.details: Functional characterization of Peritrophin-44 and Peritrophin-48 in Anopheles stephensi midgut, identified as Class III chitin-binding proteins expressed throughout larval and adult lifecycles with peak expression in 4th-instar larvae and adults. Both proteins significantly upregulated following infectious blood meal during ookinete invasion. RNAi silencing increased mosquito mortality and reduced fecundity; Per-48 knockdown most severely impaired development, causing significant oocyst burden reduction. Per-44 and Per-48 are essential peritrophic matrix components modulating vector competence, representing viable transmission-blocking intervention targets.
articles.notable_reason: First functional characterization identifies midgut peritrophins as transmission-blocking targets

articles.headline: Delayed tafenoquine efficacy insufficient for P. vivax radical cure
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Delayed Tafenoquine Administration for Radical Cure of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: A Systematic Review.
articles.details: Systematic review of five databases through January 2026 identified two randomized trials (1996-1999) meeting inclusion criteria. Both evaluated single-dose tafenoquine (500-600 mg) administered day 4-6 following 3-day chloroquine course for P. vivax treatment. Based on limited data using higher-than-recommended tafenoquine doses, insufficient evidence exists to definitively recommend delayed administration. However, data suggest delayed tafenoquine likely superior to chloroquine alone and preferable to 14-day primaquine regimen when adherence is unlikely.

articles.headline: Machine learning biosensor design for glucose and malaria detection
articles.title_original: Machine Learning Surface Plasmon Resonance Biosensor for Simultaneous Detection of Glucose and Plasmodium falciparum Biomarkers.
articles.details: Theoretically designed surface plasmon resonance biosensor for simultaneous label-free detection of blood glucose and P. falciparum HRP-II from single sample. Multilayer optical stack optimized using Transfer Matrix Method and machine learning regression model trained on 18,000 simulated data points (R²=0.997 glucose, 0.994 HRP-II). Achieved angular sensitivities of 221.3 deg/RIU for glucose and 212.7 deg/RIU for HRP-II, with detection limits of 0.15 mg/dL and 0.8 ng/mL. Estimated per-chip material cost USD 1.50-4.00.

articles.headline: High-dose primaquine cuts P. vivax recurrence from 17% to 9%
articles.title_original: Analysis of the effect of primaquine dose on efficacy, safety, and tolerability in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: a systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis of 1,378 patients across 5 Ethiopian antimalarial efficacy studies (2000-2024) with ≥28 days follow-up. Cumulative incidence of P. vivax recurrence by day 180: 61.0% (95% CI 55.3-66.8) without primaquine, 16.6% (95% CI 12.0-22.7) with low-dose (3.5 mg/kg), and 8.9% (95% CI 6.4-12.4) with high-dose (7 mg/kg). High-dose versus low-dose: adjusted HR 0.40 (95% CI 0.24-0.68; p=0.001). Both doses demonstrated acceptable tolerability with clinically significant hemolysis uncommon in G6PD ≥30%.
articles.notable_reason: IPD meta-analysis demonstrates high-dose primaquine substantially superior to low-dose

articles.headline: Vector complexity and deforestation complicate Southeast Asian zoonotic malaria
articles.title_original: Zoonotic malaria in Southeast Asia's changing landscapes: vector complexity, challenges, and future strategies.
articles.details: Comprehensive review of Plasmodium knowlesi and emerging zoonotic Plasmodium species in Southeast Asia. Key challenges include species complex morphological similarity complicating accurate vector identification, rapid landscape changes (deforestation, agricultural expansion, habitat fragmentation) altering mosquito bionomics, and limited vector control approaches suited to zoonotic transmission. Environmental disturbances promote outdoor and forest-associated biting and shift transmission toward human settlements. Misidentification of vectors leads to inaccurate assessments of vector competence and transmission dynamics. Emphasizes need for integrated, context-specific, One Health strategies.

articles.headline: Serological surveillance supplements conventional diagnostics in malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Serological surveillance for malaria elimination in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review (PRISMA-ScR) of serological surveillance in Southeast Asia, searching three databases (January 2000-May 2025); 33 studies across 7 countries met criteria (26 cross-sectional, 5 longitudinal, 1 prospective cohort, 1 control trial). Research shifted from high- to low-transmission contexts with increasing use of multiplex assays and protein microarrays. P. falciparum and P. vivax predominated; recent studies added P. knowlesi and vector antigens. Seroconversion rate estimation and machine learning effectively identified historical transmission declines and residual hotspots missed by microscopy or molecular methods.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr nets maintain 92% efficacy versus 40% for PBO nets
articles.title_original: Durability of the insecticidal activity of next-generation insecticide treated nets distributed for malaria control in Mozambique: Findings from the New Nets Project (2020-2022).
articles.details: Durability study of next-generation ITNs distributed in Mozambique (2020-2022), collected at 6, 12, and 24 months post-distribution. Net types: pyrethroid-only (MAGNet, DuraNet, Olyset Net), pyrethroid-PBO (Olyset Plus), pyrethroid-pyriproxyfen (Royal Guard), and pyrethroid-chlorfenapyr (Interceptor G2). All nets retained high efficacy against susceptible mosquitoes (100% meeting WHO standards). Against resistant mosquitoes: Olyset Plus declined 73% (6 months) to 40% (24 months); Interceptor G2 consistently ≥92%. Chemical analysis showed major PBO and chlorfenapyr losses (8% and 32% remaining at 24 months). Supports prioritizing chlorfenapyr nets in pyrethroid-resistant areas.

articles.headline: Saquinavir competitively inhibits P. falciparum Plasmepsin V
articles.title_original: Repurposing amide-based drugs unveils inhibition mechanisms of plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin V enzymatic activity for antimalarial therapy.
articles.details: Investigation of three approved amide-based drugs (favipiravir, 5-fluorouracil, saquinavir) as inhibitors of P. falciparum Plasmepsin V, an essential aspartic protease required for parasite protein export into host red blood cells. All three compounds bound with affinities ~10⁴ M⁻¹. Saquinavir showed competitive inhibition with IC50 0.35 ± 0.01 μM and Ki 0.14 ± 0.02 μM by interacting with catalytic residues Asp118 and Asp365. 5-FU showed mixed-type inhibition; favipiravir was a weak non-inhibitory binder. Results suggest repurposing potential for selective Plasmepsin V inhibitors as antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: Regional malaria burden disparities widen in Somalia despite declining incidence gaps
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal inequalities in malaria incidence and mortality among children aged 0-4 years in Somalia: a subnational analysis, 2010-2019.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of modeled P. falciparum and P. vivax incidence and mortality among children aged 0–4 years across Somalia's 18 regions, 2010–2019. P. falciparum incidence peaked at 51.6% in Awdal (2015), remained elevated in central and southern regions (Bay 32.0%, Juba Dhexe 31.1%); P. vivax stayed low. Despite narrowing incidence gaps (ratio fell from 5.6 to 1.9), mortality inequalities widened, with the difference rising from 33.9 to 118.5, and children in central and southern Somalia over four times more likely to die than those in least affected regions.

articles.headline: Prenatal malaria exposure impairs early childhood growth outcomes in Ghana
articles.title_original: Prenatal and child malaria and child growth: evidence from a Ghanaian pregnancy cohort.
articles.details: Prospective study of 1,144 children from Ghana pregnancy cohort examined associations between prenatal malaria (21.9% exposed) and early childhood malaria (45.9% infected, age 0–12 months) with growth trajectories. Prenatal malaria associated with poorer length growth (adjusted OR=1.46, 95% CI 1.05–2.02), poorer length-for-age z-scores (aOR=1.53, 95% CI 1.14–2.05), and smaller head circumference (aOR=1.43, 95% CI 1.04–1.98). Early childhood malaria associated with poorer length-for-age z-scores (aOR=1.12, 95% CI 1.01–1.24). Prenatal malaria showed trend toward persistent stunting (aOR=1.63, p=0.076).

articles.headline: P. vivax dominates malaria in Papua, Indonesia; treatment guideline adherence suboptimal
articles.title_original: Epidemiological patterns and healthcare utilization among malaria patients in Sorong City, Papua, Indonesia: A cross-sectional analysis of national surveillance data.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 3,953 laboratory-confirmed malaria cases in Sorong City, Papua, 2024. P. vivax predominated (65.5%); nearly all infections uncomplicated (99.9%); microscopy was primary diagnostic tool (70.1%). Critically, 21% of patients received non-standard antimalarial regimens. Older age (aOR 1.02), non-Papuan ethnicity (aOR 1.77), and residence in moderate- or low-endemicity areas (aOR 2.01–4.57) predicted hospital attendance; children <15 years were more likely hospitalized (aOR 1.9, 95% CI 1.18–3.06).

articles.headline: P. vivax widespread in Duffy-negative Central Africa with critical diagnostic blind spots
articles.title_original: Epidemiological insights and genetic diversity of the Duffy binding protein of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative Cameroonians.
articles.details: First integrated epidemiological and genetic characterization of P. vivax in Duffy-negative Central Africans: screening of 1,373 samples identified 10.8% prevalence in hospital patients (86/793) and 5.5% in community (32/580), all Duffy-negative. PvDBP1 sequencing of 75 isolates revealed common mutations (I379L 74.1%, E225K 61.3%) suggesting adaptive evolution; phylogenetic analysis clustered Cameroonian isolates with Botswana strains. Critically, PvLDH-based RDTs failed to detect >85% of qPCR-confirmed infections, exposing massive diagnostic gap.
articles.notable_reason: Challenges long-standing assumptions; reveals adaptive P. vivax in Duffy-negative populations and 85%+ RDT diagnostic failure in Central Africa

articles.headline: Herbal plant remedies persist for malaria in Nigeria despite prescription drug access
articles.title_original: Family traditions, accessibility and cost shape the continued reliance on Cajanus cajan and Azadirachta indica for malaria and typhoid despite prescription medicines in urban and rural Nsukka, Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 400 respondents (200 urban, 200 rural) examined use of Cajanus cajan (pigeon pea) and Azadirachta indica (neem) for malaria and typhoid. Rural residents relied more on herbal remedies due to accessibility, affordability, and family traditions; urban respondents despite higher education also used herbal extracts and alternated with prescription drugs. Residence, occupation, and age significantly predicted knowledge and use (P<0.05); education did not. Perceived efficacy and high prescription costs sustained continued reliance on herbal remedies.

articles.headline: CNBP regulates critical IL12β-IFN-γ axis during Plasmodium blood-stage infection
articles.title_original: Cellular nucleic acid-binding protein (CNBP) dependent cytokine programming shapes host defense against Plasmodium infection.
articles.details: Mechanistic study defined CNBP (cellular nucleic acid-binding protein) as transcriptional regulator of IL12β during P. falciparum, P. chabaudi, and P. berghei infections. CNBP rapidly translocated to nucleus upon exposure to infected erythrocytes and was required for optimal IL12β induction in dendritic cells. Genetic ablation in mice resulted in elevated peak parasitemia, impaired parasite clearance, and infection relapse. Spleens showed reduced IL12β-IFN-γ transcripts and altered T-cell composition. CNBP identified as pivotal modulator of immunity with potential as therapeutic target.

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum malaria with gastrointestinal onset mimics acute gastroenteritis
articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Presenting with Gastrointestinal Symptoms in South Sudan: A Case Report.
articles.authors: Tran AD, Nguyen HT, Pham HK
articles.details: Case report of 29-year-old Ghanaian man in South Sudan presenting with nausea, vomiting, and diarrhea, initially diagnosed as acute gastroenteritis. After 24 hours developed high-grade fever, headache, tachycardia, hypotension; RDT positive for P. falciparum (parasitemia ++); abnormal labs included elevated bilirubin (39.6 µmol/L), creatinine (120 µmol/L), prolonged prothrombin time (INR 1.84), thrombocytopenia (50×10⁹/L). Treated with IV artesunate and supportive care; became afebrile by day 3. Case highlights risk of diagnostic delay when severe malaria presents with gastrointestinal manifestations.

articles.headline: Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model optimized for P. vivax vaccine evaluation
articles.title_original: Development of novel Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model for the evaluation of Plasmodium vivax blood stage vaccines and immunoprophylactics.
articles.details: Characterized Aotus nancymaae non-human primate model for P. vivax blood-stage vaccine and immunoprophylactic evaluation using Vietnam-IV strain. Optimal infectious dose 2.5×10⁶ infected RBCs produced consistent infection kinetics (coefficient of variation 15–23%). CBC showed increased lymphocytes (7.0–8.5×10⁹/L) and decreased neutrophils (0.6–1.2×10⁹/L) and platelets (23–68×10⁹/L) during acute infection. Pilot testing of monoclonal antibody mAb 099100 against DBP-II showed partial protection in 1/3 monkeys versus 0/3 controls. Model established as foundation for emerging P. vivax vaccine testing.

articles.headline: P. malariae neglected parasite with widespread asymptomatic carriage in Europe
articles.title_original: Ten years of Plasmodium malariae infections in an International Health Reference Center in Barcelona: temporal trends and insights.
articles.details: Retrospective study of malaria cases at Barcelona reference center (June 2015–May 2025) screened 6,293 individuals; 404 (6.4%) malaria-positive, of which 55 (13.6%) infected with P. malariae. Most P. malariae infections (94.5%) detected in asymptomatic sub-Saharan African migrants during routine screening with very low parasitemia (≤80 parasites/µL); 13/55 (23.6%) had been living in Spain >12 months, supporting long-term persistence. One symptomatic traveler experienced clinical relapse after artemisinin-based combination therapy. Findings highlight P. malariae as silent reservoir potentially hindering elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Intravenous artesunate in artemisinin-resistant severe malaria in Uganda
articles.title_original: Intravenous Artesunate in Artemisinin-Resistant Severe Malaria in Uganda.
articles.details: No abstract available; unable to extract specific results, study design, sample size, or resistance mechanisms from title alone.

articles.headline: Anti-CSP antibodies achieve high-avidity binding through conserved or variable Fab interactions
articles.title_original: Structural basis for conserved and distinct antigen recognition by a lineage of malaria-protective antibodies.
articles.details: Structural study determined X-ray and cryo-EM structures of two malaria-protective monoclonal antibodies (Fab 7160, Fab 7118) derived from VH3-49/VK2D-29 germline lineages, both binding PfCSP NANP repeats with high affinity (KD 6–10 nM). Fab 7160 formed germline-encoded inter-Fab homotypic interactions (resembling previously characterized mAb 399), while Fab 7118 adopted distinct binding mode without inter-Fab contacts. CDR loop variations modulated antibody conformation, homotypic Fab-Fab interactions, and epitope engagement. Findings define structural versatility of VH3-49/VK2D-29 class with implications for germline-targeting vaccine design.

articles.headline: ATAD3A mitochondrial protein essential for P. falciparum parasite viability
articles.title_original: ATAD3 megadalton complex in Plasmodium falciparum is essential for mitochondrial and cellular viability.
articles.details: Investigated ATAD3A homolog (PF3D7_0707400) in P. falciparum mitochondria as potential antimalarial drug target. Protein is essential for parasite survival and present in megadalton complex critical for multiple mitochondrial processes: RNA stability, membrane potential, ultrastructure, and protein import. ATAD3A previously implicated in childhood mitochondrial diseases in multicellular eukaryotes. This is first characterization of ATAD3A in unicellular organisms. Findings expand understanding of apicomplexan mitochondrial biology and identify a new potential antimalarial drug target.

articles.headline: Burkina Faso malaria control: high intervention coverage, modest incidence decline
articles.title_original: Evolution of the Performance of the Malaria Control Program in Burkina Faso: Analysis from 2020 to 2024.
articles.details: Ecological analysis of national malaria control program (2020–2024) using secondary time-series data. LLIN use reached 81% (pregnant women) and 68% (children <5), SMC 97.8%, RDT 98.5%, and ACT 97.96%; incidence fell from 537.5 to 446.4 per 1,000 annually. 2030 forecast predicts rise to 473.06 per 1,000 (CI 242.81–703.28), with supply chain and resource constraints limiting further gains.

articles.headline: French standardized malaria diagnosis recommendations for endemic travelers
articles.title_original: [Malaria diagnosis: French recommendations ANOFEL/LABAC].
articles.details: Clinical guideline standardizing malaria diagnosis methods in France per NF EN ISO 15189. Any fever in returning travelers from endemic areas warrants urgent Plasmodium testing. Diagnostic approach: detect parasites or DNA via sensitive methods (PCR or thick smear); if positive, perform thin smear for species identification and parasitemia estimation. RDT is supplementary, not standalone.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine unaffected by CYP2D6; primaquine metabolizer-dependent recurrence
articles.title_original: Differential impact of Tafenoquine and Primaquine on Plasmodium vivax recurrence: the role of CYP2D6 variants.
articles.details: Case-control study in Manaus, Brazil, of P. vivax patients treated with tafenoquine (TQ) or primaquine (PQ) for relapse prevention with CYP2D6 genotyping. TQ efficacy independent of CYP2D6 activity (P = 0.172). PQ showed metabolizer-dependent recurrence risk: intermediate/poor metabolizers had higher recurrence than normal metabolizers (P = 0.036), with overall elevated risk by six months (P = 0.016). CYP2D6 variability significantly influences PQ but not TQ response.

articles.headline: PCR detects threefold more malaria in pregnant women than routine tests
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic malaria infections and inadequate IPTp-SP coverage among pregnant women in rural Burkina Faso: An observational study.
articles.details: Observational study (June 2023–June 2024) at one Burkina Faso health center: 515 pregnant women screened at first ANC with PCR, microscopy, and RDT. PCR prevalence 30.1% (155/515); among 183 tested by all three methods, PCR detected 23.5% vs microscopy 8.2% vs RDT 8.7%—routine diagnostics missed two-thirds. Primigravidae had 2.14× infection risk (95% CI 1.21–3.79). Each additional IPTp-SP dose reduced prevalence (aPR 0.89, 95% CI 0.80–0.99); only 18.9% achieved ≥5 doses. PCR-confirmed infection associated with low birthweight (aPR 1.52, 95% CI 1.01–2.28).

articles.headline: Francesco Torti pioneered cinchona bark for malaria in 1712
articles.title_original: Francesco Torti (1658-1741) and his description on the therapeutic use of quinine: the first effective method of treating malaria based on an extract of the bark of the Peruvian cinchona tree.
articles.details: Historical review of Francesco Torti (1658–1741) and his development of cinchona bark as antimalarial therapy. His 1712 book detailed intermittent fever forms and therapeutic responses. Torti's meticulous patient care and clinical observation during the humoral-theory era contributed to cinchona adoption across Europe and marked a shift toward patient-centered, experience-based medicine.

articles.headline: P. falciparum VEGF internalization: proposed mechanism in placental malaria
articles.title_original: Placental malaria and VEGF modulation by P. falciparum: Bridging the immunopathological gap using evidence from human cerebral and severe, non-cerebral malaria studies.
articles.details: Literature review proposing mechanisms linking placental malaria (PM) to P. falciparum VEGF biology. PM involves sequestration of parasitized erythrocytes, immune activation, and dysregulation of angiogenic factors (VEGF, sVEGFR1, Angiopoietins, sEndoglin, PLGF). Evidence from fatal cerebral malaria and in vitro 3D7 culture suggests parasites internalize host VEGF and express VEGFR2. Dysregulated VEGF signaling may cause placental insufficiency and fetal growth restriction; quantitative VEGF/VEGFR studies needed in PM.

articles.headline: Dynamic species distribution models reveal seasonal vector suitability in Brazil
articles.title_original: Predicting short-life-cycle species suitability via dynamic species distribution models: Applications in dengue and malaria epidemiology in Brazil.
articles.details: Developed Dynamic Species Distribution Models (DSDMs) integrating monthly occurrence records (2016–2022) and climate data for Ae. aegypti and An. darlingi using MaxEnt. Performance: Ae. aegypti AUC = 0.8, TSS = 0.6; An. darlingi AUC = 0.9, TSS = 0.8. Vapor pressure most influential predictor. Incidence Rate Ratio maps showed significant associations between climatic suitability, case counts, and transmission (p < 0.01), enabling identification of seasonal vulnerability windows.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria death in traveler: untreated myeloma and absent prophylaxis
articles.title_original: Malignant malaria: autopsy findings and morphological features.
articles.details: Case report of 54-year-old woman with untreated multiple myeloma who traveled to Central Africa without antimalarial prophylaxis. Presented with fever, headache, and asthenia; rapidly progressed to multiorgan failure; died day 4. Autopsy confirmed P. falciparum cerebral malaria with microvascular sequestration causing ischemia and perivascular hemorrhages. Highlights importance of malaria diagnosis in febrile travelers and clinical role of post-mortem confirmation.

articles.headline: Combined ITN and IPTp uptake only 28.9% of pregnant women in SSA
articles.title_original: Use of insecticide-treated nets (ITN) and intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) for malaria prevention and its associated factors among pregnant women in Sub-Sahara Africa.
articles.details: Pooled cross-sectional analysis of recent MIS/DHS data from 18 sub-Saharan African countries (2018–2024), weighted sample 63,561 pregnant women. Combined ITN (previous night) and IPTp (≥3 doses) utilization: 28.9% (95% CI 28.4–29.3%); neither intervention: 17.6% (95% CI 17.2–18.0%). Significant predictors identified: age, education, household head sex, wealth index, media exposure, residence, and sub-region. Authors recommend community and individual-level strategies to increase coverage toward 90% malaria reduction goal by 2030.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 34.1% in febrile patients; ITN gaps a major risk
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria and associated factors among febrile patients visiting Gissa Health Center, Awi Zone, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study (Feb–Apr 2024) at one Ethiopian health center: 411 of 424 febrile patients tested. Malaria prevalence 34.1%: P. falciparum 20.9%, P. vivax 9.7%, mixed 2.4%. Multivariable predictors: age <5yr (AOR 2.77, 95% CI 1.09–7.00), age 6–15yr (AOR 3.85, 95% CI 1.49–9.92), illiteracy (AOR 2.83, 95% CI 1.13–7.08), no regular ITN use (AOR 2.08, 95% CI 1.04–4.17), and absent IRS (AOR 1.54, 95% CI 1.29–3.24). Authors recommend strengthened integrated prevention.

articles.headline: Cambodia malaria elimination: scoping review identifies gaps and challenges
articles.title_original: Advancing malaria elimination in Cambodia: a scoping review of progress, challenges, and strategic priorities.
articles.details: Scoping review of 25 publications (2014–2024) on malaria elimination in Cambodia. While transmission and case fatality have declined through various programs, substantial barriers persist: service gaps, inadequate infrastructure, low coverage, poor access, and individual factors, especially in high-risk areas. Climate change and rising temperatures increase malaria risk. Authors recommend prioritizing surveillance, case management strengthening, and equitable access to malaria care programs.

articles.headline: pfhrp2 deletions absent in false-negative RDTs; HRP2 tests reliable
articles.title_original: Absence of false negative RDT results due to pfhrp2 deletion among symptomatic malaria infections in Kenya: findings from the 2022 Kenya national longitudinal surveillance of pfhrp2/3 deletions.
articles.details: Cross-sectional surveillance (2022) in Kenya: 5,394 symptomatic samples from 10 counties tested with PfHRP2-based and PfLDH-based RDTs; discordant and random positive samples sequenced for HRP2/3 and pfk13 mutations. HRP2 and PfLDH RDTs showed similar positivity (44% vs 45%), 1.26% discordance. None of discordant samples carried pfhrp2 deletions; overall 4.5% pfhrp2-only and 12.4% pfhrp3-only deletions, no double deletions. pfk13 mutations 0.7% (A578S, A675V). HRP2-based RDTs remain reliable; limited artemisinin resistance spread detected.

articles.headline: Pf41 antibodies detect past malaria exposure in low-transmission Indonesia
