articles.title_original: Pf41 serological profiling reveals potential malaria exposure in low transmission setting in the Menoreh Hills, Central Java, Indonesia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional seroepidemiological study of 120 endemic village residents and 24 unexposed controls in Menoreh Hills, Indonesia (January-June 2025). Measured anti-Pf41 IgG antibodies via protein microarray. Overall seropositivity 71% (85/120), with village variation: Kembaran 93%, Sedayu 83%, Wadas 60%, Kemejing 47%. Nighttime livestock feeding and repellent use associated with higher antibody levels (p<0.001 vs controls). Results indicate serological evidence of prior malaria exposure remains detectable despite decline in clinical cases, supporting Pf41 serology as surveillance complement for low-transmission settings.

articles.headline: Phase 3 trial: mechanical ventilation and hypertonic saline ineffective in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Adjunctive Mechanical Ventilation or Intravenous Hypertonic Saline in Malawian Children with Cerebral Malaria and Highly Increased Brain Volume: A Randomized, Phase 3 Clinical Trial.
articles.details: Phase 3 RCT in Malawi (2018-2023) of 57 children (6 months-12 years) with cerebral malaria and severe brain swelling (BVS 6-8), randomized to mechanical ventilation (n=23), 3% hypertonic saline (n=17), or usual care (n=17). Neither intervention reduced 7-day mortality (RD -0.075, p=0.59 for ventilation; RD 0.002, p=1.0 for HTS). Hypertonic saline increased cerebral blood flow and was associated with worse outcomes in children with baseline cerebral hyperemia (RR 3.0, p=0.04). Findings demonstrate neuroprotective strategies are ineffective without endotype-guided approaches.
articles.notable_reason: Phase 3 trial proves mechanical ventilation, hypertonic saline harmful

articles.headline: Precipitation and illiteracy predict malaria incidence in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Climatic and social health determinants of malaria incidence in Tete, Mozambique: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling of routine data (2016-2022).
articles.details: Bayesian spatio-temporal analysis of malaria in Tete Province, Mozambique (2016-2022) using INLA with Besag spatial structure and AR1 temporal process. Precipitation and illiteracy rate identified as significant predictors, associated with 6.7% and 1.6% increases in malaria incidence respectively. Incidence peaked shortly after rainy season onset and declined during winter. Macanga, Chiuta, and Moatize districts showed highest risk; Tete City lowest. Study demonstrates climatic and social determinants shape regional transmission dynamics.

articles.headline: Humidity shifts temperature-dependent population growth in malaria vector
articles.title_original: Beyond Temperature: Relative Humidity Systematically Shifts Juvenile Thermal Performance and Projected Population Growth in a Malaria Vector.
articles.details: Laboratory study of Anopheles stephensi juvenile life history traits under varying temperature and humidity conditions. Relative humidity altered thermal performance and effects on population growth rate. Integration of humidity-dependent juvenile trait responses into population growth model showed temperature-humidity interactions can change predictions of environmental suitability. Temperature-only models may over- or underestimate environmental suitability when humidity interactions omitted. Results highlight importance of incorporating humidity alongside temperature when assessing vector responses to climatic variation.

articles.headline: Historical GIS study of 19th-century malaria mortality in Ontario
articles.title_original: Ecology of Malaria Mortality: A Spatiotemporal Mapping Approach.
articles.details: Geographic analysis of probable malaria deaths in Ontario, Canada (1869-1900, n=2,683) using GIS and historical spatial datasets. Mortality was significantly clustered but became progressively less concentrated over time. Settlement proximity was the strongest predictor of mortality density; once settlement effects accounted for, distances to wetlands were not independently associated. Results indicate reorganization of malaria risk from environmentally constrained ecologies toward anthropogenic landscapes shaped by settlement and infrastructure development.

articles.headline: Theoretical photonic crystal biosensor design for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Highly Sensitive 1D Photonic Crystal Biosensor for Early Malaria Detection via Refractive Index.
articles.details: Theoretical investigation of one-dimensional photonic crystal biosensor for malaria detection using Transfer Matrix Method. Optimized structure achieved maximum sensitivity 977.74 nm/RIU (or 1,163.22 nm/RIU at increased defect thickness) with quality factor 93.8 × 10³. Detection limit approximately 10⁻⁶ RIU over refractive index range 1.371-1.402 corresponding to different malaria infection stages. Design shows strong potential for rapid, cost-effective diagnosis but remains theoretical without clinical validation.

articles.headline: Low-density P. vivax infections and G6PD deficiency in Myanmar
articles.title_original: Sub-RDT Plasmodium vivax infections and G6PD deficiency in Kayin State, Myanmar.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey in Kayin State, Myanmar (2020-2021) of 5,509 individuals screened by mRDT, rRT-PCR, ELISA, and G6PD testing. mRDT detected 0.5% positivity; rRT-PCR detected P. vivax 14.3% (317/2,219), P. falciparum 8.3% (185/2,219). Sub-RDT P. vivax prevalence 14.1% (311/2,211). G6PD deficiency 21.7% in males, 10.6% in females. No significant association between G6PD status and sub-RDT P. vivax infection. Coexistence of low-density reservoirs and G6PD deficiency poses major barrier to P. vivax elimination.

articles.headline: Poverty is dominant predictor of pediatric malaria in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Geographic Patterns and Drivers of Paediatric Malaria in Nigeria: Walking in the shadow of Poverty.
articles.details: Spatial epidemiological analysis of pediatric malaria across Nigeria using published data and GIS clustering techniques. Significant spatial clustering identified; hotspots in various regions, coldspots in southwest Nigeria. Poverty identified as dominant predictor of paediatric malaria incidence (R²=0.435). Study concludes free anti-malaria treatment for children and poverty reduction may reduce malaria burden in Nigeria.

articles.headline: Machine learning predicts malaria prevalence in rural Nigeria
articles.title_original: Application of machine learning models in predicting malaria prevalence in Nigeria: an analysis of the 2015-2020 demographic and health surveys.
articles.details: Machine learning models trained on 2020 Nigerian Demographic and Health Survey geospatial covariate datasets. Random Forest Regressor (RFR), multiple linear regression, and ridge regression models developed with rigorous cross-validation and holdout testing. Test set R² scores: RFR 0.9937, multiple linear regression 0.9916, ridge 0.9924. RFR performance improved with increased training data volume. Demonstrates potential for proactive malaria risk prediction to enable targeted resource allocation to underserved communities.

articles.headline: Autochthonous and asymptomatic malaria in elimination-phase Iran
articles.title_original: Malaria dynamics in Iran: insights from autochthonous and asymptomatic.
articles.authors: Sadeghi F, Nodez SMM, Khosravani M
articles.details: Surveillance data from Hormozgan Province, Iran (April 2018-May 2023) analyzing imported and autochthonous malaria cases (n=289). Population predominantly male (87.5%), ages 11-29. P. vivax represented 78.9% of cases. Afghans 56.7% (imported), Pakistanis 30.8% (imported), Iranians 12.5% (autochthonous). Asymptomatic cases 45.25%. Cases peaked in 2023, especially in Jask County. Imported malaria significantly contributes to maintenance of transmission among native populations in elimination settings.

articles.headline: Pfs230 shows high genetic conservation across East Africa
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 gene in four East African countries supports its potential as a malaria transmission‑blocking vaccine candidate.
articles.details: Molecular epidemiological analysis of 1,312 Pfs230 sequences from Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, and Uganda (MalariaGEN Pf7 database). Low nucleotide diversity across four countries (π=5.9×10⁻⁴). Haplotype diversity 0.999 with 496 haplotypes; 8.1% shared between populations. Non-synonymous/synonymous substitution ratio 0.43. No significant population differentiation by FST or phylogenetic analysis. Tajima's D negative in all countries. High genetic conservation supports Pfs230 as promising transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Uganda HMIS managers face gaps in data use and supervision
articles.title_original: Attitude and practices of district-level HMIS managers towards malaria routine reporting in Uganda: a 2024 cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 34 HMIS managers across 15 malaria endemicity regions in Uganda (January-June 2024). All reviewed reports; 75.8% documented errors but only 31.6% could show documentation. 81.6% of expected reports received, 25.4% only after active follow-up. 93.9% received data quality queries (mean 4.2 per facility). Approximately 70% preferred quarterly supervision; 39.4% received 1-2 visits; 51.5% provided supervision over past year. Gaps identified in standard operating procedures, scheduled supervision, and advanced analytical skills.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genome variants associate with sickle cell trait in Ghana
articles.title_original: Associations between the Plasmodium falciparum genome and sickle haemoglobin identified in mild malaria cases from Ghana.
articles.details: Genome-wide association analysis of 1,368 Ghanaian children with mild malaria identified parasite variants Pfsa1, Pfsa3, and candidate Pfsa4 (in FIKK4.2 kinase) that associate with HbS genotype. Pfsa1 and Pfsa3 replicated findings from The Gambia and Kenya; Pfsa4 replicated in Mali. No significant associations with haemoglobin C. Results add complexity to understanding human-parasite coevolution.

articles.headline: Raman spectroscopy and machine learning detect malaria parasite stages
articles.title_original: Population-Level Raman Biochemical Staging of Malaria in Human Red Blood Cells Using Interpretable Machine Learning.
articles.details: Study developed a Raman spectroscopy framework with interpretable machine learning to identify biochemical signatures of P. falciparum-infected red blood cells at different developmental stages. Using confocal and spatially offset Raman spectroscopy (SORS) on synchronized cultures, hemoglobin-associated vibrational modes were identified as discriminative features via SHAP analysis. Subsurface detectability was confirmed under tissue-like scattering conditions, suggesting potential for noninvasive, stage-aware diagnostics.

articles.headline: Malaria exposure alters neutrophil subsets; possible Burkitt lymphoma link
articles.title_original: The impact of malaria-induced neutrophil subset shift and a link to Burkitt lymphoma.
articles.details: Prospective study of Kenyan children characterized neutrophil populations using multiparameter flow cytometry in malaria-exposed, Burkitt lymphoma-diagnosed, and malaria low-exposed groups. Malaria-exposed children showed increased frequencies of aged neutrophil subsets and reduced mature active subsets compared to controls. Immature neutrophils were positively correlated between malaria-exposed and BL groups (rs=0.7; p<0.0001), suggesting malaria-associated shift toward immature neutrophils that could promote tumor development.

articles.headline: ICOS polymorphisms show no effect on P. vivax antibody responses
articles.title_original: No Evidence for Association Between ICOS (a T-Cell Co-Stimulatory Molecule) Gene Polymorphisms and Anti-MSP-1(19) IgG Response in Venezuelan Endemic Plasmodium vivax Malaria.
articles.details: Study of 91 Venezuelan patients with P. vivax infection examined the relationship between ICOS gene polymorphisms (three SNPs) and IgG antibody responses against merozoite surface protein PvMSP19. PvMSP19-specific IgG was detected in 70.3% of participants. ICOS genotypes and haplotypes showed no significant associations with antibody levels across any inheritance models, though antibody responses positively correlated with prior malarial episodes (r=0.803, p<0.0001).

articles.headline: Anopheles darlingi shows geographic structure and insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Reassessing malaria-transmitting mosquito evolution with a neotropical lens.
articles.details: Commentary on a whole-genome analysis of Anopheles darlingi, the major Neotropical malaria vector. The study revealed geographically structured populations with no evidence of cryptic sympatric species and widespread insecticide resistance achieved through convergent evolution. These findings address longstanding knowledge gaps about evolutionary drivers in this key vector.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine 96% efficacy in Laos; lumefantrine resistance emerges
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine for the treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Laos: a therapeutic efficacy study coupled with genomic and in vitro phenotypic analyses.
articles.details: Therapeutic efficacy study of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) in 195 patients (median age 16 years) across four southern Laos provinces, 2019-2023. Day-28 PCR-adjusted adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) was 96% (95% CI 92-98) with 2% treatment failure and 2% reinfection rates. One recrudescent isolate showed markedly reduced lumefantrine susceptibility (IC50 59.9 nM, 2.5-fold higher than median) with high artemisinin resistance (ring-stage survival 35.8%), harboring pfkelch13 R539T. Overall, R539T was found in 5% (9/190) and C580Y in 23% (43/190) of sequenced isolates, both associated with day-3 parasitemia (p=0.044).
articles.notable_reason: Emerging lumefantrine resistance in Southeast Asian ACT first-line therapy

articles.headline: P. vivax antigen diversity affects some but not most serological markers
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of parasite antigen genetic diversity on performance of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria: a multicentre observational diagnostic accuracy study.
articles.details: Observational diagnostic accuracy study analyzed global P. vivax genetic variation in 14 serological markers and developed new antigen haplotypes for eight antigens. Testing in Brazil (923 participants) and Thailand (774 participants) cohorts revealed extensive diversity in MSP5 (π=14.8 × 10⁻³) and DBPII (π=7.7 × 10⁻³), with minimal sequence similarity to reference Sal-1 strain (0.4% for MSP5 to 99% for S16). While two antigens showed strong regional immunogenicity differences (RBP2a, DBPII), five of eight antigens maintained performance for identifying recent P. vivax exposure despite high genetic diversity.

articles.headline: Delayed malaria diagnosis linked to severe disease in US pediatric cases
articles.title_original: Malaria in Children at 9 US Hospitals: 2016-2023.
articles.details: Retrospective study of 171 children treated for malaria at 9 US hospitals from 2016-2023. Most had traveled to West Africa to visit friends and relatives. Fever was the most common symptom (90%), followed by abdominal symptoms (66%). Severe malaria was diagnosed in 32% of patients, with no deaths recorded. Notably, delayed diagnoses occurred in 26% of cases at similar rates across all hospitals and were identified as a risk factor for severe malaria, associated with longer hospitalizations and increased morbidity.

articles.headline: Nine imported malaria cases in malaria-free Iranian province detected
articles.title_original: Challenges and strategies in malaria control: A case series from East Azerbaijan Province, 2023-2024.
articles.details: Retrospective case series of nine malaria cases identified in East Azerbaijan Province (malaria-free for ~two decades) during 2023-2024. All patients had traveled to endemic areas (southern Iran or countries including Pakistan, Kenya, and Sierra Leone). Infections comprised three P. falciparum and six P. vivax cases confirmed by rapid diagnostic tests and microscopy. Eight patients required hospitalization. The presence of potential mosquito breeding habitats combined with infected traveler influx during peak mosquito season may create conditions for local transmission.

articles.headline: Gap between malaria health facility access and utilization in India identified
articles.title_original: Comprehensive Analysis of Health Service Utilization for Febrile Illness and Malaria in Malaria-Endemic States in India: LLINs as a Pillar of Control Strategies.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 37,849 households (177,644 individuals) across ten malaria-endemic Indian states examined healthcare and preventive practices. Government health facilities were accessible within median 2.5 km (IQR 1-5 km), yet only 57.4% of febrile individuals sought care from government facilities or providers within 24-48 hours. While 89.2% of households reported adequate prevention knowledge and 73.6% reported regular LLIN use, gaps in healthcare-seeking behavior were identified as crucial drivers of malaria burden. Lack of formal education and tribal population status emerged as significant risk factors.

articles.headline: Crystal structure of P. vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor solved
articles.title_original: Crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor.
articles.journal: Acta Crystallogr F Struct Biol Commun
articles.details: Crystal structure of Plasmodium vivax macrophage migration inhibitory factor (PvMIF) solved at 1.8 Å resolution (PDB entry 9b0m). PvMIF regulates both adaptive and innate immune responses and contributes to P. vivax pathogenesis; plasma PvMIF concentrations correlate with disease severity. The parasite MIF binds host CD74 and suppresses host MIF-CD74 signaling. PvMIF crystallized in space group P63 with the biological unit forming the prototypical MIF trimer. PvMIF is recognized as a candidate malaria vaccine.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance approach proposed for insecticide resistance monitoring
articles.title_original: Polygenic scores for genomic surveillance of insecticide resistance in malaria control.
articles.details: Opinion article proposing genomic approaches, including polygenic scores, for accurate characterization of insecticide resistance patterns in malaria vectors. The authors argue that genomic methods can quantify the impact of insecticide resistance on primary vector control tools (long-lasting insecticide nets and indoor residual spraying) before intervention failure occurs. Current phenotyping approaches are limited by insensitivity, making genomic surveillance potentially crucial for evidence-based vector control strategies.

articles.headline: Computationally stabilized P. falciparum proteins enable drug discovery
articles.title_original: Stabilizing Plasmodium falciparum proteins for small molecule drug discovery.
articles.details: ProteinMPNN-guided sequence design generated stabilized surrogate constructs of three therapeutically relevant P. falciparum proteins (PfBDP1-BRD, PfBDP4-BRD, PfK13-KREP) while maintaining wild-type-like structure and binding thermodynamics. The stabilized PfBDP4-BRD enabled determination of a 1.25 Å co-crystal structure with a newly identified inhibitor. This approach provides a practical tool for biophysical screening and structure-enabled drug discovery against otherwise difficult malaria proteins.

articles.headline: P. vivax relapses sustain mosquito transmission despite treatment
articles.title_original: Parasite and gametocyte dynamics and infectivity to mosquitoes during recurrent Plasmodium vivax infections: A longitudinal study from Ethiopia.
articles.details: Longitudinal follow-up of 176 Ethiopian P. vivax patients treated with chloroquine plus 14-day primaquine revealed 54.6% (96/176) experienced recurrent parasitemia. Gametocyte density was lower during asymptomatic parasitemia (p<0.001) but similar between baseline and recurrent symptomatic infections. Mosquito infection rates were 70.2% overall (117/166 baseline, 50/72 recurrent symptomatic), both strongly correlated with asexual parasite density (ρ=0.29, p<0.0001) and gametocyte density (ρ=0.32, p<0.0001). After adjusting for gametocyte density, relapsing infections showed lower mosquito infection rates (OR 0.48, 95% CI 0.26-0.87, p=0.016).

articles.headline: Fixed pvmdr1 mutations detected in North India P. vivax isolates
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of chloroquine resistance in Plasmodium vivax isolates from North India: Analysis of single nucleotide polymorphisms in pvmdr1 gene.
articles.details: Among 7530 screened patients, 75 had P. vivax monoinfection; 36 isolates were sequenced. M908L and F1076L mutations were present in 100% of isolates, while T898A was detected in 5.6%. Phylogenetic analysis showed Indian isolates clustered with haplotypes from Madagascar and Afghanistan. Although these pvmdr1 polymorphisms were fixed, their role in clinical chloroquine resistance remains uncertain, particularly in the absence of Y976F.

articles.headline: BISSAU: genetically distinct Anopheles form in Gambia
articles.title_original: Genomic insights into the population structure of malaria vectors of the Anopheles gambiae complex in The Gambia reveals distinctiveness and spatio-temporal stability of BISSAU molecular form.
articles.details: Population genomic analysis of 2283 specimens (An. coluzzii, An. gambiae, An. arabiensis, An. melas, BISSAU molecular form) using >200K SNPs showed BISSAU as a genetically discrete, temporally stable lineage reproductively isolated from sympatric Anopheles species with no admixture detected. BISSAU occurs with higher probability on the north bank of Gambia river, 10–120 km from the seacoast, in breeding sites with brackish/fresh water and alluvial deposits.

articles.headline: Microsporidia MB infection alters mosquito cuticular hydrocarbons
articles.title_original: Behaviour of the malaria vector Anopheles arabiensis infected with the endosymbiont Microsporidia MB: host seeking and cuticular hydrocarbons associated with mating.
articles.details: Field-collected and laboratory-reared An. arabiensis infected with Microsporidia MB showed no preferential attraction to human odours versus cattle odours in olfactometer assays. Logistic regression found no association between infection and selective feeding on humans over livestock, birds, and monkeys. Chemical analysis revealed differences in cuticular hydrocarbon profiles between infected and non-infected males; however, infection status was not strongly driven by specific hydrocarbons on random forest analysis.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 vaccines provide moderate sustained child protection
articles.title_original: Efficacy and immunogenicity of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines: - Systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 8 studies showed RTS,S/AS01 provided 23% malaria risk reduction (RR 0.77) in 6–12-week-olds. In 5–17-month-olds, both vaccines achieved RR 0.61 with efficacy declining over time, supporting the need for booster doses. RTS,S generated strong antibody responses and was safe across age groups. R21/Matrix-M showed high efficacy (event rate 0.22) and strong antibody response but more frequent adverse events than RTS,S.

articles.headline: Innate immune memory may explain recurring malaria infections
articles.title_original: The case for exploring innate memory in Plasmodium infection.
articles.details: Review proposing that innate immune memory—epigenetic reprogramming of immune responses to sequential infections independent of adaptive immunity—may explain dynamics of recurrent Plasmodium infections in endemic areas where individuals receive infected mosquito bites daily. Authors postulate that innate memory in non-immune cells may have significant consequences for the complex Plasmodium lifecycle.

articles.headline: DNA markers track metabolic insecticide resistance in vectors
articles.title_original: Metabolic insecticide resistance DNA markers in malaria vectors.
articles.details: Review synthesizing knowledge on DNA markers in detoxification genes (cytochrome P450s, glutathione S-transferases) in major vectors: An. gambiae (CYP6P4, CYP6P3, GSTe2) and An. funestus (CYP6P9a/b, CYP6P4a/b, CYP9K1, GSTe2, ASL). These markers support resistance management by assessing spatiotemporal resistance evolution, impact on control tools, cross-resistance patterns, and fitness costs.

articles.headline: New technologies illuminate Plasmodium development in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Demystifying Plasmodium development within the Anopheles mosquito.
articles.details: Review summarizing how advances in microscopy, single-cell RNA sequencing, and genetic screening have revolutionized understanding of Plasmodium sporogony (parasite development within Anopheles mosquitoes). These technologies address challenges posed by parasite bottlenecks in numbers and ploidy changes that can rescue mutant phenotypes.

articles.headline: Letter raises concerns about spatial emanators for malaria
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control.
articles.details: No abstract provided. Title indicates letter expressing concerns regarding a study of spatial emanators as a malaria control method.

articles.headline: Authors reply to spatial emanators malaria concerns
articles.title_original: Concerns regarding study of spatial emanators for malaria control - Authors' reply.
articles.authors: Allan RJ, Cross CL, Messenger LA
articles.details: No abstract provided. Title indicates author response to concerns raised about spatial emanators for malaria control.

articles.headline: Pan-Plasmodium RT-qPCR assay for blood donor screening
articles.title_original: Development and validation of a pan-Plasmodium molecular assay as a confirmatory method for blood donor screening.
articles.details: RT-qPCR assay targeting conserved 18S rRNA detects all five human-infecting Plasmodium species for supplemental malaria donor screening. Analytical 95% limit of detection was 5.3 iRBCs/mL (95% CI: 3.2–8.6). Clinical sensitivity was 100% across all five species; clinical specificity was 100% (95% CI: 99–100%) with no cross-reactivity to Babesia microti. Amplicon sequencing enabled species-level identification.

articles.headline: Monocyte metabolic shift during P. vivax infection
articles.title_original: The mitochondrial metabolic shift in inflammatory monocytes during Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.details: Review of metabolic-mitochondrial changes in activated monocytes during P. vivax infection. Infected monocytes display enhanced phagocytic and microbicidal activity but produce high proinflammatory cytokines and reactive oxygen species, potentially contributing to acute P. vivax disease symptoms. Article synthesizes laboratory findings with mechanistic insights from experimental malaria models.

articles.headline: Malaria declining, Chagas increasing in non-endemic Brazil
articles.title_original: Malaria and Chagas disease in non-endemic settings in Brazil: a comparative analysis of national surveillance data (2007-2024).
articles.details: Analysis of 10,146 cases (9,755 malaria, 391 ACD) from 2007-2024 in non-endemic Brazil using national surveillance data. Malaria showed significant decrease (β = -0.0174, p=0.0397), predominantly in males and adults aged 20-39 years with 89.7% non-autochthonous; ACD increased significantly (β = 0.0342, p=0.0267) in females and those ≥40 years with 53.2% oral transmission. Forecast models indicate stable incidence through 2030.

articles.headline: Micro-level hotspot mapping enables targeted malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Identification of micro-level malaria hotspots and its implications for achieving malaria elimination target in a remote district of India.
articles.details: Surveillance analysis of 18,217 malaria cases from 2017-2024 in Sonbhadra district, India, showing 95% reduction in test positivity, with annual parasite incidence declining from 2.7 to 0.1. P. falciparum incidence fluctuated late-period, rising to 0.09 in 2024 after near-total suppression in 2022-2023. Spatiotemporal analysis identified hotspot in Chopan block with high-risk clusters of both P. falciparum and P. vivax at sub-district level.

articles.headline: Dengue-malaria coinfection prevalent in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Molecular detection and epidemiological characterization of dengue and malaria coinfection in the Guiriko region, Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 408 samples in Burkina Faso (June-September 2023) found 17.64% (72/408) with recent dengue and malaria coinfection, and 7.10% (29/408) with acute coinfection. All 29 RT-PCR-positive dengue cases were PCR-positive for malaria (68.96% by microscopy). Elevated axillary temperature and high CRP (mean 83.79 mg/l) associated with acute dengue-malaria coinfection.

articles.headline: Avian hemoparasites identified in Colombian tropical dry forest
articles.title_original: Molecular detection of avian hemoparasites (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus) in resident and migratory birds from an urban fragment of tropical dry forest in northern Colombia.
articles.authors: Rodríguez MM, Flores FS, Castro LR
articles.details: Molecular screening of blood samples from 217 birds (27 resident and 7 migratory species) in Colombian tropical dry forest fragment detected Haemoproteus or Plasmodium infection in 11 individuals across 6 species, yielding 5.1% overall prevalence. Several documented host-parasite associations represent new records for Colombia, highlighting novel avian-parasite interactions in this threatened ecosystem.

articles.headline: Erythrophagocytosis in Plasmodium falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Peripheral Blood Erythrophagocytosis in Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title, study examines red blood cell phagocytosis in P. falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Infant malaria immunity: vaccine advances and challenges
articles.title_original: Malaria immunity in the infant: progress and pitfalls.
articles.details: Review of infant malaria immunity and vaccination strategies. RTS,S/AS01E and R21/Matrix-M vaccines are now deployed; novel platforms including live-attenuated parasites and anti-CSP human monoclonal antibodies are in development. Current vaccines elicit suboptimal immunity in infants under 5 months; transplacental maternal antibodies and fundamental developmental differences in infant immune system modulate infection and vaccination responses.

articles.headline: Reimagining radical cure for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Reimagining radical cure for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title, article discusses approaches to radical cure and hypnozoite elimination for P. vivax malaria.

articles.headline: High-dose primaquine and tafenoquine reduce P. vivax recurrence
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and safety of 7-day high-dose primaquine and single-dose tafenoquine versus 14-day low-dose primaquine in patients with Plasmodium vivax malaria (EFFORT): a multicentre, open-label, randomised, controlled, superiority trial.
articles.details: EFFORT trial (960 enrolled across Ethiopia, Pakistan, Indonesia, Cambodia; 2021-2024) compared three P. vivax radical cure regimens in patients with G6PD ≥70%. At 6 months, P. vivax recurrence was 13.0% with 7-day high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg) and 12.6% with single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) versus 18.5% with standard 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg). Tafenoquine showed significantly lower recurrence (HR 0.64, p=0.041) with superior tolerability (22.2% drug-related adverse events versus 42.9% for high-dose primaquine).
articles.notable_reason: Large multicentre RCT demonstrating shorter, better-tolerated P. vivax radical cure regimens

articles.headline: WBC count discrepancy as potential malaria diagnostic clue
articles.title_original: Discrepancy Between White Blood Cell Count Channels as a Clue to Malaria Infection.
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title, study examines white blood cell count patterns or channel discrepancies as a potential diagnostic indicator for malaria infection.

articles.headline: Environmental variability critical for GMM development
articles.title_original: Beyond the static lab: environmental variability in genetically modified mosquito target gene identification for malaria control.
articles.details: Perspective article addressing the 'lab-to-field' gap in genetically modified mosquito (GMM) development for malaria control. Static laboratory conditions used for target gene discovery do not reflect natural environmental variability affecting Anopheles and Plasmodium biology, mosquito innate immunity, and vector competence. Authors argue insufficient consideration of environmental stress during gene discovery risks developing GMMs with compromised field function, particularly as climate change escalates.

articles.headline: Historical audit questions malaria therapy for neurosyphilis
articles.title_original: Malaria therapy for neurosyphilis at Mont Park Hospital for the insane in Australia, 1927-1928.
articles.details: Archival analysis of malaria therapy for general paralysis of the insane at Mont Park Hospital, Australia (1927-1928) conducted by psychiatrist Reginald Ellery. Clinical and research factors including diagnostic drift, adjunct interventions, and healthy-patient selection biased results upward. Medical record review revealed notably less positive patient outcomes than Ellery's published accounts, refuting historical claims of malaria therapy effectiveness.

articles.headline: P. vivax CSP genetic variation shapes vector competence in Thailand
articles.title_original: Genetic signatures of Plasmodium vivax Circumsporozoite Surface Protein during malaria resurgence in Thailand.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 69 P. vivax isolates from Thailand's 2022-2023 resurgence. Sequencing identified VK210 (n=66) and VK247 (n=3) variants; VK210 showed 21 haplotypes with 13-20 repeat motifs, with haplotype H2 most prevalent (~50%). In Anopheles dirus membrane feeding assays, H2 produced more salivary-gland sporozoites per oocyst than other haplotypes, suggesting repeat-region polymorphisms modulate vector competence.

articles.headline: RBC polymorphisms increase malaria infection risk in Thai border populations
articles.title_original: Red blood cell membrane protein polymorphisms related to risk of malaria infection in the population living in malaria-endemic areas of Thailand: An analytic cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 616 samples (270 malaria-infected, 346 uninfected) from Thai-Myanmar and Thai-Malaysian borders. MM antigen was more common in infected vs. uninfected individuals (61.1% vs. 46.5%, OR 3.7, 95% CI 2.0-6.7, p<0.0001). FYA/FYA or FYA/FYB genotypes with wild-type Duffy promoter showed increased risk (76.1% vs. 46.9%, OR 4.7, 95% CI 3.0-7.3, p<0.0001). Most populations in these regions carry genetic predisposition to malaria infection.

articles.headline: Novel natural product inhibitors of P. falciparum folate pathway identified
articles.title_original: Discovery of novel Plasmodium falciparum PfDHFR-TS inhibitors from ConMedNP natural compounds: a multi-computational approach.
articles.details: In silico screen of 2754 compounds from Central/West African medicinal plants against PfDHFR-TS. Mean binding affinity -8.8 kcal/mol; 75% surpassed artemether (-8.0 kcal/mol). QSAR model validation achieved test R²=0.8321. Top three candidates (RDC0118, RDC0119, CA0001) showed superior binding (-13.0 to -13.5 kcal/mol). CA0001 demonstrated favorable ADMET profile (non-hepatotoxic, low environmental toxicity) and structural stability. Positions CA0001 as promising candidate for experimental validation.

articles.headline: LNS plus IPTp shows limited impact on malaria incidence or anemia
articles.title_original: Effects of Combined Lipid-Based Nutrient Supplements and Malaria Preventive Treatment during Pregnancy on Malaria, Maternal Nutrition, and Birth Outcomes: A Scoping Review.
articles.details: Scoping review of 17 studies (based on 5 main trials) assessing lipid-based nutrient supplements (SQ-LNS, BEP, LQ-LNS) combined with intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp). LNS supplementation did not significantly reduce malaria incidence, anemia, or maternal nutritional status. Some studies showed improvements in birth outcomes among specific subgroups with baseline undernutrition. Evidence remains inconclusive; further research needed in vulnerable populations.

articles.headline: Malaria Scoring System validated for non-endemic emergency departments
articles.title_original: External validation of the Malaria Scoring System in a non-endemic emergency department.
articles.details: Retrospective single-center validation of the Malaria Scoring System in 365 ED patients (114 malaria-infected, 31.2%). Malaria patients showed higher prevalence of fever (96.5% vs. 84.1%, p<0.001), thrombocytopenia (78.1% vs. 8.4%, p<0.001), lymphopenia (71.9% vs. 32.7%), and anemia (53.5% vs. 35.1%, all p<0.001). MSS demonstrated excellent discriminatory ability (ROC AUC 0.902, 95% CI 0.869-0.935) with good calibration (Hosmer-Lemeshow X²=1.68, p=0.195).

articles.headline: Ferroptosis in macrophages drives malaria severity via HIF-1α/HMOX1 axis
articles.title_original: HIF-1α/HMOX1-Mediated Ferroptosis in Macrophages Exacerbates Malaria Progression.
articles.details: Plasmodium infection triggers ferroptosis in splenic macrophages via HIF-1α downregulation and aberrant HMOX1 upregulation, causing iron overload. Ferroptosis inhibitor Fer-1 reduced biomarkers in vitro and in vivo. P. yoelii-infected GPX4 conditional knockout mice (GPX4flox/flox Lyz2Cre+) displayed worsened health and increased parasitemia vs. controls. Identical ferroptosis signature confirmed in monocytes from malaria patient PBMCs. Targeting HIF-1α/HMOX1 represents potential host-directed intervention.

articles.headline: MSP8 knockout protects mice from lethal malaria via CCL19-CCR7 immunity
articles.title_original: Deletion of Plasmodium yoelii Merozoite Surface Protein 8 Protects Mice From Lethal Blood-Stage Malaria and Is Associated With Enhanced Splenic CCL19-CCR7 Signaling.
articles.details: P. yoelii studies combining MSP8 immunization and CRISPR/Cas9 deletion. PyMSP8 antisera partially inhibited merozoite invasion in vitro; immunization conferred partial protection against lethal challenge. ΔMSP8 knockout parasites caused reduced peak parasitemia, attenuated splenomegaly, and self-resolving infection. ΔMSP8 upregulated splenic CCL19 and preserved CCR7 with increased IFN-γ. CCR7 functional knockdown partially reversed protection. Suggests MSP8-targeted vaccines or CCL19-CCR7 augmentation as therapeutic strategies.

articles.headline: COVID-19 caused 36.9% decline in reported childhood malaria in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on reported malaria incidence in children under five years of age in Cameroon: an interrupted time series analysis with regional heterogeneity (2017-2022).
articles.details: Interrupted time series analysis of routine DHIS2 surveillance (Jan 2017–Dec 2022) assessing COVID-19 impact on reported malaria incidence in children under 5. March 2020 interruption associated with 36.9% immediate decline in reported incidence (IRR 0.631, 95% CI 0.539-0.738, p<0.001) followed by 3.1% monthly recovery (IRR 1.031, p<0.001). Counterfactual estimated 69,708 notification deficit (2020–2021) and 74,831 excess (2022). Marked regional heterogeneity observed.

articles.headline: Tropical ocean patterns drive malaria via soil moisture in Malawi
articles.title_original: Tropical oceans drive variability in soil moisture and malaria in Malawi.
articles.details: Analysis of malaria incidence with observational and modeled climate data identifying mechanistic links in Malawi. Warm tropical Atlantic drives wet conditions, increasing malaria cases; warm Indian Ocean drives hot, dry conditions, reducing cases. Soil moisture identified as critical pathway linking remote ocean patterns to local disease dynamics. Future climate change projected to reduce soil moisture by 2100 (magnitude unquantified), potentially altering transmission patterns. Findings support development of early warning systems.

articles.headline: Two RNA-binding proteins maintain P. vivax hypnozoite quiescence
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.
articles.details: Differential transcriptomics identified two hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins in P. vivax. Functional studies in P. yoelii (which lacks hypnozoites) showed individual expression of each protein blocked liver stage schizogony, leaving parasites small and uninucleate. Protein-binding screens revealed distinct, enriched RNA motifs, indicating parasite suppression via sequence-specific RNA targeting. Proteins named IESI-1 and IESI-2, providing first functional evidence for molecular mechanisms maintaining hypnozoite dormancy.

articles.headline: P. falciparum proteome maps reveal organelle organization and evolution
articles.title_original: The spatial proteome of the Plasmodium falciparum schizont illuminates the composition and evolutionary trajectories of its organelles.
articles.details: Spatial proteomics study using hyperLOPIT generating high-resolution protein localization maps for P. falciparum invasive blood stages (late schizonts). Data encompass 3000 proteins, resolving 24 distinct cellular niches. Supervised machine learning classified 1646 proteins into specific compartments including exported host cell sites. Comparative genomic analysis reveals spatial patterns of cell evolution and adaptive pressures shaping Plasmodium species development.

articles.headline: Optimal age sampling improves malaria seroconversion rate estimation
articles.title_original: Optimizing age-structured sampling for estimating the seroconversion rate in malaria seroepidemiology: a simulation study.
articles.details: Monte Carlo simulations optimizing age-structured sampling for estimating seroconversion rate (SCR) in malaria serology. In stable transmission: older sampling improved low-transmission precision; younger sampling improved high-transmission precision. With unknown seroreversion rate, younger individuals and balanced age distribution optimized precision. Under changing transmission, post-change birth cohorts best estimated new SCR. Reanalysis of published Sri Lanka data showed alternative sampling strategies could substantially improve both precision and statistical power.

articles.headline: Single-cell RNA-seq reveals distinct P. falciparum responses to antimalarials
articles.title_original: Single-cell analysis of Plasmodium falciparum transcripts after drug perturbation identifies feedback regulation as well as increased transmission potential.
articles.details: Applied scRNA-seq to millions of infected RBCs to characterize P. falciparum transcriptional response to artemisinin, atovaquone, and ganaplacide. Each drug produced distinct cell populations; artemisinin enriched gametocyte genes while atovaquone enriched pyrimidine biosynthesis. Single-cell analysis can classify drug classes and identify feedback regulation mechanisms, augmenting mechanism-of-action deconvolution.

articles.headline: Blighia sapida compounds inhibit P. falciparum DHOD in silico
articles.title_original: In-silico study of the bioactive compounds of Blighia sapida koenig revealed a novel Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor for malaria.
articles.details: In silico screening identified 21 compounds from B. sapida leaf extract with interactions to PfDHODH. Three compounds showed strong binding affinities (−8.1 to −10.0 kcal/mol) compared to positive control (−8.9 kcal/mol). Compound 2-methyl-6-(4-methylenecyclohex-2-en-1-yl)hept-2-en-4-one displayed best stability (RMSD=0.12, RMSF=0.10) with favorable drug-likeness and ADMET profiles.

articles.headline: Geographic and social disparities drive malaria vulnerability in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Topological Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process (ToF-AHP) for geospatial assessment of malaria vulnerability in Nigeria.
articles.details: Topological Fuzzy AHP framework assessed malaria vulnerability across Nigeria using five indicators. Northeast and Northwest regions showed high vulnerability driven by low maternal education, poverty, and limited ANC attendance. Maternal and gender-linked factors accounted for 34.63% of vulnerability; 62.52% of very high malaria incidence zones coincided with high vulnerability areas.

articles.headline: PfMD3 zinc-finger protein regulates male gametocyte development and transmission
articles.title_original: The CCCH-type zinc-finger Pf MD3 promotes translation for malaria parasite transmission.
articles.details: PfMD3 knockout parasites showed male fertility defect with decreased mosquito infectivity. PfMD3 is an RNA-binding protein that binds male gamete-associated mRNA and regulates lineage-specific translation through interactions with mRNA processing and nuclear export proteins. Protein depletion reduced gametocyte protein levels, establishing PfMD3 as critical for male gamete development.

articles.headline: Ecological niche modeling maps An. pseudopunctipennis habitat suitability in Argentina
articles.title_original: MODELING THE POTENTIAL CLIMATIC SUITABILITY OF ANOPHELES PSEUDOPUNCTIPENNIS, THE HISTORICAL VECTOR OF MALARIA IN ARGENTINA.
articles.details: MaxEnt model using 312 occurrence records predicted climatically suitable areas for An. pseudopunctipennis vector (validation AUC 0.83, 10% omission rate). Suitable conditions concentrated in northwestern Argentina (Jujuy, Salta, Tucumán, Catamarca) with additional areas in Santiago del Estero, Formosa, Chaco. Supports surveillance in malaria-free country at reintroduction risk.

articles.headline: Population mobility associates with malaria in low-endemic Indonesia during COVID-19
articles.title_original: Mobility and malaria incidence in Indonesia's low-endemic areas during the COVID-19 period.
articles.details: Examined 480,575 mobility events across four districts (2020-2022). Pooled multivariable analysis: increased mobility associated with higher malaria incidence (IRR 1.25, 95% CI 1.21-1.30); screening intensity (IRR 2.33, 95% CI 2.22-2.45); COVID-19 positivity (IRR 1.12, 95% CI 1.08-1.17). Malaria incidence outside Java 64% lower than Java. Relationship context-dependent, not causal.

articles.headline: South Korea malaria control shows initial gains then partial resurgence
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impact of South-North Joint Malaria Control Project on spatiotemporal transmission dynamics in South Korea using dynamic mode decomposition.
articles.details: Dynamic mode decomposition analyzed incidence across South Korea (2001-2024). South-North Joint Control Project (2008-2011) achieved 63% reduction between 2007-2011 and disrupted regional synchrony. 5-Year Re-Elimination Plan (2019-2023) decreased incidence ~49% from 2018-2021 but increased ~30% by 2023 versus 2018 baseline, with partial re-emergence of regional synchrony.

articles.headline: Extending seasonal malaria chemoprevention to older children reduces malaria in Niger
articles.title_original: Extension of seasonal malaria chemoprevention to older children: a pragmatic comparative study in two health districts in the Zinder region, Niger.
articles.details: Pragmatic comparative study (2021-2023) comparing SMC (SP+AQ) extended to ages 5-10 versus standard <5 years in Niger. Baseline uncomplicated malaria in ≥5 years: 33.34 per 1,000 (2021) versus 12.45 per 1,000 in <5-year-olds. SMC extension associated with gradual decline in uncomplicated and severe cases in intervention district but not control, supporting age expansion.

articles.headline: Oxford nanopore sequencing enables long-read P. falciparum genome surveillance
articles.title_original: Development of an Oxford nanopore sequencing technology-based whole genome sequencing method for Plasmodium falciparum to support malaria molecular surveillance.
articles.details: Optimized ONT-WGS protocol for P. falciparum from whole blood and dried blood spots. Tween-Chelex 100 extraction and combined human DNA depletion (NMDEK, sWGA) achieved >75% parasite DNA at >30× coverage. Achieved median reads >2 kb, 99.8% accuracy, detecting pfmdr1 and pfgch1 copy variations; high concordance with Sanger sequencing.

articles.headline: Psychosocial barriers dominate delays in malaria care-seeking in Malawi
articles.title_original: Three delays of malaria care-seeking in Malawi: a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 913 female caregivers identified barriers to prompt (same/next day) care-seeking. Delay 1 (decision-making) primary driver (aOR 0.58); key factors: incorrect symptom knowledge (aOR 0.71), negative care-seeking attitudes (aOR 0.58), not knowing where to seek care (aOR 0.19), far facility distance (aOR 0.67). Despite free services, initial decision phase remains critical bottleneck.

articles.headline: CD36 and ICAM-1 variants modulate sickle cell severity with malaria coinfection
articles.title_original: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes influence the severity of sickle cell anemia in a malaria-endemic region : Short title: Genetic variants in red blood cell adhesion-related genes in sickle cell anemia.
articles.details: Genetic study of 65 pediatric SCA patients identified CD36 rs3211891_C as novel anemia severity modulator and CD36 rs3211938_G as significant severity driver. ICAM-1 variants rs5491_T and rs5496_A impacted hematological severity. ICAM-1 rs5494_T associated with 5.63-fold increased malaria infection risk. Red blood cell adhesion gene variants influence SCA manifestations in malaria-endemic Angola.

articles.headline: PfGBP-130 ligand mediates NK cell activation against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: LFA-1 interaction with GBP-130 on Plasmodium falciparum-infected red blood cells mediates NK cell activation and parasite control.
articles.details: Identified PfGBP-130 as surface-expressed ligand on P. falciparum-infected RBCs that binds integrin LFA-1. LC-MS/MS confirmed high-confidence interaction with LFA-1 αI domain. PfGBP-130 promotes NK cell activation, degranulation, and contact-dependent killing of infected cells; neutralizing antibodies significantly impaired responses. Establishes PfGBP-130 as LFA-1 ligand mediating NK immunity and potential host-directed intervention target.

articles.headline: High placental malaria prevalence, adverse birth outcomes in Tanzania primigravidae
articles.title_original: High burden of histology-confirmed placental malaria and associated adverse birth outcomes among primigravidae in Northwestern Tanzania: a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 422 primigravidae at Sengerema hospital, Tanzania. Placental malaria prevalence was 39.8% by histopathology and 23.7% by microscopy; maternal peripheral malaria 19.7% and congenital malaria 6.9%. PM significantly associated with low birth weight (p=0.01), preterm birth (p=0.001), and small-for-gestational-age neonates (p=0.048). Fewer than four ANC visits (AOR 1.75, p=0.04) and moderate anemia (AOR 1.84, p=0.04) were significant predictors of PM.

articles.headline: Novel P. vivax-infecting virus increases transmission and alters immune response
articles.title_original: MaRNAV-1, an intracellular virus of Plasmodium vivax , is associated with increased parasite transmission and altered host immune response.
articles.details: Characterization of MaRNAV-1, an intracellular RNA virus present in P. vivax across blood, sporozoite, and liver stages. MaRNAV-1 presence positively associated with higher gametocyte abundance and increased oocyst prevalence/intensity in feeding assays. Viral loads higher in symptomatic versus asymptomatic infections and independently associated with elevated body temperature. MaRNAV-1 triggered antibody response, dendritic cell activation, Th1-biased immune shift with elevated IFN-γ, CXCL10, IL-1RA, and IL-6.

articles.headline: Fungal metabolites kill malaria vector larvae and adult mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Larvicidal and adulticidal activities of Purpureocillium lilacinum Secondary Metabolites against the Malaria Vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: Laboratory evaluation of secondary metabolites from Purpureocillium lilacinum against Anopheles gambiae. Purified leucinostatin A showed potent activity against fourth-instar larvae (LC90 9 µg/mL) and adult female mosquitoes (LD90 approximately 1 µg per mosquito), while pulixin had LC90 of 68 µg/mL against fourth-instar larvae. Authors conclude leucinostatin A's dual activity against larvae and adults may provide cost-effective, environmentally friendly vector control strategy.

articles.headline: pfgdh shows promise as alternative P. falciparum diagnostic target
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum glutamate dehydrogenase with known Plasmodium falciparum diagnostic targets using quantitative real-time polymerase chain reaction.
articles.details: Retrospective qPCR analysis of 150 P. falciparum-positive and 50 negative samples comparing five diagnostic targets. pfgdh sensitivity 69%, specificity 98%, AUC 83%, compared to pfhrp2 (72% sensitivity, 100% specificity, 86% AUC) and pfhrp3 (71%, 96%, 84% AUC). Authors conclude pfgdh represents a promising alternative to pfhrp2 for detecting P. falciparum infections, particularly where pfhrp2 gene deletions pose diagnostic challenges.

articles.headline: DHA-PPQ reduces maternal adverse events, not malaria in HIV+ pregnant women
articles.title_original: Maternal, Fetal, and Neonatal Outcomes of Additional Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine to Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole in Intermittent Preventive Treatment for Malaria in HIV-Positive Pregnancies: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of three randomized trials (1353 participants receiving 800/160 mg TMP-SMX with or without 40/320 mg DHA-PPQ three times). DHA-PPQ showed non-significant trends toward lower maternal parasitaemia (OR 0.83, 95% CI 0.52-1.33, p=0.44) and placental malaria (OR 0.66, 95% CI 0.40-1.08, p=0.10). Significantly reduced maternal adverse events (OR 0.78, p=0.003), including low hemoglobin and gastrointestinal events. No significant differences in fetal adverse events or birth outcomes.

articles.headline: First P. coatneyi culture reveals biological barrier to human infection
articles.title_original: First continuous in vitro culture of Plasmodium coatneyi reveals insights into barriers to human infection.
articles.details: First continuous in vitro culture of zoonotic Plasmodium coatneyi maintained in macaque erythrocytes for over 40 days (parasitaemia reaching 3% post-cryopreservation). While P. coatneyi successfully invaded human erythrocytes, asexual development was arrested post-invasion and parasitaemia fell below detection limits by day 9, identifying a putative biological barrier to sustained zoonotic transmission. Protocol enables functional studies of severe malaria pathogenesis and monitoring of host specificity.

articles.headline: EFFORT trial results on tafenoquine and primaquine for P. vivax discussed
articles.title_original: Dissemination of EFFORT trial results in Indonesia: a stakeholder meeting report on tafenoquine and primaquine for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: Report from national stakeholder meeting in Indonesia (May 2025) discussing EFFORT trial comparing high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg over 7 days) and single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) to standard 14-day low-dose primaquine for radical cure of P. vivax. Discussions addressed evidence gaps for tafenoquine with artemisinin combination therapies, feasibility of high-dose primaquine pending G6PD testing, and need for subnational strategies reflecting Indonesia's diverse epidemiology.

articles.headline: High malaria prevalence and infective vectors in Nigerian hospital wards
articles.title_original: Nosocomial malaria transmission risk in hospital wards in Abeokuta Nigeria: entomological and parasitological evidence from secondary healthcare facilities.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 134 inpatients and caregivers in two secondary hospitals in Nigeria. Malaria prevalence 74.6% overall (75.0% patients, 73.1% caregivers). Of 714 mosquitoes collected, 80 were Anopheles gambiae s.l.; 81.3% blood-fed with human blood, 38.8% parous, 2.5% positive for P. falciparum sporozoites. Only 3.7% of participants used mosquito nets during admission, indicating significant nosocomial malaria transmission risk.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 vaccines show protective efficacy in African children
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of RTS, S and R21 malaria vaccines in children under five in Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 randomized trials enrolling 27,178 children under five in Africa (21,589 RTS,S; 5,589 R21). RTS,S efficacy: 32% against first clinical malaria episode (HR 0.68, 95% CI 0.61-0.76), 29% against multiple episodes (IRR 0.71, 95% CI 0.55-0.91), 17-22% against severe malaria (RR 0.78-0.83). R21: 68% against first episodes (HR 0.32, 95% CI 0.26-0.39) and 67% against multiple episodes in phase 3 trial. SAE incidence 11.2% (RTS,S) and 3% (R21).

articles.headline: High malaria awareness but inconsistent prevention practices in South Africa
articles.title_original: Community and healthcare worker contributions to malaria prevention: a culturally tailored behaviour change approach in Jozini, South Africa.
articles.details: Cross-sectional mixed-method study in Jozini, South Africa of 384 community members and 15 healthcare workers. Community showed 96% malaria awareness, 92% correctly identified mosquito transmission, 97% high knowledge, 79% positive attitudes, and 64% good practices. Knowledge significantly associated with age (χ2=9.61, p=0.01) but not gender, education, employment, or residence duration. Healthcare workers demonstrated strong technical knowledge but faced operational challenges including training gaps, staffing shortages, and diagnostic/commodity shortages.

articles.headline: PfMDR1-ACT-451840 structure reveals antimalarial drug resistance mechanisms
articles.title_original: Structural and mechanistic insights into the inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum MDR1.
articles.details: Cryo-electron microscopy structure of P. falciparum Multidrug Resistance Protein 1 (PfMDR1) in complex with ACT-451840 (clinical phase I drug) at 3.42 Å resolution. ACT-451840 binds within PfMDR1's central cavity, locks protein in inward-open conformation, inhibits basal ATPase activity. Structure provides molecular explanation for how ACT-451840 resistance mutations lead to mefloquine sensitization and reveals selective inhibition mechanisms against PfMDR1 versus human ABCB1.

articles.headline: Multiple essential enzymes identified in P. falciparum pyrimidine synthesis pathway
articles.title_original: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Genetic analysis of three pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in P. falciparum. Knockdown of aspartate transcarbamoylase (PfATC) proved lethal, could not be rescued by apicoplast bypass; truncation of N-terminal domain impaired growth. Carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II (PfCPSII) with Plasmodium-specific insertions essential for proliferation. Deletion of dihydroorotase (PfDHO) in uracil-salvaging parasites resulted in uracil auxotrophy, confirming essential function. Findings identify multiple vulnerable nodes for antimalarial drug targeting.

articles.headline: Knowledge-practice gap drives silent malaria transmission in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and practices towards malaria in a peri-urban Cameroonian village: Persistent silent transmission despite satisfactory awareness.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 249 participants in Emana village, Cameroon (104 with complete data). KAP scores analyzed using non-parametric tests; malaria prevalence evaluated using chi-square tests and logistic regression. Overall malaria prevalence 24.9%, with prevalence in 5–18 years age group 13.25% (p=0.027). Knowledge and attitudes were satisfactory (p>0.05), but practices poor overall and significantly better in women (p=0.014). A persistent knowledge-to-practice gap contributed to silent and ongoing transmission.

articles.headline: PfEMP1 antibody gaps linked to cerebral malaria and severe anemia
articles.title_original: Distinct Plasmodium falciparum Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 1 Domain Cassette-Associated Antibody Gaps Specific to Cerebral Malaria and Severe Malarial Anemia.
articles.details: Case-control study using custom microarray of 79 PfEMP1 fragments from domain cassettes 1, 4, 5, 8, 13, 15 in Malian children with cerebral malaria (CM), severe malarial anemia (SMA), concurrent CM+SMA, or uncomplicated malaria controls. Children with severe malaria showed distinct antibody gaps: domain cassettes 1, 8, 13 for SMA; DC13 for CM. Few PfEMP1 domain cassettes showed differential seroreactivity between groups, but antibody responses to downstream domain couplets differed.

articles.headline: Essential RNA-binding protein PfSR-X2 modulates parasite gene expression
articles.title_original: The serine-arginine-rich protein PfSR-X2 modulates human malaria parasite gene expression during the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle.
articles.details: Characterized PfSR-X2 in P. falciparum asexual blood stages by sequence analysis, expression profiling, and conditional CRISPR-Cas9 knockdown. PfSR-X2 is essential—attempts to disrupt the gene were unsuccessful. Conditional depletion reduced parasite proliferation and impaired merozoite production. RNA-seq revealed marked stage-specific changes in genes for RNA metabolism, antigenic variation, host-parasite interactions, and ApiAP2 pathways, with strong perturbation of the rif gene family.

articles.headline: Cardiolipin synthase essential for parasite mitochondrial function
articles.title_original: A prokaryotic-like Cardiolipin Synthase is essential to maintain mitochondrial function, lipid homeostasis and survival of blood stage malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Demonstrated that P. falciparum possesses a prokaryotic-like cardiolipin synthase (PfCLS) essential for asexual blood-stage growth. Inducible knockdown of PfCLS kills parasites and impairs assembly of mitochondrial respiratory super-complexes. PfCLS-depleted parasites show hypersensitivity to Atovaquone and widespread perturbations in phospholipid homeostasis, TCA cycle, and amino acid metabolism.

articles.headline: Subclinical malaria common in Ugandan children with sickle cell disease
articles.title_original: Subclinical malaria in sickle cell disease in Northern Uganda: prevalence, predictors, and hematologic impact.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 274 SCD patients (median age 12 years, 54.4% male) in Northern Uganda (June–August 2025). Subclinical malaria (SCM) prevalence 23.4% by blood smear or RDT; microscopy-confirmed rate 11.7%. Rural residence was the only independent predictor. Smear-confirmed parasitemia associated with ~1 g/dL lower mean hemoglobin (6.87 vs 7.82 g/dL, p=0.0027). RDT-based definitions showed no association with hemoglobin differences.

articles.headline: LEF1 transcriptionally represses LAG3 on CD4+ T cells in malaria
articles.title_original: The LEF1-LAG3 axis regulates CD4(+) T cell function during Plasmodium yoelii NSM infection.
articles.details: Mouse model of P. yoelii NSM infection. Infection induced upregulation of LAG3 on splenic CD4+ T cells (confirmed by scRNA-seq and flow cytometry) with expanded CD4+ T cells showing increased activation markers (ICOS, CD69) and enhanced IL-10 and IFN-γ secretion. LAG3+CD4+ T cells displayed activated phenotype with elevated proliferation (Ki67+) and co-inhibitory receptors (PD-1, TIM-3, TIGIT). LEF1 expression was significantly downregulated post-infection and directly represses Lag3 transcription; Wnt agonist CHIR99021 dose-dependently reduced LAG3+CD4+ T cell frequency.

articles.headline: Prime-and-trap vaccination induces sterile liver-stage protection in mice
articles.title_original: A prime-and-trap vaccination strategy to evaluate minimal requirements for malaria liver stage protection in mice.
articles.details: Mouse vaccination study using prime-and-HDT trap with P. yoelii circumsporozoite protein (CSP). Hydrodynamic transfection trapped primed CSP-specific CD8+ T cells in the liver. Prime-and-HDT trap induced sterile protection against sporozoite challenge at CSP-HDT doses as low as 5 ng DNA, with protection threshold around 50 pg below which CSP-specific liver CD8+ T cell levels were minimal.

articles.headline: Malaria genomics field evolves toward decentralized surveillance
articles.title_original: Malaria Parasite Genomics: Decentralization, Diversification, and Development Goals.
articles.details: Review examining the evolution of malaria parasite genomics from descriptive discovery to global surveillance supporting public health decisions. Discusses decentralization and diversification of genomic data generation enabled by wider sequencing access and cost-effective targeted methods. Highlights emerging applications and key development needs for standardizing data formats, analysis pipelines, and sharing practices to translate genomic insights into action.

articles.headline: Climate change projected to expand malaria risk across Brazil by 2050
articles.title_original: Climate change and Plasmodium vivax Malaria Risk in Brazil: Developing adaptive tool for Brazilian Municipalities.
articles.authors: C M Sousa T, et al.
articles.details: Modeling study assessing P. vivax malaria risk under climate scenarios (RCP 4.5 and 8.5) for 2030 and 2050 in Brazilian municipalities using multilevel analysis. Key climate variables (maximum temperature, SDII, relative humidity) identified and binary logistic model applied. Results show growing malaria risk across Brazil under both scenarios, with greatest impact in the Amazon expanding to Southeast and Northeast by 2050. Maximum temperature increase most influential factor (β=0.35), followed by SDII (β=0.19) and relative humidity (β=0.12).

articles.headline: Genetic diversity of RIPR invasion protein characterized in P. vivax
articles.title_original: Unraveling the genetic diversity of RIPR, a key invasion protein in Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.details: Study of genetic diversity of RIPR (a key invasion protein) in P. vivax malaria. (Limited details available; abstract not provided.)

articles.headline: HDAC inhibitors reviewed as antimalarial drug leads
articles.title_original: Hydroxamic Acids as HDAC Inhibitor Drug Leads for Malaria.
articles.details: Review of histone/lysine deacetylase (HDAC) inhibitors as antimalarial drug leads for P. falciparum. Assessed structure-activity relationships for more than 750 hydroxamic acids with published in vitro activity against P. falciparum. HDACs regulate posttranslational lysine acetylation and control transcriptional and developmental processes essential to Plasmodium parasites, making them prospective drug targets. Review explores potency, selectivity, and physicochemical properties to guide investigation of hydroxamic acids for malaria drug development.

articles.headline: Complex multi-species malaria transmission and widespread insecticide resistance in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Complex non-falciparum transmission and widespread insecticide resistance in Penka-Michel, a hyperendemic malaria focus of the Cameroon highlands.
articles.details: 12-month longitudinal entomological survey (September 2023–August 2024) in Penka-Michel, Cameroon highlands. Three Anopheles vector species sustained hyperendemic transmission (EIR 839.5 infective bites/human/night): An. funestus (43.7%), An. gambiae (42.9%), An. ziemanni (13.5%). P. falciparum predominated (70.2%), but significant non-falciparum burden observed: P. malariae 27.5%, P. ovale 22.1%. An. funestus was principal driver (IR 7.8%, EIR 0.9 ib/h/n); An. gambiae (IR 5.4%) primarily vectored P. malariae. Widespread insecticide resistance in 70% of infected mosquitoes; L119F-GSTe2 mutations associated with P. ovale curtisi (IRR 1.15, p=0.03) and L1014F-kdr with P. malariae (IRR 2.2, p=0.04).

articles.headline: Regional differences in P. falciparum invasion ligand expression
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum invasion ligand gene transcript profiles in different populations.
articles.details: Comparative study of transcript levels of three eba genes and five Rh genes between Malaysian isolates from Sabah and Gambian isolates using qPCR. Significant differences found for three ligand genes: eba175 higher in Malaysia, eba181 and Rh2b lower in Malaysia. Expression patterns showed regional differentiation, suggesting vaccine candidates may need region-specific design.

articles.headline: Multiple Plasmodium species imported into Trinidad detected
articles.title_original: Imported malaria in Trinidad, West Indies: Detection of multiple non-endemic Plasmodium species.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 153 suspected malaria cases identified 56 positive (36.6%) using microscopy and PCR with sequencing. Confirmed P. vivax, P. falciparum, and P. ovale importation; P. malariae detected in 3 samples, 2 without travel history suggesting possible local transmission. Both diagnostic methods showed 100% concordance; 98.2% agreement on species identification.

articles.headline: Vector diversity and biting patterns in Benin malaria and dengue
articles.title_original: Species diversity and human biting rates of malaria and dengue vectors in central and northern Benin.
articles.details: Multimethod entomological survey across 5 communes identified 7 mosquito species from 2,337 adult mosquitoes and 1,988 emerged larvae (51% of 993 breeding sites positive). Aedes aegypti exhibited predominantly diurnal biting; Anopheles gambiae showed marked nocturnal activity. Concurrent detection during both day and night suggested combined malaria and dengue transmission risk.

articles.headline: HLA polymorphisms show pathogen-specific protection in Indian cohort
articles.title_original: HLA polymorphisms shape divergent outcomes of Toxoplasma and Plasmodium infection in Eastern Indian HbE/β-thalassemia cohort.
articles.details: NGS and functional studies in 61 HBT patients and 50 controls demonstrated HLA-A*33 carriers had significant protection against Toxoplasma gondii infection through restricted parasite replication and enhanced CD8+ IFN-γ responses. Interestingly, A*33 conferred no protection against P. falciparum; HLA-C*07 showed potential negative association with P. falciparum infection via MSP3 peptide binding.

articles.headline: Thrombocytopenia is strongest predictor of P. falciparum infection
articles.title_original: Association between thrombocytopaenia and Plasmodium falciparum infection in Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 388 participants across 3 Ghanaian sites (June-September 2025). Platelet counts significantly lower in malaria cases (152.0 vs 234.0 × 10⁹/L, p<0.001). Thrombocytopenia showed strongest association with P. falciparum infection (adjusted OR 11.8, 95% CI 6.18-24.7, p<0.001). Haematological model demonstrated superior diagnostic performance (AUC 77.6%) compared to sociodemographic factors alone.

articles.headline: Current malaria diagnostics in Ethiopia: scoping review findings
articles.title_original: Current practices and innovative diagnostics for Malaria in Ethiopia: a scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review of 13 studies (38.5% published 2008-2024; 84.6% cross-sectional design; mostly conducted in Amhara 43.75% and Oromia 31.25%). Identified four diagnostic modalities: light microscopy, rapid diagnostic test (RDT), PCR, and loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP), each with unique advantages and limitations. Authors concluded need for tailored, highly accurate, user-friendly diagnostics.

articles.headline: mRDT superior to microscopy for detecting malaria in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Evaluation of microscopy and first response™ malaria Ag PAN/Pf RDT compared to qPCR and hemoglobin levels in pregnant women at delivery in Busia County, Western Kenya.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 236 pregnant women at delivery (April 2023-March 2024) in Kenya. Peripheral malaria prevalence by microscopy/mRDT/qPCR: 7.2%/8.1%/9.0%. Placental prevalence: 7.2% (microscopy)/15.7% (qPCR). mRDT showed higher sensitivity (90%, 95% CI 0.77-1.0) but lower specificity (80%, 0.74-0.85) than microscopy (sensitivity 80%, specificity 89%). P. falciparum predominated; 69.9% had anaemia (Hb ≤11 g/dL).

articles.headline: Malaria, anemia, micronutrient deficiencies co-occur in Madagascar
articles.title_original: Burden and Co-Occurrence of Anemia, Micronutrient Deficiencies, Inflammation, and Malaria Among Women, Adolescents, and Children in Madagascar: A National Cross-Sectional Survey.
articles.details: National micronutrient survey across 23 regions (2024) in preschool children, adolescents, and women. Malaria detected in 4-14% of participants, anemia in 42% of preschool children and ~33% of older groups. Iron deficiency most prevalent in preschool children (26%), zinc deficiency affected ~33% of all groups, riboflavin deficiency 75% in preschool and ~90% in older groups. Multiple conditions frequently co-occurred.

articles.headline: Community engagement strengthens malaria priority setting in Nepal
articles.title_original: Community and stakeholder engagement in national priority setting and participatory research for HIV, Tuberculosis, and Malaria programs in Nepal.
articles.details: Qualitative participatory study (January-June 2020) involving 2,000+ participants across 700 sessions and 100 key informant interviews across Nepal's 7 provinces and 72 municipalities. Recommendations including border testing for migrants, multi-disease diagnostic integration, and community surveillance strengthening were incorporated into national strategic plans. Government pledged 20% domestic co-financing.

articles.headline: Secondary education and medicine stock affect malaria treatment delay
articles.title_original: Determinants of time to malaria treatment-seeking behaviour among caregivers of children under five in Uganda: a case study of Apac district.
articles.authors: Odur B, Opio B, Nabulya O
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 240 caregiver households in Apac District, Uganda using Cox proportional hazards regression. Secondary education increased time to treatment seeking (HR 1.67, p=0.044). Significant delays associated with lack of hospital medicines (HR 0.61, p=0.001) and financial constraints (HR 0.49, p<0.001). All caregivers sought treatment for their children.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine maintains >90% efficacy in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine for uncomplicated plasmodium falciparum malaria treatment in mozambique: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 6 studies (1,862 participants) across Mozambique regions. Uncorrected artemether-lumefantrine efficacy: 91.0% (95% CI 84.2-94.6); PCR-corrected: 98.3% (95% CI 96.9-99.1). Early treatment failure 0.05%, late clinical failure 2.73%, late parasitological failure 5.3%. Recrudescence 1.8%; new infections 8.6%. Authors conclude artemether-lumefantrine remains suitable first-line treatment.

articles.headline: South Korea's malaria eradication through public health mobilization
articles.title_original: Mobilizing Public Health in South Korea: NMES (National Malaria Eradication Service) and its Legacy, 1945-1969.
articles.details: Historical examination of South Korea's National Malaria Eradication Service (NMES) formation in early 1960s following wartime malaria surge. Analyzes Republic of Korea government strategies, language, and pedagogy for engaging rural populations post-Korean War. Demonstrates transformation of public health from external force to familiar partner dependent on citizen participation.

articles.headline: Adjuvant supply chains for TB and malaria vaccines
articles.title_original: Understanding adjuvant supply chains for new TB and malaria vaccines.
articles.authors: Gotham D, Frick M, Harrington M
articles.details: No abstract provided. Article focuses on supply chain considerations for new vaccine adjuvants in TB and malaria vaccination programs.

articles.headline: Malaria and helminth coinfection among pregnant women, Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Soil-Transmitted Helminths and Associated Factors Among Pregnant Women Visiting Mizan-Tepi University Teaching Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 330 pregnant women in southwest Ethiopia (March–June 2024) found malaria in 25.2% and soil-transmitted helminths in 29.4%, with Ascaris lumbricoides most prevalent. Rural residence (AOR=2.011, p=0.024) and farmer occupation (AOR=4.801, p=0.048) were associated with malaria; stagnant water (AOR=2.150, p=0.008) was a risk factor. Handwashing practices showed strong protective association against STH infection (AOR=6.468–8.826).

articles.headline: PvGAP microhaplotype panel for Plasmodium vivax genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: PvGAP: Development of a globally-applicable, highly-multiplexed microhaplotype amplicon panel for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: Developed PvGAP, an 88-target amplicon panel (80 high-diversity, 8 epidemiological-interest targets) for P. vivax population genomics and drug-resistance surveillance. Validated with field samples from Ethiopia and whole genomes from MalariaGEN Pv4 database. Achieves robust amplification and competitive accuracy for relatedness inference across disparate geographic regions, supporting case classification and reinfection discrimination.

articles.headline: Atriplex halimus extract repels Anopheles pharoensis mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Phytochemical profiling, metabolomics, and molecular docking studies of Atriplex halimus aerial parts revealing potential insecticidal activity against the malaria vector Anopheles pharoensis.
articles.details: Phytochemical profiling of Atriplex halimus aerial parts identified 78 secondary metabolites, predominantly flavonoids and triterpenoids. Repellent bioassays against Anopheles pharoensis starved females showed dose- and solvent-dependent effects; dichloromethane fraction at 6.67 mg/cm² achieved highest repellency of 81.92%. Molecular docking studies supported antimicrobial and insecticidal mechanisms.
