articles.headline: Open access to malaria intervention data critical for elimination
articles.title_original: Public Availability of Malaria Intervention Data Will Be Vital for the Acceleration of Its Control and Elimination.
articles.details: Policy advocacy for mandatory global open-access mandate on malaria intervention data (rapid diagnostics, antimalarial drugs, insecticide-treated nets, vector control tools). Uses India as case study, highlighting gaps in district-level data availability. Proposes standardized data platforms to accelerate innovation, transparency, and equitable resource allocation in malaria control and elimination.

articles.headline: Sustained malaria case increases in five High Burden to High Impact countries
articles.title_original: Turning the tide against malaria in high-burden African countries: Trends, threats, and solutions.
articles.details: WHO epidemiological data analysis (2015–2024) using Mann-Kendall trend tests identified sustained malaria case increases in Nigeria (p=0.002), Democratic Republic of Congo (p=0.002), Cameroon (p=0.005), Uganda (p=0.02), and Mali (p=0.04). Analysis identifies emerging biological threats: antimalarial drug resistance, insecticide resistance, pfhrp2 gene deletions, and Anopheles stephensi invasion, intersecting with climate change, armed conflict, and population displacement.

articles.headline: Geospatial malaria risk mapping in Nsanje District, Malawi
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria risk using geospatial and AHP approaches in Nsanje District, Malawi.
articles.details: Used analytical hierarchy process and weighted overlay analysis integrating environmental (temperature, rainfall, elevation, slope, river proximity) and socio-economic variables (health facility, road proximity, population density) to map malaria risk in Nsanje District. Temperature (35.1%) and rainfall (20.4%) were most influential hazard factors; 43.1% of district classified as high risk and 40.5% as moderate risk overall.

articles.headline: CDC operational guidance for locally acquired malaria investigation
articles.title_original: CDC Operational Guidance for Investigating Locally Acquired Mosquito-Transmitted Malaria - United States, 2026.
articles.details: No abstract provided. CDC operational guidance document for investigating locally acquired mosquito-transmitted malaria in the United States, updated for 2026.

articles.headline: Time-delayed malaria model reveals insecticide-treatment synergy
articles.title_original: Effects of insecticides and awareness on the dynamics of a delayed malaria model: A real-data calibration.
articles.details: Time-delayed human-malaria interaction model incorporating transmission incubation period and awareness program implementation lag, calibrated with malaria case data from India, Democratic Republic of Congo, and Uganda. Bifurcation analysis revealed supercritical Hopf bifurcation with periodic oscillations; multiple delays destabilized system earlier than single delay. Optimal control analysis showed combination of insecticide measures and treatment most effective in controlling infections.

articles.headline: Birth outcomes, not malaria, impair infant pneumococcal vaccine response
articles.title_original: Low birthweight and prematurity, but not malaria chemoprevention, are associated with reduced pneumococcal vaccine immunogenicity in Ugandan infants.
articles.details: Nested study within MIC-DroP trial of 195 infants in Uganda evaluated whether early-life malaria exposure and perennial malaria chemoprevention (PMC) modified PCV-10 antibody responses. Neither PMC nor malaria exposure associated with immunogenicity or seroconversion. Low birthweight (<2500 g) associated with 82% lower antibody fold-change (p=0.003) and reduced seroconversion (OR=0.19, p=0.003); preterm birth (<37 weeks) showed similar associations (79% lower fold-change, p=0.018).

articles.headline: IFN-γ and IL-10 biomarkers associate with severe P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Interferon-γ and Interleukin-10 Profiles Correlate with Disease Severity in Sudanese Children with Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: Case-control study of 105 Sudanese children (35 per group: severe malaria, uncomplicated malaria, healthy controls, aged 3 months–16 years) measured plasma IFN-γ and IL-10 by ELISA. Both cytokines significantly elevated in severe malaria vs uncomplicated malaria and controls (p<0.001), with strong positive correlation (rho=0.688, p<0.001). IL-10 and IFN-γ showed moderate diagnostic performance (AUC=0.720 and 0.687 respectively) discriminating severe from uncomplicated malaria.

articles.headline: Pfmdr1 copy number variation detected in DHA-PPQ–treated falciparum
articles.title_original: The Impact of pfmdr1 Gene Copy Number on the Efficacy of Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine in Treating Malaria in West Sumba and Kupang Districts, East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of 41 falciparum malaria patients treated with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) in East Nusa Tenggara, Indonesia. Real-time PCR analysis identified 36 isolates (87.81%) with single copy and 5 (12.19%) with multiple copies of pfmdr1 multidrug resistance-1 gene. All patients achieved 100% adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) on Day 42, but genetic variation indicates selective pressure against piperaquine.

articles.headline: P. falciparum resistance markers mapped across Angola provinces
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance in Angola: a systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review (2000–2024) of 20 studies on P. falciparum resistance markers in Angola. Chloroquine-resistant pfcrt CVIET persisted in northern/coastal provinces; sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-resistant pfdhfr/pfdhps IRNGE detected in Cabinda and Zaire. No validated pfk13 mutations linked to artemisinin resistance. Pfmdr1 showed declining 86Y and 1246Y mutation frequencies with wild-type NYD and NFD haplotypes predominating. Low pfmdr1 and pfpm2 copy-number variation prevalence overall, though emerging amplification noted in southern Angola.

articles.headline: Proactive community malaria case management shows no benefit Mali
articles.title_original: Impact of proactive community case management of malaria on malaria prevalence in Bankass, Mali: a cluster-randomised controlled trial.
articles.details: Three-year cluster-randomized controlled trial in Bankass, Mali (>100,000 population) comparing proactive household visits by community health workers (twice monthly) to fixed-point services. No significant difference between intervention and control arms in parasite prevalence, fever in past 2 weeks, care-seeking, or diagnostic access among children under-5. Intervention arm achieved target visit frequency in fewer than half of households.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria infects 34% African schoolchildren; anemia key risk
articles.title_original: Prevalence and determinants of asymptomatic malaria infection among schoolchildren in Africa: insights from a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis across 33 African countries (8,762+ students) found 34% asymptomatic malaria infection (95% CI: 28–40%), ranging 5% in Ethiopia to 53% in Cameroon. P. falciparum most common (29.3%), followed by mixed infections (2%), P. vivax (1%), P. malariae (1%), P. ovale (0%). Asymptomatic infection associated with male gender (OR=1.26), anemia (OR=5.51), no bednet use (OR=1.99), and prior malaria treatment (OR=3.82).

articles.headline: Community factors hinder indoor residual spraying uptake Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Community-level barriers and facilitators of indoor residual spraying for malaria control: a systematic review of qualitative evidence from sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Systematic review of 10 qualitative studies from sub-Saharan Africa identified eight major barriers to indoor residual spraying uptake: household labor burdens, concerns about nuisance insects (bedbugs), socio-political factors and trust deficits, poor communication, gender dynamics in household decisions, structural/seasonal incompatibilities, inequitable implementation, and weak local health infrastructure. Authors recommend human-centered approaches addressing labor demands, privacy concerns, and trust.

articles.headline: Apicoplast metal transporter identified; disruption impairs parasite transmission
articles.title_original: Identification of a Putative Metal Transporter in the Apicoplast of Malaria Parasites.
articles.details: Tetracycline-affinity proteomics identified an uncharacterized transmembrane protein (UCT) with CorA-family metal transporter structure in the apicoplast. Knockdown dispensable for asexual blood-stage parasites but impaired gametocyte and sexual parasite development critical for mosquito transmission. Discovery provides mechanistic insight into apicoplast metal homeostasis across parasite life stages and potential antimalarial target.

articles.headline: D-dimer elevated in severe P. falciparum malaria; moderate accuracy
articles.title_original: D-dimer as a marker of severe disease in imported malaria: a retrospective observational study.
articles.details: Retrospective study of 85 P. falciparum malaria patients (21 severe; 63.5% male; median age 39 years). Severe cases had higher median D-dimer (10,943 μg/L FEU vs 2,245 μg/L FEU; p=0.0005). Adjusted odds ratio per 1000 μg/L: 1.14 (95% CI: 1.06–1.23). At optimal cutoff 4,864 μg/L: sensitivity 0.76, specificity 0.73, diagnostic accuracy 0.74.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 7.1% Ethiopia; standing water and no ITN key risks
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated factors of microscopy-confirmed malaria among the general population in Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 14 studies (8,762 participants) in Ethiopia. Pooled microscopy-confirmed malaria prevalence 7.1% (95% CI: 4.9–10.2%; I²=95.7%), highest in Amhara (10.7%). Key determinants: stagnant water (OR=6.71), no indoor residual spraying (OR=5.90), mosquito breeding sites (OR=6.13), travel to endemic areas (OR=5.86), houses with eaves (OR=4.5), prior malaria infection (OR=3.54), no insecticide-treated net use (OR=4.8).

articles.headline: Trial protocol: PBO-ceiling nets vs LLINs for malaria prevention Kenya
articles.title_original: Evaluating the impacts of piperonyl butoxide-treated ceiling nets on malaria risk among high-risk populations with limited access to conventional LLINs in Homa Bay Country, Kenya: study protocol for a household randomized control trial.
articles.details: Study protocol for household randomized controlled trial (500 households in Homa Bay County, Kenya) evaluating piperonyl butoxide–pyrethroid-treated ceiling nets as housing modification. Three arms: PBO-ceiling nets alone, PBO-long-lasting insecticide-treated nets alone (one per two household members), or both. Primary outcome: Plasmodium infection prevalence by PCR at 12 months. Assesses superiority (combination vs single intervention) and non-inferiority (ceiling nets vs LLINs).

articles.headline: Malaria-HIV coinfection prevalence 9% Ethiopia; severe anemia 31%
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria-HIV coinfection and associated adverse outcomes in Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 10 Ethiopian studies. Pooled malaria-HIV coinfection prevalence 9% (95% CI: 3–15). Clinical outcomes in coinfected patients: 31% with severe anemia (95% CI: 12–50), 41% with hyperparasitemia (95% CI: −8–90), 35% with low CD4+ counts ≤200 cells/μL (95% CI: 14–56). Burden remained relatively stable over 2002–2025 period.

articles.headline: Landscape review identifies 67 malaria rapid diagnostic tests available
articles.title_original: Advances in Rapid Diagnostic Tests for Malaria: A Landscape Review and Analysis.
articles.details: Landscape review identified 67 rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) for malaria from 27 international manufacturers, commercially available or in late-stage development. Most are simple blood-based tests with results in ≤30 minutes and reported ≥98% diagnostic sensitivity and specificity. Many have received WHO approval. Clinical and implementation science studies needed to optimize delivery strategies in endemic settings.

articles.headline: Eswatini genomic surveillance reveals artemisinin-resistant kelch13 P553L cluster
articles.title_original: Genomic Surveillance Reveals Clusters of Plasmodium falciparum Antimalarial Resistance Markers in Eswatini, A Low-Transmission Setting.
articles.details: Multiplexed amplicon deep sequencing of 437 P. falciparum samples in Eswatini (March–December 2023). Validated artemisinin partial resistance marker kelch13 P553L identified in cluster of four infections (0.9%), likely importation-linked. Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance dhps/dhfr sextuple mutant in 7.8% within clusters. Multidrug resistance 1 N86 nearly fixed. Over 60% polyclonal infections; no hrp2/3 deletions detected.

articles.headline: Larviciding plus ITNs achieves transmission-interruption threshold Tanzania modeling
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of larviciding as a supplementary malaria vector control intervention in rural south-eastern Tanzania: A district-level simulation study.
articles.details: Vector Control Optimization Model adapted using southeastern Tanzania district-level field data (mosquito mortality surveys 2016–2021). Immature mosquito mortality from larviciding: 61% from field data. Simulation results: long-lasting insecticide-treated net coverage 80% combined with larviciding ≥60% coverage reduced annual entomological inoculation rate below 1 per person per year, the threshold to interrupt malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Crystalloid protein PyCryPH2 affects sporozoite maturation and liver infectivity
articles.title_original: A pleckstrin homology domain protein is involved in crystalloid formation in Plasmodium ookinetes and affects the maturation of infective sporozoites.
articles.details: Study of Plasmodium yoelii sporozoites developed in oocysts. Targeted disruption of pycryph2 gene, which encodes a crystalloid protein with a pleckstrin homology domain, resulted in irregular crystalloid microstructure. While pycryph2-deficient parasites developed morphologically normal sporozoites, they showed significantly reduced ability to invade mosquito salivary glands and infect mouse liver, indicating PyCryPH2 contributes to functional sporozoite maturation.

articles.headline: High overprescription of antimalarials despite negative diagnostics in Gabon
articles.title_original: Diagnostic and therapeutic practices for paediatric malaria: Gap between guidelines and clinical reality in south-east Gabon.
articles.details: Prospective observational study of 3,195 children aged 0–16 years with fever in Franceville, Gabon, 2021–2023. Overall 41.6% tested positive by thick blood smear; 21.4% had severe malaria. Despite negative blood smear results, 86.7% received presumptive diagnosis and 98.8% were treated with antimalarials. Asthenia (aOR=1.41), headache (aOR=1.71), and nausea-vomiting (aOR=1.95) significantly predicted presumptive diagnosis.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis finds no significant ITN/LLIN effect on P. falciparum infection
articles.title_original: Impact of Insecticide-Treated Nets on Plasmodium falciparum Infection Rates: A Meta-Analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 2021–2025 data on insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) and long-lasting ITNs (LLINs) efficacy. Crude-effects analysis (6 studies, 7,466 participants) yielded pooled OR=0.67 (95% CI 0.42–1.07; p=0.078), not statistically significant. Adjusted-effects analysis (9 studies) yielded aOR=0.88 (95% CI 0.42–1.86; p=0.71), also non-significant. High heterogeneity (I²=79.8–88.7%) partially explained by population type and study design. Conclusion: no conclusive evidence of statistically significant association between ITN/LLIN use and P. falciparum infection in contemporary studies.
articles.notable_reason: Challenges current understanding of ITN effectiveness in malaria control

articles.headline: Low IPTp compliance driven by infrequent antenatal care in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Compliance with intermittent preventive malaria treatment in pregnancy at a tertiary hospital in Sierra Leone.
articles.details: Non-concurrent cohort study at tertiary hospital in Freetown, Sierra Leone, 2024, including 733 pregnant women. Only 30 (4%) had ≥4 antenatal contacts; 406 (55%) received first IPTp dose, only 62 (8%) received minimum three doses. Women with <4 antenatal contacts had 2.7× higher risk of non-compliance (95% CI 1.6–4.5). Low receipt and compliance with three-dose IPTp regimen were associated with fewer antenatal care contacts.

articles.headline: Suboptimal artesunate dosing compliance linked to poor outcomes in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Parenteral artesunate compliance and hospital outcomes in children under five with suspected severe malaria in Sierra Leone.
articles.details: Cohort study at tertiary paediatric hospital in Freetown, Sierra Leone, January–December 2024, including 735 children under five admitted with suspected severe malaria. 657 (89%) received rapid diagnostic test; none had microscopic confirmation. 650 (88%) received all three recommended parenteral artesunate doses at 0, 12, 24 hours; correct weight-based dosing compliance was 57% (420/733). Among 642 discharged, 73 (10%) had unfavourable outcomes including 59 deaths. Underweight status, multiple convulsions, and failure to receive correct artesunate dosing were associated with unfavourable hospital outcomes.

articles.headline: Spatial clustering identifies persistent malaria hotspots in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria hotspots through spatial and spatio-temporal analysis in Sierra Leone, 2021-2024.
articles.details: Geospatial analysis of malaria in Sierra Leone using DHIS2 data, 2021–2024. 7.4 million cases reported; chiefdom-level incidence ranged 21.2 to >750 per 1,000 population. Significant spatial clustering observed (Moran's I>0; p<0.01). Space-time Poisson models identified persistent high-high clusters (p<0.05) and low-low clusters (p<0.05). High-risk districts had relative risk 1.2–1.9 (p<0.01); low-risk districts 0.5–0.9 (p<0.01). No significant association between malaria incidence and rainfall at national level.

articles.headline: Correction: Comprehensive genomic study of P. falciparum in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Correction: Comprehensive genomic study of Plasmodium falciparum in Sierra Leone: genetic variation and resistance patterns.
articles.details: Correction notice to published article on genomic variation and resistance patterns.

articles.headline: Livestock-associated mosquitoes sustain low-level transmission in elimination zone
articles.title_original: Anopheles ecology and serological evidence of malaria exposure in clinically malaria-free areas of Menoreh Hills Central Java, Indonesia.
articles.details: Study of Anopheles ecology and serological malaria exposure across four villages in Menoreh Hills, Central Java, Indonesia, a low-transmission region approaching elimination. Dominant vector species present throughout night, closely associated with livestock environments. Productive breeding sites shaped by habitat quality rather than number. Livestock shed placement influenced mosquito abundance. Host-feeding involved both humans and livestock. Serological evidence revealed marked differences in exposure between villages despite absence of clinical cases, indicating spatial variation in exposure.

articles.headline: CRISPR platform enables functional genomics of liver-stage P. falciparum infection
articles.title_original: Cas9-expressing HC-04 hepatocytes facilitate CRISPR-based analysis of Plasmodium falciparum sporozoite-host interactions.
articles.details: Development of HC-04 hepatocytes expressing Cas9-mCherry to study P. falciparum sporozoite-hepatocyte interactions. Cas9 activity validated and guide-RNA-directed gene disruption via non-homologous end joining demonstrated. Optimized traversal and invasion assays provided robust cytometric readout. Disruption of 10 human genes previously implicated in bacterial and viral infection confirmed utility of platform for identifying host factors in P. falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Comment on cryptic malaria genome sequencing in UK travellers
articles.title_original: Comment on Rapid Whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers provides insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.
articles.details: Commentary on rapid whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers, providing insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.

articles.headline: Post-malaria fatigue resolves by three months, unlike arboviral diseases
articles.title_original: Frequency and persistence of post-acute symptoms after chikungunya, dengue, Zika, and malaria in travellers: a prospective multi-centre study.
articles.details: Prospective multi-site study (15 GeoSentinel sites, 2016–2021) of 273 travellers with confirmed travel-associated acute infections: 35 chikungunya, 110 dengue, 19 Zika, 109 falciparum malaria. At month 3, persistent symptoms in 69% chikungunya, 25% dengue, 42% Zika, 11% malaria patients. At month 12, only 1/109 (1%) malaria patients remained symptomatic versus 17% chikungunya, 5% dengue, 16% Zika. Post-malaria fatigue resolved almost completely by month 3, whereas post-arboviral symptoms persisted beyond six months. Female sex, chikungunya, Zika, and musculoskeletal symptoms during acute infection associated with persistent month-3 symptoms.

articles.headline: Review: mechanisms of subclinical malaria and elimination challenges
articles.title_original: Mechanistic insights into subclinical Plasmodium infections: Unveiling the silent drivers of malaria transmission.
articles.details: Review article examining epidemiological burden and biological mechanisms of subclinical and asymptomatic Plasmodium infections. Parasite adaptations sustaining persistence include density thresholds, antigenic variation, cytoadhesion, multiplicity of infection, and drug resistance. Host factors include naturally acquired immunity, age, pregnancy, genetic polymorphisms, and epigenetic influences. Chronic subclinical infections associated with anemia, impaired cognitive development, and adverse pregnancy outcomes. Emphasizes diagnostic challenges and need for sensitive, field-deployable tools and integrative approaches for elimination.

articles.headline: Household-level insecticide mosaics feasible for malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Indoor residual spraying of distinct insecticide formulations in a household-scale micro-mosaic format: Feasibility and effectiveness for malaria vector control in rural Zambia.
articles.details: Zambian study piloted household-scale micro-mosaic IRS deployment (alternating DDT and clothianidin+deltamethrin formulations) in 9 catchment areas with 15 houses each over one year. Vector densities were very low overall; no significant differences observed between DDT-only, clothianidin+deltamethrin-only, or mixed regimes (p ≥ 0.14). Household-level micro-mosaics showed comparable short-term effectiveness to conventional macro-mosaic deployments, offering potential for long-term resistance management.

articles.headline: P. vivax malaria cases surge in New York City following migration
articles.title_original: An Increase in Imported Plasmodium vivax Malaria in New York City: Clinical and Demographic Trends Following Recent Migration.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of P. vivax cases in NYC (2018-2024) showed increase from 2.9% of malaria cases in 2018 to 18.2% in 2023 (63 of 346 cases). Among 41 hospital admissions, 40 (97.6%) were recent migrants from endemic regions. Severe malaria occurred in 10 patients (24.4%), with 7 relapses (17%) occurring primarily in patients without liver-stage therapy. Timely diagnosis and complete treatment, including liver-stage therapy, are critical for preventing relapses.

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum malaria in pregnancy: Diagnosis and management
articles.title_original: Severe malaria in pregnancy in the United States: A case report and management considerations.
articles.details: Case report of nulliparous woman at 36 weeks gestation with fever and abdominal discomfort after Ghana travel. Initial peripheral smear equivocal; malaria confirmed with 5.4% parasitemia after repeat testing. Treated with artemether-lumefantrine then escalated to artesunate (severe malaria criteria). Complicated by preterm PROM, thrombocytopenia, and preeclampsia with good maternal and fetal outcome. Highlights diagnostic challenges and need for pregnancy-specific treatment algorithms.

articles.headline: Novel asparagine tRNA synthetase inhibitors with nanomolar P. falciparum potency
articles.title_original: Potent reaction hijacking inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum asparagine tRNA synthetase.
articles.details: Development of 4-amino-quinazoline sulfonamides targeting P. falciparum asparagine tRNA synthetase (PfAsnRS) with nanomolar potency and up to 300-fold selectivity over mammalian cells. Compounds showed activity against transmissible parasite stages and resistance-refractory profile. Molecular studies confirmed PfAsnRS susceptibility to reaction hijacking and identified human AsnRS as less susceptible, indicating potential for selective antimalarial activity.
articles.notable_reason: Novel aaRS target with nanomolar potency, low resistance risk

articles.headline: Anopheles diversity and low zoonotic malaria risk in Thailand
articles.title_original: Systematic review of Anopheles abundance and meta-analysis of nonhuman primate malaria infection in mosquitoes in Thailand.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 92 studies on Anopheles in Thailand (2013-2025). Pooled infection prevalence for nonhuman primate malaria parasites was 0.01 (95% CI 0.00-0.03) overall, with wild-caught An. sawadwongporni, An. minimus, and An. dirus incriminated as zoonotic vectors showing 0.2% pooled prevalence. Findings emphasize need for context-specific, species-targeted vector control addressing local bionomics and high-risk populations.

articles.headline: Hybrid CNN achieves 97.3% sensitivity for automated malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: A hybrid machine learning approach for automated malaria diagnosis from thin blood smear images.
articles.details: Development and validation of hybrid Inception-v3 convolutional neural network using 15,938 preprocessed thin blood smear images from NIMR. Model achieved 98.9% training accuracy, 97.3% sensitivity, 99.9% specificity on ICMR-NIMR dataset. Performance comparable to or exceeded other machine learning classifiers and conventional microscopy. Potential to reduce reliance on expert microscopists, especially in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: High MSP-2 genetic diversity in P. falciparum from Chad
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum MSP-2 (3D7/IC) and multiplicity of infection in N'Djamena, Chad.
articles.details: Cross-sectional molecular study of 217 blood samples from N'Djamena, Chad (July-September 2024) with 23.5% P. falciparum prevalence by microscopy. Molecular analysis revealed 94.11% frequency of msp2 3D7/IC allelic family with substantial genetic polymorphism. Single-clone infections predominated (64.65%), followed by double (33.33%) and triple infections (2.08%). Phylogenetic analysis identified 8 clades, indicating ongoing high transmission.

articles.headline: New Plasmodium species identified in Brazilian seabird
articles.title_original: A new Plasmodium Marchiafava & Celli, 1885 (Apicomplexa: Haemosporida) species in Cory's Shearwater (Calonectris borealis) [Cory]) (Aves: Procellariiformes) stranded in a coastal area in Brazil.
articles.details: Description of new Plasmodium species (P. borealis) in adult male Cory's Shearwater stranded in Brazil, showing parasitemia ranging 0.04-2.09%. PCR and phylogenetic analysis (pLK06 lineage, Novyella clade) confirmed novel species with morphological features related to P. vaughani. First haemosporidian infection in this seabird and first pLK06 in Procellariiformes. Necropsy findings (hepatitis, pulmonary hemorrhage) indicate pathogenicity.

articles.headline: Most fevers in Angola are non-malarial; respiratory infections prevalent
articles.title_original: Age-Related and Seasonal Variation in Malaria and Other Causes of Fever and Their Association With Clinical Outcomes in Southern Angola: A Hospital-Based Study.
articles.details: Hospital-based study of 849 febrile patients in southern Angola (September-October 2023, May-June 2024) found 67.6% malaria-negative by microscopy. Upper respiratory (13.0%), lower respiratory (19.5%), and gastrointestinal infections (16.7%) were leading non-malarial causes. Malaria and lower respiratory infections independently associated with hospitalization (OR 3.20 and 5.17, p < 0.0001), while malaria and CNS infections linked to mortality (OR 1.90 and 6.30, p < 0.0001).

articles.headline: Wolbachia wAnD associated with reduced P. falciparum sporozoites
articles.title_original: The presence of the wAnD strain of Wolbachia is correlated with lower levels of Plasmodium sporozoites and a less diverse microbiome in wild Anopheles demeilloni mosquito cephalothoraxes.
articles.details: Analysis of wild-caught An. demeilloni from DRC (2021, 2024) with natural wAnD Wolbachia infection. Overall sporozoite prevalence 1.3% (9/704); despite no significant difference in cephalothorax sporozoite prevalence between Wolbachia-positive and -negative mosquitoes (p=0.3630), P. falciparum sporozoites detected only in Wolbachia-negative cephalothoraxes. Wolbachia-positive abdomens showed significantly lower sporozoite rates than Wolbachia-negative (p=0.0329). Cephalothorax microbiome composition differed significantly between Wolbachia status (p<0.05).
articles.notable_reason: Natural wAnD symbiosis shows transmission-reducing potential for biocontrol

articles.headline: Zoonotic malaria and leishmaniasis co-occur in Thailand forest areas
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria and leishmaniasis in Thailand (2004-2025): A systematic review.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Systematic review of 57 studies (36 on malaria, 21 on leishmaniasis) in Thailand from 2004-2025. Nine Plasmodium species documented including emerging zoonotic species P. knowlesi and P. cynomolgi; P. vivax and P. falciparum predominated with declining P. falciparum trend since 2012. Six Leishmania species identified; seven provinces reported concurrent occurrence of both diseases. Shared risk factors included forest-related occupational exposure and high population mobility.

articles.headline: IL-6 more informative than HO-1 for malaria diagnosis adjunct
articles.title_original: Plasma haem oxygenase-1 and interleukin-6 as adjunct host biomarkers associated with malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 304 participants (152 microscopy-positive, 152 microscopy-negative) at Nigerian hospital. IL-6 significantly elevated in malaria-positive participants (median 141.28 vs 106.58 pg/mL, p=0.027), with positive correlation to parasitemia (rho=0.387, p=0.015) and temperature but negative correlation with hemoglobin (rho=-0.417, p=0.008). HO-1 showed non-significant difference (p=0.131). ROC analysis: IL-6 AUC 0.709 (sensitivity 72.7%, specificity 76.5%), superior to HO-1 (AUC 0.641). Combined model AUC 0.687.

articles.headline: Deltamethrin, DEET outperform plant oils against Anopheles epiroticus field populations
articles.title_original: Do natural or synthetic excito-repellents work better? A study on coastal malaria vector Anopheles epiroticus in Ko Chang, Thailand.
articles.details: Lab and field strains of An. epiroticus from Ko Chang, Thailand showed different responses to six repellent agents tested via excito-repellency assay. In laboratory strain, deltamethrin (42%) and permethrin (35%) induced highest escape responses in contact trials; citronella oil (>50%) showed strongest non-contact repellency. The field population differed: DEET (64%) and deltamethrin (60%) were most effective in contact trials, while citronella and vetiver oils showed minimal non-contact effects (~14%). Results indicate behavioral variation between strains should inform coastal Thailand vector control strategies.

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance effects on vector competence remain poorly understood and contradictory
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and malaria transmission: divergent effects on Anopheles vector competence.
articles.authors: Adeboye SO, Nwinyi OC, Ayanda OI
articles.details: This review examines the relationship between insecticide resistance in Anopheles mosquitoes and vector competence (ability to transmit Plasmodium). Despite recognition of resistance mechanisms including target site mutation, metabolic resistance, cuticular thickening, and behavioral resistance, the effect on transmission remains unclear. While some studies report parasite development impairment in resistant mosquitoes, others report enhanced competence. The authors argue integration of omics-based approaches with ecological realism is needed to resolve contradictions and predict epidemiological implications.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid resistance escalates in An. funestus from Kenya's Rift Valley populations
articles.title_original: Signature of resistance gene evolution and pyrethroid resistance escalation in the major malaria vector Anopheles funestus across Kenyan malaria-endemic regions separated by the Rift Valley.
articles.details: A. funestus populations from western, coastal, and Kerio Valley Kenya were genotyped for resistance markers and phenotypically tested. The 4.3kb-SV and G454A-Cyp9k1 alleles were nearly fixed in western Kenya but declined toward the coast, while L119F-GSTe2 increased. Kerio Valley showed highest pyrethroid resistance intensity; coastal populations exhibited extreme permethrin resistance (<10% mortality at 10× dose). Mosquitoes with L119F-GSTe2-RR showed lower Plasmodium infection odds (OR 0.2, P=0.046).
articles.notable_reason: High pyrethroid resistance escalation; region-specific patterns inform resistance management

articles.headline: P. falciparum myosin PfMyoF identified as novel Rab-like motor protein
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PfMyoF contains a Rab-like tail domain and associates with perinuclear membrane trafficking proteins.
articles.details: PfMyoF, a class XXII myosin, was characterized as a plus-end-directed, processive motor with a long neck binding up to six calmodulin copies. The tail domain contains unprecedented WD40 and Rab-like domains not found in other eukaryotic myosins. Pull-down experiments revealed interaction with vesicle trafficking proteins including PfRab18, and microscopy showed perinuclear localization. Transient knockdown using glmS ribozyme impaired parasite growth, suggesting a role in asexual replication and membrane trafficking.
articles.notable_reason: First myosin with Rab-like domain; novel trafficking role in parasite

articles.headline: Microhaplotype genotyping reveals P. vivax cross-border transmission in South Asia
articles.title_original: Genetic evidence of cross-border Plasmodium vivax spread in a malaria pre-elimination region of South Asia.
articles.details: The vivaxGEN 93-microhaplotype panel genotyped P. vivax from patients in Nepal (n=19), Bhutan (n=27), Bangladesh (n=35; 2013-2023) and comparative data from Afghanistan (n=159), India (n=24), Pakistan (n=213; 2014-2024). High-complexity infections were more common in Afghanistan (27.04%), Pakistan (16.43%), Bangladesh (28.57%) but rare in Bhutan (3.70%) and Nepal (5.26%). Identity-by-descent analysis revealed three genetic clusters separating Bangladesh/Bhutan from other countries, with two Bhutanese sub-populations distinguishing local from imported cases.

articles.headline: Active EBV infection associated with severe malaria in infants under 24 months
articles.title_original: Infants under 24 months with severe malaria have a serological profile of active infection with Epstein Barr Virus.
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 195 infants aged 6-24 months in Cameroon found 24% serologically positive for active EBV infection. Severe malaria cases were highest in children with active EBV despite elevated antibody magnitude and breadth against P. falciparum with increased in vitro invasion-blocking capacity; in vivo parasitemia was similar. The authors conclude primary EBV infection may be a risk factor for severe falciparum malaria in this age group.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi detected in wild macaque feces; validates non-invasive surveillance method
articles.title_original: Tracking Plasmodium knowlesi Through Fecal DNA for Monitoring Zoonotic Transmission in Wild Macaques Across Southeast and South Asia.
articles.details: Noninvasive P. knowlesi surveillance via 4752 fecal samples from wild macaques across 9 endemic countries detected parasite DNA in 390 samples (8.2%), with positivity rates ranging 1.4-18.4%. This provides first field evidence that P. knowlesi DNA shed in feces can be detected under natural conditions, validating fecal sampling as a practical, scalable tool for tracking zoonotic malaria transmission.

articles.headline: DON well-tolerated in Malawian adults; advances cerebral malaria adjunct therapy
articles.title_original: Safety, Tolerability, and Pharmacokinetics of 6-Diazo-5-Oxo-L-Norleucine in Malawian Adults With and Without Malaria: A Phase 1 Dose-Escalation Clinical Trial.
articles.details: An open-label, dose-escalation phase 1 trial enrolled 40 healthy adults and 38 adults with uncomplicated malaria in Blantyre, Malawi, receiving single-dose intravenous DON at 0.1, 1.0, 5.0, or 10 mg/kg. Transient asymptomatic creatinine elevation occurred in 18% at 12 hours; nausea and vomiting were uncommon at ≤1.0 mg/kg but more frequent at 5.0-10 mg/kg. All adverse events resolved rapidly without sequelae; terminal half-life ranged 1.7-4.1 hours.
articles.notable_reason: First-in-human DON tolerability supports cerebral malaria adjunct therapy development

articles.headline: Urbanization reduces avian malaria prevalence; habitat modulates infection-microbiome link
articles.title_original: Interactions between urbanization, malaria infection and avian cloacal microbiome.
articles.details: A study of house sparrows examined associations between habitat urbanization, avian malaria infection (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus), and cloacal microbiome composition. Urban birds showed lower parasite prevalence than those from natural and rural habitats. In natural habitats, infected birds showed increased bacterial richness and different taxa composition versus uninfected birds; however, infection status was unassociated with microbiome parameters in rural and urban habitats.

articles.headline: 3% of asymptomatic carriers drive 57% of mosquito infections in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Uneven Plasmodium falciparum Transmission and Cryptic ovale Transmission From the Asymptomatic Reservoir in Bagamoyo, Tanzania.
articles.details: Among 491 RDT/PCR-positive persons in Tanzania with median parasitemia 5.1 parasites/μL, 22% were infectious to Anopheles gambiae via direct skin feeding. RDT-positive persons infected roughly twice as many mosquitoes as RDT-negative/PCR-positive persons. Importantly, 3% of transmitters (top 16 individuals) cumulatively infected 57% of infected mosquitoes, indicating highly infectious carriers drive transmission. School-age children contributed 58% of infected mosquitoes. Unexpectedly, P. ovale was detected in 44% of oocyst-positive feeds.
articles.notable_reason: Small carrier subset drives majority transmission; P. ovale co-transmission detection

articles.headline: Pfs230 domains 7-10 induce potent transmission-blocking antibodies via DNA vaccine
articles.title_original: Pfs230 Domains 7-10 Encoded by DNA Vaccines Induce Potent Complement-Independent Transmission-Blocking Antibody Against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: DNA vaccines encoding Pfs230 domains D7-D10 were evaluated in BALB/c mice for transmission-blocking vaccine development. Anti-D7D10 IgGs showed strong dose-dependent transmission-reducing activity (TRA) against P. falciparum in membrane feeding assays, independent of complement presence. Western blot analysis indicated antibodies predominantly targeted D7; DNA vaccines encoding D7 and D7D8 similarly induced antibodies with strong TRA. These results identify Pfs230-D7D10, particularly D7, as a novel transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Fever augments ring-stage P. falciparum adhesion to brain endothelial cells
articles.title_original: Febrile Temperature Augments Ring-stage Plasmodium falciparum Adhesion to Brain Endothelial Cells.
articles.details: In ring-stage P. falciparum parasites, febrile exposure increased transcript levels of genes involved in trafficking of PfEMP1 ligand responsible for endothelial adherence, accelerating PfEMP1 surface display. This led to 2-fold increase in binding in a perfusable 3D human brain microvessel model. Febrile exposure also enhanced binding of uninfected erythrocytes, suggesting complex interplay between fever and parasite biomass in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Bivalent VLP vaccine candidate matches R21 efficacy in mouse model
articles.title_original: Bivalent virus-like particles expressing SPECT1 and CSP trigger pre-erythrocytic malaria immunity and protect against transgenic Plasmodium falciparum sporozoite challenge in mice.
articles.details: Engineered hepatitis B surface antigen VLPs displaying SPECT1 and CSP epitopes were tested in a prime-boost regimen with transgenic P. berghei challenge in BALB/c mice. One bivalent candidate (N4) formulated with Matrix-M achieved similar protective efficacy to R21, while another candidate (N2) incorporating SPECT1 showed reduced efficacy. Results suggest PfSPECT1 can be co-displayed with CSP epitopes without impairing immunogenicity, though efficacy evaluation was limited to transgenic parasites in mice.

articles.headline: Apical polar ring proteins are essential for parasite invasion
articles.title_original: The apical polar ring is essential during the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Conditional knockout of PfAPR3 revealed its essential role in P. falciparum asexual replication and host cell invasion; mutant parasites contacted RBCs but failed to form tight junctions. Using PfAPR3 as bait, the study identified three additional APR proteins (PfAPR4, PfCHAKRA, PfAPR5) and delineated APR biogenesis using ultrastructure expansion microscopy. PfCHAKRA was critical for cytoskeletal organization while PfAPR3 showed dual apical-basal ring localization during schizogony.
articles.notable_reason: First molecular characterization of P. falciparum APR

articles.headline: Land use and temperature predict malaria risk in Ethiopian sub-basin
articles.title_original: Environmental factors influencing mosquito breeding and malaria transmission using geospatial technologies in the Wabi Shebele sub-basin, Ethiopia.
articles.details: Geospatial analysis of seven environmental factors in the Wabi Shebele sub-basin identified high land surface temperature (LST) areas—bare land (33.66°C), grassland (31.76°C), and agricultural land (31.42°C)—as favoring mosquito vector breeding. Agricultural land comprising 24.78% of area enhanced malaria risk. Forested areas with lower LST (26.98°C) and highland zones with high NDVI showed lower malaria risk. Recommendations focused on environmental management in high-risk agricultural and low-elevation zones.

articles.headline: Avian malaria prevalence and novel lineages in cerulean warblers
articles.title_original: EFFECTS OF AVIAN MALARIA IN A POPULATION OF CERULEAN WARBLER (SETOPHAGA CERULEA) WITH FIRST DOCUMENTATION OF HAEMOSPORIDIAN LINEAGES INFECTING THE SPECIES.
articles.details: Survey of 51 cerulean warblers (Setophaga cerulea) over 2 years using microscopy and molecular methods detected 31.4% prevalence of haemosporidian infections with 11 unique lineages, including 2 novel. Parasitemia was low (<1% of cells infected), indicating chronic infections. No negative effects of infection on body condition were detected in males, though infected individuals showed higher mean body condition, possibly reflecting survivorship bias.

articles.headline: Two sensitive amplicon panels enable P. vivax genomic surveillance
articles.title_original: PvGTSeq and PvCRiSP: Two amplicon-based targeted sequencing panels for Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: PvGTSeq (249 amplicons targeting resistance and population structure) and PvCRiSP (4 polymorphic amplicons for complexity of infection and recurrence) were developed for P. vivax surveillance. Both panels achieved ≥75% amplicon recovery at parasitemias as low as 5 parasites/μL; PvGTSeq showed high accuracy (error rate 3.85e-4% to 2.87e-3%). Validation in 5 countries and in-silico assessment across 16 countries confirmed capacity to discriminate samples and assign geographic origin to imported cases.

articles.headline: NANP2 and J1 epitope responses correlate with RTS,S vaccine protection
articles.title_original: Antibody fine specificity correlates with protection from malaria for the RTS,S vaccine in young African children: A post hoc analysis of a phase IIb randomised controlled trial.
articles.details: Post hoc analysis of RTS,S phase IIb trial (n=735 children 1–4 years) identified IgG specificity for NANP2 epitope (NANPNANP) and cross-reactivity to J1 epitope (KQPADGNPDPNANPN) as protective correlates. High IgG to NANP2 showed adjusted hazard ratio of 0.838 (95% CI [0.716–0.981]; p=0.028), while high J1 IgG showed aHR of 0.718 (95% CI [0.611–0.844]; p<0.001). These responses associated with higher antibody Fc-mediated functional activities.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies specific correlates of protection for approved vaccine

articles.headline: Brazilian Amazon malaria economic burden reaches $182 million annually
articles.title_original: Economic burden of malaria in the Brazilian Amazon from a societal perspective.
articles.details: Survey of 1,131 individuals with malaria episodes (2019–2022) in 9 municipalities across 5 Brazilian Amazon states estimated total economic burden at US$181.9 million PPP-USD in 2019. The public health system (SUS) bore 72.4% of costs, primarily in control and prevention. Household costs were predominantly driven by indirect costs, health-related quality-of-life losses, and mortality-related losses. States of Amazonas, Pará, and Roraima bore the highest total burden.

articles.headline: Activated platelets in cerebral malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: The role of activated platelets in cerebral malaria pathogenesis.
articles.details: No abstract available; summary based on title only. Title indicates focus on the role of platelet activation in cerebral malaria disease mechanisms.

articles.headline: Machine learning infers migration patterns in Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: On the use of generative models for demographic inference in malaria vectors from genomic data.
articles.details: Generative deep learning was adapted to infer joint evolutionary history of A. gambiae populations from Guinea and Burkina Faso. Model selection showed that a model with post-split migration fit these populations better than a no-migration model; the method accurately captured population genetic differentiation. Approach demonstrates potential of machine learning for understanding vector population dynamics and adaptation relevant to malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Deformable gametocytes are immediately infectious to mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Defining the signature of deformable and infectious Plasmodium falciparum gametocytes.
articles.details: Transcriptional and phenotypic analysis of P. falciparum gametocytes showed major gene expression differences between immature and deformable forms, but high similarity between deformable and infectious stages. Combined with exflagellation and mosquito feeding assays, results suggest that deformable gametocytes released from the bone marrow are immediately infectious. Study identified a comprehensive set of infectivity markers for tracking gametocyte development and mapping the infectious reservoir.

articles.headline: Ruthenium-atovaquone complexes block transmission of both sexual parasites
articles.title_original: Ruthenium Complexes of Atovaquone Acting on Multiple Stages of the Plasmodium Life Cycle.
articles.details: Ruthenium complexes of atovaquone were synthesized and characterized for activity across multiple P. falciparum life stages. Complexes demonstrated broader activity than atovaquone alone against asexual and sexual stages and blocked transmission from humans to insects for both gametocytes and oocyst stages, whereas atovaquone alone only blocked oocysts. Study represents the first example of metal complexes inhibiting both asexual and sexual parasites with similar potency, expanding therapeutic utility of metalladrugs.
articles.notable_reason: First metal complex with broad stage activity including transmission-blocking

articles.headline: Platelet mTOR pathway drives cerebral malaria in mice
articles.title_original: Heme-induced ITAM signaling exacerbates malaria-associated neuropathogenesis through activation of platelet mTOR.
articles.details: Platelet-specific mTOR knockout mice (mTORplt-/-) infected with P. berghei ANKA showed significantly improved survival from experimental cerebral malaria independent of parasitemia or thrombocytopenia. mTORplt-/- mice exhibited reduced platelet deposition in the brain, improved cerebral blood flow, and decreased vascular permeability. Heme generated during malaria activated platelet mTOR downstream of ITAM signaling via CLEC-2; cobalt protoporphyrin blockade of heme-induced activation reduced mTOR activation and ECM-associated mortality.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies platelet mTOR pathway as therapeutic target for cerebral malaria

articles.headline: P. falciparum infections do not shorten telomeres in Kenyan children
articles.title_original: Cumulative Plasmodium falciparum infections do not drive long-term telomere shortening in Kenyan children.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study in 218 children from moderate transmission area and 90 controls, measuring telomere length via qPCR and correlating with cumulative infection episodes. Telomere length declined with age (p>0.05) but was not associated with cumulative malaria exposure; asymptomatic parasitaemia at survey showed marginal positive association with 0.22 kb increase in TL (95% CI 0.02-0.42, p=0.03). Authors conclude repeated infections unlikely to cause cellular aging via telomere shortening.

articles.headline: Review of invasive Plasmodium merozoite physiology and mechanisms
articles.title_original: Physiology of invasive Plasmodium merozoites.
articles.details: Review article summarizing current understanding of merozoite invasion of red blood cells through coordinated motility, environmental sensing, ion signaling, and secretion of specialized organelles. Discusses how these physiological processes enable successful cell entry and potential strategies for malaria control and intervention.

articles.headline: Ex vivo culture selectively enriches P. vivax clones without major transcriptional changes
articles.title_original: Stage-aware transcriptomics reveals selective haplotype persistence in short-term ex vivo cultured Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: Study analyzed paired in vivo and short-term ex vivo transcriptomes from Ethiopian P. vivax clinical isolates using stage deconvolution and PvMSP1 haplotyping. After adjusting for parasite stage composition, in vivo and ex vivo transcriptomes showed no significant gene expression differences; however, culture reduced multiplicity of infection and non-randomly depleted specific haplotypes, consistent with clonal bottleneck. Authors conclude ex vivo cultures are reliable for gene expression studies but require explicit modeling of developmental composition and infection complexity.

articles.headline: FHR-1 deficiency not associated with severe falciparum malaria risk
articles.title_original: Factor H-related protein 1 deficiency and risk of severe falciparum malaria in Ghanaian children: a cohort analysis.
articles.details: Analysis of plasma from 500 Ghanaian children with different clinical manifestations. One-third of samples lacked detectable FHR-1 protein; logistic regression found no association between FHR-1 deficiency and severe malaria risk. FHR-1 deficiency was overrepresented in children presenting with anemia and associated with markedly lower hemoglobin levels. Authors conclude FHR-1 deficiency correlates with severe malarial anemia but not overall severe malaria progression.

articles.headline: Paper-based lysis device enables malaria diagnostics in low-resource settings
articles.title_original: Creating an improved workflow for paper-based malaria diagnostics by integrating total lysis of whole blood.
articles.details: Study describes development of 3D paper-based lysis device fabricated by laser cutting or wax-printing, achieving 100% cellular lysis independent of sample composition. Malaria biomarker detection from lysed samples was comparable to standard liquid lysis procedure. Device-dried samples maintained antigen stability for over 1 week at room temperature, facilitating delayed laboratory testing. Authors conclude this approach expands the environments and settings where samples can be collected for malaria testing.

articles.headline: Artemisinin derivatives more effective than iron chelators against malaria
articles.title_original: Why are iron chelators not as effective as artemisinin in killing malaria parasites?
articles.details: In vitro and in vivo study comparing artemisinin derivatives (DHA, ATM) with iron chelator desferrioxamine (DFO) in P. falciparum 3D7 and P. yoelii. DFO reduced P. falciparum in vitro but failed to inhibit P. yoelii in vivo; ATM+DFO combination was less potent than ATM monotherapy. DHA upregulated iron utilization genes at 3h post-treatment while DFO did not. Transmission electron microscopy showed artemether localized to parasites' digestive vacuoles and disrupted heme aggregation. Authors conclude artemisinin's superior efficacy stems from better accumulation in digestive vacuoles and interaction with heme/iron.

articles.headline: Fever increases P. falciparum surface protein export enhancing cytoadhesion
articles.title_original: Physiological febrile heat stress increases cytoadhesion through increased protein trafficking of Plasmodium falciparum surface proteins into the red blood cell.
articles.details: Study examined effect of physiologically relevant febrile temperature (39°C derived from patient data) on P. falciparum-infected RBCs. Febrile heat stress enhanced cytoadhesion through increased trafficking of major virulence factor PfEMP1 and nutrient channel PSAC to cell surface in 4 clinical isolates of diverse geographic origin, without changing overall protein expression or parasite developmental stage. Phosphoproteomics revealed increased trafficking and phosphorylation of exported proteins; enhanced export appears reliant on transmembrane domains. Authors conclude febrile temperatures accelerate protein export, potentially increasing disease severity through enhanced sequestration.

articles.headline: Novel dual-localized pyrophosphatase system identified as antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Pyrophosphate homeostasis in multiple subcellular compartments is essential in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study identified P. falciparum encodes two soluble pyrophosphatase isoforms (PfsPPase1, PfsPPase2) from single genetic locus. PfsPPase1 localizes to cytoplasm only, while PfsPPase2 contains 51-amino acid organellar localization peptide targeting mitochondrion, apicoplast, and cytoplasm. Both isoforms essential for asexual blood stages; this dual-targeting mechanism from single locus represents unique evolutionary trajectory compared to model eukaryotes with multiple sPPases at distinct loci. Authors highlight essentiality of PfsPPases as promising targets for antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: Hemozoin-associated proteins mediate cerebral malaria barrier disruption
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum hemozoin-associated biomolecules induce brain endothelial cell barrier disruption in an in vitro model of cerebral malaria.
articles.details: In vitro study using P. falciparum-infected RBCs and human brain microvascular endothelial cells. Endothelial barrier-disrupting activity localized entirely to hemozoin fraction; activity caused by biomolecules associated with hemozoin rather than hemozoin crystal itself. Protease treatment inhibited barrier disruption while nuclease treatment did not, indicating protein involvement. Finding that P. falciparum hemozoin also disrupts pulmonary endothelial barrier suggests this mechanism may underlie other severe malaria complications.

articles.headline: Global malaria burden decreased 1990-2021 despite increased NTD incidence initially
articles.title_original: Global epidemiological trends, distribution of NTDs and malaria, and disease burden projections for the next 15 years.
articles.details: Analysis of Global Burden of Disease 2021 data from 1990-2021 examining malaria and NTD incidence, mortality, DALYs, and age-standardized rates across regions. Global malaria burden decreased, especially in Sub-Saharan Africa, while NTD incidence increased until 2016 then decreased with steady decline in age-standardized DALYs and death rates. Vector autoregression modeling showed Socio-Demographic Index influenced disease burdens, particularly in low-SDI regions, with environmental sanitation and behavioral factors significantly impacting malaria.

articles.headline: RDT kits provide viable DNA source for malaria drug resistance monitoring
articles.title_original: Rapid diagnostic test kit as a source of DNA in comparison with filter paper for malaria molecular surveillance and drug resistance monitoring: a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study from Ethiopia comparing DNA extraction from 417 paired used RDT kits vs dried blood spot filter paper. RDT-extracted samples positive in 83.7% vs 93.8% for DBS by nested PCR for 18S rRNA. Positive percent agreement for P. falciparum detection 89.2% (95% CI 84.7-92.5) and 81.9% (95% CI 74.6-87.6) for P. vivax, with overall Kappa agreement 0.64 (improved to Kappa 0.75 in high parasite load ≥100,000/μL). pfmdr1 gene amplification success 96.7% from RDT vs 100% from DBS.

articles.headline: NEK4 kinase essential for P. berghei meiosis and ookinete development
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.
articles.details: Study identified P. berghei NIMA-related kinase NEK4 as key regulator coupling meiotic initiation with zygote morphogenesis. NEK4 accumulates at microtubule-organizing center and apical polar complex shortly after fertilization. Pbnek4 gene deletion caused complete developmental arrest: blocked MTOC duplication and microtubule formation, uncondensed chromatin, failed nuclear migration and cell polarity establishment. Transcriptomic and phosphoproteomic analyses revealed collapse in networks governing meiosis and cytoskeletal organization with reduced expression/phosphorylation of HOP1, REC8, AP2-O.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria: translating rodent models to human disease
articles.title_original: Comment on: Cerebral malaria: of mice and men.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Comment paper on cerebral malaria research comparing rodent models with human disease.

articles.headline: P. falciparum MSP2 genetic diversity and multiple infections in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity of the IC/3D7 allelic family of the merozoite surface protein 2 (MSP2) of Plasmodium falciparum and multiplicity of infection in four health facilities in the Mbouda Health District, Cameroon.
articles.details: Survey of 481 individuals in Cameroon; 137 (28.48%) tested positive for P. falciparum. MSP2 gene amplified in 64% of positive samples revealing 15 distinct alleles with 14.15% overall genetic diversity and mean multiplicity of infection 1.20. Most infections (81.68%) were monogenotype, indicating high transmission intensity.

articles.headline: Persistent cognitive deficits years after childhood cerebral malaria and severe anemia
articles.title_original: Long-Term Cognitive Ability and Academic Achievement After Childhood Severe Malaria.
articles.details: Longitudinal follow-up of 889 Ugandan children (mean age 11.1 years) tested 4–15 years after severe malaria episode. Cerebral malaria survivors showed lower overall cognition (adjusted mean difference −0.41, 95% CI −0.74 to −0.09) and math scores (−0.46, 95% CI −0.78 to −0.14); severe malarial anemia survivors showed deficits in cognition (−0.31, 95% CI −0.61 to −0.01) and math (−0.32, 95% CI −0.61 to −0.03) compared to community controls. Acute kidney injury at baseline predicted worse outcomes.

articles.headline: Long-term neurocognitive outcomes following severe malaria infection
articles.title_original: Long-Term Neurocognitive Outcomes After Severe Malaria Infection: No Peace of MIND.
articles.details: Study of long-term neurocognitive outcomes after severe malaria infection.

articles.headline: Novel PX1 haplotype marker for artemisinin and lumefantrine resistance
articles.title_original: A Plasmodium falciparum PX1 haplotype is associated with reduced susceptibility to artemisinin and lumefantrine.
articles.details: Genetic cross and drug-selection experiments identified P. falciparum px1 mutations (PX1 PIN haplotype: L1222P, M1701I, D1705N) as strongest correlates of reduced susceptibility to dihydroartemisinin and lumefantrine in Ugandan parasites. This haplotype was enriched under drug pressure and confers growth advantage in asexual blood stages. The mutation rose rapidly over two decades in Uganda, making PX1 a robust marker for surveillance of emerging artemisinin-lumefantrine resistance in African P. falciparum.
articles.notable_reason: Novel genetic marker of artemisinin/lumefantrine resistance with surveillance implications

articles.headline: Outdoor residual spraying most cost-effective for P. knowlesi control in Sabah
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of outdoor residual spraying, insecticide-treated nets, and their combination for zoonotic malaria control in Sabah, Malaysia: a comparative analysis.
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis of vector control for zoonotic P. knowlesi malaria in three Malaysian Borneo districts. Outdoor residual spraying (ORS) averted 13 cases at MYR 2046 per case averted (approximately USD 470), compared to long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) and combined ORS+LLINs, which were less effective and more costly. ORS demonstrated the most favorable cost-effectiveness profile across sensitivity analyses.

articles.headline: Global genomic surveillance reveals region-specific P. falciparum drug resistance patterns
articles.title_original: Global-scale population genetic analysis of Plasmodium falciparum identifies region-specific patterns of malaria parasite adaptation.
articles.details: Analysis of 17,565 P. falciparum isolates from 39 countries identified distinct region-specific patterns of drug selection and resistance. Vietnamese isolates exhibited high genetic relatedness and all carried pfkelch13 C580Y artemisinin-resistance mutation. Brazilian isolates showed selection around pfcrt. Alternative resistance pathways identified in South America and Horn of Africa suggest emerging artemisinin resistance via mechanisms beyond pfkelch13.
articles.notable_reason: Continental-scale genomic surveillance identifying region-specific drug resistance emergence patterns

articles.headline: Rapid nanopore sequencing enables continental P. falciparum surveillance in Africa
articles.title_original: Continental-scale genomic surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum malaria across sub-Saharan Africa with rapid nanopore sequencing.
articles.details: Novel nanopore sequencing protocol for P. falciparum genomic surveillance: rapid (~5 hours), cost-effective (<$25 USD/sample), and suitable for decentralized implementation. Surveyed 1,065 samples across six African countries tracking antimalarial resistance genes, hrp2/3 deletions, and vaccine targets (csp, ama1). Includes offline bioinformatics dashboard for real-time analysis, overcoming barriers to continental-scale genomic surveillance.
articles.notable_reason: Novel methodology enabling scalable continental genomic surveillance across sub-Saharan Africa

articles.headline: DHA-piperaquine effective for uncomplicated P. vivax malaria in Brazilian Amazon
articles.title_original: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine versus chloroquine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium vivax malaria with concurrent or delayed high-dose primaquine in Brazil (Curavivax): an open-label, single-centre, randomised clinical trial.
articles.details: Open-label RCT of 418 patients comparing dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine to chloroquine plus immediate or delayed primaquine for P. vivax. Day-42 recurrence with DHA-piperaquine+primaquine was 1.0% (95% CI 0.0–5.3%) vs chloroquine+primaquine 2.0% (0.2–7.0%, p=0.44). DHA-piperaquine alone substantially outperformed chloroquine alone (2.4% vs 27.3%, p<0.0001). All treatments well tolerated.
articles.notable_reason: Phase 3 trial supporting DHA-piperaquine as P. vivax treatment alternative

articles.headline: Plasmodium cardiolipin synthase essential for mitochondrial function and liver-stage development
articles.title_original: A bacterial-type cardiolipin synthase in Plasmodium spp. supports mitochondrial respiration and is important for liver stage maturation.
articles.details: Molecular study of bacterial-type cardiolipin synthase (Cls) in P. falciparum. Knockout in P. berghei showed impaired asexual blood-stage proliferation, reduced cardiolipin content, decreased mitochondrial membrane potential, and reduced oxygen consumption. While sporozoite formation proceeded, knockout parasites exhibited severe defects in liver-stage maturation, identifying Cls as vital for mitochondrial function and parasite development.

articles.headline: Live imaging reveals spatiotemporal dynamics of P. falciparum egress from RBCs
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal dynamics of signal dependent exocytosis and parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture during Plasmodium falciparum egress.
articles.details: Live-cell imaging study of P. falciparum merozoite egress using fluorescent reporters. Signal-dependent exocytosis of egress-specific vesicles triggered 3 hours before merozoite egress. Parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture occurred at single site and rapidly expanded. PfERC (endoplasmic reticulum calcium-binding protein) required for exocytosis of egress-specific vesicles, implicating calcium signaling in egress regulation.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol: cost-effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies for first-line treatment of malaria among pediatric and adult populations: a systematic review protocol.
articles.details: Protocol for systematic review assessing cost-effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) versus non-ACT or alternative-dose ACT regimens for first-line malaria treatment. Will follow JBI methodology for economic evidence, evaluate ICER and QALY measures, and use GRADE approach to assess evidence certainty. Aims to inform treatment policies in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum and vivax diversity high across Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and multiplicity of Plasmodium spp. infections in Southeast Asia: a scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review of 16 studies analyzing genetic diversity and multiplicity of infection (MOI) across Plasmodium species in Southeast Asia. P. falciparum showed polyclonal infection rates 3.9%-73.9% (mean MOI 1.05-4.9) in 1,609 genotyped infections; P. vivax rates 16.9%-71.4% (mean MOI 1.1-1.91, heterozygosity 0.66-0.87) in 1,526 infections; P. malariae low MOI; P. knowlesi rates 7.3%-21% (mean MOI 1.04-1.06) in 188 infections. Limited data on P. malariae, P. knowlesi, and P. ovale highlight need for standardized surveillance.

articles.headline: Severe Malaria
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria.
articles.details: No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Cultural history of malaria in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data of cultural history of malaria in Hungary].
articles.details: No abstract provided.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine reduces child mortality in Ghana, Kenya, Malawi
articles.title_original: Impact of introducing RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine on mortality in young children in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi: an observational evaluation of a cluster-randomised implementation programme.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized implementation evaluation of RTS,S/AS01E vaccine in 158 clusters across three countries (2019-2023). 1,289,504 children received first dose; coverage achieved 82.8% (95% CI 80.7-84.9) for first dose, 71.1% (68.8-73.5) for third dose. At 46 months, mortality rate ratio was 0.87 (95% CI 0.77-0.97; p=0.016) in vaccine-eligible children, representing approximately one in eight deaths averted with moderate three-dose coverage.

articles.headline: Fatal Plasmodium relictum infection in endangered Fatu Hiva monarch
articles.title_original: First record of fatal Plasmodium relictum (GRW4) infection in a highly endangered Fatu Hiva monarch (Pomarea whitneyi) fledgling: a case report.
articles.authors: Igual I Beltrán M, et al.
articles.details: Case report of fatal avian malaria caused by Plasmodium relictum (GRW4) in recently fledged juvenile Fatu Hiva monarch (Pomarea whitneyi), a Critically Endangered species with fewer than 20 individuals remaining. Necropsy revealed severe emaciation, hepatosplenomegaly, and high parasitaemia. Diagnosis confirmed via nested cytochrome b PCR on blood samples. Case highlights urgent need for disease surveillance and vector management in conservation planning.

articles.headline: Artemisinin partial resistance detected in DRC with Uganda cross-border spread
articles.title_original: Cross-border molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum drug susceptibility: insights from the Uganda-Democratic Republic of the Congo border.
articles.details: Cross-border molecular survey in northeastern DRC (May-June 2023) of 536 villagers found 49.8% P. falciparum PCR-positive. Molecular markers included: pfmdr1 N86 fixed, NFD haplotype 50.3% (reduced lumefantrine susceptibility), pfmdr1 CNV 11.7% (1.4 threshold), pfpm2/3 CNV 7.0%, pfkelch13 A675V 4.3% and C469Y 1.3% (artemisinin partial resistance). Whole-genome sequencing showed DRC parasites with resistance mutations clustered with Ugandan strains, supporting cross-border parasite migration.

articles.headline: Plasmodium PLP1 membrane pore formation mechanism revealed by simulations
articles.title_original: Deciphering Membrane Pore Formation Mechanisms of Plasmodium falciparum Perforin-Like Protein 1 (PfPLP1).
articles.authors: Patil SB, Dasgupta S, Bhaumik P
articles.details: Molecular dynamics simulations investigating Plasmodium falciparum Perforin-Like Protein 1 (PfPLP1) binding and pore formation mechanisms. PfPLP1 comprises MACPF domain and APCβ domain; initial membrane binding mediated by cationic residues interacting with lipid headgroups. In tetrameric form, water penetration initiates pore opening with lipids reorganizing into toroidal edge. Larger oligomeric assemblies show lateral lipid displacement and pore-like structure formation, providing molecular insights into a potential therapeutic target.

articles.headline: High malaria transmission accelerates tetanus antibody waning in children
articles.title_original: Assessing the effect of malaria exposure history on tetanus antibody waning rates among children in Jinja and Tororo Districts, Uganda.
articles.details: Longitudinal study of 3,064 plasma samples from 597 children (6 months to 10 years) in Tororo (high malaria) and Jinja (low malaria) districts, Uganda, 2011-2017. Tetanus antibody seroprevalence 87.5% in 6-11 month-olds; median antibody half-lives 15.8 years (95% CI 8.8-28.5) in Jinja versus 2.8 years (2.6-3.1) in Tororo. Hazard of sero-reversion 2.7-fold higher (95% CI 1.7-4.1) in high-transmission district, suggesting malaria endemicity should inform tetanus booster timing.

articles.headline: Drug resistance gene surveillance in South Korea 2022-2025
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum Drug Resistance Genes in the Republic of Korea: 2022-2025.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of drug resistance genes in P. vivax (soldiers, relapsed, imported) and P. falciparum (soldiers, civilians) isolates, 2022-2025. P. vivax: S515R, G698S, L845F, M908L, T958M, F1076L observed in nearly all isolates; S513R, Y541C, A829V, S1358 occasionally identified (imported/prophylaxis). P. falciparum: Y184F in Pfmdr-1 highly prevalent; A675V in PfK13 detected only in soldiers. No significant temporal trends observed, but significant group differences (p<0.05).

articles.headline: GMZ2.6c vaccine elicits cellular responses against malaria antigens
articles.title_original: Cellular Immune Response and T Cell Epitope Mapping of Plasmodium falciparum Chimeric Vaccine Candidate GMZ2.6c and Its Components (MSP-3, GLURP and Pfs48/45) in Individuals Naturally Exposed to Malaria in Brazilian Amazon.
articles.details: Study of cellular immune responses to GMZ2.6c (multi-stage P. falciparum chimeric vaccine containing GLURP, MSP-3, and Pfs48/45-6c) in peripheral blood mononuclear cells from malaria-exposed individuals in Brazilian Amazon. Individuals with active infection showed increased CD8+ T cell activation to MSP-3 and Pfs48/45, elevated CD4+ TCM and reduced CD4+ TEM. Both groups produced elevated IL-6 and TNF; non-infected individuals had high IL-10. Multiple predicted T cell epitopes across vaccine components elicited IFN-γ responses.

articles.headline: Influenza co-infection protects mice from malaria lung pathology
articles.title_original: Protective coinfection: influenza reprograms myeloid cells to limit CD8 T cell-mediated malaria pathology.
articles.details: Mouse model study showing co-infection with non-lethal influenza A/HKx31 protected mice from malaria-associated acute lung injury (MA-ALI) and death. X31 co-infection expanded Ly6C+ monocyte-derived alveolar macrophages that inhibited pathogenic CD8 T cells in contact-dependent manner. Protection occurred independently of parasite burden and type I interferon signaling; co-infected CD8 T cells exhibited transcriptional reprogramming and impaired inflammatory cytokine production.

articles.headline: GWAS identifies malaria resistance mechanisms in Hawaiian honeycreeper birds
articles.title_original: Evolution of Resistance to Avian Malaria Infection in an Endemic Hawaiian Honeycreeper.
articles.details: Genome-wide association study in 35 O'ahu 'amakihi (Chlorodrepanis flava) identifying 582 single nucleotide polymorphisms (SNPs) associated with Plasmodium relictum infection status. Annotations included long ncRNAs, transcription regulation genes, apoptosis, T-cell and complement activation, autophagosome assembly, and intracellular calcium flux regulators. Identified genes regulating intracellular calcium, a key signaling molecule for Plasmodium replication, suggesting host resistance involves restricting intracellular components essential for parasite life cycle.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa, WHO study reports.
articles.details: WHO study reports that malaria vaccine reduces child deaths by 13% in Africa.

articles.headline: RTS,S/AS01 vaccine projected to reduce malaria cases 40% in Benin
articles.title_original: Estimating the impact of the RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine in Benin: A mathematical modelling study.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study of RTS,S/AS01 vaccine impact in children under-5 in Benin. Vaccine at DTP3-level coverage combined with nets projected to reduce clinical cases and deaths by 40% (2025-2034) versus nets alone. However, if net use decreases by 1.5-fold, gains may be offset.

articles.headline: Delivering malaria services during COVID-19 pandemic: challenges and lessons
articles.title_original: Delivering malaria services during the COVID-19 pandemic: challenges and lessons from an international non-governmental organisation.
articles.details: NGO reflections on maintaining malaria services during COVID-19. Team established dedicated taskforce, developed outbreak management plan with four risk phases, and adapted protocols to local contexts for safer delivery. Experiences offer practical insights for organizations preparing for future operational challenges.

articles.headline: Political economy analysis of barriers to malaria elimination in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Accelerating Nigeria towards malaria elimination requires moving away from business as usual: insights from a political economy analysis.
articles.details: Political economy analysis of Nigeria's malaria program using desk review and interviews with key managers. Limited government prioritization, weak citizen advocacy, lack of decision-maker communication, and poor partner coordination hinder elimination. Sustained political will, citizen demand, better coordination, and high-level advocacy groups needed to advance progress.

articles.headline: High sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance in pregnant women parasites, Mozambique
articles.title_original: Prevalence of pfdhfr-pfdhps Sextuple and Gametocyte-Associated Quintuple Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine Resistance Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum Isolates from Pregnant Women in Mozambique.
articles.details: Molecular analysis of 100 P. falciparum isolates from Mozambique pregnant women (2014-15) found 54% with quintuple and 7% with sextuple pfdhfr/pfdhps resistance mutations. Gametocytes detected in 34% overall, significantly associated with quintuple mutants (AOR=7.5, p=0.001) which accounted for 80% of gametocyte-positive infections. Highlights need for SP resistance surveillance to guide IPTp strategies.

articles.headline: Low immunization coverage in high-malaria communities, Bioko Island
articles.title_original: Immunization Coverage and Associated Factors Among Children Aged 18 to 72 Months in Communities With High Malaria Transmission, Bioko Island.
