articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 297 children (18 months-6 years) in Bioko Island found only 29% fully immunized by national schedule. Coverage similar from vaccination cards (28%) versus caregiver recall (30%). Children 18-24 months had higher completion (45%) than 2-6 years (22%, aOR=0.42, p<0.05). Overall coverage well below 80% national target.

articles.headline: Artemisinin resistance clearance thresholds may miss African resistance spread
articles.title_original: Artemisinin partial resistance at a crossroads: evidence for continental variation in Plasmodium falciparumclearance phenotypes.
articles.details: Systematic review of 96 studies (35 with genotype-linked clearance/RSA data) comparing SE Asia and Africa. In SE Asia, kelch13 mutants show prolonged parasite clearance half-life concordant with RSA. In east/Horn of Africa, kelch13 mutants show faster clearance half-life despite elevated RSA, suggesting continental phenotype differences. Current surveillance thresholds may under-detect emerging African artemisinin resistance, risking delayed policy responses.
articles.notable_reason: Current surveillance thresholds risk under-detecting African artemisinin resistance

articles.headline: Low-dose versus standard-dose primaquine for gametocytocidal efficacy trial
articles.title_original: Protocol for a multicentric open-label randomized controlled trial comparing low-dose (0.25 mg/kg) and standard-dose (0.75 mg/kg) primaquine for gametocytocidal efficacy and safety in Plasmodium falciparum malaria.
articles.details: Multicentric open-label RCT at 4 Indian sites (target n=496) comparing low-dose primaquine 0.25 mg/kg day-1 versus standard-dose 0.75 mg/kg day-2 for gametocytocidal efficacy in adults with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. Primary outcomes: gametocyte clearance by microscopy and QT-NASBA (Pfs25 mRNA on days 1, 2/3, 7, 14); safety measured by hemoglobin changes. Enrollment began August 2025.

articles.headline: Sex, age, education disparities in bed net coverage, Tanzania
articles.title_original: Socio-demographic predictors of insecticide-treated bed net ownership and utilization for protection against malaria by rural community members across five regions of Mainland Tanzania.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 10,228 Tanzanians from 5 regions (July-Aug 2023) found 77.6% owned and 77.2% used ITNs. Females higher odds of ownership/use versus males (aOR=1.27, p<0.001). Under-fives higher odds of ownership (aOR=1.83) and use (aOR=2.26) versus adults (p<0.001). Higher education and SES independently associated with coverage. Overall coverage below 80% national target.

articles.headline: Actinomycin derivatives show improved selectivity against malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Discovery of actinomycin derivatives with improved selectivity against malaria parasites from a Streptomyces culture library.
articles.details: Large-scale fermentation and purification of Streptomyces antibioticus HUT6035 yielded actinomycins X2 and X0ß. Actinomycin X2 showed IC50=0.241 nM, CC50=6.71 nM (SI=27.9); X0ß showed IC50=7.69 nM, CC50=818 nM (SI=106). Both demonstrated 2-7 fold higher selectivity indices versus commercial actinomycin D (SI=14.3), suggesting potential as antimalarial agents.

articles.headline: PfCoronin mutations reduce endocytosis to confer artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of PfCoronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Cellular and molecular study found PfCoronin (interacting with PfActin, localizing to plasma membrane, digestive vacuole, and vesicles) mediates artemisinin resistance. PfCoronin mutations disrupt localization, perturb PfActin homeostasis, and reduce host cell/hemoglobin uptake by ring-stage parasites. Decreased hemoglobin uptake limits heme-dependent artemisinin activation, explaining reduced drug susceptibility. Identifies novel protein target for resistance mechanisms.

articles.headline: Soil-transmitted helminth exposure not linked to asymptomatic malaria
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum Infection Was Not Associated with Exposure to Soil-Transmitted Helminths in Children in Esse, Cameroon.
articles.details: Longitudinal study of 134 school children in high-malaria Cameroon found 94.8% P. falciparum positive at baseline, 85.8% asymptomatic; 87.4% seropositive for STH antigens. No significant difference in STH exposure between asymptomatic (85.7%) and symptomatic (90.9%) infections (p>0.05). No correlation between anti-STH antibody and parasite load. STH exposure not associated with persistent asymptomatic P. falciparum infection.

articles.headline: Correction to Thai-Myanmar border malaria elimination study
articles.title_original: Correction: Challenges and responses of malaria elimination in a high-endemic area along the Thai-Myanmar border: A health systems perspective.
articles.details: This is a correction notice to a previously published article on malaria elimination challenges and health systems responses in a high-endemic area along the Thai-Myanmar border. No new data presented.

articles.headline: dCas9-SSAP cleavage-free genome editing for Plasmodium parasites
articles.title_original: The dCas9-SSAP as a promising genome editing tool in malaria parasites.
articles.details: Review of dCas9-SSAP (catalytically inactive Cas9 with microbial single-stranded annealing proteins), a next-generation CRISPR variant designed to overcome double-strand break and genotoxicity limitations of classical Cas9-nuclease approaches. Discusses mechanistic framework and alignment with Plasmodium biology. Authors propose dCas9-SSAP as a promising option for future malaria parasite genome editing research.

articles.headline: Novel P. falciparum RBC surface antigens identified as vaccine candidates
articles.title_original: Identification of Novel Malaria Antigens Expressed on the Surface of RBCs Infected with Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study immunized BALB/cJ mice with live P. falciparum-infected RBCs and membrane fractions to identify surface antigens. Polyclonal sera showed significant parasite-killing activity in growth inhibition assays. Phage display screening identified both known and novel antigens predicted to be membrane-associated. Authors propose further evaluation in in vitro/in vivo challenge experiments and non-human primates toward human vaccine trials.

articles.headline: ACE polymorphism potentially linked to severe malaria
articles.title_original: Potential Role of ACE Polymorphisms in Severe Malaria: A Case Report.
articles.details: Case report of a 40-year-old woman from Seychelles/Ivory Coast with severe P. falciparum malaria complicated by thrombosis, hyperparasitemia, acute kidney injury, and metabolic acidosis. Genetic testing identified ACE I/D polymorphism and thrombophilic variants (MTHFR C677T and A1298C). Authors call for comprehensive studies to explore genetic landscape in malaria-endemic regions and implications for personalized treatment.

articles.headline: Long-acting ivermectin injection meets WHO malaria vector efficacy targets
articles.title_original: Selection of the optimal long-acting injectable formulation of ivermectin for use in humans to target malaria vectors in Western Africa: evaluation of pharmacokinetics and mosquitocidal efficacy in cattle under laboratory conditions.
articles.details: Three long-acting injectable ivermectin formulations (BEPO®-based) tested in cattle (n=5 per formulation at 0.6 mg/kg) in Burkina Faso. mdc-STM-001 showed optimal pharmacokinetics with Cmax 34.5±12.7 ng/ml (12% inter-individual variability). Ten-day hazard ratios exceeded 4 and cumulative mortalities >50% for ≥60 days against both insecticide-susceptible and wild-derived resistant Anopheles gambiae. The 10-day LC50 for resistant mosquitoes (3.66, 95% CI 2.69-4.97 ng/ml) maintained for ≥126 days. Single injection provided sustained mosquitocidal efficacy ≥2 months, meeting WHO PPC (HR>4) and supporting progression to phase 1 clinical trials.

articles.headline: 23-year climate and vector control effects on malaria incidence in Iran
articles.title_original: The influence of climatic factors and vector control on malaria incidence in Southeast Iran: a 23-year longitudinal analysis.
articles.details: A 23-year longitudinal analysis of malaria incidence in Southeast Iran examining influences of climatic factors and vector control measures. (No abstract provided; details unavailable.)

articles.headline: Substantial malaria-arbovirus co-exposure in remote Congo Basin
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Malaria and Arbovirus Co-Exposure in the Boende Health Zone, Northwestern Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.details: Cross-sectional door-to-door survey (December 2023) in Inkanamongo village, Boende Health Zone, DRC (n=379 adults). Malaria prevalence 51.7% (95%CI 46.7-56.7%); arboviral seroprevalence 78.4% (95%CI 73.1-81.5%), dominated by O'nyong-nyong (42.8%), Rift Valley fever (32.0%), and chikungunya (23.4%). Concurrent malaria and arboviral exposure in 40.4% (95%CI 35.6-45.4%). No sociodemographic factors significantly associated with co-exposure. Authors conclude integrated surveillance and improved diagnostics urgently needed.

articles.headline: Refugee camp LLINs show physical deterioration and insecticide resistance
articles.title_original: Physical integrity and bioefficacy of long-lasting insecticidal nets 18 months after use among Central African Republic refugees in Gado-Badzere, a high malaria burden camp in Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional assessment of 440 LLINs in Gado-Badzere refugee camp, Cameroon, 18 months post-distribution. Physical integrity: 60.7% good, 14.5% acceptable, 24.8% deteriorated. All 30 LLINs tested ineffective against field Anopheles gambiae s.l. with knock-down rate 1.5% and mortality 3.1%. An. gambiae (86.9%) and An. coluzzii (13.1%) identified. Authors recommend community education on LLIN care and introduction of next-generation LLINs due to apparent insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: No artemisinin resistance markers detected in Madagascar 2024 survey
articles.title_original: Updated mapping of Plasmodium falciparum kelch13 gene polymorphisms in Madagascar, 2024.
articles.details: Nationally representative cross-sectional survey (January-May 2024) of 4,850 febrile patients at 65 health facilities in Madagascar. Pfkelch13 genotyping via targeted amplicon deep sequencing: 963/1,944 (49.5%) P. falciparum samples successfully genotyped. Results: 91.9% wild-type 3D7; non-synonymous mutations 1.8% (17/963); synonymous mutations 6.2% (60/963); double mutations 0.1% (1/963). Five novel mutations identified. No WHO-validated artemisinin partial resistance markers detected across regions sampled.

articles.headline: Plasmodium infection provides cross-protection against Babesia via macrophages
articles.title_original: Immunity beyond borders: non-lethal Plasmodium confers cross-protection against lethal Babesia via macrophage activation.
articles.details: Mouse study of P. berghei XAT - Babesia rodhaini co-infection. P. berghei infection significantly reduced B. rodhaini parasitemia and splenic damage through macrophage-driven innate immunity. Protection associated with pro-inflammatory cytokines (IFN-γ, IL-12p70, TNF-α) balanced by IL-10, with increased ROS/NO. Macrophage depletion eliminated protection; SCID and NK-depleted mice retained it. Authors identify macrophage-mediated cross-protection as potential strategy for multivalent vaccines against apicomplexan parasites.

articles.headline: Computer-designed Plasmodium peptides show antibacterial and anti-inflammatory effects
articles.title_original: Computer-Aided Design of Plasmodium chabaudi-Derived Peptides with Dual Antibiofilm and Anti-inflammatory Activities.
articles.details: Three P. chabaudi-derived peptides (PcDBS1R1, PcDBS1R5, PcDBS1R9) generated using Joker computational design algorithm. PcDBS1R5 and PcDBS1R9 adopted α-helical structures with high amphipathicity and showed antibacterial activity against Acinetobacter baumannii; PcDBS1R1 showed pronounced antibiofilm effects. None exhibited cytotoxicity to murine macrophages; all significantly reduced nitric oxide production in LPS-stimulated macrophages. Authors propose these designed peptides as scaffolds for peptide-based treatments targeting biofilm-associated infections.

articles.headline: Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine improves birthweight by remodeling pregnancy gut microbiome
articles.title_original: Beyond malaria prevention: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine treatment in pregnancy selectively remodels the maternal gut microbiome to increase gestational weight gain and improve birthweight.
articles.details: Longitudinal microbiome study of intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) vs. dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP). SP improves birthweight across sub-Saharan Africa and Western Pacific independent of malaria transmission/resistance, outperforming DP. Gestational weight gain (GWG) mediates two-thirds of SP's birthweight benefit. Approximately 45% of SP's GWG advantage explained by gut microbial changes. Microbiome-mediated GWG coincided with 126g higher birthweight in SP vs. DP recipients (95%CI 22.6-229.3g; p=0.019). SP enriched anaerobic commensals with roles in mucosal immunity and host metabolism.

articles.headline: Quantized ML models vulnerable to adversarial attacks on edge devices
articles.title_original: Evaluating the Adversarial Robustness and Clinical Safety of Quantized Hierarchical Transformers for Edge-Based Malaria Microscopy.
articles.authors: Hasan U, Alghamdi TG, Nayeem MA
articles.details: Study evaluated quantized Vision Transformers for malaria microscopy on IoT devices using 27,558-image dataset. Post-Training Quantization compressed Swin-Tiny model by 3.9× with 0.11% accuracy drop and maintained 81% accuracy against black-box attacks. However, models collapsed to 0% accuracy under white-box iterative PGD adversarial attacks. Findings show integer quantization provides zero defense against targeted perturbations in diagnostic networks.

articles.headline: Computational study of erucamide as Plasmodium DXR inhibitor
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Candidate Study from Three Different Fractions of Streptomyces hygroscopicus subsp. Hygroscopicus targeting Plasmodium DXR: An In Silico Approach.
articles.details: In silico screening of compounds from Streptomyces hygroscopicus identified 86 compounds from three fractions; 9 selected for antimalarial potential. Molecular docking showed erucamide (fraction 51) exhibited higher binding affinity to DXR enzyme than native ligand. 100-nanosecond molecular dynamics simulation revealed erucamide-DXR complex was more stable than other candidates. Authors emphasize that computational results are preliminary and require further in vitro and in vivo validation.

articles.headline: Multi-channel media exposure improves malaria prevention knowledge
articles.title_original: Effect of cumulative exposure to media channels for malaria messages on knowledge of malaria prevention among women (15-49 years) in Uganda.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 7,124 Ugandan women aged 15-49 from 2018-2019 Malaria Indicator Survey. Knowledge varied: bed nets (76.5%), ITNs (9.1%), preventive medicine (6.2%), IRS (4.3%). Cumulative media exposure significantly associated with ITN knowledge (p<0.001), preventive medicine (p=0.002), and IRS knowledge (p<0.001). Women with secondary/higher education 1.86-fold more likely to report multi-channel exposure (95% CI 1.59-2.17). Structural inequities limit equitable access to information.

articles.headline: Methylene blue retains activity against artemisinin-resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Methylene blue retains in vitro activity against early ring-stage artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: In vitro ring survival assay tested methylene blue against P. falciparum isolates with kelch13 wild type (n=3), R561H (n=9), or P441L (n=10). Mean IC50 estimates: wild type 23 nM (95% CrI 15-37), R561H 27 nM (95% CrI 21-36), P441L 14 nM (95% CrI 10-20). No evidence of kelch13-mediated cross-resistance. Methylene blue suppressed ring-stage survival at low nanomolar concentrations across all genotypes.

articles.headline: P. vivax infects Duffy-negative Sudanese despite historical protection
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax infection in Duffy-negative populations in Sudan: a systematic review and meta-analysis of host-parasite genetic adaptation.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 16 studies (2005-2025) with 5,753 Sudanese participants. Meta-analysis of five studies reported pooled P. vivax prevalence in Duffy-negative individuals: 11.7% (95% CI 7.2-17.3%, moderate heterogeneity I²=56%). Substantial parasite genetic diversity in PvDBP with multiple haplotypes and Malagasy/Cambodian-type duplications. Findings challenge assumption of complete Duffy-mediated protection and indicate parasite adaptation facilitating Duffy-negative infection.
articles.notable_reason: Challenges long-established paradigm of Duffy-negative protection against P. vivax

articles.headline: Environmental factors shape avian malaria parasite communities
articles.title_original: Environmental factors affect the communities of avian Plasmodium in two species of Culex mosquitoes.
articles.details: Study of Plasmodium communities in Culex pipiens and C. perexiguus from 16 Spanish sites over 2 years. In C. pipiens, prevalence increased with NDVI while lineage richness declined with land surface temperature. In C. perexiguus, prevalence and richness increased with minimum temperature, diversity decreased with temperature. Plasmodium communities differed between mosquito species; similarity correlated with geographical distance only in C. pipiens.

articles.headline: Specific merozoite antigens associated with malaria clinical immunity
articles.title_original: Controlled human malaria infection in adults identify combinations of merozoite antigens associated with clinical immunity.
articles.details: Controlled human malaria infection study (n=86/142 immune, NCT02739763) screened 70 merozoite antigens using custom protein microarray and five statistical methods (Wilcoxon, Cox, Poisson, LASSO, random forest). Antibodies against MSP1, MSP11, RAMA, MSP7, PF3D7_1401600 (PHISTB), and PTEX150 consistently associated with clinical immunity and in combination predicted complete protection. Findings may prioritize next-generation blood-stage vaccine candidates.

articles.headline: Malaria co-infection prevalent in Yemeni IBS patients with biomarker signatures
articles.title_original: Systemic inflammation and malaria co-infection in irritable bowel syndrome: a cross-sectional study of 142 Yemeni patients.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 142 Yemeni adults (mean age 42.3 years) with IBS diagnosed per Rome IV criteria. Malaria co-infection prevalence: 45.1% (64/142, rapid diagnostic test confirmed). Malaria-positive patients showed significantly higher neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR, mean difference 0.56, p<0.001) and platelet-to-lymphocyte ratio (PLR, mean difference 22.6, p<0.001). In adjusted logistic regression, higher NLR (aOR 3.21, 95% CI 1.89-5.45) and PLR (aOR per 10-unit 4.02, 95% CI 2.24-7.18) independently associated with malaria.

articles.headline: Regulatory T cells mediate immunity and enable parasite persistence
articles.title_original: CXCR6+CD127- Tr1 cells balance immunity and persistence in Plasmodium falciparum infection.
articles.details: Study identified CXCR6+ CD127- as phenotypic markers for type 1 regulatory T (Tr1) cells using scRNA-seq. In Ugandan children, these cells comprised ~90% of P. falciparum-specific CD4+ T cell pool. Higher CXCR6+ CD127- Tr1 frequencies correlated with lower symptom probability given parasitemia but also delayed parasite clearance. Tr1 cells suppressed CD4+ proliferation via IL-10 secretion, suggesting dual role in clinical immunity and parasite persistence.

articles.headline: P. malariae shows minimal geographic structure across Africa
articles.title_original: Population genomics of Plasmodium malariae from four African countries.
articles.details: Whole genome sequencing of 77 P. malariae isolates from Cameroon (n=7), Democratic Republic of Congo (n=16), Nigeria (n=4), and Tanzania (n=50) collected 2015-2021. No evidence of geographic population structure. Nucleotide diversity significantly lower than co-localized P. falciparum; linkage disequilibrium significantly higher. Genome-wide scans identified no top-hit resistance orthologs, though targeted analysis revealed selective sweeps around 4 erythrocyte invasion ligands and 6 resistance orthologs. Demographics suggest recovery from bottleneck.

articles.headline: Pirimiphos-methyl IRS plus ITNs achieves major malaria reduction
articles.title_original: Impact of indoor residual spraying and insecticide-treated nets on malaria burden in 8 districts in West Nile and Acholi regions, Uganda: a quasi-experimental study.
articles.details: Quasi-experimental study comparing West Nile (IRS + ITNs) vs Acholi (ITNs only) using 8 malaria reference centers over 4 years. During IRS-1 with clothianidin-deltamethrin (Dec 2022-Dec 2023), 15% incidence difference non-significant (aIRR=0.85, 95% CI 0.69-1.04). During IRS-2 with pirimiphos-methyl (Jan 2024-Dec 2024), intervention arm incidence declined 79.3% from baseline (720.9 to 149.1 per 1000 person-years) vs control 24.3%, with 73% reduction in intervention vs control (aIRR=0.27, 95% CI 0.22-0.34, p<0.001).

articles.headline: Structure-based PfDHODH inhibitors show micromolar antiparasitic activity
articles.title_original: Structure-based design of new pyrazole inhibitors targeting Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH).
articles.details: Structure-based optimization of hydroxypyrazole compounds targeting P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase through 3 iterative design rounds. Best compounds (derivatives 6, 7, 12, 13, 22) demonstrated low micromolar enzymatic inhibition (IC50 1.3-5.9 μM), selective over human enzyme (SI>30), favorable physicochemical properties (PBS solubility 0.03-2.8 mM, logD7.4 0.25-1.7), and antiparasitic activity against P. falciparum 3D7 (EC50 15-22 μM) with negligible mammalian cytotoxicity (CC50>150 μM). Authors note hydroxypyrazole scaffolds show poor antiplasmodial activity and emphasize need for further optimization.

articles.headline: Novel mosquito-parasite protein interface blocks malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Disruption of the AgMOBP1-PfSyn5 interface abolishes malaria transmission.
articles.details: The study identified AgMOBP1 (Anopheles gambiae Midgut Ookinete Binding Protein 1) on mosquito midgut epithelial cells and its P. falciparum binding partner PfSyn5 (syntaxin-5-like protein). Disrupting this interaction with anti-PfSyn5 antibodies achieved complete transmission inhibition at 1 μg/mL with IC50 approximately 3 ng/mL (~20 pM), one million-fold lower than normal human plasma IgG concentration. The AgMOBP1-PfSyn5 axis is proposed as a target for transmission-blocking vaccines and monoclonal antibodies.
articles.notable_reason: Exceptionally potent transmission-blocking target with million-fold advantage

articles.headline: Calcifer stabilizes basal body during Plasmodium gamete transmission
articles.title_original: Organelle mechanics: A brace for the Plasmodium basal body.
articles.details: Commentary identifying Calcifer as a late-recruited stabilizer that protects the Plasmodium basal body from mechanical stress during male gamete formation and axoneme release in mosquito transmission.

articles.headline: Twelve-year malaria surveillance in Bishoftu, Ethiopia shows 2.3% prevalence
articles.title_original: The persistent threat of malaria in Bishoftu Town: twelve-year trends and their implications for Addis Ababa.
articles.details: Analysis of 252,610 blood films from 2011-2022 identified 5,696 malaria cases (2.3%), with P. vivax predominating at 63.8% and P. falciparum at 31.0%. Peak incidence occurred in 2017 with 804 cases; individuals over 14 years represented 66.3% of cases. Transmission showed fluctuating trends with concerns about spillover to nearby Addis Ababa.

articles.headline: Invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi detected south of equator
articles.title_original: Serendipitous detection of invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi in Kisumu, Kenya in June 2022.
articles.details: Molecular analysis of mosquitoes collected from Kisumu, Kenya in June 2022 confirmed the presence of An. stephensi (19.6% of 56 sampled), confirmed by cytochrome c oxidase 1 gene analysis. This represents the first report of this invasive vector south of the equatorial line. Some haplotypes matched specimens from northern Kenya, while others clustered with Ethiopian and Sudanese specimens.

articles.headline: Anticancer drug berzosertib shows potential for malaria repurposing
articles.title_original: Repurposing Drugs for Malaria through a Human Dose Prediction: A Case Study with Berzosertib.
articles.details: Dose-escalation studies in P. falciparum-infected humanized mice showed berzosertib activity is time-dependent above the minimum parasiticidal concentration (MPC). Pharmacokinetic modeling predicted six daily 75 mg intravenous doses would treat uncomplicated malaria, comparatively lower than the cancer therapy maximum tolerated dose (440 mg twice weekly). Further preclinical development is needed to meet antimalarial standards.

articles.headline: Repurposed antibiotics show synergistic antimalarial activity against resistance targets
articles.title_original: Repositioning Antibiotics Against Plasmodium falciparum RAD5 and WD11 as Novel Antimalarial Targets.
articles.authors: Albaqami AS, Oyewole OA, Adam KM
articles.details: Molecular docking and in vitro screening identified antibiotics targeting novel P. falciparum proteins PfRAD5 and PfWD11 implicated in drug resistance. Puromycin achieved 85% inhibition alone (100 μg/mL), while the triple combination (puromycin + raltegravir + dicloxacillin) achieved complete inhibition; puromycin with chloroquine showed concentration-dependent synergy. qPCR confirmed target downregulation with puromycin-based treatments.

articles.headline: Calcifer protein stabilizes basal body during Plasmodium gamete transmission
articles.title_original: Mechanoregulation of basal body integrity during Plasmodium exflagellation.
articles.details: The study identified Calcifer as a conserved Plasmodium protein essential for male gamete formation and mosquito transmission. Parasites lacking Calcifer form intact axonemes but show rapid disintegration during exflagellation; the defect is rescued by inhibiting axoneme beating. Calcifer forms a wrapping structure around the basal body at the axoneme minus end, providing mechanical resistance to stress from axoneme beating.
articles.notable_reason: Novel essential transmission factor with elegant mechanical stabilization mechanism

articles.headline: Seven-year malaria surveillance in Pakistani district shows declining trend
articles.title_original: Spatial Distribution, seasonal dynamics and molecular confirmation of malaria in District Malakand, Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Analysis of 41,863 tests from District Malakand 2011-2017 identified 6,246 cases (14.92% positivity). Annual test positivity declined from 32.97% (2011) to 10.77% (2017), with autumn peak (46.73% of cases) and winter low (7.09%). Males (58.89%) and ages 1-20 years predominated. Spatial analysis identified Gharhi, Dargai, and Haryankot as high-burden hotspots.

articles.headline: Artemisinin resistance emerging across eastern and southern Africa
articles.title_original: Africa cannot afford to wait for malaria treatment failure.
articles.details: Perspective highlighting the growing threat of artemisinin partial resistance (ART-R) being detected through molecular surveillance across eastern Africa, the Horn of Africa, and southern Africa. Confirmed cases in Rwanda, Uganda, Eritrea, and Tanzania threaten the long-standing effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs).

articles.headline: Fine-scale malaria hotspot analysis reveals stable clusters in Mali
articles.title_original: Analysis of clinical malaria hotspots according to transmission levels in two health areas of the Kolondieba health district, Mali.
articles.details: Analysis of 35,934 cases from two health areas (2019-2021) using Kulldorff's method identified distinct transmission dynamics. In high-transmission Kadiana, hotspots remained stable across periods (Kadiana and Tienkourani; RR 1.8-6.3), while moderate-transmission Kolondieba Central showed evolution from localized to spatially expanded clusters (RR 1.7-2.2). Both areas had persistent hotspots.

articles.headline: Culex restuans mosquitoes harbor diverse avian Plasmodium lineages in Illinois
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous distribution of avian malaria parasites across mosquito species: disproportionate involvement of Culex restuans in the Midwestern United States.
articles.details: Survey of 6,170 mosquitoes from 38 Illinois sites identified 44 of 233 pools (18.9%) positive for avian haemosporidians. Eleven distinct lineages were detected (9 Plasmodium and 2 Haemoproteus), including one novel Plasmodium lineage. Culex restuans showed the highest lineage diversity and infection prevalence, most strongly associated with avian Plasmodium detection.

articles.headline: Genome-scale regulatory elements mapped in Anopheles gambiae germline
articles.title_original: Decoding cis-regulatory elements in the germline of the human malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: In silico analysis identified hundreds of cis-regulatory elements (CREs) correlated with A. gambiae germline expression, often linked to distinct sperm developmental stages and chromosomal locations. CRE distribution aligned with experimental germline promoter patterns. In vivo dual-reporter validation confirmed a testis-enriched CRE's role in male germline expression. This provides the first nucleotide-resolution regulatory annotation for the A. gambiae germline.
articles.notable_reason: First nucleotide-resolution germline regulatory map enabling gene drive design

articles.headline: 2023 armed conflict disrupted malaria surveillance in Sudan
articles.title_original: Impact of the 2023-armed conflict on routine malaria imapact indicators in Sudan (January 2020-March 2025): a mixed-methods analysis of surveillance data.
articles.details: Mixed-methods analysis of DHIS2 data (Jan 2020-Mar 2025) from 17 Sudanese states plus qualitative interviews. Missing DHIS2 reporting increased substantially after April 2023, with regional variation. ITSA showed statistically significant decline in quarterly malaria case rates per 100,000 population at conflict start (p=0.02). Key informants identified health facility destruction, workforce shortages, and unpaid salaries as barriers to surveillance.

articles.headline: Alpha-mangostin nanoparticles show synergistic activity with chloroquine
articles.title_original: Synergistic Antimalarial Activity of Alpha-Mangostin Chitosan Alginate Nanoparticles with Chloroquine in Mice Infected with Plasmodium berghei: The Potential of a New Antimalarial Drug Combination.
articles.details: Suppressive test in P. berghei-infected mice using different alpha-mangostin chitosan alginate nanoparticles (ACAN):chloroquine weight ratios (189:1, 63:1, 567:1). ED50 values were 9.196, 7.626, and 82.13 mg/kg body weight. Fractional ED50 sums (0.069, 0.113, 0.414) all substantially below 1, indicating synergistic antimalarial activity, especially in the 189:1 ratio.

articles.headline: Malaria transmission varies by altitude in Khyber Pakhtunkhwa
articles.title_original: A Topographic Analysis of Malaria Transmission - Khyber Pakhtunkhwa, Pakistan, 2019-2022.
articles.details: Topographic analysis of malaria transmission in Pakistan (2019-2022) across different altitude regions. High-altitude areas experienced low transmission, while low-altitude regions showed high transmission rates. Findings suggest that considering topography in malaria control strategies may help policymakers design more targeted prevention measures.

articles.headline: World Malaria Day 2026: Driven to End Malaria
articles.title_original: World Malaria Day 2026: Driven to End Malaria.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria: pathophysiology and immediate artesunate treatment
articles.title_original: [Cerebral Malaria].
articles.details: Review of cerebral malaria, which occurs when parasite-infected red blood cells adhere to endothelial cells lining brain microvasculature, obstructing the vascular lumen; detailed pathophysiology remains unclear. When cerebral malaria is suspected, intravenous artesunate should be initiated as soon as possible.

articles.headline: PfARMA N-terminal region is immunodominant but not protective
articles.title_original: The N-terminal region of malaria vaccine candidate asparagine-rich merozoite antigen is immunodominant and targeted by polyreactive antibodies.
articles.details: Study of naturally acquired B cell and antibody responses to P. falciparum asparagine-rich merozoite antigen (PfARMA) in exposed individuals. B cell responses dominated by IgM+ cells recognizing N-terminal intrinsically disordered region 1 (IDR1). Plasma IgM and IgG responses predominantly targeted IDR1 with levels increasing with P. falciparum exposure. However, antibody responses to IDR1 did not correlate with protection from malaria in age- and exposure-matched children.

articles.headline: Avian malaria not associated with host genetic diversity
articles.title_original: Avian Malaria Infections Are Not Associated With Host Genetic Diversity: A Meta-Analysis.
articles.authors: Podmokła E, Pluciński B, Dubiec A
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 35 studies (27 bird species, 85 effect sizes) examining relationship between individual genetic diversity and avian malaria infections caused by Plasmodium, Haemoproteus, and Leucocytozoon. No significant association found between genetic diversity and infection prevalence or intensity, regardless of genetic marker type, parasite genus, infection assessment method, migratory behavior, or latitude.

articles.headline: Digital molecular diagnostic tools help tackle malaria
articles.title_original: How can the use of digital molecular diagnostic tools help to tackle malaria?

articles.headline: High Pfdhps mutations indicate sulfadoxine resistance in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms of the plasmodium falciparum dihydropteroate synthase gene among patients attending LEPI and ADLUCEM hospitals in the west region of Cameroon.
articles.details: Molecular survey of 286 patients (198 women, 88 men, mean age 29±20 years) at two health facilities in Cameroon; P. falciparum detected in 87 (30.41%). Pfdhps gene polymorphisms analyzed: A581G mutant allele frequency 67.60%, A437G mutant 45.07%; wild-type allele frequencies 48.87% (A437G) and 22.53% (A581G). High Pfdhps mutation frequency indicates substantial sulfadoxine resistance pressure, though pyrimethamine resistance mutations were not assessed, limiting overall SP efficacy conclusions.

articles.headline: Shared Plasmodium species between wild primates and Indigenous communities
articles.title_original: Zoonotic and Anthroponotic Plasmodium spp. Circulation between Wild Primates and Indigenous Community, Peruvian Amazon, 2007-2020.
articles.details: Molecular survey (2007-2020) of 141 Indigenous community members and 341 sympatric nonhuman primates (10 species) in Peruvian Amazon using nested/quantitative PCR targeting cytb, cox3, and 18S rRNA genes. Plasmodium detected in 43.3% humans and 51.9% NHPs. P. vivax/simium predominated in humans (42.1%), P. brasilianum/malariae in NHPs (24.6%). Phylogenetic analysis revealed >99.9% identity among human and NHP lineages, supporting shared Plasmodium circulation. One-third of human infections were asymptomatic.

articles.headline: Meteorological drivers model malaria spread in South Africa
articles.title_original: Understanding and modeling meteorological drivers of the incidence and spread of malaria in South Africa.
articles.details: Study of associations between weekly hospital malaria admissions in five Limpopo municipalities (South Africa) and meteorological variables (temperature, rainfall, evapotranspiration). Wavelet transform spectral analysis identified time lags characteristic of malaria development. Regression model developed for admission cases, identifying critical meteorological values that initiate disease spread. Disease projections made for 2021-2050 and 2051-2080 using RCP2.6 and RCP8.5 climate scenarios.

articles.headline: Long-acting injectable ivermectin more effective than oral formulations
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of ivermectin-based optimal strategies on malaria control: the role of formulation, coverage, and mosquitocidal efficacy timing.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling compared ivermectin formulations for malaria control: monthly oral IVM (3×300 μg/kg for 4 months or 1×400 μg/kg for 3 months) versus long-acting injectable BEPO® (0.6-1.0 mg/kg). Under 50-90% target population coverage with perennial transmission, oral regimens reduced total cases by 7-38% annually; single long-acting injectable campaign achieved 16-29% case reduction, increasing to 33-57%, 48-74%, and 59-78% with two, three, and four campaigns respectively.

articles.headline: Pharmacotherapy for malaria in infants
articles.title_original: The impact of state-of-the-art pharmacotherapy for malaria in infants: a breakthrough?
articles.details: Study examining pharmacotherapy approaches for treating malaria in infants.

articles.headline: Plasmodium and Haemoproteus prevalence in Malaysian poultry
articles.title_original: Molecular detection and genetic diversity of avian haemosporidia (Plasmodium and Haemoproteus) infecting red jungle fowls (Gallus gallus spadiceus) and domestic poultry in peninsular, Malaysia.
articles.details: Survey of 713 poultry samples (241 red jungle fowl, 322 village chickens, 150 broilers) from peninsular Malaysia. PCR detected Plasmodium in 30.6% and Haemoproteus in 10.7%, compared to 4.6% and 1.1% by microscopy. Genetic characterization confirmed Plasmodium gallinaceum, P. parasitense, and Haemoproteus species.

articles.headline: Plasmodium huffi detected in Brazilian vultures and falcons
articles.title_original: No host too tough: New records of Plasmodium huffi in competent avian hosts, including Brazilian vultures and falcons.
articles.details: Molecular and morphological confirmation of P. huffi in new avian hosts: one black vulture (Coragyps atratus), one yellow-headed caracara (Daptrius chimachima), and two red-legged seriemas (Cariama cristata) in Brazil. Gametocytes were present in peripheral blood, indicating transmission capability. First report of P. huffi in Falconiformes and first haemosporidian in South American black vultures.

articles.headline: Agricultural insecticides for malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Malaria vector control: Can repurposed agricultural insecticides offer solutions to resistance issues?
articles.details: Review of agricultural insecticides (neonicotinoids, pyrroles, broflanilide, meta-diamides, growth regulators, ivermectin, methoxyfenozide, halofenozide) as alternatives to overcome insecticide resistance in Anopheles, Aedes, and Culex mosquitoes. Some compounds are incorporated into WHO-endorsed tools including insecticide-treated nets and indoor residual sprays; others are under field testing. Emphasizes urgent need for novel chemistries to mitigate malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Eizo Takashima: developing new classes of malaria vaccines
articles.title_original: Eizo Takashima-developing a new class of malaria vaccines.
articles.details: Profile of vaccine development work by Eizo Takashima. Specific vaccine candidates and efficacy data are not detailed in the available information.

articles.headline: Uncomplicated malaria does not worsen COVID-19 outcomes
articles.title_original: Uncomplicated malaria as a risk factor for COVID-19 duration and severity in western Kenya and Burkina Faso (MALCOV): a prospective cohort study.
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 742 COVID-19 patients in Kenya and Burkina Faso (151 with P. falciparum co-infection, 591 without). Time to symptom resolution was similar: 9 days with malaria vs. 10 days without (aHR 1.14, 95% CI 0.91-1.42, p=0.26). Hospitalization and mortality rates ~2% and ~1% in both groups. SARS-CoV-2 clearance slower at day 7 in co-infected patients but similar by day 28.

articles.headline: Malaria-COVID-19 interactions and clinical outcomes
articles.title_original: The fascinating malaria-COVID-19 relationship.
articles.details: Commentary or review on interactions between malaria and COVID-19. Specific empirical findings are not detailed in the title alone.

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum population genomics during elimination
articles.title_original: Impact of intensive control on malaria population genomics under elimination settings in Southeast Asia.
articles.details: Analysis of 2,270 genome-sequenced P. falciparum infections from Myanmar's Kayin State (2015-2020) during intensive control achieving 97% transmission reduction. Effective population size decreased; artemisinin-resistance allele frequency remained low until 2020, when a single kelch13-R561H genotype became dominant. Mass drug administration produced founder effects, providing genomic evidence of control efficacy and rapid parasite population restructuring.

articles.headline: Plasmodium gametocyte membrane protein complexes revealed
articles.title_original: Deciphering Membrane Protein Complexes in Plasmodium falciparum Gametocytes via Integrative Structural Systems Biology.
articles.details: Integrative structural systems biology using cross-linking mass spectrometry and AlphaFold modeling to characterize membrane protein complexes in P. falciparum gametocytes. Study identified host-pathogen and pathogen-pathogen interactions across parasite and erythrocyte compartments. Findings advance molecular understanding of gametocyte remodeling and provide targets for transmission-blocking interventions critical to malaria eradication.

articles.headline: Proteomics for comprehensive malaria systems biology
articles.title_original: Integrative proteomics to illuminate host-parasite interactions in malaria.
articles.details: Perspective advocating proteomics as core to malaria systems biology, enabling quantification of protein abundance, post-translational modifications, interactions, and localization in host and parasite. Proteomics can illuminate cytoadhesion, endothelial dysfunction, immune modulation, and therapeutic response variability less amenable to nucleic-acid methods. Advances in high-throughput, single-cell, and spatial proteomics with AI enable comprehensive profiling.

articles.headline: Mining workers' knowledge and practices of malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and practices of malaria control and prevention in a high-exposure occupational group: A cross-sectional survey of mining workers in Haut-Katanga Province in the Democratic Republic of Congo.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 485 mining workers in Haut-Katanga Province, DRC. 75.3% had adequate malaria knowledge; 99.3% willing to use bed nets and 94.7% for indoor spraying. However, 91.1% actually used bed nets, revealing a knowledge-practice gap. Middle school education (aOR 0.37) and early morning/evening shifts (aOR 0.46) were independent barriers to adequate knowledge.

articles.headline: Sudanese migrants' malaria care-seeking pathways in Egypt
articles.title_original: Sudanese migrants' pathways to malaria care in Egypt: An in-depth qualitative study.
articles.details: Qualitative study of 21 Sudanese migrants (90.5% female) in Cairo; 24% developed symptoms upon arrival. 90.5% initially used traditional remedies or self-medicated without testing. Barriers included low healthcare trust, stigma, quarantine fears, and treatment misconceptions. Improved access requires targeted health education addressing misconceptions and building trust in Egypt's centralized, free malaria treatment system.

articles.headline: Malaria burden linked to child growth failure declined since 1990
articles.title_original: The unequal malaria burden attributable to child growth failure: a global analysis among children under five from 1990 to 2021.
articles.details: Global Burden of Disease analysis (1990-2021) examining malaria attributable to child growth failure in children under 5. Overall age-standardized mortality rate declined annually by 1.26% (AAPC = -1.26, 95% CI: -1.36 to -1.17), but increased 3.66% annually after 2019. Males (ASMR: 17.59 per 100,000) and infants aged 6-11 months (31.63 per 100,000) had highest burden, with West/Central Africa most affected (ASMR: 88.09 per 100,000).

articles.headline: Novel dual-antigen vaccine achieves 80% protection against malaria
articles.title_original: Robust Bioconjugated Antigens Induce Immune Responses Preventing Malaria Infection and its Transmission.
articles.details: Study developed Pfs47-RTS,S-BP vaccine combining pre-erythrocytic circumsporozoite protein (RTS,S) and sexual-stage Pfs47 using bioconjugated biopolymer particles. Compared to single-antigen formulations, the dual-antigen vaccine induced RTS,S-specific antibodies 5.3-fold higher than protective thresholds of current RTS,S/AS01 vaccine. In mouse bite challenge, achieved 80.4% protection against infection and 68.15% transmission-reducing activity.

articles.headline: Potent PfAPP inhibitors designed with improved selectivity
articles.title_original: Hydroxamate-based inhibitors reveal structural determinants of selectivity for Plasmodium falciparum aminopeptidase P.
articles.details: Structure-activity study of Plasmodium falciparum aminopeptidase P (PfAPP) inhibitors based on apstatin. Hydroxamic acid derivatives showed greatly improved potency: compound 6d (Ki = 685 nM versus apstatin Ki = 16 μM) and compound 6e (Ki = 24 nM). Crystal structure analysis revealed selectivity of 6e for PfAPP over human APP through P4' phenylalanine interaction with Tyr617.

articles.headline: Over half of febrile returning travelers remain undiagnosed
articles.title_original: High rate of undiagnosed cases of fever among returning travelers from tropical areas tested for malaria: a single-center retrospective study.
articles.details: Single-center retrospective study (Italy, 2018-2024) of 778 febrile returning travelers tested for malaria. Overall 57% (446/778) remained undiagnosed, though diagnostic capacity improved from 29.4% definite diagnoses in 2018 to 57.8% in 2024. Arboviral infections identified in 13.6%, with dengue (12.2%), malaria (9.3%), and pneumonia (5.4%) most common.

articles.headline: STANDARD G6PD Biosensor shows superior diagnostic performance
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of Point-of-Care Devices for Glucose-6-Phosphate Dehydrogenase Deficiency Detection Among Malaria Patients: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis (13 cross-sectional studies) evaluating point-of-care G6PD testing accuracy in malaria patients. At 30% activity threshold, CareStart showed sensitivity 0.82 (95% CI: 0.55-0.95) and specificity 0.96; at 70%, sensitivity dropped to 0.54. STANDARD G6PD Biosensor demonstrated superior performance with sensitivity 0.99 (95% CI: 0.96-1.00) and specificity 0.98 at 30% threshold.

articles.headline: Natural compound brazilein kills P. falciparum via iron disruption
articles.title_original: Brazilin and its oxidized form "brazilein", the natural fluorescently traceable agents, toxify Plasmodium falciparum to pyknotic death via disrupting its iron homeostasis.
articles.details: Study screened Caesalpinia sappan L. heartwood decoction and identified brazilin as active compound; its oxidized form brazilein coordinates Fe2+ ions. Through confocal imaging and multispectral flow cytometry, brazilein shown to bind labile Fe2+ pools, inhibit hemoglobin digestion, and block erythrocyte invasion via AMA1 binding, inducing pyknotic parasite death.

articles.headline: DENV-1 and DENV-3 circulating with malaria in Libreville
articles.title_original: Molecular evidence of co-circulation of different dengue virus serotypes associated with malaria co-infection among febrile children in Libreville, Gabon.
articles.details: Retrospective molecular study of 151 febrile children in Libreville, Gabon (Sept-Oct 2022). DENV RNA detected in 18.5%, with serotype identification confirming DENV-1 and DENV-3 circulation. Malaria prevalence 56.9%, and 9.3% had dengue-malaria co-infection. Children <5 years had higher dengue prevalence; those 5-10 years had higher odds of malaria and co-infection.

articles.headline: Computational study identifies tetrandrine targeting heme detoxification
articles.title_original: Atomistic simulations identify the Tetrandrine as potent anti-malarial drug candidate against Plasmodium falciparum targeting Heme Detoxification Protein (HDP).
articles.details: Computational drug discovery targeting Plasmodium falciparum heme detoxification protein (HDP) using AlphaFold modeling and docking. Among natural compounds screened, tetrandrine showed highest binding affinity (-13.0 kJ/mol) with stabilizing interactions at His122, His172, His175, and His197. Molecular dynamics simulations and MM-PBSA calculations confirmed thermodynamically favorable binding (ΔG = -30 kcal/mol).

articles.headline: P. vivax proteins detected in urine as diagnostic biomarkers
articles.title_original: Discovery of parasite proteins in the urine of vivax malaria patients through mass spectrometry.
articles.details: Mass spectrometry analysis of urine samples from Plasmodium vivax infected patients. Identified 252 human proteins and 28 P. vivax proteins in infected samples, including rifin-like protein, single-stranded DNA-binding protein, and profilin. Gene ontology analysis showed proteins involved in key biological pathways. Potential biomarkers for non-invasive malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Reconsidering biological identity of malaria-derived particles
articles.title_original: "What are we measuring?" reconsidering the biological identity and clinical interpretation of malaria-derived particles.
articles.details: No abstract available; appears to be a review or commentary article on malaria-derived particles.

articles.headline: Spatial repellent emanators reduce malaria 22.5% in Nigeria camps
articles.title_original: The effectiveness of long-lasting spatial repellent emanators against malaria in humanitarian crisis settings in northern Nigeria: a two-arm pragmatic, open-label, controlled trial.
articles.details: Pragmatic controlled trial in 24 internally displaced person camps in Maiduguri, Nigeria (June 2023-January 2024), enrolling 1,655 children aged 6-10 years. Spatial repellent emanators (12 camps, 50 households each) versus control (12 camps, no intervention). Post-intervention, emanators significantly reduced malaria incidence (IRR 0.713, 95% CI 0.584-0.869; p=0.001) with 22.5% protective efficacy (HR 0.775; p=0.011) and dramatically reduced pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles gambiae density (IRR 0.0231; p<0.0001).

articles.headline: Cyclic peptides inhibit oocyst rupture protein dimerization in vitro
articles.title_original: Targeting the histone-fold dimerization interface of oocyst rupture proteins from Plasmodium berghei for antimalarial inhibitor discovery.
articles.details: X-ray crystallographic analysis of Plasmodium berghei oocyst rupture protein (ORP) histone-fold domain heterodimer at 3.1 Å resolution revealed druggable dimerization interface. GAL4-based yeast two-hybrid screen of cyclic peptide library identified 6 candidates inhibiting ORP1-ORP2 dimerization. Molecular docking confirmed all candidates bind at the dimer interface, with ranking for in vivo efficacy testing.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry quantifies malaria-derived particles in blood
articles.title_original: A novel flow cytometry approach to quantify malaria-derived particles.
articles.details: Optimized flow cytometry method to enumerate malaria-derived particles (MDPs) in blood from P. falciparum and P. vivax infections. MDPs correlated strongly with infected RBCs (R² = 0.94 for P. falciparum, R² = 0.91 for P. vivax). Method is reliable and straightforward for routine diagnosis and treatment monitoring.

articles.headline: Multistage malaria vaccine shows efficacy in trial
articles.title_original: First efficacy evidence for a multistage malaria vaccine targeting infection and transmission.
articles.details: Title indicates first efficacy evidence for multistage malaria vaccine, but abstract not provided.

articles.headline: ProC6C vaccine achieves 76% efficacy in malaria challenge
articles.title_original: Efficacy of ProC6C-AlOH/Matrix-M against Plasmodium falciparum infection and mosquito transmission: a phase 2, randomised, controlled human malaria infection study.
articles.details: Phase 2 randomized CHMI study in 34 malaria-experienced adults (17 vaccine, 17 control). Three intramuscular injections of ProC6C-AlOH/Matrix-M (100 μg ProC6C, 50 μg Matrix-M adjuvant) versus rabies vaccine control. Vaccine recipients showed lower parasitemia incidence (6/16 vs 13/16), delayed time to infection (14 vs 12 days, p=0.049), and 76% vaccine efficacy (95% CI 36-91, p=0.0022) against CHMI at 12 weeks.
articles.notable_reason: First anti-PfCSP subunit vaccine achieving >50% CHMI efficacy

articles.headline: LSA3 critical for completing malaria parasite RBC invasion
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Liver Stage Antigen 3 plays a role in completing the final stages of merozoite invasion of red blood cells.
articles.details: Study identified LSA3, a merozoite dense granule protein, as playing a role in late-stage RBC invasion by P. falciparum. LSA3 knockout parasites showed 65% reduction in normal invasion completion and displayed abnormal accolé-form morphology. First evidence that a merozoite dense granule protein plays an important role in the late-invasion phase of erythrocytic cycle.

articles.headline: Computational study maps antimalarial target in parasite metabolism
articles.title_original: Computational characterization of a HotDog domain-containing hypothetical protein from the food vacuole of Plasmodium falciparum reveals potential allosteric regulation by antimalarials.
articles.details: Bioinformatics characterization of PfHDDCP, a hypothetical food vacuole protein with a HotDog fold domain. Structural modeling and molecular docking predicted two ligand-binding sites: Site 1 for Acetyl-CoA and Acyl Carrier Protein binding, and Site 2 (allosteric pocket) for antimalarial compounds. Molecular dynamics simulations showed stable complexes and ligand-induced conformational changes, but findings remain predictive and require experimental validation.

articles.headline: High malaria prevalence in Ethiopian highlands suggests local transmission
articles.title_original: High-Altitude Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Reservoirs in Ethiopia Are Not Linked to Recent Travel.
articles.details: Survey of 1241 subclinical and 1775 clinical individuals across altitudes <1900-2900 m in Ethiopia. P. falciparum prevalence decreased with altitude (67% at 1850 m to 16% at 2850 m), while P. vivax ranged 10-34%. Only 20.3% reported recent travel, which did not affect infection risk, suggesting sustained local transmission rather than imported cases.

articles.headline: Genomic study profiles malaria drug resistance in Zambian children
articles.title_original: National genomic profiling of Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance in Zambian children participating in the 2018 malaria indicator survey.
articles.details: Title indicates national genomic profiling of P. falciparum antimalarial resistance in Zambian children from 2018 malaria indicator survey, but abstract not provided.

articles.headline: Molecular characterization of malaria vector in rural Nigeria
articles.title_original: Molecular Characterization and transmission indices of malaria vector in a rural community in Osun State south-west Nigeria.
articles.details: Title indicates molecular characterization and transmission indices of malaria vectors in rural Osun State, Nigeria, but abstract not provided.

articles.headline: Ethiopia's test-and-treat guideline shows moderate fidelity during resurgence
articles.title_original: Implementation fidelity of Ethiopia's Malaria test-and-treat guideline amid a resurgence in Amhara Region: A mixed-methods study.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of 53 health facilities (38 public, 15 private) assessing implementation fidelity to Ethiopia's malaria test-and-treat guideline during resurgence. Mean implementation fidelity 64.3%, with only 40% achieving high fidelity (≥75%). Public facilities outperformed private (median 67% vs 63%). Higher fidelity associated with provider responsiveness (β=3.4, p<0.001) and stronger facilitation strategies (β=2.8, p<0.001).

articles.headline: Malaria vector ecology complex around dam in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Characteristics and dynamics of malaria vectors around the Soum dam in Nanoro Health District, Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Entomological survey near Soum dam collected 11,378 Anopheles mosquitoes over 12 months (March 2022-Feb 2023). An. gambiae s.l. (86.5%) predominated, with An. coluzzii comprising 91.5% of complex. Vector density highest near dam (49.7%) and decreased with distance, yet sporozoite rates higher in distant villages (9.5% vs 6.2% overall), indicating complex transmission dynamics.

articles.headline: Plasmodium parasite invasion mechanism revealed
articles.title_original: Plasmodium gets inside through the back door.
articles.details: Title suggests novel parasite invasion mechanism, but abstract not provided.

articles.headline: Long-latency P. vivax relapse in Korean Peninsula military
articles.title_original: To Break a Fever: Relapse of Long-Latency Plasmodium vivax Malaria After Service in the Korean Peninsula.
articles.details: Case series of 6 male military personnel (mean age 23 years) with P. vivax malaria relapse after deployment to Dagmar North training area in Korea in 2021. Cases presented with significant delay (average 57 days, range 8-121 days), uncomplicated disease with parasitemia <1%. Treated with artemether/lumefantrine plus primaquine with parasitemia clearance in average 2.8 days.

articles.headline: Plasmodium knowlesi adapts to invade Duffy-negative erythrocytes
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi can adapt to infect Duffy-negative erythrocytes.
articles.details: In vitro study showing P. knowlesi can bypass DARC requirement and invade Duffy-negative RBCs through a stable adaptation. Genomic analysis identified a chimeric dbpαγ gene essential for Fy- invasion. CRISPR-Cas9 reversion confirmed dbpαγ's necessity. This expanded invasion plasticity has implications for parasite spread beyond Southeast Asia and understanding of complex invasion mechanisms in related P. vivax.
articles.notable_reason: Novel adaptation mechanism enabling invasion of previously resistant host cells

articles.headline: Heme metabolism compartmentalization offers therapeutic intervention targets
articles.title_original: Russian Dolls of Heme Metabolism in Malaria-Infected Red Blood Cells: Nested Vulnerabilities and Therapeutic Opportunities.
articles.authors: Adapa SR, Siddiqui FA, Jiang RHY
articles.details: Review of heme metabolism in malaria-infected RBCs from multiple overlapping sources: host erythrocyte biosynthesis, parasite synthesis, and host hemoglobin digestion. Discusses how exogenous 5-ALA can increase protoporphyrin IX levels and sensitize parasites to oxidative stress, though effects on artemisinin susceptibility depend on parasite stage and exposure duration. Maps nested vulnerabilities for potential drug development.

articles.headline: Genetic complexity of infection correlates with transmission intensity
articles.title_original: Genetic metrics decode Plasmodium falciparum diversity: complexity of infection, parasite connectivity, and transmission intensity in Mainland Tanzania's diverse regions.
articles.details: Cross-sectional genomic surveillance of 7,199 P. falciparum samples from 100 health facilities across Tanzania. Mean complexity of infection (COI) was 1.5 countrywide (59.6% monoclonal). COI significantly higher in high and moderate transmission strata (p<0.001); FST values 0–0.006 showed low genetic differentiation. Only 1.8% of parasite pairs met identity-by-descent ≥50% threshold. Findings support using COI and polyclonality metrics for malaria surveillance.

articles.headline: Automated analyzer misclassifies Plasmodium vivax as artefactual
articles.title_original: Classification of Plasmodium vivax as artefactual cells by the CellaVision DM9600: a system limitation in a non-endemic area.
articles.details: Case report of a 46-year-old Taiwanese traveler from Indonesia presenting with fever and thrombocytopenia. CellaVision DM9600 automated cellular analyzer flagged artefactual cells without diagnosis; PCR confirmed P. vivax. Treated with artemether-lumefantrine and primaquine with rapid recovery and no relapse. Identifies system limitation in non-endemic settings.

articles.headline: Swiss malaria risk mapping methodology harmonizes travel prevention
articles.title_original: [Travel and malaria prevention : what lies behind the maps].
articles.details: Descriptive overview of a transparent methodology developed collaboratively by Switzerland and other European countries for classifying malaria risk areas based on epidemiological data. Harmonized preventive measures presented in detailed maps to support travel medicine experts and primary care physicians in providing evidence-based guidance. Maps available at www.healthytravel.ch.

articles.headline: One-third of Myanmar migrants show predicted recent P. vivax exposure
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and behavioral determinants of Plasmodium vivax-specific antibody responses among short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand.
articles.details: Cross-sectional sero-survey in March–April 2025 of 300 Myanmar migrants across six high-burden villages in Thailand (n=294 PCR-negative analyzed). 35.4% (104/294) predicted recently exposed via IgG to eight P. vivax antigens using random forest algorithm. Risk factors: age 30–39 (aOR=3.11), secondary education (aOR=3.28), Karen ethnicity (aOR=3.68). Protective: seasonal work (aOR=0.28), income >5,000 THB (aOR=0.17).

articles.headline: Malaria and anaemia prevalent among Ghanaian secondary school students
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic malaria parasite carriage and anaemia among senior high school students in Tempane District of the Upper East Region of Ghana: a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 290 students aged 15–22 years. Malaria parasite prevalence 23.1%, anaemia 49.0%. Day students had significantly higher malaria prevalence than boarders (59.2% vs 15.8%, p<0.001). Non-ITN users had higher prevalence (39.7% vs 5.0%, p<0.001). Malaria infection (aOR=9.36, p<0.001) and male sex (aOR=2.26) strongly associated with anaemia.

articles.headline: Schistosoma mansoni linked to persistent gut inflammation after treatment
articles.title_original: Schistosoma mansoni Infections Are Associated With Fecal Calprotectin Markers of Gut Inflammation After Accounting for HIV, Hepatitis B, and Malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 640 participants (age 6–85 years) in Uganda. S. mansoni prevalence 49.1%; 74.4% had detectable fecal calprotectin, 22.3% ≥100 µg/g, 7% >250 µg/g. Infection intensity positively associated with all fCal outcomes (detectable: OR 1.20; ≥100 µg/g: OR 1.11; >250 µg/g: OR 1.26). HIV associated with elevated fCal ≥100 (OR 2.52); malaria uninformative.

articles.headline: Outcrossing confers fitness advantage over selfing in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Genetic crosses using fluorescently labelled malaria parasites indicate advantages of outcrossing in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Experimental cross of fluorescently labeled parasite clones (NF54 mCherry and NHP4026 GFP) in mosquitoes; 8,540 oocysts genotyped from 435 mosquitoes at 7–14 days post-infection. Outcrossed oocysts showed faster maturation and increased relative frequency over time compared to selfed, indicating substantial fitness advantage. Likely due to removal of deleterious mutations accumulated during asexual replication.
articles.notable_reason: First demonstration of major fitness advantage of outcrossing in malaria parasites

articles.headline: CD4 T-cell responses largely similar between P. falciparum and vivax
articles.title_original: Comparison of CD4 T-Cell Response in Plasmodium falciparum and vivax Malaria.
articles.details: Comparative flow cytometry study of CD4 T-cell activation, proliferation, and intracellular cytokine production in adults with P. falciparum (n=not stated) or P. vivax malaria. Phenotype, activation, and proliferation of CD4 subsets largely comparable between species. Within peripheral T follicular helper (pTfh) compartment, modest species-dependent differences: increased pTfh1 cells and IL-10 production in P. falciparum.

articles.headline: FcγRIIa is primary receptor for antibody-dependent neutrophil phagocytosis
articles.title_original: Antibody-Dependent Neutrophil Phagocytosis of Plasmodium falciparum-Infected Erythrocytes Is Mediated by FcγRIIa.
articles.details: Experimental study of purified neutrophils treated with TNF and PIPLC to selectively remove FcγRIIIb and enhance FcγRIIa surface expression. When FcγRIIIb was depleted (79% reduction), ADNP increased 585% (p=0.042). FcγRIIa blocking reduced ADNP >5-fold (~17%, p<0.05); combined blocking reduced to ~24% (p<0.05). Demonstrates FcγRIIa mediates primary phagocytic activity.

articles.headline: Travel importation drives 14–30% of malaria cases in low-transmission
articles.title_original: Quantifying the Role of Importation on Sustained Malaria Transmission in a Low-to-Moderate Burden Region of Southwest Uganda.
articles.details: 14-month longitudinal cohort of 1,918 individuals from 400 households in Southwest Uganda. 283 infections recorded (244 symptomatic, 39 asymptomatic). Travel associated with infection in low-transmission villages (OR=4.38, 95% CI 1.80–10.64) and during low transmission periods. Population attributable fractions: travel accounted for 14% of malaria cases overall, rising to 30% during low transmission seasons.

articles.headline: Dobinin K kills resistant malaria parasites by disrupting redox system
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Effects of Dobinin K against Chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium falciparum Through Disruption of the Parasite's Redox System.
articles.details: In vitro study testing Dobinin K against chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum Dd2 strain using fluorescence, microscopy, and transcriptomic analysis. Dobinin K showed concentration- and time-dependent activity against all parasite stages by disrupting redox homeostasis: increased oxidative products, elevated GSSG/GSH ratio, inhibited antioxidant enzymes (PfGR, PfTrxR, PfSOD), disrupted mitochondrial membrane potential, and triggered apoptosis. Authors propose redox system targeting as effective strategy against resistant parasites.

articles.headline: LAMP diagnostic shows comparable sensitivity to PCR for malaria in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Assessment of automated loop-mediated isothermal amplification-(LAMP-)based xenomonitoring for Plasmodium spp. in Anopheles mosquitoes.
articles.details: Automated LAMP assay, originally designed for human blood, was evaluated for xenomonitoring of Plasmodium in Anopheles mosquitoes compared with real-time PCR. For artificially exposed P. berghei, LAMP detected 34.9% (15/43) vs PCR 51.2% (22/43). For field-collected mosquitoes, LAMP and PCR showed comparable sensitivity: 7.0% (12/172 pools) and 7.6% (13/172 pools) respectively, with overall infection rate 1.2% (20/1711). LAMP suitable for tropical field conditions.

articles.headline: Correction to published study on immunity and virulence gene expression
articles.title_original: Correction: Controlled human malaria infection with Plasmodium falciparum demonstrates impact of naturally acquired immunity on virulence gene expression.
articles.details: This article is a correction to a previously published study (DOI: 10.1371/journal.ppat.1007906) on controlled human malaria infection with P. falciparum and naturally acquired immunity.

articles.headline: Wastewater surveillance feasible for malaria detection in endemic African countries
articles.title_original: Modeling and Laboratory Experiments to Assess Detection of Plasmodium falciparum in Wastewater: Insights for Wastewater-Based Surveillance of Malaria.
articles.details: Modeling and spike-in experiments assessed P. falciparum detection in wastewater. Shedding estimated at 5.31 log10 genome copies/day per infection. Non-endemic countries would need >1000-fold case increase for 50% detection probability, but ~84% of endemic African countries could achieve this under identical conditions with PLOD 3.0. Freeze-thawing caused >70% DNA loss, recovery <2% in most cases. Lower PLODs remain achievable.

articles.headline: Marker-free CRISPR gene editing enables flexible genetic engineering in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: A heterologous marker-free selection approach for CRISPR/Cas9-based gene editing in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Developed CRISPR/Cas9pyrR strategy for P. falciparum that avoids heterologous drug resistance markers by using pyrimethamine resistance mutations in pfdhfr-ts for selection. Generated GFP-tagged transgenic lines showing PfGEX1 localizes to nuclear envelope in gametocytes and PfNUP116 to perinuclear region in early gametocytes. Successfully performed sequential genetic engineering of multiple genes. Enhances genetic toolkit for malaria parasite research.

articles.headline: Iron supplements reduce malaria-induced anemia but not growth in children
articles.title_original: Effect of Iron-Containing Micronutrient Powders on Growth and Malaria-Induced Anaemia Among Preschool Children in Ghana: A Cluster-Randomized, Double-Blind, Placebo-Controlled Trial.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized trial of 1,806 children aged 6-35 months in Ghana received 12.5mg elemental iron or placebo daily for 5 months. Iron-containing micronutrient powder had no significant effect on height-for-age (p=0.609), weight-for-age (p=0.719), or weight-for-height (p=0.463) z-scores. Intervention reduced risk of malaria-induced anemia (risk difference 0.050, 95% CI 0.004-0.096; p=0.032). Safe in malaria-endemic settings but additional strategies needed for growth.

articles.headline: Novel antimalarial compounds block parasite invasion through sterol pathway disruption
articles.title_original: Unpacking boxes: identification of novel inhibitors of malaria parasite invasion.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Screened 400 compounds and identified 20 invasion inhibitors and 2 partial egress inhibitors. Six compounds showed similar growth and invasion IC50s, suggesting invasion inhibition is central to mechanism. Two compounds (MMV006931 and MMV024850) directly block P. falciparum merozoite invasion. Resistance selection and genome sequencing identified mutations in PfNCR1 (sterol exporter), confirmed through sterol-dependent detergent lysis assay.

articles.headline: Malaria, infections, poor adherence drive anemia in HIV-positive African children
articles.title_original: Anemia in HIV-Infected Children on ART in Uganda: High Risk Associations with Malaria, Opportunistic Infections, and Poor Adherence.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 293 HIV-positive children <18 years in Uganda found anemia prevalence 6.14% (95% CI 3.4%-8.9%), with normocytic normochromic type predominant (72.2%). Multivariate analysis showed recent malaria (AOR 9.61, 95% CI 3.26-28.34), opportunistic infection (AOR 6.78, 95% CI 1.28-35.83), and poor ART adherence (AOR 14.75, 95% CI 1.31-165.65) independently associated with anemia.

articles.headline: Humidity drives seasonal malaria transmission in Rohingya refugee camps
articles.title_original: Seasonal Climate Drivers and Immunohematological Profiles of Malaria in Rohingya Refugee Camps, Bangladesh: A 4-Year Repeated Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Four-year cross-sectional study (2021-2024) of 582 individuals in Bangladesh refugee camps identified 345 malaria cases. Peak transmission during monsoon (June-September), especially August. Relative humidity showed strongest correlation with incidence (r=0.724-0.77), followed by rainfall and temperature. P. falciparum peaked earlier than P. vivax. Infected participants showed anemia, thrombocytopenia, and elevated ESR, more pronounced in P. falciparum cases.

articles.headline: Spatial disparities in malaria pregnancy linked to antenatal care access gaps
articles.title_original: Mapping inequity: spatial clustering of malaria in pregnancy in the Amhara region, Northwest Ethiopia (2018-2024).
articles.details: Retrospective spatial analysis of 5.8 million pregnant women in Ethiopia (2018-2024) identified 53,478 malaria cases with annual incidence 9.2/1,000 population. Seven high-risk clusters detected via SaTScan. Spatial regression showed malaria negatively associated with ANC4 completion (IRR 0.92, 95% CI 0.91-0.94), soil moisture (IRR 0.85, 95% CI 0.80-0.90), and temperature (IRR 0.65, 95% CI 0.58-0.73), but positively with ANC1 utilization (IRR 1.07, 95% CI 1.06-1.08).

articles.headline: Malaria-bacteria co-infection affects 10% of febrile children in Ghana
articles.title_original: Malaria and bacteremia in febrile children presenting at a teaching hospital in Kumasi, Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 270 febrile children identified malaria in 33%, bacteremia in 31%, and co-infection in 10.4% (±3.6%, 95% CI). Seizures independently associated with co-infection (aOR 3.57, 95% CI 1.28-9.95; p=0.015). Staphylococcus aureus (43.8%) and Pseudomonas aeruginosa (6.3%) were most common bacterial isolates, with 34.5% of bacteria showing multidrug resistance. Authors highlight need for antibiotic stewardship programs.

articles.headline: Vaccines and emerging tools advance malaria prevention strategies globally
articles.title_original: Malaria Prevention: Progress to Date.
articles.details: Review of malaria prevention approaches including chemoprophylaxis, vector control, and vaccination. RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M vaccines now rolled out in many sub-Saharan African countries, providing moderate protection in young children while significantly reducing severe disease and death. Monoclonal antibodies in development as preventive treatment strategy. Authors note dynamic evolution with numerous novel developments on horizon for travelers and endemic populations.

articles.headline: Plasmodium oocyst opening requires trimeric NF-Y-like protein complex
articles.title_original: Sporozoite egress from Plasmodium oocysts requires a trimeric NF-Y-like complex.
articles.details: The study identified ORP3 as the third subunit of the Oocyst Rupture Protein (ORP) complex, which facilitates sporozoite release from oocyst capsules in the mosquito. ORP3 contains a conserved helix (A1) similar to the NF-Y transcription factor. Deletion of ORP3 or its helix A1 prevented oocyst excystation, blocking parasite transmission by disrupting exoerythrocytic form development.

articles.headline: Antibody Fc region not required for malaria sporozoite protection
articles.title_original: Relevance of the antibody Fc fragment and epitope valency in protection against malaria sporozoites.
articles.details: This study examined whether the Fc region of antibodies against circumsporozoite protein (CSP) was necessary for protection against P. falciparum sporozoites using Fab fragments and Fc-mutated antibodies. Fab fragments showed comparable protective efficacy to intact antibodies, and binding to a single (NPNA)3 epitope was sufficient to reduce liver burden, while two separate epitopes achieved sterile protection.

articles.headline: Anopheles maculatus confirmed as malaria vector in India after 80+ years
articles.title_original: Anopheles maculatus s.l as potential malaria vectors with opportunistic feeding in Meghalaya, Northeast India.
articles.details: Molecular identification of 215 mosquitoes collected in Meghalaya (2019-2023) identified 177 An. maculatus s.s., 33 An. pseudowillmori, and 5 An. willmori. P. falciparum infection was detected in 27.7% of An. maculatus s.s. (49/177) and 48.5% of An. pseudowillmori (16/33). Blood meal analysis showed opportunistic feeding: 15% human, 16% cattle, 17% mixed, with genetic differentiation between regional populations.
articles.notable_reason: First confirmed malaria transmission by Maculatus Group in 80 years

articles.headline: Junctional-epitope vaccine shows promise alone or with R21
articles.title_original: Protective immunity against malaria by a nanoparticle CIS43-based junctional vaccine alone or in combination with R21.
articles.details: A nanoparticle-based vaccine targeting the junctional region of P. falciparum CSP was compared with R21 vaccine through B cell analysis and monoclonal antibody studies. At 1 μg, comparable reduction in liver burden was observed between the junctional vaccine and R21. At 0.25 μg, the junctional vaccine showed modest reduction compared to R21, and combination with R21 did not yield substantial improvement.

articles.headline: Chikungunya virus circulation detected in Nigeria with malaria co-infection
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of chikungunya virus infection and co-occurrence with malaria among patients with febrile illness in Oguma, the middle belt region of Nigeria.
articles.details: Serological screening of 200 febrile patients in Oguma, Nigeria detected anti-chikungunya antibodies in 9 patients (4.5%): 2% IgM and 2.5% IgG. Co-infection with malaria occurred in 1.5% of seropositive patients, while 30% of all patients had malaria alone. Active chikungunya infection occurred only in males and was associated with rash and back pain (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Mass drug administration strategy needs vaccines, monitoring, cooperation
articles.title_original: Application Strategy and Research Progress of Large-Scale Population Drug Intervention in Malaria Control.
articles.details: This review examines mass drug administration (MDA) as a malaria elimination strategy, noting approximately 282 million cases globally in 2024. MDA has core limitations including drug resistance risks, insufficient implementation sustainability, and poor regional adaptation rates. The authors propose an "MDA+" collaborative strategy integrating vaccines, digital monitoring, and cross-border cooperation to improve coverage and advance elimination.
