articles.headline: Pregnancy malaria chemoprevention trade-offs: efficacy versus infant growth
articles.title_original: Effect mechanisms of different malaria chemoprevention regimens in pregnancy on infant growth outcomes: causal mediation analysis of a randomized controlled trial.
articles.details: This RCT (n=761 infants) compared intermittent preventive treatment regimens in pregnancy: IPTp-DP, SP, and DP+SP. IPTp-DP+SP versus SP reduced weight-for-length z-scores by 0.18-0.28 between 1-6 months (p<0.05), particularly in first-time mothers. However, prevention of placental malaria by DP+SP partially offset growth faltering (+0.06, 95% CI 0.02-0.10), with low birthweight persistently mediating effects on length-for-age through 13 months.

articles.headline: PBO-treated nets show reduced efficacy against Kenya malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Reduced Efficacy of Piperonyl Butoxide Based Long-Lasting Insecticide-Treated Nets Against Malaria Vectors in Western Kenya.
articles.details: WHO cone bioassays tested 2,020 Anopheles mosquitoes from western Kenya against new and 24-month-old piperonyl butoxide-treated LLINs. New PBO-LLINs achieved only 68% mortality in wild-collected mosquitoes and 34% mortality in used nets. Anopheles arabiensis was the main surviving species, with very high pyrethroid resistance levels in the local population attributed to both net aging and vector resistance.
articles.notable_reason: PBO-LLIN resistance threatens core vector control intervention in Kenya

articles.headline: L9LS monoclonal antibody shows 43% protective efficacy in Kenyan children
articles.title_original: Safety and efficacy of the monoclonal antibody L9LS for malaria prevention in children exposed to perennial malaria transmission in Kenya: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.
articles.details: This phase 2 RCT tested L9LS monoclonal antibody in 324 Kenyan children aged 5-59 months in a perennial P. falciparum transmission setting. Two-dose L9LS recipients had 66% infection rate versus 83% in placebo (protective efficacy 42.7%, 95% CI 22.5-57.7, p=0.0003). Adverse events were mild, with grade 3 or worse events in only 1% of injections in both L9LS and placebo groups, with no serious adverse events.
articles.notable_reason: First monoclonal antibody efficacy demonstrated in young children with perennial transmission

articles.headline: Antibody-based malaria prevention in intense transmission settings
articles.title_original: Antibody-based malaria prevention in an intense perennial transmission setting.
articles.details: No abstract provided; article title suggests discussion of antibody-based prevention approaches in high-transmission contexts.

articles.headline: RTS,S/AS01 vaccine safety monitoring methods compared across approaches
articles.title_original: Assessing safety in the RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine implementation programme: an overview and comparison of methods.
articles.details: This overview describes safety monitoring approaches used in the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine implementation programme, including sentinel hospital and community-based mortality surveillance, strengthened pharmacovigilance to identify rare adverse signals, and cohort event monitoring for conditions of special interest. The article summarizes the complementary strengths and weaknesses of these surveillance methods.

articles.headline: Malaria research must balance current lives with future readiness
articles.title_original: Prioritising in a crisis: what next for malaria research?
articles.details: This Perspective discusses the crisis in global malaria control, with earlier gains stalling and funding shortfalls amid rising resistance, climate-driven transmission changes, and conflict-displacement. The authors propose a dual mandate: protecting lives now through implementation research optimizing existing interventions, while sustaining innovation in chemoprevention, vaccines, monoclonal antibodies, vector control, and digital surveillance for emerging threats.

articles.headline: R21/Matrix-M vaccine awareness low but acceptability high in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Awareness, acceptability and determinants of the new malaria vaccine among caregivers of under-five children in Asaba, Nigeria: a multi-centre cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 358 caregivers of under-5 children in Asaba, Nigeria (Jan-Jun 2025). Only 36.3% were aware of R21/Matrix-M vaccine, but 85.8% were willing to vaccinate their children. Fear of adverse reactions (45.1%), lack of interest (19.6%), and spousal disagreement (13.7%) were main barriers. Female caregivers and civil servants showed significantly higher acceptance (p=0.006 and p=0.018).

articles.headline: Global malaria control experts discuss challenges and opportunities
articles.title_original: Unite against Malaria: challenges and achievements in malaria control around the globe.
articles.details: Opinion/commentary article featuring three regional experts (Dionicia Gamboa from Peru, Fitsum Girma Tadesse from Ethiopia, Leanne Robinson from Asia-Pacific) sharing their experiences toward WHO's 2016-2030 goal of reducing malaria incidence and mortality by 90%.

articles.headline: Artemisinin resistance involves Kelch13 mutations and additional genetic pathways
articles.title_original: Insights into the factors contributing to artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Narrative review of molecular mechanisms underlying artemisinin resistance in P. falciparum. While Kelch13 mutations (particularly in propeller domain) are linked to resistance, in vitro and field studies indicate additional genetic determinants and pathways contribute: reduced hemoglobin endocytosis, increased phosphatidylinositol-3-kinase levels, unfolded protein response activation, and polymorphisms in various genes.

articles.headline: Structural biology and AI advance rational blood-stage malaria vaccine design
articles.title_original: Atomic clarity: how structural biology is shaping blood-stage malaria vaccines.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Narrative article discussing how cryogenic electron microscopy (cryoEM) and artificial intelligence enable atomic-level analysis of parasite multiprotein complexes without crystallization, revealing protein trafficking, nutrient uptake, and host cell remodeling. This integrated approach combined with genomics is accelerating progress toward broadly protective malaria vaccines.

articles.headline: PBO nets superior to standard LLINs in adaptive malaria vector control trial
articles.title_original: Adaptive intervention strategies for malaria vector control in western Kenya: a cluster, sequential, multiple-assignment, randomised, open-label trial.
articles.details: Large two-stage cluster-RCT of 47,614 participants in 84 clusters in Kenya (2021-2024). Stage 1: PBO nets significantly reduced malaria incidence vs standard LLINs (aIRR 0.75, 95% CI 0.69-0.81, p<0.0001 at 1-6 months; aIRR 0.87, p=0.0012 at 7-12 months). Stage 2 non-responders: PBO nets + annual IRS superior to PBO nets + larviciding (aIRR 0.89, p=0.0081). Enhanced IRS superior to biannual IRS (aIRR 0.67, p<0.0001).
articles.notable_reason: Large RCT; PBO nets superior to standard LLINs; guideline-changing

articles.headline: Tinospora crispa extract shows synergy with artesunate in mice
articles.title_original: Antimalarial Potency of Tinospora crispa Stem Extract and Artesunate Against Plasmodium berghei: A Study on Combination Therapy.
articles.details: Preclinical study in ICR mice with P. berghei ANKA infection. Tinospora crispa extract (TCE) and artesunate (ART) both showed dose-dependent activity (ED50: TCE 1.96 mg/kg, ART 200.31 mg/kg). Combination exhibited synergistic effects (combination index <1 at ED50, ED50/2, ED50/4) with higher parasitemia inhibition and enhanced protection against packed cell volume reduction and weight loss compared to monotherapy.

articles.headline: Malaria treatment guidelines inadequate for infants under six months
articles.title_original: Malaria in children aged <6 months: a narrative review of current evidence, recommendations and practice gaps.
articles.details: Narrative review of malaria in children aged <6 months or weighing <5 kg. Although clinical malaria is rare in this age group, diagnosis is challenging due to symptom overlap with other conditions. Evidence on antimalarial safety/efficacy is limited, and until recently no agents were licensed for neonates or infants <4.5 kg. Guidelines are highly variable across countries, creating practice gaps for this vulnerable population.

articles.headline: Uganda malaria hotspots concentrated in north and east; high incidence continues
articles.title_original: Hotspot Mapping, Spatiotemporal Clustering and Forecasting of Malaria Incidence in Uganda, 2014-2023: Strategies to Inform Targeted Public Health Interventions.
articles.details: Ecological study using district-level surveillance data (2014-2023) in Uganda. Hotspot analysis identified sporadic hotspots (West Nile), oscillating hotspots (Karamoja), and consecutive hotspots (Lango-Busoga corridor). ARIMA(2,1,1) modeling projected monthly cases to remain 1.0-1.2 million through 2030 with seasonal peaks in April-June. Findings support intensified, geographically targeted control in high-burden districts.

articles.headline: Systematic review of malaria rapid diagnostic test accuracy
articles.title_original: Are rapid diagnostic tests reliable for the detection of malaria in the emergency department?
articles.details: Systematic review assessing diagnostic accuracy of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in febrile adults from endemic regions. Database search identified 138 papers; 6 met inclusion criteria for analysis.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria associates with altered gut microbiota composition
articles.title_original: Gut microbiome alterations among Ghanaian children with asymptomatic malaria infections.
articles.details: Study of gut microbiota in Ghanaian children with asymptomatic P. falciparum/P. malariae infections (primarily mixed). Asymptomatic malaria associated with decreased Firmicutes (52.5% to 44.0%) and increased Bacteroidetes (34.7% to 45.6%). Faecalibacterium significantly declined in infected vs healthy controls. Beta-diversity analysis showed malaria status explained 19.1% of microbial composition variation (PERMANOVA, p<0.05).

articles.headline: Tfh2 cells drive anti-PvDBPII antibody responses in vivax malaria
articles.title_original: A functional crosstalk between circulating follicular helper 2 T cells and memory B cells drives anti-Plasmodium vivax antibodies.
articles.details: Immunological study of 40 P. vivax-infected subjects examining T follicular helper 2 (cTfh2) and memory B cell interactions. Of 23 seropositive subjects, High Responders showed higher ICOS expression on cTfh2 cells, and 3 produced inhibitory anti-PvDBPII antibodies. PvDBPII antigen specifically stimulated cTfh2 activation. In co-culture, activated cTfh2 cells promoted memory B cell differentiation into antibody-secreting cells producing anti-PvDBPII IgG.

articles.headline: Postpartum antimalarial treatment doesn't reduce infant malaria in PNG
articles.title_original: Malaria in Papua New Guinean infants: a prespecified analysis of a randomised postpartum antimalarial treatment trial.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: RCT in PNG (Madang Province) of 183 mothers randomized to no treatment or postpartum artemisinin combination therapy (artemether-lumefantrine or dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine), with infant monitoring for 6 months. Malaria incidence was low: 2 slide-positive cases (1 P. falciparum, 1 P. vivax; 1.3%, 95% CI 0.2-5.2) and 3 PCR-detected (overall 3.3%, 95% CI 1.2-8.0). Four of five cases in infants of untreated mothers; no statistically significant between-group differences.

articles.headline: Combined livestock insecticide and nets for malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Integrating zooprophylaxis, insecticide-treated livestock and mosquito nets for malaria control: a One Health proposal.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Review proposing integrated approach combining insecticide-treated livestock (ITL), zooprophylaxis, and insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) for malaria control. ITL significantly reduces mosquito survival while preserving livestock hosts to divert mosquitoes from humans. This integrated strategy targets vector control for species that feed outdoors and prefer animals.

articles.headline: Malaria shaped prehistoric human habitat choice in Africa
articles.title_original: Malaria shaped human spatial organization for the past 74 thousand years.
articles.details: Using species distribution models and palaeoclimatic data, researchers estimated P. falciparum malaria transmission risk in sub-Saharan Africa from 74,000-5,000 years ago and correlated it with reconstructed human habitat use. Results show humans avoided or were unsuccessful in regions with high malaria transmission risk, demonstrating that disease shaped past human spatial organization alongside climate.

articles.headline: Chinese health standard for Plasmodium RDT interpretation
articles.title_original: [Interpretation of a health industry standard Detection of Plasmodium spp. Immune-chromatographic test (WS/T 10029-2025)].
articles.authors: Xu S, Zhu G, Cao J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Interpretation of China's new health industry standard (WS/T 10029-2025) for detecting Plasmodium antigens using immunochromatographic rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs). Standard addresses testing principles, sample collection, detection procedures, and result interpretation to standardize malaria diagnosis across medical and disease control institutions.

articles.headline: Placental malaria vaccine cost-effective for first pregnancies
articles.title_original: Modelling the Cost-Effectiveness of a Placental Malaria Vaccine in Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Decision-analytic model evaluated a placental malaria vaccine administered before pregnancy, assuming 40% efficacy, 30% uptake, and $5 dose price. For primigravidae, incremental cost-effectiveness ratio was $321 per perinatal DALY averted (baseline) and $225 in best-case scenarios, well below the $1556 cost-effectiveness threshold. Sensitivity analyses showed robust cost-effectiveness for primigravidae but high sensitivity to costs and vaccine efficacy for multigravidae.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria diagnostic and therapeutic challenges in Africa
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria in resource-limited African regions: Diagnostic and therapeutic challenges.
articles.details: Review of cerebral malaria (CM) management in resource-limited African settings. CM remains highly severe with 15%-30% mortality and 10%-30% of survivors experiencing long-term neurological sequelae. Major challenges include widespread misdiagnosis (up to 23% of clinically diagnosed CM cases fail strict diagnostic criteria), delayed presentation, inadequate supportive care infrastructure, and limited access to neuroimaging and specialized neurological rehabilitation.

articles.headline: LLIN ownership high but utilization below threshold in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Assessment of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Net (LLIN) Ownership, Utilization, and Associated Barriers in Malaria-Endemic Communities of Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 9,222 households across 11 Ethiopian regions (October 2024–January 2025) found 71.5% LLIN ownership but only 59.9% utilization overall (72.7% rural, lower in urban areas). Pregnant women and children <5 had higher utilization (78.5% and 67.2% respectively). Major barriers included 60.5% lacking confidence in net hanging and 64.1% believing malaria risk restricted to rainy season. Overall utilization remains below 80% threshold required for community-wide protection.

articles.headline: Atypical Guillain-Barré Syndrome variant after childhood malaria
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome After Malaria: A Case Report of a 7-Year-Old Child With Asymmetric Onset of Acute Motor Axonal Neuropathy.
articles.details: Case report of a 7-year-old child with acute motor axonal neuropathy presenting with asymmetric, relapsing weakness following malaria infection. The case highlights that atypical Guillain-Barré variants can present with uncommon features in malaria-endemic settings and emphasizes importance of early recognition for improved outcomes despite diagnostic and systemic constraints.

articles.headline: Severe childhood malaria associated with long-term cognitive deficits
articles.title_original: Malaria may cause long-term brain deficits, study shows.
articles.details: Study of Ugandan children who survived severe malaria found they performed worse on cognitive tests many years later. Abstract provides limited methodological details but demonstrates persistent neurological consequences of severe childhood malaria infection.

articles.headline: Malaria exposure not associated with COVID-19 seropositivity
articles.title_original: Life Course Malaria Exposure and SARS-CoV-2 Seroepidemiology in Ugandan Adolescents: A Longitudinal Study Nested in a Birth Cohort.
articles.details: Longitudinal study of 476 Ugandan adolescents (mean age 16.4 years) from Entebbe birth cohort examined whether life-course malaria exposure affected SARS-CoV-2 infection. 61.3% had prior clinical malaria; SARS-CoV-2 seropositivity increased from 63% at baseline to 94.9% at 12 months. Multivariable analysis showed no consistent association between malaria exposure (clinical history, asymptomatic parasitaemia, or antibody responses) and SARS-CoV-2 seropositivity or antibody levels.

articles.headline: Abbott malaria RDT cross-reacts with P. malariae and P. ovale
articles.title_original: Strong Cross-Reactivity of Anti-Plasmodium falciparum pLDH Monoclonal Antibodies With Plasmodium malariae pLDH in the Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.f/P.v Rapid Diagnostic Test.
articles.details: Testing of Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/P.f/P.v RDT with blood samples (10 P. falciparum, 10 P. vivax, 15 P. malariae, 5 P. ovale) revealed significant cross-reactivity. While accurately detecting P. falciparum and P. vivax, 85.7% (12/14) of P. malariae and 80% (4/5) of P. ovale samples yielded positive results on the Pf-specific T2 line, with anti-Pf-pLDH specificity against P. malariae of only 14.29%. This cross-reactivity could lead to species misidentification.

articles.headline: Selective proteasome inhibitors for P. falciparum drug development
articles.title_original: An Optimized Route to the Syringolin Natural Products Enables Combinatorial Synthesis of Selective, Bioactive Inhibitors of the Plasmodium falciparum 20S Proteasome.
articles.details: Researchers developed a new synthetic route to syringolin-based inhibitors of the P. falciparum 20S proteasome and generated a compound library with nanomolar antiparasitic activity. Compounds showed selectivity for the P. falciparum proteasome over the human proteasome. Cryo-EM structures revealed key contacts favoring species-selective inhibition, establishing a structural framework for proteasome-targeting antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: Metal complexes enhance mefloquine antiparasitic activity selectively
articles.title_original: Examination of Coligands in Mefloquine-Metal Complexes Reveals the Structural Determinants of Activity against Plasmodium falciparum and Schistosoma mansoni.
articles.details: Synthesis of mefloquine-metal complexes [M(II)(L)(MQ)]PF6 with platinum or palladium centers and various coligands yielded MQ-Pt complexes with high in vivo efficacy against P. falciparum and S. mansoni. The metal center augmented mefloquine's antiparasitic activity by targeting thioredoxin/glutathione reductase while the coligand modulated chemical reactivity. Metal complexes selectively accumulated in parasite cells while sparing mammalian cells.

articles.headline: Malaria surveillance in sub-Saharan African pharmacies: protocol for scoping review
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers to malaria surveillance in community pharmacies and over-the-counter medicine sellers in sub-Saharan Africa's health sector: a scoping review protocol.
articles.details: This scoping review protocol aims to systematically examine facilitators and barriers to malaria surveillance in community pharmacies and over-the-counter medicine sellers across sub-Saharan Africa. The review will search PubMed, CINAHL, Scopus, Web of Science and Hinari through June 15, 2026, and synthesize findings narratively to identify gaps in capturing private-sector malaria cases in routine surveillance systems.

articles.headline: Malaria in East and South Asia: historical and archaeological evidence
articles.title_original: Historical texts and archaeological evidence for malaria and its interactions with humans from East to South Asia.
articles.authors: Li G, Yuan X, Wang T
articles.details: This review synthesizes archaeological, biological, and ecological evidence on the human-malaria interaction in East, Southeast, and South Asia over evolutionary timescales. Materials examined include genetic analysis of Plasmodium species, ancient Chinese historical and medical documents, and skeletal markers from osteoarchaeological data. The review explores how malaria evolution influenced human demographic shifts and societal resilience, and examines how human activities like rice cultivation inadvertently created transmission environments.

articles.headline: Oocyst stage of malaria parasite: biology, unknowns, and transmission opportunities
articles.title_original: Oocyst: knowns and unknowns about the lengthiest life stage of the malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Bailey AJ, Vlachou D, Christophides GK
articles.details: This review synthesizes current knowledge on the Plasmodium oocyst, the longest parasite life stage formed in mosquitoes following blood ingestion. The oocyst undergoes prolonged development on the mosquito midgut basal lamina, ultimately producing thousands of sporozoites capable of infecting humans. Major gaps remain in understanding oocyst molecular and cellular processes, including transcriptional and epigenetic regulation, nutrient acquisition, metabolic remodeling, cell cycle control, and interactions with mosquito immunity. The review highlights how deeper insight into oocyst biology could inform transmission-blocking strategies and malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Plasmodium knowlesi blood-stage human model successfully developed with dose escalation
articles.title_original: Development of an experimental human blood-stage model for studying Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.details: Researchers developed a GMP-grade P. knowlesi induced blood-stage malaria (IBSM) model using the YH1 strain adapted to human serum. Four adults received intravenous inoculations of P. knowlesi-infected erythrocytes with dose escalation (2,800 to 280,000 parasites). Two participants developed detectable parasitemia; one at ~28,000 parasites showed ~6 parasites/mL at day 21, and one at ~280,000 parasites developed patent infection peaking at 202 parasites/mL with parasite multiplication rate (PMR32) of 2.33 (95% CI 1.66-3.25). All received curative artemether-lumefantrine treatment with mostly mild adverse events.

articles.headline: China-Africa cooperation on malaria control during Year of People-to-People Exchanges
articles.title_original: [Practice and prospect of China's participation in international cooperation for malaria control from the perspective of the "China-Africa Year of People-to-People Exchanges"].
articles.authors: Liang C, Zhu G, Cao J
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: This perspective article discusses China's participation in international malaria control cooperation in the context of the 2026 China-Africa Year of People-to-People Exchanges. It presents the strategic significance and practical approaches for China's involvement in malaria control, discusses policy recommendations for optimizing cooperation models between China and African countries, and aims to provide insights for accelerating progress toward global malaria elimination and building a China-Africa health community.

articles.headline: Magneto-optical microscopy for quantitative hemozoin imaging in malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Magneto-optical microscopy platform for quantitative imaging of hemozoin in blood for malaria diagnosis.
articles.authors: Kinyua DM, Kotar J, Cicuta P
articles.details: Researchers developed a magneto-optical (MO) imaging approach integrated into conventional polarizing microscopes for label-free, quantitative malaria detection via hemozoin crystals. A magnetic field aligns paramagnetic hemozoin, producing measurable changes in polarization contrast that correlate quantitatively with hemozoin concentration and parasite stage. Ratiometric intensity analysis and segmentation provide objective diagnostic parameters. By embedding MO into the familiar microscopy framework already established in malaria-endemic regions, the method offers a practical pathway for improving malaria diagnosis without requiring expert interpretation.

articles.headline: MKT1 protein is dispensable for Plasmodium falciparum asexual and sexual stages
articles.title_original: An MKT1 domain protein is dispensable for erythrocytic stages of plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Chakraborty B, McGee SM, Kumar S
articles.details: Researchers generated P. falciparum mutants with MKT1 gene deletion (Pfmkt1¯) to examine the role of MKT1, an MKT1 domain protein involved in posttranscriptional gene regulation and cellular differentiation in protozoans. Pfmkt1¯ parasites proliferated asexually similar to wildtype NF54 parasites and differentiated normally into mature male and female gametocytes. Further analysis showed that Pfmkt1¯ gametocytes underwent normal gametogenesis and flagellar gamete formation, with no apparent developmental defects observed.

articles.headline: Antibody profiles identify malaria surveillance blind spots in low-transmission settings
articles.title_original: Malaria antibody responses augment surveillance in low-transmission settings in the Upper River Region, the Gambia.
articles.details: This study evaluated antibody responses to P. falciparum antigens as markers for identifying populations with low surveillance detection. Researchers analyzed data from 5,300 seropositive individuals across 32 villages in The Gambia using random forest classification models. Models demonstrated strong discrimination between low vs. high detection groups with precision-recall AUCs of 0.92-0.93 in <5% transmission settings and 0.89-0.90 in <10% settings using an 8-antigen panel. PfSEA, PfMSP1-19, gSG6, and Etramp4.Ag2 were consistently important predictors. Integrating serological data into surveillance systems may improve detection of hidden transmission reservoirs.

articles.headline: Rac1 promoter analysis suggests link to antihypertensive resistance and malaria
articles.title_original: The Rac1 Promoter as a Molecular Nexus for Antihypertensive Therapy Resistance and Enhanced Malaria Susceptibility.
articles.details: This in silico analysis examined the human Rac1 promoter (-2000 to +500 bp) using computational databases to identify transcription factor binding sites. The 2501 bp promoter is GC-rich (57.34%) with 164 CpG sites (o/e ratio 0.75) defining a canonical CpG island. Analysis revealed binding motifs for mineralocorticoid receptor, hypoxia-inducible factors, inflammatory mediators, and GATA-1. The authors propose the Rac1 regulatory architecture may underlie antihypertensive drug resistance while linking to enhanced malaria susceptibility through erythrocyte remodeling, suggesting a molecular basis for treatment-resistant hypertension in malaria-endemic regions.

articles.headline: Framework for cost efficiencies in malaria routine data quality audits
articles.title_original: A framework for identifying cost efficiencies in malaria routine data quality audits: methodology and application in Zambia.
articles.details: Researchers developed a systematic framework for identifying cost efficiencies in routine data quality audits (RDQA) for malaria programs. Using Zambia as a case study, they evaluated nine RDQA interventions across 1,189 health facility catchment areas from 2022-2024. Results: Average economic cost $24,938 per intervention ($205 per HFCA), with personnel (64%) and transportation (25%) as primary cost drivers. Optimized scenarios incorporating virtual orientation meetings, streamlined team composition, and harmonized allowances reduced total costs by 41%. Digital RDQA tools projected additional savings of up to 52%.

articles.headline: Baseline characteristics and readiness for RTS,S malaria vaccine rollout in Kenya
articles.title_original: The Malaria Vaccine Programme Evaluation in Kenya: results of a baseline household survey prior to the introduction of the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine.
articles.details: This household survey characterized malaria burden and intervention coverage prior to Kenya's RTS,S/AS01E vaccine introduction in 46 sub-counties. Among 4,948 children aged 5-48 months, P. falciparum prevalence was 22% (95% CI 19-26) with variation 1-71%. ITN ownership and use were high (93% and 87%). Thirty-eight percent had recent fever; 70% sought care. Measles dose 2 coverage was 49%; vaccine record retention declined with age. Only 36% of caregivers had heard of the malaria vaccine before introduction, yet >98% expressed willingness to vaccinate, establishing a favorable context for RTS,S rollout.

articles.headline: Malaria socioeconomic burden and benefit of artemether-lumefantrine in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic Impact of Artemether-Lumefantrine in Malaria Treatment across Sub-Saharan Africa: A Model-Based Historical Analysis with 2022 as Reference Year.
articles.details: This model-based analysis assessed the socioeconomic burden of malaria and benefit of artemether-lumefantrine (AL) treatment in sub-Saharan Africa using 2022 as reference year. The model included malaria prevalence across three age groups. Results: Malaria accounted for 30.8 million disability-adjusted life years (DALYs) in those ≥15 years, corresponding to a socioeconomic burden of approximately $82.8 billion. The estimated socioeconomic benefit of AL treatment compared with no treatment was $24.2 billion, derived from avoided productivity loss through reduced deaths, years of life lost, and caregiving days.

articles.headline: Transient NK cell activation during acute malaria
articles.title_original: NK cell cytotoxicity is transiently enhanced during acute malaria and modulated by the host microenvironment.
articles.details: Longitudinal study showing natural killer cells undergo transient but potent activation during acute P. falciparum infection with enhanced granzyme B-mediated cytotoxicity and elevated gene expression (PRF1, GZMB, GZMA). Increased plasma granzymes and proinflammatory cytokines enhanced NK function in vitro, but plasma from clinical malaria also contained inhibitory mediators including soluble cytokine receptors that dampened NK responses, indicating tight regulation of NK cell responses.

articles.headline: High-affinity aptamer improves malaria diagnostic detection
articles.title_original: Engineering High-Performance Aptamers via Optimized SELEX and Structure-Guided Dimer Assembly for Clinical Malaria Diagnostics.
articles.details: Researchers developed a symmetric inverted dimeric aptamer (IDA) targeting P. falciparum lactate dehydrogenase using optimized SELEX and structure-guided engineering. The IDA achieved picomolar affinity (>800-fold higher than existing aptamers) with enhanced nuclease resistance. Clinical evaluation in 52 blood samples showed 81.8% sensitivity, 100% specificity, and 3.3 ng/mL detection limit, comparing favorably to commercial rapid diagnostic tests.

articles.headline: Palmitoyltransferase inhibition blocks P. falciparum replication
articles.title_original: [Role of protein palmitoylation modification in the proliferation and gametogenesis of Plasmodium falciparum].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: In vitro study of P. falciparum treated with 2-bromopalmitate (10 μmol/L) to inhibit palmitoyltransferases. Treatment significantly reduced parasitemia area under curve (8.37 vs 25.70 in controls, p<0.001), merozoites per schizont (14.02 vs 18.05, p<0.001), and gametocyte production (7.51 vs 18.90, p<0.001). Gametocyte formation and maturation were blocked with down-regulation of genes involved in cell cycle and mitosis.

articles.headline: Recent malaria outbreak in Saudi Arabia predominantly P. vivax
articles.title_original: Clinical Characteristics and Outcomes of Malaria Patients in the Aseer Region, Saudi Arabia: A Retrospective Study (2022-2025).
articles.details: Retrospective study of 311 malaria patients (2022-2025) at a Saudi hospital, predominantly young adult male expatriates from Ethiopia (48.2%), Saudi Arabia (16.4%), and Yemen (15.1%). P. vivax predominated at 51.1% followed by P. falciparum at 40.2%. Severe malaria occurred in 43.7% with severe anemia (26%) and jaundice (23.2%). 84% of cases occurred in 2024-2025 with peak of 43 cases in October 2024. Higher parasitemia and non-Saudi nationality independently predicted severe malaria.

articles.headline: Low ITN use despite ownership among Fulani pregnant women
articles.title_original: Factors Associated With Insecticide-Treated Bed Net Possession and Utilization in Malaria Prevention Among Fulani Pregnant Women in the Savannah Hinterlands of Ghana: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 159 Fulani pregnant women in Ghana found only 54.7% owned an insecticide-treated net (ITN), and among owners only 52.9% used it. Awareness of ITN efficacy (AOR 3.45) and prior counseling (AOR 4.52) predicted ownership. Knowledge of malaria symptoms, having multiple children, and owning multiple ITNs paradoxically predicted lower utilization, highlighting gaps between ownership and consistent use.

articles.headline: Plant-based capsules show in vitro antiplasmodial activity
articles.title_original: Formulation and Evaluation of Capsules From Azadirachta indica and Khaya senegalensis for Malaria Treatment.
articles.details: Researchers formulated traditional malaria remedies from Azadirachta indica (AI) and Khaya senegalensis (KS) into capsules for improved stability and dosing. Capsules met quality control standards with good drug release profiles (AI 78.13%, KS 95.63%). AI demonstrated good antiplasmodial activity (IC50 2.74 ± 0.70 μg/mL) and KS moderate activity (IC50 24.37 ± 4.19 μg/mL), with both showing selective toxicity for Plasmodium parasites.

articles.headline: Convulsions and delayed care-seeking predict childhood malaria death
articles.title_original: Risk factors for malaria-related mortality among children under five at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda, 2020-2024: A case-control study.
articles.details: Case-control study of 100 pediatric malaria deaths versus 200 surviving controls with WHO-defined severe P. falciparum malaria at a Ugandan hospital (2020-2024), overall case fatality rate 2.7%. Convulsions (aOR 17, 95% CI 4.2-71), loss of consciousness (aOR 14, CI 1.4-113), and delayed care-seeking >24 hours (aOR 8.8, CI 2.3-34) were strongest mortality predictors, along with severe anemia and vomiting.

articles.headline: Malaria treatment in pregnancy: maternal versus fetal effects
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy: what's good for the goose may not be good for the goslings.
articles.details: No abstract available; commentary on differential effects of malaria prevention and treatment strategies on pregnant women compared to their infants.

articles.headline: IPTp-DP equivalent to IPTp-SP for infant malaria outcomes
articles.title_original: Impact of intermittent preventive treatment for malaria in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and their combination on infant outcomes: A randomized controlled trial.
articles.details: Double-blind randomized controlled trial (n=871 infants) comparing maternal intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (IPTp-DP), or combination (IPTp-DP+SP). Clinical malaria incidence was similar across arms (IPTp-DP incidence rate ratio 1.04, IPTp-DP+SP 0.93 versus IPTp-SP). Overall parasite prevalence 21.8% with no significant differences. Wasting at birth was transiently higher with DP-containing regimens but resolved by 3-6 months.

articles.headline: Malaria mechanisms may shape cancer risk in African populations
articles.title_original: Malaria driven mechanisms shaping cancer risk and aggressiveness in African populations.
articles.details: Narrative review of mechanistic links between malaria and cancer in sub-Saharan Africa identifying five principal pathways: malaria-induced oxidative stress and inflammation driving genomic instability; gut dysbiosis altering tumor immunity; shared molecular targets (EPCR, oncofetal chondroitin sulfate) exploited by parasites and cancer cells; cooperative interactions with oncogenic viruses like EBV; and vitamin D deficiency impairing immune surveillance. Notes antimalarial drugs have anticancer properties, suggesting opportunities for dual-action therapeutics.

articles.headline: P. vivax and GBS require excluding alternative causes
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Vivax Can Only Be Responsible for GBS if All Other Triggers Have Been Ruled Out.
articles.details: No abstract available; commentary on clinical diagnosis of Guillain-Barré syndrome (GBS) in the context of P. vivax infection, emphasizing the need to exclude alternative etiologies before attributing GBS to malaria.

articles.headline: Data documenting malaria's cultural history in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data for cultural history of malaria in Hungary].
articles.details: No abstract available; archival or historical article on malaria in Hungary, not a contemporary clinical or research study.

articles.headline: Workshop on malaria vaccines for pregnant women
articles.title_original: Immunization strategies to prevent malaria in pregnancy - a multistakeholder workshop.
articles.details: Multistakeholder workshop discussing malaria in pregnancy (MiP) vaccines. 12.4 million pregnant women affected globally in 2023. Vaccine candidates targeting VAR2CSA antigen (PRIMVAC, PAMVAC) in development; existing malaria vaccines being repurposed. Workshop emphasized strengthening communication, defining clinical trial endpoints, reinforcing pharmacovigilance, and planning regulatory alignment to support future MiP vaccine implementation.

articles.headline: Amplicon sequencing distinguishes P. vivax relapse from reinfection
articles.title_original: Understanding Plasmodium vivax recurrent infections using an amplicon deep sequencing assay, identity-by-descent and model-based classification.
articles.details: Developed PvAmpSeq assay targeting 11 SNP-rich genomic regions to classify P. vivax recurrences. Solomon Islands trial: >50% identity-by-descent relatedness in half of recurrences (probable relapses/recrudescences). Peru cohort: 68% of recurrences showed <25% relatedness. Assay provides high-resolution genotyping for characterizing P. vivax recurrences and offers insights into transmission and treatment outcomes.

articles.headline: Long non-coding RNAs regulate malaria parasite development
articles.title_original: Dynamic regulation of long non-coding RNAs across asexual and gametocyte development in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Integrated Nanopore direct RNA sequencing, ribosome profiling, and single-cell transcriptomics to characterize P. falciparum lncRNAs in asexual and sexual blood stages. Most lncRNAs showed pronounced stage-specific expression, particularly abundant in mature gametocytes. Many lncRNAs located antisense to protein-coding genes with co-expression from putative bidirectional promoters; differential expression between male and female gametocytes suggests roles in gametocytogenesis and transmission.

articles.headline: Between-house variation drives malaria vector density in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Variance partitioning reveals contrasting random effect contributions to the density and species composition of malaria-transmitting mosquitoes in western Burkina Faso.
articles.details: 60-month pyrethroid spray catch sampling (2012–2019) across four villages in Sudano-Sahelian Burkina Faso. For female Anopheles gambiae s.l. density: between-house variation was largest component, followed by between-year then between-village, after accounting for seasonality. Species composition varied markedly across villages; between-village variance contributed up to 76% of total random variation. Findings help parameterize vector control trials.

articles.headline: P. falciparum predominates in Niger; P. vivax documented
articles.title_original: Prevalence and spatial distribution of malaria-causing Plasmodium infections in the Niger Republic.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study (September–November 2022) of 5,096 patients across three epidemiological zones in Niger. PCR analysis of 304 samples: 55% Plasmodium-positive (166/304). Species distribution: P. falciparum 90% (149/166), P. ovale 5%, P. malariae 3%, P. vivax 2%. P. vivax identified in hypo-endemic area, highlighting need for adapted diagnostic and management strategies by National Malaria Control Program.

articles.headline: Protein-protein interactions as next-generation malaria drug targets
articles.title_original: Targeting protein-protein interactions in Plasmodium: from asexual replication to sexual development.
articles.details: Review synthesizing structural and functional advances in Plasmodium multi-subunit complexes governing invasion, intracellular survival, metabolism, transcription, proteostasis, egress, and transmission. Integrates insights from cryo-EM, proximity-dependent biotinylation, and genetic manipulation. Argues protein-protein interactions have lower mutational potential and require cooperative compensatory mutations for resistance emergence, offering resistance-resilient strategy compared to conventional enzyme inhibitors.

articles.headline: Transfluthrin emanator reduces resistant mosquito landing and feeding
articles.title_original: Experimental hut evaluation of mosquito shield, a transfluthrin spatial emanator, for control of wild pyrethroid resistant malaria vectors in Benin.
articles.details: Experimental hut trial of Mosquito Shield™ (transfluthrin emanator) against pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles gambiae s.l. in Benin (two 32-day cycles). Achieved 43.0% protective efficacy (95% CI: 24.0–57.0; p<0.001) by human landing catches, 64% personal protection (95% CI: 21.9–81.8; p<0.001), 48.5% deterrence, 29.9% blood-feeding inhibition, and 49.0% mosquito mortality. Also effective against Mansonia africana (38.0% protective efficacy, 95% CI: 12.0–57.0; >96% mortality).
articles.notable_reason: WHO-endorsed tool demonstrates strong efficacy against pyrethroid-resistant vectors

articles.headline: RDT misdiagnosis widespread in DRC; test-and-treat adherence poor
articles.title_original: Challenges in malaria diagnosis and treatment in Kinshasa Province, Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.details: Cohort study (2015–2022) of 2,269 participants across seven sites in Kinshasa Province, DRC. One-year cumulative incidences of false-positive and false-negative RDTs: 23.1% and 17.8%; by seven years: 77.7% and 83.4%. About 58% of RDT-negative participants received antimalarial treatment; 4% of RDT-positive participants did not. Misdiagnosis associated with age, recent treatment, transmission intensity, and parasite density.

articles.headline: Interceptor G2 nets reduce malaria cases 30–40% in Ghana
articles.title_original: Reductions in malaria cases after deployment of dual-active ingredient insecticide-treated nets in Ghana-a Bayesian interrupted time series analysis.
articles.details: Bayesian Poisson regression time series analysis (2018–2023) of confirmed malaria cases in Western North Region, Ghana following IG2 ITN distribution (2021). Cases reduced 30% immediately post-distribution (OR 0.696, CrI 0.623–0.778), sustained at 26% reduction at 6 months (OR 0.739, CrI 0.653–0.837) and 40% at 30 months (OR 0.594, CrI 0.492–0.718). Effect strong in 7 of 9 districts; area has confirmed pyrethroid resistance.
articles.notable_reason: 30–40% case reduction with next-generation nets in resistant area

articles.headline: DRC healthcare workers show high acceptance of Artemisia tea
articles.title_original: Public Health Perspectives on Integrating Artemisia annua Tea for Uncomplicated Malaria Treatment: A Cross-Sectional Study of Perceptions and Acceptability Among Healthcare Workers in Kalima District, Maniema, DRC.
articles.authors: Munyangi Wa Nkola J, et al.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 337 healthcare professionals in Kalima District, DRC on Artemisia annua tea for malaria treatment. Clinical acceptability: 81.0%; 82.8% perceived preparation effective. Rural residency strongest predictor of acceptance (AOR = 6.847; p = 0.003). 49.0% identified lack of clinical evidence as barrier; 91.4% demanded formal training on standardized dosage. Study notes policy-practice gap between international guidelines and field realities.

articles.headline: Culex research overshadowed in malaria and dengue-endemic regions
articles.title_original: Forgotten in the tropics: research on Culex mosquitoes is overshadowed in malaria and dengue-endemic regions.
articles.details: Systematic literature review of 1,389 mosquito-related publications (2010, 2020) in PubMed and Web of Science. Proportion reporting Culex spp. significantly higher in non-endemic countries (64.5%) versus malaria-endemic (30.2%) and dengue-endemic (34.2%) countries. Between 2010–2020, Aedes reporting increased while Anopheles reporting decreased. Study highlights neglect of Culex-associated zoonotic pathogens in endemic regions.

articles.headline: Rural, educated households perceive higher malaria risk in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Household malaria risk perception in Ethiopia: multilevel and spatial analysis using the 2022-2023 National Health Equity Survey.
articles.details: Analysis of 9,157 households from 2022–2023 Ethiopian National Health Equity Survey using multilevel logistic regression. 76.4% reported feeling at risk of malaria (95% CI: 74.9–77.9). Rural households: 54% higher risk perception (AOR = 1.54, 95% CI: 1.22–1.95); higher education: nearly twice as likely (AOR = 1.89, 95% CI: 1.36–2.63); older household heads: 50% less likely (AOR = 0.49, 95% CI: 0.35–0.67). 38% of variability between woredas.

articles.headline: Sudan malaria: P.vivax predominance, G6PD variants in conflict zones
articles.title_original: Characterisation of malaria and glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase deficiency in conflict-affected zones of southern and eastern Sudan.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 717 malaria-suspected patients in conflict-affected Sudan (Kosti n=252, Kassala n=465) conducted 2023-2024. In Kassala, malaria prevalence was 62.6% by PCR with P.vivax 54%, P.falciparum 34%, and mixed 12%; in Kosti, P.falciparum dominated at 97.7%. Submicroscopic infections detected in 8.7% overall. G6PD B variant predominated (94.2% in Kassala, 79.4% in Kosti); African A− variant identified in 3.5% of Kosti individuals. No significant association between G6PD genotype and parasite density.

articles.headline: AKI complicates 28% of malaria admissions in Southwest Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence of acute kidney injury and its associated factors among malaria patients admitted to public hospitals in the Ilu Aba Bora and Bedelle Zones of Oromia region, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 307 adult malaria patients in four Ethiopian hospitals (February-April 2024). Acute kidney injury (AKI), defined as serum creatinine increase ≥0.3 mg/dL within 48 hours, occurred in 28.3% (87/307). Independent risk factors included male sex (AOR 1.98), older age 40-59 years (AOR 2.52) and ≥60 years (AOR 4.46), dehydration (AOR 1.74), P.falciparum infection (AOR 2.73), mixed infection (AOR 3.30), and high parasitaemia (AOR 2.71). Study emphasizes need for renal assessment in malaria patients.

articles.headline: Neonicotinoid IRS linked to 26% increase in malaria in Ghana
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence rose following the introduction of neonicotinoid-based IRS in selected districts in northern Ghana: An observational analysis.
articles.details: Interrupted time-series analysis of malaria incidence in 22 northern Ghana districts from 2015-2022 comparing organophosphate and neonicotinoid insecticides. After transition to neonicotinoids (2018-2019), observed incidence exceeded counterfactual predictions. Across all districts, malaria incidence was 26% higher under neonicotinoid IRS than expected under continued organophosphate use (IRR=1.26, 95% CI 1.07-2.08). Regional increases were significant in Northern (IRR 1.76), North East (IRR 1.83), and Upper East (IRR 4.26) regions. Findings suggest neonicotinoid IRS transition reduced effectiveness.
articles.notable_reason: Shows major IRS product change had negative epidemiological impact

articles.headline: Computational models predict merozoite invasion mechanics and force requirements
articles.title_original: In silico analysis of the invasion mechanics and invasiveness of the Plasmodium falciparum merozoite.
articles.authors: Msosa C, Abdalrahman T, Franz T
articles.details: Finite element computational study modeling P.falciparum merozoite invasion of erythrocytes. Invasiveness decreases with increased erythrocyte sphericity (as in hereditary spherocytosis). Minimum force required for merozoite invasion predicted at 11 pN. Membrane damage induced early in invasion reduces invasiveness more than damage throughout the process. Nanoindentation simulations suggest potential application for screening invasion-blocking antimalarial drugs.

articles.headline: Free healthcare and differentiated diagnosis prevent pediatric malaria cases
articles.title_original: Model-Based Assessment of the Effects of Selective Free Healthcare and Differentiated Diagnosis on Malaria Burden in the Republic of Guinea.
articles.details: Modeling study using EMOD software assessing optimization scenarios in nine epidemiological archetypes in Guinea over 2025-2030. Free healthcare for children under five combined with differentiated diagnosis (appropriate care at first point of contact) raised ACT access index from 0.60 to 0.81. Combined strategy projected to prevent 13,428 complicated pediatric malaria cases (95% CI 7,768-19,088). Effects most pronounced in low-incidence areas where incidence dropped below 50 cases per 1,000 by 2028.

articles.headline: Cytokines and metabolism drive γδ T cell differentiation in placental malaria
articles.title_original: Ex vivo cytokine responses and metabolic reprogramming are associated with γδ T cell differentiation in Plasmodium falciparum placental malaria.
articles.details: Case-control study of 50 women at delivery (21 with placental malaria, 29 without) examining γδ T cell responses. IL-8 and IL-33 cytokines were associated with increased frequency of terminally differentiated effector memory (TEMRA) γδ T cells in peripheral blood and down-regulation of exhaustion markers TIM-3 and PD-1. Metabolic profiling revealed enhanced mitochondrial metabolic activity in terminally differentiated cells in peripheral blood versus glycolytic activities in other compartments.

articles.headline: Chloroquine sensitivity restored in Gabon over 20 years post-cessation
articles.title_original: Reversal of Chloroquine Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum in Gabon: A Phenotype-Genotype Relationship over the Last 20 Years.
articles.details: Long-term study (2004-2024) in Lambaréné, Gabon showing step-wise restoration of chloroquine sensitivity in P.falciparum. Ex vivo chloroquine IC50 decreased from 113.8 nM (2004) to 2.1 nM (2024), indicating shift toward wild-type susceptibility. Chloroquine-sensitive pfcrt CVMNK haplotype increased from 3.6% (2009) to 98.2% (2024), and wild-type pfmdr1 N86 increased from 23.1% (2009) to 100% (2024). Paired molecular and phenotypic analyses confirmed association between IC50 values and sensitive genotypes.
articles.notable_reason: Demonstrates decades-long reversal of chloroquine resistance after cessation

articles.headline: DRC malaria control partially achieved key 2020-2023 strategic targets
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Performance Indicators for Malaria Control in Kinshasa from 2020 to 2023, the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.details: Cross-sectional evaluation of malaria control performance indicators in 23 health zones of Kinshasa, Democratic Republic of Congo (2020-2023). Malaria incidence was 30% with upward trend peaking in 2022. Long-lasting insecticidal net distribution to pregnant women ranged 45-88% across years. Rapid diagnostic test use for suspected cases reached 83.1% overall, but no health zone achieved 100% RDT-based diagnosis and only four reached ≥95% of target. Study concluded only partial achievement of strategic plan objectives.

articles.headline: Novel 2-piperazino-pyrimidine blocks merozoite egress via plasmepsin X
articles.title_original: A novel 2-piperazino-pyrimidine compound exhibits asexual antimalarial activity by targeting Plasmodium falciparum plasmepsin X.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Drug discovery study identifying 2-piperazino-pyrimidine (FPSA) compound from Shionogi library with P.falciparum activity. FPSA inhibits merozoite egress by blocking plasmepsin X (PMX) proteolytic activity. Whole-genome sequencing of FPSA-resistant parasites identified mutations in PMX-encoding gene (I252V, E55G substitutions) and genomic duplications. Transcriptomic analysis confirmed similar gene expression patterns to dual PMIX/PMX inhibitor WM382. Identifies novel antimalarial compound class targeting PMX.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies novel drug class targeting plasmepsin X for merozoite egress

articles.headline: Computational screening reveals selectivity challenges for Plasmodium NDH2 drugs
articles.title_original: Comparative Structural and Functional Analysis of Plasmodium Type II Nicotinamide Adenine Dinucleotide: Quinone Oxidoreductase and its Human Homologue, Apoptosis-Inducing Factor, Mitochondrion-associated 1 (AIF-M1).
articles.details: Comparative computational drug design study screening South African Natural Compounds Database against Plasmodium NDH2 across five human-infecting species (P.falciparum, P.knowlesi, P.malariae, P.ovale, P.vivax). Three compounds showed strong binding to PfNDH2 (−8.6 to −10.0 kcal/mol). However, molecular dynamics simulations revealed these compounds also stabilized human AIF-M1, with Phe482 identified as critical off-target anchor. Study demonstrates selectivity challenge for effective pan-Plasmodium NDH2 inhibition.

articles.headline: Gut microbiota modulates cerebral malaria susceptibility in mouse models
articles.title_original: Gut microbiota bidirectionally influences protection and severity in cerebral malaria in mice.
articles.details: Experimental study in C57BL/6N mice using four-antibiotic cocktail (ampicillin, neomycin, metronidazole, vancomycin) to alter gut microbiota prior to P.berghei ANKA infection. Approximately 80% of mice with modified microbiota avoided experimental cerebral malaria (ECM) with reduced blood-brain barrier disruption and lymphocyte brain infiltration. 16S rRNA sequencing identified specific bacterial species that attenuated or exacerbated ECM. Mono-colonization experiments confirmed distinct bacterial species independently modulated host susceptibility.

articles.headline: Routine microscopy misdiagnoses 30% of malaria cases in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Unveiling the overlooked burden of malaria misdiagnosis using lamp-based re-evaluation of routine malaria diagnosis in health centers: implications for public health and clinical practice in Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 384 malaria-suspected individuals in four Northwest Ethiopia health centers (July-August 2024) comparing routine microscopy with Loop-Mediated Isothermal Amplification (LAMP). Among 384 participants, 30.0% showed discordant results: false positives 7.6%, false negatives 18.8%, species misidentification 3.6%. McNemar's test confirmed statistically significant difference between microscopy and LAMP (p<0.001). Study highlights persistent diagnostic accuracy challenges with routine microscopy in endemic settings.

articles.headline: Mixed Plasmodium infections increase severe malaria mortality in children
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium ovale and Plasmodium malariae Mixed Infections and Associated Mortality in Children with Severe Falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: Cohort of 440 Ugandan children with severe falciparum malaria tested via nPCR; 22.8% had P. falciparum + P. ovale mixed infection, and 2.1% had triple infections with P. falciparum, P. ovale, and P. malariae. Triple infections showed significantly higher mortality (33% vs 7%, OR=7.0, p=0.005) and respiratory distress (67% vs 35%) compared to P. falciparum mono-infection.

articles.headline: Machine learning classifier improves Plasmodium falciparum var gene prediction
articles.title_original: upsML: A high-accuracy machine learning classifier for predicting Plasmodium falciparum var gene upstream groups.
articles.details: Developed upsML, a machine-learning classifier trained on 2,530 curated var genes to assign upstream groups (upsA, upsB, upsC, upsE). Achieved 83% accuracy for DBLα-tag sequences and 92% for full-length PfEMP1 sequences, significantly outperforming existing tools. Applied globally to reveal higher frequency of internal var genes in Asia.

articles.headline: Real-time genome sequencing resolves cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers
articles.title_original: Rapid whole genome sequencing of Plasmodium DNA from cryptic malaria cases in UK travellers provides insights into infection origins, transmission, and antimalarial resistance.
articles.details: Applied whole-genome sequencing via Illumina or Oxford Nanopore to 9 malaria isolates from UK imported cases, using Malaria-Profiler for rapid drug resistance prediction and AI-based geographic origin inference. Profiled 4 cryptic P. falciparum cases and family clusters of P. ovale/P. falciparum, enabling fine-scale transmission analysis and supporting targeted public health responses.

articles.headline: Commentary on ivermectin cluster-randomized malaria control trial
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial. Reply.
articles.details: Reply article to a cluster-randomized trial on ivermectin for malaria control. No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Ivermectin cluster-randomized trial for malaria control
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial.
articles.authors: Last A, Hayes R, Bradley J
articles.details: Cluster-randomized trial of ivermectin for malaria control. Based on title only; no abstract provided to extract specific results or design.

articles.headline: Commentary on permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria in Children. Reply.
articles.authors: Boyce RM, Shook-Sa BE, Mulogo EM
articles.details: Reply article to a trial on permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention in children. No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention in children
articles.title_original: Permethrin-Treated Baby Wraps for the Prevention of Malaria in Children.
articles.authors: Javelle E, de Santi VP, Briolant S
articles.details: Trial on permethrin-treated baby wraps for malaria prevention in children. Based on title only; no abstract provided to extract specific results.

articles.headline: CLAG3 helix 44 critical for malaria parasite nutrient uptake
articles.title_original: A CRISPR-Based Mutagenesis Strategy for Examining CLAG3 Helix 44 Contribution to Malaria Parasite Nutrient Uptake Channels.
articles.details: Used CRISPR mutagenesis to examine CLAG3 helix 44 (α-H44) role in plasmodial surface anion channel (PSAC) nutrient uptake. CLAG3 truncation without α-H44 phenocopied knockout; restoration with recodonized α-H44 fully restored PSAC activity. Two engineered mutants exhibited distinct PSAC phenotypes and failed growth in modified in vivo-approximating medium.

articles.headline: Deep learning ensemble achieves 96% accuracy in malaria smear detection
articles.title_original: CT-Malaria Detection via Adaptive-Weighted Deep Learning Models.
articles.details: Developed two-track deep learning architecture (classical ML and end-to-end CNN) with weighted ensemble for malaria diagnosis on blood smears. Achieved 96.35% accuracy on test data with reduced false negatives for subtle parasites and false positives from staining artifacts. Combined augmentation, multiple modeling tracks, and optimal pairwise ensembling.

articles.headline: P. vivax T cell responses differ between symptomatic and asymptomatic infections
articles.title_original: Different clinical forms of Plasmodium vivax infection result in unique T cell epitope-specific immune responses.
articles.details: Developed 310-peptide P. vivax library (MPv310) to assess CD4+ T cell responses in symptomatic-before-treatment, symptomatic-after-treatment, asymptomatic, and uninfected individuals from Brazil. MPv310 stimulation induced increased CD154+ cells in symptomatic patients and higher IFN-γ+TNF+ frequencies in treated and asymptomatic groups. Distinct antigen-specific activation patterns differentiated symptomatic and asymptomatic infections.

articles.headline: Congenital and neonatal malaria underrecognized in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Congenital and Neonatal Malaria in Ethiopia: A Narrative Review.
articles.authors: Leake Y, Hagos HH, Reta BK
articles.details: Narrative review of congenital and neonatal malaria in Ethiopia identifying 46 total cases across 10 studies, with P. falciparum predominant (35 cases), followed by P. vivax (8 cases) and mixed infections (3 cases); 40 were congenital, 6 neonatal. Authors conclude these conditions are underrecognized despite Ethiopia's substantial malaria burden.

articles.headline: G6PD variants protect against Plasmodium knowlesi but beta-thalassemia does not
articles.title_original: G6PD variants but not Filipino beta-thalassemia are associated with reduced risk of Plasmodium knowlesi infection in Sabah, Malaysia.
articles.details: Case-control study (106 P. knowlesi cases, 89 controls) in Sabah, Malaysia assessed G6PD and β-thalassemia polymorphisms. G6PD deficiency variants present in 3.8% of cases vs 12.4% of controls (OR=0.28, p=0.034), with Viangchan (71%), Coimbra, and Mediterranean variants identified. Filipino β-thalassemia showed no association.

articles.headline: GID complex controls Plasmodium falciparum gametocyte development
articles.title_original: GID/CTLH E3 ligase complex control cell fate programs for sexual development of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study identified that the PfGID E3 ubiquitin ligase complex regulates critical gametocyte development through targeted ubiquitination. Deletion of PfGID components arrests gametocyte development and blocks transmission to mosquitoes. Two key substrates—the RNA-binding protein GD1 and cryptochrome-like protein PfDPL—control male-specific proteins and transcriptional repression essential for gametogenesis.

articles.headline: An. funestus predominates in Tanzanian malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Molecular surveillance of malaria in Tanzania: Baseline surveillance of vectors that drive persistent malaria transmission in regions with varying transmission intensities.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of mosquitoes in five Tanzanian districts (Aug–Oct 2023) collected 19,898 specimens. An. funestus group accounted for 44.3% of catch, with An. funestus s.s. being 96.3% of that group and An. arabiensis 75% of An. gambiae complex (2.2% of total). Of 1,799 vectors tested, 9 (0.5%) were P. falciparum-infected. Estimated annual EIR ranged from 13.0–242.6 infectious bites per person per year across districts, highest in Kyerwa.

articles.headline: Only 7.9% of Ethiopian pregnant women receive integrated malaria services
articles.title_original: Integrated malaria service delivery and its determinants among pregnant women in Ethiopia: Multi level analysis of 2021/22 National Service Provision Assessment Survey.
articles.details: Analysis of 4,273 pregnant women across 662 health facilities in Ethiopia (2021/22 survey) found that only 7.9% attended ANC visits where all integrated malaria service components were delivered. Factors associated with receipt included: ≥2 previous pregnancies (AOR 1.67), ≥3 ANC visits (AOR 1.58), receiving ITN during ANC (AOR 2.81), and attendance at facilities with malaria-trained providers (AOR 4.24). Rural facility attendance was also positively associated (AOR 2.73).

articles.headline: Chromatin remodeling drives var2csa expression in placenta-sequestering parasites
articles.title_original: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa expression and the placental-binding phenotype in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study established phenotypically homogenous VAR2CSA-expressing, placenta-binding P. falciparum parasites that exclusively transcribed var2csa while silencing other var genes. Transcriptional activation strongly correlated with depletion of repressive H3K9me3 histone marks and repositioning of var2csa away from heterochromatic telomeric clusters. Five-methylcytosine was present across all var genes but independent of transcriptional repression and switching.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax Malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax Malaria.
articles.authors: Menkin-Smith L, Koval K, Samandari T
articles.details: No abstract available.

articles.headline: Simpson's 1835 description of placental malaria pigmentation
articles.title_original: Placental malaria in nineteenth-century Scotland.
articles.details: Historical account of James Young Simpson's 1835 inaugural lecture as President of Royal Edinburgh Medical Society, in which he likely made the first academic description of malaria pigmentation in placental tissue using an archived placental sample from 4 months gestation. The finding connected tissue pigmentation previously described to malaria infection in pregnancy, pioneering understanding of placental inflammation and infection.

articles.headline: ACTs remain effective in Gambia despite divergent resistance patterns
articles.title_original: Chloroquine resistance transporter drives divergent multi-locus drug resistance genetic backgrounds and susceptibility in Gambian Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Genotyping and in vitro susceptibility testing of 364 P. falciparum isolates from Gambia identified two main multi-locus drug-resistance backgrounds driven by pfcrt haplotypes: CVIET (62%) conferring chloroquine resistance (highest IC50s when combined with pfmdr1-NFD), and CVMNK showing reduced sensitivity to mefloquine and dihydroartemisinin. High prevalence of pfdhfr S108N (98%) and pfdhps A437G (85%) was observed. No validated pfkelch13 mutations detected, indicating artemisinin-based combination therapies remain effective.

articles.headline: Acridone T111 shows multi-stage antimalarial activity in preclinical models
articles.title_original: Potent acridone antimalarial against all three life stages of Plasmodium.
articles.details: Lead candidate T111, an acridone antimalarial, demonstrated potent in vitro activity against cultured parasites and ex vivo activity against clinical isolates. In rodent models, oral single-dose treatment cured asexual blood-stage infections. Additionally, T111 inhibited sexual parasites, showed activity against relapsing liver-stage parasites in non-human primates, and prevented parasite development in mosquitoes, with synergy when combined with tafenoquine. Mechanism involves mitochondrial electron transport chain inhibition; safety profile in rats was favorable.

articles.headline: Review of biosensors for point-of-care malaria diagnosis advances
articles.title_original: Potential biomarkers and their recent applications ofr point-of-care biosensors for the diagnosis of malaria.
articles.details: Review of recent advances in biosensor engineering (2022–2025) for malaria point-of-care diagnostics, including electrochemical, optical, and piezoelectric approaches. Discusses challenges including asymptomatic carriers, drug and insecticide resistance, and limited healthcare infrastructure. Evaluates signal transduction mechanisms, analytical trade-offs, and microfluidic integration strategies for converting complex biosensor techniques into affordable, field-deployable POCT technologies.

articles.headline: Multiplex urine assay detects P. falciparum in pregnancy non-invasively
articles.title_original: Multiplexed Isothermal Amplification Assay for the Detection of Plasmodium falciparum and Trichomonas vaginalis in Urine.
articles.details: Developed multiplexed isothermal nucleic acid amplification assay (iso-IMRS) for simultaneous non-invasive detection of P. falciparum and Trichomonas vaginalis in urine samples. Assay achieved detection sensitivity of 100 copies/μL for both pathogens in processed human urine, addressing diagnostic challenges in pregnancy where placental sequestration limits parasite detection by microscopy or RDTs. Clinical implementation in Kenya shows promise for POCT with turnaround time within a typical prenatal visit.

articles.headline: Multiplex serology reveals pathogen exposure and immunity gaps in Guyana
articles.title_original: Hotspot-based integrated serosurveillance of neglected tropical diseases, malaria, and vaccine-preventable diseases in urban Guyana.
articles.details: Hotspot-based integrated serosurveillance using multiplex bead assay of 300 individuals per site in four urban Guyanese regions. Seropositivity to P. falciparum and P. vivax was higher in adults; lymphatic filariasis serology (Wb123, Bm14) showed elevated transmission in targeted hotspots. Seroprotection gaps identified: measles/rubella immunity strongest in children and elderly with gaps in young adults; minimal seroprotection against tetanus and diphtheria across all sites. Age-related increases in C. trachomatis and T. pallidum observed.

articles.headline: Protocol for core outcome set in severe malaria treatment trials
articles.title_original: Synthesis and Standardization of Outcomes in Severe Malaria Treatment Trials: Protocol for the Development of a Core Outcome Set (the COSSMaT Study).
articles.details: Protocol for developing the first core outcome set (COS) for severe malaria treatment trials following COMET Initiative guidance. Systematic review identified 326 records with 5 eligible RCTs (2020–2024) from Africa and Asia. Qualitative interviews with 26 parents/caregivers of severe malaria patients are completed; Delphi prioritization (2 rounds, 60–80 participants) and consensus meeting expected completion September 2026. Final COS will standardize outcome selection and measurement across future trials.

articles.headline: Minor Plasmodium species rebound after IRS discontinuation in Ghana
articles.title_original: The dynamics of asymptomatic Plasmodium spp. infections following 10 years of malaria control interventions in Northern Sahelian Ghana.
articles.details: Ten-year interrupted time-series study in Ghana (2012–2022) with five cross-sectional surveys (~2,000 participants each) tracking P. malariae and P. ovale during IRS (2012–2015) and SMC deployment (2015+). IRS reduced P. malariae from 13.7% to 1.4% and P. ovale from 5.7% to 0.4%. After IRS discontinuation in 2017, both rebounded: P. malariae to 2.9% (≈2-fold increase) and P. ovale to 4.0% (≈10-fold increase), though SMC later controlled P. ovale. IRS and SMC both reduce non-falciparum infections but require sustained deployment.

articles.headline: PfRESA3 protein recruits host chaperones to support parasite survival
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum ring-infected erythrocyte surface antigen 3 (PfRESA3) is a cytoskeleton-interacting protein and potential co-chaperone.
articles.details: Mechanistic study identifying PfRESA3 as a dense granule protein exported early in P. falciparum infection that interacts with the host erythrocyte cytoskeleton and recruits the human chaperone HsHSPA8. Recombinant PfRESA3 constructs stimulated HsHSPA8 ATPase activity and enhanced malate dehydrogenase refolding, though truncated constructs did not significantly enhance refolding. Authors propose PfRESA3 modulates host chaperone machinery to support early parasite protein folding and cytoskeletal remodeling.

articles.headline: Review of immune responses to blood-stage malaria infections
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular interactions underlying the peripheral immune responses to blood-stage malaria infections.
articles.details: An up-to-date review article covering human immune responses to blood-stage Plasmodium infections and parasite immune evasion mechanisms. The review addresses the complex multifaceted immune response required to counteract Plasmodium parasites and discusses how these parasites have coevolved with primates and developed numerous strategies to escape human immune defenses.

articles.headline: Military-civilian malaria coordination assessment in Thailand
articles.title_original: Combatting malaria together: key findings from a rapid formative assessment on Royal Thai Army malaria practices and military-civilian collaboration in Sisaket Province, Thailand.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study in Sisaket Province, Thailand (2016–2020) assessed military malaria burden and coordination between Royal Thai Army and Ministry of Public Health. Military cases (n=432) accounted for 18% of province's 2,425 total cases; all were male, median age 28, with 41% indigenous and 40% imported. Qualitative interviews (116 respondents: 72 RTA, 44 MoPH) identified improved coordination but highlighted challenges including restricted military site access, staff rotation, and communication barriers. Study recommends formalizing joint operating procedures.

articles.headline: White rice substrate optimizes Beauveria bassiana for vector control
articles.title_original: Effect of solid substrates on the production and germination of Beauveria bassiana (Balsamo) Vuillemin, and their infectivity against the malaria vector, Anopheles stephensi (Diptera: Culicidae).
articles.authors: Siddaramegowda R, Chalageri VH, Eapen A
articles.details: Evaluation of 11 solid substrates for production of entomopathogenic fungus B. bassiana against Anopheles stephensi. White rice yielded highest conidial production (6.8 × 10⁵ CFU/g) with germination rates of 37% at 8 h and 54.3% at 16 h. WHO cone bioassay showed white rice-produced conidia achieved 90% and 86.25% mosquito mortality on cement and mud panels with median survival times of 5–7 days. Barley produced lower mortality (47.5–51.25%) with 10-day median survival. Authors recommend multicentric studies before vector control deployment.

articles.headline: IPTp-SP coverage suboptimal in urban Madagascar; ANC barriers identified
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria during pregnancy and coverage of intermittent preventive treatment with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine in urban Amboasary-Sud, Southern Madagascar.
articles.details: Retrospective study of 1,262 pregnant women in Madagascar (2021) found IPTp-SP coverage suboptimal: 64.9% (819/1,262) received ≥1 dose but only 49.9% (409/819) completed ≥3 doses. Malaria RDT positivity was 21.1% (61/289) among symptomatic women; 4.8% (61/1,262) confirmed symptomatic malaria overall. Multivariable logistic regression identified living <5 km from facility (aOR=1.54, 95% CI 1.14–2.08) and attending ≥4 ANC visits (aOR=2.38, 95% CI 1.76–3.21) as associated with adequate IPTp-SP coverage. Authors recommend strengthening ANC attendance and addressing geographical barriers.

articles.headline: Seasonal shift in Anopheles funestus habitat use in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Seasonal variation in aquatic habitat availability and use by the malaria vector, Anopheles funestus.
articles.details: Cross-sectional surveys in southeastern Tanzania (dry and rainy seasons, 2021–2022) mapped 2,824 aquatic habitats and identified An. funestus in 27% of sites. Larval presence and density were significantly predicted by habitat type, village, season, and physicochemical factors (water depth, vegetation, algae, clarity, source). An. funestus consistently occupied river streams, ground pools, and ditches in both seasons but expanded habitat use during wet season (spring-fed wells, rice fields, dug pits). Authors conclude seasonal shifts reflect ecological adaptability and suggest targeting persistent habitats during dry season for effective larval source management.

articles.headline: Cabamiquine susceptibility varies among non-falciparum Plasmodium isolates
articles.title_original: Antimalarial cabamiquine inhibits blood-stage of different field isolates of Plasmodium species.
articles.details: Ex-vivo drug susceptibility testing of field isolates from Ghana and Mali showed differential cabamiquine susceptibility: P. ovale isolates susceptible (median IC₅₀ 0.944 nM, range 0.225–2.315) similar to P. falciparum, but P. malariae isolates less susceptible (median IC₅₀ 3.915 nM, range 1.410–7.010, p=0.001). P. ovale showed decreased dihydroartemisinin susceptibility (median IC₅₀ 3.962 nM) versus P. falciparum (p=0.0371), with no DHA difference for P. malariae. Cabamiquine-resistant parasites showed limited cross-resistance with established antimalarials. Authors conclude P. ovale and P. malariae remain cabamiquine-susceptible.

articles.headline: Malaria incidence risk factors and mathematical model analysis
articles.title_original: Incidence and risk factors of malaria with hematological and immunological evaluation and integrable analysis of a fractional mathematical model.
articles.details: Study examining incidence and risk factors of malaria with hematological and immunological evaluation integrated with fractional mathematical model analysis. (Specific quantitative results not available in provided title.)

articles.headline: Sex Distorter Male Drive for resistance-resilient An. gambiae control
articles.title_original: Sex distorter male drive for resistance-resilient population control of the human malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: Study characterized two germline-specific promoters (spo11 and vasa1) in Anopheles gambiae for CRISPR-based genetic drives.
