Temporal analysis revealed pfplasmepsin2 CNV emerged first post-DHA-piperaquine introduction, with subsequent pfcrt mutations; both markers exceeded 70% in Eastern Thailand 2015–2018.

articles.headline: Malaria in pregnancy: pathogenesis, immunity, outcomes, and interventions review
articles.title_original: Malaria in pregnancy at the frontline: a delicate balance.
articles.authors: Kokuhennadige V, Aitken E, Rogerson S
articles.details: Review synthesizing recent findings on malaria in pregnancy. Placental alterations and inflammation cause maternal morbidity and adverse birth outcomes; antenatal infection affects infant immune and neurocognitive development. Protection increases with successive pregnancies. Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) still improves birth weight despite parasite resistance; dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine more effective against parasitemia but does not improve birth outcomes. Authors recommend targeting first-trimester infections, blood-stage parasites, and vaccines for primigravidae.

articles.headline: Blood group and alpha-globin variants linked to vivax malaria susceptibility
articles.title_original: Genetic Determinants for Vivax Malaria Susceptibility in Chiplun (Ratnagiri), Maharashtra, India: A Case-Control Study.
articles.details: Case-control study of 228 malaria cases and 2,149 controls in Maharashtra, India. Blood group B+ associated with increased malaria risk (OR = 1.36, 95% CI: 1.02–1.80, p < 0.05); A+ protective (OR = 0.72, 95% CI: 0.52–0.99, p < 0.05). Homozygous-α3·7 deletion genotype showed significantly elevated susceptibility (OR = 3.33, 95% CI: 2.00–5.53, p < 0.001). No significant associations observed for Rh phenotype, CR1 polymorphisms, or Gerbich variants.

articles.headline: CRISPR-Cas9 review for antimalarial drug-resistance research in Africa
articles.title_original: Innovative approach in malaria research: Harnessing CRISPR-Cas9 for antimalarial drug-resistance studies in Africa.
articles.details: Comprehensive review examining CRISPR-Cas9 applications in antimalarial drug-resistance research across Africa, including roles in elucidating resistance pathways, validating molecular markers, and enhancing diagnostics. Scoping review identified significantly low adoption despite technological advances. Notes barriers exist but CRISPR-Cas9 holds strong potential for accelerating antimalarial discovery and advancing precision medicine in malaria-endemic regions.

articles.headline: Blood cell ratios predict malaria in pregnant women
articles.title_original: The clinical significance of monocyte-to-lymphocyte and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratios in malaria infection and parasitaemia among pregnant women in Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: 270 third-trimester pregnant women in Burkina Faso screened for P. falciparum by microscopy and RDT. Monocyte-to-lymphocyte ratio (MLR) significantly higher in malaria-infected women (0.48 vs 0.40; p=0.021) and in high parasitaemia (0.48 vs 0.40; p=0.029). MLR independently predicted malaria (OR=4.19) and high parasitaemia (OR=7.16) with modest diagnostic accuracy (ROC 0.60-0.61). Authors conclude MLR and neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio may serve as supportive markers for malaria in pregnancy, aiding early risk stratification in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Ferroptosis inhibitor alleviates cerebral malaria in mice
articles.title_original: Ferrostatin-1 alleviates experimental cerebral malaria by regulating immune cell functions and brain endothelial ferroptosis.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Murine cerebral malaria model using Plasmodium berghei ANKA. Ferrostatin-1 (ferroptosis inhibitor) reduced parasitaemia, extended survival time, alleviated neurological symptoms, and improved blood-brain barrier integrity. Mechanistically, ferrostatin-1 enhanced dendritic cell antigen presentation and promoted M1 macrophage polarization while inhibiting ferroptosis in brain endothelial cells, downregulating chemokines CXCL9/CXCL10 and adhesion molecules ICAM-1/VCAM-1. Authors propose ferroptosis-targeted strategy as novel approach for cerebral malaria prevention and treatment.

articles.headline: Indole alkaloids inhibit malaria transmission enzyme in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Computational evaluation of indole alkaloids as inhibitors of 3-hydroxykynurenine transaminase in malaria vector control.
articles.details: Computational study evaluating indole alkaloids as inhibitors of 3-hydroxykynurenine transaminase (3-HKT), critical for P. falciparum gametogenesis in Anopheles. Virtual screening identified spiroindolone (EP4), asperpenazine (SP4), and strychnine (ST6) with binding free energies of -7.38, -7.21, and -7.12 kcal/mol. Molecular dynamics simulations showed EP4 and SP4 with stable binding (MMPBSA values -69.658 and -68.658 kJ/mol respectively), superior to ST6. Authors suggest EP4 and SP4 as candidate compounds requiring further in vitro and in vivo validation.

articles.headline: Salivary gland mechanisms critical for genetic malaria control
articles.title_original: Genetic control of malaria transmission: a salivary gland-centric perspective.
articles.details: Review examining genetic control of malaria transmission with focus on sporozoite recognition and invasion of mosquito salivary glands. Discusses critical gaps in understanding the molecular mechanisms and protein interactions mediating this stage of Plasmodium development. Authors argue that advancing knowledge of salivary gland mechanisms is essential for designing effective transgenes for genetic control strategies, particularly those targeting mosquito population replacement.

articles.headline: High seasonal asymptomatic malaria in Lagos, Nigeria
articles.title_original: Longitudinal Assessment of Asymptomatic Malaria Infections in a Suburban Community in Lagos, Nigeria.
articles.details: 1,860 asymptomatic individuals screened for P. falciparum in Lagos. PCR detected 11.7% (95%CI 10.3-13.2), RDT 8.8% (95%CI 7.5-10.0), microscopy 2.7% (95%CI 1.9-3.4). RDT sensitivity 34.4% versus microscopy 22.0%; microscopy positive predictive value 96% versus RDT 46%. Peak infection June 36.0% (wet season 14.9% vs dry 7.2%); school-age children (5-14 years) 14.1%; males 12.9% versus females 10.7%. Authors conclude high asymptomatic malaria burden during wet season warrants nationwide surveillance intensification.

articles.headline: Third lysophospholipase required for parasite fatty acid uptake
articles.title_original: Defining the minimal enzymatic requirements for fatty acid scavenging from lysophosphatidylcholine by erythrocytic Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study identifying PARE (prodrug activation and resistance esterase) as third enzyme mediating lysophosphatidylcholine (LPC) catabolism in P. falciparum, alongside XL2 and XLH4. Loss of PARE alone had no effect, but PARE/XL2/XLH4-deficient parasites failed to replicate in defined media with LPC as sole exogenous fatty acid source. PARE is membrane-associated at parasite periphery and catalyzes hydrolysis of inwardly-diffusing LPC. Authors conclude P. falciparum depends on parasite-encoded lysophospholipases for LPC catabolism.

articles.headline: Computational screening identifies drugs for cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Computational Drug Repositioning for Targeting PfEMP1: Potential Therapeutics for Cerebral Malaria in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Computational drug repositioning study targeting PfEMP1 (P. falciparum erythrocyte membrane protein 1) for cerebral malaria. Multi-modal screening identified Lumacaftor, Vilazodone, Tucatinib, Lenvatinib, and Hydrocortisone Cypionate with favorable docking energies (-9.1 to -8.3 kcal/mol) and oral bioavailability. Molecular dynamics simulations and Gibbs free energy analysis confirmed stable drug-PfEMP1 interactions. Authors propose these FDA-approved drugs as potential repurposed therapeutics pending experimental validation.

articles.headline: High vaccine acceptance among pregnant women in Malawi
articles.title_original: Assessing Readiness for Future Maternal Malaria Vaccines: Knowledge, Practices, and Vaccine Attitudes Among Women of Reproductive Age in Malawi.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey assessing readiness for maternal malaria vaccines in Malawi. High general malaria knowledge and preventive practices; 53.4% reported fever during pregnancy. Knowledge (aOR 0.30, 95%CI 0.16-0.57) and perceived risk (aOR 0.35, 95%CI 0.18-0.68) varied by district. Despite 92% acknowledging possible vaccine harm, 97% reported willingness to accept a future maternal malaria vaccine. Authors conclude high vaccine acceptability but highlight need for context-specific delivery strategies for future vaccine introduction.

articles.headline: Humidity reshapes thermal suitability for invasive malaria vector
articles.title_original: Humidity shapes the thermal niche of Anopheles stephensi, an invasive malaria vector.
articles.details: Factorial life-table experiment with Anopheles stephensi at 5 relative humidities (30-90%) and 7 temperatures (16-38°C). Humidity markedly modifies thermal performance of fitness traits. Demographic modeling shows humidity substantially reshapes population fitness projections compared to temperature-only models—suppressing growth in hot arid regions while enhancing fitness in humid and high-elevation climates of South Asia and Africa. Authors conclude integrating multiple environmental drivers essential for accurate vector distribution projections.

articles.headline: ACP essential for parasite organelle biogenesis independent of fatty acids
articles.title_original: Acyl Carrier Protein is Essential for Apicoplast Biogenesis in Malaria Parasites Independent of Fatty Acid Synthesis.
articles.details: Study of Acyl Carrier Protein (ACP) function in P. falciparum blood-stage parasites. ACP knockout is lethal despite apicoplast fatty acid synthesis being dispensable in blood stages. ACP loss impairs isoprenoid biosynthesis and blocks apicoplast biogenesis. Proximity biotinylation identified ACP's role in binding and stabilizing apicoplast pyruvate kinase II (PKII), the only NTP source in the organelle. Authors reveal essential ACP function at metabolic hub controlling broad apicoplast metabolism independent of canonical fatty acid synthesis role.

articles.headline: Adaptive surveillance framework improves malaria vector monitoring
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the Entomological Adaptive Surveillance Framework for malaria vector monitoring: a comparative field trial with routine surveillance in Ghana and Mozambique.
articles.details: Comparative field trial of entomological adaptive surveillance framework (EASF) versus routine surveillance in Ghana and Mozambique for Anopheles monitoring. EASF uses Bayesian exceedance probability to strategically select sampling locations. EASF outperformed routine surveillance in mosquito catch rates, model robustness, and uncertainty reduction. When standardized, EASF sites yielded higher catches with lower coefficient of variation. EASF identified proportionally fewer obsolete sampling locations. Authors conclude adaptive design enhances surveillance precision and efficiency.

articles.headline: Malaria-helminth coinfection causes early blood cell pattern changes
articles.title_original: Haematological responses in children under 5 years with malaria and soil-transmitted helminth coinfections in Delta State, Nigeria: a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 269 children under 5 in Delta State, Nigeria. Co-infection most common in 1-2 year-olds (40.9%), more frequent in males. Hb AA predominated (57.3%), Hb AS 36.3%, Hb SS 6.3%. Malaria and soil-transmitted helminth infections caused macrocytic hypochromic pattern (increased MCV, reduced MCHC; p<0.001) despite mild haemoglobin reductions. Parasitaemia weakly correlated with haematological indices. Hb SS children showed greatest derangement. Authors conclude early red cell alterations precede anaemia and are influenced more by host factors than parasite burden.

articles.headline: 75% of pregnant Nigerian women miss IPTp-SP doses despite ANC visits
articles.title_original: Prevalence and inequalities of missed opportunities for intermittent preventive therapy (IPTp) of malaria among pregnant women: an analysis of 2018 Nigeria DHS.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 12,498 women from 2018 Nigeria DHS found 75% of women attending ≥4 antenatal care visits received <3 IPTp-SP doses. Prevalence of worst-case scenario (<4 ANC visits and <3 IPTp-SP doses) was highest among poorest (64.0%), least educated (61.6%), and rural women (50.4%) (all p<0.001). Education stronger than wealth in reducing missed opportunities (75% vs 5% risk comparing poorest-uneducated to wealthiest-educated).

articles.headline: Soil-transmitted helminth coinfection reduces malaria parasitemia in Mozambican children
articles.title_original: Helminth coinfections mitigate clinical, parasitological, and immune outcomes in Mozambican children with malaria.
articles.details: Among 74 malaria-symptomatic Mozambican children aged 2–10 years, 22% coinfected with STH vs 78% P. falciparum alone. STH coinfection associated with lower P. falciparum density (p=0.005), lower fever (p=0.047), reduced pro-inflammatory cytokines (IL-8, TNF, IFNα, IP-10, MCP1, MIG, MIP1β, GM-CSF p<0.05), and enhanced antibody responses to multiple P. falciparum antigens (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Community health volunteers expand malaria testing and treatment coverage in Kenya
articles.title_original: Community health volunteers improve access to malaria case management in Siaya County, Kenya.
articles.details: Analysis of 1.4 million encounters by 936 CHVs across 713 villages in Siaya County (2021–2023) found CHV coverage adequate for 82.5% of villages vs 30% for health facilities. CHVs managed 28.1% of encounters including 12.1% suspected malaria cases; CHVs recorded higher test positivity (82.0% vs 64.3% at facilities). Incorporating CHV data increased malaria incidence estimates by 18% (549 vs 467 per 1,000 population).

articles.headline: ITN use and IRS reduce under-five malaria risk by 86–88% in Zambia
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with malaria amongst under-five children in Senga Hill District, Northern Province, Zambia, a community-based cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Community cross-sectional survey of 216 under-five children in Senga Hill District (November 2022–August 2023) found malaria prevalence 31% (95% CI 26–38%). Correct ITN hanging reduced odds of malaria by 88% (OR 0.012); two ITNs plus IRS reduced odds by 86% (OR 0.14); IRS alone reduced odds by 58% (OR 0.42). Male sex increased odds by 204% (OR 3.04, p<0.05).

articles.headline: Ivermectin shows mosquito mortality but no consistent malaria reduction
articles.title_original: Efficacy of ivermectin for malaria vector control: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized clinical trials.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 10 RCTs (63,192 participants) found ivermectin increased mosquito mortality (RR 1.89, 95% CI 1.26–2.83, I²>97%) but showed no significant reduction in malaria prevalence with very wide confidence intervals. Ivermectin was well tolerated with no increase in adverse events. Authors conclude further large cluster-RCTs needed.

articles.headline: Guinea healthcare providers cite gaps in ITN distribution and SMC delays
articles.title_original: Healthcare providers' knowledge, perceptions and practices of malaria control strategies in the Republic of Guinea: insights from a qualitative study.
articles.details: Qualitative study of 63 interviews and 18 focus groups with healthcare providers across 9 Guinean districts (December 2022–March 2023) identified 45 subthemes. Providers reported high knowledge of malaria risk but cited gaps in ITN distribution, use and larval control; SMC started with delays; non-compliant case-management practices including overcharging; many patients self-medicate before seeking care.

articles.headline: 2La inversion in Anopheles gambiae shows south-north cline across Nigeria
articles.title_original: Spatial distribution of the 2La chromosomal inversion in Anopheles gambiae populations across Nigeria: ecological associations and implications for malaria control.
articles.details: PCR karyotyping of 399 An. gambiae s.s. from 12 Nigerian states found 2La/2La homokaryotype predominant in arid north (Sahelian/Sudan savannas), 2La+/2La+ dominant in humid south (forests/mangrove), heterokaryotype at moderate frequencies in transitional zones. Linear regression confirmed significant correlation between inversion frequency and latitude (R²=0.68–0.73, p<0.001). First nationwide evidence linking 2La polymorphism to ecological adaptation.
articles.notable_reason: First nationwide evidence linking vector genetics to ecological adaptation

articles.headline: Microfluidic device reveals flow-dependent effects on P. falciparum invasion
articles.title_original: Microfluidic platform for automatic quantification of malaria parasite invasion under physiological flow conditions.
articles.details: Custom microfluidic platform with post-capillary venule-sized channels and varying flow velocities quantified P. falciparum egress and invasion using automated video analysis. Deletion of erythrocyte binding antigen 175 (PfEBA175) significantly impacted invasion under flow (p<0.05) but not in static culture. Establishes flow conditions can critically affect parasite invasion in genotype-dependent manner.
articles.notable_reason: First demonstration that physiological flow critically affects parasite invasion

articles.headline: Poly(I:C) and R848 adjuvants induce antibody responses to liver-stage antigens
articles.title_original: Poly(I:C) and R848 adjuvants elicit sizeable humoral immunity to liver stage malaria antigens.
articles.details: BALB/c and C57BL/6 mice immunized with P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) and P. berghei liver-stage tryptophan-rich protein (PbSLTRiP) plus dual TLR adjuvants (Poly(I:C) and R848). Coadministered adjuvants evoked greater antibody responses than antigen alone; PfCSP-specific IgG remained detectable 80 days post-immunization. Limited statistical differences between adjuvant regimens, warranting further investigation.

articles.headline: Malaria-associated acute kidney injury drives mortality in endemic regions
articles.title_original: Malaria-Associated Acute Kidney Injury: A Key Driver of Mortality in Endemic Regions.
articles.details: Focused review of 2015–2025 literature on malaria-associated acute kidney injury (MAKI). MAKI results from intravascular hemolysis, microvascular obstruction, inflammation, endothelial dysfunction, and volume depletion. P. falciparum most common; P. vivax and P. knowlesi increasingly recognized. Associated with prolonged hospitalization, increased renal replacement therapy, higher mortality, and risk of incomplete recovery and chronic kidney disease.

articles.headline: School-aged children carry 66% asymptomatic P. falciparum parasitemia in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: School-aged children as a silent reservoir of Plasmodium falciparum: findings from a cross-sectional survey conducted in Burkina Faso in 2022.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1,127 children aged 6 months–10 years across three Burkinabe districts (July 2022) found asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence 28.0% by RDT and 31.9% by microscopy. Among school-aged children (5–10 years), 66.5% tested positive vs 21.6% under-five. Gametocyte carriage 3.9% overall, twice as high in school-aged children (67 vs 32 gametocytes/μL).
articles.notable_reason: Defines school-aged children as major asymptomatic malaria reservoir

articles.headline: P. knowlesi malaria affects forest workers in Aceh, Indonesia
articles.title_original: Socio-demographic characteristics and parasite density patterns of Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria in Aceh, Indonesia: insights from hospital-based data.
articles.details: Hospital-based retrospective study of 40 confirmed P. knowlesi cases in Aceh (January 2023–April 2024). Median age 31 years; 67.5% farmers/plantation workers, 10% orangutan caretakers. All presented with fever and headache; treated with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and primaquine; all recovered. Parasite density high in 42.9%, moderate in 45.7%, low in 11.4%. Median hospital stay 3 days.

articles.headline: PfVFT1 antibodies inhibit malaria through multiple immune mechanisms
articles.title_original: Vaccine-induced mouse antibodies targeting Plasmodium falciparum PfVFT antigen inhibit blood stages through multiple mechanisms.
articles.details: Screening of sera from protected and non-protected immunized volunteers identified PfVFT1 as a protective antigen. Vaccine-induced mouse anti-PfVFT1 antibodies inhibited parasite reinvasion into RBCs, promoted complement deposition inducing parasite lysis, and supported phagocytosis and antibody-dependent cellular inhibition.

articles.headline: Plasmodium kinases regulate male gametogenesis organelles
articles.title_original: Correction: Divergent Plasmodium kinases drive MTOC, kinetochore, and axoneme organisation in male gametogenesis.
articles.details: A study reveals how Plasmodium kinases regulate MTOC, axoneme, and kinetochore organization during male gametogenesis, providing insights into mechanisms of parasite transmission and identifying potential targets for malaria transmission control.

articles.headline: Over half of malarial children develop acute kidney injury
articles.title_original: A Study Using Point-of-Care Creatinine Testing in a Nigerian Primary Health Care Centre: Malaria as the Leading Cause of Acute Kidney Injury Particularly in Children.
articles.details: Point-of-care creatinine testing in a Nigerian primary care center identified AKI in 56.9% of pediatric patients (70/123, median age 5 years). Malaria was diagnosed in 69.9% of children, with 70% of AKI cases associated with malaria. AKI prevalence was highest in children under 5 years and declined to zero beyond age 12.

articles.headline: STAT1 deficiency improves survival in malaria-infected mice
articles.title_original: Role of STAT1 in modulating the host immune response to Plasmodium yoelii 17XL-infected murine blood-stage malaria.
articles.details: In Plasmodium yoelii 17XL-infected mice, genetic ablation of STAT1 enhanced survival rates and substantially reduced peripheral parasitemia and splenic parasite burden. STAT1 knockout decreased IFN-γ-producing CD8+ T cells and IL-10-producing CD4+ T cells at day 7, while hemoglobin and reticulocyte proportions increased in knockout mice, indicating STAT1 deficiency alleviates malarial anemia.

articles.headline: Malaria positivity rising in India's blood supply donations
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted malaria in India: A national survey of epidemiology and testing practices.
articles.details: A national survey of 262 Indian blood collection facilities found 9,275,688 donations screened from 2020-2024, with 1,231 confirmed malaria-positive donations (13.27 per 100,000 units). Annual positivity rose from 3.4 to 22.0 per 100,000 units. Most facilities (97.7%) used antigen RDTs for screening, but only 3.1% reported transfusion-transmitted malaria cases.

articles.headline: PfAP2-V master regulator controls malaria parasite virulence
articles.title_original: The DNA-binding protein PfAP2-V regulates erythrocyte invasion and pathogenesis of the human malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: PfAP2-V, an ApiAP2 transcription factor, coordinates temporal expression of P. falciparum virulence genes during blood-stage development. Conditional knockdown of pfap2-v decreased intraerythrocytic proliferation, caused structural abnormalities in infected erythrocyte surface topology, and markedly reduced cytoadhesion to human endothelial receptors including CD36, ICAM-1, and PECAM-1/CD31.

articles.headline: Tanzania vector survey reveals species-specific mosquito behaviors
articles.title_original: Nationally representative programmatic surveillance of mosquito and human behaviours that influence human exposure to malaria transmission and the impact of vector control across Tanzania.
articles.details: A three-year nationally representative survey across 25 Tanzanian sentinel districts measured vector and human behaviors influencing malaria transmission. Anopheles arabiensis was widespread; An. arabiensis and An. gambiae were predominantly outdoor (64.9% and 58.4%), while An. funestus showed higher indoor rates (42.4%). Humans were most active outdoors 18:00-21:00, with adolescent males showing lower bednet usage.

articles.headline: RDT accuracy high but false positives common in adults
articles.title_original: The accuracy of recording malaria rapid diagnostic test (RDT) results in public health facilities in Benin; results from the MaCRA project.
articles.details: Assessment of 35,720 RDTs from 16 Benin health facilities found 94.3% agreement between healthcare workers and reference panel (Cohen's kappa=0.88). False positives (5.0%) were more common than false negatives (0.7%). Accuracy was highest in children under 5 years (96.7%) and lowest in patients over 15 years (91.6%), with experience and facility infrastructure improving performance.

articles.headline: Microscopy misses majority of submicroscopic malaria infections
articles.title_original: Microscopy underestimates submicroscopic malaria infections in Ethiopia's southern rift valley: a community-based cross-sectional study.
articles.details: In a cross-sectional survey of 4,745 participants in Ethiopia's Gamo Zone, microscopy detected malaria in 2.7% (95% CI: 2.2-3.2%). Nested PCR of 1,183 samples revealed submicroscopic infections in 6.1% of microscopy-negative samples and PCR-corrected prevalence of 1.4%. Species misclassification was common: 19.5% of P. falciparum identified as P. vivax, 14.0% of P. vivax as P. falciparum.

articles.headline: VPS13L1 lipid transfer protein essential for parasite development
articles.title_original: A bridge-like lipid transfer protein is critical for generation of invasive stages in malaria parasites.
articles.details: PfVPS13L1, a bridge-like lipid transfer protein, bridges the endoplasmic reticulum to the nascent inner membrane complex (IMC) during P. falciparum schizogony. Loss-of-function of PfVPS13L1 reduced IMC growth and resulted in smaller anucleated progeny, impairing schizogony. This identifies VPS13L1 as critical for formation of apicomplexan invasive stages through mediating bulk lipid transfer from ER to growing IMC.

articles.headline: Fungicide exposure increases chronic avian malaria reactivation
articles.title_original: Chronic exposure to a common azole fungicide is associated with reactivation of chronic Plasmodium infections in farmland birds.
articles.details: House sparrows chronically infected with malaria (70% at study start) were exposed to tebuconazole fungicide for 11 weeks during winter. While malaria prevalence declined in control birds as typically expected, it increased by 20% in tebuconazole-exposed birds. Parasite loads were similar between groups, suggesting tebuconazole promoted parasite reactivation from exo-erythrocytic stages rather than enhanced replication.

articles.headline: P. vivax liver-stage antigen elicits natural antibody response
articles.title_original: Naturally acquired antibodies to Plasmodium vivax Pv_LISP-2, a potential liver stage vaccine antigen.
articles.details: This first study of naturally acquired antibodies against Pv_LISP-2, a P. vivax liver-stage protein, demonstrated it is antigenic in P. vivax-exposed individuals from the Brazilian Amazon. Acute-phase responses were characterized by high IgM followed by IgG, predominantly IgG1 and IgG3 subclasses. IgM responses correlated with parasitemia and symptom duration but showed negative association with hemoglobin and hematocrit.

articles.headline: LLIN ownership high in Ethiopia, but utilization remains below target
articles.title_original: Assessment of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Net (LLIN) Ownership, Utilization, and Associated Barriers in Malaria-Endemic Communities of Ethiopia.
articles.details: Community-based survey of 9,222 households in Ethiopia (Oct 2024–Jan 2025). LLIN ownership 71.5%, sufficient nets for every 2 people 58.3%. Overall utilization 59.9% (rural 72.7% vs urban lower); pregnant women 78.5%, children <5 67.2%. Major barriers: 60.5% lacked confidence in hanging nets, 64.1% believed malaria seasonal. Utilization below 80% threshold needed for community-wide protection; recommend skill-based interventions and communication.

articles.headline: P. falciparum resistance markers increase in Southern Senegal under chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Emergence of Plasmodium falciparum pfdhps A581G mutation in Southern Senegal under Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention pressure, 2020-2023.
articles.details: Analysis of blood samples from two Senegal SMC districts (2020–2023) using targeted amplicon deep sequencing. pfdhfr triple mutant near-fixed (>90%); pfdhps A437G common (47–100%); A581G mutation emerged in 2022 (8.1% Kolda) and rose to 14.9% Kolda and 6.5% Kédougou by 2023. Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine regimen remains clinically effective; rising A581G frequency warrants continued monitoring.

articles.headline: Imported malaria affects young male labor migrants in Nepal from Mumbai
articles.title_original: Understanding imported malaria in western Nepal: Implications for malaria control and elimination.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 113 imported malaria cases in Nepal (Nov 2024–Jan 2025). All male, mean age 20–29 years (46%), predominantly Muslim (84%), working in catering (71.4%). Nearly all (99.1%) traveled to India for work, primarily South Mumbai (95.6%). 85.8% lacked fixed housing; only 56.6% received malaria information before travel; 59.3% unwilling to use bed nets due to unsuitable living conditions. Study highlights concentrated risk in specific urban hotspots.

articles.headline: Anopheles arabiensis survives on wild hosts in natural ecosystems of Tanzania
articles.title_original: Blood host preferences and competitive inter-species dynamics within an African malaria vector species complex inferred from signs of animal activity around aquatic larval habitats.
articles.details: Survey of aquatic habitats across ecosystem gradient in southern Tanzania. An. arabiensis proportions positively associated with human/cattle activity; An. quadriannulatus dominated intact ecosystems and associated with impala, warthog, bushpig as blood hosts. An. arabiensis persisted >40 km inside Nyerere National Park, suggesting dependence on unidentified wild species. Self-sustaining refuge populations in conservation areas may confound elimination efforts but could inform pyrethroid resistance management strategies.

articles.headline: Only very low CYP2D6 activity increases primaquine relapse risk in vivax
articles.title_original: Reduced cytochrome P-450 (CYP) 2D6 activity and Plasmodium vivax malaria risk in Amazonians: A retrospective, population-based cohort study.
articles.details: Retrospective cohort of 466 P. vivax patients in Brazil treated with chloroquine plus primaquine (3.5 mg/kg). Those with severely reduced CYP2D6 activity (AS ≤0.25) had 2.22-fold increased recurrence risk vs normal (p=0.004); intermediate activity (AS 0.5–1.0) showed no increased risk. Community-wide incidence over 5 years (997 exposed individuals, 26.6 cases/100 person-years) did not differ by CYP2D6 level. Severe deficiency requires alternative relapse suppression.

articles.headline: Federated Vision Transformers achieve 98% accuracy for automated malaria detection
articles.title_original: FedMal-XAI: an explainable federated vision transformer leveraging knowledge distillation for privacy-preserving malaria detection.
articles.details: Development of privacy-preserving federated learning system using Vision Transformers for malaria detection from blood smear images. Federated ViT models achieved 98.15% accuracy (ViT-B/16), 97.79% (FedDistill-DeiT), 97.75% (FedAvg-Swin-T), with ROC-AUC 0.9977 (FedDistill-Swin-T), outperforming CNN ensemble (ResNet50+VGG16). System enables cooperative training across decentralized institutions without sharing sensitive data while providing interpretable diagnostic results.

articles.headline: Heat shock protein Gp96 reviewed as malaria biomarker and drug target
articles.title_original: Heat shock protein Gp96 (Grp94) in malaria: functional insights at the host-parasite interface and therapeutic perspectives.
articles.details: Review of Gp96 and PfGp96 in malaria pathogenesis. Host Gp96 released as DAMP under cellular stress; elevated levels associated with severe malaria phenotypes. P. falciparum expresses PfGp96, an ER-resident Hsp90 homolog with parasite-specific adaptations. Elevated circulating Gp96 supports potential value as biomarker of host stress. PfGp96 offers potential drug target, but biological functions, client repertoire, and essentiality remain poorly defined; direct validation evidence currently limited.

articles.headline: PfNCR1 HLH domain enables cholesterol transport at parasite-host interface
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Niemann-Pick Type C1-Related protein relies on its physicochemical properties for membrane contact site localization required for cholesterol homeostasis.
articles.details: Mechanistic study of PfNCR1 localization to narrow (~3–4 nm) membrane contact sites at parasite-RBC interface. Identified unique 141-amino-acid helix-linker-helix (HLH) domain required for efficient localization; localization depends on HLH physicochemical properties. Isolated HLH domain sufficient for targeting. PfNCR1 localization to contact sites qualitatively correlates with cholesterol homeostasis maintenance, establishing functional link between membrane localization and transport activity.

articles.headline: P. vivax genetic diversity high in Ethiopian sugarcane plantation workers
articles.title_original: High genetic diversity, clonal activation of hypnozoites and relapse of Plasmodium vivax isolates in low-transmission setting of Ethiopia.
articles.details: Amplicon deep sequencing of pvmsp1 in 370 blood samples from 215 individuals in southwestern Ethiopia. Found 67 unique haplotypes with high population genetic diversity (Hd=0.799, π=0.044) but low MOI (1.4) and 34.6% polyclonal infections. Among 82 patients with recurrences, 81.3% of primary-recurrent pairs showed genetic homology with 55.6% sharing identical alleles, indicating relapses. High genetic diversity suggests introduction of diverse parasite genotypes by migrant workers.

articles.headline: qPCR reveals 31% asymptomatic and 17% submicroscopic malaria in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Uncovering silent malaria reservoirs in Southern Ethiopia: community-based molecular detection of asymptomatic and sub-microscopic infections and their implications for diagnosis and control.
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional survey of 525 participants in Ethiopia (Sept–Dec 2024) using microscopy, RDTs, and qPCR. qPCR detected substantially higher asymptomatic prevalence (31.2%) compared to microscopy (17.5%) and RDTs (13.1%). Sub-microscopic infections in 16.6% (72/433): 7.9% P. vivax, 7.2% P. falciparum, 1.6% mixed. More common in ages 15–24 and females. Findings highlight limitations of symptom-based strategies and underscore need for sensitive diagnostics.

articles.headline: Pfmdr1 Y184F mutation surge from 66% to 91% in Ethiopian P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Incidence of kelch13 and Pfmdr1 gene mutations associated with antimalarial drug resistance in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Ethiopia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 studies (11,269 samples, 5,521 P. falciparum cases) from Ethiopia. Pooled kelch13 prevalence: R622I 11%, A675V 1%, P441L 0%. Pfmdr1 prevalence: Y184F 77%, D1246Y 6%. Y184F increased dramatically from 66% (2007–2014) to 91% (2015–2025). Most resistance markers remain rare, but dramatic rise in Y184F frequency over decade indicates growing concern regarding antimalarial drug resistance.

articles.headline: Cameroon malaria vaccine introduction achieved 56% first-dose coverage amid high dropout
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the first malaria vaccine introduction: ongoing learning opportunities during the introduction into routine immunisation in Cameroon.
articles.details: Mini-post-introduction evaluation (mini-PIE) of malaria vaccine introduction across 42 health districts in Cameroon (April 2024). Dose 1 coverage 56%, dose 2 coverage 31%, dose 3 coverage 28%; all regions showed high drop-out rates. Among 48 caregivers, 88% aware of vaccine (60% informed by health workers), 81% cited vaccine protection as motivation, 60% understood importance of continuing bed net use. Monthly reporting completeness >95% except 2 regions at 87%.

articles.headline: Zoonotic and human malaria impose substantial household costs in Indonesia
articles.title_original: The household economic burden of human-only and zoonotic malaria, compared to other causes of acute febrile illness in Indonesia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 2,244 patients in Indonesian health clinics (January 2022-October 2023) identified 153 malaria cases (6.8%) with multiple Plasmodium species (P. vivax, P. knowlesi, P. malariae, P. falciparum, P. ovale). Mean household cost US$33 for malaria episodes versus US$17 for non-malarial fever, with costs driven mainly by indirect losses from lost productivity (70% and 61% respectively). Catastrophic health expenditure occurred in 16% of malaria patients despite subsidized care.

articles.headline: Household occupation-activity interaction may modulate malaria infection risk
articles.title_original: The Effect of Head of Household Occupation and Activity at Sunrise and Sunset on Malaria Prevalence in Sussundenga, Mozambique.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 297 individuals in 84 Mozambique households examined occupational and behavioral associations with malaria. Main effects were not significant: outdoor vs indoor household occupation difference 0.015 (95% CI -0.12–0.15), and dawn/dusk activity difference 0.10 (95% CI -0.063–0.26). Interaction model showed those active at sunrise/sunset with outdoor-occupation heads had 0.33 higher predicted malaria prevalence (95% CI 0.037–0.62), suggesting combined exposure may increase transmission risk.

articles.headline: An. darlingi genomics reveal population structure and insecticide selection
articles.title_original: Population genomics of Anopheles darlingi, the principal South American malaria vector mosquito.
articles.details: Analysis of 1,094 whole genomes of An. darlingi from six South American countries documented deep geographic population structure, high genetic diversity with 13 segregating inversions, and no evidence for sympatric cryptic taxa. Strong selection signals plausibly driven by insecticides, especially on cytochrome P450 genes, indicating ongoing pressure from vector control efforts.

articles.headline: Plasmodium TRAg7-CD71 interaction drives inflammation via macrophage signaling
articles.title_original: Interaction of Plasmodium yoelii tryptophan-rich antigen 7 with CD71 on macrophage membrane regulates host inflammatory response.
articles.details: Py TRAg7 protein interacts with macrophage CD71 receptor and activates NF-κB p65 signaling, promoting proinflammatory factor production. Genetic deletion of TRAg7 decreased parasitemia, reduced inflammation, and improved host survival. Findings show parasite-exported proteins regulate host inflammatory responses via macrophage receptors, suggesting potential therapeutic intervention strategies targeting this mechanism.

articles.headline: SNARE proteins mediate Plasmodium nutrient acquisition via lysosome fusion
articles.title_original: Host SNARE Proteins Mediate Lysosome and PVM Fusion to Support Plasmodium Liver Infection.
articles.details: CRISPR-based in vitro study identified endolysosomal SNAREs (VAMP7, VAMP8, Vti1B, Stx7) recruited to parasitophorous vacuole membrane. VAMP7 and Vti1B localized within 30 min post-infection; VAMP8 and Stx7 appeared at 24h. Double knockouts of VAMP7-Vti1B or VAMP7-VAMP8 significantly reduced parasite infection with Vti1B playing dominant role; SNARE depletion impaired lysosome-PVM fusion and nutrient acquisition.

articles.headline: G6PD deficiency prevalence heterogeneous across Indonesia; screening recommended
articles.title_original: G6PD deficiency in Indonesia: a systematic review and update of prevalence and variant maps in the context of malaria elimination.
articles.details: Systematic review of 45 studies (1964–2024) documented G6PD deficiency prevalence of 0.0–19.9% in males (n=23,166) and 0.8–44.6% in females (n=6,729) across Indonesia, with 15 class B variants identified. Significant geographic heterogeneity and data gaps noted in high P. vivax endemic areas. Authors recommend routine G6PD screening to guide high-dose primaquine (1 mg/kg/day×7 days) treatment for P. vivax elimination.

articles.headline: Urban vector survey identifies irrigation areas as transmission hotspots
articles.title_original: Urban malaria vector dynamics in Accra, Ghana.
articles.details: Entomological survey in Accra, Ghana (2022–2024) across 15 sites collected 20,945 host-seeking and 1,613 resting An. gambiae s.l. mosquitoes. Abundance was highest in irrigation and peri-urban sites and lowest in low socioeconomic areas. An. gambiae s.s. dominated host-seeking populations while An. coluzzii dominated resting collections.

articles.headline: IL-10 deletion enhances early parasite control via non-canonical mechanisms
articles.title_original: Early enhanced control of Plasmodium yoelii infection in IL-10-deficient mice is independent of IFN-γ, IL-12, and the humoral response.
articles.details: Murine study of P. yoelii infection in IL-10-deficient mice showed enhanced parasite control despite similar or higher IFN-γ and IL-12 production. Loss of IL-10 reduced B-cell germinal center formation, but B cells were not critical for early control. Even Il10−/− mice lacking both IFN-γ and B cells controlled parasites, indicating IL-10 suppresses host-protective pathways beyond TH1 and humoral responses.

articles.headline: R21 catch-up campaigns and boosters could supplement routine vaccination
articles.title_original: Public health impact of catch-up vaccination or additional booster doses with pre-erythrocytic malaria vaccine R21/Matrix-M: a modelling study.
articles.details: Stochastic transmission model of P. falciparum estimated impact of R21/Matrix-M catch-up campaigns targeting children 6 months–2 years averted 358 (95% CrI 113–570) uncomplicated cases per 1,000 doses at 45% PfPR2-10. Extra boosters 5–10 years post-primary series provided additional benefits particularly in moderate-to-high transmission, with per-dose impact approaching routine vaccination. Conclusions dependent on equal immunogenicity assumption in older children.

articles.headline: India's malaria outbreak frequency declining; seven states remain high-risk
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria outbreak years in India provides key insights into malaria elimination.
articles.details: Analysis of district-level malaria outbreak years in India identified 402 outbreak events over the past decade with peaks in 2011, 2014, and 2015 followed by sharp decline through 2020 (lowest on record). Seven states (Mizoram, Tripura, Jharkhand, Odisha, Madhya Pradesh, Uttar Pradesh, Meghalaya) identified as outbreak-prone with >70% of districts experiencing ≥1 outbreak year during 2011–2020.

articles.headline: Macrophage depletion or SR-A blockade enhances transmission-blocking antibody efficacy
articles.title_original: Host macrophages/monocytes promote malaria transmission by modulating mosquito microbiota via SR-A-mediated phagocytosis.
articles.details: Study in rodent and human systems demonstrated macrophages/monocytes promote malaria transmission by suppressing transmission-blocking bacteria (E. anophelis, S. sonnei, K. oxytoca) via SR-A-mediated phagocytosis. Anti-SR-A neutralizing antibodies enhanced anti-Pfs25 monoclonal antibody efficacy and achieved complete transmission blockade of Pfs25-transgenic P. berghei. Findings reveal parasite strategy to eliminate transmission-blocking bacteria and suggest anti-SR-A antibodies could improve transmission control.

articles.headline: Malaria-dengue co-infection detected at 4.3% prevalence in Papua
articles.title_original: Co-infections of malaria and dengue in Timika, a highly endemic malaria area in Central Papua, Indonesia.
articles.details: Screening of 69 microscopy-confirmed malaria patients in Timika, Central Papua (2022) identified 3 co-infections (4.3%): 1 with DENV-2 and 2 with DENV-4. Malaria species included P. vivax, P. falciparum, and P. malariae. DENV-4 sequencing revealed Genotype II closely related to Makassar strains, indicating local virus circulation and highlighting diagnostic challenges from overlapping symptoms.

articles.headline: Transportability models generalize mass drug administration effects to non-trial populations
articles.title_original: Mapping the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria using transportability methods.
articles.details: Cluster RCT in Senegal found MDA reduced malaria incidence by 55% in seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) areas. Using transportability models with machine learning, researchers generalized trial effects to 116 non-trial communes accounting for weather, vegetation, and population density differences. Estimated incidence reductions of 36% to 65% across non-trial communes, with larger decreases in areas with higher precipitation, denser vegetation, and lower temperatures. MDA showed no effectiveness in the post-intervention year.

articles.headline: Dorema aucheri plant extract shows larvicidal and repellent activity against A. stephensi
articles.title_original: Eco-friendly mosquito control: Assessing the larvicidal and repellent effects of Dorema aucheri extract on Anopheles stephensi, the malaria vector.
articles.details: Laboratory study tested hydroalcoholic extracts of Dorema aucheri (green and white aerial sections) against Anopheles stephensi larvae using WHO bioassay guidelines. Green section extract achieved LC₅₀ of 98.54 mg/L and 98% mortality at 400 mg/L; white section LC₅₀ was 104.72 mg/L. Repellency assays showed 90% repellency for green section and 85% for white section at 0.5 mg/cm². Complete protection time with 5% doses was 64 minutes for green fraction and 52 minutes for white.

articles.headline: Behavioral factors fundamentally mediate malaria intervention uptake and effectiveness
articles.title_original: Investigating the impact of human behavioural factors on the effectiveness of malaria interventions in malaria-endemic regions: A systematic review.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Systematic review of 59 studies (2000–2025) from malaria-endemic regions, predominantly sub-Saharan Africa (83%) using cross-sectional designs (63%). Key behavioral barriers to ITN use included discomfort and misconceptions. Healthcare-seeking delays resulted from cost, access limitations, and trust issues; distrust diverted patients to informal providers. Protective factors included knowledge, perceived severity, and affordability; risk factors included discomfort, health system constraints, and perceived ineffectiveness. Authors conclude malaria intervention effectiveness is fundamentally contingent on human behavior.

articles.headline: Anti-GPI scFv antibody prevents cerebral malaria lethality in mouse model
articles.title_original: Anti-GPI scFv as a Promising Tool for Intervention Against Cerebral Malaria in Mice.
articles.details: Preclinical study in C57BL/6J mice infected with Plasmodium berghei-ANKA treated with anti-GPI scFv on days 0, 3, and 6 post-infection versus PBS controls. Anti-GPI scFv prevented lethality in 71.4% of infected animals and promoted recovery from weight loss. Treatment inhibited neurological and systemic signs, reduced parasitemia, and improved hematological and histopathological parameters in brain, lungs, and kidneys compared to controls.

articles.headline: Educational frameworks for Plasmodium knowlesi prevention in Southeast Asia forest regions
articles.title_original: Educational frameworks for zoonotic malaria prevention: a narrative review integrating behavioural theory, participatory design, and instructional models for Plasmodium knowlesi in Southeast Asia.
articles.details: Narrative review synthesizing evidence on educational interventions for malaria prevention, focusing on P. knowlesi in Southeast Asia where outdoor-biting vectors and forest-fringe livelihoods dominate. Educational interventions consistently improved malaria knowledge and preventive practices across school, community, and household settings, with stronger effects when grounded in behavioral theory. Key drivers of residual P. knowlesi risk included forest-edge work, land-use change, social inequities, and persistent misconceptions. Proposes integrated framework using health behavior model (HBM), community-based participatory research, and instructional design (ADDIE, CTML) approaches.

articles.headline: Systematic review of epidemiological models for malaria in Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: A systematic review of epidemiological models for malaria transmission in Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: PRISMA-registered systematic review of 102 malaria modeling studies in sub-Saharan Africa (through December 2024). Transmission-focused models comprised 52.9% (54 articles), intervention models 21.6% (22 articles), optimal control models 9.8% (10 articles), and combined optimal control-cost-effectiveness models 15.7% (16 articles). Major gaps identified: limited incorporation of drug and insecticide resistance, migration dynamics, and climate variability. Authors recommend future models be better tailored to local transmission patterns, age-specific vulnerabilities, and programmatic needs.

articles.headline: Acute liver failure from Plasmodium falciparum in non-endemic country
articles.title_original: Acute Liver Failure Due to Plasmodium falciparum: A Rare Case from a Non-Endemic Country.
articles.authors: Batıbay E, Yüksekyayla O, Polat M
articles.details: Case report documenting acute liver failure due to Plasmodium falciparum occurring in a non-endemic country.

articles.headline: CLIP protein essential for Plasmodium merozoite and sporozoite invasion
articles.title_original: The CLAMP-Linked Invasion Protein (CLIP) plays an essential role in Plasmodium berghei zoites.
articles.details: CRISPR-based conditional deletion study in Plasmodium berghei identified CLIP (CLAMP-Linked Invasion Protein) as essential for host cell invasion. Deletion of clip impaired blood-stage parasite growth and prevented merozoite erythrocyte invasion. In transmission stages, sporozoite development proceeded normally but salivary gland invasion was dramatically reduced. CLIP-deficient sporozoites showed impaired cell traversal and hepatocyte invasion with defective gliding motility, phenocopying CLAMP-deficient parasites and supporting a conserved role for the CLAMP-CLIP-SPATR complex.

articles.headline: Plasmodium knowlesi genetic programs drive severity independent of parasite burden
articles.title_original: Integrative genomics of Plasmodium knowlesi reveals parasite-intrinsic regulators of severe human malaria.
articles.details: First genome-wide association study (GWAS), transcriptomic, and expression quantitative trait locus (eQTL) analysis from clinical P. knowlesi isolates. Severe disease (WHO criteria) associated with transcriptional activation of stress-response and host-interaction pathways; invasion programs more associated with parasite burden; immune-evasion pathways showed overlapping associations with both. GWAS and eQTL revealed genetic regulation of immune-evasion variant antigen families (SICAvar and kir) and chromatin-associated regulators. Gametocyte transcripts detectable across all infections including low-parasitemia cases, indicating transmission-stage expression independent of clinical severity or parasite density.

articles.headline: Historical analysis of sustainable malaria control in Palestine from 1922
articles.title_original: The Zionist decolonisation of rural malaria control in Palestine from 1922 onwards - what it teaches.
articles.details: Historical account of malaria control in Palestine following World War I. British Army suppressed disease in 1918 through mosquito breeding site destruction but ceased operations on September 19, 1918, after which disease rapidly returned. Zionists subsequently established the first sustainable malaria control program for the entire country, pioneering decolonized control approaches conducted by and for all inhabitants—later recognized as the first step in decolonizing malaria control globally.

articles.headline: AsOBP21f odorant-binding protein drives human odor detection in A. sinensis
articles.title_original: Functional characterization of AsOBP21f, an odorant-binding protein involved in human odor detection in the malaria vector Anopheles sinensis.
articles.details: Functional characterization of odorant-binding protein AsOBP21f from Anopheles sinensis, predominantly expressed in olfactory tissues (antennae, proboscis). AsOBP21f showed selectivity for hydrophobic odor molecules with C10–C15 carbon chains; strong binding affinities for methyl tridecanoate, dodecanal, decanal, and pentadecanoic acid. Behavioral assays showed dose-dependent effects: methyl tridecanoate attracted mosquitoes; dodecanal repelled them. RNAi-mediated silencing of AsOBP21f significantly reduced antennal electrophysiological response to host odors and markedly decreased blood-feeding success.

articles.headline: China-Africa network fosters malaria control collaboration and knowledge exchange
articles.title_original: The Institution-based Network on China-Africa Cooperation for Malaria Elimination: fostering a transcontinental hub for strengthening China-Africa cooperation on malaria control and elimination efforts.
articles.details: Description of the Institution-based Network on China-Africa Cooperation for Malaria Elimination (INCAM), established 2018. Between 2019 and 2025, INCAM convened six fora to promote adaptation of China's anti-malaria practices to African contexts, facilitating demand-driven research, innovative product development, and evidence-based policy advancement. Authors highlight WHO's 2021 certification of China as malaria-free and identify opportunities to leverage China's expertise, products, and elimination strategies. Call for stronger Public-Private-Academic Partnerships and enhanced capacity-building programs to sustain progress.

articles.headline: Malaria cases rising in Central Ethiopia since 2019
articles.title_original: A six years trend analysis of malaria prevalence in Central Ethiopia Region.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 2019-2024 surveillance data from 12 districts and 2 towns in Central Ethiopia found 86,604 Plasmodium positive cases (23.5%) among 368,490 suspected: 61% P. falciparum and 39% P. vivax. Cases showed increasing trend with seasonal peaks in May (13,811; 15.9%), autumn (27,266; 31.5%), and spring (25,555; 29.5%). Authors conclude malaria interventions should be sustained and scaled up.

articles.headline: Angiotensin II for severe malaria-induced septic shock
articles.title_original: Angiotensin II use in refractory septic shock due to Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title, appears to address clinical management of severe malaria complications.

articles.headline: Malaria prophylaxis
articles.title_original: Malaria Prophylaxis.
articles.authors: Stachler L, DeVos E, Samandari T
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title, appears to be educational or review content on malaria prevention.

articles.headline: PfHP1 phase separation drives malaria var gene silencing
articles.title_original: Phase separation of heterochromatin protein 1 regulates virulence gene expression in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Mechanistic study demonstrates PfHP1 undergoes phase separation in vitro and preferentially compacts AT-rich DNA; point mutations disrupting phase separation cause dispersed nuclear localization and derepression of up to 54 of 60 var genes. Fluorescence recovery after photobleaching confirms fluid condensate behavior in vivo; Hi-C analysis establishes PfHP1-mediated phase separation as critical mechanism for heterochromatin organization and var gene silencing.

articles.headline: Anopheles pseudopunctipennis oviposits at dusk in Mexico
articles.title_original: Daily pattern of oviposition and fecundity in Anopheles pseudopunctipennis Theobald, the main vector of malaria in Mexico.
articles.details: Insectary and semi-field study of An. pseudopunctipennis in Tapachula, Mexico found primary oviposition peak between 18:00-20:00. Nulliparous females ceased oviposition after 24:00; parous females continued laying eggs until 06:00 and produced more eggs than nulliparous females. Authors recommend pyrethroid insecticide application by ultra-low volume or thermal fogging during dusk-to-midnight period in areas with active transmission.

articles.headline: Malaria vector control trials lack standardized entomological design
articles.title_original: A systematic review of entomological outcomes and sampling approaches used in the evaluation of cluster randomised controlled trials for malaria vector control products.
articles.details: Systematic review of 62 cluster randomized controlled trials of malaria vector control (1992-2021; 74% in Africa) identified 13 different entomological outcomes and 12 sampling methods with considerable variation. 70% of trials had high design risk due to lack of power analyses and inadequate randomization of sampling points. Number of entomological clusters was the only significant predictor of outcome precision. Authors recommend standardization and clearer reporting guidelines.

articles.headline: Cultural history data on malaria in Hungary
articles.title_original: [Data for cultural history of malaria in Hungary].
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title, appears to be historical or archival dataset.

articles.headline: Indole peptidomimetics potent against drug-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Indole Peptidomimetics Show Potent and Selective Activity against Drug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Indole-based peptidomimetics were designed and synthesized against P. falciparum sensitive and resistant strains. Compounds LSPN954 and LSPN959 showed IC50 of 1.7 and 1.0 μM against sensitive 3D7 strain, with CC50 ≥100 μM on human cells (selectivity indices 59 and 95). Both retained efficacy against resistant strains (Dd2, K1, Dd2R_DSM265, 3D7R_MMV848), showed no cross-resistance, demonstrated slow-acting profile, and additive effects with artesunate.

articles.headline: Review of Plasmodium falciparum vaccine development progress
articles.title_original: Recent Advances, Bottlenecks, and Future Directions in Plasmodium falciparum Vaccine Development.
articles.details: Review summarizing malaria vaccine strategies and limitations. Notes 282 million malaria cases and 610,000 deaths in 2024, disproportionately affecting children under five and pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa. Discusses challenges including parasite's complex lifecycle, identification of protective antigens, and optimal vaccine formulations. Highlights emerging opportunities for future vaccine research.

articles.headline: RTS,S vaccine modeling predicts substantial burden reduction in Benin
articles.title_original: Assessing the potential impact of the RTS,S/ASO1 vaccine on malaria burden in Benin: a mathematical modelling approach.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study using EMOD parameterized with Benin data (2010-2023) evaluated RTS,S/AS01 vaccine impact in children under five via Expanded Programme on Immunisation. Nine scenarios tested different coverage levels and dose schedules. Scenario 9 with 90% coverage and two boosters achieved 9.8% parasite prevalence averted, 11.06% clinical cases averted, and 21.83% severe cases averted by 2030.

articles.headline: Placental Plasmodium falciparum malaria common in eastern Uganda
articles.title_original: Prevalence of placental Plasmodium falciparum malaria and associated factors among pregnant women in health facilities in Eastern Uganda.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 180 pregnant women in Mayuge district, Uganda found placental P. falciparum by microscopy in 11.67% (95% CI 7.71%-17.28%) and by qPCR in 23.89% (95% CI 18.19%-30.71%). Maternal age 15-19 years associated with increased odds (aOR 5.87, p=0.023). Absence of antenatal care visits significantly associated with increased odds (aOR 4.27, p=0.011). Authors conclude increased ANC attendance is needed.

articles.headline: Plasmodium ovale recurs frequently in asymptomatic Tanzanian adults
articles.title_original: Frequent Plasmodium ovale recurrence in coastal Tanzania.
articles.details: Prospective follow-up study in Bagamoyo, Tanzania of 74 asymptomatic individuals with P. ovale parasitemia by qPCR found 26% (19/74) initial infections persisted to enrollment (median 4 days). 55% (41/74) developed recurrent Po infection at median ten weeks [IQR 4w, 12w]; 9 experienced second recurrence. Microsatellite genotyping demonstrated heterologous recurrence; risk appeared elevated in dry season preceding long rainy season.

articles.headline: P. simium originated from host shift 1-2 centuries ago
articles.title_original: Genomic Insights Into Host Shifts Between Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium simium in Latin America.
articles.details: Screened 719 monkey samples from five Latin American countries, identifying 23 Plasmodium-positives. Whole-genome analysis of 4 samples with adequate sequencing data plus 19 P. simium and 405 P. vivax isolates revealed P. simium originated from a host jump approximately 1-2 centuries ago. P. vivax found in 4 monkeys (3 Colombian, 1 Brazilian). Genome scans identified positive selection in P. simium genes for host/vector interaction (PvRBP2a, PvRBP1b, PvCMRP1, PvPAT, Pvs47).

articles.headline: DHA-rich fish oil shows strain-dependent effects in malaria
articles.title_original: Differential Effects of DHA-Rich Fish Oil Supplementation on Intestinal and Pulmonary Alterations in Experimental Malaria Caused by Plasmodium berghei NK65.
articles.details: C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei NK65 received DHA-rich fish oil supplementation. DHA was associated with partial preservation of intestinal morphology (reduced intestinal shortening) but did not improve pulmonary outcomes. Findings highlight strain- and pathogenesis-dependent effects of omega-3 supplementation, emphasizing the importance of considering Plasmodium strain diversity in investigating nutritional interventions.

articles.headline: Rural Madagascar: 20% of hospitalized children have severe malaria
articles.title_original: Clinical Characteristics and Outcomes of Hospitalized Malaria Patients in Rural Madagascar.
articles.authors: Kasprowicz D, Korzeniewski K, Wilczyńska W
articles.details: Three-year retrospective study (2023-2025) of 134 hospitalized malaria patients in rural Madagascar (median age 15 years, 52.2% female). P. falciparum predominated (94.0%); 20.1% had severe malaria including 10.4% with cerebral malaria. Co-infections were frequent (52.2%), most commonly Schistosoma (14.2%) and typhoid (12.7%). Median hospital stay was 2 days; 12.7% had prolonged hospitalization (≥5 days), associated with cerebral malaria, high parasitemia (≥5%), transfusion, and age <15 years.

articles.headline: Mosquito biting rates peak with intermediate forest degradation
articles.title_original: Reframing vector control: land use and climate shape malaria risk in Amazonian communities.
articles.details: Human landing catches at 70 sites across 4 Amazonian ecoregions recorded 14,689 Nyssorhynchus darlingi bites across 384 events. Human biting rate (HBR) was highest in areas with intermediate forest degradation and was positively associated with temperature and negatively with humidity. Floodable vegetation was the strongest land cover predictor, especially at 500-m scale, with intrazonal variation exceeding interzonal differences.

articles.headline: Multiple malaria controls reduce severe malaria more than single
articles.title_original: Progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria among children in settings receiving different combinations of malaria control interventions in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 12 studies involving 9.3 million individuals. Multiple malaria control interventions simultaneously deployed significantly reduced severe malaria (OR=0.41; 95%CI 0.30-0.56; p<0.001), parasitemia (OR=0.52; 95%CI 0.38-0.70; p<0.001), and gametocytes (OR=0.41; 95%CI 0.28-0.60; p<0.001) compared to single or no intervention. No significant difference in progression from uncomplicated to severe malaria across different intervention combinations.

articles.headline: DENV-3 detected in Haiti through discarded malaria RDTs
articles.title_original: Characterization of arbovirus infections in patients within Haiti by screening discarded malaria rapid diagnostic test cassettes.
articles.details: Nucleic acids extracted from discarded malaria RDTs collected 2021-2023 from febrile patients in Haiti (Sud and Ouest Departments) were screened for arboviruses. 68 samples tested positive for DENV-3, all from 2023. NGS sequencing confirmed high sequence similarity to Caribbean sequences from 2022-2023. No alphaviruses (0/517) or Oropouche virus (0/293) detected.

articles.headline: Malaria patients show elevated liver markers and low cholesterol
articles.title_original: Malaria parasitemia and its association with liver function parameters, and lipid profile among malaria-infected adult patients in western Ethiopia: A comparative cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Comparative cross-sectional study of 302 participants (151 with P. falciparum malaria, 151 controls; 70.2% male). Malaria patients had parasitic densities: 30.46% low, 46.36% moderate, 23.18% high. Liver markers (AST, ALT, ALP, bilirubin) were significantly higher in malaria patients (all p<0.001), while total cholesterol, HDL, and LDL were significantly lower (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Computational design of bispecific antibody targeting Plasmodium erythrocytes
articles.title_original: Computational epitope discovery and in silico design of a bispecific antibody model (COMED hook) to block Plasmodium invasion into erythrocytes.
articles.details: In silico study identified 123 antigenic P. falciparum B-cell epitopes (58 conserved across species) and 92 human erythrocyte epitopes (12 selected as host-targeting domains). Two highly conserved Plasmodium epitopes (KHVETWTQRVQNM, LHSNFYIKRF) showed favorable predicted Fab binding. Designed COMED hook bispecific antibody model exhibited computationally compatible profiles. Results require experimental validation.

articles.headline: Flow cytometry method detects non-fluorescent liver-stage Plasmodium
articles.title_original: Flow cytometry-based evaluation of hepatic infection by non-fluorescent Plasmodium parasites.
articles.details: Novel flow cytometry method using HSP70 (parasite cytoplasm marker) and UIS4 (parasitophorous vacuole membrane marker) enables quantitative assessment of hepatic infection by non-fluorescent Plasmodium parasites. Provides rapid, cost-effective quantification of liver-stage parasites and intracellular development without requiring parasite-encoded fluorescent reporters, addressing technical challenges for reporter-free whole-sporozoite vaccine evaluation.

articles.headline: Thalassemia shows weak non-significant malaria protection trend
articles.title_original: Exploring the protective effects of thalassemia against malaria in Africa: a systematic review.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 8 studies found thalassemic individuals had lower odds (OR: 0.856) of developing malaria than non-thalassemic individuals, but the association was not statistically significant with substantial heterogeneity across studies. Mechanistically, alpha thalassemia reduces cytoadhesion and rosette formation, with favorable interactions observed with haptoglobin Hp2-1 phenotype.

articles.headline: Fosmidomycin dosing adequate by weight; clindamycin suboptimal in young children
articles.title_original: Population pharmacokinetics of fosmidomycin and clindamycin in combination with artesunate for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Gabonese children and adults.
articles.details: Population pharmacokinetics study of 40 P. falciparum patients (age 3-57 years) in Gabon receiving oral artesunate 2mg/kg, fosmidomycin 30mg/kg, and clindamycin 10mg/kg every 12 hours for 3 days. Weight-based dosing provided consistent fosmidomycin exposure across all age groups. Clindamycin exposure was substantially lower in children <12 years; dose simulations suggest increasing clindamycin to 12mg/kg in children <35kg would equalize exposure.

articles.headline: RDT shows 94.8% accuracy in rural Ghana field testing
articles.title_original: Evaluation of malaria rapid diagnostic test kit performance in a high-transmission setting: a facility-based cross-sectional study in rural Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 404 children under 5 in 6 Ghanaian health facilities (July 2024) comparing MERISCREEN Pf HRP-II Ag® RDT to microscopy. Sensitivity 98.1% (95%CI 92.6-99.7), specificity 92.6% (95%CI 88.9-95.2), NPV 99.1% (95%CI 96.6-99.8), PPV 82.4% (95%CI 75.8-87.5), overall accuracy 94.8%. Real-world performance was marginally lower than manufacturer-reported values.

articles.headline: Biocredit rapid tests outperform older diagnostic tests
articles.title_original: Evaluation of NxTek™ Eliminate Malaria-PF test and biocredit rapid diagnostic tests for detecting Plasmodium species in Southern Ethiopia.
articles.details: Health facility-based cross-sectional study in Ethiopia with 384 febrile subjects (September-November 2024). Blood samples tested with microscopy, RDTs, and qPCR. Biocredit Pf/Pv RDT showed sensitivity 97.9% and specificity 97.4% for P. falciparum versus microscopy, and sensitivity 94.5% and specificity 97.5% for P. vivax. Biocredit Pf/Pv also demonstrated sensitivity 95.5%, specificity 96.4% for P. falciparum versus qPCR reference. All Biocredit RDTs met WHO diagnostic criteria and outperformed SD Bioline RDT.

articles.headline: Libreville malaria shows shifting hotspots across neighborhoods
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal heterogeneity of urban malaria in Libreville, Gabon (2012-2023): neighborhood-level hotspot analysis for micro-stratified control.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 9,507 children (January 2012-December 2023) measuring P. falciparum prevalence by neighborhood in Libreville. In 2023, prevalence varied from 38.0% (95% CI 33.1-43.2) in urban areas to 50.9% (95% CI 47.9-54.0) in semi-rural areas. WHO endemicity classification shifted from mostly mesoendemic neighborhoods in 2015 to 41.5% hyperendemic by 2023. Spatiotemporal hotspot analysis identified significant clusters that changed location across four time periods, supporting micro-stratified control strategies.

articles.headline: Review: drug resistance challenges and new antimalarial candidates
articles.title_original: Advancing global malaria control: therapeutic strategies, drug discovery, and formulation innovations.
articles.authors: Sarma K, Sarma H, Deka J
articles.details: Review article discussing malaria control challenges and recent progress. Highlights artemisinin partial resistance driven by Pfkelch13 mutations and urgent need for new treatments. Summarizes Medicines for Malaria Venture pipeline candidates including Z439, KAF156, MMV371, MMV055/167, INE963, and GSK701. Notes improvements in diagnostics from microscopy to PCR and innovations in drug formulation for enhanced bioavailability and patient compliance.

articles.headline: Proposed microbiota-based control strategy for African malaria
articles.title_original: Reinventing malaria control: hierarchical intervention strategies for African settings based on the microbiota-barrier-innate immunity axis.
articles.details: Review proposing 'microbiota-barrier-innate immunity' axis hypothesis whereby Plasmodium infection disrupts gut microbiota, metabolic function, and intestinal barrier, leading to innate immune dysfunction. Proposes hierarchical three-tier intervention framework progressing from nutritional regulation to targeted microbial intervention to precision bioengineering for resource-limited African settings. Framework addresses challenges of drug resistance and vaccine shortages through integrated eco-immunological approach.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistant K13 P441L mutations found in Ethiopian resurgence
articles.title_original: Recurrent parasitemias with artemisinin partial resistance mutations during the 2024 Ethiopia malaria resurgence: a case series.
articles.details: Case series of 15 patients with recurrent P. falciparum malaria in Ethiopia during 2024 resurgence. Three patients (20%, 3/15) carried WHO-candidate artemisinin partial resistance marker K13 P441L. Genotyping revealed resistance markers to other antimalarial drugs were largely fixed in the population, with evidence of clonal expansion or lineage dominance. Findings indicate ACT resistance-associated markers within drug-pressured parasite populations during malaria resurgence.
articles.notable_reason: First K13 P441L detection in Ethiopia; threatens first-line ACT therapy

articles.headline: Study identifies 283 immunogenic P. vivax antigens from peptide array
articles.title_original: Comprehensive characterization of Plasmodium vivax antigens using a high-density peptide array.
articles.details: Comprehensive analysis using high-density peptide array with 4.2 million peptides covering entire P. vivax proteome. Identified 283 commonly immunogenic proteins in symptomatic individuals, including erythrocyte invasion proteins, nucleoporins, and uncharacterized proteins. Asymptomatic individuals showed unique antibody response pattern against PIR proteins potentially associated with protection against clinical vivax malaria. Data provides resource for developing new vaccine candidates and serological detection tools.
articles.notable_reason: First comprehensive P. vivax antigen map; enables vaccine development

articles.headline: Rare cerebral malaria case from P. vivax reported in Colombia
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria caused by Plasmodium vivax in an afrodescendant adult in Colombia: case report.
articles.details: Case report of 43-year-old woman from southwestern Pacific coast, Colombia with severe neurological symptoms and confirmed P. vivax infection. Treated with intravenous artesunate followed by chloroquine and primaquine, achieving full recovery. Case highlights that while P. falciparum is the typical cause, P. vivax can cause cerebral malaria through mechanical and inflammatory mechanisms with complex pathophysiology.

articles.headline: RDT performance varies by parasite density and transmission setting
articles.title_original: Field and laboratory evaluation of Abbott-Bioline™ Malaria Ag Pf/Pv RDT performance in a high-transmission setting: contrasting results with a low-endemic area.
articles.details: Laboratory and field evaluation of Abbott-Bioline™ RDT in Madagascar with 218 febrile patients (70.2% PCR prevalence). Against microscopy, Abbott-Bioline™ showed sensitivity 99.1% (95% CI 94.9-100) and specificity 93.7% (95% CI 87.4-97.4); against PCR, sensitivity dropped to 73.2% with specificity 98.5%. Laboratory testing showed inter-lot variability with detection failures between 97-373 parasites/µL. RDT performance is context-dependent and correlates with parasite density in high-transmission versus low-endemic settings.

articles.headline: Plants' production of antimalarial compound explained
articles.title_original: Botanical mystery solved: how plants make a crucial malaria drug.
articles.details: Title indicates research on plant biosynthesis of a crucial antimalarial drug compound, but no abstract provided.

articles.headline: Malaria coinfection increases nematode transmission through immune modulation
articles.title_original: Coinfection with malaria alters the fecundity and within-host persistence of an intestinal nematode.
articles.details: Experimental coinfection study in mice with Plasmodium yoelii and Heligmosomoides polygyrus nematode. Coinfected mice excreted significantly more worm eggs than single-infected controls despite similar worm biomass, reflecting plastic fecundity adjustment. Coinfection downregulated Th2 cytokines (reduced IL-4, IL-13, IL-5 and GATA-3+ CD4+ T cells); neutralizing IL-13 reproduced increased egg excretion. Extended patent period in coinfected mice resulted in higher cumulative eggs to day 99 post-infection, increasing environmental STH transmission reservoir.
articles.notable_reason: Demonstrates malaria-helminth interaction increases transmission; identifies mechanism

articles.headline: Review: purine antimetabolites as potential antimalarial strategy
articles.title_original: Targeting Plasmodium falciparum with purine antimetabolites as a therapeutic strategy.
articles.details: Review article discussing P. falciparum's absolute dependence on purine salvage pathway (lacks de novo synthesis) as a drug target opportunity amid rising resistance. Summarizes potential of purine analogs targeting this pathway, with focus on using Pf Equilibrative Nucleoside Transporters (Pf ENTs) as delivery routes for therapeutic agents. Notes no purine-based antimalarial has reached clinical use yet, but pathway offers compelling opportunity for new drug development.

articles.headline: K13 C580Y confirmed to confer artemisinin resistance in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Functional validation of the Plasmodium falciparum K13 C580Y mutation in recently collected Ethiopian isolates.
articles.details: Functional validation study using CRISPR-Cas9 to introduce K13 C580Y mutation into two recent Ethiopian P. falciparum clinical isolates. Ring-stage survival assays showed significantly elevated in vitro dihydroartemisinin (DHA) survival in edited parasites compared to isogenic controls. Establishes that C580Y confers artemisinin tolerance in contemporary Ethiopian parasite genetic backgrounds, providing first causal assessment of this mutation in recent African isolates.
articles.notable_reason: First K13 C580Y validation in Africa; confirms emerging resistance threat

articles.headline: Vivax serological markers show limited utility for predicting relapse risk
articles.title_original: Predicting risk of Plasmodium vivax microscopy-detected episodes using serological markers in patients with Plasmodium falciparum malaria: a multi-country diagnostic performance evaluation.
