articles.details: Multi-country diagnostic evaluation of P. vivax serological exposure markers (SEMs) in 244 P. falciparum patients from Ethiopia, Indonesia, and Bangladesh, with 22 developing vivax microscopy-detected episodes during 63-day follow-up. Initial SEM algorithm achieved 82% sensitivity but only 27% specificity; study-specific optimization improved specificity to 68% while reducing sensitivity to 68%, suggesting limited suitability for guiding universal radical cure decisions.

articles.headline: Wolbachia naturally infects Anopheles funestus at high prevalence in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Detection of supergroup B Wolbachia strains and their co-infection with Plasmodium falciparum in wild Anopheles funestus in southeastern Tanzania: implications for malaria control.
articles.details: Survey of 400 wild An. funestus mosquitoes from five villages in southeastern Tanzania (March–June 2024) detected Wolbachia in 46.5% by standard PCR and 70.8% by nested PCR. P. falciparum sporozoites were present in 0.8% (3/400), with only one mosquito co-infected with Wolbachia (0.3%). Sequencing identified detected strains as Wolbachia supergroup B, providing the first report of Wolbachia-P. falciparum co-infection in An. funestus in Tanzania.

articles.headline: Malaria care-seeking delays in African children range from 2% to 96%
articles.title_original: Delay in malaria care-seeking for children under five in Africa: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Amoadu M, Alhassan A, Odoom E
articles.details: Systematic review of delayed malaria care-seeking in sub-Saharan African children under 5 found prevalence ranging from 2–95.8% across studies. Delays were driven by financial constraints, long travel distances, reliance on traditional treatments, and perception of malaria as mild. Delayed care was associated with increased mortality, progression to severe malaria, and serious neurological and hematological complications.

articles.headline: Stochastic metapopulation model captures malaria dynamics in western Kenya
articles.title_original: Assessing the impact of climate and control interventions on spatio-temporal malaria dynamics using a stochastic metapopulation model.
articles.details: Process-based stochastic transmission model validated against 2008–2019 malaria data from western Kenya integrates immunity, infectivity, migration, climate variability, and intervention effects while accounting for host and vector mobility. The framework accurately captured subpopulation-level heterogeneity and is generalizable to other endemic regions for localized forecasting and intervention planning under future climate scenarios.

articles.headline: LL-37 antimicrobial peptide selectively targets Plasmodium-infected red blood cells
articles.title_original: LL-37 selectively targets Plasmodium-infected erythrocytes and exhibits antimalarial activity.
articles.details: Human antimicrobial peptide LL-37 exhibits activity against both drug-sensitive and drug-resistant P. falciparum parasites in vitro by selectively targeting infected RBCs through membrane disruption via phosphatidylserine externalization and cholesterol depletion. In mouse models, exogenous LL-37/CRAMP administration reduced parasitemia and improved survival, while CRAMP-deficient mice showed higher susceptibility. However, therapeutic administration after infection showed limited efficacy due to rapid in vivo peptide degradation.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria causes neurologic deficits in 15% of children at discharge
articles.title_original: Prevalence and risk factors of gross neurologic deficits in children after severe malaria: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 41 studies involving 11,635 children from sub-Saharan Africa and Asia found gross neurologic deficits (motor, movement, sensory, speech) at hospital discharge in 15.2% (95%CI 11.5–18.8%) of children with cerebral malaria, declining to 5.7% at 12-month follow-up, versus 2.4% in broader severe malaria. Prevalence was higher in sub-Saharan Africa (14.2%) than East Asia (2.3%). Risk factors included profound coma, coma ≥48 hours, multiple convulsions, hypoglycemia, and acute kidney injury.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria parasitemia increases splenic rupture risk after trauma
articles.title_original: Chronic Malaria Is Associated With Trauma-related Splenic Rupture Requiring Splenectomy.
articles.details: Among 33 patients undergoing trauma-related splenectomy in Timika, Papua, Indonesia (2015–2021), 87.9% (29/33) had PCR-detectable asymptomatic Plasmodium parasitemia (P. falciparum or P. vivax), compared to 38.6% (697/1,807) in a 2013 general population household survey (P < 0.0001), demonstrating more than a twofold increase in parasitemia prevalence among trauma patients requiring splenectomy.

articles.headline: 10.7% of malaria-negative Ugandan children test false-positive by RDT
articles.title_original: False-positive Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests are Prevalent Among Children Under 5 Years of Age in Uganda.
articles.details: Among 6,786 Ugandan children under 5 in the 2018–2019 Malaria Indicator Survey with paired mRDT and microscopy results, false-positive RDTs (mRDT+/microscopy−) occurred in 10.7% (849/6,786), strongly correlated with region-level transmission intensity. Prevalence was higher in children with recent fever (17.2%), recent antimalarial use (14.7%), or anemia (8.1%), and lower with recent antibiotics (-17.6%). A combined clinical/environmental/household model predicted false-positive RDTs with weighted AUC 0.79.

articles.headline: Duffy blood group polymorphism modulates P. vivax immunity in Amazonians
articles.title_original: How Duffy Blood Group (FY) Polymorphism and Age Modulate Vivax Malaria Risk at the Community Level: A Population-based Retrospective Cohort Study in the Amazon.
articles.details: Population-based retrospective cohort of 1,737 Amazonians with well-balanced FY alleles followed for 7,879 person-years showed heterogeneous P. vivax incidence (0–11 cases per person; 25.8/100 person-years). Fy-negativity remained a major resistance trait contributing to structural zeroes in incidence data. Differences in infection risk associated with Fya/Fyb polymorphism observed in young participants were attenuated with age, suggesting these polymorphisms modulate the rate of clinical immunity development.

articles.headline: Praziquantel shows modest in vivo antimalarial activity against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Praziquantel for Treatment of Plasmodium falciparum Infection in Asymptomatic Gabonese Adults: A Double-Blind Placebo-Controlled Randomized Trial.
articles.details: Double-blind RCT in 44 asymptomatic Gabonese adults with P. falciparum: 40 mg/kg praziquantel once daily for 3 days caused statistically significant hourly reduction in microscopically-determined parasitemia (β=−0.006, P<0.001) compared to placebo (β=−0.0013, P=0.3). However, parasite clearance by day 7 occurred in only 41% (9/22) of PZQ-treated versus 27% (6/22) placebo recipients, indicating moderate efficacy relative to first-line antimalarials.

articles.headline: Vivax vaccine candidates show extreme genetic diversity with immune selection
articles.title_original: Extreme Genetic Diversity and Signatures of Balancing Selection in Plasmodium vivax Blood-Stage Antigens.
articles.details: Analysis of 289 P. vivax whole genome sequences from endemic regions identified high genetic diversity and balancing selection signatures in vaccine candidates AMA1, CyRPA, RBP1a, and P41 across linear and 3D protein structures, consistent across geographic regions. In contrast, CSP, TRAP, and MSP antigens showed low-to-intermediate diversity. The Sal-1 vaccine strain had extremely low global frequencies, suggesting current vaccine formulations may have limited efficacy against circulating parasite variants.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine and G6PD testing estimated at 12.5M reais over 5 years
articles.title_original: [Expansion of the use of technologies to reduce cases of malaria due to Plasmodium vivax in the Brazilian Amazon: a budget impact analysis].
articles.details: Budget impact analysis for incorporating tafenoquine and G6PD quantitative point-of-care testing into Brazil's Unified National Health System for P. vivax malaria treatment in the Amazon region over 5 years. Incremental budget impact was estimated at BRL 12.5 million. The model predicted tafenoquine would reduce hospitalization costs by approximately 9% and recurrence costs by approximately 11%, though reagent costs and waste significantly affected the economic analysis.

articles.headline: Angola P. falciparum: no artemisinin resistance markers detected
articles.title_original: Molecular markers of antimalarial drug resistance in pfk13, pfmdr1, pfdhfr, and pfdhps genes of Plasmodium falciparum in a rural municipality in Cubal, Benguela Province, Angola (2022-2023).
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 139 P. falciparum infections in rural Cubal, Angola (2022–2023) sequenced for resistance markers. No validated pfk13 artemisinin resistance mutations detected; pfmdr1 Y184F present in 43%; pfdhfr triple mutation in 77.2%; pfdhps A437G nearly fixed at 98%. Among genotypes, 58.4% were partially resistant (IRNG) and 17.8% fully resistant (IRNGE). Authors conclude AL efficacy at risk and SP remains suitable for IPTp despite emergence of fully resistant haplotypes.

articles.headline: Peru: pfhrp2-negative parasites show distinct HUVEC adhesion
articles.title_original: Phenotypic and genotypic characterization of Plasmodium falciparum field isolates lacking pfhrp2 gene.
articles.details: Small in vitro characterization of 5 P. falciparum field isolates from Peruvian Amazon (3 pfhrp2 positive, 2 lacking pfhrp2) tested for cytoadherence and rosetting. Isolates with pfhrp2 positive pattern showed rosetting or CSA adhesion; those with pfhrp2 negative pattern showed adhesion to HUVEC cells. Gene expression was higher for pfhrp2 in positive isolates; all isolates maintained genomic stability during year-long culture. Authors note findings are preliminary, requiring verification in larger panels.

articles.headline: French Guiana: 60% seropositive for multiple parasites
articles.title_original: Unravelling complex interactions during Toxoplasma, Plasmodium, and Leishmania co-infections in French Guiana.
articles.details: Seroprevalence study of 253 patients and controls from Cayenne Hospital (2012–2022) assessing T. gondii, Plasmodium spp., and Leishmania spp. co-infections using serology and multiplex cytokine assays. Approximately 60% were seropositive for ≥2 pathogens; 2.4% had concomitant infections. Significant differences in IL-10, IL-17, IL-33, and IFN-α levels between single and co-infected patients. Authors emphasize high co-infection prevalence and importance for public health strategy development in endemic regions.

articles.headline: Iran: An. stephensi habitat declining under climate change
articles.title_original: Suitable ecological niches of invasive malaria vector under present and projected climatic conditions in South of Iran.
articles.details: MaxEnt modeling study using 72 An. stephensi occurrence points from Hormozgan Province, Iran under future climate scenarios. Model performance (AUC 0.765, TSS 0.519) predicted significant habitat suitability decline, especially in western and central regions; stable areas mainly in Bashagard County. Altitude and precipitation patterns significantly influence species distribution. Authors recommend adaptive vector control strategies and continuous monitoring integrated with climate projections for Iran.

articles.headline: Bangladesh: >90% malaria reduction, but 2030 elimination threatened
articles.title_original: Malaria in Bangladesh, 2000-2025: Navigating the Headwinds From Control to Elimination.
articles.authors: Mamun AA, Kayenat MSA, Islam KAMI
articles.details: Narrative review of Bangladesh malaria epidemiology (2000–2025) documenting >90% case reduction (2008–2020) via vector control, RDTs, and ACTs, with remaining burden concentrated in Chittagong Hill Tracts. P. falciparum remains dominant with emerging non-kelch13 artemisinin-partial resistance; P. vivax presents radical cure challenges amid G6PD deficiency. Additional barriers include outdoor-biting vectors, high cross-border mobility, and large asymptomatic reservoirs. Authors conclude 2030 elimination is attainable but requires granular, intelligence-driven strategies with genomic surveillance.

articles.headline: Healthcare workers' malaria vaccine readiness in Southwest Nigeria
articles.title_original: Healthcare workers' beliefs, readiness, and acceptance of malaria vaccine in Southwest Nigeria: addressing misconceptions and uptake barriers.
articles.details: Study of healthcare workers' beliefs, readiness, and acceptance of the malaria vaccine in Southwest Nigeria, examining misconceptions and barriers to uptake.

articles.headline: Uganda RTS,S vaccine costs $141M net over 5 years
articles.title_original: The Cost and Budget Impact of Malaria Vaccine Introduction in Uganda.
articles.details: Budget impact analysis of RTS,S/AS01 vaccine introduction in Uganda examining cost-effectiveness and treatment savings. Treatment costs for complicated malaria in children <5 decreased from $1.1 million to $297,465 annually post-vaccination. Annual vaccine rollout estimated at $29 million (24% of immunization budget, 3.5% of health budget). Over 5 years, total budget impact was $145.5 million with net cost of $141 million, ranging $106.3–$175.6 million at varying coverage levels. Authors recommend government prioritization and targeting high-transmission areas.

articles.headline: SP chemoprevention coverage drives dhps A581G resistance spread
articles.title_original: Modeling the Impact of Malaria Chemoprevention with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine on the spread of Plasmodium falciparum dhps A581G mutation.
articles.details: Deterministic age-structured mathematical model simulating SP chemoprevention (IPTp/IPTi/SMC/MDA) impact on dhps A581G resistance in settings where dhps K540E is near fixation. At 45% coverage, dhps A581G prevalence increased from 11% to 20.6% (three cycles) or 27.8% (five cycles). ANOVA showed chemoprevention coverage explained 58% of resistance spread variance (ηp²=0.58), treatment cycles 9.3%, and interaction 9.0%. Authors highlight trade-off between immediate public health gains and long-term resistance acceleration, emphasizing need for careful coverage planning and molecular surveillance.

articles.headline: Sudan R21 vaccine uptake only 36.7% despite positive attitudes
articles.title_original: Suboptimal uptake of the malaria vaccine in Sudan: what's behind the blinds?
articles.details: Cross-sectional mixed-methods study of 416 mothers in Sudan (Gedarif/Blue Nile States) assessing R21/Matrix-M vaccine uptake among 256 eligible children in 2024. Only 36.7% (95% CI 31.1–42.8%; n=94) received vaccine. Critical awareness gaps: 53.1% did not perceive malaria as significant, 57.7% were aware of vaccine, only 36.7% knew required doses, 25.7% knew starting age. FGDs and KIIs identified vaccine stockouts and geographic access barriers. Authors attribute low uptake to insufficient education and health system barriers, not vaccine hesitancy.

articles.headline: Hemozoin-associated proteins drive cerebral malaria barrier disruption
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum hemozoin-associated biomolecules induce brain endothelial cell barrier disruption in an in vitro model of cerebral malaria.
articles.details: In vitro mechanistic study using human brain microvascular endothelial cells identified that P. falciparum hemozoin-associated biomolecules (not hemozoin crystal alone) cause endothelial barrier disruption in cerebral malaria. Protease treatment inhibited barrier disruption, indicating protein involvement; nuclease treatment had no effect. This mechanism underlies the endothelial barrier loss and brain swelling characteristic of cerebral malaria pathogenesis. Authors identify a key molecular target with potential for novel therapeutic approaches to prevent or treat this major complication.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies hemozoin-associated protein mechanism in cerebral malaria pathogenesis

articles.headline: RTS,S fourth dose at 6-12 months post-primary provides flexibility
articles.title_original: Timing of the fourth dose of RTS,S/AS01 malaria vaccine in perennial settings: a modelling study.
articles.details: Individual-based stochastic modeling study (OpenMalaria) of RTS,S/AS01 fourth-dose timing across transmission settings. Primary series averted 73–93% of uncomplicated and 74–92% of severe malaria cases (at 20% baseline P. falciparum prevalence). Fourth dose at 6–12 months post-primary series averts additional 8–38% of uncomplicated and 9–36% of severe cases. Modeling reveals scheduling flexibility, particularly between 6–12 months post-third dose. Authors recommend 15–21 months of age for maximum case aversion while allowing linkage to existing health touchpoints.

articles.headline: Kenya: extreme weather reduces IPTp-SP uptake via ANC disruption
articles.title_original: Extreme weather events and malaria prevention in pregnancy: a mediation analysis of antenatal care and sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine use in two malaria-endemic counties of Kenya.
articles.details: Cross-sectional mediation analysis in Kisumu and Migori counties, Kenya examining extreme weather impact on IPTp-SP uptake among women who gave birth in preceding 24 months. Shock-exposed pregnancies had lower IPTp₃+ coverage (49.5% vs 58.8%). Extreme weather reduced ANC4+ attendance (coefficient -0.23, 95% CI -0.41 to -0.05), which predicted IPTp₃+ completion (1.71, 95% CI 1.42–2.00). Unadjusted total shock effect was -0.45 with 76.6% mediated through ANC. After adjustment, effects attenuated and became nonsignificant, suggesting socioeconomic vulnerabilities as confounders.

articles.headline: Ischemic stroke from dengue-malaria coinfection: rare synergistic injury
articles.title_original: Ischemic stroke during dengue-Plasmodium vivax coinfection in a young woman: a case report.
articles.details: Case report of 23-year-old woman from Chocó, Colombia with confirmed P. vivax malaria (8200 parasites/μL) and acute dengue (NS1+, IgM+/IgG−) who developed acute left ACA ischemic stroke with monoparesis. No traditional stroke risk factors. Treated with artesunate-primaquine and aspirin with improved neurologic function. Case highlights potential for P. vivax-dengue coinfection to cause stroke through synergistic endothelial injury.

articles.headline: P. falciparum PPCS heteromeric complex as pantothenate analog target
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium falciparum PPCS is a unique heteromeric complex with prokaryote-like activity and is a target of pantothenate analogs.
articles.details: Study demonstrated that P. falciparum expresses two PPCS proteins forming a unique heteromeric complex with prokaryote-like nucleotide specificity (uses CTP rather than ATP, unlike eukaryotic PPCS). Complex is essential for CoA biosynthesis, the second of five steps in the pathway. Complex is targeted by multiple antiplasmodial pantothenate analogs. Structural and functional characterization provides opportunities for designing selective inhibitors exploiting unique features of PfPPCS while avoiding human counterpart.

articles.headline: Stakeholder preferences for next-generation malaria prevention in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Improving malaria chemoprevention coverage in pregnancy: Surveying stakeholder preferences for new product profiles and community-delivery approaches across five African countries.
articles.details: Survey across five African countries (n=75 pregnant women, 53 CHWs, 75 clinicians, 51 policymakers) examined preferences for next-generation IPTp products to replace sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), which has suboptimal coverage and limited efficacy in high-resistance areas. All stakeholders prioritized safety across trimesters; discrete choice experiment favored products without food restrictions. Long-acting injectables with ≥3 months coverage and hybrid delivery models with CHWs recommended to enhance adherence and expand access.

articles.headline: Protocol for Nigeria pediatric malaria burden meta-analysis
articles.title_original: Burden, risk factors, and clinical outcomes of pediatric malaria in Nigeria: A systematic review and meta-analysis protocol.
articles.details: Protocol for planned systematic review and meta-analysis to estimate prevalence, associated factors, and clinical outcomes of laboratory-confirmed pediatric malaria in Nigeria. Will search six databases through June 2025, assess heterogeneity and publication bias using Newcastle-Ottawa Scale and Cochrane Risk of Bias 2.0, and provide pooled estimates with 95% confidence intervals stratified by age, sex, socioeconomic status, and geographic location. Aims to inform Nigeria's malaria elimination policy.

articles.headline: Mitochondrial gene expression differs in severe P. falciparum disease
articles.title_original: Differential expression of mitochondria-associated genes in clinical samples of Plasmodium falciparum showing severe manifestations.
articles.details: Study profiled mitochondrial sense and antisense transcripts in 22 P. falciparum clinical isolates (uncomplicated malaria n=6, cerebral malaria n=4, hepatic dysfunction n=12) using strand-specific microarray and RNA-seq. Severe manifestations showed upregulation of tricarboxylic acid cycle and electron transport genes, indicating disrupted mitochondrial bioenergetics. Natural antisense transcripts showed correlated expression patterns in both severe manifestations, nominating them as potential non-conventional antimalarial targets.

articles.headline: HLA coverage of P. falciparum epitopes for multi-epitope vaccine design
articles.title_original: Population HLA coverage of experimentally characterized P. falciparum CD8 (+) T cell epitopes as potential components of a multi-epitope malaria vaccine construct: an in silico insight.
articles.details: In silico analysis evaluated 16 experimentally validated CD8+ T cell epitopes from three P. falciparum antigens (CSP, AMA1, TRAP) for conservation and HLA binding across 14 geographic subregions and 78 countries. CSP epitope IQNSLSTEW demonstrated highest global coverage (>85%, 93.35% in West Africa); others showed 55–100% conservancy across 16 P. falciparum strains. Combining conserved epitopes with broad HLA coverage could promote wide immune recognition while limiting strain-specific evasion for effective multi-epitope vaccine development.

articles.headline: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis following P. falciparum: novel post-malarial syndrome
articles.title_original: Anti-NMDAR encephalitis following malaria: expanding the spectrum of post-malarial neurological syndromes.
articles.details: Case report of 40-year-old man who developed anti-NMDAR encephalitis 11 days after recovery from P. falciparum malaria. Despite confirmed parasite clearance, presented with seizures, altered consciousness, and acute psychosis. Anti-NMDAR antibodies detected in CSF via immunofluorescence and cell-based assay with no concurrent infection or malignancy. Rapid improvement with first-line immunotherapy. Reported as first antibody-confirmed case following P. falciparum infection, expanding spectrum of post-malarial neurological syndromes.

articles.headline: Cytokine profiles associated with malaria infection in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Cytokine-Driven Immune Phenotypes at Delivery as Indicators of Malaria Infection Among Primigravidae in Burkina Faso: An Exploratory Analysis.
articles.details: Exploratory analysis of 33 primigravid women (mean age 19 years) in Burkina Faso assessed plasma cytokines (IL-4, IL-10, IL-6, TNF-α, IFN-γ) and antibodies to P. falciparum antigens (PfCSP, PfAMA-1, EBA-175) at delivery. Principal component analysis identified three distinct immune clusters: low-inflammatory (reduced IL-6/TNF-α), TNF-α-dominant, and highly pro-inflammatory (elevated IL-6/TNF-α). All women in most inflammatory cluster were P. falciparum-positive at delivery (p=0.04, exploratory association).

articles.headline: Deep learning achieves 97% accuracy for automated malaria detection
articles.title_original: Automated Malaria Ring Form Classification in Blood Smear Images Using Ensemble Parallel Neural Networks.
articles.details: Study developed parallel neural network combining convolutional neural network for morphological feature extraction with multi-head self-attention for spatial relationships to classify malaria ring forms in thin blood smear images. Evaluated on balanced Kaggle dataset of 27,558 erythrocyte crops using 10-fold cross-validation and independent test set. Model #1 achieved 0.9567 mean cross-validation accuracy and 0.97 accuracy (macro F1=0.97) on independent test. Authors note clinical use limited pending external validation and application to non-segmented images.

articles.headline: Intravenous drug abuse as risk factor for P. falciparum and HIV
articles.title_original: Intravenous drug abuse - a concern for falciparum malaria along with HIV.
articles.details: Title indicates IV drug abuse is a concern as risk factor for P. falciparum malaria coinfection with HIV. No abstract available; unable to provide quantitative results or detailed findings from the article.

articles.headline: Community innovations improve malaria access in conflict-affected Cameroon
articles.title_original: Co-creation of community-based innovations to improve access to malaria treatment in conflict-affected regions of Cameroon.
articles.details: Operational research using participatory co-creation workshop (October 2021) in South West and Littoral regions of Cameroon with government health services, IDPs, and CHWs to address malaria care barriers. Three innovations co-created: Community Health Participatory Approach (CoHPA) for inclusive community dialogue, Health Voucher System covering testing/treatment/transport, and Supportive Supervision Model for CHWs. Interventions demonstrated potential in pilot communities but concerns remain about scalability, sustainability, and integration into national health systems without continued support.

articles.headline: Ethiopia malaria resurgence driven by systemic and operational gaps
articles.title_original: Resurgence of malaria in Ethiopia: a national landscape analysis of systemic and operational gaps.
articles.details: National landscape analysis (2016–2025) assessed Ethiopia's malaria management across preparedness, detection, containment, response, recovery, and prevention. Found 7.3 million confirmed cases and 1,157 deaths in 2024 (highest in 7 years). ITN ownership averaged 64% with lower coverage in high-burden areas; IRS implemented in ~50% of high-risk zones with documented pyrethroid resistance. Digital surveillance operational in ~80% of facilities but with reporting delays and incomplete data; RDT challenges include stock-outs and pfhrp2/3 gene deletions affecting HRP2-based sensitivity.

articles.headline: Coinfection patterns of malaria and intestinal helminths in Ugandan schoolchildren
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Coinfection with Malaria and Intestinal Helminths among School-Aged Children in Nwoya District, Northern Uganda: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 190 school-aged children (5-18 years) in Nwoya District, Uganda (June 2025). 75.3% (143/190) tested P. falciparum-positive by RDT but only 13.2% (25/190) had microscopically detected parasitemia. Intestinal helminths were rare: Ascaris 0.5%, Hymenolepis 1.6%, Schistosoma 1.1% (3.2% total). All 6 children with helminth infections had positive malaria RDTs. Authors conclude low coinfection rate suggests high efficacy of national mass drug administration for helminth control.

articles.headline: Parasitemia linked to hematological and hepatic changes in falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Parasitemia and Its Relationship With Hematological, Liver, and Kidney Function of Patients With Falciparum Malaria in the Eastern Region, Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 362 participants (71.8% adults, 69.3% female) in Eastern Ghana (August-September 2023). P. falciparum parasitemia measured by microscopy; automated hematology and chemistry analyzers assessed hematological and biochemical parameters. High parasitemia associated with significantly lower Hb, RBC, MCV, MCH, platelets, and elevated ALT/AST (p<0.05), but no significant renal changes. Authors conclude high parasitemia causes hematological and hepatic alterations with variations between adults and children <5.

articles.headline: Data-driven model of imported malaria transmission in Morocco with migration effects
articles.title_original: Data-driven modeling of imported malaria in Morocco and the impact of population migration.
articles.details: Deterministic compartmental model incorporating immigration for malaria transmission. Parameters estimated from Morocco imported malaria data (1990-2023) using least-squares fit, Extended Kalman Filter, and LSTM neural networks. Model evaluated optimal control combining awareness, prophylaxis, treatment, and vector control. Results show significant reduction in human and vector infections through combination strategies. Authors conclude this provides a practical, flexible framework for analyzing imported malaria in high-mobility areas.

articles.headline: Barriers to malaria prevention uptake among pregnant women in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Factors influencing the utilisation of malaria preventive services during pregnancy in Ile Ife, Nigeria: a mixed method approach.
articles.details: Mixed-method study of 200 pregnant women across 10 healthcare facilities in Ile-Ife, Nigeria. Only 34% had good knowledge of malaria preventive services; 62.5% owned LLINs but only 47.5% used them; 50.8% received ≥1 dose IPTp-SP, 7% received ≥3 doses. IPTp-SP uptake associated with occupation, knowledge, and gravidity. Key barriers: commodity stock-outs, lack of funds, transportation costs, distance to facilities, and preference for faith-based care.

articles.headline: Adverse drug reaction trends in seasonal malaria chemoprevention Ghana 2015-2023
articles.title_original: Incidence and trends of adverse drug reactions during seasonal malaria chemoprevention among children: evidence from Ghana's pharmacovigilance database, 2015-2023.
articles.details: Analysis of 2,097 SPAQ (sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine + amodiaquine) adverse drug reactions from Ghana FDA database in children 3-59 months. Most common ADRs: fever (22.7%), vomiting (21.5%), diarrhea (16.9%); 8.6% serious with hospitalization. Incidence declined dramatically from 220.63 per 100,000 doses (2015) to 0.34 per 100,000 doses (2023). Authors conclude SMC with SPAQ is generally safe in Ghana but highlight low healthcare provider reporting versus high caregiver reporting.

articles.headline: Cryptic Anopheles species diversity revealed by COX1 DNA barcoding in Indonesia
articles.title_original: Malaria Vector Surveillance in Indonesia: COX1 Phylogenetic Reveals Monophyletic Clades and Cryptic Diversity in Anopheles Mosquitoes.
articles.details: Survey of Anopheles mosquitoes from two endemic sites in Indonesia using morphological and COX1 molecular identification. Phylogenetic analysis revealed 8 monophyletic clades with strong bootstrap support (≥99% for 6). Misidentification found: specimens identified morphologically as An. aconitus and An. minimus from Hargowilis were molecularly confirmed as An. flavirostris. Dominance: An. sundaicus 68% in Bangsring, An. flavirostris 13% in Hargowilis. Authors conclude molecular tools improve vector identification and enable targeted control.

articles.headline: GTP-cyclohydrolase copy number variation linked to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum GTP-cyclohydrolase 1 (Pfgch1) Gene Copy Number Variation and Its Implications in antimalarial resistance from Northeast India.
articles.details: Analysis of Pfgch1 gene copy number variation in 101 P. falciparum isolates from Northeast India using quantitative PCR. Higher Pfgch1 copy numbers were significantly associated with mutations in Pfdhfr and Pfdhps genes (antifolate resistance markers). Positive correlation observed between Pfgch1 amplification and Pfdhfr/Pfdhps mutations. Authors conclude further investigation needed to understand mechanism but finding may help detect and mitigate antifolate-resistant P. falciparum spread.

articles.headline: Plasmodium serine antigen 3 essential for parasite release from hepatocytes
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei serine repeat antigen 3 (PbSERA3) is required for hepatic merozoite egress.
articles.details: Conditional mutagenesis study of P. berghei PbSERA3 function. Pbsera3-edited sporozoites invaded hepatocytes and developed normally in late liver stages but failed to initiate blood-stage infection, indicating PbSERA3 is essential for hepatic egress. PbSERA3 is processed by SUB1 protease in ookinete and sporozoite stages, consistent with blood-stage processing. PfSERA6 (P. falciparum ortholog) is also SUB1-processed. Authors conclude PbSERA3/PfSERA6 maturation is critical for hepatic egress, presenting an attractive antimalarial drug target.
articles.notable_reason: Novel liver-stage drug target with therapeutic implications

articles.headline: Structural challenges in modern malaria control demand integrated vector management
articles.title_original: Musings from a Vectosaur: Malaria in 2026.
articles.details: Commentary on malaria control landscape noting paradox: encouraging global reports mask operational reality of persistent challenges. Current programs rely primarily on LLINs and IRS, both facing insecticide resistance and behavioral mosquito adaptation. New ecological challenges include climate change and Anopheles stephensi expansion into African cities. Argues malaria control is fragmented across numerous institutions and funding streams. Calls for re-establishing integrated vector management combining surveillance, larval source management, environmental control, and coordinated national programs.

articles.headline: Combined malaria and G6PD deficiency significantly impacts anemia in Nigerian children
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the impact of malaria and erythrocyte G6PD deficiency on anaemia outcomes among children in Jos Northcentral Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 100 children aged 1-5 years in Jos, Nigeria (2023). Malaria parasitemia: 55%; G6PD deficiency: 49%; anemia: 28%. Among malaria-positive children, 43.6% (24/55) were G6PD deficient; 37.5% (9/24) of G6PD-deficient malaria-positive children were anemic (F=7.327; p=0.002). Authors conclude malaria and G6PD deficiency have significant combined impact on anemia outcomes and recommend mandatory G6PD screening before antimalarial treatment in endemic areas.

articles.headline: Case fatality rate and risk factors for severe malaria in Ethiopian children
articles.title_original: Treatment outcome and associated factors of severe malaria among admitted children in the Gambella Region, Southwest Ethiopia: a retrospective study design.
articles.details: Retrospective study of 417 children (1 month to 14 years) admitted with severe malaria at Gambella Hospital, Ethiopia (May 2022 to May 2024). Overall case fatality rate: 9.11%; 91.7% had P. falciparum (89.5% of deaths). High CFR by complication: severe anemia 31.8%, convulsions 27%, pulmonary edema 19.7%, coma 18.5%, acute kidney injury 18.4%, comorbidities 16.9%. Convulsion, severe anemia, and comorbid illness independently associated with death (p<0.05).

articles.headline: Human-zoonotic parasites show divergent pyrimethamine resistance evolution under drug pressure
articles.title_original: Contrasting evolutionary trajectories of pyrimethamine resistance in human and zoonotic malaria parasites.
articles.details: Comparison of dhfr-ts genes in human and zoonotic malaria parasites from Thailand. P. falciparum (n=241): high prevalence of quadruple IRNL mutant indicating strong directional selection toward fixation. P. vivax (n=115): coexistence of wild-type and mutations suggesting incomplete fixation. Zoonotic P. knowlesi (n=18) and P. inui (n=64): markedly higher mutation counts, haplotype diversity, nucleotide diversity, and excess of rare variants, indicating purifying selection and genetic drift. Authors conclude antifolate drug pressure drives human parasite fixation while zoonotic parasites evolve under mutation-selection-drift balance.
articles.notable_reason: Different selective pressures suggest antifolates may work against zoonotic malaria

articles.headline: Iron metabolism pathways in blood-stage P. falciparum reviewed
articles.title_original: Fifty shades of iron: Unorthodox mechanisms of iron acquisition and utilization in blood-stage Plasmodium parasites.
articles.details: Review synthesizing current knowledge of iron acquisition, distribution, and regulation in P. falciparum blood-stage parasites. Highlights unconventional iron homeostasis strategies employed by parasites, including non-canonical transporters and regulatory circuits, and identifies key unanswered questions for therapeutic targeting.

articles.headline: P. vivax protective antigens identified in PNG children
articles.title_original: A longitudinal study of children identifies antibody Fc-mediated functions and antigen targets of immunity to Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.details: Longitudinal cohort study of children in Papua New Guinea evaluated antibody functions against multiple P. vivax antigens. Identified antigens targeted by protective antibodies, including those mediating Fcγ receptor interactions and complement fixation, providing leads for multi-antigen vaccine development.

articles.headline: Synthetic attractants effectively trap anthropophilic Anopheles in Madagascar
articles.title_original: Field evaluation of efficacy and selectivity of select benzopyranone attractants for anthropophilic malaria vectors in Madagascar.
articles.details: Field evaluation in Madagascar highlands tested 4-hydroxycoumarin and 7-decyloxychromone for selective capture of anthropophilic Anopheles using CDC light traps. 4-hydroxycoumarin achieved 80% collection index with 5.1 selectivity index, suggesting potential for mass trapping as complementary vector control approach.

articles.headline: High ITN ownership but widespread misuse in North Cameroon
articles.title_original: Awareness, attitudes, and control measures regarding malaria and mosquitoes in the North Region of Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 500 households across 50 villages in North Cameroon found 99.6% ITN ownership with 96.2% used previous night, yet 90.4% misused nets for other purposes (fishing 7.4%, animal tying 35.8%). Only 19% sought treatment within 24 hours of symptom onset.

articles.headline: HRP2 RDTs maintain efficacy despite pfhrp2/3 gene deletions in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Diagnostic challenges of histidine-rich protein 2-based rapid diagnostic tests due to pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 gene deletions in asymptomatic malaria in Tanzania.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 3,489 participants in Tanzania (2022-2023) compared malaria diagnostics against qPCR reference. RDT detected 77.6% of qPCR-positive infections versus 53.8% for microscopy, with overall RDT accuracy 90.0%. pfhrp2 or pfhrp3 gene deletions detected in 11.8% of samples (2.4% dual-deletions).

articles.headline: Broflanilide discriminating concentration established for An. gambiae
articles.title_original: Determination of the preliminary discriminating concentration of broflanilide against malaria vector mosquito Anopheles gambiae by multi-centre susceptibility testing.
articles.details: Multi-center WHO bottle bioassays in 9 facilities determined preliminary discriminating concentration of broflanilide (novel IRS insecticide in VECTRON T500) at 18 μg/bottle for An. gambiae susceptibility monitoring, establishing benchmark for future surveillance of wild mosquito populations.

articles.headline: Low IRS uptake despite perceived effectiveness in Uganda
articles.title_original: Utilization of malaria control interventions and associated factors among women of reproductive age in Lira City, Northern Uganda.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 629 women in Lira City found high ITN use (84.1%) and IPT uptake (68%) but low IRS coverage (32.8%). Despite 91.9% perceiving IRS as effective, uptake limited by concerns including bad odor/discomfort (51.7%), safety doubts (21.9%), and effectiveness doubts (17.3%).

articles.headline: Climate thresholds linked to malaria incidence in Colombia
articles.title_original: Lagged effect of temperature and rainfall on malaria incidence in Colombia (2013-2023): An approach with Bayesian spatiotemporal adjustment.
articles.details: Spatiotemporal analysis of 970 Colombian municipalities (2013-2023) using distributed lag non-linear models identified non-linear temperature-malaria relationship peaking at 28°C (minimum risk at 16.43°C) with 0-6 week lags, and lower rainfall associated with higher incidence, supporting climate-informed early warning systems.

articles.headline: Odor blend attracts laboratory An. stephensi mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of synthetic attractants against an important malaria vector, Anopheles stephensi (Diptera: Culicidae) mosquitoes in laboratory conditions.
articles.details: Laboratory olfactometer study of non-blood-fed female An. stephensi evaluated four chemical compounds found in host odors. Hexanoic acid, 3-methyl-1-butanol, and L-lactic acid significantly attracted mosquitoes with CO2. Most promising blend of 3-methyl-1-butanol plus hexanoic acid requires field validation.

articles.headline: Microscopy and RDTs miss infections and misidentify species in Senegal
articles.title_original: Malaria misdiagnosis among febrile patients in Senegal: implications for the underestimation of Plasmodium species diversity and malaria burden.
articles.details: Analysis of 948 febrile patients in three Senegal regions found PCR detected malaria in 75.29%, with 70.25% being non-falciparum species (P. ovale, P. vivax, P. malariae). Microscopy missed 31.5% and RDT missed 18.92% of PCR-positive infections; half of non-falciparum mono-infections misdiagnosed as P. falciparum.

articles.headline: Quinine nanoparticles improve safety and efficacy against resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Fabrication and evaluation of quinine-loaded chitosan-polycaprolactone core-shell polymeric nanoparticles for the treatment of malaria: an in vitro and in silico study.
articles.details: In vitro and in silico study of chitosan-polycaprolactone nanoparticles loaded with quinine showed 10-fold improvement in selectivity index (1.014 to 10.658) and enhanced activity against chloroquine-resistant P. falciparum FCR3 (IC50 130.12 to 32.56 ng/mL), with improved penetration into infected erythrocytes.

articles.headline: Conserved P. falciparum EF-1α shows protective vaccine potential in mice
articles.title_original: Immunological response of using emulsifying elongation factor 1 alpha of Plasmodium falciparum-based vaccines in complete Freund's adjuvant.
articles.details: In vivo study in mouse model evaluated rPfEF-1α vaccine with complete Freund's adjuvant. Survival increased by 50% with half of survivors showing no patent parasitemia (versus all BSA control parasitemic). Antigen-specific IgG titer 16-fold higher than control with dominant CD4+IFN-γ+ T-cell response.

articles.headline: Sierra Leone SORT IT course strengthens malaria research capacity
articles.title_original: Strengthening operational research to complement Global Fund grants for HIV, TB and malaria in Sierra Leone.
articles.details: Partnership-led training initiative brought together 19 institutions in Sierra Leone to foster research engagement and strengthen health system capacity. The SORT IT (Structured Operational Research and Training Initiative) course, led by TDR with various partners, aimed to make countries 'data rich, information rich and action rich' to improve health care delivery and outcomes for HIV, TB, and malaria. Initiative highlighted the convening power of SORT IT towards galvanizing research capacity at country level.

articles.headline: P. vivax rosetting transcriptomics identifies membrane protein signatures
articles.title_original: Transcriptomics of Plasmodium vivax rosetting.
articles.details: RNA-seq comparison of 10 field P. vivax isolates from Brazilian Amazon with high versus low rosetting capacity identified 492 differentially expressed genes. Key findings: 34.96% were conserved genes of unknown function, with emphasis on integral membrane proteins (10% of transcribed genes, 53 genes) including PHIST proteins and 6-cysteine family members showing positive regulation in high-rosetting isolates. Results contribute to understanding molecular mechanisms underlying P. vivax rosetting and parasite virulence.

articles.headline: WWII malaria caused prolonged disability despite low mortality
articles.title_original: Post-infection symptoms in U.S. soldiers with malaria during the Second World War: major limitation to return to duty.
articles.details: Historical analysis of malaria in U.S. military during WWII Pacific theater, with over 100,000 cases reported in 1943 alone in Southwest and South Pacific. While death rates were very low, thousands were hospitalized for weeks to months for rehabilitation, unable to return to duty. Relapsing P. vivax posed particular challenge with soldiers at Guadalcanal and New Guinea experiencing more than 10 relapses; approximately 1% were repatriated for chronic malaria symptoms.

articles.headline: Padina algae silver nanoparticles show antiplasmodial and anti-inflammatory activity
articles.title_original: Green synthesis of silver nanoparticles using Padina tetrastromatica against malaria and deciphering the mechanism through machine learning-driven metabolomics and network pharmacology.
articles.details: Green-synthesized silver nanoparticles from brown marine algae Padina tetrastromatica (Ag-PT) demonstrated potent antiplasmodial activity with lower IC50 values and superior parasite suppression compared to chemically synthesized counterparts. Untargeted metabolomics revealed Ag-PT restored malaria-induced metabolic disruptions in fatty acid and arachidonic acid pathways, elevating pro-resolving mediator precursors (DHA, 14-HDHA, 18-HEPE) and L-arginine. Network pharmacology identified COX-2 as key target, with molecular docking confirming strong binding of Ag-PT phytochemical eriodictyol to COX-2.

articles.headline: RALP1 essential for P. falciparum schizont maturation and invasion
articles.title_original: RALP1 is essential for schizont maturation and erythrocyte invasion in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: CRISPR-Cas9 conditional knockdown studies showed RALP1, a rhoptry neck protein, is essential for normal P. falciparum schizont maturation and efficient erythrocyte invasion. Partial knockdown reduced parasite proliferation and merozoite numbers and decreased invasion efficiency. RNA-seq analysis revealed widespread downregulation of invasion and host-parasite interaction genes in schizonts. The extensive disorder, epitope-rich C-terminal region, and essential function indicate RALP1 as a promising candidate for therapeutic or vaccine targeting.

articles.headline: Flooding-triggered malaria outbreak in Mali controlled with expanded prevention
articles.title_original: Extreme rainfall, flooding and malaria in the Sahara: outbreak analysis in Kidal, Mali 2024.
articles.details: Historical cohort study of 2024 malaria outbreak in Kidal, Mali following extreme rainfall and flooding. Weekly surveillance data (May 2024-February 2025) showed epidemic beginning 5 weeks after rainfall onset, peaking at 1,014 cases in week 39 with 1.688 cases per 1000 person-weeks (95% CI 1.687-1.690), highest among children <5. Extended seasonal malaria chemoprevention and mobile health teams reduced morbidity significantly (SIRs 0.50 and 0.48 respectively).

articles.headline: Guava twice as attractive to malaria vectors as sugarcane
articles.title_original: Exploring the importance and preference of sugar feeding behaviour of malaria vectors in sugar plantations of southern Malawi.
articles.details: Field and laboratory study of sugar-feeding preferences in Anopheles gambiae from Malawian sugar plantations. Of 187 wild-caught anophelines, >40% had fed on natural sugar sources. In choice experiments with lab-reared An. gambiae s.l., guava was twice as attractive as sugarcane reference (IRR=1.97, 95% CI 1.49-2.62, p<0.001), followed by banana (IRR=1.68, 95% CI 1.26-2.24) and melon (IRR=1.45, 95% CI 1.08-1.96). Findings support context-specific tailoring of attractive targeted sugar bait interventions.

articles.headline: SC83288 targets PfDNMT2 with high-fitness-cost resistance mechanism
articles.title_original: Mechanisms of PfDNMT2 inhibition and PfATP6-mediated resistance to the antimalarial candidate SC83288 in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Mechanistic studies of SC83288, a novel antimalarial with activity against artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum. SC83288 disrupts blood-stage development by blocking DNA replication and karyokinesis. The parasite DNA and tRNA methyltransferase PfDNMT2 identified as primary molecular target, affecting epigenetic regulation and SAM metabolism. Resistance arises through PfATP6 mutations enabling drug transport into endoplasmic reticulum away from nuclear targets; this resistance carries substantial fitness cost, limiting spread potential.

articles.headline: Encapsulated sea cucumber extract reduces malaria-induced organ damage in mice
articles.title_original: Hepatorenal protective role of encapsulated Holothuria atra in a mice model of Plasmodium berghei ANKA inoculation.
articles.authors: Utami PD, Setianingsih H, Sari DRT
articles.details: Experimental study in 48 BALB/c mice infected with P. berghei ANKA compared chloroquine alone versus chloroquine combined with encapsulated Holothuria atra extract at various gum Arabic/soy protein isolate ratios. Chloroquine monotherapy caused highest renal damage; combination therapy with 4:1 GA/SPI ratio at 100 mg/kg significantly reduced renal and hepatic histopathological damage scores compared to chloroquine alone, approaching levels in uninfected controls. Encapsulation enhanced therapeutic efficacy and antioxidant/anti-inflammatory compound delivery.

articles.headline: Unified culture medium enables cross-species antimalarial drug screening
articles.title_original: A Standardized Culture Medium for Comparative Drug Efficacy Evaluation Across Plasmodium and Babesia Species.
articles.details: Development of unified in vitro culture system (DFS20: DMEM/F12 + 20% fetal bovine serum) supporting robust asexual growth of multiple P. falciparum strains (3D7, Dd2, HB3, V1/S) and Babesia species (B. duncani, B. divergens, B. MO1). System enables direct comparison of drug efficacy under identical conditions. Authors demonstrated utility by screening dihydrotriazines and biguanides targeting conserved DHFR-TS, identifying potent broad-spectrum antifolate candidates with activity against both Babesia and Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: R21 vaccine cost-effective for Zambian malaria; RTS,S borderline
articles.title_original: Provincial stratification and equity-weighted cost-effectiveness of malaria vaccines in Zambia under climate variability.
articles.details: Province-stratified SEIR transmission model evaluating cost-effectiveness of RTS,S and R21 malaria vaccines in Zambia over 5 years (2024-2028) from Ministry of Health perspective. RTS,S estimated to avert median 9.80×10⁵ cases (95% UI 8.88×10⁵-1.08×10⁶) and ~1960 deaths; R21 averted 1.09×10⁶ cases (95% UI 9.91×10⁵-1.21×10⁶) and ~2188 deaths. RTS,S borderline cost-effective at current prices against US$500/DALY threshold; R21 highly cost-effective and potentially dominant.

articles.headline: Sudan parents accept malaria vaccine but lack awareness
articles.title_original: Assessment of parental knowledge, attitude, and acceptance toward malaria vaccine for children under five years in Sudan, 2025.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 706 parents in high-malaria-burden regions of Sudan (April-June 2025) assessing knowledge, attitudes, and acceptance of recently introduced malaria vaccine. While 98.2% were aware of malaria, only 38.0% had heard of the vaccine, with fathers significantly more aware than mothers (19.0% vs 10.7%, p=0.002). Higher vaccine awareness associated with postgraduate education (11.9%), higher income (26.9% for >500K SDG), public sector employment (13.1%), and Kassala residence (30.6%).

articles.headline: Emergency splenic rupture in mixed malaria infection managed surgically
articles.title_original: Malaria-associated splenic haemorrhage requiring emergency splenectomy: a rare complication managed in a resource-constrained setting.
articles.details: Two workers in a malaria-endemic region of South Sudan presented with acute febrile illness from mixed P. falciparum-P. vivax infection and hepatosplenomegaly. Both developed spontaneous splenic rupture with gross hemoperitoneum and hemodynamic instability. Each underwent emergency explorative laparotomy as life-saving surgical intervention. One patient survived following timely surgery and supportive care; the other died despite emergency management.

articles.headline: Plasmodium tubulin adaptations create distinct microtubule structures and properties
articles.title_original: Adaptations in Plasmodium tubulin determine distinct microtubule architectures, mechanics and drug susceptibility.
articles.details: Using parasite-purified tubulin and cryo-electron microscopy, researchers determined P. falciparum microtubule structures and found that parasite-specific tubulin sequences alter the dimer structure. Plasmodium microtubules have smaller but stronger lateral contacts and a stiffer lattice than mammalian brain microtubules. Non-canonical microtubule architectures found in parasites are physiologically relevant, validating in vitro observations. These evolutionary adaptations of tubulin modulate material properties of the parasite cytoskeleton and potentially inform drug susceptibility.

articles.headline: QSAR models identify potent HDAC inhibitors for malaria drug discovery
articles.title_original: Improved quantitative structure-activity relationship (QSAR) models to predict the activity of hydroxamic acids as HDAC inhibitors against malaria parasites.
articles.details: Researchers generated quantitative structure-activity relationship (QSAR) classification models using 572 hydroxamic acid structures and their P. falciparum 50% growth inhibition (IC50) values. Model B7 demonstrated 81% accuracy in predicting in vitro P. falciparum activity when applied to experimental compounds. The screening identified three potent antiplasmodial hits with PfIC50 values of 8, 14, and 80 nM, two of which hyperacetylated P. falciparum histone H4, confirming histone deacetylase inhibition.

articles.headline: Gene drive modeling predicts local malaria elimination despite functional resistance
articles.title_original: A simulation-based deep learning framework for spatially explicit malaria modeling of CRISPR suppression gene drive mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Allegretti YH, Sun W, Champer J
articles.details: Researchers built a deep learning model to simulate effects of engineered CRISPR suppression gene drives on Anopheles mosquito populations and human malaria prevalence. Results indicated that reducing embryo resistance, reducing functional resistance, and increasing drive conversion efficiency enhance suppression. The parameter space for achieving malaria elimination was substantially larger than that for complete mosquito elimination, suggesting imperfect drives may still accomplish malaria control despite functional resistance or chasing phenomena.

articles.headline: P. vivax genetic diversity sharply declines in Cambodia with transmission reduction
articles.title_original: Temporal trends in Plasmodium vivax diversity in eastern Cambodia evidence declining transmission.
articles.details: Genotyping 182 P. vivax isolates from eastern Cambodia collected in 2014, 2015, 2019, and 2023 using 93 microhaplotype markers revealed declining transmission. Polyclonal infections decreased from 22-48% in earlier years to 5% in 2023 (p<0.05), indicating reduced superinfection. In 2023, 81.4% of infections showed identity-by-descent >0.95 with other infections, versus 8.9-10.8% in 2014-2019, reflecting population bottlenecking and inbreeding. Findings support widespread use of radical cure and demonstrate microhaplotype genotyping utility for monitoring P. vivax control.

articles.headline: Yellow fever ecological disruption reduced malaria transmission in Atlantic Forest
articles.title_original: When yellow fever silenced the forest: Ecological collapse and reduced malaria transmission in the Rio de Janeiro Atlantic Forest, Brazil.
articles.details: Title-only publication examining ecological collapse resulting from yellow fever and its impact on malaria transmission in the Rio de Janeiro Atlantic Forest region of Brazil.

articles.headline: Correction: Investigating Yanomami malaria outbreak gold mining article
articles.title_original: Correction: 'Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria' (2026), by de Angeli Dutra et al.
articles.authors: de Angeli Dutra D, et al.
articles.details: Correction notice to the article 'Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria' published in 2026 by de Angeli Dutra et al.

articles.headline: Climate and interventions shape malaria resurgence in Ethiopia Amhara 2018-2024
articles.title_original: Climate, environmental, and programmatic correlates of malaria resurgence in Amhara, Ethiopia (2018-2024): a Bayesian spatiotemporal analysis.
articles.details: A district-level analysis of 13,944 district-month observations from 166 Ethiopian districts (2018-2024) modeled confirmed malaria cases using Bayesian spatiotemporal regression. Cases increased 5.5-fold from 1.19 to 6.53 per 1,000 (2018-2024); higher temperature and rainfall were associated with higher incidence, while elevation was protective. Insecticide-treated net ownership, indoor residual spraying, and larval source management were each associated with lower incidence. Climate-incidence associations were predominantly spatial rather than temporal, suggesting unmeasured factors like conflict or insecticide resistance contributed to resurgence.

articles.headline: Maternal parasitemia strongly predicts neonatal malaria infection in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic determinants and maternal malaria: impact on neonatal parasitemia in Osun State, Southwest, Nigeria.
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 85 mother-infant pairs in Osun State, Nigeria assessed associations between maternal infection and neonatal parasitemia. Neonatal peripheral parasitemia prevalence was 8.2% (7/85). All 7 infected neonates were born to mothers with positive malaria microscopy (7/19, 100%), whereas zero neonates from parasite-negative mothers were infected (p<0.001). No statistically significant associations were found between neonatal parasitemia and maternal education, occupation, insecticide-treated net use, or intermittent preventive therapy uptake.

articles.headline: Limited knowledge and negative attitudes toward malaria vaccine in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes and factors associated with the awareness of caregivers of under-five children regarding the malaria vaccine in the Tiko Health District, Cameroon: A community-based cross-sectional study.
articles.details: A cross-sectional community-based study of 410 caregivers of children aged 0-5 in the Tiko Health District, Cameroon assessed malaria vaccine (RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M) awareness and attitudes. Although 60.7% had heard of the vaccine, only 26.6% demonstrated adequate knowledge and 25.1% held positive attitudes. Caregivers who trusted health workers were more likely to be vaccine-aware (AOR 3.02; 95% CI 1.83-4.99), as were those attending routine immunization services (AOR 3.57; 95% CI 2.27-5.60).

articles.headline: Correction: Chemical targeting prolyl-tRNA synthetase malaria vector article
articles.title_original: Correction to: Chemical Targeting of Prolyl-tRNA Synthetase Stalls Ovarian Development and Kills Malaria Vectors.
articles.details: Correction notice to the article 'Chemical Targeting of Prolyl-tRNA Synthetase Stalls Ovarian Development and Kills Malaria Vectors.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance reveals regional drug resistance patterns across India
articles.title_original: Targeted Genomic Surveillance Unveils Genetic Variations Linked to Regional Malaria Drug Resistance Dynamics in India.
articles.details: Analysis of 238 P. falciparum samples from 6 Indian states using nanopore sequencing of 15 resistance genes revealed contrasting regional profiles shaped by historical drug policies. The Northeast showed high chloroquine resistance (Pfcrt K76T, CVIET) and complete sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance (quintuple/sextuple Pfdhfr-Pfdhps). Central India exhibited variable chloroquine resistance and emerging lumefantrine tolerance (Pfmdr1 Y184F, Pfaat1 S258L). No WHO-validated artemisinin resistance-associated Pfk13 mutations were detected, supporting continued artemisinin-based combination therapy efficacy.

articles.headline: Prioritizing newborn sickle cell screening sites using malaria co-risk
articles.title_original: A maximal-coverage approach to prioritizing newborn sickle cell screening sites in Uganda using a sickle cell-malaria co-risk surface.
articles.details: Geographic analysis in Uganda combining HbS allele frequency and Plasmodium falciparum parasite prevalence (PfPR2-10) to optimize sickle cell screening site placement. Greedy algorithm placed 50 candidate screening hubs capturing 65.6% of co-risk, with 28 (56%) collocatable to existing health facilities. National HbS allele frequency median 6.5% (IQR 4.8–8.5); area-weighted mean PfPR2-10 26.0%. Provides Uganda Ministry of Health a framework for phased expansion of screening programs in highest-burden districts.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid resistance widespread in malaria vectors in Benin
articles.title_original: Entomological surveillance of malaria vector: species, behaviors, and insecticide resistance in southern and central Benin.
articles.details: Entomological surveillance December 2023–October 2024 across six Benin districts. Anopheles gambiae s.l. predominated (>98%); kdr L1014F allele frequencies exceeded 65% at all sites indicating widespread pyrethroid resistance. Pirimiphos-methyl showed higher susceptibility but reduced mortality observed in some districts. Study supports deployment of next-generation nets where pyrethroid resistance is intense and IRS with site-specific insecticide rotation.

articles.headline: Genetic polymorphism of AMA-1 in Vietnamese P. malariae
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism and natural selection of apical membrane antigen-1 in Plasmodium malariae isolated from Vietnam.
articles.details: Genetic analysis of apical membrane antigen-1 (pmama-1) from 95 Vietnamese P. malariae clinical isolates generated 117 sequences grouped into 19 distinct haplotypes. Majority of amino acid polymorphisms identified in domains I and II, with amino acid changes predicted to be surface-positioned. Substantial genetic heterogeneity of pmama-1 observed; further global studies recommended to understand full polymorphic landscape.

articles.headline: Compound effector genes block malaria transmission in gene-drive strains
articles.title_original: Compound effector genes suppress malaria parasite infections in gene-drive population modification strains of the African malaria mosquitoes, Anopheles gambiae and Anopheles coluzzii.
articles.details: Development and characterization of four population modification gene-drive strains (AgTP24, AgTP43, AcTP24, AcTP43) in Anopheles gambiae and An. coluzzii expressing compound effector genes TP24 and TP43. Gene-drive inheritance 95–100%; life table analyses showed mixed fitness impacts. P. falciparum challenge assays demonstrated compound effector modules significantly reduced parasite prevalence and infection intensities in both mosquito species.

articles.headline: Long-lasting insecticidal nets reduce malaria 35% in Zambian provinces
articles.title_original: Malaria incidence before and after long-lasting insecticidal net distribution in four Zambian provinces, 2010-2021.
articles.authors: Mwenya RM, Ngulube ID, Richardson S
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Interrupted time series analysis of four Zambian provinces (Eastern, Luapula, Northern, Northwestern) with combined population 5.96 million, covering 1357 health facility catchment areas from January 2010–December 2021. LLIN distribution campaigns produced statistically significant decline in monthly malaria incidence; binomial regression showed 35% reduction in seasonally adjusted incidence (RR 0.65, 95% CI 0.50–0.85; p=0.002). Regular net replacement essential to maintain protective benefits.

articles.headline: Mouse cerebral malaria model poorly predicts human therapeutic efficacy
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria: of mice and men.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Systematic review of human and murine cerebral malaria publications since 2010. Since 2010, 149 interventions (118 adjuvants) evaluated in mouse model with 95% reported success; however no adjuvant proved effective in human clinical research. Only 26% of mouse studies tested interventions after syndrome development and 65% omitted concomitant antimalarial therapy. Murine model shows very poor predictive value for identifying adjuvant therapeutic interventions in human cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: SMC with SPAQ shows low P. falciparum efficacy in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Parasitological efficacy of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, northern Mozambique.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Pilot pharmacometric assessment of sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine-amodiaquine (SPAQ) in 460 healthy children in Nampula, northern Mozambique. Parasitaemia prevalence decreased 68% to 41% post-SPAQ; preventive efficacy 97% for Plasmodium ovale but only 42% for P. falciparum. Reinfections and recrudescences grew through high desethylamodiaquine concentrations despite susceptible Pfcrt 76K genotypes. Amodiaquine had low parasitological efficacy; sulphadoxine and pyrimethamine did not contribute significantly to chemoprevention.
articles.notable_reason: SPAQ shows low P. falciparum efficacy; undermines SMC rollout

articles.headline: CYP450 polymorphisms show unclear link to P. vivax treatment failure
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms in Cytochrome P450 genes: implications for Plasmodium vivax malaria treatment in Brazil.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Systematic review of eight studies (from 296 retrieved) examining Cytochrome P450 gene polymorphisms and Plasmodium vivax treatment response in Brazil, where P. vivax accounts for ~85% of annual malaria cases. Most studies focused on CYP2D6 polymorphisms and malaria treatment failure using genotype-based phenotype prediction. Overall review reveals lack of consensus regarding relationship between CYP450 single nucleotide polymorphisms and antimalarial drug response.

articles.headline: Reactive case detection identifies subpatent malaria in migrant populations
articles.title_original: Detection of subpatent malaria among febrile mobile and migratory population individuals: a strategy for malaria elimination.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Case investigation with reactive active case detection (RACD) among febrile mobile and migratory population (MMP) individuals in Punjab, India. Of 119 reported cases, 93.3% were P. vivax and 6.7% P. falciparum. RACD around 28 MMP index cases identified 11 infections (6.3%) among 176 sampled febrile individuals; three (27.3%) were subpatent cases detected only by nested PCR. RACD strategy helps reduce spillover risk in malaria elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Uganda sustained malaria control services during COVID-19 pandemic
articles.title_original: Ensuring continuity of malaria services in times of crisis: lessons learned in Uganda during the COVID-19 pandemic.
articles.details: Case study of Uganda's National Malaria Control Division maintaining essential malaria interventions during COVID-19. Rapid introduction of updated operational protocols and secure funding enabled supply of infection prevention and control equipment to >33,000 health workers and village health teams. Malaria Consortium provided critical partnership support. Demonstrates importance of proactive risk planning, timely data reporting, and sustainable funding and commodity supplies for continuing services during public health crises.

articles.headline: Mathematical model optimizes mass drug administration for malaria control
articles.title_original: Mathematical modeling for analyzing mass drug administration operational factors for efficient malaria incidence reduction in southern Senegal.
articles.details: Mathematical model using impulsive differential equations analyzed mass drug administration (MDA) operational factors in Tambacounda health district, southeastern Senegal. Model evaluated four scenarios: MDA rounds, interval between rounds, campaign coverage, and start date. Optimal parameters identified: high MDA coverage, multiple rounds within year, 5–6 week intervals between rounds, intervention start in July (first transmission month). Starting date and interval between rounds showed high correlation affecting campaign effectiveness.

articles.headline: Androgens suppress immune control of Plasmodium liver stage
articles.title_original: Host biological sex directs immune control of the Plasmodium parasite liver stage in mice.
articles.details: Experimental study in BALB/cJ mice examining biological sex and androgen effects on Plasmodium yoelii liver-stage infection. At 44 hours post-infection, females and orchiectomized males had 44% fewer infected hepatocytes compared to intact males. Intact males showed lower baseline densities of Kupffer cells, monocytes, and dendritic cells with reduced antigen presentation and Type I interferon responses during infection. Androgens increased parasite survival by reducing elimination of infected hepatocytes.

articles.headline: Vaccine dosing and age shape B and T cell responses to R21/RH5.1
articles.title_original: B and T cell responses to pre-erythrocytic R21/Matrix-M and blood-stage RH5.1/Matrix-M malaria vaccines in endemic settings.
articles.details: Phase 1b trials in Kenya and Tanzania analyzed immune responses to R21/Matrix-M and RH5.1/Matrix-M vaccines. R21 showed higher NANP-specific memory B cells in infants versus adults and with higher Matrix-M dose (50μg vs 25μg). RH5.1 responses were greatest with delayed booster dosing (10-10-10μg at 0-1-6months), with responses correlating with serum antibody at 2-year follow-up. Findings demonstrate that age, adjuvant dose, and booster timing impact antigen-specific responses and could inform multistage vaccine development.

articles.headline: Yeast RNAi pesticides target invasive Anopheles stephensi mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Targeting the Highly Invasive Malaria Vector Anopheles stephensi using Yeast RNAi Pesticides.
articles.details: Sh.463 yeast RNAi pesticide targeting the Shaker gene was evaluated against A. stephensi larvae and adults in insectary conditions. Treatment with Sh.463-56.10R yeast silenced the Shaker gene and produced high mortality levels in laboratory studies. Female-specific RNAi yeast larvicides targeting putative female-specific genes resulted in significant female mortality and higher male:female ratios in offspring. Results suggest RNAi pesticides may represent a novel biorational intervention for integrated A. stephensi control.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis reveals malaria hotspots in Zamfara State, Nigeria
articles.title_original: Geospatial mapping of gender-sensitive malaria vulnerability and ITN usage barriers in Zamfara State, Nigeria.
articles.authors: Atijosan A, Atijosan A, Salau AO
articles.details: Composite Gender-Sensitive Malaria Vulnerability Index (CGMVI) integrated malaria incidence, mortality, ITN access, maternal education, and household wealth using Pentagonal Fuzzy Analytic Hierarchy Process. West-to-east vulnerability gradient identified with hotspots in Gummi (3.86) and Bukkuyum (3.62) marked by low ITN usage (82–85%), high maternal education deficits (89–91%), and poverty (85–90%). Getis-Ord Gi* hotspot analysis confirmed statistically significant clustering in western LGAs (z-scores 2.11–4.89, p<0.01). Findings highlight intersections of epidemiological, socio-economic, and gender factors shaping malaria vulnerability.

articles.headline: Structure-based discovery of Plasmodium falciparum DPAP-1 inhibitor
articles.title_original: Structure-based discovery of Plasmodium falciparum DPAP-1 inhibitor and its experimental validation as an antimalarial agent.
articles.details: No abstract provided; assessment based on title only.

articles.headline: Imported malaria cases show severity linked to prior exposure history
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in Portugal: A Retrospective Analysis from a Tertiary Public Hospital.
articles.authors: Valença Ferreira T, Alves Silva G, Duque V
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 88 adult malaria cases (2020–2024) at a Portuguese tertiary hospital, mostly male expatriates with exposure in Angola; 52.3% had prior malaria history. Severe malaria occurred in 25% overall but was significantly more frequent in those without prior history (p=0.027). Severe cases had significantly higher creatinine (p=0.009) and lactate dehydrogenase (p=0.038). Urea and parasitemia independently associated with longer hospital stay (p<0.001 and p=0.016). Findings support semi-immunity hypothesis and potential of laboratory biomarkers for severity stratification.

articles.headline: HRP2/3 gene deletions undermine malaria rapid diagnostic tests globally
articles.title_original: Are HRP2/3 deletions silently crippling malaria rapid diagnostic tests?
articles.details: Systematic review of 28 studies on pfhrp2/pfhrp3 deletions in P. falciparum and RDT performance. Deletion prevalence ranged from 2.4% in Chhattisgarh, India to 57.8% in Southern Ethiopia, with Brazil at ~18.3% dual deletions. WHO recommends 5% threshold; deletions exceeding this level significantly impair RDT diagnostic performance. High-deletion regions show diagnostic failure requiring alternative tools such as PCR, whole-genome sequencing, and digital droplet PCR for effective malaria detection and elimination.

articles.headline: Drought and temperature predict lagged malaria transmission dynamics
articles.title_original: Hydroclimatic extremes indexed by SPI and malaria case dynamics in Oyo State, Nigeria: evidence for lagged, site-specific effects.
articles.details: 10-year analysis (2013–2023) of climate and malaria data from Saki and Sepeteri, Nigeria using standardized precipitation indices (SPI). In Saki, one-unit increase in SPI-12 (long-term moisture) associated with 267% rise in malaria cases (β=1.299, p=0.011), effect moderated by temperature (β=−0.049, p=0.011). One- to two-month lagged climate models substantially improved predictive performance (ΔAIC ≈47–59 in Saki; ≈45–50 in Sepeteri). Findings reveal non-linear, site-specific drought–malaria dynamics supporting integration of lagged indicators into malaria early-warning systems.

articles.headline: Plasmodium chabaudi infection causes reversible thymic atrophy
articles.title_original: Thymic Atrophy Development in a Non-Lethal Plasmodium Infection.
articles.details: Animal model study of thymic changes during P. chabaudi AS infection across parasitemia stages. Thymic atrophy marked by substantial decreases in weight and cellularity, especially at 20–30% parasitemia, with significant DP/SP thymocyte loss. Histological analysis showed cortical shrinkage and DNA fragmentation indicating increased apoptosis. Elevated serum cytokines (IFN-γ, TNF-α, IL-6, CXCL9) correlated with atrophy degree; thymocytes prematurely released to peripheral tissues, particularly spleen. After parasitemia resolved, thymus recovered structurally and functionally.

articles.headline: CCND3 enhancer variant associated with reduced malaria risk
articles.title_original: CCND3 enhancer variant reduces malaria risk.
articles.details: No abstract provided; assessment based on title only.

articles.headline: Malarial retinopathy predicts severe malaria with high specificity
articles.title_original: A Study on Prevalence of Malarial Retinopathy among Malaria Cases and to Determine Its Presence as a Marker of Severe Malaria: Observations from Eastern India.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study at tertiary hospital in Kolkata of 71 malaria cases (12 severe, 59 uncomplicated); 8 P. vivax and 3 P. falciparum in severe group, 35 P. vivax in uncomplicated. Retinopathy features (whitening, hemorrhage, tramlining) present in 75% of severe cases versus 3.4% of uncomplicated cases. Sensitivity 75%, specificity 96.6%, positive predictive value 81.8% for severe malaria. Recommended as important clinical biomarker for severity prediction, measurable by direct ophthalmoscopy.

articles.headline: Optimized nested-PCR detects Plasmodium malariae with high specificity
articles.title_original: Optimization of Nested-PCR for the detection of Plasmodium malariae in laboratory settings in Southern Vietnam.
articles.details: Nested-PCR optimization targeting P. malariae 18S rRNA gene using MAL1/MAL2 primers in Southern Vietnam. Optimal conditions determined: 35 cycles in first round, 25 cycles in second round, 54°C annealing temperature. MAL1/MAL2 pair demonstrated high specificity with no amplification of P. falciparum, P. vivax, P. ovale, or P. knowlesi. Limit of detection 0.5 parasites/μL. Optimized protocol suited for laboratory use in Southern Vietnam.

articles.headline: Climate shocks threaten malaria control efforts in Africa
articles.title_original: Climate shocks, not just warming, threaten malaria control efforts in Africa.
articles.details: No abstract provided; assessment based on title only.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance reveals divergent resistance mechanisms under different ITN types
articles.title_original: Genetic Surveillance Reveals Differential Evolutionary Dynamic of Anopheles gambiae Under Contrasting Insecticidal Tools Used in Malaria Control.
articles.details: Genomic surveillance study of Anopheles gambiae populations in Uganda exposed to standard pyrethroid ITNs or pyrethroid-PBO nets during a clinical trial. Despite substantial reductions in indoor mosquito densities, effective population size remained large with marked allele frequency shifts at resistance-associated loci.
