Cyp9k1 gene duplication increased in pyrethroid-only net populations but decreased in PBO-net populations; alternative detoxification mechanisms selected based on net type demonstrate distinct evolutionary dynamics of resistance.

articles.headline: Malaria and dengue impair reproductive health; antimalarial drugs cause infertility
articles.title_original: Consequence of malaria and dengue on reproductive vigor. A scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review (2013-2024) of malaria, dengue, and antimalarial drug effects on reproductive health. Malaria causes decreased semen quality and higher rates of miscarriage, intrauterine demise, premature delivery, low birthweight, neonatal deaths, and maternal death. Antimalarial drugs (chloroquine, quinine, quinacrine, pyrimethamine) cause male infertility. Dengue causes temporary sperm alterations; during pregnancy increases risk of premature delivery, fetal death, and hemorrhagic complications.

articles.headline: Structure-guided PfCyRPA vaccine design improves growth-inhibitory antibody potency
articles.title_original: Structure-guided design of a PfCyRPA-based vaccine against blood-stage malaria.
articles.details: Pre-clinical study of structure-guided PfCyRPA-based vaccine design. PfCyRPA-EM, containing epitope-bearing blades 1 and 2 of the six-bladed β-propeller, elicited antibodies that inhibited parasite growth at lower concentrations than full-length PfCyRPA. PfCyRPA-EM exhibited higher thermostability and improved expression as an I53-50 nanoparticle fusion, making it suitable for clinical development.

articles.headline: P. cynomolgi and P. inui prevalent in Malaysian macaques; spillover risk
articles.title_original: Molecular and phylogenetic analysis of zoonotic Plasmodium in long-tailed macaques: insights from wildlife-human conflict zones in Selangor, Malaysia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional PCR study of 100 long-tailed macaques from wildlife-human conflict zones in Selangor, Malaysia (September 2022–April 2023). Prevalence: P. cynomolgi 24%, P. inui 23%, P. knowlesi 4%, with 51% single infections, 18% dual infections, and 2% triple infections. Phylogenetic analysis identified S-type and A-type lineages for all three species, highlighting zoonotic potential in human-wildlife interface zones.

articles.headline: CD44 crosslinking enhances Plasmodium falciparum erythrocyte invasion
articles.title_original: With a little help from my friend: engaging CD44 facilitates Plasmodium falciparum invasion of erythrocytes.
articles.details: Study identifying host transmembrane protein CD44 as a critical coreceptor that, when crosslinked, significantly enhances Plasmodium falciparum invasion of human erythrocytes, providing a molecular link between early parasite attachment and final essential invasion steps.

articles.headline: Metabolic shift triggers rRNA expression changes during parasite transmission
articles.title_original: Shifting metabolism alters rRNA expression in malaria parasites.
articles.details: Study demonstrating that altered NAD+/nicotinamide ratios from the metabolic shift during transition to mosquito stages initiate derepression of mosquito-specific rDNA loci in malaria parasites.

articles.headline: Taurine maintains asymptomatic malaria infections
articles.title_original: How taurine keeps asymptomatic malaria infections in check.

articles.headline: Ivermectin mass drug administration cost-effective for malaria in Kenya
articles.title_original: Cost and cost-effectiveness of ivermectin mass drug administration for malaria control in Kwale county, Kenya: a modelling analysis of a cluster-randomised trial.
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis of BOHEMIA cluster-randomised trial in Kwale county, Kenya comparing ivermectin MDA (monthly for 3 months, ~64% coverage) to no intervention. Monthly ivermectin administration reduced malaria incidence by 26%. Intervention cost $11.83 per person; base-case ICER $905.23 per DALY averted, below Kenya's GDP-based cost-effectiveness threshold of $974.65, supporting iMDA as supplementary intervention in moderate transmission settings.

articles.headline: Novel malaria control tool for complex decision-making
articles.title_original: A new tool at a complex time for malaria decision making.

articles.headline: Taurine inhibits RBC adhesion via PfEMP1 in asymptomatic infection
articles.title_original: Longitudinal plasma metabolomics reveals a role for taurine in asymptomatic malaria and seasonal persistence.
articles.details: Longitudinal plasma metabolomics study (n=199) of individuals in the Gambia during high and low malaria transmission periods. Plasma taurine increased during low-transmission periods when asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum infections persist. Although taurine had no effect on cultured parasite growth, it strongly inhibited malaria-infected RBC adhesion via PfEMP1 to CD36 and endothelial protein C receptor, suggesting a role in reducing cytoadhesion.

articles.headline: Reduced cyclin D3 in RBC precursors protects against malaria
articles.title_original: Reduced cyclin D3 expression in erythroid cells protects against malaria.
articles.details: Investigation of genetic variant rs112233623-T in CCND3 erythroid enhancer associated with increased hemoglobin A2 and increased RBC size. The variant disrupts SMAD3 binding, reducing CCND3 expression in erythroblasts and inhibiting G1-S cell-cycle transition. Parasite growth was impaired in cultured P. falciparum-infected RBCs from carriers, with impairment correlating to ROS levels. Population genetics revealed positive selection for rs112233623-T in Sardinia, suggesting CCND3 reduction is a malaria resistance mechanism via ROS-dependent pathways.

articles.headline: Cell-cycle protein repression hinders malaria
articles.title_original: Malaria is hindered by repression of a cell-cycle protein.

articles.headline: Slow parasite clearance observed in Cambodian P. vivax treated with artesunate
articles.title_original: Parasite clearance in patients with Plasmodium vivax monoinfection treated with artesunate in Cambodia: an observational secondary analysis of trial data.
articles.details: Analysis of 161 P. vivax infections from 87 patients in Cambodia (median age 20 years) treated with 2 mg/kg/day artesunate for 7 days. All infections cleared by day 3, but 49 (30%) showed slow clearance, including 9 (6%) with clearance slope half-life >5 hours. Slow-clearing parasites displayed delayed gene expression responses with downregulation of haemoglobin metabolism genes, potentially reducing artesunate efficiency and facilitating resistance emergence to artemisinin-combination therapy.

articles.headline: mRNA vaccine restores immunity after haemozoin-mediated vaccine impairment
articles.title_original: mRNA vaccination overcomes haemozoin-mediated impairment of whole-parasite malaria vaccines in mice.
articles.details: In mouse malaria models, prior blood-stage exposure impairs CD8+ T cell responses to radiation-attenuated sporozoite (RAS) vaccines via haemozoin-mediated reduction in dendritic cell antigen uptake. A lipid nanoparticle-encapsulated mRNA vaccine encoding P. CD8+ T cell epitopes overcame this impairment and restored protection in malaria-exposed mice. Combined RAS-plus-mRNA vaccination enhanced liver-resident memory T cells, suggesting haemozoin as a key obstacle to vaccine efficacy in endemic areas.

articles.headline: P. falciparum parasite development unaffected by G6PD Viangchan variant
articles.title_original: Invasion and development of Plasmodium falciparum in erythroblasts of humans carrying G6PD viangchan.
articles.details: In vitro study of P. falciparum invasion and development in human erythroblasts from 6 subjects with normal, heterozygous, or hemizygous G6PD Viangchan (871G>A) variants. Parasites invaded polychromatic erythroblasts and developed into mature trophozoites with no significant differences in percentage or developmental stages across G6PD genotypes. Small sample size (n=6) limited statistical analysis; preliminary findings suggest G6PD Viangchan does not protect against malaria parasites in this in vitro model.

articles.headline: Droplet digital PCR assays enable precise P. vivax parasitemia quantification
articles.title_original: Development and implementation of droplet digital PCR assays for accurate quantification of Plasmodium vivax parasitemia and G6PD viangchan genotyping.
articles.details: Developed and validated duplex ddPCR assays for P. vivax parasitemia quantification and G6PD Viangchan (871G>A) genotyping in clinical samples from Kanchanaburi Province, Thailand. P. vivax parasitemia assay demonstrated limit of detection 3.2 parasites/µL, limit of quantification 400 parasites/µL, and >90% accuracy with coefficient of variation <20%. G6PD genotyping achieved 100% sensitivity and specificity. Clinical samples showed parasitemia 315–82,429 parasites/µL; chloroquine treatment caused 99.2–99.9% reduction by day 2 with complete clearance by day 3.

articles.headline: Novel triple-drug regimens show equivalent efficacy to standard artemisinin therapy
articles.title_original: Artesunate-pyronaridine-atovaquone-proguanil and artesunate-fosmidomycin-clindamycin compared with standard artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated malaria (MultiMal): a randomised, controlled, clinical, phase 2 trial in Gabon and Ghana.
articles.details: Phase 2 randomized trial in Gabon and Ghana (100 patients: 40 children, 40 adolescents, 20 adults) comparing artesunate-pyronaridine (AP) versus artesunate-pyronaridine-atovaquone-proguanil (APAP) versus artesunate-fosmidomycin-clindamycin (AFC). PCR-corrected adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) on day 28 was 100% for AP and APAP, 97% for AFC; by day 42, 87.5% (AP), 85.3% (APAP), and 94.4% (AFC). No differential efficacy across regimens; safety profiles comparable across groups.

articles.headline: Climate change projected to cause 123 million additional malaria cases in Africa
articles.title_original: Projected impacts of climate change on malaria in Africa.
articles.details: Geotemporal modeling study integrating 25 years of African climate, malaria, control, and socioeconomic data with climate projections (SSP 2-4.5 scenario) to 2050. Under current control levels, projected 123 million additional malaria cases (range 49.5–203 million) and 532,000 additional deaths (195,000–912,000) in Africa between 2024 and 2050. Extreme weather events drive 79% of additional cases and 93% of additional deaths. Most increases occur in existing endemic areas rather than range expansion.

articles.headline: Cytokine-induced killer cells reduce P. falciparum parasitemia through cytolytic activity
articles.title_original: Cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells inhibit Plasmodium falciparum parasitemia through cytolytic effector activity in vitro.
articles.details: In vitro study demonstrating that cytokine-induced killer (CIK) cells inhibit P. falciparum growth in infected red blood cells. CIK cytotoxicity dependent on secretion of lytic agents including granzyme B, granulysin, and perforin acting synergistically. Priming CIK cells with dendritic cells pulsed with P. falciparum lysate antigen diminished cytotoxicity. CIK cell efficacy comparable to chloroquine; combination with artemisinin showed slight enhancement.

articles.headline: High burden of febrile sub-microscopic P. falciparum and P. vivax mixed infections
articles.title_original: High Burden of Febrile Sub-microscopic Plasmodium Mixed Infections in Central India: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey in Central India (June–November 2020) of 1,030 samples detected 27% Plasmodium infections (280 cases): 18% P. falciparum monoinfection, 0.5% P. vivax monoinfection, 8% mixed infections. All 27% were febrile and sub-microscopic, undetected by microscopy or rapid diagnostic tests. Microscopy slide quality was unsatisfactory. Among 9 mixed-infection patients with 12-month telephone follow-up, none reported recurrences or clinical complications.

articles.headline: EMAP3 protein identified on P. berghei infected red blood cell surface
articles.title_original: Erythrocyte Membrane Protein 3 (EMAP3) Is Exposed on the Surface of the Plasmodium berghei Infected Red Blood Cell.
articles.details: Characterization of newly identified parasite protein EMAP3 exported to the membrane of P. berghei-infected red blood cells where it is exposed on the outer surface and likely interacts with EMAP1. Parasites lacking EMAP3 showed no significant reduction in growth or sequestration, indicating EMAP3 is not a major CD36-binding protein. The outer surface location of EMAP3 provides a new scaffold for displaying P. falciparum proteins to screen in vivo for putative cytoadherence inhibitors.

articles.headline: Trust and reciprocity strengthen community-based malaria control intervention
articles.title_original: The importance of values in a community-based response to malaria in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic: results from a longitudinal, mixed-methods study.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study (2020–2022) in Santo Domingo, Dominican Republic examining values in malaria control. Cross-sectional survey of 489 households measured trust in neighbors, associations, health system, and government (trust scale reliability ordinal α = 0.70). Qualitative phase included 66 interviews with 39 key informants. Companionship, reciprocity, and 'showing face' linked participation, morale, and timely diagnosis and treatment. Study identified two value domains: institutional trust (people-institutions) and community solidarity (people-people).

articles.headline: Primaquine adherence remains suboptimal despite adequate drug supply
articles.title_original: Suboptimal primaquine adherence in Plasmodium vivax malaria: Evidence from high-burden tribal districts in Odisha.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study (January–December 2024) in two high-burden tribal districts in Odisha, India enrolled 269 laboratory-confirmed P. vivax patients. Adherence to 3-day chloroquine and artesunate-SP exceeded 93%, but only 58.7% (95% CI 52.6–64.9) completed the 14-day primaquine regimen. Treatment discontinuation primarily due to symptom resolution (61.9%) and forgetfulness (21.4%); only 1 patient stopped due to adverse event. Older age and household malaria history associated with better adherence.

articles.headline: Pk32 antigen shows limited diversity and conserved B-cell epitopes
articles.title_original: In silico profiling of the plasmodium knowlesi 32 kDa antigen: Diversity, epitope prediction, and structural modeling.
articles.details: In silico analysis of 39 full-length Pk32 (32 kDa antigen) sequences from P. knowlesi predominantly from Malaysian Borneo. Sequence analysis showed low nucleotide diversity (π = 0.0061) and limited polymorphism with no geographical clustering. Purifying selection acting on Pk32 indicated functional constraint. B-cell epitope prediction identified three common epitopes, with two conserved across all sequences (145PKERES150 and 167DIGKKQNS174), suggesting Pk32 as a compelling blood-stage vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: Sugar-preserved LAMP assay enables cold-chain-free malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: A cold-chain free loop-mediated isothermal amplification test for detection of human malaria in point-of-care settings.
articles.authors: Rosli NN, Lau YL, Lai MY
articles.details: Researchers developed a room-temperature stable loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) assay for Plasmodium knowlesi detection using sucrose enzyme preservation. The assay achieved analytical sensitivity of 10 copies/μL and 100% specificity against Plasmodium species and human DNA, with 12-day room-temperature stability without requiring refrigeration. This cost-effective alternative to lyophilization eliminates dependence on specialized equipment while achieving comparable short-term stability, making it suitable for malaria surveillance and remote clinic deployments.

articles.headline: Clathrins regulate hemoglobin uptake in malaria parasite cytostomes
articles.title_original: Clathrins Are Involved in the Endocytosis of Host Cytosol in the Malaria Parasite.
articles.details: Characterization of clathrin heavy chain (PfCHC), clathrin light chain (PfCLC), and AP-1 γ subunit in P. falciparum showed these proteins localize to the parasite cytoplasm and cytostome structures. Knockdown of PfCHC led to formation of abnormally long cytostome tubes and impaired hemoglobin uptake, indicating clathrins are involved in cytostome-mediated nutrient acquisition. Live-cell imaging and electron microscopy confirmed their localization at coated vesicle-like structures.

articles.headline: Three novel SNPs found in Nigerian P. falciparum isolates
articles.title_original: Three new single nucleotide polymorphisms identified in Plasmodium falciparum isolates from Kogi State, Nigeria.
articles.details: Study screened genomic DNA from 40 P. falciparum-positive blood samples from Kogi State, Nigeria for Pfmdr1 and Kelch-13 genes. Successful amplification was obtained from 45% (18/40) of samples. Kelch-13 sequencing showed three non-synonymous mutations (K430E, W706C, Q707K) in three samples with near 100% sequence homology to wild-type P. falciparum 3D7. Authors concluded none of these mutations have been previously associated with artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: East Africa ACT quality varies widely; 11.2% failure rate, efficacy maintained
articles.title_original: Artemisinin-Based Combination Therapies in East Africa: A Systematic Review of Quality, Clinical Efficacy, and Regulatory Progress Towards Sustainable Malaria Control.
articles.details: Systematic review of 40 articles from Ethiopia, Kenya, Tanzania, Uganda examining 3,363 artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) samples. Overall failure rate was 11.2% (375/3363) for not meeting physicochemical quality specifications; country-specific rates: Uganda 33.3%, Kenya 14.1%, Tanzania 11.6%, Ethiopia 4.2%. Despite poor quality in some samples, ACTs remained highly efficacious with most therapeutic trials reporting polymerase chain reaction-corrected cure rates ≥90%. K13 mutations associated with delayed parasite clearance pose an ongoing resistance threat.

articles.headline: Amodiaquine efficiently inhibits P. falciparum plasmepsin V enzyme
articles.title_original: Inhibiting catalytic activity of Plasmodium falciparum aspartate protease plasmepsin V: A biochemical approach to malaria intervention.
articles.authors: Prajapati AK, Kumar Y, Choudhary S
articles.details: Biochemical study repurposed quinoline drugs amodiaquine (AQ) and clioquinol (CQ) to inhibit plasmepsin V (PfPlmV), an aspartate protease essential for parasite survival. Amodiaquine efficiently inhibited PfPlmV catalytic activity with IC50 of 0.42 ± 0.04 μM via competitive inhibition by binding directly to the substrate-binding site and engaging catalytic dyad residues Asp118 and Asp365. Clioquinol bound to PfPlmV but did not interact with the catalytic dyad and showed no inhibitory effect. Molecular dynamics simulations corroborated these mechanistic findings.

articles.headline: I. umbellulata bark achieves 85.65% parasite suppression in mice
articles.title_original: Sub-acute toxicity studies and dynamics-based molecular insights on the in vivo antimalarial activity of Ilex umbellulata (Wall.) Loes bark in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.
articles.details: Study evaluated the antimalarial activity of Ilex umbellulata (traditional Mizoram plant) bark extract (80% methanol) in P. berghei-infected mice. LD50 was >2000 mg/kg. Treatment with 200 mg/kg dose resulted in parasitaemia of 5.00 ± 0.97%, parasite suppression of 85.65 ± 2.77%, and survival of 15.33 ± 2.82 days post-infection. Molecular docking suggested that cyanidin-3-O-rutinoside inhibits P. berghei enoyl-acyl-carrier-protein reductase (PbEACPR), blocking fatty acid biosynthesis.

articles.headline: Zanthoxylum chalybeum shows potent in vitro antiplasmodial activity
articles.title_original: In vitro antiplasmodial activity, acute toxicity and phytochemical quantification of selected medicinal plants used for the management of uncomplicated malaria in eastern Uganda.
articles.details: In vitro antiplasmodial evaluation of five traditional Ugandan medicinal plants against chloroquine-sensitive (3D7), chloroquine-resistant (Dd2), and clinical P. falciparum isolates. Zanthoxylum chalybeum was most potent with IC50 0.81–1.28 μg/ml, followed by Albizia coriaria and Entada abyssinica (IC50 10–50 μg/ml). Maytenus senegalensis showed weak activity while Kigelia africana was largely inactive. Phytochemical screening identified alkaloids, flavonoids, tannins, and terpenoids across all plants.

articles.headline: IPTp-SP reduces maternal parasitaemia and anaemia in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Comparative effect of chemoprevention in malaria infection during pregnancy: A network meta-analysis.
articles.details: Network meta-analysis of 22 randomized controlled trials (N=14,705) comparing antimalarial chemoprevention during pregnancy (IPTp). Moderate certainty evidence showed monthly single-dose IPTp-sulphadoxine/pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) resulted in greater reduction in maternal parasitaemia and anaemia compared to placebo. Low-certainty evidence indicated fewer incidences of low birth weight with IPTp-SP than with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine combined with azithromycin. Authors recommend head-to-head comparisons with adequate sample sizes.

articles.headline: Rare complications in severe malaria: cholecystitis and hemolytic anemia
articles.title_original: Acute Acalculous Cholecystitis and Post-artesunate Hemolytic Anemia: Two Rare Complications in Severe Malaria.
articles.details: Case report describing two rare complications in severe malaria: acute acalculous cholecystitis and post-artesunate hemolytic anemia. No abstract provided; summary based on title only.

articles.headline: Mitochondrial exonuclease Exomit1 required for blood-stage establishment
articles.title_original: Plasmodium mitochondrial exodeoxyribonucleases, Exo(mit1) and Exo(mit2), are conserved within alveolates with Exo(mit1) essential for the establishment of blood-stage infection.
articles.details: Study identified two putative Plasmodium falciparum mitochondrial exonucleases, PfExomit1 and PfExomit2, involved in mtDNA processing. PfExomit1 cleaved single-stranded DNA while PfExomit2 functioned as a bipolar exonuclease on double-stranded DNA. Conditional mutagenesis of PbExomit1 in P. berghei sporozoites showed they could invade hepatocytes and develop normally in the liver, but could not transition to blood stage, demonstrating Exomit1 is essential for establishing blood-stage infection.

articles.headline: Microarray patches deliver primaquine/chloroquine with 99.8% parasite suppression
articles.title_original: Formulation and evaluation of hydrogel-forming microarray patches for transdermal primaquine and chloroquine delivery in malaria therapy.
articles.details: Hydrogel-forming microarray patch (MAP) system delivering lyophilised primaquine (PQ) and chloroquine (CQ) reservoirs achieved 70% in vitro delivery efficiency (~40 mg each drug over 24 h). In rats, MAP administration increased Cmax from 0.68 to 1.68 ng/mL for CQ and 14.32 to 47.20 ng/mL for PQ compared to oral dosing. AUC0-72h was 4.4-fold higher for CQ and 1.3-fold higher for PQ. In murine P. berghei malaria, MAP-delivered drugs reduced parasitaemia by 99.8%.

articles.headline: Pregnancy reduces lumefantrine exposure; 100% cure despite lower drug levels
articles.title_original: Population pharmacokinetics of lumefantrine in pregnant and non-pregnant women with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Western Kenya.
articles.details: Pharmacokinetic study of 75 women (25 non-pregnant, 30 second-trimester pregnant, 20 third-trimester pregnant) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria treated with artemether-lumefantrine 80/480 mg twice daily for 3 days. Pregnancy status increased lumefantrine clearance by 23% and reduced central volume of distribution by 28%. Pregnant women had lower exposure with AUC geometric mean ratio 0.76 (95% CI: 0.57–1.01), Cmax GMR 0.79 (0.63–0.99), and Day 7 concentration GMR 0.69 (0.51–0.94). Despite lower exposure, 28-day PCR-corrected cure rate was 100% in both groups.

articles.headline: Molecular targets in Plasmodium-Anopheles interactions for control
articles.title_original: Deciphering the molecular targets of Plasmodium and Anopheline interactions for malaria control.
articles.details: This review examines molecular interactions between Plasmodium parasites and Anopheles mosquitoes during midgut invasion and salivary gland development, including parasite genes involved in gametogenesis and transmission. The authors discuss how the mosquito immune system responds to invading parasites and how understanding these interactions may lead to novel vector control strategies for malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Prevalence of malaria resistance variants in Colombian population
articles.title_original: Prevalence of the Main Human Genetic Variants Related to Resistance to Malaria in a Population of the Colombian Pacific Coast.
articles.details: A cross-sectional survey of 819 individuals from Buenaventura, Colombia characterized frequencies of genetic variants conferring malaria resistance: HbS (3.1%), HbC (2.2%), Duffy (72.2%), β-thalassemia-29 (2.1%), β-thalassemia-88 (2.8%), and G6PD (11%). Adolescents and young adults (13-26 years) showed the highest proportion of resistance genotypes. The findings suggest age and geographic variation in susceptibility patterns within the urban population.

articles.headline: Bacterial larvicides reduce vector density but lack large-scale efficacy data
articles.title_original: Systematic literature review of bacterial larvicides Bacillus thuringiensis var israelensis and Bacillus (Lysinibacillus) sphaericus for malaria control in Africa.
articles.details: A systematic review of 54 studies found that Bacillus thuringiensis var. israelensis and Bacillus sphaericus reduced primary vector larval density by 50%-100%, yet only 14 studies reported subsequent malaria incidence reductions of 31-62%. Notably, only one large-scale countrywide study evaluated routine program inclusion (São Tomé and Príncipe). The review identifies a critical gap in large-scale implementation evidence across African countries, impeding evidence-based policy development.

articles.headline: Weakening TPR-MBER correlation signals elimination progress in India
articles.title_original: Weakening TPR-MBER correlation: Marker of success in India's malaria elimination progress.
articles.details: Analysis of Monthly Malaria Information System data from Madhya Pradesh (2020-2024) shows a progressively weakening correlation between Monthly Blood Examination Rate (MBER) and Test Positivity Rate (TPR), indicating improved surveillance saturation and reduced underlying transmission. This dynamic relationship may serve as a novel indicator for monitoring progress toward malaria elimination.

articles.headline: High malaria burden in Ethiopia driven by environmental and socioeconomic factors
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated risk factors of malaria among febrile patients at Kidist Hana Health Center Fogera District, Amhara Regional State, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of febrile patients at a single health center in Fogera District, Ethiopia found 40.5% malaria prevalence by microscopy, predominantly P. falciparum (78.4%). Rapid diagnostic tests showed 95.91% sensitivity and 91.24% specificity. Multivariate analysis identified significant associations with widowed marital status, age <5 years, low household income, lack of insecticide-treated nets, stagnant water near residence, and outdoor sleeping.

articles.headline: Biocredit Pf/Pv RDT shows good sensitivity for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Diagnostic accuracy of novel Biocredit species-specific LDH-based immunochromatographic assay for Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax detection: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 19 datasets from eight studies (3,923 participants) evaluated the Biocredit LDH-based RDT for P. falciparum and P. vivax detection. Pooled sensitivity was 91% (95% CI 77-97%) for P. falciparum and 96% (95% CI 88-99%) for P. vivax, with pooled specificity of 99% and 100%, respectively. Against microscopy, sensitivity was 95% for P. falciparum and 97% for P. vivax; against PCR, 81% and 90%. In high transmission settings, P. falciparum sensitivity was 94%, but in moderate transmission areas only 73%.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genetic diversity in Myanmar border regions shows regional variation
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum genetic diversity in border areas of Myanmar assessed by genotyping merozoite surface protein genes.
articles.details: Genotyping of Msp1 and Msp2 in 176 P. falciparum isolates from three regions showed MAD20 as the most frequent Msp1 allele (89% in Myitsone, 85% in Bhamo, 67% in Paletwa). Multiplicity of infection (MOI) from Msp1 was higher in migrant workers (1.87) than local residents in Bhamo (1.33) and Paletwa (1.56), with 72% of migrants harboring multi-infections compared to 43-45% of locals, suggesting lower immunity in migrant populations or higher transmission in Myitsone.

articles.headline: Heme oxygenase-1 shows dual protective and pathological roles in malaria
articles.title_original: Heme oxygenase-1 and malaria pathogenesis.
articles.authors: Wakai T, Chinedu S, Afolabi I
articles.details: This review examines heme oxygenase-1 (HO-1), an inducible enzyme induced during Plasmodium infection that modulates inflammation and oxidative damage. In murine models, HO-1 induction protects against severe malaria complications including cerebral malaria by reducing pro-inflammatory cytokines and vascular permeability. However, elevated HO-1 in specific immune cells is associated with heightened inflammation and disease severity in humans, highlighting context-dependent effects. The authors discuss HO-1's potential as a diagnostic/prognostic biomarker and therapeutic target.

articles.headline: In silico screening identifies DHODH inhibitor candidates from marine metabolites
articles.title_original: Structure-Based Screening of Deep-Sea Microbial Metabolites Against Plasmodium falciparum Dihydroorotate Dehydrogenase.
articles.authors: Singh A, Rengasamy KRR, Benjakul S
articles.details: An integrated computational pipeline screened 1,549 deep-sea marine microbial metabolites against Plasmodium falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase (PfDHODH). Five top-ranked compounds had Glide scores (-12.02 to -10.61 kcal/mol) superior to primaquine (-6.920 kcal/mol), with MM-GBSA binding free energies between -63.28 and -31.37 kcal/mol. Lead compounds (±)-puniceusine P and 15-O-methyl ML-236A demonstrated stable protein-ligand complexes in 100 ns molecular dynamics simulations and acceptable pharmacokinetic properties, pending experimental validation.

articles.headline: Malaria attributable fraction varies by age in high-transmission Uganda
articles.title_original: Estimating malaria attributable fraction using quantitative PCR in a longitudinal cohort in Eastern Uganda.
articles.details: A longitudinal study of 659 individuals from three zones in eastern Uganda over up to 3 years used qPCR parasite density distributions to estimate the malaria attributable fraction (MAF) of fever. Overall MAF ranged from 54-64%, highest in children <5 years (72%), intermediate in 5-15 year-olds (56%), and lowest in adults >16 years (45%). Notably, 50% of fevers with parasite density 10-100/µL were attributable to malaria. MAF-corrected incidence was 18% higher in children <5, 7% higher in 5-15 year-olds, and 70% higher in adults compared to the standard ≥5000/µL definition.

articles.headline: TGF-β-Tim-3 axis maintains protective macrophage function in malaria
articles.title_original: TGF-β regulated Tim-3 sustains macrophage phagocytic function and confers protection in Plasmodium yoelii NSM-infected mice.
articles.details: A murine model of Plasmodium yoelii NSM infection revealed infection-induced downregulation of Tim-3 on splenic macrophages. Tim-3+ macrophages exhibited enhanced antigen presentation and elevated proinflammatory phenotype. Tim-3 blockade in vivo increased parasitemia and impaired macrophage phagocytosis without affecting T-cell responses. TGF-β emerged as a key upstream regulator; TGF-β-induced Tim-3 upregulation enhanced macrophage phagocytosis of infected RBCs and conferred protection against cell death.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis identifies malaria vulnerability hotspots in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria vulnerability hotspots using multi-criteria decision analysis, GIS, and remote sensing: a case study in Abaya Woreda, West Guji Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.details: A geospatial study using GIS, remote sensing (Landsat 8, SRTM, CHIRPS), and multi-criteria decision analysis analyzed ten climatic, topographic, environmental, and demographic factors in Abaya Woreda, Ethiopia. Analytic Hierarchy Process and weighted overlay analysis revealed 38.3% of the area as highly vulnerable to malaria, 49.1% moderately vulnerable, and 12.6% low vulnerability. High vulnerability areas were associated with lower elevations, wetlands, water bodies, greater distances from health facilities, and high population density.

articles.headline: Iron deficiency protects against Plasmodium falciparum infection
articles.title_original: Host iron deficiency protects against Plasmodium infection and drives parasite molecular reprofiling.
articles.details: Clinical study (n=711 pregnant women, REVAMP trial) with genetic mouse models and in vitro cultures. Iron deficiency associated with 50% reduced P. falciparum parasitemia [95% CI 30–64%, P<0.0001]. Iron-deficient mice showed improved survival (median 15.5 vs 7.0 days) and protection from cerebral malaria (83% vs 17%). Iron supplementation with malaria prevention did not increase parasitemia.

articles.headline: ANG2/ACE2 biomarkers predict P. falciparum–SARS-CoV-2 coinfection severity
articles.title_original: Elevated ANG2/ACE2 and immune responses associated with Plasmodium falciparum and SARS-CoV-2 coinfection in Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 96 participants (COVID-19 n=28, malaria n=28, coinfection n=16, controls n=24) in Cameroon. Coinfected group exhibited elevated biochemical markers (ALT, AST, urea, creatinine, erythropoietin) and distinct immune profiles with increased IFN-γ and IL-1β. ACE2 demonstrated strong predictive value for disease severity (AUC 0.77–0.85) for both conditions, suggesting clinical utility for risk stratification.

articles.headline: Diagnostic-resistant hrp2-/3- P. falciparum spread in Peru
articles.title_original: Malaria control and the unexpected spread of diagnostic-resistant Plasmodium falciparum in Peru.
articles.details: Genomic analysis of 1,215 P. falciparum samples (2003–2018) from Loreto, Peru. After PAMAFRO control program, hrp2-/3- parasites became fixed despite low RDT use. MIP sequencing across >2,000 loci revealed reduced genetic diversity, increased clonality, and rapid expansion of single hrp2-/3- lineage. Simulations indicated selection coefficient ≥0.03 required; PAMAFRO demographic bottleneck increased drift likelihood 4.5–17 fold.
articles.notable_reason: Diagnostic-resistant parasites fixed after control program; threatens RDT management

articles.headline: Post-transfusion malaria cases in non-endemic African countries
articles.title_original: Post-transfusion Malaria in Morocco and Non-endemic African Countries: A Systematic Review of Reported Cases.
articles.details: Systematic review of post-transfusion malaria (PTM) cases in non-endemic African countries. Twenty cases identified; six with sufficient detail. P. falciparum predominated; P. malariae documented. Most involved immunocompromised recipients. Fever days after transfusion was typical presentation; diagnosis by peripheral blood smear. Asymptomatic donor parasitemia and gaps in donor risk assessment highlighted as key concerns.

articles.headline: UAV imagery for malaria vector breeding site surveillance
articles.title_original: High-resolution UAV dataset for identification of breeding sites for malaria and dengue vectors in rural and peri-urban Tanzania: An experimental approach with Gemma 3N E4B architecture.
articles.authors: Nyambo DG, Lyimo TM, Sauli E
articles.details: High-resolution UAV dataset from Tanzania (Mtwara, Tabora regions) with 471 aerial images identifying 19 classes of mosquito breeding sites. Images processed with Gemma 3 N E4B multimodal architecture, achieving final loss of 0.0319 (96.7% reduction from initial 9.620) within 117 training steps, demonstrating convergence on breeding site classification.

articles.headline: Molecular docking and ML identify antimalarial compounds
articles.title_original: Gene Expression, Docking and Machine Learning in Malaria Drug Discovery: A Systematic Review.
articles.details: Systematic review (PRISMA-guided, 2014–2024, 64 studies) of molecular methods for malaria drug discovery. Molecular docking was dominant technique (32.37%), followed by in vitro assays (14.39%). Key compounds—isorhamnetin and myricetin—showed high affinities to Plasmepsin II and PfLDH (ΔG < −13 kcal/mol). Machine learning models achieved AUC up to 0.87 for bioactivity prediction; flavonoids and terpenoids identified as candidates.

articles.headline: Community awareness of climate change impacts on malaria
articles.title_original: Community perceptions on climate change and its impacts on malaria transmission in South-eastern Tanzania.
articles.details: Mixed-methods cross-sectional study in SE Tanzania with 384 community members. 86% reported climate changes (altered seasons, flooding); 67.5% linked climate to malaria transmission. 59.5% reported increased vector-borne disease risk; 70% noted higher malaria during rainy season. Only 26.6% regularly received climate/health information despite 96.6% wanting it. Highlights need to integrate community knowledge in adaptation strategies.

articles.headline: Barriers and facilitators to severe malaria treatment guideline adherence
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers to adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines in children at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda: a health workers' perspective.
articles.details: Qualitative descriptive study with 21 healthcare providers at Mbale Regional Referral Hospital, Uganda. Facilitators: resource availability, training, manageable workloads, monitoring tools, caretaker cooperation. Barriers: resource constraints, staffing/workload issues, delayed referrals, lab delays, limited CME attendance, patient financial limitations. Concluded resource improvement and enhanced health system infrastructure critical for improving guideline adherence and case outcomes.

articles.headline: Homo erectus arrival triggered human malaria vector evolution
articles.title_original: Early hominin arrival in Southeast Asia triggered the evolution of major human malaria vectors.
articles.details: Phylogenomic analysis of 40 individuals from 11 Anopheles Leucosphyrus species using 2,657 nuclear genes and 13 mitochondrial genes. Ancestral state reconstruction showed monkey-feeding is ancestral (Pliocene, Sundaland). Anthropophily evolved once in early Pleistocene via adaptive introgression, likely responding to H. erectus arrival (~1.8 Mya) and rainforest loss. Provides non-archaeological evidence supporting early hominin colonization of Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: ARK1 and divergent CPC regulate Plasmodium atypical mitosis
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.
articles.details: Study identifying Aurora-related kinase 1 (ARK1) as key component of Plasmodium MTOC and spindle formation. ARK1 depletion disrupted spindle biogenesis, kinetochore segregation, karyokinesis, cytokinesis, and transmission. ARK1 forms non-canonical chromosomal passenger complex with divergent INCENPs (INCENP-A and INCENP-B), lacking canonical Survivin and Borealin. Identified ARK1-INCENP interface as potential multistage antimalarial drug target.

articles.headline: Multi-stage therapeutics enhance seasonal malaria chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Combining seasonal malaria chemoprevention with novel therapeutics for malaria prevention: a mathematical modelling study.
articles.details: Individual-based transmission model evaluating combinations of novel therapeutics (vaccines, monoclonal antibodies, long-acting injectables) with seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) in children under 5. Pre-liver stage therapeutics required extended duration (>230 days half-life) to achieve >23% reduction in severe malaria with 3 SMC cycles. Multi-stage therapeutics increased impact both during and after deployment, recommending prioritization of multi-stage candidates.

articles.headline: Compound 15 shows transmission-blocking activity and mouse efficacy
articles.title_original: Identification of Compound 15 (MMV1581361) as a PfATP4 Inhibitor with Transmission-Blocking Activity and In Vivo Efficacy in a SCID Mouse Model of Malaria.
articles.details: Structure-activity-relationship studies identified compound 15 (MMV1581361) as PfATP4 inhibitor with nanomolar activity against drug-sensitive, drug-resistant, and field isolates of P. falciparum. Efficacy in humanized mouse model at 25 mg/kg single dose. Transmission-blocking: 90% inhibition of male exflagellation, 98% reduction in oocyst intensity, 88% transmission block. Further development deprioritised due to resistance risk and more advanced candidates.

articles.headline: Correction to Raf kinase inhibitor interaction study
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Plasmodium falciparum Raf kinase inhibitor is a lipid binding protein that interacts with and regulates the activity of PfCDPK1, an essential plant-like kinase required for red blood cell invasion" [Biochem. Biophys. Res. Commun. 749 (2025) 151350].
articles.details: Corrigendum to a paper on Plasmodium falciparum Raf kinase inhibitor interactions with PfCDPK1, an essential kinase required for red blood cell invasion. No new findings in this correction notice.

articles.headline: Microscopy and nPCR reveal malaria misdiagnosis in febrile children
articles.title_original: Unmasking Malaria: Microscopy and nPCR Reveal the Hidden Misdiagnosis of Plasmodium Infections Among Febrile Pediatric in Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 302 febrile pediatric patients in Northwest Ethiopia found 22.2% malaria prevalence by microscopy and 18.2% by nPCR, with highest rates in children ≥5 years (25.6%). Age, lack of bed net use, and malaria awareness were significantly associated with infection (p < 0.05). Authors recommend improved microscopy training and quality control.

articles.headline: Anopheles cruzii comprises at least five cryptic species
articles.title_original: Anopheles (Kerteszia) cruzii, the main malaria vector in the Brazilian Atlantic Forest, is a complex of at least five cryptic species.
articles.details: Whole-genome sequencing of 55 Anopheles mosquitoes revealed at least five cryptic species within An. cruzii with high genetic differentiation (FST 0.4-0.7) and evidence of sympatry, plus at least three lineages within An. bellator. These cryptic species distinctions are important for understanding malaria transmission dynamics in the Atlantic Forest, as vector competence may differ among species.

articles.headline: Host kinases regulate P. vivax liver-stage parasite development
articles.title_original: Host kinase regulation of Plasmodium vivax dormant and replicating liver stages.
articles.details: Kinase regression analysis identified host kinases regulating P. vivax schizont and hypnozoite numbers and schizont size in liver-stage parasites. Variable host phosphosignaling dependencies exist between parasite forms and across patient isolates, suggesting parasite genetic and host-environmental interactions may underlie observed heterogeneity in relapse timing and frequency.

articles.headline: Bradykinin pathway drives cerebral malaria brain edema
articles.title_original: Bradykinin Contributes to Vasogenic Edema in Murine Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.details: Elevated bradykinin and cleaved high-molecular-weight kininogen were found in 40% of children with cerebral malaria versus 18% with uncomplicated malaria. Genetic deletion of kallikrein components or bradykinin receptors protected mice from neurologic deterioration and brain edema. Kallikrein inhibition combined with artesunate reversed neurologic deterioration and brain edema and prolonged survival versus artesunate alone.

articles.headline: Live-cell assay quantifies P. falciparum proteasome activity
articles.title_original: Profiling 26S Proteasome Activity of Plasmodium falciparum Monitored by a Live-Cell Assay.
articles.details: Researchers generated a P. falciparum line expressing a destabilization domain-fused GFP reporter providing quantitative readout of proteasomal activity. Proteasome inhibition with MG132 induced dose-dependent fluorescence increase. Shield-1 titration identified optimal stabilization range. This platform enables phenotypic screening and antimalarial resistance studies.

articles.headline: Scoping review protocol for urban malaria in Bamako
articles.title_original: Trends in malaria indicators in urban and peri-urban areas of Bamako: a scoping review protocol.
articles.details: Protocol for a scoping review to assess malaria epidemiology trends in urban and peri-urban Bamako from 2002-2025, focusing on prevalence, incidence, clinical manifestations, and transmission dynamics. Review will search multiple databases and grey literature to inform tailored urban malaria control strategies in a region facing rapid urbanization and emerging vector threats.

articles.headline: Non-falciparum malaria common in West Cameroon facility cases
articles.title_original: Distribution of falciparum and non-falciparum malaria among symptomatic malaria patients in Dschang, West Region of Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 431 clinically suspected malaria cases in West Cameroon found 54.8% positive for Plasmodium. P. falciparum dominated at 53.4%, but P. ovale (4%) and P. malariae (0.9%) were also common, with 8.9% mixed infections. Of 12 genotyped P. ovale, 6 were P. o. curtisi and 3 were P. o. wallikeri.

articles.headline: Eight ANC visits increase pregnancy malaria prevention uptake
articles.title_original: Impact of eight or more antenatal care visits on intermittent preventive treatment of malaria uptake during pregnancy and facility-based delivery in Ghana: a propensity score matched analysis.
articles.details: Propensity score analysis of 2022 Ghana DHS data found that ≥8 antenatal care visits increased facility-based delivery by 8.2 percentage points and optimal intermittent preventive treatment for malaria in pregnancy (≥3 IPTp-SP doses) by 11.1 percentage points. Overall, 38.7% met the ANC8+ threshold, 85.4% delivered in facilities, and 66.5% received ≥3 IPTp-SP doses.

articles.headline: P. falciparum RNA hijacks host splicing to evade immunity
articles.title_original: Nuclear import of malaria RNA rewires splicing in host immune cells.
articles.details: Plasmodium falciparum exports mRNAs of early transcribed membrane proteins from infected red blood cells that evade cytoplasmic degradation and are imported into host immune cell nuclei. These parasite transcripts bind host RNA-binding proteins ACIN1 and PNN—splicing machinery components—disrupting host splicing regulation, causing widespread transcript misprocessing and altered immune function protein expression.
articles.notable_reason: Novel RNA-based virulence mechanism exploiting host splicing

articles.headline: Diagnostic methods reviewed for three protozoan diseases
articles.title_original: Diagnostic methods for protozoan diseases: a review focused on leishmaniasis, Chagas disease and malaria.
articles.details: Review discussing diagnostic approaches for leishmaniasis, Chagas disease, and malaria in Brazil. Examines immunological and molecular diagnostic tools in context of Brazil's epidemiological landscape, emphasizing integration of clinical and epidemiological information with sensitive and specific diagnostic techniques for early detection and control in endemic areas.

articles.headline: Malnutrition increases malaria risk despite seasonal chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Malaria and malnutrition in children under the Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention (SMC) coverage in the health district of Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Longitudinal survey of 425 children aged 6-59 months receiving seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Burkina Faso (2020-2022) found malnourished children had higher malaria incidence (974.47 per 1000 child-months) compared to adequately nourished children (214.47). Malnourished children were 1.42 times more likely to develop clinical malaria (95% CI 1.03-1.94, p=0.028), suggesting combined SMC and nutritional supplementation is needed.

articles.headline: Malaria risk factors in Chinese workers in Uganda
articles.title_original: Epidemiological characteristics, prevention and control status, and factors associated with malaria infection among overseas Chinese workforce in Uganda.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 798 Chinese workers in Uganda (2022-2023) found 44.5% reported malaria history. Male sex (AOR=1.92), longer residence (AOR=1.38), negative prevention attitudes (AOR=2.49), and higher surrounding transmission (AOR=1.95) significantly increased infection risk, while higher education (AOR=0.65) and living with spouse (AOR=0.57) were protective. Integrated strategy needed including improved living conditions, education, and drug access.

articles.headline: Thrombotic DIC in severe falciparum malaria case
articles.title_original: Disseminated Intravascular Coagulation With a Predominant Thrombotic Phenotype in Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria: A Case Report.
articles.details: Case report of 61-year-old with severe P. falciparum malaria (parasitemia >25%) complicated by disseminated intravascular coagulation with predominant thrombotic phenotype. Despite prompt treatment with intravenous artesunate, anticoagulation, and intensive care, patient developed progressive peripheral ischemia requiring amputation and ultimately died. Report highlights an underrecognized thrombotic presentation of malaria-associated DIC.

articles.headline: Malaria programs during COVID-19: scoping review gap
articles.title_original: Implementation of malaria control programmes during the COVID-19 pandemic in the Southern African Development Community Elimination 8 countries: A scoping review.
articles.authors: Muzamhindo DN, Chironda G, Tsoka-Gwegweni JM
articles.journal: Afr J Prim Health Care Fam Med
articles.details: Scoping review examining malaria control implementation during COVID-19 in Southern African Development Community Elimination 8 countries. Of 658 articles screened, only 7 met inclusion criteria with most published in 2021. Review identified significant research gap and highlights need for policies supporting malaria program continuation during pandemics and for future pandemic preparedness.

articles.headline: P. falciparum genetic metrics predict prevalence in Uganda
articles.title_original: Accuracy of Plasmodium falciparum genetic data for estimating parasite prevalence and malaria incidence in Uganda.
articles.details: Study of 3,563 symptomatic P. falciparum cases across 26 Uganda sites found genetic diversity metrics (complexity of infection, eCOI) reliably predicted parasite prevalence (pooled cross-validated r=0.79) across spatial variation. Between-host relatedness was strongest predictor of incidence but with weaker performance (r=0.37). Microhaplotype-based metrics capture prevalence differences but have limited utility for incidence prediction.

articles.headline: Cross-border movement and malaria importation in Zimbabwe
articles.title_original: Role of cross-border movement on the importation of malaria cases in Mudzi district, Zimbabwe.
articles.details: Analysis of 619 malaria cases in Mudzi district, Zimbabwe found only 16.3% reported cross-border movement history, with highest proportion (96%) aged 10-49 years and concentrated at facilities near the border. Only 7.9% of cases were Mozambique residents. Findings suggest cross-border movement contributes minimally to overall malaria burden despite district's border location.

articles.headline: Blood purification in severe malaria with MODS
articles.title_original: Correction: The blood purification therapy-based strategy effectively rescued the severe falciparum malaria patient experiencing cytokine storm-driven MODS.
articles.details: Correction notice for article on blood purification therapy for severe P. falciparum malaria complicated by cytokine storm and multiorgan dysfunction syndrome.

articles.headline: Persistent SP resistance mutations in P. falciparum Kenya
articles.title_original: Persistent and Circulating Plasmodium falciparum dhfr and dhps Mutations in Busia County, Western Kenya.
articles.details: Molecular analysis of 66 P. falciparum isolates from Western Kenya (November 2024-January 2025) revealed high frequencies of dhfr mutations (N51I 85.2%, C59R 75.4%, S108N 32.8%) and dhps mutations (A437G 92.3%, K540E 40%). Circulation of both canonical triple-mutant (N51I+C59R+S108N) and rare quintuple haplotypes (N51I+C59R+S108T+I164L+A437G) indicates emerging non-canonical patterns. Findings highlight need for surveillance assessing impact on sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine efficacy.

articles.headline: Temperature-responsive chromatin in P. falciparum ring stage
articles.title_original: Integration of ATAC-seq and RNA-seq reveals temperature-responsive regulatory regions in Plasmodium falciparum asexual stages.
articles.details: Integration of ATAC-seq and RNA-seq in synchronized P. falciparum revealed that low temperature (26°C, mimicking mosquito) selectively induced 1,083 differentially accessible chromatin regions (1,081 gains) in ring-stage parasites but not trophozoites, with 41 genes showing concordant changes in accessibility and expression. Identified temperature-responsive transcription factor motifs. Findings suggest stage-specific thermal sensitivity could inform future chromatin-targeted antimalarial strategies.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic P. vivax detected in malaria-free Argentina
articles.title_original: Asymptomatic and submicroscopic Plasmodium vivax infection: a study previous to malaria-free certification in Argentina.
articles.details: Molecular analysis of 92 preserved blood samples collected during Argentina's 2013-2018 surveillance period identified one asymptomatic, submicroscopic P. vivax infection in a 66-year-old with no travel history from a border area with Bolivia. This represents the first molecularly confirmed submicroscopic P. vivax prior to Argentina's 2019 malaria-free certification and highlights risk of silent parasite circulation in supposedly elimination-ready areas.

articles.headline: Knowledge-practice gap in malaria prevention, Ghana
articles.title_original: Knowledge of malaria and preventive practices among Fulani pregnant women in the savannah Region of Northern Ghana.
articles.authors: Salifu Y, Nantomah B, Lasong J
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 159 nomadic Fulani pregnant women in Northern Ghana found 61% demonstrated good malaria knowledge but only 28.9% used insecticide-treated nets despite 54.7% ownership, demonstrating significant knowledge-practice gap. Antenatal care attendance associated with knowledge (AOR=0.31, p=0.020), while number of children predicted practices (AOR=0.29, p=0.032). Findings emphasize need for culturally tailored education and improved ITN access in hard-to-reach populations.

articles.headline: Spatio-temporal modeling predicts Anopheles peaks, malaria risk
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal modelling and prediction of Anopheles mosquito abundance in Tanga and Unguja, Tanzania: climatic drivers and insights for malaria early warning and vector control strategies.
articles.details: Spatio-temporal generalized additive mixed effects modeling of 4,312 (Tanga) and 1,450 (Unguja) Anopheles specimens across Tanzania revealed region-specific climatic drivers: in Tanga higher maximum temperature increased abundance; in Unguja non-linear relationship peaked below 27.5°C. Models showed strong predictive performance (correlation >0.8) and successfully forecasted Anopheles peaks in Unguja November 2023 preceding reported malaria outbreak late 2023/early 2024. Supports climate-based early warning systems.

articles.headline: Mosquito xenomonitoring and blood meal sources Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Molecular xenomonitoring for identifying Plasmodium parasites and blood meal sources of mosquitoes in malaria endemic villages adjacent to the southern Rift Valley Lakes, South Ethiopia.
articles.details: Entomological survey of 446 Anopheles specimens from 12 malaria-endemic villages in South Ethiopia detected Plasmodium vivax in 2.0% (9/446) and P. falciparum in 0.2% (1/446). Blood meal analysis showed mosquitoes fed predominantly on goats (67%), followed by humans (23%), cattle (19%), and dogs (13%), with 23% human blood meal index. Anopheles arabiensis comprised 85%. Findings suggest interventions targeting animal sources may complement human-focused control.

articles.headline: Precipitation associated with malaria in Mali children
articles.title_original: Associations between precipitation, temperature, and malaria prevalence in children under 5 in Mali.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 2012-2021 data from Mali DHS/MIS examined associations between precipitation, temperature, and RDT-diagnosed malaria in children 6-59 months. With 3-month lag, precipitation increased odds of malaria (OR 1.005, 95% CI 1.002-1.009); minimum, maximum, and average temperatures decreased odds (ORs 0.761, 0.871, 0.822 respectively). Authors concluded precipitation may be a risk factor while temperature alone likely does not contribute.

articles.headline: Complex III serves dual functions in Plasmodium falciparum
articles.title_original: Dual function of mitochondrial complex III in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Study generated transgenic P. falciparum parasites with conditional mitochondrial processing peptidase α (MPPα) expression to test whether Complex III mediates both electron transport and protein processing. MPPα was essential for mitochondrial protein processing even when electron transport chain function was bypassed; MPPα knockdown increased hypersensitivity to proguanil. Complex III appears to mediate both ubiquinone regeneration and imported protein processing, relevant to antimalarial target validation.

articles.headline: Genomics identifies imported versus local malaria cases in Malaysia
articles.title_original: Whole genome sequencing to inform the epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in the elimination setting of Malaysia.
articles.details: Analyzed 21 P. falciparum whole genomes from Malaysia (2008-2010) using nanopore sequencing versus 9,518 public isolates from Africa, Oceania, South America, and Asia. Found 13/15 cases likely imported from endemic regions; identity-by-descent analysis showed only 2 cases clustered, indicating isolated introductions rather than sustained local transmission. Two cases showed genomic evidence of origins inconsistent with reported travel histories, demonstrating the power of genomics over traditional epidemiological methods in elimination settings.

articles.headline: Transmembrane proteins essential for malaria parasite cell division
articles.title_original: Transmembrane proteins mediate basal complex assembly and individual daughter cell formation in malaria parasites.
articles.details: Used expansion microscopy and DNA-PAINT to investigate three transmembrane basal complex proteins (BTP1, BTP2, BLEB) in P. falciparum. Parasites lacking BTP2 failed to separate from each other despite intact contractile ability, resulting in multiorganellar mutants. Identified a distinct plasma membrane region excluded from daughter cells associated with BLEB. Results propose a three-step model for basal complex biogenesis highlighting specific roles of transmembrane proteins in daughter cell formation.

articles.headline: Carbamate resistance mechanisms in malaria vector Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: Mechanisms of carbamate resistance in the malaria vector: Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: Review article discussing mechanisms of carbamate insecticide resistance in female Anopheles gambiae, the primary African malaria vector. Resistance arises from target site mutations in the acetylcholinesterase gene and increased detoxification by esterases and cytochrome P450 enzymes. Understanding these mechanisms is important for insecticide resistance management, sustaining effectiveness of indoor residual spraying, and guiding malaria control policies in areas where pyrethroid resistance is increasing.

articles.headline: PCR more sensitive than RDT and microscopy for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Diagnostic challenges in malaria detection: A comparative diagnostic performance of HRP2-based rapid diagnostic tests, microscopy, and PCR at Bichena primary hospital, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Minwuyelet A, Yewhalaw D, Atenafu G
articles.details: Cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study of 274 participants in Northwest Ethiopia compared nested PCR, HRP2-based RDT, and microscopy. PCR detected 23.4% Plasmodium infections versus 19% (RDT) and 20.1% (microscopy). HRP2-RDT showed lower sensitivity (79.1%) and accuracy (94.9%) compared to PCR; microscopy had high specificity (100%) but sensitivity 85.9%. Weak RDT-PCR agreement (κ = 0.847) suggests pfhrp2 deletions may impair RDT performance in the region.

articles.headline: Ecological, health system, behavioral factors drive malaria resurgence in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Understanding malaria resurgence in the Amhara Region, northwestern Ethiopia: a qualitative study of perceived drivers from stakeholder and community perspectives.
articles.details: Qualitative study with 28 key informant interviews, 2 FGDs, and 12 in-depth interviews in Amhara Region (February 2025) investigated malaria resurgence coinciding with confirmed cases rising from 1.0 million (2018) to 7.3 million (October 2024). Stakeholders identified perceived drivers including irregular rainfall, irrigation schemes, land use change, health system stockouts of drugs and diagnostics, and behavioral factors like low perceived risk and ITN misuse. Authors concluded resurgence arises from interacting ecological, health system, and behavioral dynamics exacerbated by conflict.

articles.headline: Expired malaria RDTs valid up to nine months post-expiration
articles.title_original: Test Performance of Expired Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests: A pilot diagnostic accuracy study conducted in Western Uganda.
articles.details: Pilot diagnostic accuracy study of 200 participants in western Uganda evaluated expired malaria RDTs (SD Bioline Ag Pf/Pan, First Response Ag pLDH/HRP2) against non-expired reference tests. Of 200 participants, 21 (10.5%) tested positive and 179 (89.5%) tested negative. All tests up to 9 months post-expiration were valid and consistent with reference; all tests 10-12 months post-expiration were invalid. Authors suggest further research into using expired RDTs in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: ITN use reduces malaria odds by 13 percent in Ghana
articles.title_original: Evaluating the effectiveness of insecticide-treated net use in preventing malaria among children under five: a quasi-experimental study.
articles.authors: Osei KM, Adiak AA, Han W
articles.details: Quasi-experimental study of 11,481 children in Ghana examined ITN effectiveness using nationally representative georeferenced survey data and propensity score weighting. ITN use was associated with 13% reduction in odds of malaria (aOR 0.87, 95% CI 0.81-0.93) and 2.2% absolute risk reduction (RD -0.022, 95% CI -0.037 to -0.006). Spatial analyses revealed misalignment between ITN coverage and malaria burden. Authors concluded ITNs provide meaningful but limited protection; strengthening control requires attention to behavioral and contextual drivers.

articles.headline: Deep learning framework for automated Plasmodium vivax detection
articles.title_original: Enhanced YOLO-based framework and benchmarking for automated Plasmodium vivax detection.
articles.details: Comparative evaluation of YOLO deep learning variants (v3, cascade v3, v4, v5, v8) for automated malaria parasite detection. Developed novel framework combining YOLOv3 with modified MobileNetV2 backbone and Transformed Convolutional Layer for analyzing thick smear images of P. vivax, with multi-scale texture sensitivity for dense parasite identification. Framework assessed on precision, accuracy, F1-score, recall, and mean Average Precision. Authors propose this approach for deploying AI-based diagnostic tools in varied clinical and field settings.

articles.headline: Fluoroquinolones inhibit Plasmodium falciparum plasmepsin II through catalytic site binding
articles.title_original: Physico-Chemical Insights into the Catalytic Inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum Plasmepsin II Activity by Repurposing Fluoroquinolone Drugs.
articles.details: Biochemical study evaluated fluoroquinolone drugs (ofloxacin, levofloxacin, moxifloxacin) as inhibitors of P. falciparum plasmepsin II (aspartic protease essential for hemoglobin degradation). Moxifloxacin showed most potent inhibition with IC50 0.15 ± 0.02 μM through hydrogen bonding with catalytic dyad Asp34 and Asp214. QM/MM calculations confirmed binding interactions (donor-acceptor distances 2.8-3.3 Å); even minimal structural modifications like NH methylation impair inhibitory efficacy. Findings suggest potential for fluoroquinolone repurposing against malaria.

articles.headline: Primaquine reduces Plasmodium vivax transmission in randomized trial
articles.title_original: Efficacy of primaquine-chloroquine combination on Plasmodium vivax transmission and parasite clearance in Ethiopia: a randomized controlled trial.
articles.details: Open-label RCT in Ethiopia compared chloroquine (CQ) alone versus CQ plus primaquine (PQ, 0.25 mg/kg daily × 14 days) in patients ≥15 years with P. vivax mono-infection (n=49 at 28-day follow-up). Membrane feeding assays with Anopheles arabiensis showed adding PQ significantly reduced infectiousness at day 1 (P<0.0001), oocyst numbers (P<0.001), CSP rate (P=0.002), and gametocyte count (P=0.02). No difference in asexual parasite clearance by day 3 (P=0.42). Authors concluded PQ reduced transmission but some patients remained infectious 24 hours post-treatment.

articles.headline: An. gambiae dominates malaria vectors in Ethiopian Hadiya Zone
articles.title_original: Species Occurrence and Seasonal Variation of Malaria Vectors in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.details: Entomological survey of larval habitats (October 2023–June 2024) in 4 villages collected 721 larvae representing 4 Anopheles species. An. gambiae s.l. was dominant (43.82%, n=316) and An. pharoensis least abundant (7.9%, n=57). Ditches had highest mean larval density (2.61 larvae/sample) versus water pans (0.14), with overall mean 1.11 larvae/sample. Larval abundance significantly differed across habitat types (p=0.012).

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 14.1% in Burkina Faso under-five children
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and healthcare determinants of malaria infection among children under five in Burkina Faso: analysis of the 2021 Demographic and Health Survey data.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 5674 children (6–59 months) from 2021 Demographic and Health Survey found 14.1% malaria prevalence (95% CI 13–15) with substantial regional variation (5.1%–29.3%). Risk increased with age (adjusted prevalence ratio 2.07 at 24–35 months) and was higher in rural areas (aPR 2.21), households without electricity (aPR 2.01), and poor households (aPR 1.60).

articles.headline: Plasmodium retrocopies linked to virulence-associated molecular pathways
articles.title_original: Comprehensive computational insights into Plasmodium retrocopies reveals potential roles in virulence-associated functions.
articles.authors: Das S, Ghosh S, Thakur S
articles.details: Systematic characterization across 23 Plasmodium genomes identified 724 high-confidence retrocopies with canonical retroposition signatures. Retrocopies were frequently intact, under purifying selection, and showed detectable transcription during intraerythrocytic stages with some exceeding parental gene expression levels. Domain and pathway analyses linked retrocopies to virulence-associated processes including cytoadhesion, chromatin remodeling, and immune evasion.

articles.headline: α-Thalassemia not associated with P. falciparum carriage Ghana
articles.title_original: α-Thalassemia has no association with asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum carriage in three ecological zones of Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 1401 asymptomatic individuals (1–60 years) across 3 Ghana ecological zones found malaria prevalence by PCR ranged from 23.5% (coastal) to 36.5% (forest). Although homozygous α-thalassemia individuals had higher odds of asexual parasites (odds ratio 1.23) and gametocytes (OR 1.46) compared to wild-type, these associations were not statistically significant (p=0.447). No significant association observed between α-thalassemia genotype and P. falciparum carriage.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi awareness suboptimal in Thailand endemic communities
articles.title_original: Community awareness and health providers' perspectives on zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi malaria in Thailand: A mixed-methods assessment.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of 300 community members and 28 healthcare providers in P. knowlesi-endemic southern Thailand found moderate overall awareness (mean score 10.7/23, SD±2.9). Female individuals, those living farther from health facilities, receiving malaria education, and with higher attitude scores showed significantly increased awareness (p<0.05). Healthcare providers reported declining P. knowlesi incidence but persistent transmission in forested areas.

articles.headline: Pir gene expression differs between severe P. vivax manifestations
articles.title_original: Comparative clinical transcriptome of pir genes in severe Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.details: Transcriptomic analysis of parasites from 23 patients (uncomplicated n=6, hepatic dysfunction n=12, cerebral malaria n=5) showed differential pir gene expression across severe manifestations. Twenty-four pir genes were upregulated in cerebral malaria and 28 in hepatic dysfunction groups, each expressed in at least 50% of severe malaria patients. Cerebral malaria showed preferential upregulation of vir subfamily E (8 genes) and pvpir H (6 genes) compared to hepatic dysfunction.

articles.headline: Folic acid supplementation effect with antifolates remains uncertain
articles.title_original: Folic acid supplementation and malaria susceptibility and severity among people taking antifolate antimalarial drugs in endemic areas.
articles.details: Cochrane systematic review of 8 trials (3486 participants, 3 prevention and 5 treatment) examining folic acid supplementation with antifolate antimalarials. Most treatment trials used folic acid doses >1.0 mg/day. For malaria prevention, one trial found minimal difference in parasitemia between folic acid plus iron plus antifolates versus iron plus antifolates (risk ratio 1.21; 95% CI 0.56–2.62; p=0.63). Primary outcome data were largely unavailable.

articles.headline: Medieval malaria burden mapped in Netherlands via skeletal markers
articles.title_original: Mapping medieval malaria in the Netherlands: A bioarchaeological approach using cribra orbitalia as a proxy.
articles.details: Spatial epidemiological study of 28 medieval Netherlands archaeological sites using cribra orbitalia (orbital porosis) as proxy for malaria-related hemolytic anemia. Global Moran's I revealed significant positive spatial autocorrelation; local analysis identified High-High prevalence clusters in Southwest and Low-Low clusters in East Netherlands. Cribra orbitalia prevalence was significantly positively correlated with historical (1938) estimates of Anopheles atroparvus mosquito density.

articles.headline: Adaptive immunity imposes survival-reproduction tradeoff malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Immunity can impose a reproduction-survival tradeoff on human malaria parasites.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling of within-host malaria dynamics incorporating adaptive immunity demonstrates optimal transmission investment is reduced to approximately 5%, compared to models lacking immunity feedback. The model shows parasites benefit from delaying transmission investment to allow faster within-host multiplication. Adaptive immunity imposes an emergent survival-reproduction tradeoff affecting infection duration and timing of infectiousness.

articles.headline: P. ovale and P. falciparum show seasonal antagonism Tanzania coast
articles.title_original: Seasonal Variation and Interspecies Dynamics Among Plasmodium falciparum and ovale Species in Bagamoyo, Tanzania.
articles.details: PCR-based screening of 7173 asymptomatic individuals (≥5 years) from coastal Tanzania (2018–2022) found P. falciparum (27.4%) and P. ovale (11.5%) with distinct epidemiology. P. falciparum associated with younger age, male sex, and higher rainfall; P. ovale peaked when P. falciparum declined, with fewer co-infections during long wet season. Excess Pocurtis/P. wallikeri co-infections composed 23% of P. ovale-positive isolates across all seasons.

articles.headline: Two-thirds submicroscopic P. vivax infections clear spontaneously
articles.title_original: Spontaneous Clearance of Polymerase Chain Reaction-Positive Plasmodium vivax Parasitemia in Northwestern Thailand: An Epidemiological Perspective.
articles.details: Longitudinal cohort study in northwestern Thailand (2018–2023) with 14 mass blood surveys (~5000 individuals) followed 101 participants with PCR-positive, submicroscopic P. vivax parasitemia. Seventy-two of 101 infections (69.9%) cleared spontaneously within 3 subsequent survey rounds, with survival analysis showing 25% persistence by day 47 and 7% by day 232. Clearance was not significantly associated with demographic factors.

articles.headline: Source-sink P. vivax dynamics threaten Brazilian Amazon elimination
articles.title_original: The Source-Sink Dynamics of Plasmodium vivax May Undermine Malaria Elimination Efforts in the Amazon: An Epidemiological and Population Genomic Study.
articles.details: Analysis of 5.5 million case notifications (2003–2023) and whole-genome sequencing of 408 P. vivax isolates shows nearly one-third of infections in Brazilian Amazon residents acquired outside their locality/municipality; only 1.7% imported from other South American countries (mostly Guiana Shield). Large cities (Manaus, Porto Velho) act as receptive sinks surrounded by high-risk rural sources. Parasites >1000 km apart often remained genetically connected.

articles.headline: Novel peptide vaccine combining CSP and AgTRIO proteins improves survival in mice
articles.title_original: A Multivalent Peptide Vaccine Against Malaria, Targeting Plasmodium Circumsporozoite Protein and Mosquito AgTRIO.
articles.details: Researchers developed a chimeric peptide vaccine (PfAg) targeting Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein (CSP) and mosquito AgTRIO protein. Both inbred (C57BL/6) and outbred (CD-1) mice administered PfAg generated robust humoral immune responses. After exposure to P. berghei-infected mosquitoes, PfAg-immunized mice showed significantly improved survival compared with controls, suggesting this dual-target approach may aid vaccine development.

articles.headline: Socioeconomic status and water management shape malaria prevention in Indian cities
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic and household water management determinants of malaria and other vector-borne disease prevention in Urban Gujarat, India.
articles.details: A cross-sectional survey of 4,074 households (15,484 individuals) in Ahmedabad and Surat (September-November 2023) examined malaria prevalence and prevention behaviors. Self-reported disease incidence was 18.90 cases per 1,000 households. Socioeconomic factors including wealth quintile, caste, and family size significantly predicted prevention practices, with wealthier households more likely to adopt prevention measures but less likely to perceive mosquito-related risks.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis reviews DSM265 dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor pharmacokinetics and safety
articles.title_original: Evaluating the safety, pharmacokinetics, and antiparasitic activity of DSM265, a novel antimalarial plasmodium dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor: a systematic review and meta-analysis of early clinical trials.
articles.details: A systematic review and meta-analysis of seven phase I-II trials evaluated the novel antimalarial DSM265, a P. falciparum dihydroorotate dehydrogenase inhibitor. At 400 mg oral suspension, DSM265 achieved Cmax 9.3 μg/mL and T1/2 112.1 hours, with increased adverse events (RR 1.46 [95% CI 1.15-1.84]). The drug showed greater selectivity for P. falciparum than P. vivax and lacked anti-gametocyte activity, though preclinical toxicity signals halted development despite no clinical confirmation.

articles.headline: Blood-free diet sustains mosquito colonies while preserving malaria research capabilities
articles.title_original: Effects of a bloodless diet on fitness and malaria susceptibility in Anopheles mosquitoes from Burkina Faso.
articles.authors: de Sales Hien DF, et al.
articles.details: This study compared BLOODless™ synthetic diet versus blood feeding in Anopheles coluzzii and An. gambiae across seven generations. Wing size remained unaffected across generations, though P. falciparum oocyst prevalence was higher in blood-fed mosquitoes by the 6th generation. Longevity significantly increased in Bloodless-fed mosquitoes by the 7th generation, offering ethical and logistical advantages for vector control programs.

articles.headline: High frequency of Pfdhps mutations linked to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance in Chad
articles.title_original: Resistance profile of the Plasmodium falciparum dihydropteroate synthase gene in three hospitals in the city of Ndjamena, Chad.
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 220 individuals in N'Djamena detected P. falciparum infection in 39.54%, with highest prevalence in children aged 0-5 years. The Pfdhps resistance gene was found in 37.93% of positive cases, with A581G mutation at 54.54% and A437G at 9.09%. A581G was more prevalent in men (30%) and those reporting Fansidar use (30%), highlighting the need for strengthened antimalarial drug resistance surveillance.
