articles.details: Localization and function study of Apicortin (microtubule-associated protein with doublecortin domain) in P. berghei. Live-cell GFP imaging showed Apicortin at apical end of invasive parasite stages; super-resolution and expansion microscopy revealed ringlike structure in apical complex region. Deletion of Apicortin gene had no effect on parasite development, indicating protein is not essential and suggesting redundancy in apical complex stabilization mechanisms.

articles.headline: Community knowledge of asymptomatic malaria varies widely in endemic settings
articles.title_original: To what extent do people in malaria-endemic countries know asymptomatic malaria infections? A systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 11 articles (2010–2024) on asymptomatic malaria knowledge in endemic countries. General public knowledge ranged 14.2%–79.8%; health personnel 88%. Qualitative studies showed varied, lacking awareness and reluctance to adhere to interventions targeting asymptomatic carriers. Authors conclude education on asymptomatic malaria is necessary for elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccines show temporary negative efficacy in high-transmission settings
articles.title_original: Ensuring the Public Health Impact of Malaria Vaccination in High-Transmission Settings.
articles.details: Review of RTS,S and R21 vaccines against P. falciparum. Both showed safety and efficacy in trials but RTS,S demonstrated lower efficacy in higher-transmission settings with temporary periods of negative efficacy approximately 3 years post-primary series. Reduced efficacy partially attributable to greater natural immunity development in controls. Authors recommend enhanced surveillance and supplementary interventions during high-transmission implementation.

articles.headline: TEX1 protein essential for hepatic-stage Plasmodium development
articles.title_original: The Putative E3 Ubiquitin Ligase TEX1 Is Required for Nuclear Biology and Developmental Progression of Plasmodium berghei in the Liver.
articles.details: Functional characterization of TEX1 (putative E3 ubiquitin ligase, PBANKA_0102200) in P. berghei using knockout approaches. TEX1-KO parasites showed impaired ookinete formation, reduced oocysts and salivary-gland sporozoites, reduced hepatic growth, abnormal nuclear division, and dramatically decreased blood-stage infectivity. TEX1 localizes to nucleus, indicating role in DNA synthesis and mitotic regulation. Authors propose TEX1 as promising liver-stage antimalarial target.

articles.headline: Inland Liberia has significantly higher child malaria than coastal regions
articles.title_original: Geographic variation in malaria prevalence among children under five in coastal and inland counties of Liberia: analysis of the 2022 Malaria Indicator Survey.
articles.details: Analysis of 2022 Liberia Malaria Indicator Survey (n=2,189 children 6–59 months). Malaria prevalence: 8.3% coastal vs 12.7% inland (Z=3.33, p=0.001). Key predictors included non-ITN use (1.5× higher odds), severe anemia (AOR 6.2; 95% CI 2.34–14.81), poverty (AOR 6.4; 95% CI 3.06–13.27), and rural residence (AOR 0.6 vs urban; 95% CI 0.40–0.86). Authors recommend targeted interventions for inland and high-risk groups.

articles.headline: GIS model confirms malaria risk on ancient Anatolia trade routes
articles.title_original: A 'useless marsh … breeding fever and insect pests': assessing malaria risk on roads through Cilicia Pedias (modern Türkiye) and its historical impact in antiquity and the Middle Ages.
articles.details: GIS-based multilayer malaria risk model developed for ancient Cilicia Pedias (southeastern Anatolia). Modeled risk data for travel routes correlated extremely well with historical accounts of malarial illness from 4th century BCE through 12th century CE. Confirms endemic malaria in the region and its impact on immune-naïve travelers; provides replicable methodology combining GIS and textual analysis for premodern Mediterranean disease history.

articles.headline: Correction to metabolic rigidity malaria study
articles.title_original: Correction to "Metabolic Rigidity as a Mechanical Barrier to Malaria: Flickering Loss in PKLR-Deficient Erythrocytes".
articles.details: This is a correction notice with no abstract or quantitative data available.

articles.headline: IDO-1 inhibitor synergizes with artemether-lumefantrine in malaria
articles.title_original: 8-Fluorotryptanthrin, an immunomodulatory IDO-1 inhibitor enhances the efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine in the treatment of malaria.
articles.details: Novel compound 8-fluorotryptanthrin (C1), an IDO-1 inhibitor, showed strong in vitro activity against P. falciparum (3D7, Dd2 strains) and P. berghei ANKA. In P. berghei-infected mice, C1 (20 mg/kg) reduced parasitemia, restored organ function, and enhanced survival. Combination with artemether-lumefantrine achieved complete parasitemia suppression and full survival by day 7–14 with enhanced immune restoration (elevated nitric oxide and ROS; increased IL-10/IL-4, reduced TNF-α/IFN-γ). Authors propose C1 as promising immunochemotherapy candidate.

articles.headline: Chromatin structure and epigenetics regulate var2csa placental binding
articles.title_original: Epigenetics and chromatin structure regulate var2csa expression and the placental binding phenotype in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Mechanistic study of VAR2CSA transcriptional activation in P. falciparum. Established phenotypically homogenous VAR2CSA-expressing, placenta-binding parasites with exclusive var2csa transcription and silenced other var genes. Transcriptional activation associated with depletion of repressive H3K9me3 marks and repositioning of var2csa relative to heterochromatic telomeric clusters via chromatin conformation capture. 5-methylcytosine present in all var genes but independent of transcriptional switching.

articles.headline: Rare variants in P. falciparum bias population-genetic estimates
articles.title_original: Rare variation in malaria parasites biases population-genetic inference.
articles.details: Reanalysis of genomic data from 18 worldwide P. falciparum populations. Distributions enriched for rare variants cause effective population size estimates to vary by orders of magnitude and systematically inflate πN/πS ratio estimates independent of selection. Multiple-merger coalescent better explains observed genealogies than Kingman coalescent. Filtering rare variation reveals previously masked selection candidates including merozoite surface proteins.

articles.headline: Metabolic pathways drive permethrin resistance in A. gambiae
articles.title_original: Metabolomic changes underpinning permethrin resistance in the Anopheles gambiae malaria vector from Cameroon.
articles.details: UHPLC-HRMS/MS analysis of permethrin-resistant vs susceptible A. gambiae from Cameroon. Resistant mosquitoes showed elevated inosine, nicotinic acid, dipeptides, amino acids, fumarate, uracil, and aldopentose, suggesting altered detoxification and enhanced energy production. Susceptible mosquitoes showed increased N-acetyl-aspartic acid, xanthurenic acid, 2-hydroxyglutarate, 3-hydroxykynurenine, propanoylcarnitine, and L-pipecolic acid, associated with neurotoxicity and energy disruption. Findings elucidate metabolic mechanisms of permethrin and pyrethroid cross-resistance.

articles.headline: TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α axis controls host glucose metabolism in malaria
articles.title_original: Reprogramming of host energy metabolism mediated by the TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α axis plays a key role in host resistance to Plasmodium infection.
articles.details: Mechanistic study in P. chabaudi-infected mice. TNF signaling reduced physical activity and food intake while enhancing hepatic and splenic glucose uptake and controlling parasitemia. TNF required for monocyte iNOS expression, HIF-1α stabilization, GLUT1 expression, and enhanced glycolysis. iNOS-/-, TNFRΔLyz2, and HIF-1αΔLyz2 mice showed impaired TNF release and glycolysis, increased parasitemia, and reduced disease tolerance. Authors conclude TNF-iNOS-HIF-1α-driven monocyte glycolysis is critical for host defense.

articles.headline: Imported malaria threatens Sri Lanka despite malaria-free certification
articles.title_original: The profile of imported malaria in Sri Lanka from 2013 to 2023.
articles.details: Analysis of 532 imported cases (2013–2023) in WHO-certified malaria-free Sri Lanka. 68.5% were employment-related travelers. Species: P. falciparum 48.1% (mostly Africa), P. vivax 40.5% (mostly India). P. ovale manifested later (median 95 days) than other species (median 14 days). P. malariae diagnostic delay longest (median 15.5 days vs 4–6 days for others). Severe cases: 46 (45 P. falciparum); 1 death. Patients accessed care sooner (2.42 days) than physicians diagnosed (3.41 days, p<0.001). Key barrier: physician diagnostic delays.

articles.headline: Anopheles gambiae resting behavior varies by geography in Senegal
articles.title_original: Feeding and resting behaviours of natural Anopheles gambiae s.l. populations in an area of low malaria transmission in south-western Senegal: A comparative study between mainland and island settings.
articles.details: Comparative study of 765 Anopheles (181 mainland, 584 island) Sept 2020–Nov 2021. Anopheles gambiae s.l. comprised 91.1% of samples. Indoor resting density similar between sites (P=0.082); 0.41 females/room mainland vs 4.09 females/room island. Human blood index (HBI) significantly higher in mainland indoor-resting females (76.2%) versus outdoor (47.6%, P=0.035), but no difference in island areas. Endophilic populations showed greater HBI in mainland than island (P=2.63×10⁻⁵).

articles.headline: Malaria inequities drive Madagascar's disease burden
articles.title_original: Understanding inequities in the malaria landscape of Madagascar: a scoping review of current evidence.
articles.details: Scoping review (2015–2024) documenting urban-rural disparities in malaria prevention, diagnosis, and treatment. Remote regions experience higher burden; rural healthcare infrastructure inadequate for timely care. Key interventions include ITNs, IRS, SMC, ACTs, and RDTs. SBCC improved awareness but faces cultural barriers. Drug resistance and diagnostic failures contribute to control gaps. Recommendations emphasize strengthening healthcare systems, supply chains, and expansion into underserved areas.

articles.headline: Diagnostic and treatment gaps limit severe malaria care in Kenya
articles.title_original: Using sentinel surveillance system data to characterize severe malaria illness and quality of malaria case management among hospitalized patients in Kenya, 2017-2024.
articles.details: Analysis of 8,487 inpatients (2,197 malaria-positive, 25.9%) using 2017–2024 sentinel surveillance. Severe malaria in 32.5% of malaria cases; 0.7% mortality. Infants showed higher severe illness odds versus older ages. Fever duration ≥5 days increased odds of severe illness (OR 3.67) and death (OR 8.00). Referral from another facility increased both (OR 3.01–3.15). Microscopy concordance with PCR poor: 21% of microscopy-positive were PCR-negative. Only 15% of severe cases received IV artesunate plus artemether-lumefantrine per guidelines; 17% received IV quinine.

articles.headline: New molecular surveillance network established for malaria in Americas
articles.title_original: MMS Américas: a regional network for malaria molecular surveillance.
articles.details: MMS Américas, a regional network for malaria molecular surveillance in the Americas, launched in August 2025 to accelerate elimination efforts. The network promotes sharing of expertise, protocols, and data among international researchers and public health professionals.

articles.headline: Community-designed interventions improve malaria knowledge in conflict-affected Cameroon
articles.title_original: The role of community co-designed interventions for malaria prevention and control in conflict-affected communities in Cameroon's Southwest and littoral regions.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study (May 2021–April 2024) in conflict-affected Southwest/Littoral Cameroon evaluating three community-based interventions: Community Health Participatory Approach (CoHPA), CHW supportive supervision, and health vouchers. Qualitative analysis of 189 participants via 19 FGDs and 18 IDIs. Communities reported increased malaria knowledge, enhanced prevention participation, improved trust in CHW services and treatment access through vouchers, though questions remained regarding voucher sustainability amid insecurity.

articles.headline: Host RBC oxidative stress regulates artemisinin drug sensitivity
articles.title_original: Decoding the multifaceted role of erythrocyte PMCA4b in oxidative stress-mediated malaria protection and artemisinin resistance.
articles.details: Study in Indian population examining ATP2B4 genotypes and PMCA4b expression across severe malaria, uncomplicated malaria, and healthy controls. ATP2B4 genotypes showed no significant association with malaria protection. However, low PMCA4b expression (regardless of genotype) correlated with increased intracellular calcium, oxidative stress, reduced in-vitro parasite growth, and reduced P. falciparum artemisinin sensitivity. Oxidative-stress regulation by PMCA4b emerged as key mechanism rather than proposed dehydration pathway.

articles.headline: Novel gametocyte cytoskeleton proteins identified as transmission targets
articles.title_original: An atlas of novel microtubule-associated proteins in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: BioID-based proteomics study identifying proteins associated with subpellicular microtubules (SPMTs) in P. falciparum gametocytes. Proximity-dependent biotinylation using PfSPM3 as bait identified 37 high-confidence proteins; GFP tagging validated 11 candidates revealing distinct localization patterns within the SPMT network. Functional knockout of protein PF3D7_1003400 interfered with gametocytogenesis and prevented falciform gametocyte development, indicating potential targets for transmission-blocking approaches.

articles.headline: Haemosporidian infections threaten endangered birds in Thai zoos
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreaks in Humboldt penguins and haemosporidian infections in avian hosts in zoos across Thailand.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Survey of 406 blood and tissue samples from 146 Humboldt penguins and 260 birds across 10 avian orders (38 species) at 6 Thai zoos. Humboldt penguin deaths in 2022 confirmed as avian malaria. Haemosporidian DNA detected in 27.4% of penguin samples and 48.0% of other birds. Identified 4 confirmed Plasmodium species (P. collidatum, P. relictum, P. juxtanucleare, P. gallinaceum) plus 7 lineages, Haemoproteus species, and Leucocytozoon caulleryi, with critically endangered host species affected.

articles.headline: Specific antibodies protect against high parasite density in Malian children
articles.title_original: Antibody levels to variant and conserved Plasmodium falciparum antigens predict reduction in parasite burden in Malian children.
articles.details: IP-MS proteomics study in Malian children comparing susceptible and semi-immune populations. High antibody levels to DBLγ11 predicted reduced risk of high parasite density (OR 0.74, 95% CI 0.63–0.86) and DBLζ5 (OR 0.80, 95% CI 0.69–0.93), as did antibodies to two helical interspersed subtelomeric proteins (PF3D7_0201600 OR 0.81, PF3D7_0532300 OR 0.79). Opsonic phagocytosis assays showed significantly higher phagocytosis at top antibody tertiles, with strong correlations (r² 0.61 and 0.49 for DBLζ5 and DBLγ11), implicating phagocytosis-mediated protection.

articles.headline: AI-based malaria diagnosis achieves 87–91% sensitivity and specificity
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Accuracy of Utilizing Artificial Intelligence for Malaria Diagnostic: A Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review (PRISMA) of 10 studies with 6,754 patients analyzing AI diagnostic systems for malaria. Pooled sensitivity 87.7% (95% CI 78.2–93.4) and specificity 91.4% (95% CI 77.3–97.1) compared to reference methods. Compared directly to microscopy: sensitivity 87.7%, specificity 91.4%. Compared to PCR: sensitivity 90.7%, specificity 88.3%. AI-based systems improve diagnosis through automation, high accuracy, and lower cost compared to microscopy.

articles.headline: Urine-based malaria RDT shows limited sensitivity for febrile children
articles.title_original: Comparative Performance of Blood and Urine Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Detecting Malaria in Febrile Children: A Cross-Sectional Analysis.
articles.details: Cross-sectional diagnostic comparison in febrile children. Blood-based RDTs showed high agreement: SD Bioline 67.4% positivity and CareStart 65.3% (Cohen's kappa 0.859). Urine-based Fyodor UMT showed lower positivity (45.3%) with moderate agreement to blood tests (kappa 0.491, P<0.001). Fyodor UMT sensitivity 58.6%, specificity 100%, PPV 100%, NPV 40%, limiting utility as standalone diagnostic due to low sensitivity.

articles.headline: Malaria infection impairs adipsin's diagnostic accuracy for preeclampsia
articles.title_original: Impact of Malaria Infection on the Diagnostic Performance of Adipsin for Preeclampsia in Pregnancy: A Case-Control Study.
articles.details: Case-control study of 200 pregnant women (20–42 weeks gestation) in four groups (n=50 each): normotensive without malaria, normotensive with malaria, preeclamptic with malaria, preeclamptic without malaria. Plasma adipsin elevated in both malaria and preeclampsia (p<0.001), highest with coexisting conditions. Adipsin correlated with C5a (ρ=0.695), IL-6 (ρ=0.687), TNF-α (ρ=0.645), and parasite density (ρ=0.553). Malaria infection reduced adipsin diagnostic specificity for preeclampsia from 87.9% (AUC 0.823) to 62.1% (AUC 0.719, p=0.02).

articles.headline: P. falciparum outbreak in Southern Ethiopia driven by environmental factors
articles.title_original: Epidemiological investigation of a possible malaria outbreak in Arba Minch town, Gamo Zone, Southern Ethiopia, 2023.
articles.details: Outbreak investigation in Arba Minch Town, Ethiopia, 2022–2023: 5,181 confirmed cases (22.7% positivity, 41.2 per 1,000 attack rate), 71% P. falciparum. Case-control study (n=94 cases, 94 controls) identified significant risk factors: proximity to breeding sites (AOR 5.45), stagnant water (AOR 5.69), poor housing (AOR 4.40), inconsistent LLIN use (protective: AOR 0.30), poor knowledge (protective: AOR 0.04). Environmental conditions and inadequate vector control were primary drivers.

articles.headline: Natural compounds identified as potential P. falciparum kinase inhibitors
articles.title_original: Computational screening of natural inhibitors against Plasmodium falciparum kinases: Toward novel antimalarial therapies.
articles.details: Computational screening of natural compounds targeting casein kinase 2 (CK2) and cGMP-dependent protein kinase (PKG) in multidrug-resistant P. falciparum. Molecular docking identified Ligand-13 as best CK2 inhibitor (−11.468 kcal/mol) and Ligand-9 as best PKG inhibitor (−7.490 kcal/mol). Molecular dynamics and density functional theory analyses supported Ligand-13's stability and reactivity as a promising antimalarial candidate.

articles.headline: Malaria outbreaks in endemic settings
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreaks in endemic settings?
articles.details: No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Glutathione redox pathway as antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Glutathione redox system as a therapeutic target for Plasmodium: Opportunities, Challenges, and future perspectives.
articles.details: Review of glutathione (GSH) metabolism in malaria parasites as a therapeutic target for multidrug-resistant infections. GSH protects parasites from oxidative stress through metabolic pathways distinctly different from humans. Discusses enzyme inhibitors targeting GST, GR, GS, and GP in the GSH pathway, combination therapy potential, and challenges including selective drug targeting and resistance development.

articles.headline: Civil society perspectives on gene drive-modified mosquitoes for malaria control
articles.title_original: Exploratory conversations with biodiversity-oriented civil society groups on the potential applications of gene drive-modified mosquitoes for malaria control in Tanzania.
articles.details: Qualitative study of ten biodiversity-focused civil society organizations in Tanzania regarding gene drive-modified mosquitoes (GDMMs): eight interviews, one focus group discussion, three large group discussions. CSOs expressed cautious support but emphasized scientific uncertainty, local expertise gaps, transparency concerns, potential transboundary effects, and accountability issues. Recommendations included robust evidence before environmental release, independent local oversight, and sustained stakeholder engagement.

articles.headline: Haematology analyzer cost-effective for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: A comparison of the cost-effectiveness of the current standard of care and of the haematology analyser XN31-microscopy combination for diagnosing malaria in a nonendemic country.
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis comparing malaria diagnostic methods in France: microscopy, rapid diagnostic test + microscopy, loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) + microscopy, and haematology analyzer XN-31 + microscopy. Study population based on 1,000 tests annually. Haematology analyzer combined with microscopy demonstrated superior cost-effectiveness due to diagnostic accuracy and rapid time-to-result, though findings are France-specific.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention reduces non-malarial pediatric infections
articles.title_original: Indirect benefits of seasonal malaria chemoprevention for non-malarial pediatric infections and routine antibiotic use in real-world programmatic settings: a pre-post study using positive and negative controls.
articles.details: Pre-post study in rural Burkina Faso (51 health facilities) comparing seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine) to post-intervention period. SMC associated with reductions in pneumonia (IRR 0.86), diarrhea (IRR 0.83), acute malnutrition (IRR 0.71), and routine antibiotic prescriptions (IRR 0.88), alongside malaria reductions (IRR 0.62). Absolute reductions were modest (0.7–0.9 per 1,000 person-weeks).

articles.headline: Anopheles funestus populations in Tanzania show regional genetic differentiation
articles.title_original: Microsatellite analysis of genetic differentiation among populations of the malaria vector Anopheles funestus across mainland Tanzania reveals contrasted patterns of geographic isolation and gene flow.
articles.details: Microsatellite analysis of An. funestus populations across ten administrative regions in mainland Tanzania (n=654 An. funestus s.s., 28 An. parensis outgroup). Allelic richness and genetic diversity did not differ significantly among populations. Southeastern coastal populations (Mtwara, Ruvuma, Lindi) showed highest divergence from inland populations (FST up to 0.288). Neighbor-joining and Bayesian clustering identified three distinct population groups.

articles.headline: Real-time surveillance system accelerates malaria elimination in Cambodia
articles.title_original: A bespoke, real-time surveillance system for malaria elimination in Cambodia: architecture, applications and impact.
articles.details: Cambodia's locally developed Malaria Information System (MIS) integrates surveillance, diagnostics, treatment, logistics, entomology, and operational monitoring. Mobile and web applications enable real-time case reporting and intervention tracking by village workers and health facility staff, including offline capability. System supports 1-3-7 surveillance model and integrated drug efficacy surveillance, with real-time analytics driving timely interventions and cross-border coordination.

articles.headline: Community factors shape P. knowlesi prevention and healthcare-seeking in Malaysia
articles.title_original: Synthesis of qualitative evidence on community experiences and perceptions of Plasmodium knowlesi malaria and factors influencing prevention and healthcare-seeking behaviours in Malaysia.
articles.details: Qualitative evidence synthesis of five studies (2022–2024) on P. knowlesi malaria in Malaysia. Three themes identified: (1) community knowledge and risk perception influenced by fear of hospitalization and income loss; (2) environmental, structural, and social barriers constraining prevention and healthcare seeking; (3) prevention practices reflecting local knowledge alongside formal preventive measures. Integration of community perspectives into prevention programs recommended.

articles.headline: Baseline survey establishes RTS,S vaccine implementation comparability in Ghana
articles.title_original: The Malaria Vaccine Implementation Programme study area in Ghana: results of a household survey prior to the introduction of the RTS,S/AS01 vaccine.
articles.details: Baseline household survey (February–March 2019) prior to RTS,S/AS01 vaccine introduction: 6,778 households across 66 districts; 7,768 children aged 5–48 months. Malaria RDT positivity 21% (95% CI 18–23). ITN ownership 87%, ITN use among children 62%. Fever reported in 22% within 2 weeks; 72% sought treatment, 40% tested, 42% received antimalarials. EPI coverage >90%; deworming 29%, vitamin A 36%.

articles.headline: Long-term P. falciparum genome evolution reveals key adaptation mechanisms
articles.title_original: Major features of parasite adaptation revealed by genomes of Plasmodium falciparum population samples archived for over 50 years.
articles.details: Whole-genome sequencing of 54 archived P. falciparum samples from the Gambia (1966–1971) with comparisons over ~50 years. No drug-resistance alleles detected in archived samples. Strong positive selection at loci encoding erythrocyte-binding surface proteins targeted by acquired antibodies. Major allele frequency changes at gdv1 (regulates sexual conversion for transmission) and Pfsa1/Pfsa3 (associated with sickle-cell trait infection) revealed key long-term adaptations.

articles.headline: Spleen retains far more RBCs in malaria than blood circulation
articles.title_original: The role of the spleen in red blood cell loss caused by malaria: A mathematical model.
articles.details: Mathematical model of RBC dynamics in P. falciparum and P. vivax infections, calibrated with data from splenectomy patients in Papua, Indonesia. In chronic infections, the ratio of uninfected RBCs retained in the spleen to circulating RBC loss is 17:1 for P. falciparum and 82:1 for P. vivax. The spleen removes both infected and uninfected RBCs, with uninfected RBC retention substantially exceeding circulating loss from parasitization.

articles.headline: Wolbachia bacteria shows promise for Anopheles vector control
articles.title_original: The symbiotic Wolbachia in Anopheles and its role in reducing the transmission of Plasmodium: updates and prospects.
articles.details: Review of Wolbachia pipientis, an intracellular symbiont in Anopheles mosquitoes, as a vector control strategy. Found naturally in ~31 Anopheles species at varying prevalence (8-24% in An. gambiae, 91% in DRC An. species A). Experimental transinfection of An. stephensi achieved 100% infection frequency with nearly complete cytoplasmic incompatibility and moderate fitness costs. Partial protection against Plasmodium observed with wAlbB strain. Persistent challenges include rare natural infections and need for host-symbiotic optimization.

articles.headline: Malaria and onchocerciasis overlap in Ghana populations
articles.title_original: Malaria in onchocerciasis-endemic populations: patterns in two districts in the Oti and Volta regions of Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 345 participants in Nkwanta North and Adaklu districts, Ghana, examining malaria prevalence in onchocerciasis-endemic areas. Overall malaria prevalence 42.6% (95% CI 37.5-47.8%), with 53.26% in Nkwanta North and 38.7% in Adaklu. Co-infection rate 81.7% in Nkwanta North. Significant association between malaria and onchocerciasis (OR=2.03 overall; OR=3.52 in Adaklu after adjustment). RDT showed 89.2% sensitivity and 71.9% specificity versus nested PCR.

articles.headline: Cameroon plants show potent antimalarial activity in vitro
articles.title_original: Targeting the intra-erythrocytic life cycle of Plasmodium falciparum unveils antimalarial drug discovery starting points from Drymaria cordata and Macaranga monandra.
articles.details: Screening of extracts and fractions from Drymaria cordata and Macaranga monandra against chloroquine-sensitive (Pf3D7) and multidrug-resistant (PfDd2) P. falciparum strains. M. monandra methanolic bark extract showed high potency and selectivity (IC50 0.60 and 0.92 μg/mL for PfDd2, IC50 1.02 and 3.42 μg/mL for Pf3D7; selectivity indices >203-487). Two active fractions (F2, F3) arrested ring stages, lysed trophozoite-infected RBCs, and blocked merozoite egress. UHPLC-MS identified 17 metabolites including lecanoric acid.

articles.headline: Hepatocyte autophagy limits Plasmodium liver-stage development
articles.title_original: Hepatocyte Autophagy in Malaria: Current Concepts, Emerging Mechanisms, and Future Therapeutic Directions.
articles.details: Review synthesizing recent advances in hepatocyte autophagy responses to liver-stage Plasmodium infection. Hepatocytes activate noncanonical autophagy (CASM/PAAR) to limit parasite development via LC3 lipidation on the parasitophorous vacuole membrane. Parasites counteract this through UIS3/UIS4 proteins that sequester LC3 and remodel perivacuolar actin. Parasites also exploit host autophagy components (GABARAP paralogs) to promote lysosomal biogenesis. Selective modulation of noncanonical autophagy is proposed as a host-directed therapy avenue to restrict liver-stage development.

articles.headline: Serologic survey reveals micro-epidemiological heterogeneity in low-transmission setting
articles.title_original: Serological evidence of continued exposure to Plasmodium falciparum among residents in a near-elimination setting.
articles.details: Cross-sectional serological study of 120 malaria-exposed individuals and 24 controls from four villages in Menoreh Hills, Indonesia, assessing antibody responses to P. falciparum antigens. PfEBA175 seroprevalence ranged 10-40%, highest in Kembaran (40%) and Wadas (23%), p<0.001. PfRh5 showed universal immunogenicity (53-70% seroprevalence, p<0.001) with highest in Sedayu (70%) linked to nocturnal livestock activities. Village-specific behavioral and environmental factors significantly associated with heterogeneous antibody profiles.

articles.headline: High caregiver acceptance of malaria vaccine in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Determinants of caregivers' acceptance of the malaria vaccine: The case of Bamenda and Bamenda III Health Districts in Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1,000 caregivers in Bamenda and Bamenda III health districts, Cameroon, assessing malaria vaccine (RTS,S/AS01 or R21/Matrix-M) acceptance. Overall 89.6% indicated willingness to vaccinate. Significant factors: information source (OR=1.663 for community health vs social media), perception (OR=0.098 for very rejecting vs very accepting), sex (OR=0.704 male vs female), religion (OR=0.386 other vs Christian), occupation (OR=2.92 NGO vs self-employed). Positive perceptions and trusted healthcare sources were key drivers.

articles.headline: Transient neuropsychiatric manifestations after P. falciparum
articles.title_original: A Case Series of Transient Neuropsychiatric Manifestations Following Plasmodium Falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: Case series documenting transient neuropsychiatric manifestations following P. falciparum malaria infection.

articles.headline: Health spending associated with reduced malaria mortality in children
articles.title_original: Funded investments contributed to the reduction of malaria morbidity and mortality in children under five: fifteen years retrospective study from 2009 to 2023 in Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis (2009-2023) of malaria funding impact on children <5 in Burkina Faso. Population grew 50.7%; children <5 increased 41.4% in number. USD 13.5 billion invested in health sector, 15.3% for malaria. Malaria case fatality rate decreased from 3.4% to 1.3% in children <5. Healthcare access improved: distance to facilities decreased from 7.2 to 6.0 km; contacts per capita increased from 0.8 to 2.1 per year. Three regions had 3-fold higher mortality risk.

articles.headline: Triple co-infection of malaria, dengue, and COVID-19 case
articles.title_original: Sepsis secondary to co-infection with malaria, dengue and COVID-19: a case report.
articles.details: Case report of 36-year-old woman in Burkina Faso with P. falciparum malaria, dengue (DEN3 serotype), and COVID-19 complicated by sepsis and multiple organ dysfunction. Presented with severe hypoxemia, hepatic dysfunction (cytolysis and cholestasis), SOFA score 7. COVID-19 diagnosis delayed 10 days due to overlapping symptoms with malaria and dengue. Case illustrates diagnostic complexity in endemic areas where systemic multiplex PCR testing is recommended.

articles.headline: Dengue virus detected in non-malaria febrile illness in Kenya
articles.title_original: Molecular diagnosis of arboviruses (dengue, Chikungunya, yellow fever, Rift Valley fever, Mayaro, West Nile, and Zika viruses) in non-malaria acute febrile illnesses in Kenya's Garissa County, July 2023, and Mombasa County, July 2024.
articles.details: Prospective arboviral surveillance in Kenya using molecular diagnostics. Garissa County: 326 non-malaria febrile patients screened; 4 dengue PCR-positive (1.23%, 95% CI 0.36-3.23%). Mombasa County: 289 non-malaria febrile patients screened; 26 dengue PCR-positive (9.00%, 95% CI 6.17-12.90%). No other arboviruses (Chikungunya, yellow fever, Rift Valley fever, Mayaro, West Nile, Zika) detected. Results highlight need for scaling arboviral diagnostic capacity in peripheral health centers.

articles.headline: P. malariae neglected parasite poses emerging drug resistance threat
articles.title_original: Plasmodium malariae is an overlooked malaria parasite with emerging challenges.
articles.details: Review of Plasmodium malariae biology, epidemiology, and public health impact. Least studied of five human-malaria species despite global distribution. Challenges include diagnostic difficulties (no P. malariae-specific tests), chronic low-grade parasitemia, suboptimal clinical features, emerging antimalarial drug resistance, and reduced susceptibility to first-line treatments. Chronic infection and diagnostic gaps threaten malaria elimination efforts. Enhanced molecular diagnostics, targeted therapeutics, and improved surveillance urgently needed.

articles.headline: Surveillance system assessment reveals rising malaria cases in Ethiopian town
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the malaria surveillance system and trends in Jimma town, Southwest Ethiopia, 2024: a mixed-method study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional evaluation of 10 health facilities in Jimma, Ethiopia assessing surveillance performance (2012-2016 data). Malaria cases increased from 432 to 11,824; P. falciparum accounted for 56.4% of infections with largest increases in school-aged children and adults. System functional but limited by incomplete reporting, weak feedback mechanisms, inadequate private-sector engagement; private facility contributions to reporting grew from 47% to 66%. Authors recommend strengthening data quality assurance and private-provider collaboration.

articles.headline: Community perspectives on non-invasive malaria diagnostic tools
articles.title_original: "It doesn't require blood!": Perceptions around non-invasive malaria testing tools in Indonesia, Peru, and Rwanda.
articles.details: Qualitative study in Indonesia, Peru, and Rwanda (24 interviews, 16 focus groups, 139 participants) exploring acceptance of non-invasive diagnostics using saliva, volatile organic compounds, or transdermal detection. Participants valued comfort vs blood draw and portability, particularly for remote areas. Major concerns: lack of accuracy validation vs established methods, distrust of non-blood specimens, and inability to differentiate malaria species. Authors recommend early stakeholder engagement in technology development.

articles.headline: Iron supplementation does not increase malaria risk in children
articles.title_original: Oral iron supplements for children in malaria-endemic areas.
articles.details: Cochrane systematic review of 40 RCTs (33,785 children) in malaria-endemic areas. Iron vs placebo showed little/no difference in clinical malaria (RR 0.93, 95% CI 0.87–1.00; high certainty) and slight reduction in severe malaria (RR 0.90, 95% CI 0.81–0.98; high certainty). Iron plus folic acid similarly showed no increased risk of severe malaria (RR 1.11, 95% CI 0.96–1.28; low certainty). Mortality and hospitalization rates similar between groups. Authors conclude iron is safe and does not increase malaria.
articles.notable_reason: Definitive high-certainty evidence refuting major safety concern about iron supplementation

articles.headline: Paracetamol showed no benefit for kidney injury in severe malaria
articles.title_original: A phase I/II randomized controlled clinical trial to assess the feasibility, safety, and preliminary effectiveness of paracetamol in resolving acute kidney injury in children with severe malaria.
articles.details: Phase I/II RCT of 40 children (6 months–12 years) with malaria-induced acute kidney injury in Uganda. Paracetamol 20 mg/kg 6-hourly × 48 hours vs tepid sponging for fever control. Mean time to renal recovery: paracetamol 0.491 hours longer than control (95% CI −9.265 to 10.248; p = 0.921), not statistically significant. No difference in adverse events or hepatotoxicity. Authors conclude paracetamol is safe but did not improve renal recovery.

articles.headline: Vaccination attitudes inform strategy for future malaria vaccine rollout
articles.title_original: Exploring factors affecting malaria vaccination intention and COVID-19 vaccine uptake: evidence from a joint analysis in rural Senegal.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 795 rural Senegalese adults (2023) examining malaria vaccination intention and COVID-19 uptake. 35.6% received COVID-19 vaccine; 58.6% expressed favorable intentions for hypothetical free adult malaria vaccine. COVID-19 vaccinated individuals 60% more likely to favor malaria vaccine (p < 0.001). Perceived disease severity predicted COVID-19 uptake; perceived susceptibility more strongly predicted malaria vaccine intention. Women more supportive of malaria vaccination (p = 0.005); adults ≥59 years had higher COVID vaccination.

articles.headline: Thymol derivatives show antimalarial activity against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Thymol Derivatives as Antimalarial Agents: Synthesis, Activity Against Plasmodium falciparum, ADMET Profiling, and Molecular Docking Insights.
articles.details: Synthesis and in vitro evaluation of 7 thymol derivatives against P. falciparum (sensitive and resistant strains) and P. knowlesi. Compounds 4 and 6 demonstrated EC50 6.01 ± 1.7 µM and 6.8 ± 1.1 µM against P. falciparum 3D7 with selectivity indices 16.5 and 14.6. Most compounds non-toxic (CC50 > 99 µM). Molecular docking showed strong binding to PfCRT protein (−8.3 to −8.4 kcal/mol). Authors identify compounds 4 and 6 as lead antimalarial candidates.

articles.headline: Primaquine addition blocks malaria transmission completely by day 7
articles.title_original: The Effect of Artemether-Lumefantrine Combined with a Single Dose of Primaquine on Plasmodium falciparum Gametocyte Clearance and Post-Treatment Infectivity to Anopheles arabiensis.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study of 96 P. falciparum gametocytemia cases in Ethiopia comparing artemether-lumefantrine (AL) alone vs AL plus single low-dose primaquine (SLD-PQ). AL+SLD-PQ achieved faster gametocyte clearance (0% by day 7 vs 12.2% for AL alone, p = 0.033). Membrane feeding assays showed AL+SLD-PQ completely blocked mosquito infection by day 7 (0% vs 7.3% for AL, p = 0.01). High baseline gametocyte density (95%) predicted infectivity; SLD-PQ reduced day 3 infectivity from 28.6% to 6.8% (p = 0.001).

articles.headline: Apical polar ring protein identified as essential invasion target
articles.title_original: The essential function of the apical polar ring during the blood stage of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Molecular study identifying PfAPR1 as an essential apical polar ring (APR) resident protein in P. falciparum. Conditional PfAPR1 knockout causes defects in daughter cell segmentation and subpellicular microtubule organization. Critical finding: PfAPR1-deficient parasites contact host RBCs but completely fail to form tight junctions, resulting in complete invasion block. Authors identify additional APR proteins, delineate APR biogenesis using ultrastructure expansion microscopy, and highlight APR as a promising antimalarial drug target.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies essential parasite invasion mechanism and novel antimalarial drug target

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum genetic diversity characterized in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum Merozoite Surface Protein 1 and 2 Genes and Their Allele Association With Malaria Severity, Seasons, and Sex in Bamenda, North West Cameroon.
articles.journal: Can J Infect Dis Med Microbiol
articles.details: Genetic survey of Msp1 Block 2 (281 samples) and Msp2 Block 3 (194 samples) in Bamenda, North West Cameroon. Msp1 Block 2: all family alleles present (RO33, K1, MAD20); RO33 most prevalent at 44.7%; K1 linked to severe malaria. Msp2 Block 3: all alleles present (3D7, FC27); 3D7 most prevalent at 56.7%. Msp2 alleles associated with sex (χ² = 8.856, p = 0.012); no seasonal variation. Authors note findings useful for assessing prevention measure efficacy.

articles.headline: Community co-creation approach sustains malaria services in low-transmission areas
articles.title_original: Sustaining community-based malaria services through stakeholder engagement: lessons from co-creation in northeastern Thailand.
articles.details: Implementation study in Buntharik district, northeastern Thailand (April 2023–June 2024) describing co-creation process with 36 engagement activities involving ~550 participants. Stakeholders co-created 2024 health calendar integrating malaria with local priority health issues. Key enabler: responding to community health concerns and expectations. Identified challenges: sustaining capacity with changing policy; future initiatives require additional funding sources. Authors conclude co-creation process shows promise for maintaining malaria awareness in declining transmission settings.

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance emerging in Indian malaria vectors during elimination phase
articles.title_original: A multicentric study on understanding the bionomics of Indian malaria vectors across diverse eco-epidemiological settings.
articles.details: Multicentric entomological survey across 14 districts in 8 Indian states (2021–2023) characterizing vector bionomics. Anopheles culicifacies prevalent in all states with highest indoor man-hour density 11.95 (Kanker, Chhattisgarh) and outdoor 27.16 (Bareilly, Uttar Pradesh). An. minimus showed altered resting behavior in Assam/Tripura; An. baimaii highly exophilic with high sporozoite infection rate (5.88%). Pyrethroid resistance detected: An. culicifacies resistant to alpha-cypermethrin and deltamethrin in multiple districts; An. stephensi showed multiple insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: School malaria prevention treatment reduces absenteeism in Tanzanian children
articles.title_original: The impact of intermittent preventive treatment for malaria on school absenteeism among primary school-aged children in Handeni District Council, Tanzania: quasi-experimental study.
articles.details: Quasi-experimental pre-post study of 346 school-aged children in Handeni District, Tanzania (January–May 2024 vs 2025). IPTsc using dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine reduced overall absenteeism from 13% to 10% (p = 0.001); malaria-related absences declined from 27.8% to 11.3%. School attendance increased 6.7%; 91.9% received all three IPTsc doses. SAC not engaged in economic activities had higher baseline absenteeism (β = 0.26; 95% CI 0.17–0.36; p < 0.001). Authors conclude IPTsc provides dual health-education benefit.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence trends in northwestern Ethiopia: seven-year retrospective
articles.title_original: Analysis of a seven-year trend in malaria prevalence at Pawe General Hospital, Northwestern Ethiopia: a retrospective cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Retrospective cross-sectional analysis of 155,669 suspected cases (11,495 confirmed, 7.4%, 95% CI 7.2–7.5%) at Pawe General Hospital (2015–2021). P. falciparum predominated (90.8%), followed by P. vivax (6.2%) and mixed infections (3.0%). Males had higher prevalence (8.8% vs 6.2%, OR=1.48, p<0.001). Prevalence fluctuated with resurgence post-2018, indicating need for strengthened surveillance.

articles.headline: Modeling reveals 41% malaria incidence reduction from ITNs in Kenya
articles.title_original: High resolution physically based modelling reveals malaria incidence reduction by vector control measures.
articles.details: High-resolution hybrid modeling framework (WRF/WRF-Hydro + VECTRI) analyzed western Kenya (2007–2022) during large-scale insecticide-treated net deployment, reproducing observed trends with mean monthly deviation ±100–150 cases. ITN coverage reduced entomological inoculation rate by 58% and malaria incidence by 41%, with highest efficacy in warm temperatures (~°C) and moderately wet conditions (150–250 mm).

articles.headline: ANC provider training improves IPTp-SP delivery in Mali and Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of antenatal care delivery of intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine in the context of an integrated strategy with seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Mali and Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized implementation trial in Mali and Burkina Faso (November 2023–January 2024) assessing integrated sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP) delivery through antenatal care with seasonal malaria chemoprevention. Enhanced provider training increased directly observed therapy delivery: Mali 79.2% (95% CI 74.0–84.3%) vs 68.7% (63.4–73.9%, p=0.006); Burkina Faso 52.3% (46.9–57.8%) vs 28.7% (23.9–33.6%, p<0.0001). Providers found the integrated strategy acceptable and feasible.

articles.headline: P. ovale cytoadherence demonstrated in splenic rupture case
articles.title_original: Splenic accumulation of intact Plasmodium ovale sensu lato-infected red blood cells in a patient presenting with splenic rupture.
articles.details: Case report of 35-year-old man with splenic rupture after starting antimalarials (atovaquone-proguanil then artesunate) for acute Plasmodium ovale sensu lato infection. Spleen histology revealed 10-fold accumulation of intact infected erythrocytes compared to general circulation, with additional accumulation in small extra-splenic vessels suggesting cytoadherent phenotype.

articles.headline: Systematic review protocol on miRNAs in malaria-arbovirus coinfection
articles.title_original: MicroRNAs profiling in malaria and arbovirus coinfection: A systematic review protocol.
articles.details: Protocol for systematic review (PROSPERO CRD42024600379) following PRISMA-ScR guidelines investigating microRNA expression in malaria-arbovirus coinfections (dengue, chikungunya, Zika). Will search six databases from January 2000 onwards for observational studies and clinical trials reporting primary miRNA expression data. Aims to identify differentially expressed miRNAs, advance understanding of coinfection pathogenesis, and identify biomarkers for diagnostics and therapeutics.

articles.headline: China's malaria elimination: health systems and social development factors
articles.title_original: Malaria elimination practices in China from the perspective of health system and social development.
articles.details: Qualitative study analyzing China's 70-year malaria elimination journey through semi-structured interviews (n=42 key informants, July 2023–July 2025). Thematic analysis identified seven key social development factors: health education (66.67%), living environment (71.43%), water and sanitation (59.52%), agricultural patterns (57.14%), employment opportunities (52.38%), working environment (64.29%), and health system strengthening. China achieved WHO malaria-free certification on June 30, 2021.

articles.headline: Hemozoin-based malaria diagnostics: scoping review of eight technology classes
articles.title_original: Hemozoin as a Diagnostic Biomarker: A Scoping Review of Next-Generation Malaria Detection Technologies.
articles.details: Scoping review synthesizing 24 studies on hemozoin-targeted next-generation diagnostics across eight technology classes. Magneto-optical systems (Hz-MOD, Gazelle, RMOD) demonstrated highest operational readiness with robust specificity but reduced sensitivity at low parasitemia. Photoacoustic detection showed promising sensitivity and noninvasive in vivo potential; Raman/SERS platforms achieved sub-100 infected cell/mL analytical sensitivity. Performance varied by parasite stage; further clinical validation and standardized comparisons needed for field deployment.

articles.headline: One Health approach improves childhood malaria management in Senegal
articles.title_original: How the One Health approach can make effective the World Health Organization's recommendations on integrated management of childhood illnesses: Case of community-based home management of malaria in the Ferlo of Matam, Senegal.
articles.details: Cross-sectional evaluation of community-based home management strategy in Ferlo, Senegal, integrating One Health principles with community-selected health care providers for children under five. Among 2,307 cases managed (1,464 fevers), malaria testing rate was 98.9% with 21.1% positivity; 96.4% of positive cases received artemisinin-based combination therapies. Also documented diarrhea (67.9% ORS + zinc treatment), respiratory infections (55.6% pneumonia treated with amoxicillin), and preventive interventions (1,156 vitamin A supplementation, 443 dewormed).

articles.headline: Gold mining linked to 300% malaria increase among Yanomami
articles.title_original: Investigating the Yanomami malaria outbreak: gold mining and malaria.
articles.authors: de Angeli Dutra D, et al.
articles.details: Quantitative analysis using satellite imagery and panel regression (2016–2023) in Yanomami territory, Amazon. Observed approximately 300% increase in malaria cases associated with illegal gold mining expansion. Each 1 standard deviation increase in gold mining associated with 20–46% rise in malaria incidence 1–2 years later. Each 1 standard deviation increase in forest edge perimeter associated with 55% rise in malaria cases, highlighting forest fragmentation impact.

articles.headline: High treatment delays in Ethiopian malaria: 62% pooled prevalence
articles.title_original: Determinants of delay in treatment-seeking among malaria patients attending health facilities in Ethiopia from 2010 to 2024: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis (12 studies, 4,511 participants) of treatment-seeking delays in Ethiopia (2010–2024). Pooled prevalence of delay (>24 hours after symptom onset) was 62.00% (95% CI 46.00–77.00%). Key determinants: lack malaria knowledge, distance from health facility, treatment costs, transportation barriers, farmer occupation, self-medication practice, no formal education, fear of drug side effects, and low family income.

articles.headline: Placental malaria linked to decreased child height and increased BMI
articles.title_original: Association between placental malaria, postnatal linear growth, and body mass index in the Dogon Longitudinal Study, Mali.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study (Dogon Longitudinal Study, n=317) in Mali examining placental malaria effects on child growth to age 5 years using linear mixed models. Severe parasite density associated with decreased height at 6 months (−0.83 cm, 95% CI −1.63 to −0.03, p=0.042) and declining linear growth through age 5 years. Severe parasite density also associated with increased BMI at ages 2–5 years (effect range 0.56–1.35 kg/m², all p<0.05). First evidence linking placental malaria to increased postnatal BMI.

articles.headline: RDT recording accuracy in Côte d'Ivoire shows systematic positive bias
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.details: Mixed-methods evaluation of RDT result recording accuracy across 16 primary health facilities, Côte d'Ivoire (August–December 2023). Among 11,161 RDT-register pairs, external reviewer panel and register results agreed strongly (Cohen's κ=0.83, 95% CI 0.77–0.88; PPV 90.7%; NPV 94.8%). However, negative or invalid results were disproportionately misrecorded as positive (5.9% vs 2.0% reverse error), particularly when patient had documented malaria diagnosis or antimalarial prescription, suggesting systematic reporting bias affecting surveillance data.

articles.headline: Elevated IL-10 and IFN-γ define symptomatic malaria in children
articles.title_original: Distinct systemic cytokine signatures define symptomatic malaria in children: insights from a longitudinal study in Togo.
articles.details: Longitudinal study of 60 children (6-10 years) in Togo, Nov 2023-Feb 2024. Among 60 children, 25% PCR-positive for P. falciparum, with 33.3% (n=5) progressing to symptomatic malaria. Symptomatic cases showed significantly elevated IL-6 (p=0.0004 vs non-infected), IFN-γ (p<0.0001 vs non-infected), and IL-10 (p<0.0001 vs non-infected) compared to asymptomatic carriers or controls. IL-10 strongly correlated with parasite density (r=0.911, p=0.001) and negatively with Ct values (r=-0.973, p=0.001), suggesting potential biomarkers for symptomatic disease.

articles.headline: Environmental barriers restrict insecticide-resistance gene flow in West Africa
articles.title_original: Genomic population structure and insecticide resistance mechanisms in the malaria vector An. coluzzii across contrasting bioclimatic zones in West Africa.
articles.details: Population genomic analysis of An. coluzzii across Ghana, Mali, and Côte d'Ivoire using whole-genome SNPs. Restricted gene flow between northern savannah and southern forested regions limits adaptive allele dispersal. Northern populations showed greater target-site pyrethroid resistance at Vgsc and metabolic resistance markers (carboxylesterases, Gste2) reflecting regional insecticide use patterns. Coastal Greater Accra region harbored unique selection signals including Cyp12f (cytochrome P450) and UDP glucuronosyltransferase, possibly linked to urban agricultural intensity.

articles.headline: Chromatin dynamics and transcription factor binding coordinate parasite development
articles.title_original: Chromatin state dynamics during the Plasmodium falciparum intraerythrocytic development cycle.
articles.authors: Brown AS Jr, Llinás M, Mahony S
articles.details: Genome-wide characterization of chromatin accessibility, histone modifications, and HP1 occupancy throughout P. falciparum's 48-hour asexual intraerythrocytic developmental cycle (IDC). Some chromatin states remained stable while others showed dynamic changes linked to gene activation or repression. Analysis grouped transcription factors by their chromatin preferences, providing mechanistic insight into how chromatin state dynamics coordinate periodic gene expression during parasite blood-stage development.

articles.headline: Intervention reduced malaria prevention disparities in poorest households
articles.title_original: Development of a new wealth index for Tanzania: the moderated effect of the implementation of 1,7- malaria reactive community-based testing and response (1,7-mRCTR) by socioeconomic position (SEP) with malaria prevention.
articles.details: Cross-sectional surveys of 19,686 households in Tanzania (2015 baseline, 2018 endline) during 1,7-malaria reactive community-based testing and response (1,7-mRCTR). Poorest tertile had lower baseline odds of owning LLINs (OR=0.62, 95% CI 0.54-0.70) or using LLINs (OR=0.53, 95% CI 0.45-0.62) vs wealthiest tertile. Difference-in-differences analysis showed 1,7-mRCTR produced disproportionately larger increases in LLIN ownership (OR=1.26, 95% CI 1.03-1.56) and use (OR=1.88, 95% CI 1.25-2.82) among poorest households, reducing wealth-based inequalities.

articles.headline: Clinical algorithm proposed for congenital malaria diagnosis in non-endemic areas
articles.title_original: Congenital Malaria in Non-Endemic Settings: A Literature Review and Update on Diagnosis and Management.
articles.details: Scoping literature review of 26 papers (2013-2023) on congenital malaria (CM). Proposes definition: peripheral blood smear or DNA detection of asexual Plasmodium spp. in infants <28 days old regardless of symptoms, or infants <1 year with no travel history to endemic areas. Neonates typically asymptomatic at birth with delayed symptom onset if present. Proposes diagnostic algorithm emphasizing polymerase chain reaction for detecting parasitemia below microscopy detection limits, addressing diagnostic challenges in both endemic and non-endemic settings.

articles.headline: Azithromycin reduces malaria parasitemia at delivery vs sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine
articles.title_original: Safety and efficacy of azithromycin-containing regimen as an intermittent preventive therapy for malaria in pregnant women: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 8 randomized controlled trials in moderate sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance areas. Azithromycin (AZ)-containing intermittent preventive therapy (IPTp) regimens reduced risk of peripheral parasitemia at delivery vs SP-based IPTp alone (odds ratio 0.71, 95% CI 0.57-0.88). No significant differences in adverse pregnancy outcomes or maternal anemia. AZ-containing regimens showed safety profile similar to SP-based IPTp while providing additional sexually transmitted infection coverage.

articles.headline: Drug stockouts and supply delays undermine malaria control in Africa
articles.title_original: Drug stockouts and treatment delays in African health systems: impact on malaria morbidity and mortality.
articles.authors: Obeagu EI, Abdulrahman AO, Anetor JI
articles.details: Narrative review examining multifactorial causes of artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) stockouts and treatment delays in African health systems, including weaknesses in procurement, supply chain management, infrastructure, and human resources. Systemic challenges lead to treatment interruptions, increased morbidity and mortality, spread of drug resistance, and erosion of healthcare trust. Review emphasizes comprehensive strategies for supply chain strengthening, improved forecasting and inventory management, and enhanced healthcare delivery capacity.

articles.headline: Atypical presentation delays P. falciparum diagnosis in returned child
articles.title_original: Alert for Imported Malaria in Non-Endemic Areas: A Case Report of Atypical Falciparum Malaria in a Young Child and Diagnostic Experience.
articles.details: Case report of 2-year-11-month-old boy returning from Sudan presenting with fever and diarrhea. P. falciparum malaria confirmed by rapid diagnostic testing and microscopy after laboratory investigation prompted by epidemiological history. Illustrates diagnostic challenges of pediatric imported malaria in non-endemic areas due to atypical clinical presentation and proposes clinical suspicion-driven testing pathway for early detection.

articles.headline: Baseline malaria and schistosomiasis prevalence documented in Malawi school children
articles.title_original: Repeated biannual cross-sectional surveys in primary schools set baseline seasonal and spatial surveillance for malaria and schistosomiasis in the Shire Valley Transformation Programme (SVTP), Malawi.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Four biannual cross-sectional surveys in 21 primary schools in Chikwawa District, Malawi (4,176 children aged 7-13 years) across two wet and two dry seasons. Malaria prevalence 10.8% (95% CI 9.8-11.7%), urogenital schistosomiasis 36.5% (95% CI 35.1-38.0%), intestinal schistosomiasis 1.9% (95% CI 1.5-2.4%), and co-infection 5.2% (95% CI 4.5-5.9%). Seasonal fluctuations noted for malaria; both diseases showed strong spatial heterogeneity. Malaria exceeded 25% and urogenital schistosomiasis surpassed 50% in certain schools.

articles.headline: Plant extract reduces parasitemia and restores spleen structure in infected mice
articles.title_original: Spleen protective role, Antiplasmodial and Antioxidant activity of Polyalthia longifolia in mice infected with Plasmodium berghei parasite.
articles.details: Preclinical study in 36 Swiss albino BALB/c mice inoculated with P. berghei NK 65. Doses of ethanol extract of Polyalthia longifolia (EEPL) at 100, 200, and 400 mg/kg/day dose-dependently reduced parasitemia, increased antioxidant enzymes (GSH, GPx, CAT, SOD, TAC) and ferritin in spleen tissue, and reversed structural disorganization of infected spleens. Standard artemether-lumefantrine (2 mg/kg/day) showed highest survival rate; 400 mg/kg EEPL showed comparable survival benefit.

articles.headline: Digital platform improves seasonal malaria chemoprevention adherence in Mozambique
articles.title_original: Lessons from using SALAMA (DIGIT HCM-health campaign management platform) to implement and optimize seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, Mozambique.
articles.details: Implementation study transitioning from paper to digital (DIGIT Health Campaign Management platform) seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Nampula, Mozambique, 2024. Digital approach improved sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine administration and enabled real-time data use and early identification of outliers (e.g., >10% wastage prompting targeted supervision). Administrative coverage 75-109% in digital vs 93-105% in paper campaigns; digital coverage more closely matched survey-based household coverage (~74% vs ~79%). Platform tracked first-dose adherence directly.

articles.headline: pfhrp2/pfhrp3 deletions explain widespread RDT failure in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Molecular, Transcriptomic, and Proteomic Characterization of Plasmodium Infections that Evade Detection by Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Mizan Aman, Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 148 suspected malaria cases in Mizan Aman, Ethiopia. Among 29 P. falciparum PCR-positive individuals, 26/29 (89.7%) tested false-negative by PfHRP2/pLDH rapid diagnostic test (RDT). Molecular analysis revealed 27/29 (93.1%) carried deletions in both pfhrp2 and pfhrp3 genes; 22/29 (75.9%) had negligible pfhrp2 transcripts. Proteomic analysis detected PfLDH peptides in 10 samples but no PfHRP2 or PfHRP3 peptides in any sample, confirming gene deletions explain RDT failure mechanisms.

articles.headline: GPT-4o outperforms probabilistic models in assigning causes of death
articles.title_original: Comparison of verbal autopsy using a large language model to biologically confirmed causes of death for malaria and other communicable diseases among children in six sub-Saharan African countries.
articles.details: This study examined 3,129 under-five deaths from six African CHAMPS sites (2016-2022) to compare three computational models (GPT-4o, InSilicoVA, InterVA-5) for assigning causes of death against biologically confirmed diagnoses (MITS). GPT-4o correctly classified 46.2% of malaria deaths versus 30% for InSilicoVA and 23.1% for InterVA-5, achieving cause-specific mortality fraction accuracy of 0.36 compared to 0.07–0.08. Authors recommend integrating LLM-driven tools into vital registration systems for enhanced diagnostic precision.

articles.headline: Fellowship program strengthens malaria surveillance capacity in Uganda
articles.title_original: Uganda Public Health Fellowship Program's Contributions to Malaria Control Programs 2015-2022: Strategies, Implementation Challenges, and Opportunities.
articles.details: The Uganda Public Health Fellowship Program documented contributions from 2015–2022, leading or supporting 50 malaria projects including 14 surveillance projects, 11 outbreak investigations, 7 epidemiological studies, 5 case studies, 6 quality improvement projects, 3 policy briefs, and 4 training initiatives integrated with the Ministry of Health. These projects strengthened surveillance, coordination, and outbreak response. Primary challenge was single-source funding vulnerability.

articles.headline: LILRB1 regulatory variant reduces malaria susceptibility in Senegalese populations
articles.title_original: A Functional Intronic Variant of LILRB1 Associated with Clinical Malaria in the Senegalese Population.
articles.details: This study genotyped 267 Senegalese individuals (116 with severe malaria, 74 mild malaria, 77 controls) for four LILRB1 variants identified through African eQTL analysis. The rs7246537 A allele significantly reduced risk of clinical malaria (OR=0.50, 95% CI: 0.28–0.88, p=0.0165) and cerebral malaria (OR=0.44, 95% CI: 0.22–0.86, p=0.0176). Functional assays confirmed the A allele reduced promoter activity twofold, demonstrating mechanistic plausibility.

articles.headline: Natural killer cells show reduced cytokine production in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Altered natural killer cell function in children with severe malaria.
articles.details: This study compared NK cell phenotypes and function in Ugandan children <5 years with severe malaria (n=21) versus community controls (n=19) from low and moderate transmission areas. While total NK cell counts were similar, severe malaria cases had higher proportions of LILRB1+ NK cells. The consistent finding across both transmission settings was reduced IFN-γ production, suggesting impaired cellular immunity in severe disease.

articles.headline: Conservative management preserves splenic function in malaria rupture
articles.title_original: Spontaneous splenic rupture as a rare complication of Plasmodium vivax: a case report.
articles.details: Case report of a 30-year-old Moroccan soldier with Plasmodium vivax malaria who developed acute splenic rupture within 24 hours of admission, confirmed by CT imaging showing subcapsular hematoma and hemoperitoneum. Conservative management with bed rest, hemodynamic monitoring, rehydration, and transfusion—rather than splenectomy—resulted in full recovery with defervescence, complete pain resolution, and preserved splenic function.

articles.headline: Machine learning forecasts malaria with high accuracy using climate data
articles.title_original: Application of a Temporal Fusion Transformer and Long-Term Climate and Disease Data to Assess the Predictive Power and Understand the Drivers for Malaria and Dengue.
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health
articles.details: This study applied Temporal Fusion Transformers to long-term climate and weekly malaria and dengue case data from South Africa and Vietnam. The best malaria forecasting model achieved R²=0.95 with MAE of 4.98; dengue models reached R²=0.90. Extreme temperature and rainfall were consistently the strongest environmental drivers, with ENSO and IOD improving longer-range malaria forecasts and revealing non-stationary climate-disease relationships across decades.

articles.headline: Novel chalcogen-phosphoranes show promise against resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Fast-Acting Chalcogen-Phosphoranes Inhibit Sensitive and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.
articles.details: This study evaluated eight sulfur-containing chalcogen-phosphorane compounds against Plasmodium falciparum 3D7, finding submicromolar potency (IC50s=0.7–1.9 μM) with good selectivity over human cells (SI≥12). Compound 5 demonstrated fast-acting activity with no cross-resistance to standard antimalarials and additive effects with artesunate. In a P. berghei mouse model, compound 5 showed modest in vivo activity but improved survival.

articles.headline: DHAP and AP maintain high efficacy against uncomplicated malaria in Ghana
articles.title_original: Therapeutic efficacy of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-pyronaridine combinations in the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Ghana, 2023.
articles.details: This WHO surveillance study evaluated therapeutic efficacy of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHAP, five sites) and artesunate-pyronaridine (AP, four sites) in Ghanaian children (6 months–9 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in 2023. DHAP achieved 99.2% PCR-uncorrected day-42 cure rate (100% PCR-corrected); AP achieved 96.2% PCR-uncorrected (97.3% PCR-corrected). Neither drug resulted in early treatment failure, with day-3 parasitemia prevalence of 0.3% (DHAP) and 0.4% (AP).

articles.headline: Replication stress accelerates genomic copy number variations in parasites
articles.title_original: Replication stress increases de novo CNVs across the malaria parasite genome.
articles.details: This study investigated de novo copy number variation (CNV) dynamics in Plasmodium falciparum using low-input genomics and specialized computational tools. Following treatment with an antimalarial compound that inhibits replication, genome-wide de novo CNVs significantly increased, affecting genes in various cellular pathways relevant to human infection. The findings emphasize the connection between replication stress, DNA repair, and CNV generation in this rapidly evolving pathogen.

articles.headline: Automated microscopy equals human microscopists for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Automated microscopy for malaria diagnosis in a reference laboratory in nonendemic settings.
articles.details: This study evaluated miLab™ automated microscopy against conventional microscopy and nested-multiplex PCR in 400 samples from a nonendemic reference laboratory (2021–2024). miLab showed high concordance with conventional microscopy (90.8%, kappa=0.8, sensitivity 92.1%, specificity 89.4%) but lower sensitivity versus PCR (62.8% vs 95.4% specificity). Parasite density estimates were 11.6% lower than conventional microscopy; miLab performs well without expert microscopists in nonendemic settings.

articles.headline: Mosquito species ecology shapes malaria risk patterns across Kenya
articles.title_original: Mapping mosquito diversity in Kenya correlating species distribution with malaria prevalence across varied climatic parameters.
articles.details: This study analyzed data from 23 Kenyan counties (January 2019–June 2021) to assess relationships between climate variables, mosquito species distribution, and malaria prevalence. Malaria prevalence ranged from 19% in Lake Endemic zones to 0.9% in low-risk zones. Anopheles gambiae dominated coastal and semi-arid areas while A. funestus was highest in highland and lake zones. Malaria prevalence varied with environmental factors and ITN coverage, suggesting context-specific control strategies.

articles.headline: Outdoor malaria transmission documented in urban Benin despite net use
articles.title_original: An underestimated risk? Analysis of outdoor malaria transmission in an urban area of Southern Benin.
articles.details: This study compared indoor and outdoor malaria transmission in Porto-Novo, Benin (April–September 2019), collecting 6,201 mosquitoes. Anopheles gambiae s.l. human biting rates were similar or higher outdoors in some neighborhoods. Plasmodium falciparum infections were found indoors (Tokpota 4.41%, Djegan-Daho 3.57%) and outdoors (Djassin 1.26%). Among 250 surveyed households, 86% slept indoors and most owned and used nets; site-specific variations in vector behavior suggest need for context-specific strategies.

articles.headline: Healthcare workers lack knowledge for non-malarial febrile illness management
articles.title_original: Practices and challenges related to management of chikungunya and non-malarial acute febrile illnesses in malaria-endemic areas of Afar region, Northeast Ethiopia.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study across 6 healthcare facilities in Afar region, Ethiopia, surveyed 82 healthcare workers and conducted 10 in-depth interviews. While malaria was the primary diagnosis (95.1%), 59.2% of febrile cases were non-malarial; only 23.4% of workers demonstrated good knowledge of chikungunya transmission and prevention. Key gaps identified included lack of diagnostic tools, inadequate training, and community misconceptions about non-malarial febrile illnesses.

articles.headline: Secondary Anopheles vectors sustain malaria transmission in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Beyond Anopheles gambiae sensu lato: exploring the impact of non-dominant Anopheles species on malaria persistence in high-transmission endemic areas of Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Mosquito surveys across Burkina Faso's three ecological zones (2023–2024) identified 1,718 non-gambiae Anopheles: 688 in 2023, 1,030 in 2024. Five species found; Anopheles nili overwhelmingly dominant (87.5% of 2023 captures, 93% of 2024). Plasmodium falciparum detected in all species except An. funestus, with highest infection in Diébougou. Most specimens collected outdoors with strong anthropophilic tendencies, suggesting current indoor-focused interventions are insufficient.

articles.headline: Mobile malaria workers maintain services during pandemic restrictions
articles.title_original: Progress towards malaria elimination: insights from Cambodia's mobile malaria workers during the COVID-19 pandemic.
articles.details: Narrative account of Cambodia's malaria elimination progress during COVID-19 pandemic. Mobile malaria workers faced logistical and communication challenges while traveling to remote regions under movement restrictions. Through locally tailored implementation and close coordination with health authorities, mobile workers maintained case detection and treatment services among hard-to-reach populations, protecting staff and beneficiaries while supporting Cambodia's 2025 elimination target.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine outperforms pyronaridine-artesunate in malaria-COVID-19 co-infection
articles.title_original: Artemether-lumefantrine versus pyronaridine-artesunate for the treatment of malaria in patients with mild to moderate COVID-19 in Kenya and Burkina Faso: a randomised open-label trial (MALCOV).
articles.details: Randomized trial (n=143, median age 19 years) in Kenya and Burkina Faso (January 2021–January 2022) compared artemether-lumefantrine (AL) and pyronaridine-artesunate (PA) for uncomplicated malaria in SARS-CoV-2 co-infected patients. SARS-CoV-2 clearance by day 7: PA 41% vs AL 58% (aRR=0.78, p=0.37); median viral load day 7: PA 855 vs AL 81 copies/mL (p=0.023); time to clearance slower with PA (aHR=0.55, p=0.004). Both achieved high parasitological cure (PA 100%, AL 99%) and similar symptom resolution.

articles.headline: Web-based tool for exploring Plasmodium genomic variation
articles.title_original: Malaria-GENOMAP: a web-based tool for exploring genomic variation of malaria parasites.
articles.details: Malaria-GENOMAP integrates genomic variant data, allele frequencies, and geographical maps for exploring Plasmodium species. Database includes: P. knowlesi (n=139), P. malariae (n=158), P. ovale curtisi (n=36), P. ovale wallikeri (n=47), P. simium (n=38), and P. vivax (n=1,359). Tool enables investigation of population structure, geographic associations of mutations, and putative drug resistance markers, supporting malaria control surveillance efforts.

articles.headline: HRP2-based RDTs overestimate malaria prevalence in rural Ghana
articles.title_original: False-positive and false-negative malaria rapid diagnostic test results: evidence from a nationally representative survey of children in Ghana.
articles.details: National survey of 4,417 Ghanaian children <5 comparing HRP2-based rapid diagnostic tests with microscopy (2022 Ghana DHS). RDT prevalence 20.3% vs microscopy 9.9%; RDT sensitivity 89.7% (95% CI 86.5–92.2), specificity 87.4% (95% CI 86.3–88.4), but positive predictive value only 43.9%. False-negative results more likely in rural areas and insecticide-sprayed dwellings, with regional variation. Low PPV may lead to malaria overestimation in high-transmission settings.

articles.headline: Higher-dose primaquine and tafenoquine options for P. vivax relapse prevention
articles.title_original: Moving towards high-dose primaquine or single-dose tafenoquine for Plasmodium vivax treatment in Cambodia: a meeting report from dissemination of results of the EFFORT trial to stakeholders.
articles.details: Meeting report on EFFORT trial dissemination in Cambodia (4-country RCT). Trial compared 7-day unsupervised high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg total dose) and single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) versus Cambodia's standard 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg total dose) for P. vivax treatment. Previous regimen limited by poor adherence and suboptimal efficacy. Trial also evaluated feasibility and cost-effectiveness to inform Cambodia's progress toward 2025 malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Iron, copper, and zinc isotope shifts reflect malaria infection responses
articles.title_original: Iron, copper and zinc isotope ratios in malaria infection reveal signatures of the Plasmodium biology and host mice response to the infection.
articles.details: Mechanistic study of metallome (iron, copper, zinc) in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice using isotope ratio analysis. Parasitized red blood cells showed altered Fe, Cu, and Zn isotopic compositions. Hepatic iron overload with isotopically lighter iron suggested compensatory mobilization during anemia. Copper deficiency in liver with lighter isotopic composition and release into bloodstream via ceruloplasmin indicated copper's auxiliary role in iron mobilization. Zinc isotopic changes suggested response to host immunity.

articles.headline: Transcriptional repressor controls Plasmodium berghei blood stage development
articles.title_original: Precise gene regulation through transcriptional repression is essential for Plasmodium berghei asexual blood stage development.
articles.details: Mechanistic study identified PbAP2-TR, an AP2-family transcription factor essential for P. berghei asexual blood stage development. Conditional knockout caused developmental arrest at trophozoite stage. PbAP2-TR functions as a transcriptional repressor recruiting co-factor PbMORC for chromatin remodeling. Time-course transcriptome analysis shows PbAP2-TR controls transcription patterns during early to late trophozoite development, enabling precise gene expression timing.

articles.headline: Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine reduces maternal chlamydia and group B streptococcus
