articles.title_original: Impact of intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine on sexually transmitted and reproductive tract infections: results from a randomized trial in Uganda.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of RCT (n=2,265 pregnant women in Uganda) comparing monthly intermittent preventive treatment regimens: SP, DP, or DP+SP. At delivery, IPTp-SP reduced Chlamydia trachomatis prevalence by 80% (2.5% vs 12.4%, 95% CI 67%–88%) and GBS by 35% (7.7% vs 11.7%, 95% CI 1%–57%) versus IPTp-DP. C. trachomatis associated with preterm delivery (PR=1.86, 95% CI 1.07–3.25) and GBS with term low birthweight (PR=2.08, 95% CI 1.06–4.08).

articles.headline: Microscopy misidentifies nearly all mixed Plasmodium infections in India
articles.title_original: Plasmodium mono and mixed-infections in India: a tale of see-saw in species misidentification.
articles.authors: Deora N, Pande V, Sinha A
articles.details: Systematic analysis of 2,706 microscopy-PCR paired samples from 16 locations across 11 Indian states found 15% overall misidentification rate (408/2,706). Strikingly, >98% of mixed-infections (400/405) were misidentified as mono-infections, almost all as P. falciparum. Jharkhand and Madhya Pradesh accounted for >20% of misidentifications. Findings reveal systematic overestimation of P. falciparum burden and underestimation of non-falciparum species, potentially misdirecting elimination resources.

articles.headline: Plasma exchange and hemodiafiltration reduce malaria cytokine storm
articles.title_original: The blood purification therapy-based strategy effectively rescued the severe falciparum malaria patient experiencing cytokine storm-driven MODS.
articles.details: Case report of 63-year-old with severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria and multiple organ dysfunction syndrome from cytokine storm treated with therapeutic plasma exchange (TPE) plus continuous venovenous hemodiafiltration (CVVHDF). After first treatment, interleukin-6 decreased from 729.65 to 26.42 pg/mL and interferon-gamma from 170.38 to 42.47 pg/mL; patient recovered. Literature review of 29 patients receiving blood purification as adjuvant therapy showed 19 with cytokine storm/MODS all achieved good prognosis.

articles.headline: P. vivax congenital and neonatal malaria cases in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax congenital and neonatal malaria with atypical presentation in Western Ethiopia: four cases highlighting diagnostic pitfalls.
articles.details: Case series of 4 neonates (median age 18 days) with P. vivax infection confirmed on Giemsa smears. Presentations included fever and poor feeding in 3 infants, tachypnea in 2, with one afebrile case presenting with omphalitis. All received IV artesunate 3 mg/kg plus empiric antibiotics and were discharged by hospital day 7 with no deaths. The report highlights the diagnostic challenges of P. vivax congenital/neonatal malaria in Ethiopia.

articles.headline: Natural esters inhibit P. falciparum aminopeptidase PfA-M17
articles.title_original: Natural triterpenic phenolic esters target PfA-M17 in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: A mixture of 8 triterpenic esters (8TTE) from Keetia leucantha twigs was evaluated against P. falciparum using phenotypic analysis, enzymatic tests, molecular docking, and metabolomic profiling. The compounds demonstrated inhibition of aminopeptidase PfA-M17 with activity onset in early-ring stage and perturbations in parasite hemoglobin metabolism. The authors conclude that further structure-activity studies are warranted to develop 8TTE-related molecules as future antimalarial drugs.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr bednets overcome pyrethroid resistance in Uganda
articles.title_original: Chlorfenapyr bednets effectively overcome pyrethroid resistance escalation in highly resistant Anopheles malaria vectors in Uganda.
articles.details: Experimental hut trials in Uganda evaluated bednets against highly resistant Anopheles mosquitoes. Interceptor G2 (chlorfenapyr) demonstrated superior mortality (OR 18.7, 95% CI 8.05-48.6, P<0.0001) with 70.6% mortality against An. funestus and 63.2% against An. gambiae, compared to pyrethroid-only nets (~25%). PermaNet 3.0, Olyset Plus, and Royal Guard showed substantially lower efficacy. Genetic analysis showed pyrethroid resistance markers were not associated with Interceptor G2 efficacy loss.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic P. falciparum shows modest metabolomic changes
articles.title_original: Exploratory Serum Metabolomic Profiling of Asymptomatic Malaria Children Infected with Plasmodium falciparum in Tanzania.
articles.details: 1H NMR spectroscopy study of 48 school-aged children from high-transmission Tanzania (27 malaria-positive, 21 malaria-negative) revealed modest group-level metabolic alterations including lower lactate, pyruvate, branched-chain amino acids, and threonine in infected children. A metabolically distinct subgroup (18.5% of infected children) displayed shifts in amino acid and lipid metabolism resembling symptomatic malaria profiles. Results highlight the importance of accounting for intragroup metabolic variability and coinfections in endemic settings.

articles.headline: Pv3Rs statistical tool infers P. vivax recurrence types
articles.title_original: Pv3Rs: Plasmodium vivax relapse, recrudescence, and reinfection statistical genetic inference.
articles.authors: Foo YS, White MT, Taylor AR
articles.details: Pv3Rs is a statistical inference tool for determining the cause of P. vivax recurrence—relapse, recrudescence, or reinfection—from genetic data. The tool models genetic relationships between parasites within and across infections to compute posterior probabilities of recurrence states. It is applicable to various genetic data types including length polymorphic loci and amplicon sequencing. The software is freely available on CRAN.

articles.headline: PI4Kβ emerges as dual-action malaria drug target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium Phosphatidylinositol 4-Kinase Beta (PI4Kβ): An Emerging Target at the Interface of Parasite Biology and Host Immunity.
articles.details: Review article discussing phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase beta (PI4Kβ) as an essential lipid kinase in Plasmodium central to intracellular signaling, vesicular trafficking, and parasite development. PI4Kβ also modulates host immune responses through regulation of protein export, immune evasion mechanisms, and extracellular vesicle formation. Inhibition of PI4Kβ disrupts parasite survival and may enhance immune recognition. The review highlights PI4Kβ as a promising target combining antiparasitic and immunomodulatory effects.

articles.headline: Exported parasite Hsp70 may stimulate NK granzyme B release
articles.title_original: Is the Association of Granzyme B and the Exported Parasite Hsp70 a Possible Trojan Horse of Severe Malaria?
articles.details: This paper proposes a hypothesis whereby Plasmodium falciparum-exported Hsp70 (PfHsp70-x) on infected RBC membrane may stimulate natural killer cells to release granzyme B, potentially contributing to severe malaria pathogenesis. The hypothesis is based on observations that extracellular Hsp70 recruits NK cells and triggers granzyme B release, that PfHsp70-x is present on infected RBC surfaces, and that granzyme B levels are elevated in severe malaria. The authors discuss prospects for Hsp70-GrB-mediated antimalarial therapy.

articles.headline: Global malaria mortality declining; GCC mostly malaria-free
articles.title_original: Malaria Public Health Status: Global Context and Update on Gulf Cooperation Council Countries.
articles.details: Global malaria mortality fell from 864,000 deaths in 2000 to 576,000 in 2019, though progress has slowed with COVID-19 contributing to increased mortality since 2020. Widespread P. falciparum resistance to first-line drugs has prompted development of new antimalarials. Recent vaccine successes (RTS,S/AS01 and R21/MM) have renewed promise. The Eastern Mediterranean region has experienced increased cases and deaths since 2015. Most GCC countries are malaria-free; Saudi Arabia and Oman remain endemic with threats from drug resistance and imported cases.

articles.headline: Unusual Plasmodium presentation in pediatric blood smear
articles.title_original: An unusual presentation of Plasmodium spp. in peripheral blood smear.
articles.details: A 7-year-old boy presented with abdominal pain and fever for 6–7 days. Laboratory findings included leukocytosis with left shift, hemoglobin 9.2 g/dL, platelets 35,000/µL, and elevated C-reactive protein 144.8 mg/L. Peripheral blood smear examination revealed unusual microscopic findings. The abstract does not specify what the unusual findings were.

articles.headline: Severe imported P. falciparum malaria in Moscow 2020–2023
articles.title_original: Clinical and epidemiological profile of severe imported Plasmodium falciparum malaria in travelers at hospital admission: A retrospective single-center study in Moscow, Russia.
articles.authors: Emerole K, Tokmalaev A, Mvuania V
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 32 imported severe malaria cases at a Moscow hospital (2020–2023). Patients had median age 41 years, were 89% male and 91.2% Russian, and acquired P. falciparum in sub-Saharan Africa. At admission, 99% presented with high parasitemia and 98% with altered consciousness; additional WHO severity criteria included profound weakness (78.1%) and jaundice (52%). No patients used chemoprophylaxis, emphasizing the importance of antimalarial prophylaxis.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to inflammatory biomarker analysis study
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Analysis of the Relationship Between Malaria and Inflammatory Biomarker Levels in Children in Rural Iraq: Impact of Nutritional Deficiencies, Anemia, and Hematological Disorders" published in Trop Parasitol. 2025 Jul-Dec;15(2):110-117. doi: 10.4103/tp.tp_26_25. Epub 2025 Oct 25. PMID: 41244076; PMCID: PMC12617583.
articles.details: Corrigendum to a previously published article on malaria and inflammatory biomarkers in Iraqi children.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine integration into Southeast Asian elimination
articles.title_original: Integrating malaria vaccines into Southeast Asian elimination strategies: A cautious path forward.
articles.details: This article discusses strategies for integrating malaria vaccines into Southeast Asian elimination programs. The abstract was not provided in full.

articles.headline: Malaria causes 2.4 million pregnancy anemia cases in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: The burden of malaria-attributable maternal anaemia and the impact of preventive treatment across sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Individual-level analysis of 12,608 pregnancies across 7 African countries. In 2023, 13.1 million pregnancies had P. falciparum exposure; without prevention, 2.41 million (95% CI 1.98–3.04 million) would develop moderate-severe anemia (Hb<9 g/dL), including 600,000 severe cases (Hb<7 g/dL). Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine preventive treatment averted approximately 1.10 million (0.72–1.61 million) moderate-severe and 330,000 (225,000–523,000) severe anemia cases in 2023. Malaria remains a major driver of maternal anemia risk.

articles.headline: Routine pre-eclampsia diagnosis shows moderate accuracy in malaria-endemic areas
articles.title_original: Diagnostic Concordance and Misclassification of Pre-Eclampsia in a Malaria-Endemic Setting: A Comparative Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 618 pregnant women in malaria-endemic settings comparing routine clinical diagnosis with reference criteria. Overall diagnostic concordance was 50.2% (κ=0.233, p<0.001). Pre-eclampsia detection sensitivity 61.8%, specificity 62.2% (PPV 65.9%, NPV 57.9%). Malaria parasite density was not independently associated with blood pressure but was associated with elevated urine protein-creatinine ratio (β=0.17, p<0.001). Routine diagnosis demonstrates moderate performance with considerable misclassification, particularly when concurrent malaria is present.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 13.6% among pregnant women in Northern Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence, Associated Risk Factors, and the Impact of Malaria on Anemia in Pregnant Women Attending ANC in North Gondar Zone, Northern Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Alula GA, Endale T, Yibeltie G
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study of 308 pregnant women in North Gondar Zone, Ethiopia. Malaria prevalence 13.6%. Risk factors included age ≥40 years (AOR 4.8; 95% CI 1.4–16.0), rural residence (AOR 2.8; 1.2–6.5), low education (AOR 2.6; 1.0–6.7), living within 1 km of water bodies (AOR 3.0; 1.4–6.5), unscreened houses (AOR 3.6; 1.6–8.2), and non-use of insecticide-treated nets (AOR 7.2; 2.9–18.0). Among malaria-infected women, 40% were anemic. Malaria significantly increased anemia risk.

articles.headline: DBLMSP genes organized as discrete recombining modules in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Module-level recombination drives DBLMSP polymorphism and functional conservation in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Analysis of DBLMSP1 and DBLMSP2 surface antigen genes from P. falciparum identified nine genotypes each consisting of discrete, recombinable sequence modules (labeled 1M2a, 1M3c, 2M3b, etc.). Some modules were shared between paralogs, suggesting inter-locus recombination. Within dominant genotype DBLMSP1-1, nucleotide diversity and Tajima's D peaked in variable modules while conserved structural elements (receptor-binding cleft, SPAM domain) showed purifying selection. Long-range linkage disequilibrium aligned with module junctions, suggesting modular structure shapes recombination independently of selection.

articles.headline: Review of genetic approaches for malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Advancing Malaria Vector Control: Insights Into Mosquito Immunity and Genetic Strategies.
articles.details: Review examining mosquito innate immunity and genetic strategies (CRISPR-Cas9, Wolbachia, RNA interference, gene drives, RIDL, pgSIT, mosGILT) for malaria vector control. Describes how Anopheles mosquitoes identify Plasmodium through pathogen recognition receptors and effector mechanisms (antimicrobial peptides, melanization), and how parasites evade these defenses. Genetic modification techniques aim to reduce vector competence and transmission. Review highlights advantages and limitations of various genetic approaches for reducing malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Cameroon nationwide malaria surveillance: 43% prevalence with P. falciparum dominance
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium Species Using Rapid Diagnostic Tests in Cameroon: First-Year Data From Routine Malaria Surveillance.
articles.details: Nationwide malaria surveillance across all regions and health facility levels (January–December 2024) testing 3,843,507 individuals using P.f/Pan RDTs. Overall malaria prevalence 43%. P. falciparum monoinfection 82% of cases, mixed infections (P. falciparum + Pan) 15%, Pan infections 3%. Regional variation: Far North 92.1% Pf monoinfection, North 85.7%, Central 82.5%, West 81.7%, East 80.6%. Seasonal analysis showed peak positive cases June–July. Authors note RDT limitations in species differentiation and recommend molecular techniques and genomic surveillance.

articles.headline: Circulating T follicular helper cells altered in placental malaria
articles.title_original: cTfh Cell Activation and Changes in BTLA and LAG-3 Expression Differentially Associate With Poor Pregnancy Outcome in Plasmodium falciparum Placental Malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 49 women at delivery in Cameroon (March 2022–May 2023) examining circulating T follicular helper (cTfh) cells in peripheral, placental, and cord blood. Placental malaria (PM) associated with increased cTfh frequencies in both PBMC and intervillous blood. PM-negative women had higher ICOS- cTfh cells while PM-positive women had elevated ICOS+ cTfh cells correlating with parasitemia levels. BTLA expression was downregulated in bulk cTfh during PM. cTfh frequencies in intervillous blood positively correlated with infant birth weight, while cTfh and BTLA/LAG-3 expression negatively correlated with maternal hemoglobin and birth weight.

articles.headline: Anti-gSG6-P1 biomarker reveals seasonal and behavioral dynamics of Anopheles exposure
articles.title_original: Immunological surveillance using anti-gSG6-P1 IgG biomarker reveals spatio-temporal dynamics of Anopheles exposure and gaps in malaria risk assessment in the Greater Mekong Subregion.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 184 participants in Sisaket Province, Thailand sampled across three seasons (August 2022, December 2022, April 2023). Anti-gSG6-P1 IgG biomarker quantified by ELISA showed seasonality as the strongest influence on exposure, driven by human behavioral factors particularly rubber tapping activity. Higher rubber plot entry frequency (5–7 days/week) significantly increased anti-gSG6-P1 IgG responses (1.08±0.36) compared to lower frequency (0–4 days/week) (0.96±0.35). Study revealed significant limitations of the anti-gSG6-P1 IgG biomarker in the Greater Mekong Subregion due to lack of well-characterized serological kinetics for predominant local vector species.

articles.headline: Brazilian Amazon malaria control shows reactive, inefficient insecticide distribution
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the insecticide custody chain and its relationship with malaria burden in the Brazilian Amazon: a process and exploratory impact assessment (2019-2023).
articles.details: Mixed-methods assessment (2019–2023) examining indoor residual spraying (IRS with Etofenprox 20%) custody chain across Brazil and three high-incidence municipalities in Amazonas. National analysis: insecticide volume significantly associated with malaria cases (β=0.161; p=0.038; R²=0.74) and deforestation (β=0.626; p=0.034). Time-series analysis revealed reactive allocation pattern lagging behind malaria incidence peaks. Local models lacked statistical significance. Risk matrix identified systemic weaknesses in planning, storage, application, and monitoring. Authors recommend risk-based planning incorporating environmental variables and improved surveillance.

articles.headline: Tandem repeats in P. falciparum proteins drive structural and functional diversity
articles.title_original: Structural and functional insights from detailed computational analysis of Plasmodium repetome.
articles.details: Comprehensive computational analysis of tandem repeat (TR) content across Plasmodium species proteomes identified P. falciparum and P. vivax as exceptionally rich in TRs. P. falciparum carries longer TRs predominantly in exported parasite proteins. Short and intermediate TRs show Asparagine bias reflecting evolutionary pressure from replication slippage and positive selection. Gene ontology analysis: largest TR-containing protein proportion involved in nucleic acid, protein, and small molecule binding. Variable surface antigen families had low repeat content despite their immune evasion roles. 3D structural predictions show TRs form ordered conformations facilitating intermolecular interactions.

articles.headline: Quality improvement increased malaria reporting sensitivity to 100% in 5 weeks
articles.title_original: Quality Improvement Initiative to Improve the Sensitivity of Malaria Reporting under Integrated Health Information Platform, Raigarh, Chhattisgarh, 2023.
articles.details: Quality improvement project in four primary health centers in Raigarh, Chhattisgarh using Plan-Do-Study-Act method. Initial Integrated Health Information Platform (IHIP) sensitivity was 44% (percentage of laboratory-tested cases entered into system). Root cause analysis identified inadequate laboratory technician knowledge of data entry and poor feedback from district surveillance unit. Intervention: refresher training for technicians plus weekly feedback mechanism. Results: achieved 100% sensitivity within 5 weeks across selected PHCs, demonstrating feasibility of QI methods for addressing setting-specific reporting barriers.

articles.headline: District Kanker malaria burden declining with increasing P. falciparum dominance
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria Cases Reported from Government Health Facilities in District Kanker, Chhattisgarh, January 2018 - December 2022.
articles.details: Analysis of surveillance data from District Kanker, Chhattisgarh (January 2018–December 2022). Annual Parasite Index (API) decreased from 3.0 to 1.3 and Annual Falciparum Index (AFI) from 2.5 to 1.1. In 2022, 2 of 7 blocks had API >2 and 1 of 7 had AFI >2. Proportion of P. falciparum cases increased to >90% (2020–2021). Test Positivity Rate declined by half (2018–2022) while Annual Blood Examination Rate remained approximately 30%. Authors note need to investigate reasons for persistent higher API in two blocks.

articles.headline: Malaria resurgence in Koraput district despite prior control gains
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria in Koraput District, Odisha, 2019-2023: A Descriptive Analysis of National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme Surveillance Data.
articles.details: Descriptive analysis of 2019-2023 National Vector Borne Disease Control Programme surveillance data from Koraput district, Odisha. Overall annual parasite incidence was 2.7 per 1000, rising sharply to 4.5 per 1000 in 2023. Narayanpatna had highest API of 38 per 1000, Bandhugaon 25 per 1000. Male API consistently higher than female. Authors recommend pre-emptive measures before monsoons.

articles.headline: Malaria surveillance system shows low sensitivity in Chhattisgarh state
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Malaria Surveillance System in Government Health Facilities in Six Districts of State Chhattisgarh, India, 2023.
articles.details: Cross-sectional evaluation of surveillance in six Chhattisgarh districts (18% of India's malaria burden) using CDC guidelines across 1276 syndromic, 110 presumptive, and 100 laboratory reporting units. Of 1442 presumptive forms, only 84 (5.8%) captured cases; of 11,510 laboratory forms, 3334 (29%) captured cases. Data quality variable: 52.4% and 72% had valid key entries respectively. System useful for trend monitoring but requires major sensitivity and quality improvements.

articles.headline: First congenital Plasmodium knowlesi case in Bahrain with CMV co-infection
articles.title_original: Co-infection of Neonatal Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria With Congenital Cytomegalovirus: The First Reported Case From Bahrain.
articles.details: Case report of 26-day-old infant with congenital P. knowlesi malaria and cytomegalovirus co-infection presenting with fever, jaundice, lethargy, and hepatosplenomegaly. Rapid malaria antigen test negative but blood smear positive for P. knowlesi; mother negative for malaria. Treated with IV artesunate and ganciclovir/valganciclovir. Case highlights importance of blood smear even when rapid tests negative and need for clinician awareness of imported infections.

articles.headline: NETosis as potential therapeutic target in severe malaria
articles.title_original: [Malaria: Could NETosis, neutrophils' defense mechanism involved in severe cases of the disease, be a new therapeutic target?].
articles.authors: Kociánová L, Larribau S, Veltz N
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title questioning whether NETosis (neutrophil extracellular trap formation), a neutrophil defense mechanism, could be a therapeutic target in severe malaria cases.

articles.headline: High genetic diversity in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax merozoite surface protein
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphisms of merozoite surface protein-1 ICB 5-6 in Vietnamese Plasmodium vivax isolates.
articles.details: Analysis of pvmsp-1 ICB 5-6 region from 117 Vietnamese P. vivax isolates (2018-2022) across four provinces. Found 139 sequences clustered into 34 distinct amino acid haplotypes with recombinant types predominant. Central Highlands provinces showed high polymorphism; Khanh Hoa less polymorphic. Highly diverse poly-glutamine variants identified. No significant country/region-specific clustering in global comparison, suggesting substantial genetic heterogeneity.

articles.headline: Partial artemisinin resistance with treatment failure in returned traveler
articles.title_original: Treatment Failure and Post-Artesunate Delayed Haemolysis in a Returned Traveller From Uganda With Partially Drug-Resistant Severe Plasmodium falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: Case of man in 40s from Uganda with P. falciparum malaria and hyperparasitemia (26%, ~1.5×10⁶ parasites/μL). Treated with IV artesunate then artemether-lumefantrine but experienced delayed parasite clearance and recurrent parasitemia 3 weeks later. Genotyping revealed A675V pfk13 mutation, reduced lumefantrine susceptibility, and increased ring-stage survival consistent with partial artemisinin resistance.

articles.headline: Gene drives evaluated against natural malaria parasite diversity
articles.title_original: Gene drives tested against real-world malaria diversity.
articles.details: No abstract available; based on title describing evaluation of gene drive technology against real-world malaria parasite populations.

articles.headline: Environmental change drove persistent malaria in 19th-century Ontario
articles.title_original: Ecologies of Risk: Malaria and Settler Landscape Transformation in 19th-Century Ontario.
articles.details: Historical analysis of 2702 malaria-attributed deaths 1831-1900 using civil and cemetery records with regional climate data. Children under 5 accounted for over half of deaths; agricultural workers also disproportionately affected. Generalized additive modeling showed strong nonlinear association between rainfall and malaria mortality (p<0.001); temperature not significant. Deforestation, altered hydrology, and agricultural intensification created conditions for mosquito breeding in settler-transformed landscapes.

articles.headline: Proposed validation framework for microglial endotype in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: CXCL10(high) Microglia in Cerebral Malaria: Toward Translational Validation.
articles.details: Commentary proposing CXCL10high TNFαhigh Ki67+ microglia as candidate neuroimmune endotype in cerebral malaria. Authors caution that translational relevance requires validation through single-nucleus and spatial transcriptomics of human postmortem tissue, paired plasma/CSF biomarkers, and mechanistic assays of microglial antigen presentation. Emphasizes need to establish human signatures and clinical correlations before proposing precision-targeted interventions.

articles.headline: Public sentiment on RTS,S vaccine rollout splits positive-negative in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Assessing RTS, S malaria vaccine rollout perception in Cameroon: Sentiment analysis from X and facebook using hugging face.
articles.details: Social media sentiment analysis of Twitter (X) and Facebook posts regarding RTS,S (Mosquirix) vaccine rollout in Cameroon using Hugging Face Transformer library. Results: 42% negative sentiment, 40% positive, 18% neutral. Authors conclude that targeted communication strategies addressing public concerns, evidence-based messaging on vaccine safety and efficacy, and engagement with community leaders needed to counter misinformation.

articles.headline: R21 vaccine highly protective against intradermal but not intravenous sporozoites
articles.title_original: Malaria vaccine protection against intradermal or venous parasites: a randomized phase 2b human challenge trial.
articles.details: Phase 2b randomized controlled trial in Kenya comparing R21/Matrix-M (n=38), ME-TRAP (n=24), and control (n=18) vaccination with controlled human malaria infection using intradermal (ID) or direct venous injection (DVI) sporozoites. R21: 0 of 12 met treatment endpoint with ID challenge (100% protection), but 5 of 5 met endpoint with DVI sporozoites (0% protection, p<0.0005). Findings indicate vaccine efficacy differs dramatically by inoculation route and assessment methodology must evaluate routes separately.
articles.notable_reason: R21 efficacy dramatically differs by sporozoite inoculation route

articles.headline: Frequent over-treatment with artesunate for non-severe malaria in Spain
articles.title_original: Appropriateness of malaria treatment according to World Health Organization criteria for severe malaria in a non-endemic country.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 64 malaria patients (median 39.5 years, 75% visiting friends/relatives) at Madrid hospital 2015-2023. Severe malaria in 15 patients (23.4%); treatment appropriate to severity in 46 (71.8%). Three severe cases misclassified and received oral treatment; 15 apparently non-severe received parenteral artesunate. Main reason for artesunate overuse was thrombocytopenia (median 33,000/mm³), which is not included in WHO severe malaria criteria.

articles.headline: Genetic screen for P. falciparum gametocyte development genes
articles.title_original: Large-Scale Mutagenesis Screening for Genetic Determinants of Plasmodium falciparum Sexual Development.
articles.details: Describes piggyBac mutagenesis methodology for identifying genes affecting gametocyte production and development in P. falciparum blood-stage forms. Genes categorized as either hypo-producers (reduced gametocyte production) or hyper-producers (increased production), enabling identification of genetic factors driving gametocyte conversion and growth relevant for transmission-blocking vaccine and drug development.

articles.headline: ELISA predicts Pfs230D1 vaccine efficacy in clinical trials
articles.title_original: Epitope-specific competitive ELISA predicts malaria transmission-blocking vaccine Pfs230D1 activity measured in standard membrane feeding assay.
articles.details: Developed epitope-specific competitive ELISA (P230Compete) using 171 serum samples from phase I Pfs230D1-EPA/AS01 vaccine trial. EUF and IgG1F antibody features showed strong correlation with transmission-reducing activity (AUC 0.81 and 0.80 after dose 3; 0.76-0.80 after dose 4). Combined EUF and IgG1F testing showed even better predictive performance, offering promising replacement for membrane feeding assay in late-stage trials.

articles.headline: P. yoelii immune models reveal severity driven by response timing
articles.title_original: Immune Modulation by Plasmodium yoelii: Insights From Lethal and Non-Lethal Strains.
articles.details: Review synthesizing P. yoelii lethal (17XL) vs non-lethal (17XNL) strain findings. 17XL infections trigger excessive pro-inflammatory cytokine release and immune exhaustion; 17XNL sustain regulated Th1/Th2 responses enabling parasite control and survival. Emerging pathways involve MIF, TLR7 signaling, and immune checkpoints (PD-1, LAG-3). Demonstrates malaria severity reflects timing and resolution of immune responses, not parasite load alone.

articles.headline: Vaccine availability limited by manufacturing, funding, trust barriers in Africa
articles.title_original: Challenges to malaria vaccine availability and successful implementation of malaria vaccine program in Africa.
articles.authors: Ojo RJ, Igbayilola YD, Gyebi GA
articles.details: Review of obstacles to malaria vaccine implementation in Africa. Supply constraints are major factor with insufficient manufacturing capacity to meet demand. Additional barriers include limited national health budgets, vaccine hesitancy from misinformation and past distrust of Western health initiatives, cold chain requirements, multiple-dose schedules, shortage of skilled workers, and weak pharmacovigilance systems. Recommends scaling manufacturing, enhancing vaccine effectiveness, stable funding, and strengthening community trust.

articles.headline: Malaria in Niger young adults: 11% infection rate, 2000 workdays lost
articles.title_original: Burden of malaria in young adults in Niger: A prospective study in a military setting.
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 2840 military recruits (median age 27, 91.5% male) monitored in Niger Sept-Dec 2023 in three training centers. Among 353 confirmed malaria cases (11% prevalence), 74.5% had anemia, 44% were hospitalized, and 55.8% were temporarily unfit for duty. Study estimated approximately 2000 workdays lost annually in this cohort, suggesting ~12 million days nationally.

articles.headline: Malaria reduces trace elements; zinc supplementation shows limited benefit
articles.title_original: Essential Trace Elements and Their Impact on Immune Response and Disease Severity in Malaria Infection: A Systematic Review.
articles.authors: Hanifian H, Nateghpour M, Naderi M
articles.details: Systematic review (Jan 2010-Nov 2025) of trace element changes during malaria infection across human and experimental studies. Malaria consistently associated with reduced serum iron, zinc, and magnesium; copper showed variable trends (often elevated in acute infection). These alterations linked to dysregulated cytokine production (increased TNF-α and IL-10), higher parasitemia, and worse clinical outcomes. Zinc supplementation improved micronutrient status but showed limited impact on malaria incidence.

articles.headline: First artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum cases detected in Italy
articles.title_original: Recrudescence of Plasmodium falciparum malaria imported from Sub-Saharan Africa: a report of two cases and the first detection of artemisinin resistance in Italy.
articles.details: Two Italian travelers with recurrent P. falciparum malaria imported from sub-Saharan Africa showed recrudescence after standard artesunate and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine treatment. Molecular characterization revealed one case carried R561H mutation (validated artemisinin partial resistance marker). Second case lacked Pfkelch13 mutations; recrudescence possibly due to low nasogastric tube drug concentration or Kelch13-independent resistance pathways. Highlights need for continuous surveillance and potential treatment protocol adaptation.

articles.headline: We have the means to beat malaria, do we have the will?
articles.title_original: We have the means to beat malaria, do we have the will?

articles.headline: Low-density P. falciparum infections evade HRP2 RDTs in Dominican Republic
articles.title_original: Molecular Detection of Plasmodium falciparum Infections Missed by Microscopy and HRP2-Based RDTs in the Dominican Republic.
articles.details: Study re-examined 1095 malaria-suspected cases initially negative by HRP2-RDT and microscopy using PCR from RDT cassettes in Dominican Republic pursuing malaria elimination. Nucleic acid extraction successful in 969/1095 (88.5%) samples; P. falciparum DNA detected in 18/969 (1.9%). Of PCR-positive samples, 16.7% (3/18) carried pfhrp2 deletions that compromise HRP2-RDT performance, underscoring need for improved diagnostics in low-transmission elimination settings.

articles.headline: Plasmodium nanoparticle vaccine achieves complete sterile protection in mice
articles.title_original: A Plasmodium-derived nanoparticle vaccine elicits sterile protection against malaria in mice.
articles.details: Developed engineered nanoparticle vaccine using P. falciparum pyridoxal 5'-phosphate synthase presenting P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein (CSP) and P. vivax cell-transversal protein for ookinetes/sporozoites (CelTOS) antigens. Three vaccine doses elicited high anti-CSP and anti-CelTOS antibody titers and provided complete sterile protection against malaria in mouse model. Cryo-EM resolved 2.95-Å nanoparticle structure. PLP synthase lacks human ortholog, limiting autoimmunity and pre-existing immunity risks.

articles.headline: One-step artesunate faster and cheaper than conventional two-step formulation
articles.title_original: Single-step versus conventional injectable artesunate for severe malaria in children: an open label, non-inferiority randomized clinical trial, Democratic Republic of the Congo and United Republic of Tanzania.
articles.details: Open-label randomized non-inferiority trial with 200 children (ages 3 months-16 years) in DRC and Tanzania comparing one-step vs conventional two-step injectable artesunate for severe malaria. One-step formulation significantly faster to administer (2 min 22 s vs 3 min 41 s; p<0.0001) and cheaper (US$0.53 vs US$0.84 per patient; p=0.0001). Parasite clearance half-lives equivalent (2.1 vs 2.0 hours; p=0.173) with similar safety (1 severe AE per arm).

articles.headline: Chloroquine-resistant P. vivax prevalence 7.3% in Ethiopia; clinically significant
articles.title_original: Chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium vivax malaria in Ethiopia: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review of 13 studies (n=2190) using PRISMA guidelines found pooled prevalence of chloroquine-resistant P. vivax in Ethiopia of 7.32% (95% CI: 2.55-12.08; I² = 95.1%). Higher prevalence in studies using microscopy and PCR (7.32%), during 2003-2009 period (10.70%), and in Oromia region (9.58%). At 42-day follow-up, prevalence increased to 17.91% (95% CI: -9.34-45.15). Authors note moderate but clinically significant resistance warranting surveillance and alternative therapeutics.

articles.headline: P. vivax circumsporozoite protein vaccine strategies reviewed
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein and vaccine strategies in murine models: A scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review of vaccine strategies targeting P. vivax circumsporozoite protein in murine models.

articles.headline: Monoclonal antibodies show 75-88% malaria protection for 6 months
articles.title_original: Efficacy and safety of Monoclonal Antibodies in Malaria Protection and Prevention of Transmission: A Systematic Review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 7 Phase I-II trials (776 participants) of antimalarial mAbs. CIS43LS achieved 75-88% protective efficacy against P. falciparum at ≥10 mg/kg IV with 56-80 day half-life; L9LS showed 66-77% efficacy subcutaneously (46.1 day half-life); TB31F demonstrated >80% transmission-reducing activity sustained up to 160 days. All exhibited favorable safety profiles with only grade 1-2 adverse events, supporting feasibility for seasonal public health deployment.

articles.headline: Falcilysin identified as druggable P. falciparum drug target
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum falcilysin as an emerging potential drug target for antimalarial drug discovery.
articles.details: Review of falcilysin (FLN), a conserved zinc metalloprotease essential for P. falciparum survival with roles in hemoglobin degradation and apicoplast transit peptide processing. Multiple small-molecule classes have demonstrated potent FLN inhibition in biochemical, cellular, and in vivo models. Key challenges include dual-organelle localization and selectivity over human homologs; future efforts should expand chemical diversity and explore rational drug design approaches.

articles.headline: Gene-drive mosquitoes suppress diverse patient-derived malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Gene-drive-capable mosquitoes suppress patient-derived malaria in Tanzania.
articles.details: Researchers engineered transgenic Anopheles gambiae expressing antimicrobial peptides with non-autonomous gene drive capabilities. The strain robustly suppressed genetically diverse P. falciparum isolates obtained from naturally infected Tanzanian children, with genetic modifications efficiently inherited by progeny when supplemented with Cas9 from a second engineered strain. This demonstrates gene drive technology's first practical effectiveness against circulating parasites, advancing potential for real-world malaria eradication.
articles.notable_reason: First demonstration of gene drive against patient-derived diverse parasites

articles.headline: DARC genetic diversity and P. vivax emergence in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Genetic dynamics of the Duffy antigen receptor for chemokines gene and Plasmodium vivax circulation within sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Analysis of 223 DARC genes from sub-Saharan Africa revealed minimal genetic diversity overall, with evidence of selection potentially attributed to emerging P. vivax infections. South Africa showed the greatest haplotype and nucleotide diversity with distinct genetic lineage from other regions; Cameroon, Nigeria, and Ethiopia clustered together. Detection of P. vivax among Duffy-null individuals raises public health concerns for the region.

articles.headline: P. cynomolgi DBP binding activity varies between haplotypes
articles.title_original: Characterisation of erythrocyte binding activity of Plasmodium cynomolgi duffy binding protein haplotypes from Macaca fascicularis in Peninsular Malaysia.
articles.details: In vitro study of Plasmodium cynomolgi duffy binding proteins (PcDBP1 and PcDBP2) from macaque-derived isolates revealed significant differences (P<0.05) in PcDBP1(II) binding activity between recent and historical isolates to macaque erythrocytes, with recent isolates showing enhanced binding activity. Neither DBP showed binding to human erythrocytes or reticulocytes, suggesting additional parasite-host interactions are required for human cell invasion.

articles.headline: PCR detects significantly more malaria cases than microscopy
articles.title_original: Performance comparison of rapid diagnostic test, microscopy, and polymerase chain reaction for Malaria detection in high-transmission area, Keerom Regency, Papua Province, Indonesia.
articles.details: Comparative diagnostic study of 234 suspected uncomplicated malaria cases in Keerom Regency, Papua, Indonesia found PCR identified 42.3% of cases, while microscopy and RDTs detected only 26.5% and 22.6% respectively. PCR revealed substantial cases missed by conventional methods, particularly in low-parasitemia situations, demonstrating significant diagnostic limitations of microscopy and RDTs.

articles.headline: Climate warming increases avian malaria parasite prevalence
articles.title_original: Hidden effects of avian malaria under climate warming.
articles.details: Long-term study of wild bird population in temperate Northern Europe revealed significant increase in avian malaria parasite prevalence under climate-warming scenario, illustrating potential threats to global ecosystems from temperature-humidity disruption.

articles.headline: NAT screening detects asymptomatic malaria in blood donors
articles.title_original: Improving blood safety: NAT-based detection of Plasmodium spp. in blood donors in endemic areas of Brazil.
articles.authors: Gomes de Almeida AC, et al.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 67,114 blood donors screened with NAT PLUS assay in Brazilian Amazon found 0.01% (8/67,114) Plasmodium spp. positivity; all positive donors were asymptomatic at screening. One donor (12.5%) subsequently developed symptomatic P. falciparum confirmed by microscopy. Malaria-related deferrals accounted for 2.6% of all deferrals, highlighting need for molecular testing protocols to identify at-risk donors.

articles.headline: Hypofuran B analogs show promise as antimalarial leads
articles.title_original: Synthesis and In Vitro Activity of Hypofuran B and Analogs Against Plasmodium Falciparum and Trypanosoma Cruzi.
articles.details: Chemical synthesis of hypofuran B and related analogs via crossed aldol condensation yielded compounds with IC50 values of 5.35-10.35 µg/mL against P. falciparum and favorable cytotoxicity profiles (CC50 >400 µg/mL), lower than chloroquine. Structure-activity relationships were consistent across the analog series, with in silico predictions supporting favorable drug-like profiles.

articles.headline: Malaria PCR screening prevents transmissions in cell transplant
articles.title_original: Malaria Screening and Treatment in Hematopoietic Cell Transplant Donors and Sickle Cell Disease Candidates/Recipients: A Case Series Using Malaria Polymerase Chain Reaction Testing and a Literature Review.
articles.details: Retrospective review of malaria screening in 57 hematopoietic stem cell transplant (HCT) donors and 52 recipients identified 3 asymptomatic donor infections and 2 recipient malaria cases; one donor with negative smear but positive PCR transmitted malaria to recipient. Malaria PCR offers greater diagnostic sensitivity than conventional methods. Literature review of 10 detailed HCT malaria cases supports routine PCR screening for exposed donors.

articles.headline: Bone marrow microscopy diagnoses malaria missed by blood tests
articles.title_original: Out of Sight, Should not be out of mind: A perplexing case of Malaria.
articles.details: Case report of 30-year-old male with fever, splenomegaly, and cytopenias in whom repeated peripheral blood smears and RDTs were negative, but bone marrow examination and PCR identified Plasmodium spp. Patient completely recovered with artemether-lumefantrine and primaquine, illustrating value of bone marrow evaluation in diagnosing malaria with low parasitemia.

articles.headline: IPTp-SP may reduce small vulnerable newborns beyond malaria
articles.title_original: Beyond malaria: can intermittent preventive treatment with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine reduce the number of small vulnerable newborns globally?
articles.details: Commentary examining whether intermittent preventive treatment with sulphadoxine-pyrimethamine (IPTp-SP), originally designed to protect pregnant women from malaria, could reduce small vulnerable newborns through antimicrobial activity against genitourinary infections, effects on maternal gut physiology, or anti-inflammatory actions. Proposed mechanisms remain speculative; authors call for mechanistic studies and placebo-controlled randomized trials in non-malarious settings.

articles.headline: Corrigendum: R21/MM vaccine and alpha-gal immune responses
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing with R21/MM" [Vaccine 68 (2025) 127897].
articles.details: Published corrigendum to prior study on naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing with R21/MM vaccine.

articles.headline: Mosquito feeding preferences and avian malaria in Balkans
articles.title_original: Feeding Preferences of Mosquitoes (Culicidae) from the Eastern Balkans and Their Role in Transmission of Avian Malaria.
articles.details: Two-year survey (2017, 2019) of engorged mosquitoes from deciduous forest and reedbeds in Eastern Balkans identified blood meal origin and avian haemosporidian parasites. Among 75 mosquitoes: 58.7% fed on birds (25 species), 40.0% on mammals (13 species), 1.3% on reptile. Avian haemosporidian parasites detected in 11.6% of abdominal and 4.2% of head-thorax samples, with P. relictum SGS1 most prevalent.

articles.headline: Deforestation and anopheline ecology in Amazon malaria
articles.title_original: Deforestation, Mosquito Ecology, and Malaria Elimination in the Amazon Region.
articles.authors: Grillet ME, Sallum MAM, Conn JE
articles.details: Review examining how Amazon forest degradation affects anopheline vector ecology and malaria transmission. Discusses how mosquito population dynamics (abundance, human-vector contact rates) mediate relationship between environmental change and malaria risk, and addresses increasingly important role of zoonotic malaria in the region.

articles.headline: RTS,S/AS01E vaccine effectiveness sustained in real-world settings
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine in a real-world setting over 1 year of follow-up after the three-dose primary schedule: an interim analysis of a phase 4 study in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi.
articles.details: Phase 4 study of 39,463 children in Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi over 1 year following 3-dose RTS,S/AS01E vaccination. Country-adjusted incidence rate ratios for vaccinated vs. unvaccinated: any malaria 0.70 (95% CI 0.67-0.73, p<0.001), severe malaria 0.42 (0.30-0.60, p<0.001), malaria-related hospitalization 0.64 (0.56-0.72, p<0.001), all-cause hospitalization 0.79 (0.74-0.84, p<0.001), and anaemia prevalence 0.81 (0.73-0.90, p<0.001). All-cause mortality reduction was non-significant (0.83, p=0.18).
articles.notable_reason: Large phase-4 real-world effectiveness confirms RTS,S vaccine impact

articles.headline: Serological surveillance for India's malaria elimination program
articles.title_original: India should consider sero-epidemiology as an additional tool to bolster malaria elimination.
articles.details: Review proposing integration of serology into India's malaria elimination strategy. India accounts for >50% of Southeast Asia's malaria burden (P. falciparum, P. vivax). Malaria cases increased from 161,753 (2021) to 255,500 (2024), representing ~58% increase since 2021. Serology could detect past exposure, low-density/dormant infections, and guide targeted interventions.

articles.headline: Traditional plant extract shows osteoprotective effects in malaria
articles.title_original: Osteoprotective Effects and In Silico Profiling of Parinari curatellifolia Planch. Ex Benth. (Chrysobalanaceae) Extract on Acute Plasmodium berghei-Compromised Rat Bone.
articles.details: Preclinical study of Parinari curatellifolia aqueous leaf and bark extract on malaria-induced bone alterations using Plasmodium berghei ANKA-infected rats. Extract demonstrated in vitro antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum 3D7 and Dd2 strains and in vivo reduced parasitemia (p<0.001) while increasing bone osteocalcin, TGF-β1, and ALP activity (p<0.001) and reversing malaria-induced inflammation. LC-MS identified 12 compounds.

articles.headline: Malaria epidemiology and co-infections in Delhi hospital
articles.title_original: Malaria epidemiology, clinical characteristics, and concurrent infections in Delhi, India: A hospital-based study.
articles.details: Hospital-based study (July 2022–November 2023) screened 4,259 febrile patients in Delhi, identifying 87 (2.04%) with P. vivax or P. falciparum malaria. Predominant clinical features: chills (80.46%), hepatosplenomegaly (64.37%), myalgia (56.32%), and jaundice (51.72%). Approximately 45% of malaria cases co-infected with dengue, chikungunya, or Salmonella typhus. July–September season and male sex were significant risk factors (aOR 2.18, 1.42; p<0.05).

articles.headline: Artemisinin resistance markers detected in Ghana despite maintained efficacy
articles.title_original: Molecular Markers of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum From Kumasi, Ghana, 2023.
articles.details: Genotyping of 200 P. falciparum isolates from Kumasi, Ghana (2023) identified 2.2% with non-synonymous pfk13 mutations including P527H (candidate artemisinin partial resistance marker), S522C, C532S, and I590V. Pfcoronin P76S present in 29.7%, pfmdr1 pattern NFD predominant (suggesting impaired lumefantrine sensitivity), and pfaat1 S258L at 64.7%. Artemisinin-based combination treatment efficacy remains high, but continuous molecular monitoring recommended.

articles.headline: Cannabis compounds show activity in malaria preclinical studies
articles.title_original: Cannabis-Derived Compounds Against Plasmodium sp.: A Systematic Review of Preclinical Studies.
articles.authors: de Mendonça Lima T, et al.
articles.details: Systematic review of 8 preclinical studies (2001–2022) on cannabis-derived compounds against Plasmodium sp., predominantly from North America (n=5). In vitro studies showed antimalarial activity with IC50 ranging from 0.16–4.1 μg/mL (mild to high activity). All in vivo studies reported positive effects including moderate antimalarial activity and disease tolerance. Toxicity profiles inadequately characterized; authors call for comprehensive study design and validation.

articles.headline: 4-nitrobenzoate enhances atovaquone efficacy in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: 4-nitrobenzoate inhibits 4-hydroxybenzoate polyprenyltransferase in malaria parasites and enhances atovaquone efficacy.
articles.details: Study identifying Plasmodium falciparum 4-hydroxybenzoate polyprenyltransferase (PfCOQ2) as druggable target for ubiquinone biosynthesis inhibition. 4-nitrobenzoate inhibited Pf4-HPT and blocked UQ biosynthesis in S. cerevisiae model expressing PfCOQ2. In Plasmodium berghei-infected mice, 4-nitrobenzoate improved atovaquone efficacy while preserving proguanil synergy and selectivity. Findings support rational combination therapy approach to overcome atovaquone/proguanil resistance.
articles.notable_reason: Novel drug target and in vivo proof against resistance

articles.headline: GEP1 knockout blocks P. falciparum gametogenesis completely
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum gametogenesis essential protein 1 (GEP1) is a transmission-blocking target.
articles.details: CRISPR-Cas9 study showing PfGEP1 loss-of-function lines completely blocked male and female gametogenesis, even when stimulated by xanthurenic acid or temperature drop. Defect persisted despite phosphodiesterase inhibitor treatment, confirming GEP1's essential role independent of xanthurenic acid signaling. Field analysis of 49 cases identified GEP1 SNPs (V241L in 12%, S263P in 20%), validating GEP1 as transmission-blocking drug target.
articles.notable_reason: Functional validation of transmission-blocking target blocking life-cycle

articles.headline: New Plasmodium Novyella species identified in Brazilian bird
articles.title_original: A New Species of Plasmodium of the Subgenus Novyella Infecting White-Shouldered Fire-Eyes (Pyriglena leucoptera) (Aves: Thamnophilidae) in Brazil.
articles.details: Researchers identified a new Plasmodium species (lineage PYLEU01) infecting the white-shouldered fire-eye (Pyriglena leucoptera) in Brazil. Molecular analysis shows 4.18% genetic divergence from Plasmodium homopolare. The new species is distinguished by morphological characteristics including mature amoeboid trophozoites and specific merozoite counts, differing from related species P. vaughani and P. rouxi.

articles.headline: Volume electron microscopy reveals ookinete developmental ultrastructure
articles.title_original: Cellular Hallmarks From Volume Electron Microscopy Reveal Developmental Progression of Plasmodium Ookinetes.
articles.details: This study applied volume electron microscopy to analyze subcellular changes during Plasmodium zygote-to-ookinete transformation in the mosquito vector. The approach identified intimate mitochondrion-nucleus interactions, distinct microtubule arrangements, elongated micronemes, and close apicoplast interactions. The authors provide an open-access subcellular atlas to support mechanistic molecular insights from reverse genetic studies.

articles.headline: PfSPZ vaccine shows poor efficacy in Gabonese children
articles.title_original: Safety, tolerability, and protective efficacy of a radiation-attenuated, whole sporozoite malaria vaccine in children in Gabon: a randomised, double-blind, placebo-controlled, phase 2 trial.
articles.details: A phase 2 randomized controlled trial evaluated the radiation-attenuated PfSPZ Vaccine in 192 children (aged 1-12 years) in Gabon receiving three vaccinations or placebo by direct venous inoculation. Adverse events occurred in 25% of vaccine and 23% of placebo recipients, with no treatment-related deaths. At 2-26 weeks, 19% of vaccine recipients versus 23% of placebo became P. falciparum-positive by thick blood smear, yielding age-stratum-adjusted vaccine efficacy of 9% (95% CI -75 to 53; p=0.78).

articles.headline: Malaria epidemiology in Meta department, eastern Colombia
articles.title_original: Malaria in the department of Meta, eastern Colombia: 2017-2021.
articles.details: This descriptive study examined malaria cases from 2016-2021 in Meta department, eastern Colombia, where highest incidence occurred in Puerto Gaitán, Villavicencio, and Mapiripán. A significant relationship was found between age category and malaria acquisition. Although Meta is not officially designated as malaria-endemic, proximity to rivers enhanced mosquito vector contact in several municipalities.

articles.headline: South-South collaboration opportunities for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Can India-Africa synergies accelerate malaria elimination? A perspective on South-South Collaboration.
articles.details: This perspective examines the potential of South-South collaboration among low- and middle-income countries to advance malaria control, drawing from initiatives including ANDI, the Elimination 8 group, and the India-Africa Health Sciences Platform. Partnerships can foster innovation, strengthen local research capacity, improve diagnostic tools, and enhance regional surveillance systems. Barriers include limited funding and fragmented systems; proposed strategies include regional investment and regulatory harmonization.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine efficacy lower than other ACTs in Uganda
articles.title_original: Efficacies of artemether-lumefantrine, artesunate-amodiaquine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in children aged 6 months to 10 years in Uganda: a randomised, open-label, phase 4 clinical trial.
articles.details: This phase 4 trial evaluated four artemisinin-based combination therapies in 793 Ugandan children (6 months-10 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria across three districts. Uncorrected adequate clinical and parasitological response for artemether-lumefantrine was substantially lower (51.8-79.4%) versus dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (98%), artesunate-amodiaquine (99%), and artesunate-pyronaridine (73.7%). PCR-corrected 28-day efficacies for artemether-lumefantrine ranged 81.5-97%, while other regimens achieved 92.4-100%. Parasites carried PfK13 mutations (Cys469Tyr, Ala675Val) with prolonged parasite clearance half-lives.

articles.headline: Diversifying antimalarial treatment in Uganda
articles.title_original: Diversifying antimalarial treatment for uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Uganda.
articles.details: No abstract provided. Based on title, this appears to discuss strategies for using multiple antimalarial treatments for uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in Uganda.

articles.headline: Nuclear medicine imaging applications and limitations in malaria
articles.title_original: Mini-review and situation report on the role and usefulness of nuclear medicine imaging for malaria.
articles.journal: Eur J Nucl Med Mol Imaging
articles.details: This mini-review examines clinical nuclear imaging techniques for malaria diagnosis and understanding severe malaria pathogenesis. Although radiopharmaceuticals have provided insights into severe malaria—a multisystem disease affecting organs and causing serious neurological complications—development of malaria-specific radiopharmaceuticals has stagnated. The authors highlight current implementation limitations and provide recommendations for advancing nuclear imaging in malaria.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria prevalence comparable to symptomatic in Nigeria
articles.title_original: A cross-sectional survey of malaria in asymptomatic and symptomatic individuals in an endemic area of Northcentral Nigeria.
articles.authors: Akoma ON, Okoh FN, Amoke OC
articles.details: A cross-sectional survey of 328 participants in Lokoja, Northcentral Nigeria during high transmission season (April-October, 2018-2021) found 12.4% (25/202) asymptomatic and 85.7% (108/126) symptomatic individuals with P. falciparum parasitemia by light microscopy. Overall prevalence was 40.5% (133/328). No significant difference (p>0.05) in malaria distribution between asymptomatic and symptomatic groups. Authors recommend renewed monitoring and control strategies targeting the asymptomatic reservoir.

articles.headline: Global malaria burden assessment projects continued rise through 2030
articles.title_original: The past, present and future of global malaria and neglected tropical diseases: a disease burden assessment from 1990 to 2030.
articles.details: This study applied joinpoint regression, Spearman correlation, and ARIMA modeling to Global Burden of Disease 2019 data assessing malaria and neglected tropical disease trends 1990-2030. Western Sub-Saharan Africa accounted for one-half of global cases in 2019. Age-standardized incidence rates in high sociodemographic index and high-income regions showed upward trends 1990-2019, with dengue as the highest NTD burden. Projections indicate slow upward trends in age-standardized mortality through 2030.

articles.headline: Quadruple dhfr/dhps mutations rising in Chad SMC areas
articles.title_original: Trends in Plasmodium falciparum resistance markers to sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine over ten years of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Moissala Health District, Chad.
articles.details: A 10-year surveillance study examined P. falciparum resistance markers in Moissala Health District, Chad, where seasonal malaria chemoprevention with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine has been implemented since 2013. In children with clinical malaria (136 in 2014, 256 in 2021, 219 in 2023), triple dhfr mutants decreased from 100% to 83.9%, while quadruple mutants (triple dhfr + dhps A437G) increased from 28% to 47.9% (p<0.001). Quintuple mutants remained low. Authors conclude SP and AQ remain effective but recommend surveillance for emerging quintuple mutants.

articles.headline: VAR2CSA antibodies associated with reduced adverse pregnancy outcomes
articles.title_original: VAR2CSA-specific IgG and IgM antibodies are markers of exposure and protection against adverse malaria pregnancy outcomes.
articles.details: This study followed pregnant Malawian women (enrolled at 16-28 weeks gestation) with malaria infection during pregnancy, measuring IgG and IgM antibodies against VAR2CSA DBL1X-ID2a domain at enrollment and delivery. In women uninfected at enrollment, delivery IgG was associated with reduced low birth weight (aOR 0.43; 95% CI 0.19-0.97; p=0.04). In infected women, each log-unit increase in enrollment IgM associated with 23% decrease in maternal anemia (aOR 0.77; 95% CI 0.60-0.99; p=0.04). Authors conclude VAR2CSA-specific antibodies are markers of placental malaria protection.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria prevalence 33% in Nigeria, highest in children
articles.title_original: Prevalence and associated risk factors of asymptomatic malaria in Nigeria: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 25 studies in Nigeria found pooled asymptomatic malaria prevalence of 33% (95% CI 26-41), ranging from 25% in general population to 52% in children. Risk factors included education level (19%), sex (36%), and insecticide-treated net usage (39%). Asymptomatic infections sustain transmission, necessitating transmission-reduction tools.

articles.headline: OpIE2-DsRed fluorescent marker disrupts female mosquito blood-feeding
articles.title_original: An OpIE2-DsRed marker disrupts female blood-feeding and shortens lifespan in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: Transgenic Anopheles gambiae females carrying OpIE2-DsRed marker exhibited female-specific sterility due to inability to blood-feed, caused by distally curved proboscis affecting both sexes. OpIE2b variant with different flanking sequences drove marker expression without functional impairment. Findings emphasize need for rigorous validation of transgenic components in research and applied releases.

articles.headline: Livelihoods and practices shape malaria exposure in Uganda
articles.title_original: Livelihoods as a key social determinant of malaria: Qualitative evidence from Uganda.
articles.authors: Deane K, Atusingwize E, Musoke D
articles.details: Qualitative study (14 key informant interviews, 10 focus groups, 11 household interviews) in Uganda identified livelihoods as critical social determinants: crop production, livestock husbandry, construction, and dusk/night activities increased mosquito exposure and breeding sites. Gendered care responsibilities also increased children's exposure. Findings support integrating malaria prevention into socio-economic development strategies.

articles.headline: Mathematical model analyzes malaria-COVID-19 co-infection dynamics
articles.title_original: Optimal control analysis of a mathematical model of malaria and COVID-19 co-infection dynamics.
articles.authors: Diabaté AB, Sangaré B, Koutou O
articles.details: Theoretical analysis of deterministic malaria and COVID-19 co-infection model examined basic reproduction numbers, backward bifurcation, competitive exclusion, and co-existence conditions. Applied Pontryagin's maximum principle for optimal control strategies. Numerical simulations supported theoretical findings regarding disease dynamics and control strategies.

articles.headline: Parasitemia, not blood type, associated with malaria liver injury
articles.title_original: The Association between Parasitemia and Liver Enzyme Alterations in Malaria Patients: ABO Blood Group as A Non-Contributory Factor.
articles.details: Case-control study (50 malaria patients, 50 controls) at Tehran University found malaria patients had significantly higher liver enzymes—AST, ALT, ALP (p=0.001)—compared to controls. ABO blood group showed no association with liver enzyme levels (p>0.05). Higher parasitemia significantly correlated with greater hepatic dysfunction (p<0.001), suggesting liver enzyme profiles as biomarkers for severity.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention feasible in Turkana, Kenya
articles.title_original: A Baseline Mixed-Methods Study to Inform Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention Delivery in a Semi-Nomadic Population in Turkana, Kenya.
articles.details: Baseline mixed-methods study (198 households, children 6 months to 5 years) in Turkana Central Subcounty found P. falciparum prevalence of 21% by RDT and PCR (range 9-73%). Identified barriers: physical access, stigma, acceptability concerns. Recommends tailored SMC combining centralized and door-to-door delivery, leveraging pastoralist and religious leadership structures for implementation success.

articles.headline: Vaccination registries essential for evaluating malaria vaccines
articles.title_original: Need for Real-World Evaluation of Malaria Vaccines and Reliable Vaccination Records in Africa.
articles.details: Commentary on real-world effectiveness evaluation of RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccines in underrepresented populations. Observational study designs including cohort and test-negative approaches require accurate individual-level vaccination data. Recommends strengthening electronic immunization registries and improving paper-based records across African countries for vaccine monitoring and policy decision-making.

articles.headline: Plant-based production of anti-malaria monoclonal antibodies feasible
articles.title_original: Plant-produced monoclonal antibodies recognize the native circumsporozoite protein on Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites.
articles.details: MS-1797 monoclonal antibodies against PfCSP NANP repeat region produced in glycoengineered Nicotiana benthamiana yielded 457.3 µg/g (MS-01) and 415.7 µg/g (MS-02) fresh weight at 6 days. Both variants achieved >90% purity, retained antigen specificity, and bound native PfCSP on sporozoites by immunofluorescence. Demonstrates feasibility of affordable mAb production for research and prophylactic development.

articles.headline: School-age children highest-risk group in Ghanaian malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Malaria test positivity, Plasmodium species distribution, and risk factors in Ho Municipality, Ghana: a retrospective analysis of seasonal and demographic trends (2020-2022).
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 27,171 malaria tests from Ho Municipality (2020-2022) found overall test positivity 8.8%, declining from 9.9% to 7.2%, with seasonal peak in August (13.9%). School-age children (5-12 years) had highest TPR at 18.0% and higher parasite densities (aGMR=3.69). P. falciparum dominated (79.9%). Facility disparities ranged from 5.2% to 21.0% TPR.

articles.headline: Young primigravida women face highest malaria risk in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Maternal and household risk factors for malaria in pregnancy and low birthweight: a prospective cohort study from Uganda.
articles.details: Prospective cohort (2,757 pregnant women, Busia District, Uganda, 2020-2024) found 38.1% had parasitemia at enrollment, declining to 6.5% after IPTp initiation. Young primigravida women (16-21 years, RR=1.62 at enrollment) and those in traditional housing (RR=1.17) had highest parasitemia risk. Primigravida strongly associated with high-grade placental malaria (RR=4.20) and low birth weight (RR=2.14).

articles.headline: Temperature and terrain predict malaria spatial distribution in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Spatial analysis of malaria incidence and environmental determinants in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.details: Geostatistical analysis in Hadiya Zone woredas (2022-2023) identified significant spatial clustering (Moran's I: 0.558-0.483, p<0.01) with hot spots in Shashogo, Soro, Misrak Badawacho and cold spots in Misha, Duna, Gombora. Spatial autoregressive model identified average maximum temperature (β=0.945, p=0.017) and highland terrain proportion (β=0.543, p=0.040) as key environmental predictors.

articles.headline: Arts-based community engagement increases Sterile Insect Technique acceptance
articles.title_original: From song to stories: assessing the impact of exposure to arts-based community engagement tools in shaping knowledge, attitudes, and acceptability toward the Sterile Insect Technique for malaria control in South Africa.
articles.authors: Manana PN, Jewett S, Munhenga G
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey (614 participants, South Africa) assessed arts-based community engagement (music, drama, radio) impact on Sterile Insect Technique knowledge and acceptance. Among 26.2% exposed to arts-based CE, exposure significantly improved correct identification of female mosquito blood-feeding (95.0% vs 85.8%, p=0.008) and support for SIT release (98.1% vs 89.4%, p=0.003).

articles.headline: XN-31 analyzer shows 98.4% sensitivity for P. vivax diagnosis in Thailand
articles.title_original: Performance evaluation of the automated haematology analyzer XN-31 for malaria diagnosis in Plasmodium vivax-dominant regions of Thailand.
articles.details: This was a 349-patient study conducted 2019-2022 at a Thai tropical medicine hospital evaluating the XN-31 automated analyzer for malaria diagnosis in a region where P. vivax predominates. Compared to thick film microscopy as reference, XN-31 achieved 98.4% sensitivity (vs 88% for RDTs), correctly identified parasite species in 100% of samples, and showed strong quantitative correlation (r=0.903) with microscopy for parasitemia levels. This is the first evaluation confirming XN-31's diagnostic accuracy in a P. vivax-dominant setting.

articles.headline: P. vivax in Duffy-negative Sudanese shows genome conservation, limited diversity
articles.title_original: Highly conserved Plasmodium vivax genomes in Duffy-negative individuals from Sudan.
articles.details: This genomic study from Sudan compared P. vivax infections in Duffy-positive versus Duffy-negative individuals to understand mechanisms of P. vivax infection in people lacking the Duffy antigen. Duffy-positive individuals had significantly higher parasitemia and genetically more diverse parasites across all 14 chromosomes, with differences especially in 44 erythrocyte binding gene candidates. In contrast, Duffy-negative infections contained highly conserved genomes with fewer variants, and several binding genes showed evidence of selection pressure with amino acid changes affecting binding affinity.

articles.headline: Correction to machine learning-based phenotypic imaging study
articles.title_original: Correction: Machine learning-based phenotypic imaging to characterise the targetable biology of Plasmodium falciparum male gametocytes for the development of transmission-blocking antimalarials.
articles.details: This is a correction to a previously published article (DOI: 10.1371/journal.ppat.1011711) regarding machine learning phenotypic imaging of P. falciparum male gametocytes for transmission-blocking antimalarial development.

articles.headline: SLI system enables functional PfEMP1 studies and novel virulence factor research
articles.title_original: A system for functional studies of the major virulence factor of malaria parasites.
articles.details: This study developed a selection-linked integration (SLI) system to standardize PfEMP1 expression in P. falciparum parasites, overcoming previous technical challenges with expression heterogeneity that prevented effective genetic modification. The researchers adapted the system from reference strain 3D7 to IT4, generated parasites uniformly expressing the same PfEMP1 variant with functional receptor binding capacity, and introduced an enhanced SLI2 version. Using these tools, they characterized PfEMP1 trafficking, generated cell lines with common endothelial receptor binding, and identified novel proteins required for PfEMP1-mediated sequestration.

articles.headline: Drug-induced hepatitis following antimalarial treatment in P. falciparum patient
articles.title_original: Deciphering Hepatic Dilemmas: A Case of Acute Hepatitis Following Malaria Treatment.
articles.details: A 55-year-old woman with P. falciparum malaria treated in Cameroon developed jaundice and elevated transaminases with liver biopsy showing cholestatic hepatitis. Despite only one of four follow-up blood smears detecting P. falciparum, her liver function abnormalities persisted. After more than 17 months of follow-up without additional antimalarial treatment, her liver enzymes and bilirubin normalized, with findings attributed to drug-induced liver injury from her initial antimalarial medications.

articles.headline: dbPCR-NALFIA achieves 96.5% sensitivity, 98% specificity for P. falciparum diagnosis
articles.title_original: Field evaluation of the miniature direct-on-blood PCR nucleic acid lateral flow immunoassay (mini-dbPCR-NALFIA) for the detection of Plasmodium falciparum in high seasonal malaria transmission setting in Burkina Faso.
articles.details: This phase 3 field evaluation in Burkina Faso enrolled 438 febrile patients in a high transmission setting to assess the novel mini-dbPCR-NALFIA diagnostic test using qPCR (targeting varATS gene) as reference. The dbPCR-NALFIA demonstrated 96.5% sensitivity and 98.0% specificity, comparable to RDT sensitivity (95.1%) and microscopy specificity (98.7%), but superior to RDT specificity (80.8%) and microscopy sensitivity (90.2%), with highest agreement (kappa=0.90). Importantly, it detected 67.9% of submicroscopic infections missed by microscopy, addressing a key diagnostic limitation in resource-limited endemic settings.

articles.headline: High malaria knowledge but low preventive behavior among Zimbabwe miners
articles.title_original: An assessment of knowledge, practices, and malaria parasitaemia among artisanal gold miners and community members in Mudzi, Zimbabwe.
articles.details: This mixed-methods cross-sectional study of 241 artisanal gold miners and community members in Mudzi, Zimbabwe (277 total tested) revealed high knowledge of malaria transmission but paradoxically low risk perception and limited preventive practices despite health education. Malaria prevalence was <1% overall. The study found no significant differences in knowledge or education between miners and community members, though miners' open-air work lacked protective measures. Findings suggest that knowledge alone is insufficient; interventions addressing livelihood needs alongside malaria prevention are needed to change behavior.

articles.headline: PDGFRA inhibitor GSK190937 blocks hemozoin formation in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Repositioning a Human Kinase Platelet-Derived Growth Factor Receptor Alpha Type II Inhibitor for Malaria and Inhibition of Hemozoin Formation.
articles.details: Drug screening identified GSK190937, a type II PDGFRA inhibitor, as having antiplasmodial activity against asexual P. falciparum (PfNF54 IC50=0.22 μM). Through medicinal chemistry optimization, compounds 20, 23, and 29 were developed with improved metabolic stability and potency. Mechanism of action studies revealed that this series inhibits hemozoin formation, resulting in death of late-stage trophozoites, suggesting potential for novel antimalarial agents targeting this parasite stage.

articles.headline: Coinfection with helminths doesn't alter P. yoelii virulence or adaptation
articles.title_original: Experimental evolution of Plasmodium yoelii in single and helminth-coinfected mice.
articles.details: Using experimental evolution, researchers passaged P. yoelii parasites through either single-infected or helminth-coinfected (H. polygyrus) mice for five to seven passages and compared their replication rates and virulence. Serial passages increased parasitemia and virulence in both groups due to selection for faster-replicating genotypes. Despite different infection environments, single-infected and coinfected lines showed relatively similar replication rates and virulence, with no strong adaptive divergence, possibly reflecting virulence costs in the coinfected setting.

articles.headline: Isfahan malaria: declining cases, P. falciparum linked to severe disease
articles.title_original: Epidemiological and clinical profile of malaria patients in Isfahan Province, Iran: a retrospective analysis from 2009 to 2025.
articles.details: This 16-year retrospective surveillance study of 569 malaria cases in Isfahan Province, Iran documented a sharp initial decline in incidence after 2009, with cases predominantly imported (80.8%) from Afghanistan. P. vivax accounted for 88% of infections, while the less frequent P. falciparum showed strong association with severe disease (aOR 22.6, p=0.035). Cases occurred predominantly in males aged 15-24 years, with seasonal peaks in spring/summer and higher per capita incidence in rural areas despite absolute urban predominance.

articles.headline: Water and sanitation access strongly linked to lower malaria incidence
articles.title_original: Investigating the relationship between malaria incidence and public health infrastructure in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: This analysis of country-level data from sub-Saharan Africa examined associations between malaria incidence and public health infrastructure indicators (clean water/sanitation access and vitamin A supplementation). Using statistical tests and machine learning, researchers found significant differences in malaria incidence across water and sanitation access categories, with consistently lowest incidence in high-access groups and large effect sizes. Vitamin A supplementation showed weaker associations with smaller effect sizes. Tree-based models demonstrated moderate predictive performance, underscoring the importance of basic infrastructure in malaria control.

articles.headline: Insect-specific viruses don't affect P. falciparum sporozoite formation in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Systemic infection with insect-specific viruses does not affect Plasmodium sporozoite formation in Anopheles mosquitoes.
