articles.notable_reason: Identifies mechanistic basis for optimizing vaccine delivery

articles.headline: Monthly prophylaxis superior to screening-treatment for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Evaluating malaria elimination strategies among military forces in Cambodia: a multi-arm clinical trial comparing monthly prophylaxis and focused screening and treatment.
articles.details: Multi-arm randomized trial in 1,050 military volunteers along the Cambodian-Thai border compared monthly prophylaxis (MMP: dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine plus 22.5 mg weekly primaquine) to focused screening and treatment (FSAT). MMP reduced P. falciparum positivity 90% (10% to 1%; absolute risk reduction 9%) versus 66% reduction in FSAT (7.6% to 2.7%). P. vivax cleared to 0% by month 3 in MMP but rebounded to 6.7% by month 6; FSAT failed to reduce it. Low-dose primaquine was safe despite 15% G6PD-deficiency prevalence.
articles.notable_reason: Demonstrates practical elimination strategy for endemic region

articles.headline: Validates plasmepsin V as bona fide antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Deconvolution of the On-Target Activity of Plasmepsin V Peptidomimetics in Plasmodium falciparum Parasites.
articles.details: Study used chemoproteomic methods and resistant parasite lines to validate plasmepsin V (PMV) as an on-target antimalarial. Peptidomimetic inhibitors engaged PMV and blocked protein export. Whole-genome sequencing of resistant lines revealed a single nucleotide variant in the pmv gene; reverse genetics confirmed its role in resistance. These data provide strong validation of PMV as a promising drug target.
articles.notable_reason: Validates novel drug target with clear resistance mechanism

articles.headline: Humanized P450 mouse model improves antimalarial drug metabolism translation
articles.title_original: Humanization of Drug Metabolism in the Plasmodium berghei Mouse Model for Antimalarial Drug Discovery.
articles.details: Describes the 8HUM P. berghei mouse model with cytochrome P450 enzymes genetically humanized to better predict clinical drug metabolism. While P. falciparum infection was unsuccessful (even with human RBC engraftment and NK depletion), P. berghei infection in 8HUM permitted efficacy assessment. Approved antimalarials showed greater metabolic stability in 8HUM than wild-type mice, and species differences in drug elimination routes were corrected.

articles.headline: Essential oils from eucalyptus and lemongrass repel malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Spatial Exposure Responses of Malaria Vectors to Eucalyptus grandis (W. Hill ex Maiden) and Cymbopogon citratus (DC.) Stapf Essential Oils.
articles.details: Laboratory study evaluated repellent activity of Eucalyptus grandis and Cymbopogon citratus essential oils against An. gambiae using Y-tube olfactometry. Eucalyptus oil contained L-α terpineol and eucalyptol as major components; Cymbopogon oil contained lavandulol methyl ether and citral. Both oils demonstrated dose-dependent repellent effects, suggesting potential for botanical vector repellent development.

articles.headline: P. vivax modulates sterol carrier protein to enable transmission in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax infection-driven modulation of sterol carrier protein reveals a metabolic link to reproductive physiology in Anopheles stephensi.
articles.details: Study in Anopheles stephensi showed that P. vivax infection upregulates sterol carrier protein (SCP) in midgut and salivary glands but suppresses it in hemocytes. SCP mediates blood-derived sterol transport essential for mosquito reproduction. RNAi-knockdown of SCP significantly reduced fecundity, indicating SCP is vital for reproductive physiology and parasite transmission.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies novel transmission-blocking target via metabolic pathway

articles.headline: Splenic CD103+ CD8+ T cells show limited role in P. yoelii infection control
articles.title_original: Characteristics of CD103(+)CD8(+) T cells in the spleen of Plasmodium yoelii NSM-infected mice.
articles.details: Single-cell RNA sequencing study in C57BL/6 mice infected with P. yoelii NSM showed that CD103+CD8+ T cells in the spleen declined after infection. These cells expressed higher CD62L and lower activation markers (CD44, CD69, TIGIT) and rarely produced IFN-γ or granzyme B compared to CD103− CD8+ T cells. Differentially expressed genes pointed to ribosomal/translational pathways, and LEF1 transcription factor likely regulates CD103 expression.

articles.headline: Malaria burden in Uganda declined 4% over eight years with regional variation
articles.title_original: Spatial and Temporal Distribution of Febrile Illnesses in Uganda: Case Studies of Brucellosis, Malaria and Typhoid Fever (2016-2023).
articles.details: National surveillance data analysis (2016–2023) tracked 110,134,705 malaria cases with 4% decrease in prevalence per 100,000 (43,316 to 29,271). Concurrent declines in typhoid (2% decrease) and brucellosis (8% decrease) were noted. Regional analysis showed northern Uganda carried the highest malaria and brucellosis burden, while central region had lowest malaria but highest typhoid, indicating disproportionate burden distribution.

articles.headline: Community acceptance factors for perennial malaria chemoprevention in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Investigating factors enabling the acceptability of perennial malaria chemoprevention implementation in Osun State, Nigeria: evidence from a qualitative process evaluation.
articles.details: Qualitative study using focus groups and interviews with caregivers, community leaders, and health workers in Osun State, Nigeria assessing acceptability of PMC for children under 24 months. High acceptability reported; influenced by perceived effectiveness, child-friendliness, and free services. Barriers included transportation costs and fear of side effects. Health worker attitudes significantly influenced caregiver acceptance.

articles.headline: High malaria vaccine acceptance despite limited prior awareness in Sudan
articles.title_original: Awareness and acceptance of the malaria vaccine among caregivers attending primary health care centres in Sudan: a mixed-methods study.
articles.details: Mixed-methods cross-sectional study of 159 caregivers at Sudanese primary health centers (November 2024–January 2025). Caregiver acceptance of malaria vaccine was 86.2% despite only 11.3% prior awareness. Acceptance significantly associated with perceived malaria severity (p<0.001), caregiver vaccination history (p=0.034), and children's complete immunization (p=0.047). Qualitative analysis identified vaccine safety concerns and healthcare system skepticism as barriers.

articles.headline: Behavioral interventions critical for achieving malaria elimination targets in Africa
articles.title_original: Insecticide-treated net use and elimination of malaria in Sub-Saharan African countries: assessing the Global Technical Strategy using an evolutionary game approach.
articles.details: Behavior-incidence modeling study analyzing historical malaria case reports and ITN usage data from Sub-Saharan African countries to assess progress toward Global Technical Strategy targets. Analysis classified countries based on transmission rates, mosquito prevalence, and socioeconomic conditions. While some countries can meet 2025–2030 targets through improved ITN use, others require enhanced social awareness campaigns, economic assistance, and ITN efficacy improvements.

articles.headline: Geostatistical modeling identifies persistent malaria hotspots in Myanmar
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal trends in P. falciparum malaria and identification of high-risk villages in Eastern Myanmar: an 8-year observational study.
articles.details: Eight-year observational study (2014–2021) using geostatistical modeling of routine surveillance data from 507 village-based malaria posts in Hpapun Township, Myanmar. P. falciparum incidence decreased 93.9% from 2014–2019. Intervention villages had significantly higher pre-intervention incidence (IRR 4.72, 95% CI 4.56–4.90) and lower post-intervention incidence (IRR 0.26, 95% CI 0.24–0.27). In 2021, 12 high-risk villages and 8 persistently high-risk villages were identified.

articles.headline: Systematic review reveals variable malaria RDT performance across Africa
articles.title_original: Performance and challenges of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in endemic regions of Africa.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 48 studies evaluating diagnostic performance of malaria rapid diagnostic tests across sub-Saharan Africa. Found considerable variability in RDT sensitivity and specificity influenced by parasite genetic diversity, antigen persistence, cross-reactivity with other infections, and HRP2 gene polymorphisms. These factors lead to false positives and negatives; region-specific diagnostic strategies and advanced tools for low-level parasitemia detection are recommended.

articles.headline: High artemisinin-resistant kelch13 R622I and RDT-negative parasites in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: High prevalence and emerging positive association of kelch13 R622I and HRP2-based RDT negativity in Plasmodium falciparum in northern Ethiopia.
articles.details: Year-long genomic surveillance of 903 P. falciparum infections in northwestern Ethiopia. Kelch13 R622I mutation (conferring artemisinin partial resistance) found in 44.3% overall, more prevalent in Gondar Zuria (52% vs 35%, p<0.001). HRP2-based RDT negativity in 39.3% (48.7% vs 27.6%, p<0.001). Critical finding: R622I and HRP2-RDT negativity co-occurred in 22% (28.9% vs 12.9% by district, p<0.001), with HRP2-negativity significantly more common in R622I parasites (48.3% vs 30.7%, p<0.05). First detection of k13 C580Y in Horn of Africa.
articles.notable_reason: Artemisinin-resistant parasites coupled with diagnostic test failure emerging

articles.headline: Vector species replacement drives malaria resurgence in Uganda after IRS change
articles.title_original: Disentangling the roles of different vector species during a malaria resurgence in Eastern Uganda.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study (2021–2023) of 422 children in 94 households across Tororo and Busia Districts, Uganda, investigating vector species role in malaria resurgence following IRS formulation change. Surveillance included passive/active P. falciparum detection by qPCR and biweekly mosquito collections. Overall, doubling of entomologic inoculation rate (aEIR) associated with 29% increase in P. falciparum hazard (HR 1.29, 95% CI 1.25–1.33). An. funestus showed comparable increase (HR 1.29); An. gambiae showed minimal effect (HR 1.04).

articles.headline: Widespread insecticide resistance documented in Anopheles gambiae across Ghana
articles.title_original: Trends and patterns of insecticide susceptibility of Anopheles gambiae between 2015 and 2020: implications for malaria vector control interventions in Ghana.
articles.details: Five-year surveillance study (2015–2020) using WHO insecticide susceptibility assays at 30 sentinel sites across Ghana testing 7 insecticides in 4 classes. High resistance to pyrethroids (deltamethrin, permethrin) and DDT detected in all sites with decreasing susceptibility over time. Carbamate and organophosphate susceptibility also declined. L1014F (kdr-west) mutation frequency significantly higher than G119S (ace-1) across sites. Resistance widespread to pyrethroids, DDT, organophosphates, and carbamates.

articles.headline: Diagnostic delay drives severe malaria complications during COVID-19
articles.title_original: Diagnostic delay inducing severe imported falciparum malaria: a time-sensitivity comparison of two imported cases during COVID-19 co-circulation.
articles.details: Comparative case report of two imported P. falciparum cases in Shanghai, 2021. Case 1: 96-hour diagnostic delay progressed to severe malaria with delirium, thrombocytopenia (33×10⁹/L), acute kidney injury (creatinine 278 μmol/L), requiring ICU. Case 2: 24-hour diagnosis via rapid diagnostic test and microscopy, prompt artesunate treatment, no severe complications. Demonstrates diagnostic delay directly impacts severe malaria risk when COVID-19 co-circulation creates diagnostic challenges.

articles.headline: High SP resistance but no artemisinin resistance documented in northern Nigeria
articles.title_original: Molecular epidemiology of Plasmodium falciparum drug resistance and vaccine targets in high-transmission settings in Africa.
articles.details: Oxford Nanopore amplicon sequencing study profiling 5 drug resistance genes and vaccine target (csp) in P. falciparum clinical isolates from high-transmission northern Nigeria. High dhfr-IRNI (90%) and dhps-SGKAA (45%) haplotypes indicate sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance markers, but no quintuple/sextuple mutations associated with SP-IPTp failure detected. Pfcrt-76T (chloroquine resistance) at ~25%. No WHO-validated k13 mutations linked to artemisinin resistance detected, but novel nonsynonymous variants present. CSP polymorphisms predicted to affect R21/Matrix-M vaccine protein stability.

articles.headline: Blood group antigens modulate malaria infection and disease severity
articles.title_original: Blood group antigens and malaria susceptibility.
articles.details: Review synthesizing evidence on how erythrocyte surface antigens (ABO, Duffy, MNS, Gerbich, Knops, Diego, and others) modulate malaria susceptibility through mechanisms affecting parasite invasion, cytoadherence, immune evasion, and disease severity. Discusses population heterogeneity, parasite-host adaptations and counter-adaptations, and clinical implications for risk stratification, transfusion safety, and vaccine design.

articles.headline: Healthcare-associated P. falciparum transmission documented in New York
articles.title_original: Case Report and Epidemiological Investigation of Healthcare-associated Plasmodium falciparum Malaria Transmission in Westchester County, New York-2023.
articles.details: Case report and epidemiological investigation of P. falciparum infection in 65-year-old Westchester County, New York resident with no travel to endemic area, diagnosed October 2023. Extensive investigation examined multiple potential exposure routes including domestic travel, overlapping hospitalizations with travelers, and outdoor exposure. Genetic analysis and epidemiologic investigation identified blood-borne transmission in healthcare setting from concurrently hospitalized traveler, with mechanism likely involving infection control lapse. No mosquito-transmitted or additional healthcare-associated cases identified.

articles.headline: Suriname's malaria elimination strategy applicable to Amazon regions
articles.title_original: Is malaria elimination in the Amazon rainforest feasible? The case of Suriname.
articles.details: Opinion paper summarizing Suriname's malaria elimination strategy combining robust surveillance, early diagnosis and treatment, retreatment strategies, and insecticide-treated net distribution. Sustained community engagement over decades contributed to sustainability. Authors believe similar strategies could be effectively adapted for other Amazon regions.

articles.headline: Perceptions of malaria prevention measures in Burundi study
articles.title_original: Understanding perceptions of malaria control measures in Burundi: a qualitative descriptive study using a socio-ecological approach.
articles.details: Qualitative descriptive study conducted March-May 2023 with 60 interviews and 28 focus group discussions examining perceptions of LLINs, indoor residual spraying, IPTp-SP, and environmental management. Identified intrapersonal, organizational, and policy/environmental factors affecting intervention acceptance. Recommends multifaceted approach addressing communication, context-sensitive adaptation, and socio-economic constraints.

articles.headline: Genetically engineered mosquitoes block malaria development
articles.title_original: Genetically engineered mosquitoes block development of circulating malaria strains.
articles.details: Study addressing genetically engineered mosquitoes as approach to malaria control.

articles.headline: Inositol polyphosphate multikinase identified as antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: Unveiling the Plasmodium inositol (pyro)phosphate pathway: Highlighting inositol polyphosphate multikinase as a novel therapeutic target for malaria.
articles.details: Exploratory in silico and molecular study identifying P. falciparum inositol polyphosphate multikinase (IPMK) as promising antimalarial drug target. Characterized sequences and structures of P. falciparum inositol phosphate pathway kinases and confirmed p.Pro375Gln substitution in IPMK1 in field isolates. Results suggest targeting this pathway could be detrimental to parasites, offering new mechanism for drug development.

articles.headline: Rapid diagnostic tests insufficient alone for malaria in Angola
articles.title_original: The risks of exclusive reliance on rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in Angola.
articles.details: Opinion on limitations of exclusive reliance on rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in Angola. RDTs limit species identification and parasite quantification. Recommends strengthening microscopy and integrating molecular diagnostics to improve clinical management, surveillance capacity, and detection of emerging resistance.

articles.headline: Blood donor screening during Florida autochthonous malaria outbreak
articles.title_original: Heightened Nontesting Risk Mitigation Blood Donor Screening with Retrospective Transcription-Mediated Amplification Testing during a 2023 Autochthonous Florida Malaria Cluster.
articles.details: Response to 2023 P. vivax autochthonous outbreak in Sarasota County, Florida, where seven cases were detected from May to early July without recent travel history. Blood center implemented enhanced screening of 258 Sarasota and 178 Miami donor samples using transcription-mediated amplification (detection limit 2-7 infected erythrocytes per mL). All samples nonreactive; no local transfusion transmission cases reported.

articles.headline: Adjuvants enhance antibody responses to malaria vaccine
articles.title_original: A Comparative Evaluation of Adjuvants for Enhancing Antibody Responses to a Nanoparticle-Based Malaria Vaccine.
articles.details: Study evaluating adjuvants for enhancing immunity of P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein epitope-targeted virus-like particle vaccine. Five formulations significantly increased anti-CSP IgG titers, with several also enhancing antibody durability. Identifies promising adjuvants for improving both magnitude and longevity of VLP-induced immunity.

articles.headline: Parasite genetic metrics estimate malaria prevalence in Uganda
articles.title_original: Accuracy of Plasmodium falciparum genetic data for estimating parasite prevalence and malaria incidence in Uganda.
articles.details: Analysis of dried blood spots from 3,563 symptomatic cases across 26 Uganda health facilities using amplicon deep sequencing of 165 polyallelic microhaplotypes. Parasite prevalence ranged 5.0–49.23%; incidence 91–1,667 cases per 1,000 person-years. Complexity of infection and effective complexity strongly associated with prevalence (r=0.79), while between-host relatedness best predicted incidence (r=0.37). Genetic metrics reliably capture spatial transmission variation but have limited ability to predict incidence.

articles.headline: NEK4 kinase regulates malaria parasite meiosis
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.
articles.details: Study identifying P. berghei NIMA-related kinase (NEK4) as key regulator of post-zygotic meiosis and zygote morphogenesis. NEK4 accumulates at microtubule-organizing center and apical polar complex after fertilization. Deletion of nek4 causes complete developmental arrest with blocked MTOC duplication, failed nuclear migration, and collapsed meiotic and cytoskeletal transcriptomic/phosphoproteomic networks, establishing NEK4 as essential regulator.

articles.headline: Fractional-order malaria model incorporates transmission memory effects
articles.title_original: Analytical and data-driven fractional-order malaria transmission model with vector and non-vector pathways.
articles.details: Development of Caputo fractional-order mathematical model incorporating vector and non-vector transmission pathways with memory effects in human and mosquito dynamics. Adams–Bashforth–Moulton numerical scheme implemented and validated. Modeling shows decreasing fractional order delays epidemic peaks and prolongs oscillatory persistence; including non-vector exposure increases persistence and improves correspondence with Nigeria field data.

articles.headline: Meta-analysis shows RDT limitations in Nigeria settings
articles.title_original: Performance of rapid diagnostic tests for malaria diagnosis in Nigeria: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 15 Nigerian studies comparing malaria RDT performance to microscopy. Pooled sensitivity 69.7% (95% CI 67.7–71.6%), specificity 81.5% (95% CI 79.9–83.1%), diagnostic odds ratio 21.87 (95% CI 7.45–64.21). High heterogeneity (I²=96.8%); better performance in urban settings. RDTs show suboptimal sensitivity and specificity compared with WHO benchmarks, necessitating integrated diagnostic algorithms combining RDTs with microscopy or molecular methods.

articles.headline: Machine learning designs malaria vaccine antigens targeting L9
articles.title_original: Machine learning enables de novo multiepitope design of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein to target trimeric L9 antibody.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Use of machine learning pipeline MESODID to design P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein minor repeat antigens targeting protective monoclonal antibody L9. Top design M-TIM demonstrated via 3.6 Å cryo-EM structure to successfully orient three copies of L9, recapitulating critical homotypic antibody interactions needed for protection. Authors suggest MESODID broadly applicable to vaccine targets with repeated epitopes including HIV-1, SARS-CoV-2, and Influenza.

articles.headline: Novel nucleotide scaffolds show early antimalarial activity
articles.title_original: Acyclic Nucleoside Phosphonates As Potential Inhibitors of Plasmodium Falciparum Egress.
articles.details: Researchers synthesized acyclic nucleoside phosphonates targeting P. falciparum development. Several compounds showed in vitro activity against chloroquine-sensitive 3D7 strains with IC50 values of 11.3–38.8 µM. Molecular docking confirmed interactions with the putative target. The authors conclude this family warrants further optimization as a promising antimalarial scaffold.

articles.headline: Environmental factors map malaria hotspots in Ghana's Oti Region
articles.title_original: Mapping Malaria Susceptibility in the Oti Region of Ghana Using Remote Sensing and GIS: A Retrospective Observational Study.
articles.details: This retrospective study used remote sensing, GIS, and machine learning to map malaria risk in Ghana's Oti Region. Krachi East and Nkwanta South were persistent hotspots with 91,376 and 73,592 cases over three years. Elevation (27.60% importance), land surface temperature 22–32°C (25.48%), vegetation index (23.12%), and proximity to rivers (20.00%) were key environmental risk factors, targeting priorities for resource allocation.

articles.headline: Pyrethroid resistance detected in Nigerian Anopheles gambiae
articles.title_original: Pyrethroid Resistance in Anopheles gambiae s.l.: A Focus on Permethrin and Deltamethrin for Malaria Vector Control.
articles.authors: Adesoye OA, Awoniyi AM, Adeogun AO
articles.details: CDC bottle bioassays tested An. gambiae s.l. susceptibility to permethrin and deltamethrin at standard WHO doses and sub-lethal concentrations. Permethrin induced 84.4% mortality at 30 min (P=0.031), reaching 100% at 35 min. Deltamethrin achieved 76% at 30 min and 100% at 40 min (P=0.04). No mortality occurred at sub-lethal doses, indicating resistance levels requiring routine surveillance and tailored control strategies.

articles.headline: Smartphone location data rapidly identifies malaria transmission hotspots
articles.title_original: Smartphone-based retrospective analysis for malaria hotspot detection.
articles.details: Researchers developed the Sickness Positioning System using anonymized Google Takeout location history from over 200 malaria-infected individuals in Brazil. Density-based clustering identified multiple transmission hotspots; field validation confirmed two previously unrecognized active mosquito breeding sites. The tool provided more precise and timely spatial assessment compared to Brazil's annual federal surveillance system.

articles.headline: Low-dose primaquine well tolerated for P. vivax radical cure in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Haematologic responses to primaquine used for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax in public health facilities in Ethiopia: a prospective observational study.
articles.details: Prospective observational study of 879 P. vivax/mixed malaria patients receiving 14-day primaquine 0.25 mg/kg without prior G6PD testing. Median hemoglobin fractional drop was −0.9% on day 3 and 0.8% on day 7; nine patients experienced >25% decline but none had clinical hemolytic symptoms. By day 13, 91.9% achieved hematological recovery with no serious adverse events, supporting the national policy for low-dose primaquine without prior G6PD testing when adverse-event monitoring is in place.

articles.headline: Vector behavior and human activity shape malaria exposure in Kenya highlands
articles.title_original: Human and vector behavioural determinants of malaria transmission in the Nandi highlands, western Kenya.
articles.details: Human landing catches and behavioral observations in Nandi highlands identified diverse Anopheles species with site-specific dominance (An. arabiensis, An. christyi, and novel An. spp. 14 BSL-2014) exhibiting variable indoor/outdoor biting patterns. Mosquito exposure peaked outdoors in early evening (1800–2100h) and indoors during first half of night (1900–0100h). LLINs prevented 24.5–44.9% of bites, leaving significant residual transmission when nets are ineffective, highlighting need for behavior-informed interventions.

articles.headline: Solar geoengineering may reduce malaria in South Asia with regional variation
articles.title_original: Malaria transmission dynamics under climate change and solar geoengineering in South Asia: a GLENS-based assessment.
articles.authors: Hussain A, Shoaib M, Latif M
articles.details: Using VECTRI malaria model, researchers simulated malaria transmission under Stratospheric Aerosol Injection (GLENS-SAI) versus unmitigated warming (RCP 8.5) in South Asia from 2020–2097. GLENS-SAI reduced entomological inoculation rate (EIR) across India, Nepal, Bangladesh, and most of Pakistan, but increases occurred in southeastern Pakistan, western Afghanistan, and eastern Iran. These shifts, driven by SAI-induced temperature and precipitation changes, suggest region-specific control strategies are needed.

articles.headline: In silico screening identifies potential GSK-3β antimalarial candidates
articles.title_original: In silico investigation of novel Plasmodium Falciparum glycogen synthase kinase (pfGSk3β) inhibitors for the treatment of malaria infection.
articles.details: In silico investigation identified three GSK-3β inhibitors (S20, S39, S56) from 53 compounds mined from CHEMBL as potential antimalarial candidates. Molecular docking and 100 ns dynamics simulations showed S56 had superior binding stability (1.0 nm RMSD) and favorable MMPBSA energy (−14.45 kJ/mol) compared to the co-crystallized control. Protox III toxicity predictions indicated possible adverse effects requiring further experimental validation.

articles.headline: COVID-19 disrupted malaria surveillance; seasonality persisted in Brazil
articles.title_original: Impact of the COVID-19 pandemic on malaria transmission in the state of Pará, Brazil.
articles.details: Secondary data analysis of 170,245 malaria cases in Pará, Brazil (2018–2023) showed 79,125 pre-pandemic, 44,830 during critical period (2020–2021), and 47,502 in less critical period (2022–2023). Miners comprised increasing proportions (17.2% to 58%), and P. falciparum cases rose from 2.6% to 11.3%. Seasonal transmission patterns remained similar to pre-pandemic levels, with case reduction attributed to control programs and increase attributed to vaccination-enabled social resumption.

articles.headline: CD44 cross-linking enhances P. falciparum RBC invasion via Basigin signaling
articles.title_original: CD44 cross-linking promotes Plasmodium falciparum invasion.
articles.details: A forward genetic screen identified CD44 as a host factor for P. falciparum invasion. Anti-CD44 antibody BRIC 222 promoted invasion through CD44 cross-linking, which altered RBC cytoskeletal phosphorylation and increased surface accessibility of the essential invasion receptor Basigin. The parasite ligand EBA-175 similarly enhanced invasion and binding of the PfRH5/PCRCR complex, suggesting CD44 cross-linking coordinates ligand-receptor interactions to promote invasion.

articles.headline: Genomic resources enable vector surveillance in Southeast Asian malaria
articles.title_original: A genomic toolkit for surveillance and elimination of the principal malaria vectors in Southeast Asia.
articles.details: Researchers released Adir1.0, a curated catalog of 540 An. dirus whole-genome sequences, and enhanced the Amin1.0 API for An. minimus, enabling interactive cloud-based genomic analysis. Exploratory population genomic analysis of An. dirus from Bangladesh, Thailand, and Cambodia revealed population structure, candidate regions of physiological insecticide resistance, and genomic structural variation, providing a foundation for genomic surveillance to inform vector control.

articles.headline: Ivermectin's potential and complexity in malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Ivermectin's role in malaria control: the simplicity and the complexity.
articles.authors: Schwartz E, Orshan L, Schlagenhauf P
articles.details: Title indicates discussion of ivermectin's benefits and limitations in malaria control strategy. Specific findings cannot be summarized without the article abstract.

articles.headline: Novel artemisinin combination formula targets host immunity in mice
articles.title_original: Proteomics-driven screening of artemisinin-based combination ratios and mechanistic insights into Plasmodium berghei infection in mice.
articles.details: Researchers screened antimalarial compound ratios in murine malaria models and optimized a combination of arteannuin B, artemisinic acid, and scopoletin. Proteomics analysis revealed the formula strengthens host defense by modulating phagocytic activity in splenic macrophages, dendritic cells, and natural killer cells via Fcγ receptor-mediated pathways. Authors propose this novel approach may circumvent emerging artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: Host defense peptides in innate immune malaria response
articles.title_original: Host defense peptides in malaria infection: their contributions, significance and constraints.
articles.details: Review examining host defense peptides (HDPs) as key components of innate immunity targeting multiple Plasmodium development stages. HDPs including defensins, cathelicidins, NK-2 peptide, platelet factor 4, macrophage inflammatory protein-3α, and hepcidin protect against malaria through direct antimicrobial activity and immunomodulation. Authors discuss HDP interactions at various infection stages and their roles in modulating host immune responses while highlighting current limitations in understanding HDPs' full implications in malaria infection.

articles.headline: Scoping review of malaria control strategies in Northern Ghana
articles.title_original: Malaria control in Northern Ghana: a scoping review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 38 studies identified from 1782 sources on malaria control in Northern Ghana (2013-2023). Studies focused predominantly on case management (28.9%), Intermittent Preventive Treatment in pregnancy/IPTp (23.7%), and Long-Lasting Insecticidal Nets/LLINs (15.8%). Authors conclude malaria vaccines remain understudied (2.6%) and recommend further research on combined intervention effects and larval source management.

articles.headline: Inconsistent antimalarial treatment in neonates across case series
articles.title_original: Management of malaria in newborns: a systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 26 publications (22 case reports, 3 case series, 1 cross-sectional) documenting antimalarial treatment in 40 neonates with confirmed Plasmodium infection. Plasmodium falciparum and P. vivax accounted for 45% and 43% respectively; median age at diagnosis was 19 days. Treatment was highly inconsistent: only 28% of P. falciparum cases received artemisinin-based therapy (most received oral amodiaquine/chloroquine), and P. vivax was mostly treated with chloroquine (64%). All patients survived with parasitemia clearance in 2-7 days.

articles.headline: Review of interventions for outdoor malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Outdoor biting by malaria vectors: what are the options for intervention?
articles.details: Comprehensive literature review of vector control options for outdoor biting mosquitoes including larval source management, spatial repellents, topical repellents, endectocides, and gene drive approaches. Larval Source Management is effective in some contexts and has WHO endorsement as supplemental method. Spatial emanators received recent WHO recommendation for indoor application; topical repellents are effective short-term. Authors conclude there is no universally applicable tool and recommend integrating complementary techniques with community engagement.

articles.headline: Cambodia's malaria surveillance system assessment shows strong performance
articles.title_original: Key findings and recommendations from assessment of Cambodia's national malaria surveillance system.
articles.details: Assessment of Cambodia's surveillance system using WHO Malaria Surveillance Assessment Toolkit evaluated 68 indicators and found strong overall performance: 82% of Performance indicators, 77% of Context and Infrastructure, 79% of Technical and Process, and 100% of Behavior indicators met. The country reported only 1,384 cases in 2023 while progressing toward elimination goal of 2025. Key successes included improved completeness and timeliness of reporting; identified areas for improvement include revising case investigation and enhancing data quality processes.

articles.headline: Alpha-gal immune response shows promise in endemic infants
articles.title_original: Naturally acquired immune responses to alpha-gal in malaria endemic settings and pre-clinical efficacy testing with R21/MM.
articles.details: Study assessed naturally acquired anti-alpha-gal (α-gal) antibodies in UK volunteers post-sporozoite challenge, Kenyan adults, and Burkina Faso infants. While UK volunteers showed no significant antibody increases at 90 days post-challenge, Burkina Faso infants developed increasing anti-α-gal IgG and IgM levels over time, with protection correlating to higher anti-α-gal IgM antibody levels. Pre-clinical testing of BSA-linked α-gal with Matrix-M adjuvant in mice elicited IgG/IgM responses but failed to provide protection against sporozoite challenge.

articles.headline: R21/Matrix-M vaccine and next-generation tools advance malaria control
articles.title_original: Advances in population-based interventions to control falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Glossop SE, Peto TJ, Adhikari B
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Review of population-level malaria prevention strategies. Insecticide-treated nets (ITNs) have been most impactful but face insecticide resistance challenges; chemoprevention protects tens of millions primarily in sub-Saharan Africa but threatened by emerging drug resistance. The R21/Matrix-M vaccine represents significant advance over RTS,S/AS01 with greater efficacy, lower cost, and scalable mass production. Field trials suggest next-generation monoclonal antibodies could provide seasonal chemoprophylaxis, while gene drive technology may offer longer-term potential for vector eradication.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic P. knowlesi detected in North Sumatra island setting
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic Burden of Zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi Malaria on Mursala Island and Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Transmission in Mainland North Sumatra, Indonesia.
articles.details: Ultrasensitive reverse-transcriptase qPCR detected zoonotic malaria in North Sumatra, Indonesia. On Mursala Island, 7 of 64 participants (10.9%) had Plasmodium knowlesi infections including 5 agricultural workers and 2 with severe anemia; all were microscopy-negative and rapid test-negative. Among 947 mainland participants, 26% had confirmed Plasmodium species (17.5% P. vivax, 7.5% P. falciparum), with 30% being submicroscopic. Authors conclude P. knowlesi transmission is low but can cause severe malaria, highlighting importance of molecular diagnostics.

articles.headline: mRDT accuracy varies by transmission intensity and demographics
articles.title_original: Varying Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Test Accuracy by Regional Transmission Level and Demographics in Tanzania.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study in Tanzania comparing three national standard malaria rapid diagnostic tests (mRDTs) and one research mRDT against qPCR reference standard. Found significant variation in test accuracy by age, sex, and regional transmission intensity. The research mRDT underperformed overall with poor sensitivity across transmission strata; transmission intensity appears to affect mRDT diagnostic agreement and performance. Results highlight importance of considering test accuracy variations by population and transmission setting to reduce measurement bias.

articles.headline: Uridine supplementation reduces parasitemia in mouse and parasite models
articles.title_original: Temporal multi-tissue profiling reveals metabolites linked to immune response and malaria parasitemia control.
articles.details: Metabolomic study using LC-MS/MS in infected C57BL/6n mice revealed tissue- and strain-specific metabolic alterations in glycolysis intermediates, amino acids, and nucleotides. Glycolysis intermediates including bisphosphoglycerate, phosphoenolpyruvate, and dihydroxyacetone phosphate were negatively correlated with parasitemia. Supplementing P. berghei ANKA-infected mice with uridine significantly reduced parasitemia and MCP-1 levels; uridine supplementation also lowered P. falciparum parasitemia in vitro. Authors propose metabolic interventions could enhance malaria treatment strategies.

articles.headline: Malaria case clusters surge in South Korea's border regions
articles.title_original: Individual Malaria Case and Case Cluster Status in the Capital Region of the Republic of Korea, 2024.
articles.details: Analysis of malaria cases in South Korea's capital region (2022-2024) found 620 cases reported in 2024 (94.1% of national total), representing 78.7% increase from 2022. Identified 47 case clusters with 5 in military units; clusters concentrated near the North Korean border in northwestern Gyeonggi, Ganghwa-gun, and Incheon. Primary risk factors included residence (57.8%), work (12.9%), military service in high-risk areas (12.9%), and outdoor leisure activities. Authors attribute post-COVID resurgence to resumed social activities and enhanced surveillance.

articles.headline: Barriers to adopting multiple first-line malaria therapies in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Barriers and facilitators to the adoption of multiple first-line therapies for management of uncomplicated malaria in Tanzania: a multi-method qualitative study.
articles.details: Multi-method qualitative study examining adoption of multiple artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACT) to slow resistance in Tanzania. Desk review (2020-2024) found malaria cases 8.9M→8.1M; artemether-lumefantrine comprised 73.5% of ACT imports versus 12% artesunate, 6% artemether, 2.6% dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine. Barriers included high cost of alternatives, limited provider training, and weak supply chains. Facilitators: political commitment, capacity-building, and pharmacovigilance data use. Conclusion: Multiple first-line therapy adoption requires ACT import diversification and supply chain strengthening.

articles.headline: Imported malaria cases remain barrier to Pemba Island elimination
articles.title_original: Epidemiological and entomological analysis of imported malaria in Pemba Island, Zanzibar: a study from 2020-2022.
articles.details: Retrospective epidemiological analysis of 2,646 malaria cases in Pemba 2020-2022; 1,411 (53.3%) were imported. Imported cases higher in males (57.3%, p<0.0001) and adults >18 years (68%, p<0.0001). Source regions: Tanga (61%), Dar es Salaam (12%), Coastal Region (10%). Importation odds were significantly higher in 2021 (OR=3.58, p<0.001) and 2022 (OR=5.62, p<0.001) compared to 2020. Human Biting Rate (HBR) associated with imported cases (84.81-unit increase per HBR unit, 95% CI 13.29-156.32, p=0.021). Conclusion: Enhanced cross-border surveillance and integrated interventions needed.

articles.headline: Critically low sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine uptake in pregnant Somali women
articles.title_original: Factors associated with intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp-SP) use for malaria during pregnancy in Somalia: a multilevel analysis of the 2020 demographic and health survey.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 7,124 women from 2020 Somalia DHS examining malaria prevention in pregnancy. Overall reported sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP/Fansidar) use during pregnancy was critically low at 6.3%. Only 31% attended antenatal care (ANC); 69% reported no visits. Paradoxically, women with 4+ ANC visits had lower odds of reported SP use (AOR=0.38, 95% CI 0.25-0.58) versus no visits. Nulliparous women had lower odds (AOR=0.6, 95% CI 0.4-0.9). Nomadic women had higher odds (AOR=3.7, 95% CI 2.2-6.4) than rural women. Conclusion: SP coverage far below WHO goals, driven by extremely low ANC attendance.

articles.headline: Random Forest machine learning improves childhood malaria risk prediction
articles.title_original: From risk factors to predictive modelling: applying machine learning to childhood malaria surveillance in resource-limited settings.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 693 under-5 children in Nigerian internally displaced persons camps with malaria prevalence of 68.5% by RDT. Risk factors: caregiver with no education (aOR=3.23, p=0.026); female caregiver protective (aOR=0.53, p=0.024). Four machine learning models tested; Random Forest performed best with AUC=0.892, identifying caregiver occupation and residential camp as most significant predictors. Despite 60.3% of caregivers having malaria prevention knowledge, bed net usage was only 2%. Conclusion: ML integration into surveillance enables precision public health targeting education and vector control.

articles.headline: Indonesia and Papua New Guinea coordinate island-wide malaria response
articles.title_original: Two Nations, One Front: Indonesia and Papua New Guinea forge a One-Island approach to fight persistent malaria on New Guinea.
articles.details: No abstract provided. Based on title, appears to describe bilateral coordination for malaria control on New Guinea island.

articles.headline: Malaria and dengue show region-specific spatiotemporal associations in Peru
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal co-distribution and time lagged cross correlation of malaria and dengue in Loreto, Peru.
articles.details: Time-lagged cross-correlation analysis of district-level dengue and malaria incidence in Loreto, Peru (2000-2021). Growing number of districts reported both diseases over time. Temporal patterns varied by region: in Northwest, malaria increases often preceded dengue increases; in Northeast, malaria increases preceded dengue decreases. Spatial correlation between associations found in some regions. Conclusion: Districts with strong malaria-dengue associations identified for targeted integrated vector control; distinct regional patterns suggest different drivers warrant further investigation.

articles.headline: Novel Plasmodium falciparum glutamyl-tRNA synthetase inhibitors computationally identified
articles.title_original: Rational design of novel Plasmodium falciparum glutamyl-tRNA synthetase inhibitors for the development of next-generation antimalarial drugs.
articles.details: Structure-based virtual screening of 1,830 compounds from Medicinal Fungi Secondary Metabolites library targeting P. falciparum glutamyl-tRNA synthetase (GluRS). Molecular dynamics simulations (100 ns) evaluated candidates. Two lead compounds identified: MSID000152 (binding affinity -10.4 kcal/mol, RMSD 3.94 Å) and MSID000974 (binding affinity -10.1 kcal/mol, RMSD 2.35 Å). MSID000152 showed favorable MMPB/GBSA binding free energy. Conclusion: Both compounds are promising leads requiring in vitro and in vivo validation as potential antimalarials.

articles.headline: Placental malaria prevalence 25.1% in sub-Saharan Africa with major adverse outcomes
articles.title_original: Prevalence, predictors and adverse perinatal outcomes of placental malaria in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 178 observational studies (2000-2024) from 23 sub-Saharan African countries involving 93,809 parturient women. Overall placental malaria prevalence 25.1% (95% CI 22.0-28.3), varying by detection method: RDT 16.9%, microscopy 19.1%, PCR 24.8%, histopathology 34.8%. Predictors included younger age (OR=2.50), primigravidae (OR=2.65), no insecticide-treated nets (OR=2.11), no ANC (OR=7.43), no IPTp (OR=1.98), and HIV infection (OR=2.67). Placental malaria increased odds of maternal anemia (OR=2.18) and low birth weight (OR=1.71). Conclusion: Multidimensional approaches targeting identified predictors are needed.

articles.headline: India should expand artemisinin-based therapy for Plasmodium vivax elimination
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax elimination from India may need therapeutic efficacy studies - Informed shift to artemisinin-based treatment.
articles.details: Review synthesizing clinical, molecular, and pharmacological evidence supporting shift from chloroquine to artemisinin-based combination therapies (ACTs) for P. vivax in India. ACTs demonstrate superior efficacy versus chloroquine, including rapid parasite clearance, improved tolerability, effectiveness against mixed infections, and reduced relapse when combined with primaquine. Context: India has reduced P. vivax burden but chloroquine resistance is emerging; sparse therapeutic efficacy study (TES) coverage and limited molecular resistance data exist. WHO recommends change if failure rate exceeds 10%. Conclusion: India should prioritize TES expansion and adopt ACT-based regimen to sustain elimination momentum.

articles.headline: Plasmodium berghei proteasome subunit Rpt3 activates protein phosphatase 1
articles.title_original: Characterization of the Regulatory AAA-ATPase Subunit Rpt3 in Plasmodium berghei as an Activator of Protein Phosphatase 1.
articles.details: Characterization of P. berghei AAA-ATPase subunit Rpt3 demonstrating binding to and activation of Protein Phosphatase 1 catalytic subunit (PP1c). PbRpt3 enhanced PP1c activity both in vitro and in Xenopus oocyte heterologous model. RVxF motifs of PbRpt3 involved in binding and regulatory function; ATP-binding capacity may contribute to phosphatase activity. Reverse genetics studies suggest essential role for PbRpt3 during erythrocytic cycle. Interactome analysis confirmed PbRpt3 is part of 19S regulatory particle. Conclusion: Describes novel proteasome-phosphatase interaction in the malaria parasite.

articles.headline: Malaria pigment hemozoin promotes pro-inflammatory macrophage activation
articles.title_original: Malaria pigment hemozoin drives M1 pro-inflammatory macrophage polarization in vitro.
articles.details: In vitro study of hemozoin (HZ; malaria pigment byproduct) effects on macrophage polarization using primary human monocyte-derived macrophages (MDM) and THP-1 cells. HZ induced M1/pro-inflammatory phenotype in non-polarized cells, increasing gene expression and protein secretion of CXCL8, TNF-α, and IL-1β. In presence of M1-polarizing stimuli, HZ further increased CXCL8 and IL-1β in MDM but reduced TNF-α in THP-1 cells. HZ did not affect M2 markers (PPARG, IL-10, STAT6). Results varied by cell model. Conclusion: First study demonstrating HZ promotes M1 pro-inflammatory differentiation, contributing to understanding of malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Molecular qPCR survey reveals 63% Plasmodium prevalence in Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: Molecular investigation of Plasmodium spp. in Sierra Leone: recommendations on diagnostic methods for malaria control in high-epidemic regions - a nationally representative cross-sectional epidemiological survey.
articles.details: Nationally representative, age-stratified cross-sectional survey in Sierra Leone (2022, rainy season) of 2,112 individuals aged ≥2 years. qPCR positivity rate was 63.0% (95% CI 61.0-65.1), with regional weighted rate 62.9% (95% CI 58.3-67.4). In 400 randomly selected positive samples: 97.75% P. falciparum, 20.5% carried multiple Plasmodium species. Positivity was higher in males and lower in those aged ≥60 years and healthcare workers. 18.6% of positive samples showed low parasitemia by Ct values; 93.5% of infected individuals were asymptomatic. Conclusion: High prevalence of asymptomatic and low-density infections indicates sustained transmission reservoir; molecular diagnosis critical for surveillance.

articles.headline: Maternal angiogenic proteins linked to malaria-associated newborn growth restriction
articles.title_original: Association between peripheral markers in women with malaria in pregnancy and small newborns: A cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 386 pregnant women with and without malaria in Brazil (2013–2015). Measured maternal peripheral proteins (Tie2, sFlt1, sENG, leptin, and others) at delivery and assessed associations with newborn anthropometric parameters. Tie2, sFlt1, sENG, and leptin were associated with newborn growth restriction. sFlt1 correlated with reduced weight and length in P. vivax-infected mothers; Tie2, sFlt1, and leptin with reduced length in P. falciparum-infected mothers.

articles.headline: HBV and P. falciparum co-infection in pregnant women suggests antagonistic replication
articles.title_original: Hepatitis B Virus and Plasmodium falciparum Co-Infection Among Pregnant Women in Gabon: Prevalence, Biochemical Impact, and Antagonistic Replication Dynamics.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 222 pregnant women in Gabon (May 2022–May 2023). HBV infection detected in 4.05% (9/222), with 1.35% (3/222) co-infected with P. falciparum and 2.7% (6/222) HBV mono-infected. P. falciparum parasitemia present in 26.1% (58/222). Elevated liver enzymes (AST) in HBV mono-infected but normal in co-infected. Inverse relationship between HBsAg levels and parasitemia suggested antagonistic dynamics, though not statistically significant (ρ = −0.5, p = 0.67).

articles.headline: Dietary compounds modulate New World vector susceptibility to rodent malaria
articles.title_original: Dietary para-aminobenzoic acid, uric acid, and antibiotics modulate the susceptibility of Anopheles darlingi and Anopheles albimanus to Plasmodium berghei.
articles.details: Experimental study evaluating susceptibility of Anopheles darlingi and A. albimanus to Plasmodium berghei under dietary post-infection treatments (para-aminobenzoic acid, uric acid, penicillin/streptomycin). Dietary PABA supplementation influenced susceptibility in both species. P. berghei sporozoites were detected in hemolymph but not salivary glands until day 28 post-infection, suggesting the dietary treatments may affect parasite development.

articles.headline: High genetic conservation of k13 in Ethiopia indicates no artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: Preliminary analysis of the conserved Plasmodium falciparum k13 gene in Arbaminch and Mirab Abaya, Ethiopia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 52 P. falciparum samples from Ethiopia (May–December 2023). Sanger sequencing of the k13 gene propeller domain revealed no mutations. Pairwise similarity index was 0.982, indicating high genetic conservation. Mirab Abaya isolates showed strong genetic homogeneity; Arbaminch samples showed slight divergence but remained largely conserved. No k13 mutations detected, supporting continued effectiveness of artemisinin-based combination therapy.

articles.headline: Multi-epitope diagnostic antigen achieves 89.7% sensitivity for malaria detection
articles.title_original: In silico design and validation of a Plasmodium falciparum multi-epitope diagnostic antigen (PfMEDA1) for the diagnosis of falciparum malaria.
articles.details: In silico-designed multi-epitope antigen (PfMEDA1) constructed from five immunogenic P. falciparum proteins. Tested against serum samples from 223 malaria-infected and 81 uninfected individuals from Cameroon using indirect ELISA. Diagnostic sensitivity 89.69% (95% CI: 84.93–93.35), specificity 75.31% (95% CI: 64.47–84.22), Cohen kappa 0.64. Authors conclude PfMEDA1 shows potential as diagnostic antigen, pending field validation in varied endemicity settings.

articles.headline: Erosion-control pits create breeding sites driving Uganda malaria outbreak
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreak facilitated by increased vector-breeding sites from erosion control pits sustained by intermittent rainfall, Mbale District, Uganda, January-June 2019.
articles.details: Outbreak investigation in Uganda (January–June 2019) identifying 8,827 case-patients with 33% overall attack rate (36% females, 27% males; peak in age 5–18 years). Case-control study (150 cases, 150 controls) found stagnant water in soil erosion control pits strongly associated with infection (OR = 18, 95% CI: 7.0–50). Bed net use protective (OR = 0.17, 95% CI: 0.07–0.38). Anopheles gambiae was predominant vector.

articles.headline: Metagenomic method characterizes multiple viral pathogens in acute febrile illness
articles.title_original: NOMAD: metagenomic characterisation of the viral pathogen composition in outbreaks of non-malaria acute febrile illness cases.
articles.details: Methods paper describing NOMAD, a metagenomic approach using profile Hidden Markov Models derived from Swiss-Prot viral sequences to identify viral pathogens in non-malaria acute febrile illness. Tested on simulated and existing datasets (Ebola, hepatitis C, chikungunya, arboviruses). Successfully identified combinations of multiple viral etiological agents within single disease samples, providing granular perspective of circulating viral pathogens for surveillance.

articles.headline: Pyrimidine pathway enzymes identified as essential vulnerabilities in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Genetic analysis of pyrimidine biosynthetic enzymes in P. falciparum. Knockdown of aspartate transcarbamoylase (PfATC) proved lethal; N-terminal truncation impaired parasite growth. Carbamoyl phosphate synthetase II (PfCPSII) was essential for proliferation. Dihydroorotase (PfDHO) deletion in engineered uracil-salvaging parasites caused uracil auxotrophy, confirming essential function. Identified multiple vulnerable nodes in pyrimidine synthesis pathway.

articles.headline: Novel heterospirocyclic compounds active against artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Novel heterospirocyclic antimalarials with activity against artemisinin- and multidrug-resistant P. falciparum malaria.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Chemical synthesis and evaluation of heterospirocyclic compounds against drug-resistant P. falciparum strains. Compounds 25 and 26 demonstrated antimalarial activity within 24 hours against a panel of resistant isolates. Metabolomics analysis suggested mechanism via disruption of pyrimidine biosynthesis and hemoglobin catabolism. No major toxicity observed in kidney or hepatic cell lines, indicating specificity.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria with multiorgan failure after travel to Indonesia
articles.title_original: [Cerebral malaria after travel to Indonesia].
articles.details: Case report of a young Danish boy with severe P. falciparum malaria contracted during travel to Indonesia without chemoprophylaxis or mosquito protection. Flu-like symptoms were initially misinterpreted, delaying diagnosis. Patient presented with cerebral malaria and multiorgan failure requiring intensive care. Rapid IV artesunate treatment resulted in full recovery, highlighting importance of early recognition and rapid testing.

articles.headline: Geospatial analysis reveals malaria hotspots across eight West African countries
articles.title_original: Malaria hotspots and risk factors among children under-five years of age across eight West African countries: A geospatial analysis of DHS data.
articles.details: Analysis of Demographic and Health Surveys data from eight West African countries using generalized linear geostatistical models. Insecticide-treated net use significantly reduced odds of infection in 4/8 countries. Child age <2 years consistently associated with lower risk. High malaria prevalence predicted in Benin and Côte d'Ivoire; lower prevalence in Ghana and Liberia. Findings support tailoring interventions to local spatial context.

articles.headline: Relative humidity strongly associated with malaria incidence in The Gambia
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the significant association between Climatic Variables and Malaria Cases in the Upper River Region of the Gambia.
articles.details: Analysis of 12 years (2011–2022) of monthly malaria cases in Upper River Region, The Gambia, linked with meteorological data. Odds of malaria increased 3–4% in children <5 and 6–9% in those ≥5 years per 1% increase in relative humidity (p < 0.01). Odds decreased 20–14% in children <5 and 21–15% in those ≥5 with maximum temperature increase (p < 0.01). Relative humidity identified as major influencing factor for malaria incidence.

articles.headline: Plasmodium vivax evolves to infect Duffy-negative populations in Africa
articles.title_original: Adaptive evolution of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-negative hosts: insights from East African genomes.
articles.details: Genome-wide SNP analysis of 110 P. vivax isolates from Ethiopia (38 Duffy-negative, 72 Duffy-positive) revealed markedly reduced diversity in Duffy-negative parasites (197,461 vs 477,561 SNPs). Duffy-negative Pv showed selection signatures in genes linked to organellar maintenance and vesicle trafficking, distinct from drug-resistance and erythrocyte-binding signatures in Duffy-positive parasites. These findings suggest P. vivax in Duffy-negative populations maintains a genetically conserved background reflecting stringent evolutionary bottlenecks and specialized host-parasite interactions.
articles.notable_reason: First genetic evidence of P. vivax adaptation to Duffy-negative populations

articles.headline: Climate warming reduces Anopheles aquasalis survival but not Plasmodium vivax infectivity
articles.title_original: Predicted future climate scenarios impact survival and immune response but not Plasmodium vivax infection in Anopheles aquasalis (Diptera, Culicidae), the primary vector of coastal Central and South America and the Caribbean Islands.
articles.details: Female An. aquasalis mosquitoes infected with P. vivax were exposed to IPCC climate scenarios (RCP 4.5, 6.0, 8.5). Parasite development was supported in all conditions (54-68% infection rates, no significant differences in oocyst intensity). Mosquito survival declined under RCP 6.0 and 8.5 scenarios (18.6% and 6.5% by day 7 post-infection vs 40% controls, p<0.001), with reduced immune gene expression. Future climate stress may compromise vector fitness while maintaining P. vivax transmissibility.

articles.headline: Artemisinin toxicity and resistance in nonendemic malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Treatment of Plasmodium falciparum malaria in the nonendemic setting: a narrative review of toxicity, failure, and emerging resistance.
articles.details: This narrative review examines treatment challenges for P. falciparum malaria in nonendemic countries, specifically artemisinin-related hemolysis and increasing treatment failures potentially related to drug resistance. The authors emphasize that nonimmune travelers differ from endemic-country residents in vulnerability to complications and propose specific management adaptations including structured clinical follow-up to detect toxicity and failure, and systematic genomic surveillance alongside endemic-country initiatives.

articles.headline: High community awareness of traditional malaria remedies in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Assessment of knowledge, attitudes, and practices regarding the use of traditional medicine for treating malaria among residents of Woreta Town, Amhara Regional State, Northwest Ethiopia.
articles.details: A community-based cross-sectional survey of 390 residents in Woreta Town, Ethiopia (January-June 2024) assessed knowledge of traditional medicine for malaria. Most respondents (65.1%, 254/390) were aware of plant remedies including Bisana (27.9%), Girawa (26.7%), Shifarraw (20.1%), and Nechshinkurt (14.6%); 19.3% (33/390) mentioned animal byproducts like goat bile; and 3.1% (12/390) mentioned Yekoye Kibe. Overall, 66.4% (259/390) believed combining traditional and modern medicine could be beneficial.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine continues failing in Burkina Faso; alternatives superior
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Burkina Faso, 2020-2021.
articles.details: A therapeutic efficacy study of 1078 children aged 6 months-12 years with uncomplicated P. falciparum in Burkina Faso (2020-2021) compared artemether-lumefantrine (AL), dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP), and artesunate-pyronaridine (AS-PYR). Uncorrected 28-day efficacy was 57.1-64.4% for AL versus 96.6-98.9% for DP and 82.2-90.7% for AS-PYR. PCR-corrected efficacy met 90% WHO threshold for all drugs except AL at one site (89.4%). AL's persistently low efficacy warrants treatment guideline change to multiple first-line non-AL ACT.
articles.notable_reason: Large efficacy study confirms AL failure; supports treatment guideline revision

articles.headline: 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research abstracts
articles.title_original: Abstracts of the 9th International Conference on Plasmodium vivax Research (ICPvR) 2025.
articles.details: This is a collection of abstracts from the 2025 ICPvR conference.

articles.headline: Highly sensitive RDT limited for low-density P. falciparum detection
articles.title_original: Assessment of a highly sensitive rapid diagnostic test for reactive case detection of Plasmodium Falciparum infections in Myanmar.
articles.details: A Myanmar reactive case detection study (2017-2018) evaluated a highly sensitive RDT (hsRDT: NxTek Eliminate Malaria Ag P.f) versus conventional RDT in 1990 contacts of 51 index P. falciparum cases. Using ultra-sensitive qPCR as gold standard (171/1990 positive), hsRDT had sensitivity 27.9% (95% CI 18.2-39.6%) and specificity 98.8%, versus cRDT sensitivity 13.7% (95% CI 7.6-20.7%) and specificity 100% (p<0.01). hsRDT's low sensitivity limits utility for low-density detection despite detecting more infections than cRDT.

articles.headline: Malaria in Burundian children imposes substantial household economic burden
articles.title_original: Economic burden of malaria in children under the age of five in Burundi.
articles.details: A micro-costing study across 15 Burundi healthcare sites estimated average societal cost per malaria episode in children under five at US$12.10, with households bearing US$3.30 out-of-pocket costs and US$4.70 productivity losses from 8 days caregiving. Health system costs were US$2.60 for outpatient and US$44.00 for inpatient treatment. Household burden equaled 44% of monthly income per episode. The authors concluded investment in preventive strategies and vaccines could reduce government health expenditure and enable household economic growth.

articles.headline: Mathematical fractional-order model reveals endemic malaria equilibrium dynamics
articles.title_original: Fractal fractional approach to investigate the forecasting and dynamics of malaria disease for early control precaution.
articles.details: A fractional-order mathematical model under the Mittag-Leffler kernel examined malaria dynamics in Northern Cyprus. The eight-compartmental model incorporating human and mosquito subpopulations was analyzed for equilibrium points and basic reproduction number. Numerical simulations revealed an asymptotically stable worldwide endemic equilibrium and indicated considerable increase in susceptible human populations with decline in infectious mosquito numbers, suggesting need for major control efforts.

articles.headline: Malaria risk mapping across India reveals persistent P. vivax expansion
articles.title_original: Mapping malaria risk in India between 2019 and 2023: a tool for the public to track malaria.
articles.details: A spatiotemporal analysis mapped malaria across 700+ Indian districts (2019-2023) using district data from the National Center for Vector Borne Disease Control Programme. Cases declined from 0.33 million in 2019 due to COVID-19 impacts. P. falciparum remains widespread in North-eastern and Central India, while P. vivax showed emerging expansion into north-eastern regions post-pandemic. Vulnerable populations included scheduled caste/tribe individuals and economically marginalized persons; risk factors included suboptimal drinking water practices, low maternal education, and limited healthcare access. A public digital dashboard was developed for malaria tracking.

articles.headline: Child age and bed net use drive malaria prevalence variation in Niger
articles.title_original: Exploring spatial variation and multilevel modeling of malaria prevalence among children aged 6-59 months based on RDT in Niger: Insights for public health decision-making.
articles.authors: Mulugeta SS, Masresha BM, Ejigu LA
articles.details: A multilevel spatial analysis of 2021 Niger Malaria Indicator Survey data examined 4724 children aged 6-59 months, with 1121 (23.7%) RDT-positive for malaria. Significant spatial clustering was detected (Moran's I = 0.434, p<0.001). Multilevel logistic regression revealed malaria prevalence was significantly associated with child age, anemia levels, maternal education, and insecticide-treated bed net use. The authors concluded that improved net coverage and tailored interventions based on local conditions are needed to address geographic heterogeneity in malaria burden.

articles.headline: Ethnicity, occupation, and parasite species delay malaria care-seeking
articles.title_original: Drivers of delay in reported malaria care-seeking in Myanmar and Thailand.
articles.details: A longitudinal study followed 2960 febrile patients in Myanmar (2017-2021) and 15,576 in Thailand (2018-2024) seeking malaria care. In Myanmar (mean fever 1.8 days), delays were associated with Shan ethnicity (TR: 1.48), farming (TR: 1.12), uncertain malaria history (TR: 1.26), P. falciparum (TR: 1.10), and P. vivax (TR: 1.23) diagnoses. In Thailand (mean fever 2.9 days), farming (TR: 1.07), P. falciparum (TR: 1.27), and P. vivax (TR: 1.20) were associated with delays. Daily bed net use and prior malaria episodes were protective. These risk factors should guide targeted interventions.

articles.headline: MAP-X maps 20,000+ protein interactions across P. falciparum development
articles.title_original: MAP-X reveals distinct protein complex dynamics across Plasmodium falciparum blood stages.
articles.details: MAP-X uses thermal proteome profiling to systematically map protein complexes in intact P. falciparum cells. Applied across 7 timepoints of the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle, it predicted more than 20,000 interactions, identified conserved complexes and previously unreported associations, and revealed moonlighting subunits that dissociate to assume different biological functions, providing a resource for understanding Plasmodium biology.

articles.headline: RIFIN subset protects against severe malaria via LILRB1 engagement
articles.title_original: A subset of Plasmodium falciparum RIFINs is linked to severe malaria risk reduction and engages LILRB1 through a conserved structural motif.
articles.details: Immunoproteomic analysis identified a RIFIN subset associated with reduced severe malaria risk in malaria-infected individuals. These RIFINs engage the inhibitory receptor LILRB1 through a conserved structural motif anchored by disulfide-bonded cysteines. The findings suggest a bifunctional RIFIN mechanism balancing clinical immunity and immune evasion, with potential to guide rational vaccine design targeting the structurally constrained functional LILRB1-binding site.

articles.headline: HbS allele frequency 6.72% in uncomplicated malaria in Chad
articles.title_original: Allele frequency of Hemoglobin S among patients with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in N'Djamena, Chad.
articles.details: Study of 320 patients with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria in N'Djamena, Chad found HbS allele frequency of 6.72%. No significant association was detected between HbS genotypes and parasite density. Authors note findings represent the first such data from Chad but may not be generalizable beyond the surveyed population.

articles.headline: Ivermectin MDA reduces scabies by 82% versus albendazole control
articles.title_original: Direct and indirect protection against scabies through ivermectin mass drug administration designed for malaria in Mozambique: a substudy nested within a cluster-randomised, controlled trial.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized trial in Mozambique (39 clusters, ~13 participants per arm) compared ivermectin 400 μg/kg to albendazole control for scabies. At 3 months, ivermectin reduced scabies prevalence from 8.10% to 2.11% versus 12.30% to 13.71% in controls (aOR 0.18, 95% CI 0.07-0.45, p=0.0002). Protection extended to indirectly exposed children <5 years (aOR 0.17 at 3 months and 0.21 at 6 months). No safety signals detected.

articles.headline: Review: liver stage immunity and malaria intervention strategies
articles.title_original: Exposing the hidden: establishing immunity to Plasmodium liver stage infection.
articles.details: Review highlighting immune mechanisms against Plasmodium liver stage, the critical gateway for parasite entry before blood-stage infection. Notes that robust immunity against pre-erythrocytic stages fails to develop during natural exposure in endemic areas. Discusses how expanding mechanistic knowledge is shaping current and future intervention strategies targeting the liver stage.

articles.headline: Case: cerebral malaria with sudden coma onset before fever
articles.title_original: When malaria hides: a case report of sudden coma without prior fever in cerebral malaria.
articles.details: Case report of 19-year-old woman presenting with sudden unconsciousness and rigor without antecedent fever, later developing fever during hospitalization. Rapid diagnostic test and microscopy confirmed P. falciparum infection. Patient was treated with intravenous artesunate followed by primaquine and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine, showing clinical improvement with confirmed parasite clearance.

articles.headline: Malaria knowledge highest in young, urban, educated Tanzanians
articles.title_original: Evaluating the General Public's Knowledge of Malaria: A Nationally Representative Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of 18,747 Tanzanians aged ≥15 years from the 2021-2022 Demographic and Health Survey examined malaria knowledge across demographics. Knowledge varied significantly by age (highest in 20-24 years, lowest >50 years, p<0.05), location (urban > rural), education level (secondary or higher associated with better scores), and gender (slight male advantage). Media exposure and mobile phone ownership were associated with increased malaria awareness.

articles.headline: HbC offers stronger malaria protection than HbS in Saudi children
articles.title_original: Distribution of HbS, HbC variants, ABO blood groups in relation to malaria infection among Saudi children in Jazan region.
articles.details: Case-control study of 587 Saudi children in Jazan compared genetic protective factors across malaria-free controls (n=196), uncomplicated malaria (n=215), and severe malaria (n=176, median age 4-5 years). HbS carriers showed reduced odds of severe malaria (OR=0.40, 95% CI 0.22-0.72, p=0.002), while HbC carriers demonstrated stronger protective associations (p<0.01). Total IgG and IgG subclass levels were significantly higher in uncomplicated versus severe malaria (p<0.001).

articles.headline: Adhesion-blocking antibodies protect against placental malaria
articles.title_original: A high-throughput assay to measure antibodies that block adhesion of Plasmodium falciparum infected erythrocytes to chondroitin sulfate A.
articles.details: Study of 466 pregnant Malawian women (321 P. falciparum-infected, 145 uninfected) developed a simplified high-throughput adhesion inhibition assay measuring antibodies blocking infected erythrocyte binding to placental chondroitin sulfate A. Adhesion-inhibitory antibodies were significantly higher in multigravidae versus primigravidae (46.3% vs 9.7% in infected women, p<0.001). Women without placental malaria had higher antibody levels (33.8% vs 12.5%, p=0.002), showing protection. Assay demonstrated strong reproducibility (r=0.82).

articles.headline: IPT with monthly DHP averts 70-90% school-child malaria cases
articles.title_original: Effect of malaria chemoprevention for school-age children across transmission archetypes: a modelling study.
articles.details: Transmission modeling study across three epidemiological archetypes tested intermittent preventive treatment (IPT) of school-age children with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHP), artesunate-amodiaquine, or sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-amodiaquine. Monthly DHP administration averted 70-90% of cases in school children (up to 2.0 cases per child per year) and 20-60% in younger children and adults (up to 0.5 cases per person per year), with greater community benefit at lower transmission levels (PfPR2-10 5-10%).

articles.headline: fikk, phist, surf genes implicate antigens in severe malaria pathogenesis
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum gene expression signatures and antibody profiling implicate the fikk, phist, and surf multigene families in severe malaria syndromes.
articles.details: Mali case-control RNA-seq study compared parasite gene expression in cerebral malaria, severe malarial anemia (SMA), and concurrent disease versus uncomplicated malaria controls. Cerebral malaria and concurrent disease showed significant differential expression of fikk, phist, and surf multigene families implicated in cytoadherence and erythrocytic remodeling. Children with severe malaria developed antibodies to subsets of these antigens, suggesting exposure during acute illness and potential as vaccine or therapeutic targets.

articles.headline: Review: molecular markers of P. falciparum drug resistance mechanisms
articles.title_original: A review on molecular markers of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Comprehensive review of P. falciparum drug resistance mechanisms and molecular markers. Covers Kelch13 mutations (artemisinin), cytochrome b mutations (atovaquone), CRT/MDR1/plasmepsin changes (chloroquine), DHFR/DHPS mutations (antifolates), and MRP1/NHE1 mutations (amino alcohols). Notes P. falciparum has developed resistance to nearly all antimalarials. Recommends future research using transcriptomic and genomic technologies, proteomic studies, multi-drug combination therapies, and strengthened global monitoring systems.

articles.headline: Pfhrp2/3 deletions: challenges and solutions for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Overcoming the challenges of Plasmodium falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 and 3 deletions in malaria diagnosis and control.
articles.details: Systematic review of 83 studies (2018–2024) on P. falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 and 3 (Pfhrp2/3) deficient parasites. The review highlights significant diagnostic and control challenges posed by Pfhrp2/3 deletions and identifies potential solutions, including alternative diagnostic approaches and novel rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) targeting multiple antigens.

articles.headline: P. vivax MSP-3α shows highest genetic diversity in Pakistan
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphisms of merozoite surface protein-3α in Plasmodium vivax isolates from Pakistan.
articles.details: Genetic analysis of pvmsp-3α in 129 Pakistani P. vivax isolates (2020–2021) revealed substantial genetic diversity driven by size polymorphisms in block I and amino acid changes/recombination in block II. Comparative analysis showed Pakistani pvmsp-3α had the highest genetic diversity among global populations studied, with haplotype network analysis indicating substantial heterogeneity in global P. vivax populations.

articles.headline: PvCyRPA binds human erythrocytes, supporting P. vivax vaccine potential
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax cysteine-rich protective antigen (PvCyRPA), an important element for vaccine strategies targeting P. vivax, interacts with human erythrocyte surface.
articles.details: Functional study demonstrating that recombinant P. vivax cysteine-rich protective antigen (PvCyRPA) binds to both CD71+ reticulocytes and normocytes, confirming erythrocyte interaction. Over 90% of sera from malaria-exposed individuals recognized PvCyRPA, indicating strong antigenicity. These findings provide first evidence of PvCyRPA's functional relevance and support its potential as a vaccine candidate.

articles.headline: P. yoelii infection inhibits colon cancer growth in mice
articles.title_original: Plasmodium yoelii infection inhibits colon cancer in mice via modulation of hypoxia-inducible factor-1α expression.
articles.details: Experimental study in tumor-bearing BALB/c mice: P. yoelii-infected group (0.2 mL, 5×10⁶/mL inoculum) showed significant tumor shrinkage by day 21 (P<0.001) compared to tumor-only controls. In infected group tumors, HIF-1α and VEGFA were downregulated (P<0.01) while VHL and p53 were upregulated (P<0.01), implicating these pathways in tumor suppression.

articles.headline: Malaria and neglected infections prevalent in pregnant migrants
