articles.title_original: Malaria and other neglected infections in pregnant migrants: A retrospective study in Northern Italy.
articles.details: Retrospective screening study of 85 pregnant migrant women (median age 28) from 22 countries at an Italian hospital (Feb 2023–Mar 2025). Malaria detected in 5/29 tested (17.2%), all asymptomatic or mildly symptomatic. Additional neglected infections: Schistosoma serology 10/36 (27.8%), Strongyloides 5/84 (5.9%), HTLV-1/2 in 3/60 (5.0%). Routine screening identified 3 syphilis, 1 chronic HBV, 1 past HCV.

articles.headline: ART-heme adduct: biomarker for artemisinin resistance mechanism
articles.title_original: Elucidating artemisinin (ART)-heme as a biomarker for ART resistance and ATP-dependent transport of ART in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: In vitro mechanistic study comparing ART-sensitive (Pf3D7/PfDd2) and ART-resistant (Pf6320 K13-C580Y) P. falciparum strains. ART-heme adduct identified as major metabolite with weak antimalarial activity (µM level); formation was rapid and dose-dependent (50–450 nM) in sensitive parasites but concentration-independent in resistant parasites. ART transport is ATP-dependent (inhibited by orthovanadate); verapamil (efflux inhibitor) showed synergistic activity with ART.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies ART-heme biomarker and ATP transport mechanism

articles.headline: T follicular helper cells and malaria immunity: current review
articles.title_original: T follicular helper cell development during malaria.
articles.authors: Antonetti RM, Shah M, Butler NS
articles.details: Review synthesizing knowledge on phenotype and function of Tfh cell subsets induced during Plasmodium infection. While Th1 and Tfh cells are critical for phagocyte activation and humoral immunity, sterilizing protective responses rarely develop. Highlights how malaria-associated physiologic changes induce nonconventional Tfh subsets and inefficient memory responses, identifies knowledge gaps regarding mixed-phenotype Tfh populations and Tfh memory development.

articles.headline: Single-cell mapping reveals P. falciparum development and transmission biology
articles.title_original: Mapping Plasmodium transitions and interactions in the Anopheles female.
articles.details: Single-cell RNA sequencing of parasites and mosquito cells across developmental stages revealed key P. falciparum transitions in the Anopheles midgut. The transcription factor PfSIP2 was identified as essential for sporozoite infection of human hepatocytes. Parasites preferentially interact with midgut progenitor cells during epithelium crossing; tight connections form between late oocysts and muscle cells. Findings confirmed across multiple mosquito-parasite combinations including field-derived parasites.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies PfSIP2 as essential for hepatocyte infection

articles.headline: Novel antimalarial compound with protein-synthesis inhibition mechanism
articles.title_original: A Novel Antimalarial Agent that Inhibits Protein Synthesis in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Compound 31 (2-hydroxyphenyl benzamide scaffold) shows potent activity against blood-stage and mature sexual stages of P. falciparum with no human cell toxicity. Resistance selection identified novel pfmdr1 point mutation (confirmed by CRISPR/Cas9). No cross-resistance with first-line antimalarials. Proteomics revealed primary mode of action: direct binding to cytosolic ribosomal subunits inhibiting protein synthesis.
articles.notable_reason: Novel antimalarial with unique MOA addressing drug resistance

articles.headline: Sickle cell trait paradox: age-dependent malaria protection in Guinea-Bissau
articles.title_original: Association between sickle cell trait and Plasmodium falciparum infection: evidence for age-specific protection in a cross-sectional study in Guinea-Bissau.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 601 samples from Guinea-Bissau's 2017 survey examined SCT (HbAS, 32.0% prevalence) association with P. falciparum. SCT carriers were more likely to have microscopic infection in unstratified analysis (OR=2.3, 95% CI=1.4–3.5) and age-stratified analysis (<5 years OR=2.3, 95% CI=1.0–5.2; 15–24 years OR=5.0, 95% CI=1.8–13.8). However, infected children <5 years with SCT had lower parasitemia and higher hemoglobin.

articles.headline: Sex determination mechanisms in P. falciparum gametocytes reviewed
articles.title_original: Pink or blue: sex determination in malaria gametocytes.
articles.details: Review of molecular mechanisms regulating sex determination in malaria gametocytes at two critical checkpoints. First checkpoint: chromatin remodeling activates master regulator AP2-G, triggering transcriptional cascade that commits parasites to sexual development. Second checkpoint: post-transcriptional processes—including transcript stabilization/suppression via RNA-binding proteins—guide branching into male or female gametocytes through threshold-trait-dependent mechanisms.

articles.headline: GFAP as cerebral malaria biomarker: systematic review of evidence
articles.title_original: Current status of the GFAP's potentials in cerebral malaria, a systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review (24 studies: 9 human, 15 experimental per PRISMA) examining glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP) in cerebral malaria. Experimental models showed consistent GFAP elevation in brain and retinal tissues correlating with astrocyte activation. Human studies showed variability; several reported no significant plasma GFAP increase in cerebral malaria patients vs. controls. Variations in sample type, disease severity, patient age, and methods contributed to inconsistencies. Authors conclude GFAP shows promise for astrocyte activation assessment but lacks diagnostic specificity and clinical consistency.

articles.headline: Review: P. falciparum remodels host RBC proteome during blood stage
articles.title_original: Crosstalk between malaria and host proteome during the intraerythrocytic developmental cycle.
articles.details: This review examines how P. falciparum engages, modifies, and imports specific RBC proteins during blood stage development. The parasites remodel RBC cytoplasm and surface, altering deformability. The article discusses potential for targeting host proteome engagements to design novel host-directed antimalarial therapies.

articles.headline: Vesicle adaptor proteins AP-1/AP-3/AP-4 essential for parasite survival
articles.title_original: Vesicle adaptors in malaria parasites show conservation and flexibility of protein sorting machinery.
articles.details: AP-1, AP-3, and AP-4 adaptors are critical for P. falciparum survival. AP-1 enabled intracellular growth, specialized organelle biogenesis, and invasive progeny formation via sortilin receptor. AP-3 and AP-4 were required for host cell invasion. Proxiomes revealed the parasite's adaptor sorting machinery shares remarkable conservation with evolutionarily distant organisms, yet shows unconventional features including tepsin with AP-1 and clathrin with AP-4.

articles.headline: CRT protein expressed across malaria parasite life stages in digestive vacuole
articles.title_original: Stage-dependent expression and vacuolar localization of Plasmodium berghei chloroquine resistance transporter (CRT).
articles.details: This study profiled chloroquine resistance transporter (CRT) expression and localization during P. berghei life cycle progression via CRT-mCherry parasites. CRT was expressed during asexual blood stage growth and localized to the hemozoin-containing digestive vacuole, remained abundant in gametocytes and ookinetes, switched off during sporogony and early liver infection, then reappeared in mature liver stages. These findings reveal ongoing digestive vacuole function beyond asexual erythrocyte propagation.

articles.headline: Clinical risk scoring system predicts artesunate treatment failure in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Development of a clinical risk scoring system for artesunate treatment failure prediction in malaria patients.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Among 98 malaria patients receiving IV artesunate in South Korea (2014-2023), treatment failure occurred in 12.2%. Multivariable logistic regression identified female sex, parasitemia >5%, and impaired consciousness as independent risk factors. A risk scoring system predicted failure probabilities: 0 points=5%, 1=16%, 2=41%, 3=72%, 4=90% (AUROC 0.768, 95% CI 0.605-0.931). Authors propose this tool for identifying high-risk patients requiring intensified monitoring, though findings from a non-endemic cohort should be cautiously extrapolated to endemic populations.

articles.headline: Review: antimalarial drug resistance mechanisms and urgent need for surveillance
articles.title_original: The evolving threat of drug-resistant malaria in a globalized world.
articles.details: This review highlights molecular mechanisms of drug resistance against malaria, emphasizing P. falciparum's status as the most lethal parasite species responsible for the highest death toll. Despite availability of antimalarial drugs and preventive measures, malaria continues to threaten millions due to spread of drug-resistant parasites. Authors stress the importance of understanding drug mechanisms of action and resistance genetics, and emphasize urgent need for real-time surveillance systems to inform improved treatment and control strategies.

articles.headline: Review: malaria drives telomere shortening and cellular senescence in immune cells
articles.title_original: Malaria infection and telomere length: A review.
articles.details: This review examines telomere dynamics in malaria pathogenesis. Acute malaria infections initiate rapid telomere degradation, promote accelerated cellular senescence, and suppress telomerase expression with possible partial recovery during treatment. Prolonged Plasmodium exposure in endemic regions, especially with coexisting infections, exacerbates telomere shortening and increases age-related disease susceptibility. Authors discuss oxidative stress and inflammatory mediators' roles and propose telomere length as a potential biomarker for malaria severity and treatment outcomes.

articles.headline: Malawi: chloroquine-sensitive parasites emerge; lumefantrine and SP resistance rates high
articles.title_original: Emergence of chloroquine-sensitive Plasmodium falciparum and rising resistance to first-line artemisinin partner drugs in Malawi.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of 1,582 P. falciparum isolates from Malawi (December 2020-June 2021) assessed drug resistance markers using Sanger sequencing. No pfk13 or pfcrt mutations detected, supporting continued artemisinin and chloroquine susceptibility. However, pfmdr1-NFD (lumefantrine resistance marker) was present in 42.9% of isolates, and the quadruple pfdhfr-pfdhps mutant haplotype (SP resistance) in 87.5% of isolates. Absence of pfcrt mutations and wild-type pfmdr1 alleles suggests CQ-sensitive parasite re-emergence. Authors recommend continuous molecular surveillance and clinical efficacy studies.
articles.notable_reason: High lumefantrine and SP resistance threatens ACT and IPTp

articles.headline: Novel FIKK kinase-based malaria diagnostic achieves 95% accuracy in samples
articles.title_original: Antigenic determinant analysis of FIKK Kinase family to identify novel candidates for diagnosis of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: This study developed a chimeric FIKK antigen by selecting highly antigenic, species-specific B- and T-cell epitopes from six P. falciparum FIKK kinases. Purified anti-FIKK antibodies demonstrated high sensitivity (3 μM detection limit for FIKK9.1), selectivity for malaria parasites with no cross-reactivity to host blood or non-Plasmodium proteins. Validation with mock patient samples demonstrated semi-quantitative parasite load detection with 95.45% average accuracy. Authors propose FIKK kinase-based RDTs as next-generation diagnostic targets.

articles.headline: AMP once-daily dosing equivalent to AL twice-daily in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Arterolane and piperaquine Vs. artemether and lumefantrine in uncomplicated malaria: A randomized study in Nigeria.
articles.details: A phase IV randomized multicenter trial in Nigeria (N=350, ages 12-65 years) compared Arterolane-Piperaquine (150+750mg once daily) to Artemether-lumefantrine (80+480mg twice daily) in uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria. PCR-corrected ACPR was 100% on Day 28 and sustained to Day 42 in both groups. PCR-uncorrected ACPR rates were comparable: AMP 98.2% vs AL 99.4% (Day 28, p=0.352). No drug-related adverse events. Authors conclude AMP demonstrates comparable efficacy and safety with once-daily dosing convenience.

articles.headline: PBPK modeling reveals lysosomal sequestration drives pyronaridine accumulation
articles.title_original: Importance of Lysosomal Trapping and Plasmodium Parasite Infection on the Pharmacokinetics of Pyronaridine: A Physiologically Based Pharmacokinetic Model-Based Study.
articles.details: This PBPK modeling study analyzed data from 52 healthy individuals and 25 malaria patients to characterize pyronaridine pharmacokinetics. Lysosomal sequestration was essential for capturing drug distribution. In malaria patients, low hemoglobin and drug accumulation in parasite-containing RBC compartments enabled adequate blood PK description. Simulations showed free pyronaridine concentrations in parasite compartments were >10-fold higher than whole blood. Higher parasitemia on day 1 increased AUC and Cmax, but subsequent hemoglobin decrease reduced exposure on later days.

articles.headline: Proteasome inhibitor ONX-0914 shows potent activity against drug-resistant parasites
articles.title_original: Dual-targeting proteasome inhibitor ONX-0914 demonstrates potent antiplasmodial activity for malaria treatment.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: ONX-0914, a dual-targeting proteasome inhibitor, showed potent antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum strains including CQ- and ART-sensitive and -resistant isolates (IC50 <50 nM in vitro), with low human cell toxicity (CC50 >100 μM). It exhibited synergistic activity with ART. In vivo (P. berghei ANKA), ONX-0914 suppressed parasites by >95% after 4 days, with ED50 of 7.62 mg/kg (oral) and 6.52 mg/kg (IP), <2% recrudescence rate, favorable PK (AUC >20,960 h·μg/ml, bioavailability 23.83%), and reduced organ lesions.

articles.headline: ICAM-1 and CD36 predominate in cerebral malaria sequestration
articles.title_original: Mapping the expression of endothelial adhesion receptors for Plasmodium falciparum-infected erythrocytes in fatal cerebral malaria in Malawian children.
articles.details: Post-mortem study of 50 Malawian children (27 cerebral malaria, 23 non-malarial deaths) examined 5 cytoadhesion receptors in 12 brain regions. ICAM-1 was most widely expressed, followed by CD36, VCAM-1, E-selectin, and thrombospondin. Colocalization of receptor-expressing microvessels with infected RBCs was highest for ICAM-1 and CD36 and greatest in frontal lobe. Sequestration ratios approached 100% across all receptors and regions. Cerebral endothelial receptor expression exceeded expression in other organs.

articles.headline: TLR4 and TLR9 variants associated with P. vivax parasitaemia and symptoms
articles.title_original: New Evidence of TLR4 and TLR9 Variants Influencing Parasitaemia and Symptoms of Plasmodium vivax Infection.
articles.details: Study of 148 individuals with symptomatic uncomplicated P. vivax malaria in Brazilian Amazon examined TLR polymorphisms. TLR9 rs352140TT homozygotes had higher parasitaemia (p=0.034); TLR4 rs1927911GG homozygotes had higher clinical index (p=0.018) than A carriers. First description of rs1927911 association with P. vivax symptoms in Amazonian population.

articles.headline: ACKR1/Duffy antigen emerging roles beyond malaria parasites
articles.title_original: The emerging roles of ACKR1/Duffy antigen beyond malaria.
articles.authors: Shaikh R, Ghosh K, Gorakshakar A
articles.details: Perspective reviewing ACKR1 (atypical chemokine receptor 1, formerly Duffy antigen), traditionally recognized for P. vivax erythrocyte invasion. Recent research reveals emerging roles in inflammation, cancer biology, hematopoiesis, and other diseases with potential therapeutic applications.

articles.headline: STEVOR multiepitope fusion vaccine: broad HLA coverage and immunogenicity
articles.title_original: Conserved multiepitopes in Plasmodium falciparum STEVORs enable rational design of a fusion antigen vaccine construct with broad immunogenicity.
articles.details: Computational approach identified conserved multiepitopes in 4 STEVOR antigens across 138 clinical isolates and 342 global strains linked to severe P. falciparum malaria. Designed multiepitope fusion antigen (MEFA) achieved 97.15% global HLA coverage and elicited CD4+/CD8+ T-cell and IgG responses in mice. MEFA recognized more anti-STEVOR IgG in nonsevere versus severe malaria cases.

articles.headline: Lepidobotrys staudtii extract shows potent in vitro antiplasmodial activity
articles.title_original: In vitro antiplasmodial activities of extract and fractions from Lepidobotrys staudtii against sensitive and resistant blood-stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Plant stem bark extract and fractions tested against P. falciparum drug-sensitive (Pf3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (PfINDO) strains. Ethyl acetate and hexane fractions most potent (IC50 3.4-3.73 μg/mL) without cytotoxicity to 500 μg/mL. Ethyl acetate fraction acted rapidly (12 h), arrested parasite development, and synergized with standard antimalarials.

articles.headline: Piperaquine binding to resistant PfCRT and PfDHFR revealed by molecular dynamics
articles.title_original: Computational investigation of mutations in PfCRT and PfDHFR proteins for emerging resistance of Plasmodium falciparum to antimalarial drugs.
articles.details: Molecular docking and dynamics simulations analyzed interactions of mutated PfCRT and PfDHFR with 9 antimalarial drugs including piperaquine. PfCRT-K76A piperaquine showed strong binding (-9.5 kcal/mol) with favorable solvent accessibility. PfDHFR-N51I-piperaquine showed stronger binding (-10.8 kcal/mol) but higher structural fluctuation (RMSD 4.491±1.462 nm), suggesting restricted ligand accommodation.

articles.headline: PfHsp90 C-terminal nucleotide binding site identified as drug target
articles.title_original: Investigation of a cryptic ligand binding site on Plasmodium falciparum Hsp90.
articles.details: Characterized ligand binding to C-terminal domain of Plasmodium falciparum Hsp90 using in silico and in vitro methods. Found specific interaction with ATP, dATP, and ADP (but not AMP or GTP) at C-terminal site. Computational and mutagenesis studies interrogated molecular basis of interaction, providing foundation for selective inhibitor development.

articles.headline: pfdhps 431V mutation emerging in West and Central African P. falciparum
articles.title_original: A novel intron variant is associated with emerging pfdhps mutant haplotypes in West and Central African Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Sequencing of 929 blood samples from Nigeria, Cameroon, Tanzania, DRC, and Côte d'Ivoire identified non-coding intron variant (a548383t) in pfpppk-dhps strongly associated with pfdhps 431V mutation and SP-resistance haplotypes. Strongest association with VAGKGS haplotype (OR=211.7, P<0.001); reduced genetic diversity in 431V-positive variants. Functional significance requires further investigation.

articles.headline: Malaria guideline adherence varies by province in DRC ICCM
articles.title_original: Assessment of adherence to the National Malaria Guidelines in treatment management of malaria among children under five in integrated community case management in the DRC.
articles.details: Assessment of 2,326 children from 30 community case management sites in DRC found 63.8% malaria prevalence. 94.8% of diagnosed cases received rapid diagnostic test; 93.4% with malaria received treatment. However, 18.5% without malaria received treatment. Adherence to RDT testing varied by province: Kasai Oriental greater (AOR=1.89), Tanganyika lower (AOR=0.40).

articles.headline: HDAC inhibitors potent in vitro against asexual P. falciparum parasites
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of pentyloxyamide-based histone deacetylase inhibitors against Plasmodium falciparum parasites.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Panel of 24 pentyloxyamide-based HDAC inhibitors screened against asexual P. falciparum. Lead compound (4o) showed IC50 of 20 nM with >250-fold selectivity over human cells and activity against exoerythrocytic stage (IC50 24 nM). Compound caused histone H4 hyperacetylation and stabilized PfHDAC1. Lacked activity against gametocytes (IC50 > 2 μM).

articles.headline: P. vivax resurgence ongoing in multiple Asian and Pacific countries
articles.title_original: Indigenous Plasmodium vivax upsurge in the Eastern Mediterranean, Western Pacific, and South East Asia regions - beyond the constant culpability of climate change, COVID-19, and armed conflicts.
articles.details: Analysis of epidemiological data from Eastern Mediterranean, Southeast Asia, and Western Pacific found P. vivax increases in >50% of countries. Papua New Guinea and Yemen showed consistent rises since 2015; Indonesia and Pakistan experienced sharp increases 2020-2023 (100% and 83.4% respectively). Thailand unlikely to achieve WHO E-2025 elimination. Factors beyond climate and COVID include chloroquine resistance.

articles.headline: Microbial metabolite library yields potent antiplasmodial compounds
articles.title_original: Screening and hit evaluation of a microbial metabolite library against the pathogenic Plasmodium falciparum and Toxoplasma gondii parasites.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Screened 812 microbial natural products from BioAustralis Discovery library against P. falciparum and T. gondii. Found 219 compounds inhibiting P. falciparum ≥80% at 2 μg/mL and 149 with equivalent T. gondii activity. After cytotoxicity filtering, identified 4 compound groups with low nanomolar IC50. Monocyclic thiazole peptides and pleuromutilins showed best combination of potency and low toxicity.

articles.headline: Flap endonuclease NMR assignments enable PfFEN drug target studies
articles.title_original: (1)H, (15)N and (13)C backbone resonance assignments of flap endonuclease from Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Reported 1H, 15N, and 13C backbone resonance assignments for 90% of Plasmodium falciparum flap endonuclease (PfFEN349) using heteronuclear multidimensional NMR. Assigned 298 out of 337 theoretical backbone amide resonances in substrate-free state. Chemical shift analysis predicts secondary structure in good agreement with AlphaFold model, providing foundation for PfFEN inhibitor design.

articles.headline: Most HDP recombinant constructs fail; one successful but inactive
articles.title_original: Recombinant expression and purification of Plasmodium heme detoxification protein in E. coli: Challenges and discoveries.
articles.details: Attempted recombinant expression of Plasmodium heme detoxification protein (HDP) in E. coli using multiple strategies. Only one construct (HDPpf-C10 with 44-residue N-terminal truncation) was successfully expressed in soluble form. However, this construct showed no detectable heme-to-Hz transformation activity, revealing that the N-terminal region is essential for HDP function. Findings suggest the flexible N-terminal region is critical for both heme binding and conversion to hemozoin.

articles.headline: SP resistance reduces IPTp efficacy; dose-response varies by region
articles.title_original: The impact of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance on the effectiveness of intermittent preventive treatment for the prevention of malaria in pregnancy in Africa: an updated systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 122 studies (148,693 participants) assessing sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance impact on IPTp effectiveness in pregnancy. For West/Central Africa, three vs. two doses showed aRR 0.71 (95% CI 0.65-0.78) in very low resistance but 0.83 (0.72-0.95) in low resistance. For East/Southern Africa, aRR was 0.63 (0.57-0.69) with moderate resistance, declining to 0.93 (0.85-1.01) with very high resistance. Clear dose-response threshold effects demonstrated resistance-dependent efficacy loss.
articles.notable_reason: Major meta-analysis demonstrating SP-resistance thresholds driving IPTp-SP efficacy loss

articles.headline: Pyruvate kinase deficiency impairs parasite-induced RBC remodeling; possible resistance mechanism
articles.title_original: Metabolic Rigidity as a Mechanical Barrier to Malaria: Flickering Loss in PKLR-Deficient Erythrocytes.
articles.details: High-speed flickering spectroscopy compared ATP-dependent membrane properties in PKLR-mutant versus healthy erythrocytes. PKLR-deficient RBCs showed reduced ATP-dependent flickering amplitude and membrane fluidity. When infected with Plasmodium yoelii, these metabolically rigid cells exhibited mechanical properties that hindered parasite-induced membrane remodeling, suggesting a mechanobiological barrier against malaria infection.

articles.headline: Fetal Doppler monitoring during severe malaria and mechanical ventilation in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria and Mechanical Ventilation in Pregnancy: The Crucial Role of Fetal Doppler in Monitoring Fetal Well-Being.
articles.details: Case report of 33-year-old woman at 33 weeks gestation presenting with five-day fever, myalgia, and chills, rapidly developing respiratory failure requiring mechanical ventilation. Continuous fetal monitoring via Doppler showed reassuring parameters with normal umbilical artery and middle cerebral artery flow patterns, no signs of fetal hypoxia. Case highlights feasibility of fetal Doppler surveillance in pregnant patients undergoing mechanical ventilation for severe malaria.

articles.headline: Broflanilide IRS achieves 98.7% mortality against pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae; superior to pirimiphos-methyl
articles.title_original: Community evaluation of VECTRON™ T500 (broflanilide) for indoor residual spraying for malaria vector control in Siaya county, Kenya.
articles.details: Two-arm study in Kenya comparing VECTRON T500 broflanilide (100 mg a.i./m²) to Actellic 300CS (1 g a.i./m²) over 6 months. Against pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae Bungoma strain, VECTRON achieved 98.73% mortality (95% CI 97.95-99.51%) vs. 80.22% for Actellic (p<0.001). VECTRON maintained >95% efficacy throughout 6 months while Actellic declined below 80% after month 4. No adverse events in VECTRON households vs. 8% in Actellic households.
articles.notable_reason: Novel GABA-receptor mechanism achieves superior efficacy against pyrethroid-resistant vectors

articles.headline: Systematic review identifies 45 Anopheles species across Eastern Mediterranean region; Iran leads research
articles.title_original: Diversity and spatial distribution of malaria vectors in the WHO Eastern Mediterranean region from 1900 to 2024: a systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 276 studies from 3,637 publications documenting Anopheles diversity in 18 Eastern Mediterranean countries from 1900-2024. Identified 45 Anopheles species with research concentrated on five: An. stephensi (102 studies), An. superpictus (57), An. dthali (49), An. culicifacies (47), and An. maculipennis (45). Iran contributed largest share (122 studies), followed by Pakistan (35), Sudan (31), and Egypt (30). Findings emphasize ongoing need for surveillance to identify distribution shifts.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistance K13-R561H shows eastward spread in Tanzania; prevalence 5.5%-11.3%
articles.title_original: Temporal Trends in Artemisinin Partial Resistance and Other Antimalarial Drug Mutations in Plasmodium falciparum from Kagera Region, Northwestern Tanzania, 2021-2023.
articles.details: Longitudinal molecular surveillance in Tanzania's Kagera region (2021-2023) genotyping 2,826 P. falciparum isolates. K13 mutation R561H prevalence rose from 5.5% (2021) to 11.3% (2022), stabilizing at 6.9% (2023), with geographic expansion from border districts (14-26%) to new areas (Muleba 5.0%, Bukoba 0.7%). Additional candidate mutations (V568G, P441L) detected at low frequencies. Antifolate resistance entrenched with high-level DHFR I164L (38.1%) and DHPS A581G (48.1%) in eastern districts.
articles.notable_reason: Ongoing geographic expansion of artemisinin-resistance K13-R561H threatening ACT efficacy

articles.headline: Two P. vivax hypnozoite proteins (IESI-1/2) suppress schizogony; explain dormancy mechanism
articles.title_original: Two Plasmodium vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins inhibit liver stage replication.
articles.details: Identified two P. vivax hypnozoite-expressed RNA-binding proteins (IESI-1 and IESI-2) that suppress liver stage replication. When individually expressed in P. yoelii liver stages, each protein blocked schizogony with parasites remaining small and uninucleate. RNA-binding domain screening revealed enrichment of specific motifs targeted by these proteins. Findings provide unprecedented functional evidence for a potential molecular mechanism of hypnozoite formation and quiescence.
articles.notable_reason: First functional evidence for hypnozoite dormancy mechanism; potential drug target

articles.headline: P. falciparum schizogony dynamics remain unclear; may reveal new antimalarial vulnerabilities
articles.title_original: Unusual replication dynamics during Plasmodium falciparum schizogony.
articles.details: Review of P. falciparum replication via schizogony—asynchronous DNA replication and nuclear division followed by mass cytokinesis producing numerous daughter cells. Despite recent advances in single-molecule techniques, clarity on replication dynamics remains elusive. Dynamics likely shaped by highly AT-rich genome and unique schizogony pressures. Understanding these pressures may reveal parasite-specific vulnerabilities and inform development of new antimalarials.

articles.headline: High malaria knowledge in community but no post-intervention KAP change
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitude and practice in high endemic settings for malaria: a cross-sectional study in a rural community in central Mozambique.
articles.details: Cross-sectional KAP study in Tambai community, Mozambique before (May 2021) and after (June 2022) intervention. Survey of household heads showed high knowledge of symptoms, transmission, prevention, and treatment (96.8% pre-intervention, 91.1% post-intervention). No significant differences in KAP between intervention and control groups. Authors concluded high malaria burden not explained by knowledge gaps, suggesting other factors drive disease.

articles.headline: Housing quality and WASH infrastructure protect children against recurrent malaria
articles.title_original: Sociodemographic and ecological factors associated with malaria and malaria recurrence risk in children in Msambweni, Kwale County, Kenya.
articles.details: Child cohort (1-18 years, 2014-2018) at Msambweni County Referral Hospital; 2,371 children with 2,610 febrile visits; 40.6% with malaria, 2.5% with recurrent malaria (≥2 infections). Risk factors: older age (2-5 years aRR 1.38, ≥5 years aRR 1.57 vs <2 years). Protective factors: higher maternal education (aRR 0.66), piped/well water (aRR 0.78), electricity/solar (aRR 0.61). Recurrent malaria linked to poor housing, dirt floors, proximity to rice fields.

articles.headline: Carboxylesterase Coeae6g mediates cross-resistance to four insecticides; predicts field resistance
articles.title_original: Predictive chemoproteomics and functional validation reveal Coeae6g-mediated insecticide cross-resistance in the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: Activity-based protein profiling of susceptible An. gambiae identified 18 active serine hydrolases using pirimiphos-methyl-oxon bioactive metabolite. Carboxylesterase Coeae6g was functionally validated to confer resistance to pirimiphos-methyl and mediate cross-resistance to malathion, bendiocarb, and permethrin. Framework bridges genotype-phenotype gaps, aligns with emerging field genomic signatures, and provides scalable surveillance tool for predicting resistance and guiding insecticide management.

articles.headline: Placental malaria prevalence 17% globally, highest in Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Placental malaria: a systematic review and meta-analysis of global burden, risk factors, and maternal and foetal outcomes.
articles.details: Systematic review of 50 studies (45 from Sub-Saharan Africa) assessing Plasmodium falciparum placental malaria. Global prevalence 17% (95% CI 12-21), rising to 23% in histology-based studies and 19% in Sub-Saharan Africa. Prevalence 45% in HIV-positive women versus 17% in HIV-negative women. Stillbirth odds ratio 3.81 (95% CI 1.22-11.94). Wide confidence intervals limited conclusions on other fetal outcomes.

articles.headline: Age-structured migration drives malaria transmission dynamics in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Impact of age-structured migration on malaria burden: A modelling-empirical analysis in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Compartmental mathematical model incorporating age-specific migration patterns, validated with multiyear case data from Ouagadougou and migration records from Kossi province, Burkina Faso. Model explained incidence patterns across age-cohorts and revealed migration-driven nonlinear amplification and synchrony in infection prevalence. Findings support design of targeted intervention strategies in regions with pronounced seasonal migration.

articles.headline: Climate variability impacts malaria risk as strongly as interventions in Kenya
articles.title_original: Climate, Interventions, and Malaria Outcomes in a Warming World: Towards Climate-Smart Malaria Control in Kenya.
articles.details: Evidence review from Kenya showing climate variability exerts comparable or stronger effects on malaria outcomes than vector control and case management interventions at local scales. Transmission displays non-linear, lagged relationships with temperature and rainfall. Authors propose climate-smart interventions with dynamic targeting according to observed climate patterns and locally-tailored forecasts embedded in routine planning.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic congenital malaria detected in 12% of cord blood in Guatemala
articles.title_original: Prevalence of submicroscopic congenital malaria and associated risk factors in an area of low endemicity of Guatemala.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study (2009-2011) of 442 cord samples from pregnant women in Guatemala; 12.2% positive for submicroscopic Plasmodium (95% CI 9.3-15.6%) by qPCR. Nested case-control (54 cases, 51 controls) found submicroscopic maternal infection strongly associated with congenital infection (adjusted OR 7.55, 95% CI 1.84-51.5) and gravidity increased risk (adjusted OR 1.49, 95% CI 1.13-2.04).

articles.headline: P. knowlesi transmission shaped by climate change and land-use alterations
articles.title_original: Plasmodium knowlesi and global changes: socio-ecological drivers and challenges.
articles.details: Review of Plasmodium knowlesi malaria in Southeast Asia addressing how climate change, deforestation, and land-use changes influence transmission dynamics and facilitate zoonotic transmission from simian hosts to humans. Climate variability affects temperature, precipitation, mosquito habitats, and human exposure. Attitude-behavior gap limits effectiveness of awareness campaigns. Human-macaque-mosquito interactions intensifying due to environmental and developmental changes.

articles.headline: Combined IPTp-SP and ITNs highly cost-effective for maternal malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness of intermittent preventive therapy and insecticide-treated bed nets for malaria prevention among pregnant women in Jos North, Plateau State, Nigeria.
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis of 1,000 pregnant women in Nigeria comparing IPTp-SP (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine) alone versus combined IPTp-SP plus insecticide-treated nets. Combined intervention averted 15.63 disability-adjusted life years with incremental cost of US$100.19, yielding incremental cost-effectiveness ratio of US$2.12 per disability-adjusted life year averted. Combined approach had ~60% probability of being cost-effective at willingness-to-pay threshold of US$150 per disability-adjusted life year.

articles.headline: RDT misrecording 5-7% positive, 1-4% negative across sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Characteristics of healthcare workers and health facilities associated with inaccurate recording of malaria rapid diagnostic test results: a multi-country study.
articles.details: Observational study in 64 sub-Saharan African health facilities across 4 countries documented that among >100,000 rapid diagnostic tests performed by 499 healthcare workers, 5.1-7.3% were misrecorded as positive and 0.7-3.7% as negative. Overall agreement between external panel interpretation and facility records ranged 90.2-94.3%. Misrecording associated with healthcare worker characteristics (age, education, knowledge, cadre), facility-level factors (volume, test positivity rate), and country context.

articles.headline: Novel bite prevention tools show high acceptability among Cambodian forest-exposed populations
articles.title_original: Evaluating the acceptability and feasibility of new mosquito bite prevention tools in a "forest pack" to support malaria elimination in Cambodia.
articles.details: Implementation science evaluation in Cambodia distributing 28,000 forest packs (volatile pyrethroid spatial repellent, topical repellent) and insecticide-treated clothing to 2,935 high-risk individuals across 2 districts. Product acceptability was high (96% spatial repellent, 93% topical repellent, 100% insecticide-treated clothing) among forest dwellers and goers. Community delivery through trusted health workers and behavioral communication were key to adoption, though insecticide-treated clothing coverage remained low (1-40%).

articles.headline: P. vivax dbp gene amplification varies across Amazon regions, 25% in west
articles.title_original: The pattern of gene amplification of members of the Plasmodium vivax erythrocyte binding-like proteins family across the Amazon rainforest.
articles.details: Molecular analysis of 191 Plasmodium vivax isolates from 3 Amazon regions examined copy number variation in dbp and ebp2 genes (vaccine candidates). dbp amplification varied geographically: 25% of isolates in western Amazon with up to 8 gene copies versus 2-9% in southwestern and eastern regions (2-3 copies). ebp2 amplification less frequent (15% in western, 1-4% in other regions). No association between gene copy number variation and DARC-positive host genotypes.

articles.headline: Repellent awareness 64% but consistent use only 8% in Uganda
articles.title_original: Knowledge, attitudes, practices and determinants of mosquito repellent use for malaria prevention in Nangabo Ward, Wakiso District, Uganda.
articles.details: Cross-sectional mixed-methods survey of 185 household heads in Uganda found 63.8% aware of mosquito repellents and 59.5% had positive attitudes, yet only 8.2% reported consistent use. Knowledge gaps were substantial: only 38.4% knew repellents safe for children under 5 years, 35.7% for pregnant women. Multivariable analysis identified age ≥60 years (adjusted OR 2.50), large households 8-10 members (adjusted OR 2.59), and tertiary education (adjusted OR 0.42) as predictors. Cost and lack of policy support were major barriers.

articles.headline: Larviciding reduced Anopheles larvae 50-80% across transmission strata
articles.title_original: A large-scale mosquito larviciding in Tanga Region, Tanzania, reduced mosquito densities to varying degrees across malaria transmission risk strata.
articles.details: Pilot implementation of large-scale Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis and Bacillus sphaericus larviciding in Tanga Region, Tanzania (June 2022–April 2024) covering 3 intervention councils across high, moderate, and low malaria-risk strata with entomological monitoring in 120 villages. Six larvicide application rounds reduced late-stage Anopheles larvae: high-risk incidence rate ratio 0.20 (95% CI 0.12-0.35), moderate-risk 0.51 (95% CI 0.26-1.01), low-risk 0.40 (95% CI 0.24-0.68). Adult Anopheles gambiae densities reduced in moderate/low strata; no significant effect on Anopheles funestus.

articles.headline: Iso-mukaadial acetate targets P. falciparum glycolysis and DNA pathways
articles.title_original: The impact of Iso-mukaadial acetate on Plasmodium falciparum transcriptional gene regulation.
articles.details: RNA sequencing of Plasmodium falciparum NF54 exposed to iso-mukaadial acetate (extracted from Warburgia salutaris) revealed downregulation of glycolytic and peptidyl-proline modification pathways, with KEGG analysis identifying glyceraldehyde-3-phosphate dehydrogenase (GAPDH) and glucose-6-phosphate dehydrogenase (G6PD) as potential targets. Like chloroquine, iso-mukaadial acetate upregulated adhesion-related genes (PfEMP1, RIFIN, STEVOR). Proposed mechanism: impaired ATP and NADPH production, weakened energy metabolism, redox balance, and DNA replication. Authors suggest potential as antimalarial candidate warranting biochemical validation.

articles.headline: Novel protein controls P. falciparum organelle fission and inheritance
articles.title_original: An essential adaptor for apicoplast fission and inheritance in malaria parasites.
articles.details: Identifies PfAnchor as an essential regulator of P. falciparum apicoplast fission. Conditional depletion disrupts apicoplast fission and causes parasite death. PfAnchor interacts with dynamin-like GTPase PfDyn2, establishing it as the first apicoplast-specific dynamin adaptor protein. Azithromycin treatment, which disrupts apicoplast morphology, rescues fission defects, indicating specificity for apicoplast division. Authors propose parasite-specific organelle division as a potential therapeutic vulnerability.

articles.headline: Mefloquine resistance undermines proposed triple combination therapy
articles.title_original: Tolerance of Plasmodium falciparum mefloquine-resistant clinical isolates to mefloquine-piperaquine with implications for triple artemisinin-based combination therapies.
articles.details: Cambodian P. falciparum isolates with mefloquine resistance exhibit significant cross-tolerance to mefloquine-piperaquine (MQ-PPQ) combination, while piperaquine-resistant strains remain susceptible. Under four months of repeated MQ-PPQ pressure, parasites rapidly acquire tolerance via pfmdr1 amplification. Mechanistically, mefloquine inhibits piperaquine accumulation in a dose-dependent manner. Authors conclude that mefloquine resistance alone compromises MQ-PPQ triple combination therapy efficacy, questioning this strategy's rationale.

articles.headline: Community engagement accelerates malaria elimination in Laos
articles.title_original: Early and sustained community engagement to reach unreached populations for malaria elimination in Lao People's Democratic Republic.
articles.details: In 2022, Lao People's Democratic Republic implemented 'Accelerator Strategies' targeting forest goers and farmers on forest fringes with targeted drug administration and intermittent preventive treatment regardless of symptoms. Three key enablers identified: service delivery by community members, advocacy and political commitment from local leaders, and people-centered services with granular risk-behavior data and GIS mapping. Early sustained community engagement achieved high intervention coverage and decreased malaria burden.

articles.headline: Hospital surveillance reveals malaria elimination vulnerability in Ecuador
articles.title_original: Dengue and malaria mortality in Ecuador: A population analysis of hospital deaths across 24 provinces, 2015-2023.
articles.details: Cross-sectional analysis of hospital deaths from malaria and dengue across Ecuador's 24 provinces (2015–2023). Dengue caused 125 hospital deaths concentrated in Amazonian and coastal provinces. Malaria resulted in five adult deaths (predominantly P. falciparum): three in 2019, one in 2021, one in 2023. Highest malaria mortality rate occurred in Napo province at 16.3 per million in 2021. Study reveals fragmented mortality patterns and persistent underreporting in endemic zones.

articles.headline: Gestational malaria impairs physical health of pregnant women
articles.title_original: Health-related quality of life among pregnant women exposed to malaria in an endemic region of Colombia.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of 400 pregnant women in endemic Colombian region measuring health-related quality of life (WHOQOL-BREF). Six percent reported malaria during current pregnancy; 44% had experienced malaria previously. Psychological health domain scored highest (median 78), while physical health scored lowest (median 43). Gestational malaria, comorbidities, and rural residence were associated with poorer physical and environmental health. Despite documented negative impact, women viewed gestational malaria as normalized within endemic settings.

articles.headline: Malaria discourse on Chinese social media follows event-driven patterns
articles.title_original: Event-driven dynamics, regional disparities, and netizen engagement: characterizing and exploring malaria discourse on Sina Weibo for enhanced health communication.
articles.details: Analysis of 81,010 malaria-related posts on Sina Weibo (January 2019–August 2024) using natural language processing. Netizens' attention is event-driven, peaking around China's health assistance to Africa, public health incidents, and malaria awareness days (accounting for approximately 20% of annual posts). Negative events sustain engagement approximately 1.5 times longer than positive events. Africa discussions comprise over 60% of region-specific posts annually; discussions of Asia and Americas remain fragmented. Official health accounts show limited engagement.

articles.headline: Indoor residual spraying identifies pregnant women for malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Indoor residual spraying household visits as a platform for engaging pregnant women in malaria prevention on Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea.
articles.details: Implementation study on Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea leveraging indoor residual spraying (IRS) campaigns to identify and engage pregnant women (1,567 households visited, 589 pregnant women identified). High awareness of long-lasting insecticide-treated nets (LLINs) as prevention method (76.9%), but low awareness of intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy (IPTp: 34.5% cited benefits; 37.1% aware of availability). LLIN regular use 54.8% (nightly), IPTp uptake 35.2% (on track for three doses). Knowledge of prevention methods and dose requirements associated with uptake.

articles.headline: Seasonal migration drives malaria parasite gene flow in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: High connectivity and low differentiation of Plasmodium falciparum parasite populations in a setting with high seasonal migration.
articles.details: Genomic study in Ethiopia examining P. falciparum genetic diversity between low-transmission highlands and endemic lowlands. Lowland populations (seasonal workers and residents) showed higher complexity of infection (mean 2.62, 62% polyclonal) compared to highland residents (mean 2.00, 40% polyclonal). Similar expected heterozygosity (~0.4) between regions. Extensive parasite sharing identified: 27 related clusters in highland cases, 10 in seasonal workers. High genetic connectivity and low differentiation between regions linked to seasonal agricultural migration.

articles.headline: Methylene blue shows promise for cerebral malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Methylene blue treatment of fatal cerebral malaria and identification of potential blood biomarkers.
articles.details: Using P. coatneyi-infected rhesus macaques, differential gene expression analysis showed methylene blue reverses infection's detrimental effects on brainstem tissue. Comparative analysis with P. coatneyi-infected macaque blood and P. falciparum-infected human blood identified nine genes associated with cerebral malaria severity, predominantly expressed in neutrophils. Authors propose these neutrophil-derived genes as candidate blood biomarkers for diagnosing P. falciparum-induced cerebral malaria and highlight the need for new cerebral malaria treatments.

articles.headline: JAK inhibitor reduces inflammation and enhances malaria immunity
articles.title_original: Adjunctive ruxolitinib attenuates inflammation and enhances immunity in volunteers experimentally infected with Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Double-blind randomized placebo-controlled trial in 20 malaria-naïve volunteers inoculated with blood-stage P. falciparum. On day 8, participants randomized to artemether-lumefantrine plus ruxolitinib (JAK1/2 inhibitor) or placebo. Ruxolitinib was safe and well-tolerated. Attenuated inflammatory responses on initial infection with reduced post-treatment increases in C-reactive protein, angiopoietin-2, and intercellular adhesion molecule-1. After second inoculation, elevated HLA-DRA and 4-1BB indicating increased T cell activation. Authors recommend further evaluation as adjunctive treatment.

articles.headline: Systematic review: AI applications for malaria diagnosis and forecasting
articles.title_original: The role of artificial intelligence (AI) in the elimination of malaria: a systematic literature review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 62 research articles (2014–2025) on artificial intelligence applications in malaria diagnosis, treatment optimization, and outbreak prediction for India's 2030 elimination goal. AI models achieved improved diagnostic accuracies and significantly reduced diagnostic time and human error. Major limitations identified include poor data interoperability, limited rural infrastructure, and gaps in healthcare worker training. Review identifies globally-used AI techniques adaptable for India and suggests areas for future research.

articles.headline: Non-falciparum malaria species circulate widely across Senegal
articles.title_original: Common Molecular Detection of the Neglected Human Malaria Parasite Among Febrile Patients in Southern Regions in Senegal.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of 1990 febrile patients across four southern Senegal regions (Kedougou, Kolda, Tambacounda, Ziguinchor) during 2020–2022 transmission seasons using Plasmodium genus-specific qPCR and species-specific nested PCR. Overall Plasmodium positivity 73.43%, comprising 67.92% single-species infections and 32.08% co-infections (two to three species). Detected ongoing circulation of P. vivax, P. ovale, and P. malariae in three regions. First detection of non-falciparum species in Kolda and Tambacounda; first P. ovale report in Ziguinchor.

articles.headline: Malaria-STH co-infection linked to high anemia in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Malaria and Soil-Transmitted Helminth Co-Infection and Its Association With Anemia Among Pregnant Women Visiting Mizan-Tepi University Teaching Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia.
articles.journal: Can J Infect Dis Med Microbiol
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 332 pregnant women in Ethiopia (Sept-Dec 2024). Malaria prevalence 24.4%, STH 33.7%, co-infection 12.7%. Anemia prevalence 57.5% overall; 18.4% in malaria-infected, 26.2% in STH-infected, 9.6% in co-infected women. Strong statistical association between infection and anemia demonstrated; rural residence and poor hygiene practices associated with co-infection.

articles.headline: Fever and cough predict malaria in respiratory symptoms
articles.title_original: Syndromic presentation by patients with malaria and respiratory symptoms in a malaria-endemic urban-and-peri-urban setting.
articles.details: Among 332 adults with respiratory symptoms in Uganda, 16.0% had malaria confirmed by RDT and microscopy. Fever and cough were strongest independent predictors. Malaria positivity increased from 0.8% (one symptom) to 100% (four symptoms). Four-symptom score achieved 98.1% sensitivity and 47.3% specificity at ≥2 symptoms, offering practical triage tool for resource-limited outpatient settings.

articles.headline: Community IPTp with SP acceptable but faces supply barriers
articles.title_original: Initiating community intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy in Senegal: qualitative evaluation of stakeholder acceptability and experience.
articles.details: Qualitative evaluation (111 interviews, 16 focus groups) in high-transmission Senegal districts. Providers appreciated reduced workload and improved access in remote villages; SP stockouts and pregnancy test unavailability were key barriers. Pregnant women found IPTp acceptable but expressed concerns about adverse events and socio-cultural stigma. Recommendations emphasize ensuring supplies, incentivizing health workers, and community involvement.

articles.headline: Kenya P. falciparum shows temporal drug resistance shifts
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity, population structure, and drug resistance of Plasmodium falciparum in Kenya: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review of 58 articles (2002-2023) on P. falciparum in Kenya. High genetic diversity (He 0.79-0.96 by region); multiclonal infections predominant (34-80%). Pfcrt CVIET haplotype declined from 96% (1999) to 1% (2017), while CVMNK rose from 6.8% to 98.8%. Temporal increases in Pfdhfr and Pfdhps mutations; Pfk13 mutations detected in 2018-2024.

articles.headline: Global NTDM burden shifted: incidence up, mortality down
articles.title_original: Global, regional, national burden, trends and health inequality of neglected tropical diseases and malaria from 1990 to 2021.
articles.details: GBD 2021 analysis (1990-2021). Age-standardized incidence rate increased 0.59%, while prevalence decreased 58.05%, death rate declined 25%, and DALY rate declined 32.36%. Western Sub-Saharan Africa bore highest burden; children under five had highest incidence and mortality. Projections to 2050 forecast continued prevalence decline but rises in incidence, mortality, and DALYs driven by population growth.

articles.headline: CNN achieves 92.4% accuracy detecting malaria parasite stages
articles.title_original: Mobile phone-based plasmodium parasites stage detection from Giemsa stained blood smear by convolutional neural networks.
articles.details: Convolutional neural network trained on Giemsa-stained blood smear images for automated parasite detection, classifying Ring Form, Trophozoite, Schizont, and Uninfected RBCs. Overall accuracy 92.4% with precision 93.1% and recall 92.8%. Schizont stage highest accuracy (94.7%), Ring Form lowest (91.2%). Cross-validation demonstrated robustness; approach offers scalability for resource-constrained diagnostic settings.

articles.headline: Ethiopia Amhara Region malaria resurgence, P. vivax surge
articles.title_original: Resurgence of malaria in the Amhara Region, Ethiopia (2014-2024): trends, spatial expansion, and control challenges.
articles.details: Retrospective surveillance of 166 districts (2014-2024). 7.7 million cases, 162 deaths. Trend inflection in late 2018 from -13.2% to +12.6% annual percent change. Annual Parasite Incidence rose from 10.9 to 74.8 per 1000 (2018-2024). P. vivax contribution increased from 25% to 43%; P. vivax incidence surged 11-fold. Test positivity rate increased from 10% to >50%; resurgence in 83% of districts.

articles.headline: Imported malaria control needs surveillance plus interventions
articles.title_original: Imported malaria: a silent and forgotten threat in malaria free zones?
articles.details: Systematic review (149 articles screened, 24 analyzed) of strategies for preventing malaria reintroduction in eliminated regions. Case-based surveillance alone was insufficient; successful prevention required complementary interventions including vector surveillance, vector control, mass drug administration, chemoprophylaxis, cross-border initiatives, and health promotion. Reintroduction driven by imported cases, population movement, conflicts, and presence of competent vectors.

articles.headline: Low asymptomatic P. falciparum in Mogadishu pregnant women
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic malaria and associated factors among pregnant women in Mogadishu, Somalia: a cross-sectional study.
articles.authors: Abdi MY, Mohamud FA, Abdullahi SA
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 171 asymptomatic pregnant women (Feb-Mar 2025) in Mogadishu. 2.9% had asymptomatic Plasmodium infections; only P. falciparum detected. No significant associations with sociodemographic or obstetric factors. Younger age (OR 0.24), urban residence (OR 0.12), and ITN use (OR 0.06) trended toward protective effects but were not statistically significant.

articles.headline: Eschweilenol C disrupts hemoglobin metabolism in malaria
articles.title_original: Eschweilenol C and subfractions from Terminalia plants disrupt haemoglobin metabolism, inhibiting Plasmodium falciparum growth at rings and trophozoite stages.
articles.details: Natural product fractionation of Terminalia species screened against P. falciparum strains (Dd2, 3D7, Dd2-GNF156). Eschweilenol C isolated from T. brownii with IC50 490.74 nM (Dd2) and 379.20 nM (3D7). Subfractions Ti03 and Ti04 also potent (IC50 <1 µg/ml). Compounds showed selectivity (SI >10), no loss of efficacy against resistant strain, killing activity on ring and trophozoite stages.

articles.headline: Malaria-anemia co-occurrence in 6.4% of Ghanaian under-5s
articles.title_original: The double burden: co-occurrence of malaria and anaemia in children under five in Ghana - a multilevel mixed-effects logistic regression analysis.
articles.details: Analysis of 2022 Ghana Demographic and Health Survey using multilevel logistic regression. Double burden prevalence 6.4% [95% CI 5.5-7.5]. Associated with fifth birth order (aOR 3.92), recent fever (aOR 2.49), and maternal primary education (aOR 1.87). Protective factors: older household head (aOR 0.33) and higher socioeconomic status (aOR 0.31-0.51). Study emphasizes integrated malaria and nutrition interventions.

articles.headline: Reversible paralysis in pregnancy from neuroschistosomiasis
articles.title_original: Reversible paralysis in pregnancy, thinking beyond malaria: a case report.
articles.details: Case report of 17-year-old gravida in Malawi presenting with fever, headache, neck pain, abdominal pain, initially diagnosed with malaria. Despite IV artesunate, developed progressive bilateral lower extremity weakness and neurogenic bladder. Diagnosis of neuroschistosomiasis confirmed by urine microscopy; treatment with prednisone followed by praziquantel resulted in full ambulatory recovery within three weeks.

articles.headline: PfPI3K inhibitor idelalisib reverses artemisinin resistance
articles.title_original: PfPI3K inhibition reversed artemisinin resistance by increasing ROS and ubiquitinated protein levels in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Combination treatment with PfPI3K inhibitor idelalisib and dihydroartemisinin (DHA) showed synergistic antimalarial effects and reversed artemisinin resistance. The treatment decreases PI3P levels, causing reactive oxygen species accumulation, parasite apoptosis, ubiquitinated protein accumulation, and dysregulated antioxidant and proteostasis systems. Authors conclude targeting PfPI3K with idelalisib is a successful strategy for controlling artemisinin resistance development.

articles.headline: Acute P. falciparum malaria shifts T-cell response toward IL-10
articles.title_original: CD4 and CD8 T-cell response is dominated by IL-10-secreting cells in children with uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria.
articles.details: Study of 10 Kenyan children with acute P. falciparum malaria compared to matched 4-week recovery samples and 10 healthy controls. Flow cytometry characterized T-cell activation and cytokine secretion across EBV- and cytomegalovirus-specific responses. Acute malaria induced a shift in cytokine secretion favoring IL-10 in both CD4 and CD8 T-cell subsets. Authors conclude this represents a systemic shift toward immune suppression rather than T-cell impairment.

articles.headline: Acute kidney injury strongly predicts mortality in severe malaria
articles.title_original: Identification of acute kidney injury in African children with severe malaria: a multinational individual participant data meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 18 studies across 8 African countries with 13,528 children with severe P. falciparum malaria found KDIGO-defined acute kidney injury in 47% (95% CI 46-48%). AKI was associated with 2.87-fold increased odds of mortality (95% CI 2.28-3.61), with monotonic increases across AKI stages. Simplified age-based creatinine thresholds enabled bedside AKI recognition; WHO thresholds missed 93% of additional cases. Authors conclude AKI is strongly associated with mortality and kidney-protective care implementation is critical.
articles.notable_reason: Large multinational meta-analysis establishing AKI as major diagnosable complication with clear mortality associations and improved bedside diagnostic criteria.

articles.headline: Plasmodium NEK4 kinase essential for meiosis and zygote maturation
articles.title_original: A divergent Plasmodium NEK4 acts as a key regulator driving the early events of meiosis.
articles.details: Study identified NEK4 as a key regulator in P. berghei coupling meiotic initiation with zygote morphogenesis. NEK4 accumulates at the microtubule-organizing center and apical polar complex shortly after fertilization. Deletion of nek4 caused complete developmental arrest with blocked MTOC duplication, microtubule formation, chromatin condensation, and cell polarity. Transcriptomic and phosphoproteomic analyses revealed NEK4 controls regulatory networks including HOP1, REC8, and AP2-O.

articles.headline: Locally acquired P. falciparum malaria reported in non-endemic Lebanon
articles.title_original: Locally Acquired Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in a Non-endemic Region of Lebanon: A Case Report.
articles.details: Case report of a 70-year-old Lebanese woman presenting with P. falciparum malaria with no travel history to endemic regions. Presented with intermittent fever, chills, headache, nausea, and abdominal pain with laboratory findings of anemia, thrombocytopenia, and leukopenia. Microscopy revealed intraerythrocytic ring forms confirmed by rapid diagnostic test. She was treated with intravenous artesunate followed by oral artemether-lumefantrine and achieved full recovery.

articles.headline: Computational screening identifies potent PfMDR1 inhibitor candidate
articles.title_original: In-silico identification of antimalarial compounds targeting PfMDR1 of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.authors: Preetha TL, Yadav V, Subbarao N
articles.details: In-silico drug discovery screened compounds from Chembl and IUPHAR databases against the P. falciparum MDR1 transporter (PDB 8JWI). Top-ranking compound demonstrated superior binding with GLIDE docking score of -12.169 kcal/mol versus benchmark -5.435 kcal/mol and binding free energy of -130.971 ± 14.283 kJ/mol. Molecular dynamics simulations showed stable binding throughout the trajectory. Authors conclude the identified inhibitor exhibits greater potency against PfMDR1, making it a promising candidate for overcoming drug resistance.

articles.headline: Clothianidin indoor residual spraying reduces transmission 75%
articles.title_original: Entomological impact of three years of clothianidin-based indoor residual spraying in two high malaria endemic districts in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.details: Pre- and post-IRS entomological survey in two Côte d'Ivoire districts receiving three rounds of clothianidin-based indoor residual spraying versus two control districts (April 2019-December 2022). In IRS districts, human biting rates declined 60% by third round (IRR=0.40, p=0.002), with four-fold entomological inoculation rate decrease from 3.92 to 1.25 infective bites/person/night. Sporozoite infection odds decreased 49% after first round (OR=0.51, p=0.023) but plateaued by third round. Authors conclude clothianidin-based IRS is an effective vector control tool.

articles.headline: Only 33% adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines in Uganda
articles.title_original: Adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines in Northern Uganda: a mixed-methods study.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study at three Northern Uganda hospitals reviewed 384 patient records and conducted nine key informant interviews. Adherence to severe malaria treatment guidelines was 33.07%, with major gaps including 38.3% non-adherence to recommended IV-then-oral therapy and 25% treating only confirmed cases. Medical doctors were more likely to adhere (aIRR 1.7, p=0.035) than other healthcare workers. Authors conclude adherence is suboptimal and recommend routine refresher training and supervision.

articles.headline: Hybrid seasonal chemoprevention substantially improves malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Rethinking a hybrid malaria chemoprevention delivery strategy for children in sub-perennial settings: a modelling study integrating age- and seasonally-targeted delivery.
articles.authors: Sen S, Schellenberg D, Penny MA
articles.details: Modeling study (OpenMalaria) evaluated hybrid malaria chemoprevention combining perennial sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine with additional monthly doses during rainy seasons in sub-perennial settings. For children aged 3-24 months, hybrid strategy predicted 31.8% protective efficacy against clinical malaria and 16.1% against severe cases, representing 2.1-fold and 2.0-fold increases versus standard perennial chemoprevention. Age-expanded strategy (3-36 months) achieved 44.9% and 26.4% efficacy with 2.9-fold and 3.3-fold burden reductions. Authors conclude substantially increased benefits are possible but effectiveness-implementation studies are needed.

articles.headline: Secondary anopheline vectors sustain malaria in northeastern India
articles.title_original: Evidence of secondary anopheline vectors in sustaining malaria transmission in Kokrajhar District, Assam, Northeastern India.
articles.details: Entomological survey in Kokrajhar District, Assam collected 1794 female mosquitoes and identified Plasmodium positivity rates: An. maculatus 4% (5/80), An. minimus 4.8% (1/21), and An. kochi 4.6% (1/22). Despite lower sporozoite rates than traditional primary vectors, these secondary species appear capable of sustaining malaria transmission. Authors conclude secondary vectors beyond traditionally recognized An. minimus and An. baimaii require integration into malaria control programs.

articles.headline: Commentary celebrates scaling of malaria rapid diagnostic testing
articles.title_original: A reason to celebrate in a time of uncertainty. Response to: Linblade et al. "Assessing the accuracy of the recording and reporting of malaria rapid diagnostic test results in four African countries-methods and key results".
articles.details: Editorial commentary on assessment of malaria RDT accuracy across four African countries. Authors emphasize that findings demonstrate success of country and global efforts to scale up malaria diagnostic testing over the past 15 years, placing recent results in historical context of diagnostic progress.

articles.headline: Novel antimalarial UA2239 blocks parasite egress via cGMP
articles.title_original: An acyclic nucleoside phosphonate effectively blocks the egress of the malaria parasite by inhibiting the synthesis of cyclic GMP.
articles.details: UA2239, an acyclic nucleoside phosphonate with guanine nucleobase, blocks egress of merozoites and gametes by inhibiting the cGMP-dependent egress pathway. The compound decreases parasite cGMP levels through inhibition of Plasmodium falciparum guanylyl cyclase α. Notably, parasites develop resistance through mutations of downstream effectors rather than the target itself. Authors conclude UA2239 is a first-in-class candidate with dual-stage antimalarial activity (growth inhibition and transmission blocking) with significant therapeutic potential.

articles.headline: Father-dependent decisions, poverty delay childhood severe malaria care
articles.title_original: Influence of Sociodemographic Factors and Household Decision-Making on the Delayed Presentation of Childhood Severe Malaria at a Secondary Facility in North-Central Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 146 Nigerian children admitted with severe malaria. 69.2% (101/146) presented >24 hours after symptom onset. Common reasons for delay: failure to recognize illness (44.6%) and lack of funds (38.6%). Socioeconomic class independently predicted delayed presentation (AOR 3.05, 95% CI 1.10-8.48).

articles.headline: Migratory, colorful birds at higher risk of haemosporidian infection
articles.title_original: Host Traits and Avian Malaria Parasites: Understanding the Intrinsic Connections.
articles.details: Phylogenetically informed comparative analysis of 115 Colombian bird species examined host traits affecting haemosporidian susceptibility. Migratory species, larger body sizes, and more colorful plumage significantly increased infection probability. Habitat specialization was protective. Results suggest increased vector exposure explains differential infection risk in tropical bird communities.

articles.headline: Author correction: male-drive female-sterile mosquito control system
articles.title_original: Author Correction: A male-drive female-sterile system for the self-limited control of the malaria mosquito Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: Author correction for previously published study on a genetic control system targeting Anopheles gambiae malaria mosquito population suppression using male-drive female-sterile approach.

articles.headline: Plasmodium infection disrupts salivary glands, reduces immune proteins
articles.title_original: High-resolution proteomics unveils salivary gland disruption and saliva-hemolymph protein exchange in Plasmodium-infected mosquitoes.
articles.authors: Alves E Silva TL, et al.
articles.details: High-resolution proteomics of Anopheles gambiae salivary glands infected with P. berghei or P. falciparum sporozoites revealed structural damage, differential protein expression, and hemolymph protein enrichment in infected glands. Saliva showed reduced immune proteins relative to uninfected mosquitoes. Changes were conserved between both Plasmodium species, providing mechanistic insight into sporozoite-salivary gland interactions critical for transmission.

articles.headline: Ganaplacide shows promise as existing antimalarials face resistance
articles.title_original: 'A sigh of relief': New malaria drug succeeds in clinical trial as existing treatments falter.
articles.details: Ganaplacide, developed by Novartis, succeeded in clinical trial, providing new hope as existing antimalarial treatments encounter resistance challenges. Strategic deployment decisions remain pending to optimize use of this novel agent.

articles.headline: Low-density malaria persists undetectable in supposedly elimination zones
articles.title_original: Persistent low-density infection in areas at risk of malaria reintroduction in Malaysia.
articles.details: Community-based survey of 3,322 asymptomatic residents in 23 Malaysian localities declared malaria-free revealed 1.86% (62/3,322) prevalence by nested PCR versus 0% by microscopy. Infections comprised P. malariae (40.3%), P. vivax (29.0%), P. knowlesi (24.2%), P. falciparum (1.6%), and mixed infections. Hidden parasite reservoirs in supposedly malaria-free areas pose reintroduction risk, necessitating sensitive diagnostic tools in elimination surveillance.
articles.notable_reason: Hidden infections persist in eliminated settings; challenges elimination surveillance

articles.headline: Plasmodium detected in 24.4% of acute febrile illness patients
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Plasmodium spp. Detection Among Acute Febrile Illness Patients in Two Regions of Nigeria.
articles.details: Multi-pathogen screening study of 1,202 acute febrile illness patients across two Nigerian tertiary hospitals (Aug 2023-Sep 2024) detected Plasmodium spp. in 293 enrollees (24.4%). Risk factors: larger household (COR 3.70), healthcare worker status (COR 3.39), age 11-14 (COR 1.86). Coinfections occurred in 37.2% (109/293) cases; biodefense pathogens detected in 105.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccines cost-effective in Africa despite substantial budget impact
articles.title_original: Cost-effectiveness and budget impact of malaria, measles, and meningitis vaccines in Africa: a scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review of 41 studies (2004-2024) on vaccine cost-effectiveness in African countries. Cost per fully vaccinated child: malaria $4.20-$52.35, measles $1.66-$4.31. Cost per DALY averted: malaria $22.63-$3,314.01, measles $1.60-$1,239.35, meningitis $66.64-$4,586.72. Budget impact of malaria vaccine across 41 African countries approximately $185 million. Vaccines generally more cost-effective than no intervention.

articles.headline: Invasive An. stephensi coexists with native vectors in urban Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Co-occurrence of native and invasive malaria vectors in anthropogenic habitats in Metehara, Ethiopia: Opportunities for urban malaria control.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Bioecological assessment (Nov 2023-Oct 2024) in Metehara, Ethiopia documented 767 larval breeding habitats, 98.3% anthropogenic. One-third of Anopheles-positive habitats contained both An. stephensi and An. arabiensis. Adult collections (n=2,078): An. arabiensis dominant (63.7%), followed by An. pharoensis (26.4%). Sporozoite rates: An. pharoensis highest (4.2%), An. arabiensis (0.4%), An. stephensi (0%).

articles.headline: Ultrasensitive assay detects P. falciparum at 10-fold improved sensitivity
articles.title_original: Ultrasensitive ELISA for Accurate Detection of Plasmodium falciparum Infection.
articles.details: Ultrasensitive protein detection system combining ELISA and thio-NAD cycling detected P. falciparum histidine-rich protein 2 (PfHRP2) at 0.782 pg/mL and pan-lactate dehydrogenase (pLDH) at 1.33 pg/mL. Parasitemia detection limits: 2.57×10⁻⁵% (PfHRP2) and 2.41×10⁻⁵% (pLDH) in culture; 0.5 parasites/μL in whole blood. Sensitivity approximately 10-fold greater than current ultrasensitive tests; successfully detected pfhrp2-deletion strains via pLDH.

articles.headline: Guillain-Barré syndrome develops after P. vivax malaria infection
articles.title_original: Guillain-Barré Syndrome Following Malaria: A Rare Case Report From Rural Ethiopia and Literature Review.
articles.authors: Abdela HA, Kimo NH, Woyimo TG
articles.details: Case report of 21-year-old Ethiopian male with Guillain-Barré syndrome following P. vivax malaria. Presented with ascending paralysis, areflexia, dysphagia, dysarthria, and autonomic dysfunction. CSF analysis showed albumin-cytologic dissociation. Treated with intravenous immunoglobulin and supportive care; achieved full recovery after 8 weeks. Highlights importance of considering GBS in malaria patients with neurological symptoms.

articles.headline: Malaria protein VAR2CSA enhances CAR-T targeting of cancer tumors
articles.title_original: Protein catenation potentiates antitumor activity of malaria VAR2CSA navigation CAR-T cells in a mouse model of hematological malignancies.
articles.details: Development of navigable CAR-T cell system using recombinant malaria protein VAR2CSA (rVAR2) to target chondroitin sulfate A on tumor cells. Created interlocking rVAR2 catenane (AXVB) navigator with third-generation CAR-T expressing CD28 and 4-1BB. AXVB-CAR-T cells potently eliminated CD19+ and CD19- tumor cells in vitro and in vivo, significantly prolonging survival in tumor-bearing mice versus rVAR2 monomer.

articles.headline: ITN use among pregnant women in sub-Saharan Africa: 63.8%
articles.title_original: Pooled prevalence and factors associated with insecticide-treated net use among pregnant women in malaria high-burden countries in sub-Saharan Africa: a multilevel mixed-effects analysis.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 2017-2021 MIS data from 7 high-burden SSA countries included 4,950 pregnant women aged 15-49. Pooled ITN use prevalence was 63.8% (95% CI 61.8–65.9), ranging from 49.2% (Ghana) to 90.5% (Niger). Primary education (aOR=1.36) and 1–2 prior births (aOR=1.24) associated with higher ITN use. Substantial regional variation (ICC=0.32) indicates regional context critically shapes ITN utilization patterns.

articles.headline: Artemisinin production and resistance management for malaria control
articles.title_original: Safeguarding artemisinin: integrating bioproduction innovation and resistance management for malaria eradication.
articles.details: Review examines biotechnological advances in artemisinin biosynthesis via metabolic engineering, approaches to improve Artemisia annua cultivation, and molecular mechanisms of artemisinin resistance including pfk13 mutations. Current artemisinin-based combination therapy (ACT) and novel triple/quadruple ACT strategies (TACTs/QACTs) are evaluated for advantages, drawbacks, and geographical suitability. Proposes bridging production and resistance paradigms through interdisciplinary collaboration to ensure artemisinin's enduring efficacy.

articles.headline: High pyrethroid resistance but DDT susceptibility in Anopheles
articles.title_original: Insecticide resistance and malaria transmission indicators in Anopheles gambiae s.l. in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso: implications for vector control strategies.
articles.details: WHO-standard susceptibility bioassays on Anopheles gambiae s.l. from 6 neighborhoods in Burkina Faso revealed high resistance to DDT and pyrethroids (L995F/L995S mutations) but susceptibility to bendiocarb and pirimiphos-methyl. Of 622 specimens, An. arabiensis predominated (90.8%). Sporozoite infection rates: 45% (An. coluzzii), 27.4% (An. arabiensis), 16.2% (An. gambiae s.s.). Entomological inoculation rate was 10.6 infectious bites/person; An. arabiensis contributed 91.2% of transmission.

articles.headline: KDR mutations absent in organochlorine-resistant Anopheles species
articles.title_original: Knockdown resistance (kdr) and organochlorine insecticide resistance in malaria vectors: a systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 5 studies examining kdr mutations (codons 1010, 1013, 1014 in VGSC gene) in An. albimanus, An. darlingi, An. dirus, and An. punctipennis found no kdr mutations in any species. Resistance attributed to metabolic mechanisms (elevated mixed-function oxidases and non-specific esterases) rather than target-site insensitivity.
