Authors conclude further research with broader genetic screening is needed to fully understand resistance dynamics in these vectors.

articles.headline: Combining ITNs, IRS, vaccines needed to overcome pyrethroid resistance
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of different intervention packages for malaria control under varying intensities of pyrethroid resistance.
articles.details: Compartmental transmission model for Tanzania simulated impacts of ITNs, IRS, biolarvicides, ACT, and RTS,S/AS01 or R21/Matrix-M vaccines (≥40% coverage) under varying pyrethroid resistance. At 50% resistance, 71% ITN + 50% IRS or 32% biolarvicide coverage achieved Re<1. At >75% resistance, Re remained >1 regardless of vector control type. Adding immunization to 80% ITN + 80% ACT reduced infectious individuals to <20% and Re<1 even at high resistance.

articles.headline: Mobile surveillance platforms improve malaria reporting and response
articles.title_original: Mobile app-based malaria surveillance and vector control integration: a scoping review of evidence from endemic settings.
articles.details: Scoping review of 20 studies (2010–2024) examined mHealth malaria surveillance integration with vector control. Systems like MCBR (Myanmar), SOCH/MCS (India), MRRS (Zambia) significantly improved reporting timeliness and enabled precise vector control targeting. GIS-enabled tools identified hotspots; community platforms like FeverTracker enhanced case detection. Challenges included poor internet access, low digital literacy, and limited policy integration. Success depends on interoperability, community participation, and long-term policy support.

articles.headline: High P. falciparum genetic diversity and polyclonal infections
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum using merozoite surface proteins 1 and 2 (msp-1 and msp-2) genes in Oveng and Mintom, South region of Cameroon.
articles.details: Genotyping 315 P. falciparum isolates from Cameroon identified 12 msp1 alleles and 28 msp2 alleles. FC27 (100%) and 3D7 (98.4%) prevalent msp2 families; K1 (87.8%), MAD20 (75.2%), RO33 (74.9%) for msp1. Mean MOI significantly higher for msp2 vs msp1 (4.85 vs 2.40; p<0.05). Expected heterozygosity 0.83 (msp1) and 0.98 (msp2). High allelic diversity and elevated MOI indicate intense transmission, suggesting need for strengthened control and vaccine development baseline.

articles.headline: IPTsc with dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine reduces malaria prevalence
articles.title_original: Implementation of the intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in school-aged children (IPTsc) in moderate and high endemic areas in Tanzania: a policy brief.
articles.details: Policy brief summarizing successful clinical trial and large-scale implementation research in Tanzania. Intermittent preventive treatment for school-aged children (IPTsc) using dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DP) delivered through schools proved safe, cost-effective, acceptable, with significant malaria prevalence reductions. In high-transmission settings, 70% of school-aged children (5–15 years) harbor parasites asymptomatically, contributing to absenteeism (13–50%) and anemia (61%). Supports IPTsc as complementary control strategy in moderate-to-high transmission areas.

articles.headline: Rituximab increases malaria risk in DLBCL patients
articles.title_original: Rituximab is associated with an increased risk of malaria among adult lymphoma patients in Malawi: a prospective observational cohort.
articles.details: Prospective observational cohort of 96 DLBCL patients treated with CHOP or R-CHOP (2013–2019). Symptomatic malaria developed in 8% (5/59) CHOP vs 24% (9/37) R-CHOP (OR 3.5, 95% CI 1.1–12; p=0.039). Among HRP2-positive cases, antigenemia significantly higher in R-CHOP (3.6 ng/mL vs 0.92 ng/mL; p=0.011). Authors suggest rituximab may increase symptomatic malaria risk with potential implications for antimalarial chemoprophylaxis in endemic settings.

articles.headline: Strategies to improve RTS,S four-dose vaccine delivery
articles.title_original: Recommendations to mitigate barriers to uptake and delivery of a four-dose malaria vaccine schedule: insights from the MVIP's qualitative evidence.
articles.details: Qualitative analysis from 2019–2023 Malaria Vaccine Pilot in Ghana, Kenya, Malawi examined barriers to RTS,S/AS01E uptake and delivery. Despite good vaccine acceptance, many children missed doses due to challenges with four-dose schedule complexity and routine immunization integration. Healthcare Utilization Study and expert interviews identified barriers including schedule complexity and tracking difficulties. Paper provides guidance, tools, and materials to support program implementation strategies for schedule management and dose tracking.

articles.headline: Five-decade malaria decline in Rajasthan linked to rainfall
articles.title_original: Echoes of resilience: spatio-temporal analysis of five-decade battle against malaria in Rajasthan, India.
articles.details: Spatio-temporal analysis across 4 ecological regions of Rajasthan (1975–2023) found malaria peaked in 1976 and 1996, then sharply declined post-2000. Median API ranged from 1 (Gangetic Plain) to 1.93 (Hadoti Plateau). Rainfall positively correlated with API in Desert (r=0.39; p=0.006) and Gangetic Plain (r=0.35; p=0.014). An. stephensi and An. culicifacies predominant, resistant to organochlorines/organophosphates but susceptible to synthetic pyrethroids.

articles.headline: High R21/Matrix-M vaccine acceptance despite low awareness
articles.title_original: Awareness, acceptability, and willingness to pay for the R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine: a cross-sectional study among pregnant women and nursing mothers in Enugu State, Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 310 pregnant women and nursing mothers in Enugu State, Nigeria found 84.5% unaware of R21/Matrix-M vaccine, but 94.2% agreed to vaccinate their child and 79.7% expressed willingness to pay. Mean maximum WTP was US$0.82±1.41 (median US$0.38). Age (p=0.005) and health insurance (p=0.021) significantly associated with WTP; health insurance predicted lower WTP (OR 0.417, 95% CI 0.19–0.92).

articles.headline: mRNA nanoparticle vaccines achieve sterilizing immunity in mouse malaria
articles.title_original: mRNA delivery of circumsporozoite protein epitope-based malaria vaccines induces protection in a mouse model.
articles.details: Mouse model study of self-assembling nanoparticle immunogens displaying different P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein epitope regions. mRNA-delivered immunogens with junctional and NANP repeat epitopes achieved 99% reduction in liver burden and sterilizing immunity from parasitemia in some mice. Authors conclude these represent promising candidates for rapid translation to human challenge studies.

articles.headline: PBO long-lasting nets show borderline protection over conventional nets in Kenya
articles.title_original: Do Piperonyl Butoxide Long-Lasting Insecticide Treated Nets Provide Additional Protection Against Malaria Infections Compared with Conventional Nets in an Operational Setting in Western Kenya?
articles.details: Cohort of 768 Kenyans followed June 2017–December 2023 with monthly active and passive malaria surveillance. Compared piperonyl butoxide (PBO)-treated long-lasting insecticide nets versus conventional pyrethroids. PBO nets showed lower infection risk (OR 0.70, 95% CI 0.47–1.03) but findings were not statistically significant. Authors recommend PBO nets in pyrethroid-resistant areas despite added cost.

articles.headline: Healthcare access inequities strongly predict malaria mortality in Colombia
articles.title_original: Inequitable Access to Healthcare and the Malaria Burden in Colombia: A Value-Based Policy Approach for Sustainable Elimination.
articles.authors: Olivera MJ, Magali Caro H, Ricardo Gómez J
articles.details: Longitudinal ecological study of Colombian municipalities (2010–2023) examining healthcare access and malaria burden. Lower access was strongly associated with higher case fatality rates. Diagnostic delay >48 hours (aOR 4.1, 95% CI 2.24–7.11) and very low access (aOR 3.5) were strongest predictors; extended travel time >2 hours (aOR 2.8) and age <5 (aOR 1.9) independently increased poor outcomes. Only 39.7% diagnosed within 48 hours.

articles.headline: Vivax malaria recurrence and genetic diversity elevated in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Parasite genotypes and host immune mediators in primary and recurrent episodes of vivax Malaria in Colombia.
articles.authors: Duque-Isaza YY, Carmona-Fonseca J, Arango-Flórez EM
articles.journal: Rev Peru Med Exp Salud Publica
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 41 pregnant and 46 non-pregnant Colombians with uncomplicated P. vivax malaria followed 120 days. Pregnant women had higher recurrence rates (41.6% vs 8.7%) with greater parasite genetic diversity; only 23–33% of recurrences were genetically identical (homologous). Fox-P3 expression varied in pregnant women between primary and recurrent episodes.

articles.headline: Indigenous communities blend traditional and Western approaches to malaria care
articles.title_original: Between their own and the western: the experience of Emberá communities in Chocó with Malaria.
articles.journal: Rev Peru Med Exp Salud Publica
articles.details: Ethnographic study of 6 Emberá communities in Colombia (October 2022–November 2023) with 250 hours observation and 25 interviews. Communities perceive malaria as a 'Western disease' controlled through mosquito nets and hygiene. Jaibaná (spiritual healer) address spiritual conditions alongside Western treatment. Authors note communities do not recognize humans as primary source of transmission.

articles.headline: Merozoite antigen mismatches associated with cerebral malaria and sequelae
articles.title_original: Antibody responses to polymorphic Plasmodium falciparum merozoite antigens in Malawian children with severe and uncomplicated malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 269 Malawian children with cerebral malaria, severe anaemia, or uncomplicated malaria examined IgG responses to polymorphic merozoite surface proteins (MSP-1, MSP-2). Lower IgG to MSP-2 type B (FC27) correlated with cerebral malaria; lower MSP-2 type A (IC1/3D7) correlated with severe anaemia. Discordant antibody-genotype responses were associated with neurological sequelae after cerebral malaria.

articles.headline: Weather forecasting predicts malaria outbreaks four months ahead in Zambia
articles.title_original: Malaria outbreak prediction at the sub-district level in Zambia using remote sensing satellite data.
articles.details: Developed predictive model for sub-district in southern Zambia using 15 years of facility data and satellite remote sensing (temperature, rainfall). Nighttime temperature (November–January) and December rainfall identified as optimal predictors. Malaria incidence increased with higher temperatures (β=2.1, p=0.003) and precipitation (β=23.5, p=0.057). Model predicted 2020 outbreak within 4% of observed cases (2,367 observed vs 2,467 predicted).

articles.headline: Time-series modeling forecasts malaria trends across eight Indian states
articles.title_original: Forecasting Malaria in Indian States: A Time Series Approach with R Shiny Integration.
articles.details: Developed time-series forecasting models for malaria across 8 Indian states (Jharkhand, Chhattisgarh, Maharashtra, Meghalaya, Mizoram, Odisha, Tripura, Uttar Pradesh). Log-transformed polynomial regression outperformed polynomial, lagged-difference, and ARIMA models with lowest RMSE and MAPE; 5-fold cross-validation confirmed robustness. Authors created interactive R Shiny tool for real-time forecasting and decision support.

articles.headline: Parasite genotypes differ between cerebral malaria and severe anaemia
articles.title_original: Genotypic and antigenic diversity of Plasmodium falciparum merozoite proteins in Malawian children with severe and uncomplicated malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 261 Malawian children (93 cerebral malaria, 50 severe anaemia, 92 uncomplicated) examined genetic diversity of P. falciparum merozoite proteins (MSP-1, MSP-2). Severe anaemia had higher parasite diversity (2.70±1.20 vs 2.25±0.89, p<0.01). MSP-2 FC27 type associated with severe anaemia (66%); MSP-2 IC1 type with cerebral malaria (61.5%, p<0.025). Authors suggest parasite antigenic variability contributes to disease severity.

articles.headline: LLINs plus treatment is most cost-effective malaria control strategy
articles.title_original: Optimal control analysis of malaria infection in children under 5 years and pregnant women under the influence of temperature.
articles.authors: Robert PK, Irunde JI, Mtunya AP
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study evaluated three control measures (treatment, long-lasting insecticide nets, indoor residual spraying) using optimal control theory. Treatment alone was more effective than nets or spraying individually. Combining LLINs with treatment emerged as most effective and cost-efficient strategy; adding all three interventions provided no significant improvement over LLIN plus treatment.

articles.headline: Deep learning with segmentation achieves 97.96% malaria diagnosis accuracy
articles.title_original: Optimized CNN framework for malaria detection using Otsu thresholding-based image segmentation.
articles.authors: Singh R, Prabha C, Abdulla S
articles.details: Developed CNN framework for P. falciparum detection in blood smears using 43,400 images (70:30 training/test split). Baseline 12-layer CNN: 95% accuracy; hybrid EfficientNet-B7: 97%; with Otsu thresholding segmentation preprocessing: 97.96% accuracy. Manual validation of 100 images (Dice 0.848, Jaccard 0.738) and 5-fold cross-validation (94.8–97.8%) confirmed robustness.

articles.headline: WHO vaccine value profile for next-generation malaria vaccines and antibodies
articles.title_original: Value profile for Malaria vaccines and monoclonal antibodies.
articles.details: WHO-convened expert working group assessment of malaria vaccines and prophylactic monoclonal antibodies. Licensed RTS,S and R21 vaccines showed 13% reduction in all-cause mortality and 22% reduction in severe malaria hospitalizations in pilot introduction. Reviews development status, WHO's 2022 revised preferred product characteristics for improved vaccines, and emerging mAb prophylaxis as prevention tools for African children.

articles.headline: Type-P5 ATPases in malaria parasites structurally characterized by homology modeling
articles.title_original: Homology Modeling of Type-P5 ATPases from the Malaria Parasite: Insight into Their Functions and Evolution, and Implications About the Effect and Role of Intrinsically Disordered Protein Structure.
articles.details: This structural bioinformatics study characterized type-P5 ATPases in Plasmodium parasites using homology modeling based on yeast and human templates. Malaria parasites contain both subtype-P5A and P5B ATPases with distinct structural features including an additional α-helix unique to P5A. The parasite enzymes contain multiple intrinsically disordered loops not found in templates, suggesting lineage-specific functional divergence from yeast and human homologs.

articles.headline: Piper alatipetiolatum oil and constituent 6-ishwarone inhibit P. vivax in vitro
articles.title_original: First Evidence of Anti-Plasmodium vivax (Plasmodiidae): Activity of the Essential Oil and 6-Ishwarone Isolated from Piper alatipetiolatum Yunck. (Piperaceae).
articles.details: Essential oil and 6-ishwarone from Piper alatipetiolatum were tested against P. vivax with IC50 values of 9.25 µg/mL (oil) and 3.93 µg/mL (6-ishwarone), showing dose-dependent inhibition up to 100%. Compounds were selective over mammalian cells (94.7–98.3% viability at 24h). Molecular docking showed 6-ishwarone preferentially binds DHFR (Ki = 2.27 µM) over LDH. ADMET predictions indicated favorable drug-like properties including high GI absorption, BBB permeability, and low toxicity.

articles.headline: Subnational malaria stratification guides intervention targeting in Liberia
articles.title_original: Epidemiological stratification and sub-national tailoring of malaria interventions in Liberia.
articles.details: Liberia conducted epidemiological stratification of malaria risk across 98 health districts using modeled parasite prevalence from the 2022 Malaria Indicator Survey. Median PfPR was 29% (range 17–37%), with 84 districts classified as moderate transmission and 14 as high transmission. Stratification results informed revision of the national operational plan and targeting of eight key interventions including dual-active nets and vaccine scale-up.

articles.headline: Living systematic review of primaquine dosing in P. vivax malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Automated reporting of primaquine dose efficacy, tolerability and safety for Plasmodium vivax malaria using a systematic review and individual patient data meta-analysis.
articles.details: A living systematic review identified efficacy studies of uncomplicated P. vivax malaria with daily primaquine regimens (published since January 1, 2000), integrating individual patient data from 41 studies (9,270 patient records) as of January 16, 2025. An R Shiny app generates automated reports assessing the impact of primaquine dose on efficacy, hematological safety, and gastrointestinal tolerability for user-selected countries/regions to inform local policy decisions.

articles.headline: Malaria documentation largely absent in formal drug retail shops, Ghana
articles.title_original: Exploring documentation and reporting practices in malaria tracking: a mixed-methods study among formal drug retail shops in Hohoe Municipal, Ghana.
articles.details: A concurrent mixed-methods study examined malaria documentation and reporting in 57 formal drug retail facilities (9 community pharmacies, 48 over-the-counter medicine sellers) in Hohoe Municipality, Ghana. Only 5 (10.5%) shops used any documentation/reporting tools; 51 (89.5%) lacked documentation systems. Digital reporting was preferred by 61.4% of attendants. Barriers included stress and resource constraints; improvements required staffing, training, and digital infrastructure.

articles.headline: Blighia sapida leaf extract shows antimalarial activity in mice
articles.title_original: Antimalarial, Antioxidant and Hepatoprotective Activities of Ethanol Extract of Blighia sapida K.D. Koenig (Sapindaceae) in Plasmodium berghei-infected Mice.
articles.details: The ethanol extract of Blighia sapida leaves was evaluated in Swiss mice inoculated with chloroquine-resistant P. berghei using Peter's 4-day suppressive test and Rane's curative test at doses of 50–800 mg/kg. Maximum suppressive and curative activities were 77.02% and 71.94% at 800 mg/kg. The 400 mg/kg dose showed strongest hepatoprotective effects with reduced serum AST (84.93 U/L) and ALT (68.48 U/L) versus negative control (133.88 and 93.92 U/L).

articles.headline: Plant smoke shows knockdown and repellent effects on malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Field evaluation of ethnomedicinal plants' smoke knockdown and repellent efficacy against pre-bedtime biting malaria vectors in Mazowe district, Zimbabwe.
articles.details: The knockdown and repellent efficacy of smoke from three plants (Vitex payos, Peltophorum africanum, Adansonia digitata) was evaluated against An. gambiae s.l. and An. funestus s.l. in Zimbabwe. V. payos and P. africanum achieved 100% knockdown within 150 minutes with kdT50 values of 37.8 and 44.8 minutes respectively and 24-hour mortality of 91.7% and 80%. Smoke from V. payos (86% biting reduction, p = 0.008) and P. africanum (73% reduction, p = 0.028) significantly reduced An. funestus biting.

articles.headline: β-carboline PRC1584 maintains potency against artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Characterization of β-Carboline Derivatives Reveals a High Barrier to Resistance and Potent Activity against Ring-Stage and DHA-Induced Dormant Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: The β-carboline antimalarials PRC1584 and PRC1697 were characterized for activity against P. falciparum including fresh Ugandan isolates. Both compounds demonstrated strong in vitro potency against actively proliferating ring stages and dihydroartemisinin-induced dormant stages, with high resistance barrier to selection of resistant parasites. PRC1584 was unexpectedly associated with increased susceptibility when PfKelch13-C580Y mutation was present. These findings suggest a novel mechanism of action targeting DHA dormancy.
articles.notable_reason: Novel resistance-resistant class active against DHA-dormant parasites

articles.headline: Review of P. falciparum histone lysine methyltransferases as antimalarial targets
articles.title_original: Yet to SET: Plasmodium falciparum Histone Lysine Methyltransferases.
articles.details: This review examines histone lysine methyltransferases (SET domain proteins) in P. falciparum as potential antimalarial drug targets. The parasite has an AT-rich genome with limited heterochromatin and relies heavily on epigenetic control for gene regulation and virulence. While histone acetylation has been extensively studied, other epigenetic effectors including lysine methylation remain understudied despite representing promising intervention opportunities.

articles.headline: Platelet TGF-β1 implicated in cerebral malaria pathogenesis; new therapeutic target
articles.title_original: Platelet-Derived Transforming Growth Factor-β1: A New Hope for Cerebral Malaria Treatment.
articles.details: This review discusses the role of platelet-derived TGF-β1 in P. falciparum cerebral malaria pathogenesis. Platelets interact with infected RBCs and brain endothelium to elevate local TGF-β1 production. Reduced TGF-β1 levels correlate with cerebral malaria severity. TGF-β1 induces endothelial cell apoptosis causing microangiopathy, blood-brain barrier dysfunction, brain edema, and inflammation. The review proposes TGF-β1 as a therapeutic target and suggests exosome-based delivery of TGF-β1 antibodies for brain-targeted treatment.

articles.headline: Cell-permeable FdUTP derivatives inhibit P. falciparum with therapeutic potential
articles.title_original: Membrane-Permeable 5-Fluorodeoxyuridine Triphosphate Derivatives Inhibit the Proliferation of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Cell-permeable derivatives of 5-fluorodeoxyuridine triphosphate (cpFdUTP) were tested for anti-P. falciparum activity in infected human RBCs. cpFdUTP rapidly inhibited parasite proliferation by stalling DNA replication at the trophozoite-to-schizont transition. While cpFdUTP impaired human cell growth, supplementation with thymidine or cell-permeable deoxythymidine triphosphate (cpdTTP) selectively rescued human cells while maintaining parasite inhibition, suggesting a potential therapeutic window.

articles.headline: AsSGU protein from mosquito midgut characterized as vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Cloning, expression and purification of AsSGU protein and its immunogenic response analysis as target candidate for transmission blocking vaccine against malaria.
articles.details: The AsSGU (Anopheles stephensi Secretory Glycoconjugate) protein, a GPI-anchored protein expressed in mosquito midgut after blood feeding, was cloned and expressed in E. coli BL21(DE3) with a yield of 0.2 mg/L. In silico analysis confirmed its immunogenic potential. AsSGU is highly conserved across Anopheles, Culex, and Aedes species and has been shown to have a critical role in ookinete invasion and transmission-blocking activity.

articles.headline: Rainfall and humidity drive malaria risk in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Spatio-temporal dynamics of malaria incidence in children under five years of age and correlation with meteorological variability in the North and Far North regions of Cameroon between 2015-2022.
articles.details: Spatio-temporal analysis of malaria incidence in North and Far North Cameroon (2015-2022) correlated with meteorological factors using distributed lag nonlinear models. Maximum incidence 190.6–243.8 per 1000 person-years. Rainfall >1 mm/day increased transmission risk 1.2–1.4-fold; 70% humidity increased risk 2–3.7-fold by region. Strong year-to-year variation correlated with environmental factors.

articles.headline: Pfsa1 and PfCRT selection signals shape Mali P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Genome-wide SNP genotyping of Plasmodium falciparum isolates across Mali reveals major impacts of Pfsa1 and PfCRT K76T selections on parasite populations.
articles.details: Genome-wide SNP analysis of 458 P. falciparum isolates from Mali (2023) via molecular inversion probes. Strongest selection signals around pfcrt (chloroquine-resistance transporter) gene. Pfsa1 (ACS8 Y40F) mutation associated with sickle trait showed higher PfCRT K76T prevalence (55.8% vs 26.6% wild-type); 80% of K76T mutants harbored linked haplotype with 64% carrying Pfsa1, suggesting co-selection by drug pressure and host genetics.

articles.headline: Differential DNA repair pathways maintain parasite chromosome structure
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum DNA repair dynamics reveal unique roles for TLS polymerases and PfRad51 in genome diversification.
articles.details: Functional study of P. falciparum DNA repair via disruption of PfRad51 and TLS polymerases (PfPolζ, PfRev1). PfRad51 essential for homologous recombination in core genome; subtelomeric regions employ Rad51-independent, homology-directed repair pathway independent of TLS polymerases. Differential repair mechanisms maintain unique chromosomal organization of var and other hypervariable gene families.

articles.headline: Reactive surveillance fidelity varies by region, lowest in Africa
articles.title_original: Implementation and fidelity of reactive surveillance and response strategies for malaria elimination: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 69 studies (43 in meta-analysis) on reactive surveillance and response for malaria elimination. Case investigation completeness and timeliness 100% in China; lowest in Africa (56%) and Greater Mekong Subregion (57%). 1-3-7 strategy adopted in Mekong region; Africa relies mainly on reactive case detection alone. Implementation fidelity differences attributed to regional health system capacity variation.

articles.headline: Migrants in MENA face disproportionate NTD and malaria burden
articles.title_original: The burden, clinical outcomes and risk factors related to neglected tropical diseases and malaria in migrant populations in the Middle East and North Africa: a systematic review and meta-analyses.
articles.details: Systematic review of 39 NTD and 16 malaria studies among MENA migrants. Migrants had higher prevalence vs non-migrants: hookworm 1.8% vs 0.03%, Ascaris 0.3% vs 0%, Trichuris 0.5% vs 0%, dengue 26% vs 3.5%, chikungunya 4.2% vs 0.5%. Malaria case fatality 0.2–1.5%; incidence in Sudan 2.72–7.3/1000. Gaps identified in evidence on risk factors and North African subregion.

articles.headline: Heme detoxification shows stage-dependent parasite development
articles.title_original: Heme processing in the malaria parasite, Plasmodium falciparum: a time-dependent basal-level analysis.
articles.authors: Garnie LF, Egan TJ, Wicht KJ
articles.details: Confocal microscopy and immunofluorescence characterization of P. falciparum digestive vacuole physiology across intraerythrocytic cycle in CQ-sensitive NF54 and CQ-resistant Dd2 parasites. Three developmental phases: lag-type growth (20–28 h), rapid growth (28–40 h), and plateau (40–48 h), each with distinct hemozoin synthesis and hemoglobin degradation kinetics. Key differences between resistant and sensitive strains identified.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria diagnosis delayed in adolescent with sickle cell
articles.title_original: Protracted Febrile Illness and Coma in an Adolescent with Sickle Cell Disease and Malaria.
articles.details: Case report of 15-year-old with sickle cell disease presenting with severe hemolysis, fever, and neuropsychiatric symptoms over 5 weeks after West Africa travel. Cerebral malaria eventually diagnosed and treated with artesunate; recovery occurred but hemolysis recurred 12 days post-treatment. Case illustrates complex interplay of malaria, sickle cell disease, and autoimmune hemolytic anemia; highlights diagnostic delays in non-endemic countries.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi metabolomics reveal stage-specific lipid biosynthesis
articles.title_original: Mass spectrometry-based metabolomics uncovers distinct metabolic signatures and potential therapeutic targets in Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.details: Mass spectrometry-based metabolomic analysis of P. knowlesi across ring, trophozoite, and schizont blood stages. Distinct metabolic profiles identified particularly in ring stage. Glycerophospholipid metabolism and sphingolipid de novo biosynthesis emerged as conserved pathways; phosphatidylserine synthesis linked to ring-stage-biased metabolites. CDP-diacylglycerol-inositol 3-phosphatidyltransferase highlighted as potential drug target.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention regimen questioned in East Africa
articles.title_original: Is Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention with Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine/Amodiaquine Appropriate in East Africa?
articles.details: No abstract provided. Title poses question about appropriateness of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-amodiaquine seasonal malaria chemoprevention in East Africa.

articles.headline: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine far superior to SPAQ for SMC
articles.title_original: Pharmacometric evaluation of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine- amodiaquine and dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine seasonal malaria chemoprevention in northern Uganda.
articles.details: Prospective 28-day pharmacometric assessment of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in 1,250 children in Northern Uganda (baseline parasitemia 46%). Breakthrough parasitemia SPAQ 31% vs DP 7%; clinical malaria SPAQ 8% vs DP 2%. Protective efficacy SPAQ 56–60% vs DP 84–86%. All 260 P. falciparum genotyped were PfCRT K76 (CVMNK, sensitive haplotype) and mostly quintuple Pfdhfr/dhps mutants. DP emerged as significantly more effective.
articles.notable_reason: Comparative efficacy showing DP substantially outperforms SPAQ in high-resistance setting; directly informs SMC policy for East Africa.

articles.headline: Selective proteasome inhibitors show nanomolar P. falciparum potency
articles.title_original: Optimization of Species-Selective Reversible Proteasome Inhibitors for the Treatment of Malaria.
articles.details: Structure-activity relationship optimization of species-selective reversible inhibitors of P. falciparum 20S proteasome, starting from carboxypiperidine scaffold. Lead compounds demonstrated nanomolar potency against blood-stage parasites with selective inhibition over human proteasome. Cryo-EM confirmed binding to β5 subunit. In vivo pharmacokinetics identified promising candidates for further development.

articles.headline: Malaria-typhoid coinfection prevalent among febrile Cameroon patients
articles.title_original: Malaria and Typhoid Fever Coinfection Among Febrile Patients Attending a Health Facility in Yaoundé, Cameroon: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 288 febrile patients in Yaoundé, Cameroon (June–August 2023). Malaria prevalence 44.79%, typhoid fever 37.15%, coinfection 19.79%. Coinfection significantly associated with fever and profuse sweating; jaundice linked to typhoid. Risk factors included stagnant water/grass around house and drinking water source. Malaria identified as leading cause.

articles.headline: β-dystroglycan loss disrupts brain barrier in experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: β-Dystroglycan Downregulation and Astrocytic Alterations: A Possible Role in Blood-Brain Barrier Disruption During Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.details: Experimental cerebral malaria in mice showed increased blood-brain barrier leakage and brain edema by day 7 post-infection, with loss of β-dystroglycan, altered astrocytes, and reduced tight junction proteins (ZO-1, collagen IV). β-dystroglycan cleavage by MMP-9 disrupts astrocytic connections with vasculature.

articles.headline: PbIMC1j protein controls microtubule organization and parasite motility
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei IMC1j interacts with δ-tubulin to orchestrate subpellicular microtubule organization in ookinetes.
articles.details: P. berghei IMC1j deletion impaired asexual stage fitness, virulence, and ookinete motility. PbIMC1j undergoes palmitoylation and interacts with ISC1 and δ-tubulin through its IMCp and C-terminal coiled-coil domains; deletion disorganized subpellicular microtubules.

articles.headline: RON6 protein essential for parasite infectivity and virulence
articles.title_original: Plasmodium berghei rhoptry neck protein 6 maintains parasite infectivity and virulence.
articles.details: P. berghei RON6-depleted parasites failed to maintain infectivity and virulence, resulting in prolonged mouse survival and chronic malaria with hyper-reactive splenomegaly (decreased B/T lymphocytes, loss of follicles). RON6 localizes to sporozoite membrane with extracellular C-terminal domain.

articles.headline: P. falciparum degrades ribosomal RNA during gametocyte development
articles.title_original: Degradation of ribosomal RNA during Plasmodium falciparum gametocytogenesis.
articles.details: Oxford Nanopore RNA sequencing revealed P. falciparum utilizes A-type rRNAs predominantly during blood stages and gametocytogenesis; S-type rRNAs increase in mosquito stages. Extensive rRNA degradation occurs during stage II–V gametocytogenesis, potentially linked to translational repression and metabolic quiescence.

articles.headline: Gene drive mutations form at measurable rates in mosquito germline
articles.title_original: Mutant allele formation and inheritance during Cas9/guide RNA-mediated gene drive in a population modification mosquito strain for human malaria control.
articles.details: Cas9/gRNA-based gene-drive system in Anopheles gambiae generated mutations at average rate of 0.21% per target gene per generation (0.08% paternal, 0.33% maternal testcross). Germline-derived mutations represented 47% of inherited mutations in testcross progeny.

articles.headline: Complement inhibition proposed to reduce malarial anemia via C3 blockade
articles.title_original: Inhibition of Complement Will Substantially Reduce the Anemia of Malaria.
articles.details: Review proposes complement C3b opsonization drives malarial anemia, with ≥10 non-parasitized erythrocytes cleared per parasitized RBC destroyed. Authors hypothesize FDA-approved C3 inhibitors could reduce anemia and propose in vitro validation followed by clinical trial combining artesunate with complement inhibitor.

articles.headline: Review: sporozoite navigation in skin influenced by environmental cues
articles.title_original: Influence of biophysical and biochemical cues on Plasmodium sporozoite dynamics in skin tissue.
articles.details: Review of P. sporozoite motility literature examining how extracellular matrix composition, tissue stiffness, topography, temperature, and humidity influence movement. Identifies knowledge gaps on how age, inflammation, or mechanical stress-induced skin changes affect sporozoite navigation and infection success.

articles.headline: Seasonal chemoprevention reduces childhood malaria by 50% in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Impact of seasonal malaria chemoprevention: a plausibility evaluation of routine data from health facilities in three implementing states in Nigeria.
articles.details: Pre-post evaluation of SMC in Nigeria (36 facilities, 3 states) in children 3-59 months found uncomplicated malaria incidence decreased 50% (95% CI: 39-60%; 20 to 9 per 1000) in 2022 vs 2021. All-cause fever 29% lower (95% CI: 14-41%); impact greatest in Oyo state.

articles.headline: Uganda 24,2 Hours Initiative framework for rapid malaria case management
articles.title_original: The 24, 2 hours initiative: a game changer in malaria mortality reduction.
articles.details: Describes Uganda's 24,2 Hours Initiative emphasizing timely malaria case management: uncomplicated cases treated within 24 hours, severe cases receive pre-referral treatment within 2 hours, post-discharge care within 24 hours. Includes free community-based case management for all ages with results-based monitoring.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine single-dose effective for P. vivax radical cure in EFFORT trial
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of tafenoquine and primaquine for radical cure of Plasmodium vivax: a meeting report from dissemination of results of the EFFORT trial to stakeholders in Pakistan.
articles.details: EFFORT trial across 4 countries including Pakistan found single-dose tafenoquine (300 mg) and 7-day high-dose primaquine (7 mg/kg) well tolerated and effective at preventing P. vivax relapse compared to standard 14-day low-dose primaquine (3.5 mg/kg). Tafenoquine offers significant advantages beyond current WHO 2024 guidelines.
articles.notable_reason: Single-dose tafenoquine validated; paradigm shift for vivax radical cure

articles.headline: Randomized trial protocol: sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine vs artesunate prevention
articles.title_original: Parasite clearance and protection from Plasmodium falciparum infection (PCPI): a two-arm, parallel, double-blinded, placebo-controlled, randomised trial of presumptive sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine versus artesunate monotherapy among asymptomatic children 3-5 years of age in Zambia.
articles.details: Protocol for randomized controlled trial in Zambia (n=600, children 3-5 years) comparing presumptive SP vs 7-day artesunate for perennial malaria chemoprevention, with parasite genotyping to examine K540E dhps mutation association with treatment failure. Primary outcome: parasite clearance time to day 63.

articles.headline: High prevalence of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Polymorphism of the dihydropteroate synthase resistance gene of Plasmodium falciparum in three hospitals in Mbouda, Western Cameroon.
articles.details: Molecular survey of 296 samples from 3 Mbouda hospitals found P. falciparum prevalence 25.34%, with Pfdhps resistance gene in 74.66% (A437G: 56%, A581G: 64%). Mutant alleles predominated (A437G 57.14%, A581G 91.66%), with highest frequencies in ages 6-35 and females.

articles.headline: Malaria vaccine awareness varies widely across Sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Public awareness and acceptance of malaria vaccine in Sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 33 studies published January 2021–March 2025. Malaria vaccine awareness ranged from 7.26% (Democratic Republic of Congo) to 95.6% (Ghana); acceptance from 10.3% to 100%. Education and occupation were consistently associated with greater awareness and acceptance. Healthcare workers were the main information source.

articles.headline: Community trust and prior experience shape MDA program participation
articles.title_original: Community Experiences and Perceptions of the Broad One Health Endectocide-Based Malaria Intervention in Africa Trial of Ivermectin Mass Drug Administration: A Longitudinal Qualitative Study in Kwale County, Kenya.
articles.details: Longitudinal qualitative study within BOHEMIA ivermectin MDA trial in Kwale County, Kenya (22 in-depth interviews, structured observations, 15 focus group discussions). Community widely perceived MDA as effective for reducing mosquitoes and malaria. Trust in implementing institution and prior positive experiences fostered participation; previous negative MDA experiences reduced involvement.

articles.headline: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine maintains efficacy; no resistance detected
articles.title_original: Treatment Efficacy of Dihydroartemisinin-Piperaquine for Uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax Malaria in Timika, Papua, Indonesia.
articles.details: Efficacy study in Timika, Papua, Indonesia (75 P. falciparum and 75 P. vivax patients). Three-day supervised dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine showed cumulative same-species recurrence of 1.5% (95% CI 0–7.9%) for P. falciparum and 5.2% (95% CI 1.1–14.1%) for P. vivax by day 42. No Kelch13 mutations or plasmepsin 2-3 copy number increases detected despite dual use for treatment and intermittent preventive treatment in pregnancy.

articles.headline: Actin-like proteins essential for male gametogenesis and transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium actin-like proteins are essential for DNA segregation during male gametogenesis and malaria transmission.
articles.details: Molecular study of P. berghei identifying Alp5a and Alp5b as essential for DNA segregation during male gametogenesis (three rounds DNA replication producing eight nucleated flagellar gametes). While individually dispensable during asexual blood stages, Alp5a or Alp5b knockout parasites produced defective male gametes, resulting in significantly smaller oocysts that failed to mature and blocked transmission.
articles.notable_reason: Novel transmission-blocking mechanism via male gametogenesis

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure reveals Pfs230:Pfs48/45 vaccine target architecture
articles.title_original: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Pfs230:Pfs48/45 complex with six antibodies reveals mechanisms of malaria transmission-blocking activity.
articles.details: Cryo-electron microscopy structure of endogenous Pfs230:Pfs48/45 complex bound to six transmission-blocking antibodies. Revealed Pfs230 with multiple domain clusters and membrane-anchored Pfs48/45 forming disk-like structure. Identified conserved epitopes and structural basis for complement-dependent activity of Pfs230-targeting antibodies, informing design of next-generation P. falciparum transmission-blocking vaccines.
articles.notable_reason: Structure of leading transmission-blocking vaccine candidate

articles.headline: Majority of Sub-Saharan African malaria trials lack post-trial access
articles.title_original: Post-trial access practices in conducted clinical trials for Malaria, Tuberculosis, and Neglected Tropical Diseases (NTDs) across Sub-Saharan African countries: A quantitative study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 37 principal investigators, coordinators, and sponsors conducting malaria trials in sub-Saharan Africa (2008–2019). 43% did not provide post-trial access (PTA) plans for participants. Overall lack of formalized PTA policies and commitments documented. 70.3% expressed need for PTA training, highlighting significant gaps in research ethics and benefit-sharing practices.

articles.headline: Severe malaria in children: 4.5% mortality; respiratory distress key risk
articles.title_original: Characteristics and mortality factors of severe malaria in children and teenager aged from 5 to 15 years in Thiès region, Senegal.
articles.details: Retrospective multicenter study of 109 children aged 5–15 years with WHO-defined severe malaria in Senegal (July 2019–June 2024). Common features: severe anemia (33%), thrombocytopenia (61.6%), prostration (27%), convulsions (21.1%). Treated with injectable artesunate followed by artemisinin-based combination therapy (82%); outcomes favorable in 90%, with 4.5% mortality and 5.5% neurological/functional sequelae. Mortality significantly associated with respiratory distress and ≥2 WHO severity criteria.

articles.headline: VAR2CSA epitopes preferentially recognized by protective antibodies identified
articles.title_original: Identification of Plasmodium falciparum VAR2CSA peptides differentially recognized by IgG of multigravidae through epitope excision.
articles.details: Epitope excision study comparing VAR2CSA-specific IgG in multigravidae with functional anti-adhesion antibodies versus primigravidae without such antibodies. Multigravidae IgG differentially recognized ten epitopes conserved across multiple VAR2CSA alleles (Log2 fold change >1, p<0.05). Identified epitopes preferentially recognized by immune multigravidae will inform VAR2CSA subunit vaccine design.

articles.headline: IMC55 protein not required for P. falciparum asexual replication
articles.title_original: The inner membrane complex protein, IMC55, is dispensable for intra-erythrocytic development of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Functional study of IMC55, an inner membrane complex protein, using inducible conditional systems. Despite genome-wide transposon mutagenesis predictions of essentiality, IMC55 was not required for asexual replication, with no defects in replicative fitness during blood stage development. Function is either dispensable or redundant for intra-erythrocytic development.

articles.headline: Malaria burden and parasite density vary significantly by ecological zone
articles.title_original: Clinical malaria in three ecological zones in the Volta and Oti Regions of Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 459 participants in three ecological zones (forest, coastal, savannah) in Ghana. Malaria prevalence 15.2% by RDT (highest in savannah). Geometric mean parasite density significantly higher in coastal zone (11,715.6 parasites/µL) versus savannah (7,616.4) and forest (5,880.1; p=0.0003). Anemia prevalence 79.3% overall with location-specific variation (94.1% savannah vs 69.8% forest).

articles.headline: Healthcare workers show strong RDT interpretation agreement in Uganda
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording of malaria rapid diagnostic test results in Uganda.
articles.details: Prospective study of 40,049 RDT images from 16 public health facilities in Uganda (June–November 2023) comparing healthcare worker interpretations to external panel readings. Overall agreement strong (κ 0.81, 95% CI 0.76–0.85), higher in Busoga (κ 0.86) than Lango (κ 0.78). HCWs more frequently misrecorded results as positive (7.2%) than negative (1.9%). Certain RDT products and faint lines associated with lower agreement.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence and mortality fluctuate in Sennar State, Sudan
articles.title_original: Epidemiological trends of malaria in Sennar State, Sudan.
articles.details: Descriptive cross-sectional survey in Sennar State, Sudan (2018–2020) of 2,027 households covering 8,372 individuals. Malaria prevalence rose from 2.8% (2018) to 4.8% (2019) then fell to 3.3% (2020). Abu Hujar locality reported highest prevalence (13.6% in 2019, 9.5% in 2020). Malaria-related deaths increased from 12 annually (2018–2019) to 22 in 2020, predominantly in rural areas.

articles.headline: Cuproptosis-driven astrocyte reactivity exacerbates cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Cuproptosis-driven astrocyte reactivity exacerbates experimental cerebral malaria pathogenesis.
articles.details: Experimental study in P. berghei ANKA-infected mice investigated cuproptosis—a copper-dependent cell death pathway—in cerebral malaria pathogenesis. Disulfiram (copper ionophore) exacerbated disease with amplified parasitemia, BBB permeability, and cerebral edema; tetrathiomolybdate (copper chelator) counteracted these effects. Results demonstrate cuproptosis drives astrocyte reactivity and suggest copper homeostasis modulation as a potential therapeutic approach.

articles.headline: Laboratory workers rapidly develop LAMP competency through training
articles.title_original: Evaluation of laboratory workers competency on loop mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) technology for malaria diagnostics: a technology transfer assessment study (LAMP-TTA) during a multi-site clinical trial.
articles.authors: Tegegne B, Kamaliddin C, Pillai DR
articles.details: Technology transfer assessment of loop-mediated isothermal amplification (LAMP) training for 15 laboratory technicians (14 with no prior LAMP experience). Post-training technical knowledge score increased to median 5/5, and LAMP chemistry knowledge to median 10/10. Follow-up assessments confirmed sustained competency with median raw results interpretation score of 10/10.

articles.headline: Malaria messages increase insecticide-treated net use in Uganda
articles.title_original: Effect of exposure to malaria messages on insecticide-treated net use among women and under-five children in Uganda: a propensity score matched analysis.
articles.details: Propensity score matched analysis of Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey 2018-19 (8,868 women, 6,915 children under-five). Exposure to malaria behavioral change messages significantly increased ITN use with treatment effects of 5.1% (95% CI: 1.5-8.9%) in women and 4.3% (95% CI: 1.0-7.6%) in children under-five. Authors conclude health communications effectively increase ITN uptake.

articles.headline: Submicroscopic infections sustain residual malaria in Amazonian Peru
articles.title_original: Submicroscopic infections and two malaria vectors are major contributors to residual malaria in an Amazonian village in Peru.
articles.details: Year-long cohort study (n=353) in Loreto, Peru documented 17:1 ratio of submicroscopic to microscopic Plasmodium vivax infections. Despite high net usage, outdoor workers had significantly higher P. vivax infection rates (35% vs 21%, p=0.012). Both Nyssorhynchus darlingi and Anopheles near costai G1 detected with Plasmodium infections; degraded forest identified as most pathogenic landscape.

articles.headline: Bayesian method improves classification of falciparum treatment failure
articles.title_original: Probabilistic classification of late treatment failure in uncomplicated falciparum malaria.
articles.details: Developed PfRecur software using Bayesian posterior probabilities to distinguish recrudescence from reinfection in recurrent Plasmodium falciparum malaria. Addresses limitations of match-counting methods in polyclonal infections common in high-transmission settings. Software demonstrated using Angola data; provides more accurate treatment efficacy estimates by resolving complex polyclonal parasite genotypes.

articles.headline: Non-pyrethroid IRS dramatically reduces malaria in Zambian district
articles.title_original: Impact of malaria vector control interventions implemented in Luangwa District southeastern of Zambia: A 13-year observational time series analysis of malaria trends.
articles.details: Thirteen-year observational study (2009-2021) in Luangwa, Zambia analyzed HMIS data using generalized linear mixed models. IRS with pirimiphos-methyl CS reduced inpatient severe malaria admissions (RR 0.28-0.46, P<0.05) and total cases (RR 0.25-0.66, P<0.01) for up to 12 months. Over the full period, >90% reduction in severe admissions and >80% reduction in total cases attributed to non-pyrethroid IRS scaling in 2014 and increased health facilities.

articles.headline: Community experiences with malaria control vary in rural South Africa
articles.title_original: Experiences of Community Members and Health Workers Regarding Malaria Control Programmes in the Rural Ingwavuma Community, uMkhanyakude District Municipality, KwaZulu-Natal Province, South Africa.
articles.authors: Thembakazi MT, Herbert C, Nelisiwe K
articles.details: Qualitative study with 8 healthcare worker interviews and 8 focus groups (88 community members) in Ingwavuma, South Africa. Communities generally supported malaria control activities (health education, IRS, vector surveillance), with strong rapport with control personnel. Healthcare workers noted challenges from cross-border movements affecting surveillance. Some communities reported negative experiences with IRS and occasional resistance to spraying activities.

articles.headline: Leveraging malaria microscopy infrastructure for skin disease diagnosis
articles.title_original: Leveraging malaria microscopy infrastructure to diagnose common and neglected skin diseases using direct microscopy in Sumba, Indonesia.
articles.details: Study title suggests using existing malaria microscopy infrastructure in Sumba, Indonesia to diagnose skin diseases. Specific diseases, sample sizes, diagnostic accuracy, and detailed methods unavailable from title alone.

articles.headline: Ivermectin trial in Mozambique did not reduce malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to reduce malaria transmission- safety and efficacy results from the BOHEMIA cluster randomized trial in Mozambique.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized trial (n=22,724 from 100 clusters) in Mopeia, Mozambique evaluated ivermectin (400 mcg/kg monthly for 3 months) to humans alone or with livestock, compared to albendazole control. Six months post-treatment, malaria incidence comparable across arms (adjusted IRR 0.99, 95% CI 0.86-1.14, P>0.9 for human-only). Severe adverse events similar across groups (IRR 1.29, 95% CI 0.62-2.68). Trial failed to meet implementation goals; efficacy data inconclusive.

articles.headline: School-based dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine reduces malaria in Tanzania children
articles.title_original: Implementation and effectiveness of intermittent preventive treatment in school aged children using dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine to reduce malaria burden: an implementation research of a cluster randomised trial in Tanzania.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized implementation trial (February 2020-July 2021) in Tanzania with 1,971 schoolchildren receiving three annual dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine doses via schoolteachers versus 1,781 controls. IPT reduced clinical malaria incidence 41% overall (95% CI: 31-49, p<0.001) and 54% in high-endemic areas (95% CI: 44.2-62.6, p<0.001). Parasite prevalence decreased 81% overall. Coverage reached 73-80%; drug well-tolerated.

articles.headline: Metabolic resistance genes metabolize PAH pollutants in malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Investigating the potential role of metabolic resistance genes in conferring cross-resistance to pyrethroids and polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon pollutants in the major malaria vector Anopheles coluzzii.
articles.details: Laboratory study tested metabolism of polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbons (PAHs) by pyrethroid-resistant Anopheles coluzzii microsomal preparations and recombinant P450 enzymes. Microsomes efficiently depleted fluorene (73%±0.5) and fluoranthene (43%±2.2). Recombinant CYP6P3 and CYP6Z3 metabolized fluorene (47.9% and 52.8%); CYP6Z2/Z3 metabolized fluoranthene (50.4% and 60.3%). Results suggest environmental PAH exposure could select for metabolic insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: High MSP2 genetic diversity indicates intense P. falciparum transmission
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of MSP2 and its implications for Plasmodium falciparum resistance in N'Djamena, Chad.
articles.details: Cross-sectional molecular epidemiology study (n=302) in N'Djamena, Chad determined Plasmodium falciparum prevalence of 40.72%. MSP2 gene analysis of 101 positive samples revealed 12 alleles with genetic diversity index of 9.37% and multiplicity of infections of 1.26 (73% monoclonal, 25.74% double-clonal). Phylogenetic analysis identified 11 clades across four health facilities.

articles.headline: RDT recording accuracy in Nigerian health facilities assessed
articles.title_original: Accuracy of recording and reporting of malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study in 16 health facilities across Oyo and Sokoto States analyzing 18,319 RDTs. Overall agreement 90.2%, pooled kappa 0.80 (95% CI 0.75–0.85), PPV 87.2%, NPV 92.9%. 6.2% misrecorded as positive, 3.7% as negative. Faint test lines (8.8% of RDTs) associated with increased misrecording as negative. Study highlights need for improved training and supervision.

articles.headline: Malaria knowledge and prevention success in Upper River Region, Gambia
articles.title_original: Evaluation of knowledge, perception, and attitude of malaria burden in the Upper River region of The Gambia.
articles.details: Descriptive survey of 381 household heads across 7 districts in Upper River Region. Knowledge index 81%. Over 12 years (2011–2023), malaria cases reduced 87% in individuals >5 years and 45% in <5 years; mortality decreased 98% and 89% respectively. Bed nets identified as most effective prevention tool. Study demonstrates high population understanding of malaria concepts supporting infection control.

articles.headline: Antenatal care adequacy linked to antimalarial uptake in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Association between adequacy of antenatal care and uptake of intermittent preventive treatment of malaria in pregnancy among women in Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 3,692 pregnant women from 2022 Ghana DHS. IPTp-SP uptake ≥3 doses was 61.3%. Women with ≥8 ANC visits had 1.50-fold higher odds of ≥3 IPTp-SP doses (p=0.001); first ANC in first trimester had 1.74-fold higher odds (p<0.001); receipt of all ANC components associated with 1.62-fold higher odds (p<0.001). Adequate ANC independently predicts IPTp-SP uptake.

articles.headline: Structural modeling reveals child health factors driving malaria in Uganda
articles.title_original: Structural equation modelling (SEM) for malaria prevalence and risk factors in Uganda.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 2018–2019 Uganda Malaria Indicator Survey using structural equation modeling. Child Health strongest predictor of malaria (β=0.22, p<0.001); Environment showed marginal negative association (β=−0.36, p=0.056). Model fit indices: CFI=0.786, TLI=0.630, RMSEA=0.064, SRMR=0.053. Socioeconomic Status and Maternal Health not statistically significant. Framework captures meaningful relationships in malaria transmission.

articles.headline: Modeling mobility reveals regional malaria elimination priorities in Bangladesh
articles.title_original: Identifying malaria elimination strategies in the presence of human movement in Bangladesh.
articles.details: Metapopulation malaria model parameterized by human mobility data applied to Chittagong Hill Tracts. Substantial heterogeneity in transmission intensity across districts despite similar observed incidence. Greatest impact of vector control interventions in high-transmission areas with high connectivity to populated western districts. Local elimination in low/intermediate incidence areas had moderate overall impact, indicating that high-incidence areas alone insufficient for elimination.

articles.headline: Pfs230D12 identified as potent transmission-blocking vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Pfs230 domain 12 is a potent malaria transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.
articles.details: Eight Pfs230 single-domain fragments assessed in mouse immunizations. D12-specific antibodies showed strong functional transmission-reducing activity in membrane feeding assays with complement dependence. D12 murine antibodies reduced mosquito transmission from naturally infected parasite carriers. D12 antigen recognized by naturally exposed individuals with antibody levels increasing with age. Study identifies Pfs230D12 as promising transmission-blocking vaccine candidate.
articles.notable_reason: Novel TBV domain with demonstrated functional transmission-blocking activity

articles.headline: Malaria risk stratification guides intervention selection across Mali
articles.title_original: Geo-epidemiological risk stratification to select malaria interventions, case of Mali.
articles.details: Analysis of all 75 Health Districts in Mali (2018–2022) using WHO stratification framework. Four strata defined; 54 HDs in highest/moderate transmission covering 84% population (~18 million). Identified 61/75 HDs eligible for LLIN distribution, 57 for SMC, 67 for IPTp, 19 for vaccination. Strategy prioritized 55 high-burden districts for continued SMC, ITN distribution, and vaccination introduction.

articles.headline: US babesiosis burden exceeds malaria with substantial funding disparity
articles.title_original: Babesiosis and Malaria in the United States: Epidemiology, Research Funding, Medical Progress, & Recommendations for Improvement.
articles.details: Analysis of CDC and NIH data for 2018–2022. Confirmed babesiosis cases 9,799 versus malaria 7,722; babesiosis exceeded malaria in 4 of 5 years. Deaths: 70 babesiosis versus 32 malaria. NIH funding ~$4 million for babesiosis versus ~$169 million for malaria in 2024. For malaria: >90% imported cases, ~95% linked to no chemoprophylaxis, P. falciparum most common cause. Babesiosis highlighted as neglected disease despite high domestic burden.

articles.headline: Ensemble deep learning achieves 97.93% malaria microscopy detection accuracy
articles.title_original: An optimized transfer learning approach integrating deep convolutional feature extractors for malaria parasite classification in erythrocyte microscopy.
articles.details: Ensemble combining VGG16, ResNet50V2, DenseNet201, and VGG19 for automated malaria diagnosis. Test accuracy 97.93% with F1-score and precision 0.9793 each, outperforming Custom CNN (97.20%), VGG16 (97.65%), and CNN-SVM (82.47%). Method effectively classifies parasitized and uninfected blood smears with high reliability, offering scalable automated solution reducing manual microscopy reliance in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: First in situ P. falciparum ribosome structures visualized with cryo-ET
articles.title_original: Seeing is believing-Plasmodium falciparum translation in action.
articles.details: Cryoelectron tomography and cryoelectron microscopy resolve ten P. falciparum ribosomal states within infected erythrocytes, five previously unseen in eukaryotes. Describes parasite-specific translation dynamics and demonstrates how antimalarial cabamiquine disrupts elongation. Provides more comprehensive understanding of parasite translation elongation cycle.
articles.notable_reason: First in situ P. falciparum ribosome visualization; novel parasite biology insights

articles.headline: Severe thrombocytopenia present in 27.4% of hospitalized malaria patients
articles.title_original: Prevalence and factors associated with severe thrombocytopenia among hospitalized patients with malaria attending Kigeme District Hospital, Southern Province, Rwanda: cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 124 hospitalized malaria patients in Rwanda (Dec 2023–Nov 2024). Severe thrombocytopenia prevalence 27.4%. Associated factors: age ≤5 years (AOR=5.17), Plasmodium species type (AOR=3.4), positive CRP (AOR=3.45), jaundice (AOR=2.875), epistaxis (AOR=1.84), high serum urea (AOR=1.73). Cerebral malaria associated with lower thrombocytopenia likelihood (AOR=0.189). Highlights need for early identification in severe malaria patients.

articles.headline: Optimized artemether-lumefantrine dosing safe, efficacious in infants <5kg
articles.title_original: Pharmacokinetics, safety and efficacy of an optimized dose of artemether-lumefantrine in the treatment of acute uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in neonates and infants of less than 5 kg body weight: a multicentre, open-label, single-arm phase 2/3 study (CALINA).
articles.details: Phase 2/3 study of 5mg:60mg artemether-lumefantrine dispersible tablet (vs. standard 20mg:120mg) in infants <5kg with P. falciparum malaria. Cohort 1 (N=22): artemether Cmax 68.0 ng/mL (90% CI 45.1–103 ng/mL) meeting primary endpoint; Cohort 2 (N=6): Cmax 62.2 ng/mL. PCR-corrected Day 29 ACPR 95.5% (Cohort 1) and 100% (Cohort 2). Well-tolerated; developmental assessments at 12 months normal.
articles.notable_reason: First evidence-based optimal dosing for P. falciparum in infants <5kg

articles.headline: Climate change linked to malaria increase in Zimbabwe
articles.title_original: Climate change and malaria in Chiredzi District, Zimbabwe: emerging evidence and pathways towards malaria prevention.
articles.details: Study combined weather station data, malaria incidence, and community insights from 2010-2022 in Chiredzi District to examine climate-malaria links. Evidence showed increasing rainfall and temperature correlated with higher malaria incidence (p=0.0479), though socioeconomic factors including poverty and migration also influenced transmission. Results suggest need for climate-adapted prevention programs.

articles.headline: SMC provides temporary malaria reduction in seasonal transmission
articles.title_original: Trends in uncomplicated and severe malaria following seasonal malaria chemoprevention administration in Nouna, Burkina Faso: a quasi-experimental pre-post study.
articles.details: Pre-post analysis of 285 villages in Nouna, Burkina Faso assessed seasonal malaria chemoprevention impact on under-5s across four rounds. Uncomplicated malaria rates of 8.5 per 1000 person-weeks during administration dropped 41% (95% CI 31-50%), 34% (23-43%), and 22% (12-31%) in the three weeks post-SMC, with severe malaria declining 47%, 47%, and 34% respectively. Protection was substantial but temporary, with rates increasing by week three.

articles.headline: COVID-19 caused dramatic collapse in malaria services in Africa
articles.title_original: Malaria healthcare service utilization before, during, and 1 year into the COVID-19 pandemic in 20 sub-Saharan African countries using Generalized Estimating Equation.
articles.details: Analysis of 4,726 adults across 20 malaria-endemic African countries showed malaria service utilization collapsed one year into COVID-19 pandemic. Testing dropped from 72% to 59%, healthcare visits plummeted from 80% to 1% (AOR=0.003, 95% CI 0.002-0.005), and treatment fell from 96% to 15% (AOR=0.004, 95% CI 0.002-0.006). This disruption highlights vulnerability of malaria control services to pandemic-related interruptions.

articles.headline: Education and social context shape malaria vaccine uptake in Ghana
articles.title_original: Untangling choices: how awareness and socio-economic status shape malaria vaccination decisions among Ghanaian women.
articles.details: Analysis of 3,004 Ghanaian women from 2019 malaria survey using statistical mechanics framework showed 93.54% intended to vaccinate children. Education and vaccine awareness were significantly associated with vaccination choices when social interactions included. Rural uneducated women showed greater social influence on vaccination decisions, while urban educated women demonstrated limited imitative effects (-4.06%), highlighting importance of education and community-specific messaging.

articles.headline: Health facility messages predict higher mosquito net use in West Africa
articles.title_original: A comparative analysis of the predictive influence of access to malaria messages on utilization of mosquito nets by under-five children in West Africa.
articles.details: Analysis of post-2020 Malaria Indicator Surveys across Liberia, Mali, Niger, and Nigeria (nationally representative women 15-49) found net use by under-5s ranged from 47% to 86%. Multilevel regression showed malaria information from health facilities significantly predicted net use (country-specific ORs: Mali 9.90, Niger 3.67, Liberia 1.31, Nigeria 0.31), emphasizing importance of facility-based health communication.

articles.headline: Avian malaria exposure slightly alters mosquito microbiota composition
articles.title_original: Effects of avian Plasmodium exposure on the microbiota of Culex pipiens.
articles.details: Culex pipiens mosquitoes that fed on sparrows infected with Plasmodium relictum (lineage SGS1) showed modest microbiota changes compared to unexposed controls. While alpha and beta diversity were unchanged, exposed mosquitoes had higher abundance of Bacteroidaceae and Bacteroides families. Pathway enrichment analysis showed biosynthesis and metabolism pathways enriched in exposed mosquitoes, suggesting Plasmodium exposure modulates bacterial communities.

articles.headline: Correction: EEG features and cerebral malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Correction to: Electroencephalogram Features Distinguish Cases of Cerebral Malaria Among Malawian Children With Fever and Coma.
articles.details: Correction to a previous study on electroencephalography features in cerebral malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: EEG accurately distinguishes cerebral malaria from febrile coma causes
articles.title_original: Electroencephalogram Features Distinguish Cases of Cerebral Malaria Among Malawian Children With Fever and Coma.
articles.details: Retrospective case-control analysis of 203 Malawian children with cerebral malaria and 87 with nonmalarial febrile coma (2013-2021). Qualitative and quantitative EEG features distinguished coma etiology: qualitative AUROC=0.86, quantitative AUROC=0.85, combined AUROC=0.90. Cerebral malaria showed higher voltage, slower frequency, reduced variability, and abnormal organization, providing diagnostic differentiation in malaria-endemic settings.

articles.headline: Venezuelan migration predicts imported malaria risk in Trinidad and Tobago
articles.title_original: Assessing the Potential for a Malaria Outbreak in Trinidad and Tobago Due to Migrant Populations From Guyana and Venezuela for the Period 2009-2019.
articles.details: Census of 211 malaria cases in Trinidad and Tobago (2009-2019) showed 91.9% were imported, predominantly P. vivax (68.2%) from Guyana (39.7%) and Venezuela (38.7%). Linear regression found correlation between Venezuelan arrivals and malaria cases (r=0.642, R²=0.412, p=0.033), indicating population movement from high-risk areas significantly predicts malaria incidence in the country.

articles.headline: Antimalarial drugs differ in mitochondrial effects on infected hosts
articles.title_original: Comparative effects of antimalarial drugs on oxidative phosphorylation, mitochondrial dynamics and mitophagy in Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.
articles.details: Study in chloroquine-resistant Plasmodium berghei-infected mice compared effects of amodiaquine artesunate (AA), artemether-lumefantrine (AL), sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), and artesunate (ART) on mitochondrial function. ART alone opened mitochondrial permeability pore (7-fold, p<0.01). SP and AA induced mitochondrial lipid peroxidation (p<0.01) and silenced complex expressions. Drugs differed significantly in effects on fission/fusion and autophagy genes, suggesting host mitochondrial response varies by drug.

articles.headline: Environmental barriers restrict resistance gene flow in West African vectors
articles.title_original: Genomic population structure and insecticide resistance mechanisms in the malaria vector An. coluzzii across contrasting bioclimatic zones in West Africa.
articles.details: Genomic study of Anopheles coluzzii populations across West African bioclimatic zones revealed restricted gene flow between northern savannah and southern forest regions, impacting insecticide resistance distribution. Ghana analysis showed differential resistance profiles: northern regions had greater pyrethroid and metabolic cross-resistance from different insecticide use patterns, while coastal areas showed distinct mechanisms linked to agricultural intensity.

articles.headline: 4-Quinolone compounds show multistage antimalarial activity in vitro
articles.title_original: Evaluation of the Activity of 4‑Quinolones against Multi-Life Stages of Plasmodium spp.
articles.details: Evaluation of novel 4-quinolone derivatives, particularly natural compound 1 and synthetic derivatives 2-6, demonstrated significant activity against multiple Plasmodium life stages. Compounds showed activity against sexual stages (ex vivo and in vivo assays) and prevented hepatic cell infections, with compound 1 displaying highest activity against both sexual and asexual stages. Authors conclude these multistage antimalarials warrant further development.

articles.headline: Drug resistance SNPs and vaccine antigen in Southwest Cameroon
articles.title_original: Targeted Sequencing Identifies SNPs Associated with Antimalarial Drug Resistance and the CSP Vaccine Antigen in Plasmodium falciparum from Southwest Cameroon.
articles.details: Targeted Oxford Nanopore sequencing of resistance genes in 72 P. falciparum samples from Southwest Cameroon. High pfdhfr mutation prevalence: 98.6% N51I, 98.6% C59R, 97.7% S108N; pfmdr1 Y184F 76.1%; pfdhps mutations (54.2% S436A, 38.9% A581G); no WHO-validated pfkelch13 artemisinin resistance markers but K189T 63.4%, R255K 4.2%; 19 non-synonymous SNPs in pfcsp. Supports continued ACT use; sustained molecular surveillance recommended.

articles.headline: Travel and rainfall drive malaria importation to Zanzibar
articles.title_original: Integrating mobility, travel survey, and malaria case data to understand drivers of malaria importation to Zanzibar, 2022-2023.
articles.details: Prospective travel surveys at 94 health facilities in Zanzibar (May 2022-Dec 2023) combined with digital mobility and rainfall analysis. Of 3,875 malaria cases, 1,172 reported mainland travel within 30 days (55% in 2022, 27% in 2023). Children and females had higher odds of travel-associated malaria (aOR 3.2, 95% CI 2.2-4.8; aOR 1.5, 95% CI 1.3-1.8). Imported cases correlated with rainfall at mainland destinations, not Zanzibar.

articles.headline: Insecticide-treated net use factors in Burkina Faso
articles.title_original: Factors associated with insecticide-treated mosquito nets utilization for malaria prevention in Burkina Faso: finding from cross-sectional household survey.
articles.details: Cross-sectional household survey in Burkina Faso assessing factors associated with ITN use. Polyester net textile increased use odds (OR 1.23, 95% CI 1.13-1.33); older nets (≥12 months, OR 0.52, 95% CI 0.401-0.675) decreased use. Excessive ITN possession (OR 0.66, 95% CI 0.597-0.74) and higher household wealth (rich OR 0.656, very rich OR 0.557) associated with reduced use. Strong associations identified between textile type, net age, possession level, and wealth with ITN utilization.

articles.headline: Ferulenol inhibits blood and sexual stages of P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Disruption of mitochondrial function in blood and sexual stages of Plasmodium falciparum by ferulenol.
articles.details: In vitro study of ferulenol effects on P. falciparum lifecycle stages. Highest dose inhibited asexual blood-stage proliferation 88%, early-stage gametocytes 82%, stage V gametocytes ~90%. Male gamete formation inhibited 81%, female 27%. Ferulenol targets mitochondrial function in both asexual and sexual stages with greater impact on male gametogenesis, suggesting dual antimalarial activity with transmission-blocking potential.

articles.headline: Insecticide resistance management plans in three African countries
articles.title_original: Managing insecticide resistance in malaria vectors in Africa: case studies from Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire and Tanzania.
articles.details: Mixed-methods evaluation of 5-year insecticide resistance management (IRM) plans in Cameroon, Côte d'Ivoire, and Tanzania. Plans exist and align with guidelines but implementation hampered by inadequate domestic funding and heavy external donor reliance. Limited sentinel site coverage; ITN campaigns increasingly use targeted approaches; IRS sparsely deployed. Major challenges include inactive monitoring sites and limited data availability. Increased domestic financing, realistic plan updates aligned with available resources, and localized surveillance critical for effective vector control.

articles.headline: IL-2 levels not associated with malaria severity
articles.title_original: Association between interleukin-2 cytokine levels and Plasmodium infections: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 30 studies on IL-2 levels in malaria. No significant difference between malaria patients and controls (P=0.25, SMD 4.56, 95% CI [-3.16 to 12.29], n=1,074) or between severe and non-severe malaria (P=0.57, SMD 0.37, 95% CI [-0.91 to 1.67], n=694). High heterogeneity observed (I²=98.6%, I²=97.4% respectively). Geographic region significantly influenced IL-2 patterns. IL-2 is not a consistent biomarker for malaria infection or severity.

articles.headline: Prolonged dysregulation in pediatric central nervous system malaria
articles.title_original: Prolonged dysregulation of gene expression and biomarkers of coagulation, fibrinolysis, and endothelium in pediatric central nervous system malaria.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study in Kenyan children with central nervous system malaria (n=37), uncomplicated malaria (n=20), or acute febrile non-malaria illness (n=12). CNS malaria showed prolonged dysregulation at 6 weeks post-treatment with elevated biomarkers of coagulation, fibrinolysis, neutrophil activation, and cell-free DNA persisting 6 weeks, while markers normalized in other groups. CNS-malaria-specific transcriptome pathways included megakaryocyte production and IL-4/IL-13/IL-10 signaling. Persistent dysregulation may associate with neurocognitive sequelae.

articles.headline: Structural equation modeling of urban malaria in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Modeling urban malaria infection in Anopheles stephensi hotspot area in Eastern Ethiopia: application of Structural Equation Modeling.
articles.details: Facility-based cross-sectional study of 329 febrile patients in Dire Dawa, Ethiopia using structural equation modeling. Wealth index negatively affected ITN use (-0.66, p<0.001) and malaria knowledge (-0.63, p<0.001). Attitude positively affected ITN use (0.16, p=0.049). History of travel outside city significantly increased malaria infection risk (0.969, p=0.01). Indirect pathways through attitude and ITN utilization significantly influenced infection risk (p=0.038, p=0.048 respectively). Results demonstrate complex relationships between wealth, knowledge, and malaria outcomes.

articles.headline: Magnetic nanoparticles for sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine drug delivery
articles.title_original: Advancing malaria treatment with magnetic and mesoporous silica nanoparticles loaded with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine: a review.
articles.authors: Egza TF, Kebede EM, Perumal RD
articles.details: Review of magnetic and mesoporous silica nanoparticles as drug delivery platforms for sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP), addressing reduced bioavailability and drug resistance. In vitro and in vivo studies demonstrated improved pharmacokinetics, reduced toxicity, and enhanced antimalarial efficacy against resistant parasites. Nanoparticles enable targeted delivery via magnetic fields, sustained release, and potential for drug codelivery. While promising for resource-limited settings, scalability, biocompatibility, and regulatory pathway challenges must be resolved prior to clinical translation.

articles.headline: Novel food vacuole transporter mutations and drug resistance
articles.title_original: Role of novel mutations in food vacuole transporters beyond K13-mediated artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Proteome analysis identified 16 food vacuole transporter proteins; SNP analysis of 2,517 field isolates from Pf3K and MalariaGEN databases revealed coassociation of six mutations in four transporter genes (PfCRT, PfNT1, PfCTR2, PfMDR2) with PfKelch13 polymorphisms (P<0.0001). Yeast complementation studies showed mutant phenotypes (PfNT1-F394L, PfMFR5-S278T, PfMFR5-Y570F) conferred resistance to dihydroartemisinin. Study identifies novel food vacuole transporters contributing to artemisinin resistance beyond K13 mutations.

articles.headline: ASHA worker fidelity in malaria case management
