articles.title_original: Implementation Fidelity of Accredited Social Health Activist (ASHA) Workers in Malaria Case Management in a High-Burden District of North India.
articles.details: Community-based cross-sectional study in Nuh district, Haryana, India assessing ASHA (accredited social health activist) worker fidelity in malaria case management (May-Sept 2019). Of 440 under-five children, 222 (50.5%) had fever in previous month; only 4 (1.8%) mothers first contacted ASHA (benchmark 80%, p<0.05). Among 8 malaria-positive cases: treatment initiation and completion 100%, follow-up 87.5% (7/8). ASHAs demonstrated strong protocol adherence but remain underutilized as first contact for fever cases.

articles.headline: Malaria's immune and hematological costs in chimpanzees
articles.title_original: Hidden hematological, biochemical and immune costs of asymptomatic malaria infections in semi-wild chimpanzees.
articles.details: Survey of 27 semi-wild chimpanzees (Pan troglodytes) in Gabon using MinION sequencing showing 48.15% Plasmodium infection rate with multi-species co-infections (P. gaboni, P. reichenowi, P. vivax-like). Younger animals significantly more infected with higher parasitemia, especially triple infections. Infected animals showed elevated urea, reduced creatinine, and increased pro-inflammatory (TNF, IL-1β, CCL3) and anti-inflammatory (IL-10) cytokines, indicating systemic immune modulation and potential renal dysfunction. Challenges assumption that chronic Plasmodium infections are benign in great apes.

articles.headline: High genetic diversity of P. falciparum vaccine-targeted circumsporozoite protein
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of Plasmodium falciparum circumsporozoite protein in Kigali, Rwanda.
articles.details: Study of 245 P. falciparum isolates from Kigali, Rwanda identified 48 distinct circumsporozoite protein (csp) haplotypes with high haplotype diversity (Hd=0.8899) using Oxford Nanopore sequencing. Fewer than 1% matched 3D7 vaccine strain; multiple amino acid positions showed high mutation frequencies associated with vaccine escape. Authors conclude genetic divergence of circulating csp variants could influence vaccine performance in Rwanda.

articles.headline: Global research priorities identified for seasonal malaria chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Global medium-term research priority setting for seasonal malaria chemoprevention: results from an eDelphi study.
articles.details: eDelphi consultation with 67 experts across 20 countries identified research priorities for seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine and amodiaquine (SPAQ). Highest-ranked priorities: evaluating duration of SPAQ prophylactic protection, effect of integrating SMC with other interventions on antimalarial resistance development, and sustainability of SMC programs. Results aim to guide future research and program adaptation.

articles.headline: Misconceptions and incomplete knowledge limit malaria prevention in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Assessing the knowledge, attitudes and practices towards malaria prevention and determinants of antenatal care utilization among pregnant women in Sekyere South district, Ghana; a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 422 pregnant women in Ghana found 78% had adequate knowledge but 56% incorrectly believed malaria spreads person-to-person; only 48% knew recommended ≥3 intermittent preventive therapy in pregnancy (IPTp) doses; 63% used insecticide-treated nets (ITN), with non-use linked to heat discomfort and unavailability. Knowledge of IPTp dosage was significantly associated with uptake (p<0.001). Authors conclude barriers stem from misconceptions and incomplete adherence rather than supply shortages.

articles.headline: Recombinant P. ovale GAMA antigen demonstrates strong immunogenicity
articles.title_original: Immunogenicity and gene polymorphism analysis of Plasmodium ovale wallikeri glycosylphosphatidylinositol-anchored micronemal antigen (GAMA).
articles.details: Analysis of 56 P. ovale isolates (38 P. ovale wallikeri, 18 P. ovale curtisi) identified 13 haplotypes of the glycosylphosphatidylinositol-anchored micronemal antigen (GAMA) gene. Recombinant PowGAMA protein induced robust Th1-polarized immune response in mice with high interferon-gamma production, endpoint antibody titer 1:5,120,000, avidity 78.37%, and 83.3% sensitivity/70% specificity for detecting natural infections. Authors conclude potential for eliciting dual protective immunity against this understudied species.

articles.headline: Climate temperature and rainfall drive malaria transmission across Africa
articles.title_original: Climate change and malaria: an old enemy of Africa is back.
articles.details: Analysis of malaria incidence trends over two decades identified mean temperature as the main climatic factor affecting transmission in Angola, Cameroon, Ivory Coast, Ghana, Mozambique, and Uganda, with warmer temperatures generally increasing malaria risk (exception: Ghana where higher temperatures associated with decreased risk). Increased precipitation reduced transmission in Burkina Faso and Uganda. Authors emphasize need for policymaker and social partner engagement to address climate-driven transmission.

articles.headline: High malaria-typhoid co-infection exceeds national rates in northern Ghana
articles.title_original: Malaria and typhoidal co-infection among febrile patients: a health facility-based cross-sectional study in Northern Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 332 febrile patients in Upper East Ghana during 2023-2024 rainy seasons found prevalence of 37.7% malaria, 36.7% typhoid, and 14.7% co-infection (60% total). Nabdam district associated with increased malaria odds (adjusted odds ratio [aOR]=3.87, p<0.01); tap water source reduced both typhoid (aOR=0.36, p=0.02) and co-infection odds (aOR=0.07, p=0.02). Findings exceed national averages, highlighting regional disparities and need for integrated diagnostic and treatment strategies.

articles.headline: Artesunate-amodiaquine and artemether-lumefantrine remain effective in Madagascar
articles.title_original: Efficacy and Safety of Artesunate + Amodiaquine and Artemether + Lumefantrine for the Treatment of Uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum Malaria in Madagascar, 2020.
articles.details: Randomized trial of 709 children with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria across four sites in Madagascar. Artesunate-amodiaquine (ASAQ) achieved 100% polymerase chain reaction (PCR)-corrected adequate clinical and parasitological response (ACPR) at all sites; artemether-lumefantrine (AL) achieved 97.6-100% PCR-corrected ACPR. No mutations in the artemisinin-resistance marker pfK13 detected in 727 samples. Mild-to-moderate adverse events (mostly gastrointestinal) occurred in 10.2%. Both drugs remain safe and efficacious.

articles.headline: qPCR reveals underestimated Trichuris trichiura prevalence in malaria-endemic area
articles.title_original: Molecular diagnosis of Trichuris trichiura: Prevalence and associated risk factors in children under five living in a malaria-endemic area in Papua, Indonesia.
articles.details: Study of 181 children aged <5 years in malaria-endemic Papua, Indonesia found T. trichiura prevalence 31.5% by quantitative polymerase chain reaction (qPCR) vs 13.8% by Kato-Katz microscopy (17.7% increase, p=0.0001). Children aged >3 years had 3-fold increased risk (aOR=3.29, p=0.006); hemoglobin ≤10 g/dL associated with 2-fold increased risk (OR=2.46, p=0.020). Malaria co-infection increased anemia risk (OR=11.7, p=0.004).

articles.headline: Novel lupeol derivatives show potent in vitro antimalarial activity
articles.title_original: Hemisynthesis of lupeol and betulin derivatives and their in vitro antimalarial activity against Plasmodium falciparum FCR3.
articles.details: Hemisynthesis and testing of lupeol and betulin derivatives against P. falciparum FCR3 strain identified eight compounds with inhibitory activity (half-maximal inhibitory concentration [IC50] 0.03-9.9 µM). Three most active compounds—lupenone and derivatives 4a and 4b—had IC50 values of 0.3±0.3, 0.03±0.05, and 0.7±0.8 µM, respectively, comparable to or superior to artemisinin (0.7±0.3 µM) and chloroquine (10.3±2.9 µM). Molecular docking showed binding affinity to P. falciparum ATP6 protein.

articles.headline: Hydrogen peroxide propels hemozoin crystals in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Chemical propulsion of hemozoin crystal motion in malaria parasites.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Mechanistic study reveals hemozoin crystal motion in malaria parasites is driven by hydrogen peroxide via surface-catalyzed peroxide decomposition generating propulsive kinetic energy. Motion is much faster than thermal diffusion but tightly confined within the food vacuole. Surface-exposed metals on hemozoin catalyze peroxide decomposition. Authors propose this self-propulsion mechanism serves physiological role in reducing oxidative stress from high hydrogen peroxide levels generated during large-scale hemoglobin digestion.
articles.notable_reason: Novel mechanism of parasite oxidative-stress management; potential drug target

articles.headline: Outdoor exposure and substandard nets drive transmission in mining communities
articles.title_original: Factors Associated With Plasmodium falciparum Infection Transmission in Artisanal Mining and Mining-Free Communities: The Case of Fanteakwa South District, Ghana.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 341 participants in artisanal mining vs mining-free communities in Ghana found asymptomatic P. falciparum prevalence 64.6% in mining vs 27.7% in non-mining areas (p<0.001). In mining communities, prolonged outdoor night exposure (>2 hours; aOR=8.4, p<0.001) and use of low-quality nets with sharing (aOR 4.2-4.9) were strongest risk factors. Authors highlight need for quality-assured insecticide-treated net (ITN) distribution and behavior change communication in high-transmission areas.

articles.headline: Severe malaria dramatically reduces health-related quality of life in children
articles.title_original: Determining health-related quality of life among children with malaria in Kenya: does the choice of EQ-5D value set matter?
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 95 Kenyan children (45 with uncomplicated, 50 with severe malaria) assessed health-related quality of life via EQ-5D-5L proxy questionnaire. Severe malaria substantially reduced utility scores (0.30 with Ethiopian value set, -0.13 with Ugandan, 0.09 with Dutch) vs uncomplicated malaria (0.82, 0.59, 0.73 respectively). Pain/discomfort and usual activity were most affected domains. Authors highlight choice of EQ-5D value set significantly influenced utility estimates, underscoring need for Kenya-specific value set.

articles.headline: Vector bionomics and larval habitats in Kebbi State Nigeria
articles.title_original: Utilization of vector bionomics, remote sensing and larval habitat characterization data to inform larval source management decision in the high malaria burden state of Kebbi, Nigeria.
articles.details: Descriptive study of malaria vectors and larval habitats across Kebbi and 5 other Nigerian states. An. gambiae s.l. was predominant (13,201 specimens, 100% in Kebbi), with highest human biting rates (15.2–42.2 bites/person/night indoors, 8.7–14.3 outdoors across seasons). Of 635 larval habitats surveyed, 460 (97.9%) were positive for Anopheles larvae, mostly in rice fields near human habitations. Authors conclude high entomology indices and abundant habitats near homes indicate need for larval source management.

articles.headline: Correction to P. pitheci mitochondrial genome sequence
articles.title_original: Correction: The mitochondrial genome sequence of the Bornean orang-utan (Pongo pygmaeus) malaria parasite, Plasmodium pitheci.
articles.details: Correction to published mitochondrial genome sequence of Plasmodium pitheci, the malaria parasite of Bornean orangutans.

articles.headline: Cause-specific mortality patterns in pediatric cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Cause-specific mortality in a cohort of paediatric cerebral malaria patients.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 393 children with cerebral malaria from 2018–2025 trials in Blantyre; 52 (13%) died. Cerebral herniation (46%), status epilepticus with apnea (17%), and non-neurologic causes (cardiac/shock 13%, respiratory 12%, renal 6%, hepatic 4%) were identified as immediate causes of death. Admission coma score, oxygen saturation, and lactate levels differed by cause of death (p ≤0.04). Authors note multiple causes of death may explain why brain-focused therapies have failed in clinical trials.

articles.headline: Illegal mining expansion linked to malaria surge in Yanomami
articles.title_original: Mining and malaria in the Brazilian Amazon and in the Yanomami indigenous land.
articles.details: Analysis of mining and malaria patterns in Brazilian Amazon indigenous lands, 2018–2023. Each 1% increase in annual mining area was associated with 24% increase in monthly malaria cases (95% CrI 17–32%) in the Yanomami. Estimated 102,870 excess malaria cases attributable to mining from 2018–2023, with 2022 cases likely underreported by 83%. Authors conclude rethinking malaria control is essential given health, environmental, and indigenous justice impacts.

articles.headline: RDT and microscopy performance for P. falciparum and vivax India
articles.title_original: A cross-sectional survey of Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax in India using rapid diagnostic test and microscopy across 12 sites of varying transmission, 2023-2024.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 10,290 febrile participants across 12 Indian sites (September 2023–April 2024). Rapid diagnostic tests detected 1,516 positives (14.7%), microscopy 1,436 (14.0%): 72.9% P. falciparum mono-infections, 19.1% P. vivax, 8% mixed infections. RDT sensitivity was 95% for P. falciparum (95% CI 94–96%) and 83% for P. vivax (78–87%), with 99–100% specificity. Authors highlight urgent need for improved P. vivax diagnostics, as 0.4% RDT-negative cases were missed.

articles.headline: Two-dimensional Raman spectroscopy analysis of parasitemia levels
articles.title_original: Two-dimensional correlation spectroscopy (2D-COS) in the analysis of parasitemia in Raman spectra of red blood cells of patients diagnosed with malaria.
articles.details: Methodological study using 2D correlation spectroscopy (2D-COS) to analyze Raman spectra of P. falciparum-infected red blood cells at varying parasitemia levels. Two laser wavelengths (442 nm for heme resonance, 785 nm for broader molecular structure) revealed different spectral signatures reflecting heme, hemozoin, and protein/lipid changes correlating with parasitemia. Authors discuss potential use of heme intensity ratios (I1370/I1354) as markers for malaria parasitemia detection.

articles.headline: Integrated vector management effectiveness for malaria control
articles.title_original: Integrated vector management for malaria control: a review of approaches and effectiveness.
articles.journal: Trans R Soc Trop Med Hyg
articles.details: Systematic review synthesizing 14 studies (2009–2024) on integrated vector management (IVM) for malaria control. Studies employed observational, quasi-experimental, and cluster-randomized designs measuring incidence, vector density, prevalence, and entomological inoculation rates. Six studies reported statistically significant reductions in transmission (p<0.05), with longitudinal studies in Uganda, Ethiopia, and Nigeria showing sustained case and vector reductions. Authors conclude IVM combining traditional and emerging tools offers sustainable global control.

articles.headline: Zoonotic malaria emerging threat in India
articles.title_original: Tackling the Emerging Threat of Zoonotic Malaria in India.
articles.details: Article addresses emerging threat of zoonotic malaria in India. No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Cross-specific anti-CelTOS antibodies inhibit liver stage
articles.title_original: [Cross-specific anti-CelTOS antibodies for different Plasmodium species inhibiting the liver stage and transmission to the vector].
articles.authors: Varloteau A, Fouquoire L, Ravier L
articles.details: Study on cross-specific antibodies against CelTOS protein that inhibit liver-stage development and vector transmission. No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Cysteine protease inhibitor of P. vivax characterized
articles.title_original: Partial characterization of a cysteine protease inhibitor of Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: Study characterizing an endogenous inhibitor of cysteine protease (PvICP) from P. vivax. Recombinant PvICP expressed in E. coli showed broad inhibitory activity against vivapain-3, vivapain-4, falcipain-3, malapain-2, and malapain-4, with highest potency against vivapain-3 and vivapain-4. pH-stable inhibitory activity. Authors suggest PvICP may serve as therapeutic target in malaria research.

articles.headline: Climate change shifts African malaria vector distributions
articles.title_original: Climate Change Favors African Malaria Vector Mosquitoes.
articles.authors: van der Deure T, et al.
articles.details: Species distribution modeling of six dominant African Anopheles species using extensive mosquito observations and climate/land-use projections. Anopheles gambiae, An. coluzzii, and An. nili show substantial expansion of suitable habitat, with approximately 200 million additional people conservatively estimated to inhabit high-suitability zones by end of century, particularly in Central and East Africa. Authors emphasize species-specific climate responses and urgent need for adapted control strategies.

articles.headline: Vector diversity shapes malaria prevalence globally and regionally
articles.title_original: Vector diversity and malaria prevalence: global trends and local determinants.
articles.authors: Hoi AG, Gilbert B, Mideo N
articles.details: Macroecological analysis of open-access data examining relationships between vector species richness, malaria prevalence, and environmental factors. Results showed latitude-dependent associations between vector diversity and malaria prevalence. In Africa, environment shaped vector communities top-down; in Southeast Asia, complex interactions of rainfall, GDP, and vector diversity determined prevalence. Authors conclude vector diversity is fundamental determinant of disease distribution.

articles.headline: HSP90 inhibitors show divergent resistance profiles despite shared binding site
articles.title_original: Shared Binding Site but Divergent Resistance Profiles Uncover Novel Resistance Mechanisms in Plasmodium HSP90 Inhibitors.
articles.details: Comparative study of HSP90 inhibitors geldanamycin and AUY-922 in P. falciparum. Geldanamycin selected 10 distinct resistance mutations conferring up to 22-fold resistance; AUY-922 required 44 weeks for single A41S mutation with only 2-fold resistance. A41S paradoxically enhanced AUY-922 binding while conferring resistance. Conditional HSP90 knockdown increased geldanamycin but not AUY-922 sensitivity, suggesting multi-target mechanism underlies AUY-922's lower resistance risk.

articles.headline: Optimized low-volume transcriptomics protocols for resource-limited malaria settings
articles.title_original: Low-volume Plasmodium blood sample processing protocols for untargeted transcriptomics optimized using Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.details: Methods study comparing WBC, rRNA, and globin depletion techniques with library prep kits for low-volume P. vivax transcriptomics using P. knowlesi mock infection. WBC depletion increased Plasmodium reads from 5.4% to 37.2–54.5%; combined globin and rRNA depletion reduced human content by 90%. mRNA library kit outperformed total RNA kit. Protocol enables untargeted transcriptomics in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Malaria control in Mandate Palestine: historical obstacles and solutions
articles.title_original: Why and when successful rural malaria control became a local problem - Palestine 1922.
articles.details: Historical examination of early 20th-century Zionist malaria control program in Palestine, contextualized against modern political challenges. Study explores obstacles overcome in launching the first sustainable rural malaria control program, drawing parallels to contemporary geopolitical events including recent USAID changes and regional conflict.

articles.headline: First new malaria treatment in decades shows drug resistance promise
articles.title_original: First new type of malaria treatment in decades shows promise against drug resistance.
articles.details: Title indicates a novel antimalarial treatment class effective against drug-resistant parasites. Specific drug identity, mechanism of action, population, and quantitative efficacy data not provided in title.

articles.headline: DeepSeek AI successfully identifies Plasmodium parasites in blood smears
articles.title_original: A Case of Plasmodium Identification in Peripheral Blood Smears based on the DeepSeek Intelligent Algorithm.
articles.details: Case report of imported malaria using DeepSeek artificial intelligence for parasite identification in peripheral blood smears. AI successfully identified Plasmodium parasites and their developmental stages with high accuracy in minutes versus hours for traditional microscopy, reducing misdiagnosis and missed diagnosis risks. Proposed workflow: AI primary screening plus manual review for grassroots malaria control.

articles.headline: Plasmodium life stages show heterogeneous selection pressures during evolution
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous constraint and adaptation across the malaria parasite life cycle.
articles.authors: Perkins SA, Neafsey DE, Early AM
articles.details: Population genetic analysis comparing diversity patterns of P. falciparum genes expressed across discrete life cycle stages. Sporozoite-specific genes showed signals of weaker negative selection, consistent with absence of proliferation, limited clonal competition, and transmission bottleneck. Findings suggest that timing of therapeutic interventions targeting specific life stages may differentially impact rate of parasite resistance evolution.

articles.headline: Plasmodium relictum responds to any mosquito bite not just competent vectors
articles.title_original: Plastic responses of Plasmodium relictum to competent and non-competent mosquito bites.
articles.details: Experimental infection study in birds with avian malaria parasite P. relictum exposed to bites from competent (Culex quinquefasciatus) and non-competent (Anopheles gambiae) mosquito species. Both exposures induced parasitemia increase; response significantly stronger to non-competent vector. No species-specific modulation of gametocyte conversion rate or sex ratio observed. Parasites employ generalist bet-hedging strategy, potentially affecting transmission dynamics with vector shifts from climate change.

articles.headline: Fitsum Tadesse: malaria research leader in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Fitsum Tadesse: shaping malaria research in sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Biographical profile of malaria researcher Fitsum Tadesse and his contributions to malaria research across sub-Saharan Africa. No specific research findings, data, or quantitative results provided in title.

articles.headline: Anopheles aquasalis black scale phenotype affects vivax infection intensity
articles.title_original: Phenotypic variation and vector competence in Anopheles aquasalis: A comparative analysis of susceptibility to Plasmodium vivax.
articles.details: Laboratory experimental infection study comparing two An. aquasalis phenotypes (30BS and 50BS) from Brazil for susceptibility to P. vivax using membrane feeding assays. Infection prevalence similar between phenotypes (67.63% in 50BS versus 55.10% in 30BS, IRR=1.11, p=0.68). However, 30BS mosquitoes harbored significantly higher parasite loads (mean 72.91 oocysts versus 56.94, p=0.042). Phenotypic variation influences vector competence.

articles.headline: NOMAL Italian network: severe malaria epidemiology and outcomes in non-endemic setting
articles.title_original: The Italian network for severe malaria treatment (NOMAL): epidemiology, clinical presentation, management and outcomes in a non-endemic setting.
articles.details: Network registry study describing epidemiology, clinical presentation, management, and outcomes of severe malaria cases in Italy (non-endemic setting) from the NOMAL (Italian network for severe malaria treatment). No specific findings, patient numbers, or quantitative results provided in title.

articles.headline: Chemical adjuvants boost whole sporozoite malaria vaccine immune response
articles.title_original: A Trojan horse: Chemical boosting of Plasmodium falciparum whole sporozoite vaccine immunogenicity.
articles.details: Preclinical immunology study developing chemically adjuvanted P. falciparum whole sporozoite vaccines using positively charged dye-adjuvant complexes (Cy5-CL307 and Cy5-MDP). SPZCL307 successfully adjuvanted approximately 90% of sporozoites while maintaining viability (84%), motility (78.7%), and infectivity. SPZCL307 increased immune activation markers in macrophages (CD80, CD25, IL-6, IL-8, TNF, IL-1β, IL-10; p=0.018–0.043) and enhanced CD137 in CD8+ T cells (18%, p=0.01).

articles.headline: CDC emergency drug distribution: 83% delivered within 8 hours
articles.title_original: A Review of Malaria, Diphtheria, and Botulism Medication Distributions by CDC Port Health Stations From May 2006 to February 2024.
articles.details: Retrospective review of CDC port health station emergency drug distributions (May 2006–February 2024) including IV artesunate for severe malaria. Of 850 recent shipments (December 2019+), 83% (n=702) delivered to 54 US jurisdictions and 3 countries within 8 hours of release authorization. Commercial air transport (aOR 11.43, p<0.001) and work-hour authorization (aOR 5.10, p<0.001) associated with delivery delays. Staffing and transportation expansion can minimize delays.

articles.headline: Subclinical P. falciparum highest in teenagers under seasonal chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum carriage in a population under long-term, intensive malaria control in Kedougou region, Senegal: a 1-year cohort study.
articles.details: One-year cohort of 763 individuals in four Senegalese villages showed P. falciparum prevalence <10% in SMC-eligible children but 32.1% in 15-24 year-olds and 24.7% in 35-49 year-olds during wet season. Highest clinical burden occurred in 10-14 year-olds (527 cases/1000 person-years) and 15-19 year-olds (631 cases/1000 person-years), five-fold higher than younger children. Outdoor night-time activity was associated with infection. Despite intensive control, older age groups harbor significant parasite reservoir requiring community-wide interventions.

articles.headline: Malaria burden in a region under long-term chemoprevention
articles.title_original: Malaria burden in a region under long-term chemoprevention.
articles.details: Title indicates a study of malaria epidemiology in a chemoprevention setting. No abstract available.

articles.headline: Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine effectiveness stable over decade in Africa
articles.title_original: Effectiveness of dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine for treating Plasmodium falciparum malaria from sub-Saharan Africa: a retrospective study.
articles.details: Retrospective study of 89 patients treated with three-day dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine (DHA-PPQ) for P. falciparum malaria imported from sub-Saharan Africa (2013-2024). Day-3 parasitaemia clearance achieved in 83 of 89 patients (93.3%); only 6.7% retained parasites with no early or late treatment failures. No significant trend in day-3 positivity across three periods (p=0.339), indicating stable drug effectiveness over time, though continued surveillance remains essential.

articles.headline: P. falciparum disrupts blood-brain barrier by suppressing pericyte Ang-1
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum impairs Ang-1 secretion by pericytes in a 3D brain microvessel model.
articles.details: Three-dimensional microfluidics brain microvessel model with endothelial cells and pericytes showed that P. falciparum-infected red blood cell products decreased angiopoietin-1 (Ang-1) secretion by pericytes and increased vascular permeability. Pre-treatment with recombinant Ang-1 and Tie-2 activator AKB-9778 partially reversed parasite-mediated barrier disruption. Identifies pericytes as a novel target in cerebral malaria pathogenesis and suggests angiopoietin-Tie axis therapeutics may counteract vascular dysfunction.

articles.headline: Malaria and microgravity model age-related red blood cell changes
articles.title_original: Exploring erythropoiesis: aging through the lens of malaria and microgravity.
articles.authors: Foucambert P, Hang JW, Malleret B
articles.details: Review examining erythropoiesis across lifespan, focusing on age-related changes in stem cells, transcription factors, erythropoietin, and iron regulation. Discusses mechanisms of anemia in elderly including chronic disease and sepsis. Proposes Plasmodium infection and spaceflight as underexplored experimental models for studying age-related erythropoietic dysfunction and RBC morphological alterations.

articles.headline: Nanoparticles enhance malaria drug efficacy and vaccine immunogenicity
articles.title_original: Nanotechnology-based delivery strategies for drugs and vaccines targeting blood stage malaria: A systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 40 studies (2005-2025) on nanotechnology-based delivery for blood-stage malaria. Functionalized nanocarriers targeting infected red blood cells enhanced drug efficacy and reduced systemic toxicity. In vaccine development, nanoparticles achieved up to 83.3% survival in in vivo preclinical models through strong immune responses. Challenges persist in scalable production, clinical translation, and regulatory approval before clinical application.

articles.headline: Anopheles arabiensis dominates urban Burkina Faso; P. falciparum year-round
articles.title_original: Influence of the dry and humid seasons on the assessment of human exposure to malaria vector bites in Bobo-Dioulasso, Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Entomological survey in Bobo-Dioulasso (2023-2024) collected 1,455 mosquitoes via human landing catch (n=922) and pyrethrum spray (n=533). An. arabiensis comprised 91.2%, An. coluzzii 5.1%, An. gambiae 3.7%. P. falciparum infections detected year-round with 0.16% dual P. falciparum/P. malariae co-infections. Blood-meal analysis showed 59.6% mixed feeding, 24.4% zoophilic, 15.9% anthropophilic behavior. Ecological flexibility and high trophic plasticity sustain residual transmission.

articles.headline: New avian Plasmodium species identified in Brazilian wood rails
articles.title_original: Hidden in plain sight: Morphological and molecular description of Plasmodium aramidis n. sp. in blood and tissues from Brazilian Wood Rails.
articles.details: Morphological and molecular description of Plasmodium aramidis n. sp. identified in five Grey-necked Wood Rails (Aramides cajaneus) from southeastern Brazil. Histopathology revealed pulmonary edema and hemorrhage, hepatic hemosiderosis, and exoerythrocytic meronts in lung, heart, and skeletal muscle tissues. Species appears common in this bird host but pathogenic; further studies needed to identify additional hosts and characterize disease mechanisms.

articles.headline: Anopheles vagus peaks at 23.5°C in Indonesian highland rice agroecosystems
articles.title_original: Ecological diversity of Anopheline mosquitoes in highland agroecosystems in relation to microclimate dynamics: implications for malaria control.
articles.details: Ecological survey of 6,899 mosquitoes in South Sumatra rice fields (April-September) dominated by Culex spp. (56.6%) and Anopheles spp. (15.6%), with An. vagus most abundant. Human biting rate peaked at 23.5°C and >88% relative humidity (R²=0.656). Species showed strong exophagic and exophilic behaviors. First integrated ecological assessment of Anopheles in tropical highland rice-farming landscape, revealing species-specific climatic sensitivities informing vector surveillance.

articles.headline: Atovaquone-proguanil attenuates P. falciparum sporozoites but shows inferior protection
articles.title_original: Complete attenuation of Plasmodium falciparum sporozoites by atovaquone-proguanil.
articles.details: Clinical trial of PfSPZ-CVac combining three doses of 5.12×10⁴ or 1.5×10⁵ sporozoites with oral atovaquone-proguanil (1000/400 mg). Complete parasitemia prevention during immunization; however, challenge with 3.2×10³ sporozoites at 10 weeks yielded only 25% and 20% protection, inferior to chloroquine-based chemo-attenuation. Comparative immune analysis revealed protection correlated with antibodies to liver-stage antigens LISP2, LSA1, and multi-stage PfMSP5, not circumsporozoite protein.

articles.headline: RTS,S and R21 vaccines licensed; over 500,000 children die annually
articles.title_original: Better late than never: defining the ideal vaccine that targets pre-erythrocytic malaria infection.
articles.details: Review of pre-erythrocytic malaria vaccine progress following licensure of RTS,S/AS01 (Mosquirix, GSK; WHO prequalified 2022) and R21/Matrix-M (Serum Institute of India; WHO prequalified 2023). Both require four-dose primary series over 12-18 months targeting circumsporozoite protein on sporozoites. Despite landmark vaccine licenses, protection level and durability remain uncertain in infants and high-transmission areas. Over 500,000 children under 5 still die annually, driving continued vaccine development efforts.

articles.headline: Delayed treatment and indigenous status drive malaria deaths in Brazil
articles.title_original: Towards Malaria Elimination: A Nationwide Case-Control Study to Assess Risk Factors for Severe Malaria-Related Deaths in Brazil.
articles.details: Nationwide case-control study of 632 malaria-related deaths (2011-2020): 454 (71.8%) in Amazon region. Amazon risk factors included age <6 months or >60 years, delayed treatment >48 hours (p<0.001), illiteracy, and living in indigenous villages (p<0.001); active case detection was protective (p=0.01). Extra-Amazon deaths associated with delayed treatment, P. falciparum or mixed infections, and foreign-acquired cases. Socially vulnerable groups bear highest mortality risk; improved timely diagnosis and active surveillance essential for elimination.

articles.headline: CSP repeats show elastic properties essential for sporozoite motility
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium CSP repeats have elastic properties with a critical role in sporozoite motility.
articles.details: Mutant sporozoites with truncated or scrambled CSP repeats showed impaired motility due to altered adhesion site dynamics. Using single-molecule fluorescence-force spectroscopy, researchers demonstrated that CSP repeats function as stiff, linear elastic springs whose elastic properties depend on repeat length and are lost when scrambled. These findings suggest the CSP repeat region plays a critical functional role in sporozoite motility during Plasmodium infection.

articles.headline: IPT reduces malaria in HIV-positive pregnant women; no birth outcome benefit
articles.title_original: Intermittent preventive treatment regimens for malaria in HIV-positive pregnant women: Systematic review and meta-analysis of clinical trials.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 13 randomized clinical trials (5226 HIV-positive pregnant women) found IPT reduced malaria odds by 55% (OR=0.45, p≤0.001) and placental malaria by 48% (OR=0.52, p≤0.001). IPT also reduced maternal anemia odds by 18% (OR=0.82, p=0.03), with IPTp-DP being most effective. However, IPT showed no significant effect on low birth weight, fetal loss, stillbirth, or preterm labor.

articles.headline: Imaging flow cytometry enables detection of parasitized erythroblasts in vivo
articles.title_original: Quantification and visualization of malaria-infected erythroblasts by imaging flow cytometry.
articles.details: In P. yoelii 17XNL-infected mice, imaging flow cytometry with GFP-expressing parasites enabled detection and visualization of parasitized erythroblasts in spleen and bone marrow, though not in peripheral blood. Parasitized erythroblast frequencies measured by imaging flow cytometry closely matched those by conventional FACS analysis. Imaging revealed erythroblastic islands and apoptotic-like blebbing structures, demonstrating a powerful platform for studying host-parasite interactions in hematopoietic tissues.

articles.headline: Systematic review of AI techniques for Plasmodium blood-stage diagnosis
articles.title_original: Insights into AI-Driven malaria diagnosis: A systematic review with implications for Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.details: This systematic review examines deep learning techniques for Plasmodium blood-stage classification, with focus on P. knowlesi diagnostics. Reviews convolutional neural networks, transfer learning, ensemble learning, YOLO, and Faster R-CNN methods across published studies. Identifies key challenges including limited annotated datasets, class imbalance, and lack of model interpretability. Concludes enhanced dataset curation, domain adaptation, and explainable AI essential for advancing P. knowlesi diagnostic accuracy.

articles.headline: Twenty-year Kenya malaria analysis reveals declining prevalence with geographic shift
articles.title_original: A multidimensional space-time geospatial analysis for examining the spatial trends of vector-borne diseases: 20 years of malaria in Kenya.
articles.details: Analysis of 20 years (2000-2020) of P. falciparum incidence, mortality, and prevalence data in Kenya using multidimensional space-time pattern mining. Found overall malaria prevalence decline with slight increase 2016-2018; identified four key malaria zones and two areas with increasing disease. Malaria burden concentrated in cross-border regions despite increased bed net and antimalarial use. Space-time methods useful for identifying high-burden areas and refining malaria elimination strategies.

articles.headline: P. vivax hemoglobin degradation genes highly conserved despite mutations
articles.title_original: Characterization of genes involved in hemoglobin degradation in Plasmodium vivax isolates from Chennai, India, and species of non-human primate malaria.
articles.details: Study analyzed 30 P. vivax isolates from India and 3 chloroquine-resistant reference strains for mutations in three food vacuole enzymes: plasmepsin IV, heme detoxification protein, and falcilysin. Found multiple nonsynonymous mutations including Val165Ile in PM_IV, Asn513Asp and Gly676Asp in FLN, and novel in-frame duplication in HDP. Structural modeling showed mutations did not affect active binding sites. Genes highly conserved across isolates, underscoring essential roles as potential drug targets.

articles.headline: Malaria eradicated from Europe but climate change threatens re-emergence
articles.title_original: Historical evolution of the epidemiology of Malaria in Spain and Europe. Future perspectives.
articles.journal: Eur J Clin Microbiol Infect Dis
articles.details: Review of malaria epidemiology in Spain and Europe from historical eradication (Spain 1964, Europe 1970s) through current status. Disease now seen primarily as imported cases from travelers and immigrants from endemic regions. Key future concerns include climate change potentially favoring Anopheles proliferation in southern Europe and migration from endemic areas creating parasite reservoirs. Maintaining surveillance and vector control essential to prevent re-emergence despite current low transmission risk.

articles.headline: NLRP1 inflammasome pathway identified as novel mechanism in gestational malaria
articles.title_original: Impact of NLRP1 Met1154Val and IL1B variants on gestational malaria: an unexplored role of NLRP1 in inflammasome activation by Plasmodium spp.
articles.details: Association study in pregnant women from malaria-endemic Brazil found NLRP1 p.Met1154Val variant associated with protection against Plasmodium infection; IL1B variants more prevalent in severe cases. For first time, demonstrated infected red blood cells induce NLRP1- and caspase-1-dependent pyroptosis in trophoblast cells, identifying novel inflammasome pathway in gestational malaria. NLRP1 variant correlated with healthier placental state, suggesting protective role in gestational malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Commercial malaria RDTs detect simian Plasmodium with variable sensitivity
articles.title_original: Can malaria rapid diagnostic tests be used to detect simian malaria?
articles.details: Evaluated 14 commercial malaria RDT brands on 131 blood samples from non-human primates infected with P. cynomolgi, P. inui, or P. coatneyi. Overall RDT sensitivity ranged 34.2-94.3%, with best-performing brand exceeding 90% sensitivity when parasite density exceeded 200 parasites/μL. Observed 1-17% PfHRP2 false positive rates in malaria samples; no false positives in negative controls. pLDH- and aldolase-based tests showed similar performance.

articles.headline: High Wolbachia prevalence in Iranian An. dthali suggests biocontrol potential
articles.title_original: Wolbachia Infection in Iranian Malaria Vectors: Prevalence and Biocontrol Implications.
articles.details: Survey of 1094 mosquito specimens from 9 Iranian provinces (2016-2019) examined Wolbachia infection in malaria vectors. Detected Wolbachia in 73.4% of An. dthali and 77.78%-96.77% of Culex species; notably absent from other major malaria vectors. Phylogenetic analysis identified supergroup B strains similar to those in African Anopheles. High Wolbachia prevalence in An. dthali, combined with strain similarity to those blocking malaria transmission, suggests promising biocontrol potential.

articles.headline: Novel RBC glycan determinant identified for P. falciparum invasion
articles.title_original: Sialyl-T Antigen: A Novel Red Blood Cell Determinant for Plasmodium falciparum Invasion.
articles.details: Through RNA interference screening and CRISPR/Cas9 knockout in human hematopoietic stem cells, identified C1GALT1 glycosyltransferase as novel host determinant for P. falciparum invasion. C1GALT1-dependent α(2-3) sialic acid structures in mucin-type O-glycans crucial for efficient invasion by both sialic acid-dependent and -independent P. falciparum strains, though not P. knowlesi. Different P. falciparum strains show variable dependencies on distinct sialic acid configurations, highlighting role of RBC glycans in infection.

articles.headline: NOX2-driven oxidative stress causes cardiac hypercontractility during severe malaria
articles.title_original: NOX2-induced oxidative stress triggers cardiac hypercontractility during severe malaria.
articles.details: In P. chabaudi-infected mice, isolated hearts showed increased contractility at baseline and with isoprenaline stimulation, associated with increased lipid peroxidation (TBARS) and reduced superoxide dismutase activity. Treatment with apocynin (30 mg/kg/day) or NOX2 deletion reversed cardiac hypercontractility without affecting parasitemia. Treatments reduced cardiac oxidative stress and suppressed upregulation of pro-inflammatory cytokines IL-1β and IL-6. Findings establish NOX2-induced oxidative stress and inflammation as mechanism of cardiac complications during malaria.

articles.headline: Monocyte dysfunction identified in symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax
articles.title_original: Systems approach identifies monocyte imbalance in symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax malaria.
articles.details: Systems approach integrating transcriptional profiling and mass cytometry in individuals with symptomatic and asymptomatic P. vivax malaria. Symptomatic infections showed upregulation of anti-inflammatory pathways, downregulation of monocyte function transcripts, and depletion of CCR2+CXCR4+ classical monocytes. Asymptomatic infections displayed dysregulation of monocyte activation and inflammasome genes. Results suggest asymptomatic P. vivax is not innocuous and may impair immune elimination of parasitemia and vaccination responses.

articles.headline: WHO reference serum harmonizes malaria antibody detection across laboratories
articles.title_original: Evaluation of 1(st) WHO anti-malaria reference reagent for competition ELISA harmonisation and development of ADAMSEL analytical platform.
articles.details: Harmonized competition ELISA for P. falciparum AMA1 antibodies across three laboratories using WHO reference serum 10/198, addressing challenges of AMA1 sequence diversity and cross-reactivity. Developed ADAMSEL software and R-based analytical tool for automated cELISA analysis. Freely available tools improve efficiency and minimize human error, benefiting resource-limited laboratories.

articles.headline: Phenolic compounds inhibit P. falciparum PfATP6 calcium pump
articles.title_original: Small phenolic inhibitors of PfATP6, a Plasmodium falciparum calcium ATPase, as prototype antimalarials.
articles.details: Evaluated structurally simple phenolic compounds as antimalarial targeting PfATP6, a calcium-transporting protein. Several compounds inhibited parasite growth including di-alkylated hydroquinones, naphthoquinone disulfide, and hexachlorophene. Some active compounds also inhibited purified PfATP6 activity. Molecular modeling revealed hydrogen bonding and hydrophobic interactions as main binding mechanisms, representing early steps toward improved small-molecule PfATP6 inhibitors.

articles.headline: Genetic screen identifies P. falciparum genes needed for HbAS survival
articles.title_original: Phenotypic screens reveal Plasmodium falciparum genetic factors associated with infection of sickle-trait cells.
articles.details: PiggyBac transposon-based phenotypic screen identified P. falciparum genetic factors essential for survival in sickle-trait (HbAS) RBCs cultured in vitro. Quantitative Insertion Site Sequencing determined mutant sensitivity. Genes associated with parasite sensitivity to HbAS involve signaling pathways, exported proteins, and host-interaction, overlapping with oxidative stress and artemisinin drug sensitivity pathways.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 variants show no clear link to P. vivax relapse risk
articles.title_original: Polymorphisms of the CYP2D6 gene and its relationship with Plasmodium vivax relapses after chloroquine-primaquine treatment in Turbo, Colombia.
articles.details: Analyzed CYP2D6 polymorphisms in 71 P. vivax patients on supervised chloroquine-primaquine treatment. Most diplotypes (81.7%) were normal/ultrarapid metabolizers; 18.3% poor/intermediate metabolizers. Impaired metabolizers had higher plasma primaquine day-2 concentrations (660.4 vs 313.5 ng/mL, r = −0.51), but no significant relapse difference (adjusted HR 1.45, 95% CI 0.39–5.39). Authors call for further research.

articles.headline: Metabolomic profiles reveal shared and distinct malaria-babesia pathways
articles.title_original: Shared host, distinct invaders: metabolomic footprints of plasmodium and babesia in host red cells.
articles.authors: Beri D, Rodriguez M, Lobo CA
articles.details: Review synthesizing metabolomic data from Plasmodium and Babesia infections in RBCs. Both parasites show enhanced glycolysis, lipid scavenging, and perturbations in sugars, amino acids, lipids, redox homeostasis, and heme utilization. Key difference: parasites differ in hemoglobin breakdown and amino acid usage. Comparative understanding may identify new biomarkers and targeted therapies.

articles.headline: Deep learning with uncertainty attention improves parasite detection accuracy
articles.title_original: Uncertainty-guided attention learning for malaria parasite detection in thick blood smears.
articles.details: Developed uncertainty-guided attention learning network for parasite detection in thick blood smear images using pixel and Bayesian channel attention mechanisms to handle noisy, artifact-laden images. Demonstrated superior performance compared to state-of-the-art baselines on two public datasets, achieving highest average precision at both parasite and patient levels.

articles.headline: AI applications and challenges in Sub-Saharan malaria control programs
articles.title_original: Artificial intelligence in African malaria control programs: opportunities and risks.
articles.details: Narrative review of AI applications in African malaria control including Kenya drone vector surveillance, Nigeria mobile health platforms, and Tanzania climate-informed forecasting. Discusses AI-driven bioinformatics for Plasmodium genomic surveillance and parasite lifecycle modeling. Examines challenges including data quality, ethical considerations, infrastructure limitations, and potential inequities.

articles.headline: Dissolving microarray patches deliver amodiaquine-artesunate transdermally
articles.title_original: Polymeric microarray patches for transdermal delivery of amodiaquine and artesunate: A novel strategy against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Developed dissolving microarray patches (MAPs) for transdermal ACT delivery. In vitro: 40% amodiaquine and 90% artesunate deposited/permeated into skin. Extended artesunate half-life 8.5-fold (54.05 vs 6.37 h) and increased Cmax 21%; amodiaquine half-life prolonged ~1.4-fold. In P. berghei-infected mice, combined MAP treatment reduced parasitemia 99.5% in 7 days, matching oral efficacy.

articles.headline: Mathematical model optimizes RTS,S vaccine integration with malaria strategies
articles.title_original: An age-structured mathematical model to assess the combined effects of vaccine and non-vaccine interventions on malaria transmission and burden.
articles.details: Age-structured mathematical model assessing combined effects of RTS,S and R21 vaccination with non-vaccine interventions (LLINs, case treatment). Integrated demographic parameters, vaccine efficacy, and waning immunity. Calibrated to Ethiopia (low transmission) and Nigeria (high transmission). Preliminary findings: vaccination integrated with existing strategies significantly reduces incidence and mortality.

articles.headline: PSSP17 transmission-essential biomarker for non-invasive malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Evaluation of Plasmodium falciparum PSSP17 essentiality in human-to-mosquito transmission and its prospective candidacy as a biomarker.
articles.details: Investigated essentiality of Plasmodium sexual stage protein 17 (PSSP17), detected in saliva from subclinical infections. Found pssp17 dispensable in asexual stages but essential for mosquito transmission. Review of 19,280 public P. falciparum genomes revealed no naturally occurring deletions. Since Δpssp17 mutants cannot be effectively transmitted, PSSP17 is proposed as a non-invasive saliva-based RDT biomarker.

articles.headline: Karius cfDNA sequencing detects P. vivax when microscopy fails
articles.title_original: Travel-Related Malaria Diagnosis on Karius Test Despite Negative Blood Smear.
articles.authors: Weigold JE, Lal S, Youssef DA
articles.details: Case report of 59-year-old returned traveler with fever, rash, myalgia from Amazon. Laboratory findings: leukopenia, thrombocytopenia, transaminitis, hyperbilirubinemia. Multiple malaria smears negative despite clinical suspicion. Karius cfDNA test detected P. vivax. Sequential treatments administered: chloroquine-primaquine, artemether-lumefantrine, then quinine-doxycycline with prolonged primaquine, achieving complete symptom and laboratory resolution.

articles.headline: Moderate-high seroprevalence of SARS-CoV-2 and malaria in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of anti-SARS-CoV-2 and anti-Plasmodium falciparum and vivax antibodies in urban and rural zones in the West Region of Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 805 participants in urban and rural Cameroon (April–May 2022). Overall SARS-CoV-2 seroprevalence was 60.1% (urban 66.6%, rural 54.0%), while Plasmodium seroprevalence was 22.2% (rural 31.4% vs. urban 14.8%), with P. falciparum accounting for 21% and P. vivax 2.11%. No association was found between anti-SARS-CoV-2 and anti-Plasmodium antibodies.

articles.headline: Ivermectin shows high contact toxicity against malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Contact exposure to ivermectin induces acute mortality and inhibits parasite development in malaria vectors.
articles.details: Laboratory study of ivermectin exposure in An. stephensi and An. culicifacies mosquitoes infected with P. berghei. Ivermectin induced 100% mortality within 24–48 hours at higher dosages (topical LD50: An. stephensi 0.017 ng/mg, An. culicifacies 0.002 ng/mg). Contact exposure reduced oocyst development by 71% at 0.01 µM, demonstrating dual mosquitocidal and transmission-blocking effects.

articles.headline: Gut dysbiosis and E. coli predict post-discharge mortality in severe pediatric malaria
articles.title_original: Gut bacterial dysbiosis in pediatric severe malaria associates with post-discharge mortality.
articles.details: Analysis of gut bacteria in children with severe malaria from two African studies found dysbiosis with increased Enterobacteriaceae abundance associated with multiple clinical complications. Increased E. coli abundance was a predictor of post-discharge mortality. Metagenomic analysis identified elevated metabolic pathways supporting Enterobacteriaceae survival, suggesting potential therapeutic targets.

articles.headline: MALDI-TOF MS reduces vector surveillance costs by 83% in Kenya
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for malaria vector surveillance: A cost-comparison analysis using a decision-tree approach.
articles.details: Decision-tree cost-comparison analysis using Kenya's national malaria program (15,000 mosquitoes annually) comparing current molecular methods to MALDI-TOF MS for vector identification. MALDI-TOF MS would result in 83% direct cost savings, 74.48% time savings, and 77% labor cost reduction, making it a feasible alternative for routine entomological surveillance in low- and middle-income countries.

articles.headline: Declining P. vivax transmission increases primaquine efficacy in Thailand
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax Malaria Relapse Risk Depends on Transmission Intensity: Evidence From a Longitudinal Study in Northwest Thailand.
articles.details: Prospective observational cohort of 380 participants in northwest Thailand (March 2010–September 2014) given supervised 14-day primaquine (0.5 mg base/kg/day). Among 92 who developed symptomatic P. vivax, annual incidence declined from 0.19 to 0.09 infections/person-year. Primaquine failure rate within 4 months was 75% less than predicted, suggesting declining transmission reduces hypnozoite burden and improves radical cure efficacy.

articles.headline: NUAK1 identified as critical host factor for malaria
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum exploits NUAK1 to establish infection in human erythrocytes.
articles.details: Study identifying NUAK1 (AMPK-related kinase) as critical host factor in P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes. Two selective NUAK1 inhibitors (HTH-01-015, WZ4003) blocked P. falciparum growth throughout the asexual lifecycle including erythrocyte invasion. Genetic NUAK1 overexpression partially rescued signaling and invasion phenotypes, establishing NUAK1 as a vulnerable host-directed therapeutic target.
articles.notable_reason: Host-directed target with potential for novel antimalarial class

articles.headline: PfEMP1-specific antibody responses and cerebral malaria protection
articles.title_original: Breaking the bind: PfEMP1-specific antibodies in cerebral malaria.
articles.details: Review synthesizing evidence on PfEMP1-specific antibodies and their role in cerebral malaria protection. Antibodies to EPCR-binding CIDR-α1 and DBL-β domains associate with reduced CM risk, while responses to rosetting domains are less well characterized. Identifies research priorities including mapping immunodominant epitopes and developing broadly inhibitory monoclonal antibodies.

articles.headline: Deep learning achieves 94.4% accuracy for malaria parasite detection
articles.title_original: Rapid and accurate recognition of erythrocytic stage parasites of Plasmodium falciparum via a deep learning-based YOLOv3 platform.
articles.details: Development and validation of AI-assisted diagnostic tool using YOLOv3 deep learning for P. falciparum detection in thin blood smears. Tested on 262 images; detected 358 infected RBCs with 94.41% overall accuracy, false negative rate 1.68%, and false positive rate 3.91%. Provides feasible technical support for malaria diagnosis in resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: An. coluzzii and gambiae dominate P. falciparum transmission in Benin
articles.title_original: Spatial and temporal distribution of four malaria vector species and their relative contributions to Plasmodium falciparum transmission along the south-north transect of Benin, West Africa.
articles.details: Entomological survey across 18 communes in Benin determining vector contributions to P. falciparum transmission. An. coluzzii accounted for 63.01% of transmission (EIR 87.7 infecting bites/person/year), An. gambiae 36.7% (EIR 51.1), and An. funestus 0.24%. An. gambiae carried higher P. falciparum sporozoite loads, with approximately 30% having >10,000 sporozoites versus <10% for An. coluzzii.

articles.headline: Coustani Group mosquitoes are not major malaria vectors in DRC
articles.title_original: Coustani Group mosquito (Diptera: Culicidae) diversity and malaria transmission in the Democratic Republic of the Congo.
articles.details: Field study in DRC examining Coustani Group mosquito diversity using morphological and molecular approaches. Despite morphological identification as An. paludis, molecular sequencing revealed species complexity (2 species groups in Lodja, 4 in Kapolowe). Low sporozoite detection suggested these mosquitoes are not major vectors of malaria, though species identification challenges complicate conclusions.

articles.headline: Thailand's 1-3-7 malaria surveillance strategy mostly timely but challenged
articles.title_original: Evaluating Thailand's malaria reactive surveillance and response strategies for malaria elimination: a mixed-method study.
articles.details: Mixed-method evaluation of Thailand's 1-3-7 reactive surveillance and response strategy in three provinces (January–April 2023). About 54.1% of cases were notified within 1 day, while 98.7% reported case investigation and response activities completed within timeframes except in border areas with high burden. Despite good acceptability, challenges include limited human resources, low community participation, and difficult terrain.

articles.headline: Antimalarial prophylaxis does not increase drug-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Impact of Trimethoprim-Sulfamethoxazole and Chloroquine Prophylaxis on Drug-Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Mutations in Adults with HIV in Malawi.
articles.details: Randomized controlled trial of HIV+ adults in Malawi comparing chloroquine with trimethoprim-sulfamethoxazole prophylaxis. Pyrosequencing of resistance markers (pfdhfr, pfdhps, pfcrt) revealed no significant differences in resistance mutation prevalence between treatment arms or versus controls. No chloroquine-resistance mutations were detected in any breakthrough infections.

articles.headline: IL-35+ Bregs controlled by distinct STING-IRF3 pathways in rodent malaria
articles.title_original: Distinct STING-IRF3 activation determines IL-35(+) Breg differentiation in rodent malaria.
articles.details: Investigated regulatory B cell responses in C57BL/6 mice infected with P. berghei ANKA versus P. yoelii YM. IL-35+ Breg expansion occurred in P. yoelii YM-infected mice but not in P. berghei ANKA-infected mice. STING-mediated IRF3 phosphorylation suppressed IL-35+ Breg differentiation, potentially contributing to experimental cerebral malaria pathology.

articles.headline: First imported babesiosis case in Honduras misdiagnosed as malaria
articles.title_original: First documented case of imported human babesiosis in Honduras: Diagnostic challenges with malaria falciparum.
articles.details: Case report of 72-year-old returning from Massachusetts who developed fever, neurological symptoms, and ischemic stroke. Initially suspected P. falciparum and treated with chloroquine-primaquine followed by artemether-lumefantrine but developed hyperparasitemia and multiorgan failure, dying on day 33. Molecular PCR negative for Plasmodium but positive for Babesia 18S rRNA.

articles.headline: mRNA vaccine restores Plasmodium-specific CD8+ immunity impaired by hemozoin
articles.title_original: mRNA vaccination overcomes hemozoin-mediated impairment of whole parasite vaccine efficacy for malaria.
articles.details: Murine model demonstrated prior blood-stage P. falciparum exposure compromises RAS-induced CD8+ T cell responses through hemozoin-mediated impaired dendritic cell antigen uptake. Lipid nanoparticle-encapsulated mRNA vaccine encoding CD8+ T cell epitopes restored protection in previously exposed mice. Combined RAS+mRNA vaccine enhanced liver resident memory T cells even in malaria-experienced hosts.

articles.headline: P. knowlesi in macaques predicts human transmission risk in Malaysia
articles.title_original: Multi-pronged surveillance to understand the spatiotemporal correlations among macaques, vectors and humans in Plasmodium knowlesi malaria transmission.
articles.details: Integrated surveillance of humans, macaques and vectors in Peninsular Malaysia 2019-2022. Positive partial correlation between human P. knowlesi cases and Anopheles Leucosphyrus-group mosquitoes within 10-km proximity (rs=0.228, P=0.042). Proportion of P. knowlesi-positive macaques correlated with historical human cases within 6-20 km spatially, suggesting macaque prevalence may serve as proxy for human infection risk.

articles.headline: Mologic LDH RDT effective for detecting HRP2-deleted P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Clinical evaluation of a novel Mologic malaria Plasmodium falciparum LDH-dependent lateral flow rapid diagnostic test diagnostic accuracy: a cross-sectional study, Sudan.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 500 malaria-suspected patients in Sudan. Mologic LDH RDT sensitivity 91.9% (95% CI 87.3-95.2) versus microscopy 95.24% and comparator RDT 93.81%. All tests approximately 100% specific. Mologic RDT detected 6 HRP2 antigen-negative P. falciparum samples. Concordance with PCR reference: κ = 0.929.

articles.headline: 60% of African children face >24-hour malaria treatment delays
articles.title_original: Estimating rates of treatment delay for malaria fevers among children in Sub-Saharan Africa 2006-2022.
articles.details: Analysis of 177 nationally representative surveys in Sub-Saharan Africa 2006-2022. 60% [95% UI 45.7-72.8] of treated children experienced >24-hour treatment delay; 29% [95% UI 18.9-41.2] faced >48-hour delays, affecting 33 million and 16 million children respectively. Somalia highest delay rate (76%), Tanzania lowest (35.3%). Post-2015 improvements stagnated overall.

articles.headline: Nanosd integral gene drive achieves high transmission in A. gambiae
articles.title_original: The nanosd integral gene drive enables population modification of the malaria vector Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: CRISPR-based homing gene drive targeting nanos gene achieves 98.4% transmission in females and 99.5% in males in caged populations. Homozygous females show impaired fecundity with ovary underdevelopment but no widespread transcriptomic perturbations affecting vector competence or Plasmodium susceptibility. Drive maintains Cas9 source after emergence of drive-resistant alleles and mobilizes non-autonomous antiparasitic effector modifications.

articles.headline: Machine learning accelerates antigen discovery and vaccine efficacy prediction
articles.title_original: From data to immunity: the role of machine learning in advancing malaria vaccine research: a scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review of ML/AI applications in malaria vaccine research 2017-2025. Identified five key areas: antigen discovery using supervised/semi-supervised learning, immune signature identification and efficacy prediction, computational tool development, AI/ML application reviews, and epidemiological modeling. Most studies from Europe/North America with African collaborations.

articles.headline: PbGAC protein mediates P. berghei merozoite invasion via heparan sulfate
articles.title_original: The Plasmodium berghei merozoite protein PbGAC is critically involved in erythrocyte binding during invasion.
articles.details: Characterized PbGAC protein (PbANKA_1137800) in P. berghei ANKA merozoites located at extreme apical region, binding heparin and erythrocyte surface during invasion. Interacts with secreted proteins critical for invasion. Mice immunized with recombinant PbGAC or passively immunized with PbGAC-sera demonstrated enhanced immunity against lethal P. berghei challenges.

articles.headline: Correction to P. vivax infection in human liver organoids
articles.title_original: Correction: Human liver organoids are susceptible to Plasmodium vivax infection.
articles.details: Correction notice to prior publication on human liver organoid susceptibility to P. vivax infection.

articles.headline: SNP barcode reveals low genetic differentiation in imported P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Genetic diversity and population structures of Plasmodium falciparum parasites imported to China from Central and West Africa determined using a SNP barcode.
articles.details: Applied 24-SNP HRM barcode to 181 P. falciparum samples imported to China from Central and West Africa. Observed low nucleotide diversity across populations with pairwise FST values 0.001-0.054 (low to moderate genetic differentiation). Lack of prominent clustering among populations; barcode insufficient for distinguishing isolates between regions.

articles.headline: African malaria vectors show expanded olfactory gene repertoires
articles.title_original: Olfactory gene dynamics in invasive Indian and non-invasive African malaria vectors at the crossroads of development, infection and resistance.
articles.details: Genome-wide analysis of olfactory genes in four malaria vectors: A. stephensi and A. culicifacies (Indian), A. gambiae and A. funestus (African). African species had significantly higher numbers of odorant binding proteins, odorant receptors, and ionotropic receptors. Pyrethroid-resistant A. stephensi larvae exhibited elevated CSP expression (SAP2 and SAP3), suggesting role in insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: Historical malaria seasonality dataset assembled for sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: A malaria seasonality dataset for sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: This study created a publicly available geolocated dataset of malaria seasonality timeseries published since 2000 for sub-Saharan Africa, using literature review with natural language processing, routine surveillance data, and entomological datasets. The data include malaria prevalence, incidence, mortality, and entomological timeseries, along with qualitative descriptions of seasonality patterns from published literature.

articles.headline: Poor antimalarial adherence despite high knowledge in Rwanda
articles.title_original: Understanding Malaria Treatment Adherence in Rwanda: Implications for Artemisinin Resistance.
articles.details: A cross-sectional study of 406 febrile patients at Rwanda health facilities (December 2023–February 2024) found high malaria knowledge (81% identified symptoms, 72% understood transmission), but poor treatment adherence. Of those receiving antimalarial treatment in the last 6 months (44.3%, n=180), only 46% completed the full 3-day course, 37% stopped within 2 days, and 27% of all participants self-treated with antimalarials from pharmacies. Poor adherence exacerbates artemisinin resistance risk.

articles.headline: Anopheles mascarensis reviewed as primary malaria vector Madagascar
articles.title_original: Anopheles mascarensis, a Malaria Vector Endemic to Madagascar and the Comoros Archipelago: A Review.
articles.details: This review of Anopheles mascarensis, endemic to Madagascar and the Comoros Archipelago, clarifies its distribution and role in malaria transmission. A. mascarensis is the principal vector in east and southeast Madagascar but secondary in other regions, with behavioral variations between coastal and highland populations suggesting it may comprise a sibling species complex. Published data highlight significant differences between populations from different climatic and biogeographic regions.

articles.headline: Novel var diversity metric tracks parasite population dynamics
articles.title_original: Measuring changes in Plasmodium falciparum census population size in response to sequential malaria control interventions.
articles.details: This study introduces a novel parasite census population size endpoint based on var gene multiplicity of infection (MOIvar) to evaluate Plasmodium falciparum control. In northern Ghana (2012–2017), indoor residual spraying reduced transmission by >90% and prevalence by ~40–50%, with significant reductions in var diversity and parasite population size in ~2000 humans. However, 32 months after IRS discontinued and seasonal malaria chemoprevention began, var diversity rebounded in most age groups except 1–5 year-olds, demonstrating parasite population resilience.

articles.headline: Red fluorescent probe enables rapid malaria diagnosis distinction
articles.title_original: Advanced Clinical Diagnosis of Malaria Using a Simple Red-Emissive Smart Fluorescent Probe.
articles.details: A rationally designed hemicyanine fluorescent probe (MR-1) with blue absorption and red emission was developed for DNA-specific detection of malaria parasites. The probe shows excellent cell permeability and a detection limit of approximately 20 parasites/μL. Flow cytometric analysis and fluorescence microscopy of clinical samples enable precise detection and differentiation of malaria parasites from Babesia-infected cells with similar clinical symptoms.

articles.headline: Pfkelch13 mutations prevalent in Rwanda artemisinin resistance surveillance
articles.title_original: Genomic Surveillance of Plasmodium falciparum Drug Resistance Markers Between October 2021 and June 2023 in Kigali, Rwanda.
articles.details: Genomic surveillance of 250 P. falciparum isolates from Kigali (October 2021–June 2023) revealed high prevalence of resistance markers: pfcrt 76T (26%), pfmdr1 184F (72.8%), universal pfdhfr/pfdhps mutations, and pfkelch13 mutations in 50.4% including validated R561H (25.6%). Novel haplotypes including K189T + R561H (24.8%) were identified for the first time in Rwanda. These complex, evolving resistance patterns emphasize the need for continued surveillance and functional validation to inform control strategies.

articles.headline: Anopheles gambiae G3 strain genome sequence and assembly
articles.title_original: Whole-Genome Shotgun Sequencing and Assembly of Anopheles gambiae G3, the Host of Malaria Parasite Plasmodium sp.
articles.details: A draft whole-genome shotgun sequence and assembly of Anopheles gambiae strain G3 (isolated from Gambiae in 1975) is reported, totaling 216.3 megabase pairs with approximately 82,000 scaffolds, 53X coverage, and a G+C content of 44.5%.

articles.headline: Ethiopia regional surveillance shows 15.89% malaria confirmation rate
articles.title_original: Retrospective analysis of malaria surveillance data in the former Southern Nations, Nationalities, and peoples' region, Ethiopia (2017-2021).
articles.details: Analysis of 5-year malaria surveillance data (2017–2021) from the former SNNPR, Ethiopia revealed 6,292,147 suspected cases with 999,687 (15.89%) confirmed by laboratory, including 679,787 P. falciparum (68%) and 319,900 P. vivax (32%). Average annual incidence was 206,942 cases (12 per 1,000 population), with cases increasing from 214,095 in July 2021 to 270,274 in December 2021. Highest burden occurred in South Omo, Gamo, and Wolaita zones.

articles.headline: Male-sterile gene drive strategy shows promise for mosquito control
articles.title_original: A male-drive female-sterile system for the self-limited control of the malaria mosquito Anopheles gambiae.
articles.details: A novel self-limiting gene drive strategy called Male Drive Female Sterile (MDFS) was developed targeting Anopheles gambiae. The genetic construct causes dominant female sterility while transgenic males remain fertile and transmit the female-sterility trait at super-Mendelian rates. Laboratory studies demonstrate that repeated releases of MDFS can eliminate caged populations, and mathematical modeling suggests MDFS could be highly effective and self-limiting for suppressing wild malaria mosquito populations.
articles.notable_reason: Self-limiting gene drive eliminates caged populations; novel vector control

articles.headline: PfATP2 phospholipid flippase identified as antimalarial drug target
articles.title_original: PfATP2 is a flippase on the Plasmodium falciparum surface that is important for growth and influences parasite sensitivity to antiplasmodial compounds.
articles.authors: Qiu D, England E, Lehane AM
articles.details: This study identifies PfATP2, a phospholipid flippase localized to the Plasmodium falciparum parasite surface, as important for parasite growth and a target of antimalarial compounds MMV007224, MMV665852, and MMV665794. Parasites overexpressing PfATP2 show reduced sensitivity to these compounds while knockdown results in hypersensitivity. PfATP2 functions as a major ATP-dependent phosphatidylserine internalisation mechanism, making it a promising target for novel antimalarials.

articles.headline: LDH-based RDTs show superior sensitivity for vivax malaria detection
articles.title_original: First field evaluation of novel LDH- and HRP2-based rapid tests for Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum malaria diagnosis.
articles.details: A field evaluation in southern Ethiopia of four rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) in 708 patients (46% qPCR-positive: 77 P. falciparum mono-infections, 198 P. vivax mono-infections, 51 mixed infections) compared novel BIOCREDIT and other RDTs. At parasite densities >20/μL, all RDTs achieved >96% sensitivity for P. falciparum and >92% for P. vivax, substantially outperforming health center microscopy (63% and 72% respectively). LDH-based RDTs reliably detected P. falciparum infections with hrp2/3 deletions.

articles.headline: Kenya under-five malaria prevalence 22.72% with poverty as risk factor
articles.title_original: Individual and community level factors of malaria among under-five children in Kenya: Based on the Kenya Demographic and Health Survey.
articles.details: Using 2022 Kenya Demographic and Health Survey data on 3,146 children, multilevel logistic regression analysis found malaria prevalence of 22.72% (95% CI: 21.29–24.22). Protective factors included maternal age 25–34 years (AOR 0.75), richest wealth (AOR 0.48), treated bed net use (AOR 0.39), and breastfeeding (AOR 0.66). Community-level poverty was a significant risk factor (AOR 1.82), highlighting the relationship between socioeconomic factors and malaria burden in children.

articles.headline: Temperature and precipitation linked to malaria risk in SSA children
articles.title_original: Associations of temperature and precipitation with malaria in children under 5: A multi-country study in Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 15,009 children <5 years in 6 SSA countries (Burundi, Burkina Faso, Malawi, Nigeria, Tanzania, Uganda). Malaria prevalence 25.9% (Nigeria 38.1%, Burundi 38%). One-degree Celsius temperature increase raised risk 1.77-fold (95% CI: 1.297-2.414, p<0.001); annual precipitation 250-500mm associated with 72% higher risk versus >500mm (95% CI: 1.331-2.212, p<0.001). Authors conclude climate data should inform malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Phase Ib trial of blood-stage MSP1 vaccine in malaria-exposed adults
articles.title_original: Full-length merozoite surface protein 1 formulated with GLA-SE adjuvant in malaria pre-exposed adults: a randomised, controlled, double-blind, parallel-group, single-centre Phase Ib trial.
articles.details: Phase Ib RCT of 40 adults (18-45 years) in Tanzania. 20 received 3 monthly doses of SUM-101 (150 μg MSP1 FL from 3D7 strain plus 5 μg GLA-SE adjuvant) versus 20 rabies vaccine control. Safety: 26 solicited adverse events in SUM-101 arm (50% of participants) and 26 in control (65%), mostly mild-moderate (injection site pain, headache, fatigue). No serious adverse events. SUM-101 elicited robust MSP1-specific IgM and IgG titers peaking on days 56 and 84.

articles.headline: Correction to malaria epidemics and climate study
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "Malaria epidemics in India: Role of climatic condition and control measures" [Sci. Total Environ. Volume 712, 10 April 2020, 136368].
articles.details: Corrigendum to prior publication on malaria epidemics in India and climatic factors.

articles.headline: Antigen-specific antibodies associated with malaria protection in children
articles.title_original: A Multiplex Serological Assay to Evaluate the Antibody Responses to a Set of Plasmodium falciparum Antigens and Their Protective Role Against Malaria in Children Aged 1.5 to 12 Years Living in a Highly Seasonal Malaria Transmission Area of Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Cross-sectional and longitudinal study of children 1.5-12 years in highly seasonal malaria area of Burkina Faso. IgG responses to 16 P. falciparum antigens measured via multiplex assay. Anti-GLURP R2 and anti-Rh5.1 antibodies associated with reduced malaria risk (adjusted IRR 0.52 and 0.40, respectively). Antibody combination of AMA1, GLURP R2, and Etramp5 Ag1 showed best predictive performance (AUC 0.70). Authors propose less-studied antibodies offer prospects for improved diagnostics and vaccine development.

articles.headline: TCF25 transcription factor regulates parasite transmission and ribosome biogenesis
articles.title_original: Transcription factor 25 modulates gametocytogenesis and ribosome biogenesis in the malaria parasite Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Laboratory study using tcf25 knockout P. falciparum parasites with transcriptomic analysis. At ring stage: 168 genes downregulated, 24 upregulated; at schizont stage: 53 downregulated, 4 upregulated. ChIP-seq identified 44 TCF25 target genes including rDNA; TCF25 deficiency increased rRNA expression, particularly 28S rRNA. Authors identify TCF25 as key regulator of gametocytogenesis via ap2-g pathway and novel regulator of ribosomal biogenesis through direct control of A-type rRNAs.

articles.headline: PfEBA-175 shows high antigenic diversity yet remains vaccine candidate
articles.title_original: Micro-geographic variation in antigenic diversity of PfEBA-175 region II in asymptomatic Plasmodium falciparum infections in Tanzania.
articles.details: Analysis of 172 asymptomatic P. falciparum isolates from Geita and Kigoma, Tanzania.
