articles.title_original: Status and prospects of seasonal malaria chemoprevention among children in Sahelian countries: A systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of studies from 2013-2023 on seasonal malaria chemoprevention (sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine given monthly) in children under five in Sahelian countries. Meta-analysis of randomized trials, cohort, and observational studies showed SMC significantly reduced incidence of uncomplicated malaria, severe malaria, and mortality compared with control groups. Changing epidemiology with dry-season transmission persistence and increased cases in older children requires strategy adaptation.

articles.headline: Seaweed extract shows antiplasmodial activity against P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial activity of Gracilaria sp. ethanol extract against Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Gracilaria sp. ethanol extract was tested for in vitro activity against P. falciparum strain D37. Phytochemical analysis identified saponins, tannins, flavonoids, and alkaloids. Alkaloids, flavonoids, and derivative chemicals inhibited parasite growth with an IC50 of 43.38 μg/mL.

articles.headline: Detailed biophysics of malaria parasite endothelial cell adhesion
articles.title_original: Contact Dynamics of Cytoadhering Plasmodium falciparum-Infected Erythrocytes in Flow.
articles.details: Researchers investigated dynamic adhesion behavior of P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes on surfaces with ICAM-1, CD36, or both using molecular force sensors and high-speed microscopy. Trophozoite-stage parasites showed complex motion including flipping and sliding with patchy adhesion footprints, while schizont-stage parasites displayed more uniform rolling motion. CD36-containing surfaces increased sliding incidence for both stages, providing insights into cytoadhesion mechanisms.

articles.headline: High natural immunity to malaria transmission-blocking vaccine targets
articles.title_original: Seroprevalence of antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum transmission-blocking target proteins Pfs230D1M and Pfs48/45 in Tanzanian populations of diverse malaria transmission intensity.
articles.details: Serosurvey of 290 Tanzanians (114 children, 44 adolescents, 132 adults) tested for IgG antibodies against transmission-blocking antigens Pfs230D1M, Pfs48/45, and Pfs25. Seroprevalence was 56% for Pfs230D1M and 49% for Pfs48/45, with approximately 30% seropositive for both. Seroprevalence increased significantly with age (Pfs230D1M adjusted OR 3.16, 95% CI 1.81-5.53, p≤0.0001). Transmission-reducing activity detected in 2/10 tested sera.

articles.headline: Malaria compound library screens reveal SARS-CoV-2 inhibitors
articles.title_original: Screening of Medicines for Malaria Venture Open Boxes Identifies Potent SARS-CoV‑2 Papain-like Protease (PL(pro)) Inhibitors.
articles.details: Target-based screening of malaria-focused compound libraries against SARS-CoV-2 proteases identified MMV1634397 as a papain-like protease inhibitor (IC50 0.7 μM). Optimized analogs achieved IC50 values as low as 0.06 μM (compound 13). Lead compounds demonstrated antiviral activity in cell culture with EC50 values of 2.9-3.7 μM and favorable pharmacokinetic properties.

articles.headline: Historical analysis of malaria's military impact during Vietnam
articles.title_original: US Malaria Casualties and Chemoprophylaxis during the Vietnam War.
articles.details: Historical review of malaria's impact on US military during Vietnam War, with hospitalization rates matching combat wounds in 1965 and rates exceeding 10 per 1,000 man-days in front-line infantry. Chloroquine-primaquine chemoprophylaxis proved inadequate; addition of dapsone improved efficacy but caused rare agranulocytosis and at least 8 deaths. Post-war relapsing P. vivax caused thousands of US infections, with substantial lost time averaging over 1 month per case.

articles.headline: Helminth-malaria co-infection uncommon in severe malaria Uganda
articles.title_original: Low prevalence of helminth infection in Ugandan children hospitalized with severe malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 177 Ugandan children hospitalized with severe malaria (ages 6-48 months, 2014-2017) tested via qPCR for six helminth species, with 25 community controls. Helminth prevalence was low in severe malaria cases (5.1%, 9/177) and controls (4.0%, 1/25). Helminth infection did not modify risk of severe malaria (aRR 1.0, 95% CI 0.82-1.3, p=0.78), reflecting success of Uganda's national deworming program.

articles.headline: Non-falciparum malaria common and widespread across Rwanda
articles.title_original: Prevalence of asymptomatic non-falciparum and falciparum malaria in the 2014-15 Rwanda Demographic Health Survey.
articles.details: Quantitative PCR survey of 5,050 primarily adult Rwandans from 2014-2015 Demographic Health Survey testing for four Plasmodium species. Overall asymptomatic infection prevalence 22.3% (95% CI 20.3-24.3): P. falciparum 16.3% (14.5-18.1%), non-falciparum species 8.0% (6.6-9.3%), with P. vivax rare. Mixed-species infections were common where transmission was highest. P. falciparum associated with lower wealth, rural residence, and low elevation.

articles.headline: Cryo-EM structure enables transmission-blocking vaccine development
articles.title_original: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous Plasmodium falciparum Pfs230 and Pfs48/45 fertilization complex.
articles.details: Cryo-EM structure of endogenous P. falciparum Pfs230-Pfs48/45 fertilization complex revealed Pfs48/45 interaction with Pfs230 domains 13-14. Transgenic parasites lacking these domains showed defective gamete localization and reduced oocyst formation. Nanobodies against domains 13-14 inhibited complex formation and reduced transmission. These domains were targets of naturally acquired immunity and mRNA-LNP vaccine-induced antibodies that blocked parasite transmission.
articles.notable_reason: First structural basis for transmission-blocking vaccine targets

articles.headline: Geographic patterns of imported malaria cases in New York State
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria in New York: Geographic Patterns and Implications for Emergency Physicians.
articles.details: Surveillance study of imported malaria in New York State 2018–2024. NYC accounted for 79.1% of cases; 11 counties outside NYC contributed 88.3% of remaining state cases. Cases declined in 2020 (coinciding with COVID-19 travel restrictions) and rebounded in 2021. Findings highlight the need for travel history assessment and targeted prevention strategies in identified county-level hotspots.

articles.headline: Walnut leaf extract reduces oxidative stress in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Effect of walnut leaves on oxidative stress caused by murine cerebral malaria.
articles.details: Murine study of Plasmodium berghei-induced cerebral malaria in 30 female C57BL/6 mice assigned to six treatment groups. Juglans regia leaf extract (JRLE) treatment significantly reduced oxidative stress markers (nitric oxide, malondialdehyde, hydrogen peroxide) and increased antioxidant enzyme activities (catalase, superoxide dismutase, reduced glutathione, total antioxidant capacity) compared to infected controls. JRLE also mitigated elevated iNOS expression, providing neuroprotection.

articles.headline: Novel antimalarial strategy targeting parasite telomere maintenance
articles.title_original: Plasmodium telomere maintenance: uncovering the Achilles' heel for novel antimalarials.
articles.authors: Wakai TN, Anzaku DO, Afolabi IS
articles.details: Review examining telomere maintenance in Plasmodium as an antimalarial target. Plasmodium maintains continuous telomerase activity and unique telomere-binding proteins; telomeres harbor G-quadruplex DNA structures that could be drug targets. Inhibition of PfTERT (parasite telomerase catalytic subunit) has shown promise in blocking telomere elongation and impairing replication. Authors propose combining telomere-directed therapies with existing antimalarials to overcome resistance.

articles.headline: SARS-CoV-2 and malaria co-infection prevalence in Equatorial Guinea
articles.title_original: Circulation of SARS-CoV-2 and Co-Infection with Plasmodium falciparum in Equatorial Guinea.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 1,556 participants in Equatorial Guinea (June–October 2021). SARS-CoV-2 prevalence was 3.03%; malaria prevalence was 22.25%; co-infection prevalence was 0.37% (six cases). Genome sequencing of 43 SARS-CoV-2 isolates identified mostly Delta variant lineage introduced from Europe on multiple occasions. Malaria was most frequent in children and teenagers; SARS-CoV-2 distribution was similar across all age groups.

articles.headline: Plant-derived compounds show antimalarial activity against drug-resistant strains
articles.title_original: Plant-derived secondary metabolites for malaria treatment: extraction, mechanisms, and therapeutic potential.
articles.details: Review of 34 medicinal plants and their secondary metabolites with antimalarial potential. Compounds demonstrate potent activity against P. falciparum: joziknipholone A (IC50 0.17 μM), joziknipholone B (IC50 0.26 μM), and knipholone clooxanthrone (IC50 9.42–14.58 μM). Key metabolites include alkaloids, flavonoids, terpenoids, and derivatives (quinine, artemisinin, quercetin, chalcones). Review highlights these as templates for novel drugs against both drug-sensitive and drug-resistant strains.

articles.headline: Correction to ecological drivers study
articles.title_original: Correction: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission over 1 year of long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.details: Correction notice to a prior publication.

articles.headline: High metagenomic complexity in asymptomatic malaria infections
articles.title_original: Metagenomic complexity of high, seasonal transmission of Plasmodium spp. in asymptomatic carriers in Northern Sahelian Ghana.
articles.details: Metagenomic study of 188 afebrile residents in high-transmission Northern Ghana. Asymptomatic submicroscopic infection rate was 2.67% (by qPCR). Infections showed single-, double-, and triple-species complexity involving P. falciparum, P. malariae, P. ovale curtisi, and P. ovale wallikeri. Prevalence and metagenomic complexity were significantly higher in larger blood volumes. Findings underscore the need for sensitive measurement of within-host complexity for optimized surveillance and control.

articles.headline: PfPP1 phosphatase regulates parasite transmission through gametocyte signaling
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum PP1 phosphatase is a key regulator of malaria parasite transmission.
articles.details: Functional study using conditional depletion of PfPP1 in Plasmodium falciparum. PfPP1 regulates deformability and permeability of mature gametocyte-infected erythrocytes through dephosphorylation of PKA substrates, crucial for gametocyte persistence in blood. PfPP1 also controls gametocyte activation via the cGMP/PKG pathway. Results demonstrate PfPP1's pivotal role in both gametocyte development and gamete formation, suggesting this pathway is a potential transmission-blocking target.

articles.headline: Melochia umbellata achieves 86% parasitemia suppression in rat malaria
articles.title_original: Curative antimalarial efficacy and hepato-renal protective effects of Melochia umbellata leaf extract in rats infected with Plasmodium berghei.
articles.details: In vivo study of Melochia umbellata aqueous leaf extract (MULAE) in Plasmodium berghei-infected rats. LD50 >5,000 mg/kg. Five-day curative assay with MULAE (100, 200, 400 mg/kg) vs chloroquine (10 mg/kg) control: MULAE 400 mg/kg achieved 86.20% chemosuppression and restored hemoglobin to 14.9 g/dL. Treatment improved hepatic and renal markers, mitigated weight loss, and ameliorated histopathological damage in liver, kidneys, and spleen.

articles.headline: High drug resistance mutations in asymptomatic malaria in Vietnam
articles.title_original: Molecular Profiles of Antimalarial Drug Resistance in Plasmodium Species from Asymptomatic Malaria Carriers in Gia Lai Province, Vietnam.
articles.details: Survey of 2,171 individuals in endemic Gia Lai province, Vietnam for asymptomatic submicroscopic malaria (qPCR). Infection rate was 2.67%; P. vivax more prevalent than P. falciparum with mixed infections present. Adults predominantly affected; more common in females. High mutation frequencies observed: pfk13 C580Y (100%), pfcrt M74I/N75E/K76T (100%), pvmdr-1 F1076L (100%). Findings emphasize urgent need for improved surveillance to prevent silent spread of resistance.

articles.headline: Public-private partnerships for climate-resilient malaria control in Africa
articles.title_original: Challenges and opportunities for greater public-private partnership for the implementation of the WHO operational framework for building climate resilient health systems to improve malaria control and elimination in Sub-Saharan Africa: a rapid review.
articles.details: Systematic review (PRISMA/CRD guidelines) of 11 studies on public-private partnerships for malaria control implementing WHO's climate resilience framework in Sub-Saharan Africa. Key challenges identified: ineffective legal and policy frameworks, bureaucracy, limited partner adherence to guidelines, insufficient climate risk assessment integration. Opportunities exist in workforce training, medicines and technologies, and emergency management. PPPs remain viable alternatives despite challenges given the technical and financial capacity constraints in SSA.

articles.headline: Risk factors for delayed malaria care-seeking in Myanmar and Thailand
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking among febrile patients in Myanmar and Thailand: an accelerated failure time analysis.
articles.details: Longitudinal analysis of febrile patients in Myanmar (n=2,960, mean 1.8 day fever duration) and Thailand (n=15,576, mean 2.9 days). Accelerated Failure Time models identified delays in Myanmar: Shan ethnicity (TR 1.48), P. vivax diagnosis (TR 1.23), farming occupation (TR 1.12). In Thailand: farming (TR 1.07), pre/primary education (TR 1.02), P. vivax (TR 1.20), P. falciparum (TR 1.27). Shorter delays associated with daily bed net use in both countries.

articles.headline: Voltage-switched mass spectrometry perfectly classifies malaria in blood
articles.title_original: Voltage Switching on a Dual Nano-Electrospray/Atmospheric Pressure Chemical Ionization Platform Provides Higher Discrimination Power for Malaria Detection via Direct Infusion Mass Spectrometry.
articles.authors: Ferguson R, Kulyk DS, Badu-Tawiah AK
articles.details: Compared voltage-switching mode nanoelectrospray/APCI mass spectrometry to constant-voltage methods for malaria detection in 9 infected and 9 uninfected blood samples. Voltage switching correctly classified all 18 samples (100% accuracy), while constant APCI misclassified 4 samples and constant nESI misclassified 2. Composite spectra provided superior discrimination power.

articles.headline: Novel selective proteasome inhibitor J-80 blocks malaria drug target
articles.title_original: A recombinant expression system for the Plasmodium falciparum proteasome enables structural analysis of its assembly and the design of selective inhibitors.
articles.details: Developed a recombinant Pf20S proteasome expression system for drug discovery. Tripeptide-epoxyketone J-80 inhibited Pf20S β5 with IC50 of 22.4 nM and 90-fold selectivity over human β5. Novel inhibitors TDI-8304 and analogs also showed potency and selectivity over human proteasome. Platform accelerates development of selective antimalarial therapeutics.

articles.headline: BinaxNOW rapid test validates with 100% sensitivity in US hospitals
articles.title_original: BinaxNOW vs malaria: A diagnostic showdown: Design and implementation of a verification strategy across 4 academic hospitals in an area with low malaria prevalence.
articles.details: Evaluated FDA-cleared BinaxNOW Malaria rapid diagnostic across four academic hospitals in low-malaria-prevalence US region using 294 initial specimens and archived positive/negative samples at verification sites. Initial evaluation showed 100% sensitivity and specificity; 100% of P. falciparum specimens correctly identified, 96% to species level. Subsequent site-based verification: 100% positive percent agreement, negative percent agreement, concordance, and reproducibility.

articles.headline: Anopheles maculatus wings show seasonal morphometric variation in Thailand
articles.title_original: Seasonal variation in wing geometry of the malaria vector Anopheles maculatus (Diptera: Culicidae) in Western Thailand.
articles.authors: Sumruayphol S, Chaiphongpachara T, Laojun S
articles.details: Analyzed seasonal wing structure and contour variation in A. maculatus (malaria vector) from western Thailand using landmark-based and outline-based geometric morphometrics across hot, wet, and dry seasons. Wing size differed significantly between hot and dry seasons (p<0.05); wing shape differed between dry-hot and dry-wet populations (p<0.05). Findings enhance understanding of vector population dynamics for surveillance.

articles.headline: Pfcoronin mutations drive artemisinin resistance independent of Pfkelch13
articles.title_original: Cellular and molecular basis of Pf Coronin function in artemisinin resistance in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Demonstrated that resistance-associated Pfcoronin mutations (reported from African treatment failures) disrupt Pf Coronin-Actin interaction, impair ring-stage endocytosis and hemoglobin uptake, and reduce heme availability for artemisinin activation. Pfcoronin mutations function in distinct cellular regions from Pfkelch13 but converge on limiting heme availability. Identifies a second major mechanism of clinical artemisinin resistance alongside Pfkelch13.
articles.notable_reason: New mechanism of artemisinin resistance beyond Pfkelch13

articles.headline: Fever drives malaria cytoadhesion through endothelial glycocalyx shedding
articles.title_original: Febrile temperature enhances Plasmodium falciparum cytoadhesion by disrupting the endothelial glycocalyx.
articles.details: 3D fever-on-a-chip model of human brain and lung microvessels showed febrile temperatures at 40°C rapidly enhanced infected RBC and immune cell binding. Mechanistically, fever caused endothelial glycocalyx shedding exposing EPCR and ICAM-1 receptors. MMP inhibitor prevented temperature-induced cytoadhesion increase. Identifies fever as host-specific amplifier of cerebral malaria vascular pathology, suggesting antipyretic and endothelial-protective interventions.
articles.notable_reason: Mechanistic basis for fever-worsened cerebral malaria with intervention targets

articles.headline: Junctional epitope vaccine shows comparable protection as R21 benchmark
articles.title_original: Protective immunity against malaria by a nanoparticle CIS43-based junctional vaccine alone or in combination with R21.
articles.details: Developed self-assembling nanoparticle vaccine displaying CIS43 junctional epitope of PfCSP and compared to WHO-recommended R21 vaccine in liver burden reduction assays. At 1 μg dose, junctional vaccine induced comparable liver burden reduction to R21. At 0.25 μg, R21 performed better. Combined junctional and R21 vaccines induced modestly enhanced protection versus either alone. Junctional vaccine elicited antibodies against both junctional and major repeats.

articles.headline: COVID-19 vaccination patterns predict malaria vaccine acceptance in Senegal
articles.title_original: Attitudes to malaria vaccination in children: what can be learned from their consistency with the temporal dynamics of COVID-19 vaccination intention? A prospective cohort study in rural Senegal (July 2020-November 2023).
articles.details: Six-wave telephone survey of 558 households in rural Senegal (2020-2023) on malaria vaccination attitudes for children and COVID-19 vaccination dynamics. 65.28% expressed very strong positive attitudes to malaria vaccination, associated with higher COVID-19 uptake (p<0.001). Three COVID-19 temporal patterns identified: continuously strong intention (34.88%), increasingly strong (33.40%), increasingly less strong (31.72%). Households with both members having continuously strong COVID-19 intention more likely to accept malaria vaccination (p=0.001).

articles.headline: Malaria resurgence in Africa requires urgent intervention strategy
articles.title_original: The resurgence of malaria in Africa is an avoidable crisis-here's what we must do.
articles.details: Opinion/commentary on malaria crisis in Africa. No abstract available; article appears to advocate for action on malaria resurgence.

articles.headline: High P. falciparum genetic diversity documented in northern Côte d'Ivoire
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity of Plasmodium falciparum in Korhogo Health District, Northern Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.details: Genotyped P. falciparum from 179 dried blood spot samples (Korhogo district, 2019-2020) using msp1, msp2, and glurp markers with amplification success: 82% (msp1), 85% (msp2), 75% (glurp). Detected high genetic diversity: msp1 K1 (20 genotypes), MAD20 (23), RO33 (1); msp2 3D7 (59) and FC27 (33); glurp (45). Expected heterozygosity ≥0.9 for all markers. Provides baseline data for assessing control interventions.

articles.headline: Four interlocking pathways drive malaria-induced dyserythropoiesis in bone marrow
articles.title_original: Malaria and dyserythropoiesis: a mini review.
articles.details: Mechanistic review synthesizing recent studies on malaria-associated anemia via bone marrow dysfunction. Proposes four convergent pathways: cytokine storm (IL-6, TNF-α, IFN-γ, MIF) suppresses erythropoietin and upregulates hepcidin; hemozoin-generated oxidative stress damages GATA-1 triggering erythroblast apoptosis; parasites infect erythroblasts and release extracellular vesicles disrupting differentiation; hemozoin-laden macrophages remodel erythroblastic islands causing iron restriction. Creates "developmental sanctuary" favoring parasite persistence while impairing erythropoiesis.

articles.headline: H. pylori and T. gondii coinfections frequent in HBV-positive Ghanaians
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Helicobacter pylori, Salmonella typhi, Plasmodium falciparum, and Toxoplasma gondii infections and levels of liver function markers among Hepatitis B Virus infected Ghanaians: A cross-sectional study in the Greater Accra Region.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 120 HBV-infected persons in Greater Accra, Ghana screened for H. pylori, S. typhi, P. falciparum, and T. gondii. Coinfection prevalence: H. pylori 40.8% (95% CI 32.5-49.8), S. typhi 2.5%, T. gondii 19.2% (95% CI 13.1-27.1), P. falciparum 2.5%. HBV-H. pylori coinfected persons had elevated ALT (33.5 vs 23.5 IU, p<0.001). Highlights need for expanded HBV coinfection screening beyond malaria to identify biochemical derangements.

articles.headline: PlasmoDocking web tool automates P. falciparum drug screening
articles.title_original: PlasmoDocking: A User-Friendly Open-Source Web Tool for Virtual Screening Targeting Plasmodium falciparum Enzymes.
articles.details: Web-based platform enabling users to submit up to 10 molecular structures for automated AutodockGPU screening against 38 pre-configured P. falciparum targets, facilitating systematic comparison of binding energies with co-crystallized ligands. Developed using Python and Next.js, the open-source platform accelerates malaria drug discovery through simultaneous multi-target docking simulations.

articles.headline: 55% of sub-Saharan Africans self-medicate for malaria
articles.title_original: Issues associated with malaria self-medication in sub-Saharan Africa: A systematic literature review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 27 studies in sub-Saharan Africa found pooled prevalence of 55.3% for malaria self-medication. Key drivers include low income, cheap non-prescription drug availability, large family size, lack of health insurance, and difficulty accessing formal healthcare. Study emphasizes need for regulatory frameworks to encourage proper antimalarial use.

articles.headline: Spatial repellents reduce malaria infection risk 52%
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Spatial Repellents in Malaria Prevention: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 4 RCTs involving 6,745 participants from China, Indonesia, and Kenya found spatial repellents reduced malaria infection risk from 31.0% in controls to 20.0% (pooled RR 0.48, 95% CI 0.33-0.70, P<0.001), a 52% relative risk reduction. No significant effects on mosquito density or sporozoite rates observed. High heterogeneity limited certainty of findings.
articles.notable_reason: Substantial malaria risk reduction from novel vector control approach

articles.headline: Burkina Faso halts transgenic mosquito malaria control program
articles.title_original: Burkina Faso halts plan to fight malaria with transgenic mosquitoes.
articles.details: Policy brief on halted program to fight malaria with transgenic mosquitoes. Misinformation campaign may have eroded trust and led to a raid on research center.

articles.headline: Correction: Aspartic protease inhibitors for P. vivax
articles.title_original: Correction: Computational identification of aspartic protease inhibitors for antimalarial drug development against Plasmodium Vivax.
articles.details: Correction notice to previously published article on computational identification of aspartic protease inhibitors for P. vivax antimalarial drug development. No new data presented.

articles.headline: In-silico design of FREP1-targeted transmission-blocking vaccine
articles.title_original: Designing a multi-epitope vaccine against the midgut-specific fibrinogen-related protein 1(FREP1) of Anopheles stephensi to enhance protection against the malaria parasite: a step beyond traditional vaccine development approaches.
articles.details: Immunoinformatic design of multi-epitope vaccine targeting An. stephensi FREP1 protein for transmission-blocking. Vaccine construct assessed for allergenicity, antigenicity, toxicity, and immunogenicity. Molecular docking revealed efficient TLR4 receptor interaction; in-silico immunization profiling indicated high immune response potency. Codon optimization demonstrated efficient protein expression in E. coli.

articles.headline: Drug combinations show additive killing in malaria model
articles.title_original: Fractional curative killing of pyronaridine or artesunate combinations with tafenoquine, 4-aminoquinolines, or azithromycin in a murine malaria-luciferase model.
articles.authors: Lee G, Aneke JS, Sullivan DJ Jr
articles.details: Investigation of curative interactions between artesunate or pyronaridine combined with tafenoquine, chloroquine, amodiaquine, or azithromycin in P. berghei ANKA-luciferase mouse model. Fractional combination curative doses: tafenoquine/artesunate 0.83, pyronaridine/amodiaquine 0.93; others 0.9-1.1. All combinations in additive range. Time to parasitemia return imprecise for predicting cure.

articles.headline: Azithromycin analogs kill parasites via multi-mechanism pathway
articles.title_original: Characterizing the quick-killing mechanism of action of azithromycin analogs against malaria parasites.
articles.details: Five azithromycin analogs showed improved quick-killing: non-chloroquinoline analogs 10-to 42-fold better than parent, chloroquinoline-containing analogs 2-to 17-fold better. Compounds linked to chloroquine-like mechanisms via hemoglobin digestion inhibition, though maintained activity against chloroquine-resistant parasites. Cellular thermal shift proteomics revealed stabilization of 34-155 distinct proteins with minimal overlap to chloroquine. Most potent analog showed significant gametocytocidal improvement.

articles.headline: P. ovale dominates asymptomatic infections; novel Pfk13 SNPs found
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium species in asymptomatic individuals in North-Eastern South Africa: 2018 - 2019.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 985 asymptomatic individuals in Ha-Lambani, South Africa (2018-2019) found 7.1% prevalence (95% CI: 0.054-0.087). P. ovale predominated (57.14%), followed by P. falciparum (37.1%), P. malariae (1.43%), and P. vivax (1.43%). Pfk13 sequencing detected 57 SNPs with 70.1% non-synonymous; none previously associated with artemisinin resistance. Five novel SNPs detected in propeller domain (H719Q, P701T, M472I, I526R, P443S).
articles.notable_reason: Novel Pfk13 mutations; P. ovale prevalence threatens vaccine coverage

articles.headline: Armed conflict in DRC associated with malaria outbreak risk
articles.title_original: The impact of armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on the risk of malaria outbreaks in children under five years old.
articles.details: Study examining the impact of armed conflict in eastern Democratic Republic of Congo on malaria outbreak risk in children under five years old. Abstract not provided.

articles.headline: Tafenoquine cost-effective for vivax malaria radical cure in Brazil
articles.title_original: The cost-effectiveness of tafenoquine following screening with STANDARD™ G6PD screening for the treatment of vivax malaria in the Brazilian Public Health System.
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis in Brazilian Public Health System comparing tafenoquine after G6PD screening versus current practice (7-day primaquine without screening) for vivax malaria radical cure. Tafenoquine cost US$2,894 per DALY averted, well below willingness-to-pay threshold of US$7,752, with >98% likelihood of cost-effectiveness. Tafenoquine dominated primaquine screening strategy with cost savings of US$13.
articles.notable_reason: Tafenoquine proven cost-effective; could change practice in endemic settings

articles.headline: Preterm birth increases malaria susceptibility 2.7-fold in offspring
articles.title_original: Preterm Birth and Malaria Susceptibility in Offspring of Uninfected Multigravid Women.
articles.details: Prospective cohort of 1,679 children in Mali followed up to 5 years found pregnancy malaria increased hazard of first P. falciparum infection across all maternal gravidities. Preterm delivery (vs full-term) increased hazard in multigravid women (HR 1.76, 95% CI 1.05-2.95, P=0.03). Parasitemia incidence rate ratio for preterm offspring was 2.17 (95% CI 1.25-3.75) in secundigravida and 2.74 (95% CI 1.80-4.18) in multigravida women.
articles.notable_reason: Novel preterm-gravidity interaction; 2.7-fold increased parasitemia risk

articles.headline: Asymptomatic Plasmodium infections common in migrant screening
articles.title_original: Clinical Findings in Migrants With Asymptomatic Plasmodium Infections.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 65 Plasmodium PCR-positive and 54 PCR-negative Sub-Saharan African migrants in Stockholm. Plasmodium-infected individuals had significantly higher rates of anemia (21.1% vs 6.1%, P=0.048), elevated ESR (58.1% vs 25.0%, P=0.008), elevated IgM (30.5% vs 10.5%, P=0.030), and splenomegaly (25.4% vs 2.5%, P=0.002). After antimalarial treatment, these laboratory parameters improved.

articles.headline: Anopheles species identification in Colombian gold mining areas
articles.title_original: Anopheles species associated with malaria transmission in gold-mining areas of northwest Colombia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey identified 11 Anopheles species in five gold-mining localities in El Bagre, Colombia. Six specimens tested Plasmodium-positive: Anopheles darlingi infected with P. falciparum; Anopheles nuneztovari and Anopheles punctimacula infected with P. vivax. Entomological inoculation rates ranged 1.5–6.6 infective bites per person per month across localities.

articles.headline: MALDI-TOF mass spectrometry identifies secondary malaria vectors
articles.title_original: MALDI-TOF MS for identification of Afro-tropical secondary malaria vectors.
articles.details: MALDI-TOF MS evaluated for identifying secondary malaria vectors from Kenya and Mozambique using cephalothorax spectra. Overall accuracy was 91% (kappa 0.87), with 100% sensitivity and specificity for most species except Anopheles cf. coustani 2 and Anopheles ziemanni. Performance was equivalent to DNA sequencing, supporting sustainable vector surveillance.

articles.headline: EPI performance affects malaria chemoprevention delivery in Togo
articles.title_original: Assessing immunization coverage in Southern togo: implications for perennial malaria chemoprevention delivery.
articles.details: Among 583 children aged 12–23 months in southern Togo, 60.7% achieved full immunization and 15.8% had zero-dose status. Key barriers included family discouragement (17.4%), lack of time (15.1%), and COVID-19-related issues (14%). Zero-dose status was significantly associated with low caregiver education (aOR 5.02, 95% CI 1.85–13.60), negative vaccination attitudes (aOR 8.60, 95% CI 3.32–22.30), and lack of modern healthcare reliance (aOR 3.06, 95% CI 1.26–7.48).

articles.headline: Designed multistage vaccine targets infection and transmission blocking
articles.title_original: A combined designed CSP and Pfs48/45 infection and transmission blocking vaccine for malaria.
articles.details: Ferritin nanoparticle vaccine presenting structure-based designed CSPj5c and 17-4 immunogens from circumsporozoite and Pfs48/45 antigens. Immunization protected mice against challenge with transgenic sporozoites expressing P. falciparum CSP, and purified IgGs from immunized rabbits elicited potent transmission-reducing activity.
articles.notable_reason: Novel multistage vaccine design targeting infection and transmission

articles.headline: Herbal extract combination protects in cerebral malaria model
articles.title_original: Antimalarial and neuroprotective effects of ethanolic extracts of the five-flower remedy in an experimental cerebral malaria model.
articles.details: Male C57BL/6 mice induced with P. berghei ANKA experimental cerebral malaria treated with ethanolic five-flower remedy extract (600 mg/kg) combined with artesunate for seven days. The combination reduced parasitemia 80%, increased survival, reduced neurological deficits, and decreased brain hemorrhage, leukocyte infiltration, and neuronal apoptosis on histology.

articles.headline: Cyclic nucleotide signaling essential for malaria parasite transmission
articles.title_original: A divergent cyclic nucleotide binding protein promotes Plasmodium ookinete infection of the mosquito.
articles.details: CBP-O, a cryptic cyclic nucleotide-binding protein, localizes to the ookinete apex. Disruption of PbCBP-O severely compromised ookinete invasion of mosquitoes. The protein exhibits dual binding specificity to both cGMP and cAMP, suggesting the ookinete apical complex acts as a platform for transducing cyclic nucleotide signals essential for transmission.

articles.headline: Flavin deficiency in red blood cells confers malaria protection
articles.title_original: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes offer protection against malaria parasites.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Flavin-deficient erythrocytes (FDE) identified in 23% of screened individuals in Ferrara, Italy (n=150) and 13% in Huye, Rwanda (n=169). Both FDE and in vitro flavin-depleted erythrocytes inhibited intraerythrocytic proliferation of P. falciparum through increased susceptibility to oxidative stress. Genetic mutations in the FAD synthase gene were identified in three FDE individuals from Rwanda.
articles.notable_reason: Natural genetic protection mechanism against malaria parasites

articles.headline: Fc engineering enhances anti-CSP antibody malaria protection
articles.title_original: Analysis of Fc-dependent effector functions of anti-malaria circumsporozoite protein antibodies.
articles.details: Fc-dependent effector functions were required for maximal prophylactic protection via CSP monoclonal antibody 317. mAbs L9 and CIS43 were potent drivers of antibody-dependent phagocytosis, NK cell activation and killing, and complement deposition. mAb 317 distinctly enhanced activation of CSP-responsive T cells after dendritic cell acquisition of mAb-complexed antigens.

articles.headline: Plant compound shows repellent activity against malaria vectors
articles.title_original: COMPARATIVE BEHAVIORAL RESPONSES OF Β-CARYOPHYLLENE AGAINST ANOPHELES MOSQUITO SPECIES, POTENTIAL VECTORS OF MALARIA IN THAILAND.
articles.details: β-caryophyllene evaluated for excito-repellency against four Anopheles species. Anopheles minimus showed highest escape rates (100% at 2.5% contact and 5% noncontact concentrations). Anopheles harrisoni exhibited strong responses at 7.5% (94.34% contact, 72.73% noncontact escape). Anopheles epiroticus showed minimal response (12–25.42%). No knockdown or mortality occurred.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence high in under-five febrile children, Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Malaria and Associated Factors among Under Five Febrile Children Attending Ener Amanuel Health Center, Southern Ethiopia: An Institution-based Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Tseha ST, Ebabu M, Aberar T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 218 febrile children under five in southern Ethiopia; malaria prevalence was 21.1%, with P. falciparum predominant at 60.9%. Risk factors included proximity to mosquito breeding sites (P=0.025), not sleeping under insecticide-treated nets (P=0.021), and having beds unsuitable for ITN hanging (P=0.002).

articles.headline: Stakeholder analysis guides malaria intervention scale-up strategy
articles.title_original: Assessing scalability factors of a malaria intervention using Malakit and target drug administration among hard-to-reach populations: An original approach focusing on key stakeholders - implementers and decision-makers - in the Guiana shield.
articles.details: Qualitative pre-planning assessment of Malakit (self-testing and self-treatment kits) and targeted P. vivax drug administration scalability in Guiana Shield (Brazil, French Guiana, Suriname) conducted June 2023–March 2024. Mendelow's Matrix and SWOT analysis identified strengths (intervention effectiveness), weaknesses (data gaps, regulatory challenges), opportunities (elimination initiatives), and threats (funding, regulatory issues).

articles.headline: Challenges to malaria control in hard-to-reach populations
articles.title_original: Malaria in hard-to-reach populations: Challenges and solutions.
articles.details: Review of challenges to malaria control in hard-to-reach populations and potential solutions.

articles.headline: Transmission diversity and mobility challenge malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Biodiversity of malaria ecosystems, challenges for control: When human diversity is forgotten.
articles.details: Review traces malaria elimination from temperate countries (150 years ago) and post-2000 global decline, but notes resurgence in sub-Saharan Africa ~50 years ago. Argues transmission diversity and human population mobility prevent continued progress. Identifies access to health and reaching marginalized/mobile populations as key to advancing elimination.

articles.headline: Suriname eliminated P. falciparum through targeted community interventions
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Suriname: how targeted interventions in marginalized populations led to elimination.
articles.details: National surveillance data 2000-2024 showed P. falciparum declined from endemic disease in indigenous/tribal communities to occupational disease in remote gold mining sites. Last indigenous case 2018. Strategy focused on low-barrier care access for all populations. Demonstrates country-level P. falciparum elimination but notes continued re-introduction risk from neighboring countries and need for regional cooperation.

articles.headline: Village health workers in malaria elimination: systematic review
articles.title_original: Role of village health worker in control activities for malaria elimination efforts: A systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 44 studies (14,884 articles screened) mostly from Africa. VHWs primarily engaged in diagnosis/treatment (21 studies), prevention with LLINs/IPTc (4 studies), seasonal chemoprevention (2). Review finds VHWs mostly involved in routine control activities rather than elimination efforts. Authors recommend integration of VHWs into elimination programs as countries pursue national elimination goals.

articles.headline: Vernonia amygdalina extract shows antiplasmodial activity against P. knowlesi
articles.title_original: Vernonia amygdalina (Delile) exhibits in vitro anti-plasmodial activities against Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.details: In vitro evaluation of Vernonia amygdalina leaf extracts against P. knowlesi. Methanol extract showed IC50 = 1.131 ± 0.16 μg/mL with CC50 = 63.73 ± 0.78 µg/mL (selectivity index >10). Extracts likely non-toxic to mammalian cells. Authors conclude crude extracts show anti-plasmodial activity and suggest further studies for identification of active metabolites.

articles.headline: Cost analysis of precision-guided sterile insect technique for vectors
articles.title_original: Estimated cost and operational structure of pgSIT malaria vector control programs in selected West African countries.
articles.details: Economic analysis of precision-guided sterile insect technique (pgSIT) for Anopheles gambiae suppression in West African countries. Previous cost-effectiveness estimates used only 15-31% sex-sorting capacity. Authors developed optimized facility size (9.2 million mosquitoes suppressed) to improve cost-effectiveness. Intervention could interrupt transmission, strengthen health institutions, and create local capacity and jobs.

articles.headline: Severe falciparum malaria causes electrolyte and renal abnormalities
articles.title_original: Exploring the Effect of Malaria on Biochemical Parameters Among Sudanese Patients with Complicated Falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: Case-control study (200 severe malaria, 200 controls) in Sudan (Aug 2022-Jan 2023). Children with severe falciparum malaria showed decreased plasma potassium and sodium (p < 0.05). Adults showed increased urea (p < 0.05). Authors conclude malaria causes hyponatremia and hypokalemia in children and azotemia in adults; early detection of abnormalities may prevent complications.

articles.headline: Fatal P. falciparum malaria complicated by acute subdural hematoma
articles.title_original: A fatal case report of Plasmodium falciparum malaria complicated by acute subdural hematoma.
articles.authors: Xie N, Ye H, Jiang X
articles.details: Case report of 59-year-old traveler from Burkina Faso with P. falciparum infection. Presented with fever, headache, myalgia; 9-day delay before diagnosis and artesunate initiation. Despite anti-malarial treatment, patient died from multiple organ failure complicated by acute subdural hematoma. Case illustrates severe complications and importance of rapid diagnosis.

articles.headline: Stachytarpheta jamaicensis shows antiplasmodial activity in infected mice
articles.title_original: Antiplasmodial and immunomodulatory activities of dichloromethane extract of Stachytarpheta jamaicensis in Plasmodium berghei infected mice.
articles.authors: Amaechi EC, Owolodun OA, Lambe KO
articles.details: Mouse study of Stachytarpheta jamaicensis dichloromethane extract in chloroquine-resistant P. berghei infection. At 200 mg/kg dose, extract mediated parasite clearance and improved survival with significant antiplasmodial effect by Day 11 (p < 0.05). Extract modulated TGF-β and both pro/anti-inflammatory cytokines. Authors suggest potential for novel antimalarial and immunomodulatory drug development.

articles.headline: Crateva adansonii extracts show larvicidal activity against A. gambiae
articles.title_original: Bioactive profiling, larvicidal and pupicidal activities of Crateva adansonii against two developmental stages of malaria vector, Anopheles gambiae.
articles.authors: Ileke KD, Babatunde NA, Akeju AV
articles.details: In vitro study of Crateva adansonii extracts (leaf, stem, root) against Anopheles gambiae larvae and pupae using ethanol extraction and GC-MS analysis. Root extract achieved 100% larval mortality at 15-20% concentration within 12-24 hours (LC50 = 0.04% at 24h). Leaf extract achieved 100% mortality at 20% in 6 hours. Authors propose plant-based vector control as eco-friendly alternative.

articles.headline: Host immune mechanisms in P. falciparum malaria: review
articles.title_original: Overview of the host immune response to P. falciparum malaria.
articles.authors: Antwi-Baffour S, Mensah BT, Annison L
articles.details: Review of innate and adaptive immune responses to P. falciparum malaria. Describes roles of macrophages, dendritic cells, NK cells, T lymphocytes, and B lymphocytes. Notes that protective immunity is complex, influenced by parasite diversity, host genetics, and prior exposure. Some individuals develop partial immunity while others remain susceptible; understanding mechanisms is essential for vaccine development.

articles.headline: High P. falciparum carriage in households despite children's SMC coverage
articles.title_original: Patterns of patent and sub-patent Plasmodium falciparum infections in household members of children under seasonal malaria chemoprevention coverage in the health district of Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 650 household members of children under seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) in Nanoro, Burkina Faso (2022). Found 68.6% harbored P. falciparum infections: 27.4% patent infections, 41.2% sub-patent (varATS qPCR-positive/RDT-negative). Patent prevalence declined with age but sub-patent infections remained high (38-49%) across all ages. Authors recommend extending SMC to school-aged children and implementing household member testing/treatment.

articles.headline: Indonesia verifies 389 districts malaria-free by 2030 timeline
articles.title_original: Shrinking the malaria map in Indonesia: progress of subnational control, elimination, and future strategies.
articles.details: Review of Indonesia's subnational elimination strategy from 1950–2023. As of 2023, 389 of 514 districts and 5 of 38 provinces verified as having no local transmission. Malaria cases decreased significantly by 2015 but rose during COVID-19. Over 90% of remaining cases concentrated in Papua region with incidence exceeding 400 cases per 1,000 person-years. Challenges include climate-driven highland expansion and increasing zoonotic P. knowlesi reports. Achievement of 2030 elimination requires intensified interventions, especially in high-transmission Papua.

articles.headline: Migrant care-seeking delays common despite positive malaria attitudes
articles.title_original: Malaria care-seeking behaviours and infection prevalence among short-term Myanmar migrants in Thailand.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 300 Myanmar migrants in Thai endemic villages (mean age 34.5 years, 47% male). While 60% recognized need to seek care for fever, only 35% actually did so within 24 hours. Six asymptomatic P. vivax infections detected (2% prevalence). Lower malaria knowledge (OR 13.5, 95% CI 1.58–177.0) and lower care-seeking scores (OR 5.86, 95% CI 1.15–57.7) significantly associated with infection. Socioeconomic status, migration patterns, ethnicity, and knowledge gaps drive care-seeking disparities.

articles.headline: LLIN education boosts community acceptance but not timely diagnosis-seeking
articles.title_original: Contributions of health education and social mobilization in the implementation of a malaria control programme using long-lasting insecticidal nets.
articles.authors: da Costa FM, de Souza Rodrigues MM, Scarpassa VM
articles.details: Implementation of intensive health education and social mobilization in 10 Brazilian villages (2010–2012) with distribution of 8,083 long-lasting insecticidal nets. Achieved 99.93% LLIN acceptance and 92.13% adequate net availability with 57.41% consistent use; however, intervention did not increase diagnosis-seeking within 24 hours of symptom onset. Two villages with baseline malaria showed monthly median cases of 24 and 7 respectively, with treatment-seeking rates remaining at 65% and 61%.

articles.headline: Malaria parasites show epigenetic host-dependent gene expression shift
articles.title_original: Generalist Malaria Parasites and Host Imprinting: Unveiling Transcriptional Memory.
articles.details: Experimental study inoculating avian malaria parasite P. homocircumflexum into different bird species revealed gene expression shifts from donor-dependent (2,647 differentially expressed genes at 8 days post-infection) to recipient-dependent (271 DEGs at 16 dpi). Low genetic differentiation ruled out selection for specific haplotypes. Findings support epigenetic regulation and transcriptional plasticity as mechanisms enabling generalist parasites to adapt to diverse host environments over time.

articles.headline: Congenital P. falciparum transmission confirmed 13 months post-maternal infection
articles.title_original: Imported Malaria and Congenital Acquisition in Infant, Portugal, 2024.
articles.details: Case report of P. falciparum diagnosed in 3-month-old infant in Portugal via optical microscopy and molecular confirmation. Mother had malaria in Angola 13 months prior and remained asymptomatic throughout and after pregnancy, yet successfully transmitted infection to infant. Case documents congenital P. falciparum transmission with delayed presentation in non-endemic setting.

articles.headline: Nosocomial malaria transmission response letter, Spain 2024
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024 (Response).
articles.details: Response article addressing nosocomial P. falciparum transmission in Spain (2024). Specific details not available from title alone.

articles.headline: Nosocomial P. falciparum transmission documented in Spain 2024
articles.title_original: Nosocomial Transmission of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria, Spain, 2024.
articles.authors: Gómez Perales JL, Mendoza AG, Amares MTG
articles.details: Report of nosocomial malaria transmission in Spain (2024). Abstract not provided; details limited to title.

articles.headline: Imported malaria diagnosis and emergency management in children
articles.title_original: Diagnosis and Management of Imported Malaria in the Emergency Department.
articles.details: Review of diagnosis and management of imported malaria in children presenting to emergency departments in non-endemic countries. Covers key travel history features, diagnostic challenges, and acute management strategies for this growing phenomenon driven by global migration.

articles.headline: CYP2D6 poor metabolizers common in Venezuelan malaria patients
articles.title_original: CYP2D6 Genotype and Primaquine Treatment in Patients with Malaria, Venezuela.
articles.details: Study of 96 Venezuelan patients with suspected malaria found intermediate or poor CYP2D6 metabolizer phenotypes in approximately 25% of cases. Among 44 confirmed malaria patients, 9 had P. vivax recurrence. Authors recommend public health evaluation of increased primaquine dosing given high prevalence of poor metabolizers in this population.

articles.headline: P. vivax microgamete morphology presents diagnostic challenge
articles.title_original: Plasmodium vivax microgametes in a Colombian malaria case: A diagnostic challenge.
articles.details: Case highlighting diagnostic challenge presented by P. vivax microgametes in Colombian malaria case. Abstract not provided.

articles.headline: Eave screening achieves 5.5-fold reduction in malaria vector exposure
articles.title_original: A low-cost house eave screening technique complements insecticide-treated nets in reducing indoor human exposure to the bites of anopheles mosquitoes in a malaria endemic area of south forested Cameroon: results from a pilot study.
articles.details: Pilot study in forested Cameroon testing wire mesh eave screening combined with insecticide-treated nets. Baseline malaria prevalence was 38.85% with entomological inoculation rate (EIR) of 73 infected bites per person-year. Eave screening intervention reduced EIR 5.5-fold to 6.90 ib/p/y in improved houses (versus 37.70 in control), cost $23.6 per house, and was highly appreciated by community. Only pyrethroid + PBO ITNs were effective against local pyrethroid-resistant An. gambiae.

articles.headline: Non-pyrethroid IRS and BCC each reduce malaria incidence by ~22–23%
articles.title_original: Efficacy of non-pyrethroid indoor residual spraying or intensive behaviour change communication in combination with long-lasting insecticidal nets for malaria control in west Africa: a pragmatic, cluster-randomised, controlled trial.
articles.details: Pragmatic cluster-randomized trial in 39 West African villages (10,750 inhabitants, Burkina Faso and Côte d'Ivoire) over 10 months. Primary outcome was passive case detection (3,612 malaria cases from 215,000 person-months follow-up). LLIN combined with pirimiphos-methyl IRS achieved 23% incidence reduction (rate ratio 0.77, 95% CI 0.64–0.93, p=0.0073). LLIN plus intensive behavior change communication (home visits, group talks) achieved 22% reduction (RR 0.78, 95% CI 0.63–0.96, p=0.020) versus LLIN alone. No IRS-related adverse effects. Study provides first trial evidence supporting intensive BCC as effective malaria control complement.

articles.headline: Surveillance system sensitivity predicts malaria elimination success
articles.title_original: Evidence-based decision making for malaria elimination applying the Freedom From Infection statistical framework in five malaria eliminating countries: an observational study.
articles.details: Multi-country observational study (1515 facilities across 5 countries) applied the Freedom From Infection framework to assess surveillance sensitivity for P. falciparum and P. vivax across passive case detection, active case detection, and community health worker data. Findings: only ~55% of facilities achieved sufficient sensitivity for elimination confidence (P. falciparum 841/1515; P. vivax 771/1455). Strong systems had diagnostics training, treatment supply access, and reduced travel times. Combined surveillance components improved estimates over passive surveillance alone.

articles.headline: Behaviour change communication and malaria control
articles.title_original: Can behaviour change communication improve malaria control?
articles.details: No abstract provided; title suggests review of communication interventions for malaria control.

articles.headline: Robust surveillance systems essential for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: The road to malaria elimination: why a robust surveillance system is imperative.
articles.details: No abstract provided; title discusses surveillance system requirements for achieving malaria elimination.

articles.headline: Novel pfDHFR inhibitors identified against antifolate-resistant malaria
articles.title_original: Integrated computational strategies for the identification of Plasmodium falciparum dihydrofolate reductase inhibitors.
articles.details: Pharmacophore modeling and virtual screening identified 28 compounds targeting mutant P. falciparum dihydrofolate reductase (antifolate resistance mechanism). Top compound showed IC50 values of 30.822 μg/mL (3D7 strain) and 126.077 μg/mL (Dd2 strain), significantly outperforming others (p<0.05). Computational and in vitro integration approach identified alternatives to overcome antifolate resistance.

articles.headline: Stearylamine shows fast-acting activity against sensitive and resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Cationic Lipid Stearylamine Displays Fast-Acting Antimalarial Action Against In Vitro Blood Stages of Chloroquine Susceptible and Resistant Plasmodium falciparum Strains.
articles.authors: Rajendran V, Gurukkalot K, Naveen NC
articles.details: In vitro study evaluated cationic lipid stearylamine (SA) against drug-sensitive (Pf3D7) and chloroquine-resistant (PfINDO) P. falciparum. IC50 values for Pf3D7: 2.17 μg/mL (12h), 1.97 μg/mL (24h), 1.33 μg/mL (48h), 1.15 μg/mL (72h); similar potency in PfINDO strain. SA induced ROS with minimal hemolysis and showed irreversible growth arrest. Further preclinical development needed for drug delivery optimization.

articles.headline: BioMalPar: malaria research and intervention contributions over 21 years
articles.title_original: BioMalPar at 21 - a coming of age for malaria research and interventions.
articles.details: No abstract provided; title references BioMalPar coming of age in malaria research and interventions.

articles.headline: Naphthoquinone-triazole hybrids potent against atovaquone-resistant P. falciparum
articles.title_original: A dual In vitro and In silico approach to evaluate 1,4-naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant malaria.
articles.details: Dual in vitro and in silico evaluation of 10 naphthoquinone-1,2,3-triazole hybrids against atovaquone-resistant FCR3 P. falciparum. Hybrids D12 and D13 demonstrated 61- and 52-fold greater potency than atovaquone respectively, with predicted good oral bioavailability and GI absorption, no significant toxicity risk. Structure-activity relationships identified key features for binding to mutant cytochrome bc1 complex.

articles.headline: PCR diagnostic failure in mixed malaria infections: case report
articles.title_original: Polymerase chain reaction fails to detect mixed malaria infections in siblings from Ethiopia.
articles.details: Case series of two pediatric siblings returning from Ethiopia where PCR initially failed to detect P. vivax in a P. falciparum/P. vivax co-endemic region. Mixed infection (P. falciparum/P. vivax co-infection) eventually confirmed by PCR and blood smear after recurrent symptoms. Highlights diagnostic limitations of current methods in detecting mixed infections and importance of high clinical suspicion in travelers from co-endemic regions.

articles.headline: Two-tier strategy proposed for sustaining malaria control in Africa
articles.title_original: Safeguarding malaria control gains in Africa through 'species sanitation' and structural resilience.
articles.details: Strategic perspective proposing expanded species sanitation targeting ecological vulnerabilities of primary Anopheles vectors in Africa, combined with structural resilience through improved housing and environmental management. Future innovations (gene-drive mosquitoes, longer-lasting vaccines) could amplify impact. Emphasizes need for human-centered policies, domestic funding, cross-sector partnerships, and long-term planning beyond 5-year cycles to preserve control gains.

articles.headline: Travel blog malaria information shows inconsistency and commercial bias
articles.title_original: Content analysis of malaria-related health information published in online travel blogs.
articles.details: Content analysis of 100 online travel blogs found 92 contained malaria information. Ten different drugs mentioned; 5 inappropriate for P. falciparum prevention. Pre-travel consultation recommended by ~33% of blogs. Most blogs (majority) supported by commercial sponsors (travel industry or travel health products). Highlights inconsistency in preventive advice and need for travel medicine community to provide reliable, lay-accessible malaria prevention materials.

articles.headline: Intermediate forest cover increases malaria transmission risk in Amazon
articles.title_original: Intermediate forest cover and malaria risk in an Amazon deforestation frontier.
articles.details: Pilot natural experiment across 40 sites in Acre state, Brazilian Amazon found intermediate forest cover (~50% remaining) associated with higher Nyssorhynchus abundance and anopheline infection rates, increasing P. vivax and P. falciparum risk. All 16 detected human infections were asymptomatic, representing persistent transmission reservoir. Recommends integrating radical cure treatments with alternative vector control approaches and landscape-based strategies (conservation/restoration) to support elimination.

articles.headline: Spontaneous splenic rupture after malaria: case series and management
articles.title_original: Case series of spontaneous splenic rupture in patients with malaria: A rare surgical emergency in non-traumatic cases.
articles.details: Case series of 4 spontaneous splenic rupture (SSR) patients following malaria treatment; one 30-year-old from Ethiopia presented with acute left upper quadrant pain and hemoperitoneum requiring emergency splenectomy, three managed conservatively with imaging and monitoring without requiring surgery during follow-up. Mechanism: vascular congestion, reticuloendothelial hyperplasia, fragile capsule. Management depends on hemodynamic stability; splenectomy reserved for uncontrolled bleeding.

articles.headline: Malaria shows slower resistance emergence to monotherapies than HIV and TB
articles.title_original: A timeline of reckoning: Tracking the historical rise of antimicrobial resistance across HIV, TB, and malaria.
articles.details: Review comparing antimicrobial resistance emergence timelines across HIV, TB, and malaria. Malaria develops resistance to monotherapies more slowly than HIV or TB. Combination therapies reduce de novo resistance, but pre-existing drug resistance persists as a major challenge requiring continuous surveillance and innovative management approaches.

articles.headline: Novel genetic drive renders mosquitoes refractory to malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Driving a protective allele of the mosquito FREP1 gene to combat malaria.
articles.details: Study of congenic Anopheles stephensi strains carrying either susceptible FREP1L224 or protective FREP1Q224 alleles. The FREP1Q224 allele conferred robust resistance to both human and rodent malaria parasites with negligible fitness costs. A linked allelic-drive system efficiently drove Q224 into L224 populations, rendering them refractory to parasite infection, providing a novel genetic approach for malaria elimination.
articles.notable_reason: Novel genetic drive system with broad malaria elimination potential

articles.headline: Dual-dye LAMP assay achieves 100% sensitivity and specificity for P. knowlesi
articles.title_original: Establishment of a visual mixed-dye loop mediated isothermal amplification assay for detecting Plasmodium knowlesi.
articles.details: Development of mixed-dye loop-mediated isothermal amplification assay using phenol red and hydroxynaphthol blue for Plasmodium knowlesi detection. Tested on 55 clinical samples against nested PCR as reference. Achieved 100% sensitivity (95% CI: 91.9–100%) and 100% specificity (95% CI: 78.2–100%), offering a rapid, cost-effective diagnostic tool suitable for resource-limited settings.

articles.headline: Mycosporine-like amino acids screen as falcipain-2 inhibitors in silico
articles.title_original: Screening mycosporine-like amino acids (MAAs) as potential inhibitors of a malaria-associated cysteine protease falcipain-2.
articles.details: Molecular docking screening of 27 mycosporine-like amino acids as falcipain-2 inhibitors targeting Plasmodium falciparum haemoglobinase. Seven compounds showed favorable ADME properties without toxicity; shinorine and mycosporine-hydroxyglutamicol were top hits with robust protein-ligand complexes. Results suggest significant antimalarial potential through FP2 active-site inhibition.

articles.headline: RDT and microscopy show complementary performance for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Comparative evaluation of rapid diagnostic test and peripheral smear microscopy for malaria diagnosis.
articles.details: Retrospective comparison of 150 suspected malaria cases in India using rapid diagnostic tests (RDT) and peripheral blood smear (PBS) microscopy. PBS detected 30 positives (20%); RDT detected 20 (13.33%). PBS identified both P. vivax and P. falciparum; RDT missed P. falciparum but detected 2 P. vivax cases PBS missed. RDT cannot fully replace microscopy but serves as a useful complementary tool.

articles.headline: Transmission-blocking bacterium shows no fitness cost in South American vector
articles.title_original: Compatibility of Serratia ureylitica Su_YN1, Malaria Transmission-Blocking Bacterium, with the Anopheles aquasalis Vector.
articles.details: Assessment of Serratia ureilytica Su_YN1 compatibility with Anopheles aquasalis, a South American malaria vector. The bacterium was previously shown to strongly impair P. falciparum and P. berghei development. Testing found S. ureilytica Su_YN1 had no negative effects on mosquito longevity, blood feeding, fecundity, or fertility, supporting potential implementation as transmission-blocking tool in South America.

articles.headline: Congenital malaria with P. falciparum and P. vivax coinfection in neonate
articles.title_original: Congenital malaria in a neonate born in a malaria-endemic area: a case report.
articles.details: Case report of 48-hour-old neonate with parasitemia from both P. falciparum and P. vivax coinfection at parasite density of 1120 parasites/μL. Mother had been treated with artemether-lumefantrine one week prior. Neonate presented with respiratory distress and fever and was successfully treated with intravenous artesunate followed by oral artemisinin-lumefantrine with complete clinical recovery.

articles.headline: First case of concurrent severe malaria and amoebic liver abscess in pregnancy
articles.title_original: Management of a Pregnant Woman With Malaria, Stillbirth, and a 12 × 11-cm Amoebic Liver Abscess in a Burundian Hospital: A Case Report.
articles.details: Case report of pregnant woman at 22 weeks gestation with fetal death complicated by both severe Plasmodium falciparum malaria and large (12 × 11 cm) hepatic amoebic abscess in rural Burundi. This represents the first reported case of these two parasitic infections occurring together during pregnancy. Successfully managed in rural hospital setting using ultrasound guidance.

articles.headline: Artemether-lumefantrine and dolutegravir show clinically significant drug interaction
articles.title_original: Drug-drug interaction between dolutegravir and artemether-lumefantrine in HIV and malaria mono- and co-infections: a pharmacogenetic analysis from Ghana.
articles.details: Pharmacogenetic study of 261 HIV-malaria participants in Ghana receiving artemether-lumefantrine and dolutegravir. Day 1 parasitemia: 947.34 parasites/μL (malaria monoinfection) vs 5287.36 parasites/μL (coinfection), clearing to 0 by days 3–7. Coadministration decreased lumefantrine plasma concentration at day 7 (741.5 ng/mL monoinfection vs 426.0 ng/mL coinfection) and DTG levels, with reductions associated with CYP450 and UGT polymorphisms.

articles.headline: Bibliometric analysis identifies HIV-malaria research hotspots and future directions
articles.title_original: Unraveling the HIV-malaria interactions: a bibliometric analysis of global research trends and emerging insights.
articles.details: Bibliometric analysis of 4,717 articles on HIV-malaria interactions published 1995–2024 showing fluctuating upward publication trends. Key research hotspots: epidemiological studies in African populations, pathogenic mechanisms of coinfection, prevention and treatment strategies, antimalarial-antiretroviral drug interactions, and malaria vaccine responses in HIV-infected individuals. Highlights need for pharmacokinetic and pharmacodynamic research.

articles.headline: Acute kidney injury occurs in 49.2% of severe malaria, increases mortality 3.6-fold
articles.title_original: Prevalence, Associated Factors, and Prognosis of Acute Kidney Injury in Severe Malaria Among Sub-Saharan Africans.
articles.details: Multicenter retrospective study (2019–2023) of 321 severe malaria patients in Dakar hospitals; 158 (49.2%) had acute kidney injury (AKI). Associated factors: herbal medicine use (OR=1.509, p=0.045), diabetes (OR=3.375, p=0.044), respiratory distress (OR=2.758, p=0.034). AKI independently increased mortality risk (OR=3.584, p=0.004). Overall mortality 12.77% with 19 patients requiring hemodialysis.

articles.headline: Artemether faster parasite clearance than quinine in cerebral malaria meta-analysis
articles.title_original: Comparison of Artemether Versus Quinine for the Treatment of Cerebral Malaria in Children: A Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 7 randomized controlled trials comparing artemether (n=618) versus quinine (n=607) for cerebral malaria in children. Artemether significantly improved parasite clearance time (mean difference: –7.63 hours, 95% CI: –11.06, –4.21, p<0.0001). Fever clearance, coma clearance, and mortality showed no significant differences between groups.

articles.headline: P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein shows high genetic polymorphism in Congo
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of circumsporozoite protein of Plasmodium falciparum isolates in children in Brazzaville, Republic of Congo.
articles.details: Researchers analyzed the circumsporozoite protein (PfCSP) gene in 202 children with P. falciparum infection in the Republic of Congo (March-October 2021) using nanopore sequencing. They identified 30 haplotypes in the N-terminal region, 50 in the central repeat region, and 76 in the C-terminal region, with key mutations including K317E (87.4%) and N321K (83.9%). The authors conclude this baseline data on PfCSP genetic diversity is relevant to assessing vaccine efficacy concerns of recently approved RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix M vaccines.

articles.headline: Climate change accelerates malaria transmission across Africa
articles.title_original: Impact of climate change on malaria transmission in Africa: A scoping review of literature.
articles.authors: Chapoterera B, Naidoo K, Marume A
articles.details: A scoping review of 90 studies (2010-2024) examined the relationship between climate change and malaria transmission in Africa. The analysis found that temperature increases significantly accelerate mosquito vector and parasite development, while changes in rainfall patterns affect mosquito breeding sites and malaria incidence. The authors recommend integrating ecological data into malaria control strategies to maintain intervention effectiveness as climate conditions shift.

articles.headline: Garlic extract shows potent insecticidal activity against Anopheles vectors
articles.title_original: Insecticidal Activities of Plants Extract Against Malaria Vectors in Hadiya Zone, Ethiopia.
articles.details: Researchers evaluated ethanol extracts of neem (Azadirachta indica) and garlic (Allium sativum) against malaria vectors in Ethiopia. Garlic extract demonstrated 90.66% mortality of larvae at 250 ppm concentration with an LC50 of 39 ppm, and showed significant effects against Anopheles gambiae s.l. (p=0.008687) and Anopheles pharoensis (p=0.02655). The authors conclude garlic extract was more effective than neem in reducing malaria vector populations, though further studies at pupal and adult stages are needed.

articles.headline: Plasmodium chromatin states regulate periodic gene expression during blood stage
articles.title_original: Chromatin state dynamics during the Plasmodium falciparum intraerythrocytic development cycle.
articles.authors: Brown AS Jr, Llinás M, Mahony S
articles.details: Researchers analyzed chromatin accessibility, histone modifications, and transcription factor binding across the 48-hour intraerythrocytic developmental cycle of P. falciparum. They found that periodic gene expression is accompanied by dynamic shifts in chromatin states, with some states remaining stable while others link to gene activation or repression. The authors characterized chromatin state preferences of different transcription factors, providing a global overview of chromatin dynamics coordinating asexual blood stage development.

articles.headline: Insecticide-treated nets reduce malaria infection 86% in pregnant Nigerian women
articles.title_original: Do women attending antenatal clinics who use only intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) have higher Plasmodium falciparum prevalence compared to those who used both IPTp and slept under insecticide-treated nets?
articles.details: In a cross-sectional study of 143 pregnant women in Nigeria, researchers found P. falciparum infection in 65 (45%) participants. Prevalence was 61.2% among those using only intermittent preventive treatment (IPTp) versus 17.0% among those using IPTp plus insecticide-treated nets (ITNs). Logistic regression adjusted for gestational week, income, and education showed that IPTp+ITN use conferred 86% reduced odds of infection, with the protective effect largely driven by ITN use.

articles.headline: PfMYST histone acetyltransferase controls artemisinin ring-stage survival in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Epigenetically conferred ring-stage survival in Plasmodium falciparum against artemisinin treatment.
articles.details: Researchers systematically evaluated histone acetyltransferases in 73 field isolates and identified PfMYST as the primary epigenetic modulator of ring-stage survival (RS), the phenotype associated with artemisinin partial resistance. Through knockdown experiments, single-cell RNA sequencing, ChIP-seq, and CRISPR manipulation, they identified and functionally validated PfMYST-targeted genes. Multi-omics analysis revealed significant interplay between PfMYST and PfK13 mechanisms, and PfMYST modulation extends to other antimalarials including amodiaquine, pyrimethamine, chloroquine, and pyronaridine.
articles.notable_reason: Novel epigenetic mechanism of artemisinin resistance; potential drug target

articles.headline: Novel PI4K inhibitor shows efficacy against all P. falciparum life stages in mice
articles.title_original: Novel Inhibitors of Plasmodium Phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase IIIβ with Low Propensity for Resistance: Life Cycle Stage Activity and In Vivo Efficacy in a Humanized Mouse Malaria Infection Model.
articles.details: Researchers developed a novel antimalarial chemotype based on the ATM kinase inhibitor AZD0156 that inhibits phosphatidylinositol 4-kinase IIIβ (PI4K). The lead compound (18) demonstrated activity against all life stages of P. falciparum and was efficacious in a humanized mouse model of malaria with an ED90 of 4.6 mg/kg. The compounds showed lower propensity for resistance and toxicity compared to previous PI4K inhibitors.

articles.headline: Plasmodium ARK1 kinase controls spindle formation and parasite transmission
articles.title_original: Plasmodium ARK1 regulates spindle formation during atypical mitosis and forms a divergent chromosomal passenger complex.
articles.details: Researchers identified Aurora-related kinase 1 (ARK1) as a key component of spindle formation and kinetochore dynamics in P. falciparum. Conditional ARK1 depletion disrupted spindle biogenesis, kinetochore segregation, and nuclear/cell division in both schizogony and gametogony stages, and impaired parasite transmission. Interactome analysis revealed ARK1 as the catalytic core of a divergent chromosomal passenger complex containing two inner centromere proteins but lacking canonical Survivin and Borealin, representing unique mitotic machinery in Plasmodium.
articles.notable_reason: Novel divergent mitotic machinery; multistage therapeutic target

articles.headline: Salacia debilis compounds inhibit Plasmodium enoyl-ACP reductase via conserved binding
articles.title_original: Computational elucidation of Plasmodium falciparum enoyl-ACP reductase inhibition by Salacia debilis compounds: adaptive ligand binding drives antimalarial activity.
articles.details: Researchers screened compounds derived from Salacia debilis and identified three (SD03, SD04, SD05) that inhibit P. falciparum enoyl-acyl carrier protein reductase (PfENR), an enzyme in the parasite's type II fatty acid biosynthesis pathway absent in humans. Molecular docking and 500-nanosecond molecular dynamics simulations showed binding affinities of -8.50 to -8.90 kcal/mol and stable conformation-specific complexes. The compounds engage key active site residues similar to triclosan, with SD04 identified as the lead candidate based on favorable pharmacokinetic predictions.

articles.headline: Widowhood and early cohabitation drive HIV infection in Mozambican youth
articles.title_original: Factors associated with HIV infection among adolescents and young people in Mozambique: sub-analysis of immunization, malaria and HIV/AIDS indicator survey in Mozambique-IMASIDA 2015.
articles.details: Analysis of 4,247 youth aged 15-24 years from the 2015 Immunization, Malaria and HIV/AIDS Indicators Survey in Mozambique identified risk factors for HIV infection (overall prevalence 6.9%, 9.8% in females). Key factors included widowhood (AOR=52.69 in males, AOR=15.59 in females), ages 20-24 (AOR=2.06 in females), and cohabitation before age 18 (AOR=1.71). Higher education was protective (AOR=0.07), and the authors emphasize the need for interventions addressing gender inequalities and expanding educational opportunities.

articles.headline: Dietary L-DOPA enhances Anopheles immunity but shortens lifespan
articles.title_original: Dietary L-3,4-dihydroxyphenylalanine (L-DOPA) augments cuticular melanization in Anopheles mosquitos reducing their lifespan and malaria burden.
articles.details: Researchers investigated the effects of dietary L-DOPA (a tyrosine derivative abundant in mosquito habitats) on Anopheles mosquito physiology and immunity. L-DOPA was incorporated into mosquito melanin via a non-canonical pathway, resulting in enhanced immunity to P. falciparum and C. neoformans infections, increased pigmentation, altered thermoregulation, and reduced lifespan. The authors suggest L-DOPA has potential as an environmentally friendly vector control agent, warranting further research and field studies.
articles.notable_reason: Novel dietary intervention reduces vector competence and lifespan

articles.headline: Anopheles albitarsis dominates parasite-free Paraguay despite suitable transmission habitats
articles.title_original: Unravelling Anopheles Dynamics in a Malaria-Free Paraguay: Species Distributions, Bioclimatic Niches, and Implications for Resurgence Risks.
articles.details: In 2016-2017 surveillance of malaria-free Paraguay, researchers found Anopheles albitarsis (59.4%), A. strodei (21.5%), and other species, all negative for Plasmodium spp. Bioclimatic modeling identified areas of environmental suitability for Anopheles across multiple ecoregions, with precipitation patterns (80.4% contribution from wettest month) being the most influential climatic variables. The main malaria vector A. darlingi was absent, though species richness indices suggest cryptic or sibling species may be present, warranting continued surveillance.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence rising in Madagascar (4.5-12.4%)
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of Malaria in Northern Madagascar as Exemplified by the Mampikony District.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 782 residents in Mampikony District during 2023-2024 using rapid diagnostic tests (mRDTs) and RT-PCR.
