Malaria prevalence increased from 4.5% (mRDTs) and 8.2% (RT-PCR) in 2023 to 8.4% (mRDTs) and 12.4% (RT-PCR) in 2024. Cases predominantly caused by P. falciparum, with P. vivax and mixed infections also detected. Authors recommend more sensitive diagnostic methods and updated treatment strategies due to rising asymptomatic carriage.

articles.headline: Simplified malaria control model preserves full model dynamics
articles.title_original: Model reduction and analysis: A case study of a malaria control model.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study employing a reduction technique on a comprehensive malaria control model. The reduced system was validated against the full model using Bayesian MCMC and sensitivity analyses. The simplified model captures essential transmission dynamics while providing improved computational efficiency and flexibility for evaluating malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Vaccine-evolved malaria parasites alter virulence beyond target antigens
articles.title_original: Understanding the traits underlying vaccine-driven virulence evolution in malaria parasites.
articles.authors: Jeong Y, Kamiya T, Mideo N
articles.details: Experimental study in mice using hierarchical Bayesian modeling of within-host infection dynamics. Parasites evolved in vaccinated mice showed lower targeted immune killing and reduced anemia-driven erythropoiesis compared to control parasites, but no genetic changes at vaccine target sites (AMA-1). Results suggest virulence evolution involves parasite modulation of host responses, not just escape mutations at vaccine targets.

articles.headline: Neutrophil extracellular traps drive cerebral malaria via TLR9 pathway
articles.title_original: TLR9/NF-κB-mediated dendritic cell activation by neutrophil extracellular traps drives pathogenesis in experimental cerebral malaria.
articles.details: In a murine cerebral malaria model using Plasmodium berghei ANKA, neutrophil depletion protected mice from neuropathology and reduced brain infiltration of CD8+ T cells. Neutrophil extracellular traps (NETs) activated dendritic cells via TLR9/NF-κB signaling, promoting pathogenic T cell responses. Sivelestat, a NET inhibitor, impeded CM onset and progression, suggesting a potential therapeutic target.

articles.headline: Yemen's malaria crisis and implications for GCC region
articles.title_original: Yemen's Malaria Crisis and its Implications for the GCC (Gulf Cooperation Council) Countries.
articles.details: No abstract provided; unable to extract specific findings or quantitative data from title alone.

articles.headline: Broad anti-PfEMP1 antibodies target conserved EPCR-binding epitope
articles.title_original: Identification of broadly inhibitory anti-PfEMP1 antibodies by mass spectrometry sequencing of plasma IgG from a malaria-exposed child.
articles.journal: Proc Natl Acad Sci U S A
articles.details: Mass spectrometry sequencing of plasma IgG from a 9-year-old child in a malaria-endemic region identified a clonal family of broadly reactive monoclonal antibodies against P. falciparum PfEMP1 CIDRα1 domains. These antibodies potently inhibited EPCR binding across diverse CIDRα1 variants and infected erythrocyte sequestration. Structural analysis revealed a conserved epitope overlapping EPCR binding site, demonstrating that broadly inhibitory antibodies against severe malaria commonly target similar epitopes in naturally immune individuals.
articles.notable_reason: Identifies conserved epitope for severe malaria immunity; vaccine target

articles.headline: Three microRNAs predict cerebral malaria with high accuracy
articles.title_original: Potential of microRNAs as diagnostic markers for distinguishing malaria severity in samples from an Indian cohort.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study measuring six plasma microRNAs in 301 patients with varying malaria presentations (cerebral malaria n=43, severe non-cerebral n=50, uncomplicated n=79, asymptomatic n=80, non-malarial fever n=69). Three microRNAs (hsa-miR-21-5p, hsa-miR-150-5p, hsa-miR-3158-3p) correlated with severe malaria and cerebral malaria (p<0.0005). A machine learning model achieved 83% sensitivity, 100% specificity, and 92% balanced accuracy for cerebral malaria prediction on admission, suggesting a non-invasive diagnostic approach.

articles.headline: Triple artemisinin-combination therapies most effective against drug resistance
articles.title_original: Effects of recombination on multi-drug resistance evolution in Plasmodium falciparum malaria.
articles.details: Individual-based transmission model with 25 drug-resistance loci simulating multi-clonal infections and mosquito feeding patterns. Over 15 years, triple artemisinin-combination therapies (ACTs) showed the largest reductions in treatment failures and longest delays until artemisinin resistance reached critical 1% threshold (compared to multiple first-line therapies and cycling strategies). Eighty to 97% of multi-drug resistant recombinants arose from multi-clonal infections within single hosts.

articles.headline: Airport/seaport malaria in Europe from 1969-2022: 115 cases documented
articles.title_original: Airport/seaport and autochthonous malaria in Europe from 1969 to 2022: A systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review following PRISMA guidelines of 68 papers documenting 115 malaria cases in Europe: 68 airport/seaport malaria cases and 47 autochthonous cases. France, Germany, Italy, and Spain reported both types; Belgium, Luxembourg, Switzerland, and UK reported imported cases only; Greece, Malta, and Netherlands reported only autochthonous cases. Case fatality rates were 13.24% for imported malaria and 2.13% for autochthonous transmission. Rising temperatures and increased human migration may increase reemergence risk.

articles.headline: High malaria prevalence in pregnant women in Somalia (59.8%)
articles.title_original: The Prevalence of Malaria Among Pregnant Women at a Tertiary Care Hospital in Somalia: A Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 398 pregnant women at a tertiary hospital in Mogadishu (November 2022–January 2023) using Giemsa blood smear microscopy. Malaria prevalence was 59.8% (238/398), predominantly P. falciparum (91.6% of cases). Second and third trimester pregnancy and indoor residual spraying were associated with significantly reduced malaria odds (p=0.044 and p<0.001 respectively). Authors recommend routine antenatal malaria screening and targeted prevention campaigns.

articles.headline: Time-lapse imaging reveals exocytosis timing in P. falciparum egress
articles.title_original: Spatiotemporal dynamics of signal dependent exocytosis and parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture during Plasmodium falciparum egress.
articles.details: Live-cell time-lapse imaging of P. falciparum parasites using superecliptic pHlourin (SEP) to track exocytosis of egress-specific vesicles. Signal-dependent exocytosis was triggered as early as 3 hours before merozoite egress, with parasitophorous vacuolar membrane rupture occurring at a single site that rapidly expands. Conditional knockdown of P. falciparum endoplasmic reticulum calcium-binding protein (PfERC) inhibited signal-dependent exocytosis, demonstrating its requirement for the egress pathway.

articles.headline: Review: PEXEL and non-PEXEL protein export in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: An overview of the interconnectedness of the PEXEL and non-PEXEL mediated protein export in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Review article summarizing the mechanisms of protein export in P. falciparum, including PEXEL-mediated export (proteins with conserved pentameric PEXEL motif) and PEXEL-negative exported proteins (PNEPs) that follow similar trafficking routes despite lacking the motif. Discusses structural diversity of PNEPs, their functions in parasite biology, and outstanding questions regarding protein export mechanisms in P. falciparum and related apicomplexans.

articles.headline: P. falciparum-dengue coinfections in febrile children, western Kenya
articles.title_original: Correlation and epidemiologic distribution of emerging coinfections of Plasmodium falciparum and dengue virus among febrile children in malaria-endemic zones in western Kenya.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study of 380 febrile children screened from 1,004 total. P. falciparum prevalence 21.4%, dengue 8.9%, coinfections 7.5%. Distribution differed significantly by location (Busia: 31% P. falciparum-only; Kisumu: 16.6% dengue-only; χ²=19.45, p<0.001). Authors conclude active dengue spread creates coinfection risk in this malaria-endemic region.

articles.headline: Low malaria knowledge in rural Ethiopia despite prevention awareness
articles.title_original: Malaria signs, symptoms, prevention knowledge and its associated factors among rural Ethiopians.
articles.journal: Afr J Prim Health Care Fam Med
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 634 household members in three rural districts. Only 44.2% demonstrated good understanding of malaria signs, symptoms, and prevention. Education, religion, marital status, family size associated with better knowledge. Authors conclude malaria knowledge is low and knowledge-transfer strategies are needed to accelerate elimination.

articles.headline: Persistent socioeconomic inequalities in malaria prevention uptake, Ghana
articles.title_original: Between-and within-socioeconomic groups temporal inequality in the uptake of malaria prevention strategies among pregnant women and under-five children in Ghana (2003-2022).
articles.details: Analysis of 2003-2022 Ghana DHS data. ITN use increased from 58.1% to 62.9% in under-5 children; IPTp rose from 0.85% to 60%. ITN use remained pro-poor but increasingly concentrated among poorest (ENCI -0.04 to -0.32 for pregnant women); IPTp shifted to pro-rich distribution. Within-SES inequality dominated (85% of ITN inequality for pregnant women). Authors conclude substantial inequalities persist despite overall improvements.

articles.headline: Diabetes and metabolic syndrome increase severe P. falciparum malaria
articles.title_original: Diabetes and metabolic syndrome in adults with malaria and associations with severe disease: results from two tertiary hospitals in Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 289 adults with P. falciparum malaria; 120 (41.6%) had severe disease. Diabetes present in 21.7% severe vs 6.0% non-severe (p<0.001); metabolic syndrome in 32.5% severe vs 16.3% non-severe (p=0.001). Diabetes (aOR 3.24, 95% CI 1.38-7.62) and metabolic syndrome (aOR 3.18, 95% CI 1.60-6.37) independently associated with severe malaria. Authors recommend screening for diabetes in severe malaria cases.

articles.headline: Hematological abnormalities more severe in HIV-malaria coinfection
articles.title_original: Hematological Profiles of Adults Coinfected With HIV and Malaria Receiving Highly Active Antiretroviral Therapy at Bonga Gebretsadik Shawo General Hospital, Southwest Ethiopia: A Comparative Cross-Sectional Study.
articles.authors: Demango F, Tadasa E, Kiya GT
articles.details: Hospital-based comparative study of 196 HIV-infected adults (98 HIV-only, 98 HIV-malaria coinfected) on HAART. Significant differences in RBC, hemoglobin, and hematocrit between groups (p<0.05). Anemia 44.9% overall (61.2% coinfected), leukopenia 39.3% (43.88% coinfected), thrombocytopenia 25.5% (30.6% coinfected; p<0.001 for anemia difference). Negative correlations between viral load and hematological parameters. Authors conclude coinfection causes greater hematological compromise.

articles.headline: Phase-based classification improves P. falciparum-infected RBC detection
articles.title_original: Enhanced detection of malaria infected red blood cells through phase driven classification.
articles.details: Digital holographic microscopy using phase-based features to classify P. falciparum-infected RBCs. Phase-based detection rate 61% versus 48% with feature-specific methods on same samples, with high specificity (98-100%). Underestimates versus Giemsa staining (61% vs 90%) but offers real-time, label-free diagnosis. Authors propose as promising tool for rapid, automated malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Low LLIN use and poor care practices in Dominican Republic malaria foci
articles.title_original: Insights from LLIN post-distribution monitoring surveys in the malaria transmission foci of the Dominican Republic: implications for quantification and distribution strategies.
articles.details: Cross-sectional household survey 4-6 months and 1 year post-LLIN distribution. Coverage with available LLINs 64% (Azua) and 63% (San Juan); overall use 32.3% (Azua) and 50.5% (San Juan). Most LLINs physically serviceable (96.4% Azua, 88.9% San Juan) but washed aggressively with frequent washing (65-86% used aggressive products, 52-73% washed >20 times in 3 years). Authors conclude poor washing/drying practices and low use require urgent behavioral interventions.

articles.headline: Aspartyl protease inhibition targets asexual stages and gametocytes
articles.title_original: Aspartyl protease inhibition interferes with Plasmodium falciparum asexual blood-stage and early gametocyte development.
articles.details: In vitro study of pepstatin A (aspartyl protease inhibitor) on P. falciparum developmental stages. Pepstatin A at 100 µM inhibited asexual stage 47% and early-stage gametocytes 73%, with morphological defects (chromatin condensation, vacuolization, hemozoin clumping). No effect on late-stage gametocytes or gametes. Authors conclude aspartyl protease inhibition shows potential for reducing disease severity and transmission, but further development needed.

articles.headline: Irrigated urban farming drives malaria transmission in Accra
articles.title_original: Urban malaria in Accra, Ghana: transmission risk and influence of irrigated vegetable farms.
articles.details: Entomological survey collecting 41,747 mosquitoes across urban zones. Anopheles 52.54% of host-seeking collections (95.49% caught by HLC). Irrigated farming sites had highest An. gambiae abundance (48.63% of total), followed by peri-urban (18.06%). Indoor sporozoite infectivity 4.56% versus 3.78% outdoor (χ²=6.78, p=0.009); Tuba irrigated site highest at 6.41%. Authors conclude irrigated agriculture drives urban malaria transmission with high indoor infectivity.

articles.headline: Community case management of malaria: facilitators and persistent barriers
articles.title_original: Facilitators and barriers of Community Case management of Malaria implementation in Homabay, Busia and Kakamega Counties, Kenya.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study of 528 households plus 4 FGDs and 20 KIIs in three Kenyan counties. 72% received community case management (Busia 75%, Homabay 72%, Kakamega 71%); 85% satisfied. Facilitators: training, sensitization, commodity availability, referral systems. Barriers: myths about nets, stockouts of mRDTs and antimalarials, inaccessible roads. Authors conclude barriers remain unaddressed; sustained commodities and CHV support essential for sustainability.

articles.headline: Risk factors for malaria parasitemia in pregnant women, Cameroon
articles.title_original: Epidemiology of malaria in pregnant women attending antenatal consultation in Dschang, West Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional hospital study of 314 pregnant women; 46 (14.6%) positive for P. falciparum. Bushes around houses increased infection risk (OR 2.40, p=0.03); ceiling (OR 0.20, p<0.01), IPTp (OR 0.23, p<0.01), and window screens (OR 0.14, p=0.01) protective. Geometric mean parasite density highest in second trimester (2190/µL, p=0.016) and >3 gravidities (1022/µL, p=0.009). Authors conclude prevention effectiveness varies by housing quality and pregnancy factors.

articles.headline: Indigofera oblongifolia extract reduces lung inflammation in P. chabaudi
articles.title_original: Morphometric analysis and immunobiological investigation of Indigofera oblongifolia on the infected lung with Plasmodium chabaudi.
articles.details: Mouse model study (50 C57BL/6 females) comparing Indigofera oblongifolia leaf extract with silver nanoparticles and chloroquine 10 mg/kg against P. chabaudi lung pathology. I. oblongifolia significantly restored normal lung architecture and reduced alveolar congestion, edema, and necrosis compared to chloroquine. Immunohistochemistry showed reduced TNF-α and IL-6 expression with I. oblongifolia. Authors conclude plant extract has therapeutic potential superior to chloroquine for malaria-related lung pathology.

articles.headline: Drone-collected eDNA enables real-time Plasmodium detection in Amazon
articles.title_original: Real-time malaria detection in the Amazon rainforest via drone-collected eDNA and portable qPCR.
articles.details: A proof-of-concept study coupled drone-based environmental DNA collection with portable qPCR to detect Plasmodium in the Amazon canopy during a 24-hour field exercise. Of 12 samples (10 canopy swabs, 2 blanks), one canopy swab amplified in duplicate (Ct = 28.7 and 29.23) with confirmed howler monkey presence. The end-to-end workflow averaged 1.5 hours per assay without cold-chain requirements, demonstrating feasibility for landscape-level pathogen surveillance in remote settings.

articles.headline: Occupational risk factors identified for malaria in Zanzibar
articles.title_original: Occupations at high risk for malaria in Zanzibar: a case-control study, may-august 2023.
articles.details: A case-control study (197 cases, 557 controls, May-August 2023) identified occupational and behavioral risk factors for malaria in Zanzibar. In urban districts, night watchmen/police had highest risk (OR 5.3, p<0.001), followed by construction workers (OR 3.0, p=0.007) and farmers (OR 1.6, p=0.01). Night-time activities and outdoor meals increased risk in both urban and rural settings. Tailored occupational interventions could reduce malaria in urban areas.

articles.headline: African Plasmodium parasites transmitted by invasive Anopheles stephensi
articles.title_original: Genetic surveillance of Plasmodium-Anopheles compatibility markers during Anopheles stephensi associated malaria outbreak.
articles.details: During a 2022 malaria outbreak in Dire Dawa, Ethiopia, genetic analysis of 160 An. stephensi mosquitoes revealed 6.21% infected with P. falciparum and 4.37% with P. vivax. SNP analysis demonstrated P. falciparum strains were of African origin. Molecular analysis of P47 and P47Rec sequences identified a single amino acid change in An. stephensi P47Rec that may serve as a molecular marker for outbreak propensity.

articles.headline: Temperature and nutrition stress alter vectorial competence in Anopheles stephensi
articles.title_original: Effects of temperature and nutrition stress on Anopheles stephensi, an Indian urban malaria vector.
articles.details: Laboratory study exposed An. stephensi to low-temperature (18±1°C), high-temperature (35.5±1°C), or low-nutrition conditions for 8 consecutive generations. High and low nutrition stress decreased fecundity, hatchability, longevity, and developmental time. P. falciparum oocyst infection rates increased 1.46-fold under heat stress, 1.28-fold under cold, and 1.15-fold under nutrition stress compared to control. These findings suggest climate-driven changes may alter mosquito vectorial competence.

articles.headline: Data quality gaps in Uganda malaria surveillance systems
articles.title_original: Concordance of data on key malaria indicators between DHIS2 and source documents, and influencing factors at public primary health facilities in eastern Uganda: a mixed methods study.
articles.details: A mixed-methods study in 12 Ugandan health facilities found low concordance between DHIS2 and source documents on malaria indicators: suspected cases 36.7%, tested cases 53.6%, test positivity rate 56.8%—all below WHO's ≥80% standard. Presence of HMIS personnel (aPR 1.41) and timely reporting (aPR 1.15) improved concordance, while RDT stockouts (aPR 0.55) reduced it. Trained data staff and electronic data capture recommended to improve surveillance quality.

articles.headline: Age-structured malaria transmission model incorporating temperature dynamics
articles.title_original: Age-structured malaria model with temperature-dependent dynamics and optimal control analysis within a partial differential equation framework.
articles.details: A mathematical model for malaria transmission using partial differential equations incorporates temperature dependence and age structure with optimal control strategies. Empirical validation using 6 years of prevalence data from Jimma zone, Ethiopia, demonstrated R² = 0.68 and adjusted R² = 0.63. The model showed superior predictive accuracy compared to existing age-structured models and captured temperature-dependent transmission trends.

articles.headline: Climate and environmental factors drive malaria spatial heterogeneity in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Modelling the impact of climatic and environmental variables on malaria incidence in Tanzania: Implications for achieving the WHO's 2030 Targets.
articles.details: Analysis of five surveys (~2,000 participants each) from 2000-2020 documented malaria incidence decline from 0.347 (95% CI 0.336-0.357) to 0.118 (0.114-0.122) with ITN coverage increase from 0.037 to 0.496. Higher temperatures increased incidence (OR 1.06-1.13), enhanced vegetation increased incidence (OR 5.28-6.22), and aridity increased incidence (OR 1.07-1.11). Spatial clustering remained significant, indicating fine-scale region-specific interventions are needed.

articles.headline: Imported Plasmodium ovale misidentified as P. falciparum in Colombia
articles.title_original: Imported plasmodium ovale malaria from Côte d'Ivoire: a case report.
articles.authors: Plata-Puyana JD, Vanegas Beltrán JK, Martínez-Ávila MC
articles.details: A 29-year-old male returning to Colombia after 3 months in Côte d'Ivoire presented with fever, chills, and myalgia. Initial microscopy misidentified the parasite as P. falciparum; PCR confirmed P. ovale infection. After initial artemether-lumefantrine, the patient was switched to chloroquine followed by primaquine (15 mg daily × 28 days) for radical cure and tolerated treatment without adverse effects. Case highlights diagnostic challenges with imported malaria.

articles.headline: Malaria patients report complicated post-discharge symptoms in South Africa
articles.title_original: Post-discharge experiences of Malaria patients in a Vhembe district village, South Africa.
articles.authors: Kwinda W, Luhalima TR, Mudzweda AD
articles.details: A qualitative study at Mhinga village, South Africa, using semi-structured interviews explored post-discharge experiences of malaria patients. Participants reported experiencing complicated symptoms even after treatment at both clinic and hospital. Two main themes emerged describing post-discharge experiences from clinic and hospital settings, indicating challenges in post-discharge management. Enhanced surveillance, vector control, and public awareness campaigns recommended.

articles.headline: Minor Plasmodium species rebound after IRS discontinuation in Ghana
articles.title_original: Longitudinal analysis of the prevalence of minor Plasmodium spp. in the reservoir of asymptomatic infections through sequential interventions in Northern Sahelian Ghana.
articles.details: An interrupted time-series study (2012-2022, ~2,000 participants per survey) in Bongo District, Ghana, tracked P. malariae and P. ovale using molecular diagnostics. After indoor residual spraying, prevalence declined: P. malariae from 13.7% to 1.4% and P. ovale from 5.7% to 0.4%. Upon IRS discontinuation and seasonal malaria chemoprevention (SMC) introduction, both rebounded 2-10 fold, though remained controlled with sustained SMC (P. malariae 5.8%, P. ovale 1.3% by 2022).

articles.headline: Invasive Anopheles stephensi threatens malaria elimination in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Challenges to Malaria Elimination in Ethiopia by 2030: A Review.
articles.details: A review identifies multiple barriers to Ethiopia's 2030 malaria elimination targets. Over 42 Anopheles species circulate, with invasive An. stephensi (resistant to all four insecticide classes) joining primary vector An. arabiensis. Both P. falciparum and P. vivax have developed resistance to multiple antimalarials. Recent civil war caused alarming malaria spikes, with disease spreading rapidly in Amhara and Oromo regions.

articles.headline: Anopheles darlingi shows bimodal biting pattern in Peruvian Amazon
articles.title_original: Experimental hut resting and entrance behaviour of Anopheles darlingi from Zungarococha, a malaria endemic community in Loreto, Northern Peruvian Amazon.
articles.details: A behavioral study in Zungarococha, Peru, compared resting and entry behaviors of wild and colonized An. darlingi using experimental huts and human landing collections. Outdoor biting showed bimodal pattern with peaks at 1800h and 2200h, with densities correlated to Amazon River levels. Both colonized and wild mosquitoes preferred lower resting sites (door ~30%, window 14%, roof 12%).

articles.headline: Plasmodium falciparum piperaquine resistance marker at 16% globally
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum plasmepsin2/3 gene duplication in Africa and Asia: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 34 studies comprising 4,005 P. falciparum isolates from Africa and Asia found pfpm2/3 gene amplification (piperaquine resistance marker) in 16.0% (95% CI 8.0-26.0%) of isolates overall. Prevalence was significantly higher in Asia (25.0%, 95% CI 9.0-45.0%) than Africa (8.0%, 95% CI 2.0-15.0%), and increased from 11% before 2016 to 19% in 2016-2020. Authors conclude substantial pfpm2/3 amplification requires monitoring to prevent total ACT resistance.

articles.headline: Malaria burden in Sindh Pakistan: 1.28 million cases yearly
articles.title_original: Subnational malaria burden in Sindh, Pakistan: over a decade of evidence for tailored strategies.
articles.details: Analysis of 13 years (2012-2024) of data from 1088 primary healthcare facilities across 23 districts in Sindh revealed suspected malaria incidence of 92 per 1000 people annually, with 16.7 million total cases (1.28 million annually) and 12.3% positivity rate. Six districts accounted for over 53% of burden. Since 2022 floods, malaria incidence doubled, persisting through 2024. Study highlights substantial underestimation by global reports and need for district-targeted control.

articles.headline: In silico identification of antimalarial compounds targeting invasion proteins
articles.title_original: Targeting invasion-associated proteins PfSUB2 and PfTRAMP in Plasmodium falciparum: identification of potential inhibitors via molecular docking.
articles.details: Molecular docking of 519 compounds against PfSUB2 (Plasmodium falciparum subtilisin-like protease 2) and PfTRAMP (thrombospondin-related apical merozoite protein), both essential for asexual blood-stage growth and RBC invasion, identified three compounds with binding affinities superior to standard inhibitor PMSF. Compound 155,204,487 showed dual targeting potential for both proteins. Study recommends these compounds for further antimalarial drug development.

articles.headline: Fractional calculus models reveal malaria transmission complexity
articles.title_original: Complex travelling wave solutions of fractional nonlinear coupled malaria model: bifurcation, chaos, and multistability.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study using a fractional nonlinear coupled malaria model with beta derivative analyzed disease transmission dynamics through traveling wave solutions, including kink, anti-kink, and dark solitons. Analysis revealed bifurcation, quasi-periodic and chaotic patterns, multistability, and sensitivity, emphasizing the elaborate nature of malaria transmission. Study provides theoretical framework for developing more accurate predictive models with applications in biomedical sciences.

articles.headline: High P. vivax diversity persists in Peruvian Amazon community
articles.title_original: High Prevalence, Genetic Diversity and Temporal Differentiation of Plasmodium vivax in a Remote Hard-to-Reach Community from the Peruvian Amazon Region.
articles.details: Longitudinal surveillance in Santa Emilia, Peru (2013-2016) genotyped 221 of 3,434 P. vivax samples using 16 microsatellites, plus 139 samples from 2013. Parasite prevalence was 56% by qPCR with 44% submicroscopic infections. High genetic diversity and population differentiation persisted across years; 40-44% of 2013-2015 infections were polyclonal versus 29% in 2016. Four genetically differentiated populations detected with increasing admixture, suggesting complex transmission dynamics challenging Peru's elimination plan.

articles.headline: Parasite importation hinders malaria elimination on Bioko Island
articles.title_original: Regular Plasmodium falciparum importation onto Bioko Island, Equatorial Guinea, hampers malaria elimination from the island.
articles.details: Whole-genome sequencing of P. falciparum from 2019 Malaria Indicator Survey on Bioko Island showed parasites had higher genetic diversity than expected for an area of moderate transmission, consistent with substantial imported infections. Parasite flow appeared more pronounced with Gabon than other continental neighbors. Study concludes elimination requires interrupting local transmission while accounting for importation through targeted traveler interventions and cross-border collaboration.

articles.headline: Novel atypical Arp2/3 complex discovered in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: Discovery of an Atypical Arp2/3 Complex in Malaria Parasites Sheds New Light on Nuclear Actin.
articles.authors: Hentzschel F, Frischknecht F, Marti M
articles.details: Study identified an atypical Arp2/3 complex in Plasmodium falciparum (previously thought absent in apicomplexans) consisting of five divergent subunits plus kinetochore-associated factor. Complex nucleates actin at the mitotic spindle during male gametogenesis, ensuring proper kinetochore-spindle attachment. Disruption causes defective DNA segregation and developmental arrest in the mosquito vector. Findings reveal unexpected diversity in Arp2/3 composition and specialized adaptations in malaria parasites.
articles.notable_reason: First atypical Arp2/3 complex identified; essential for transmission

articles.headline: Identity-by-descent detection benchmarked for P. falciparum surveillance
articles.title_original: Benchmarking and optimization of methods for the detection of identity-by-descent in high-recombining Plasmodium falciparum genomes.
articles.authors: Guo B, Takala-Harrison S, O'Connor TD
articles.details: Comprehensive benchmarking of five IBD (identity-by-descent) callers using population genetic simulations and MalariaGEN Pf7 empirical data revealed low marker density significantly compromises IBD detection accuracy in high-recombination genomes like P. falciparum, particularly for shorter IBD segments. Parameter optimization partially mitigated false negatives and improved selection signal capture and population structure inference. hmmIBD provided least-biased effective population size estimates, making it recommended for Plasmodium genomic surveillance.

articles.headline: COVID-19 disruptions drove malaria resurgence in Gabon
articles.title_original: Malaria amidst the COVID-19 pandemic in Gabon: an application of autoregressive integrated moving average (ARIMA) models within an interrupted time series (ITS) framework to hospital-based data.
articles.details: Interrupted time-series analysis of hospital malaria diagnosis rates (2018-early 2023) using ARIMA modeling showed initial drop of 47.32% at pandemic onset, followed by gradual increase above pre-pandemic peak (linear trend +7.32, p=0.001). Resurgence was attributed to disruptions in malaria control activities and waning efficacy of pre-pandemic interventions. Maternal malaria showed no meaningful increase, providing reassurance that pregnancy-specific control remained unchanged.

articles.headline: Plasmodium detected in wild coatis from Peruvian Amazon
articles.title_original: Molecular Screening of Plasmodium spp. in Free-Living Ring-Tailed Coatis (Nasua nasua) and Nine-Banded Armadillos (Dasypus novemcinctus) in the Peruvian Amazon.
articles.details: Screening of 44 ring-tailed coatis and 66 nine-banded armadillos from an Indigenous community in the Peruvian Amazon detected Plasmodium spp. DNA in 2 coatis (4.7%) by nested PCR targeting mitochondrial cytb. Sequencing identified one P. vivax/simium lineage and one P. malariae/brasilianum; P. malariae/brasilianum was confirmed by independent cox3-based PCR. No infections detected in armadillos. Findings suggest coatis may serve as incidental hosts for diverse Plasmodium species.

articles.headline: Urban malaria prevalence in sub-Saharan Africa: 23%
articles.title_original: Urban malaria and population mobility in sub-Saharan Africa: systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 39 studies found pooled prevalence of urban malaria in sub-Saharan Africa was 23.01% (95% CI 19.42-26.59; I2=99.8%), with substantial heterogeneity across studies and countries. Travel history was associated with increased odds of malaria (POR=4.17, 95% CI 2.33-7.46), as was owning livestock in the house (POR=4.1, 95% CI 1.62-10.39). Study concludes effective urban malaria control requires integrated, targeted approaches addressing socio-economic, environmental, and behavioral factors.

articles.headline: Invasive An. stephensi fully susceptible to novel insecticides
articles.title_original: Susceptibility of the invasive malaria vector Anopheles stephensi in Ethiopia to novel chemical insecticides and insect growth regulator.
articles.details: Susceptibility testing of Anopheles stephensi (resistant to pyrethroids, carbamates, and organophosphates) from Ethiopia showed 100% mortality to broflanilide and clothianidin at 24 hours, and 100% mortality to chlorfenapyr by 48 hours. Pyriproxyfen (insect growth regulator) achieved 100% oviposition inhibition in exposed females despite low direct mortality (15% at 72 hours). Findings support use of these novel compounds for indoor residual spraying and larviciding.

articles.headline: Temperature patterns predict child malaria deaths in Uganda
articles.title_original: Climate-driven malaria mortality among children in malaria-endemic areas of Uganda.
articles.details: Population-based cohort study of weekly malaria mortality in Iganga-Mayuge, Uganda using distributed lag non-linear models. Found increased mortality risk 11-12 weeks after heavy rainfall (>646 mm) and at 5-11 week lag during temperatures 25.2-29.9°C. Children under 5 were more sensitive to temperature than rainfall, with male children aged 5-14 showing highest temperature-related mortality vulnerability.

articles.headline: Vulnerability factors improve malaria risk maps in Senegal
articles.title_original: Integrating vulnerability and hazard in malaria risk mapping: the elimination context of Senegal.
articles.details: Bayesian geostatistical study predicting malaria prevalence in children under 5 across Senegal at 1 km resolution. Combined environmental hazard data (rainfall, temperature) with vulnerability factors (healthcare access, housing) from Demographic and Health Survey and Malaria Indicator Survey. Combined hazard-vulnerability models achieved median R²=0.64, with vulnerability-only models outperforming hazard-only models.

articles.headline: Sex differences in experimental cerebral malaria brain pathology
articles.title_original: Characterization of experimental cerebral malaria by volumetric MRI A comparative study across the sexes.
articles.authors: Comino Garcia Muñoz A, et al.
articles.details: In vivo MRI volumetric analysis of experimental cerebral malaria in C57BL/6J mice infected with Plasmodium berghei ANKA. Revealed sex-dependent differences in brain swelling, microhemorrhages, and lesion distribution, with swelling in cortex and pons common to both sexes and sex-specific alterations in colliculi and midbrain. Experimental cerebral malaria was more severe in male than female mice.

articles.headline: PBO nets show better retention and lower malaria in Kenya
articles.title_original: Net ownership, utilization and malaria burden in the context of Piperonyl butoxide-LLINs intervention in western Kenya.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study comparing 181 households with pyrethroid-only nets and 199 with piperonyl butoxide (PBO) long-lasting insecticidal nets in Kisumu County, Kenya one year post-distribution. PBO-nets showed higher ownership (96% vs 89%), usage (87.6% vs 80.2%), and lower attrition (7.6% vs 17.9%). Malaria prevalence by qPCR was 29.0% with PBO-nets vs 34.7% with pyrethroid-only nets (prevalence ratio 0.84, 95% CI 0.65-1.03).

articles.headline: Understanding mosquito biology for malaria elimination
articles.title_original: Know your enemy: understanding mosquito biology to advance malaria elimination in Africa.
articles.authors: Zembere K, Kalonde PK, Bickton FM
articles.details: Review article discussing key aspects of anopheline mosquito biology relevant to malaria transmission, including adult and juvenile life stages, incubation period, and blood-seeking behavior. Emphasizes how understanding mosquito biology informs vector control strategies that have achieved 65% reduction in malaria cases between 2000-2015.

articles.headline: Malaria impairs kidney function and electrolyte balance
articles.title_original: Evaluation of serum electrolytes and kidney function among untreated malaria (Plasmodium falciparum) patients in health centers, Konso zone, South Ethiopia.
articles.details: Case-control study of 54 untreated P. falciparum patients and 54 controls examining kidney function and electrolytes. Malaria cases had significantly elevated creatinine (1.32 vs 0.92 mg/dL) and urea (39.8 vs 25.8 mg/dL), with decreased sodium (132.2 vs 137.1 mmol/L) and potassium (3.44 vs 3.94 mmol/L). Findings suggest malaria is a determinant factor for kidney dysfunction and electrolyte imbalance.

articles.headline: CUT&Tag enables efficient chromatin profiling in P. falciparum
articles.title_original: CUT&Tag and DiBioCUT&Tag enable investigation of the AT-rich epigenome of Plasmodium falciparum from low-input samples.
articles.details: Methods paper adapting CUT&Tag to P. falciparum chromatin profiling despite AT richness of genome, producing reproducible heterochromatin profiles concordant with ChIP-seq while requiring minimal material (≤10,000 nuclei) and labor. Also developed DiBioCUT&Tag using dimerization-induced biotin ligase recruitment for proximity labeling of core chromatin components. Methods provide improved genome-wide chromatin-protein profiling from low-input samples.

articles.headline: Mosquito hunger shifts malaria vector biting time earlier
articles.title_original: Biting time of day in malaria mosquitoes is modulated by nutritional status.
articles.details: Semi-field study of female Anopheles gambiae s.l. mosquitoes varying nutritional resources (0.5%, 10% sucrose, ±additional blood meal). Low-nutrition mosquitoes were 2-3 fold more likely to host seek overall and 5-10 fold more likely to seek at earlier time of day compared to well-fed mosquitoes, which predominantly sought hosts in second half of night. Demonstrates nutritional status drives plasticity in biting behavior.

articles.headline: RDT cassettes enable retrospective malaria PCR testing
articles.title_original: [Use of genetic material extracted from rapid malaria diagnostic tests using PCR during the Grand Magal of Touba in Senegal].
articles.details: Study of 2,381 malaria RDTs collected from health facilities during Grand Magal of Touba in Senegal 2022-2023. Extracted genetic material from Bioline™ and ParaHIT® RDT cassettes for qPCR confirmation of P. falciparum. Bioline™ tests were 97.3% specific (PPV 71.1%) with 39 false positives, while ParaHIT® were 92.5% specific (PPV 26.9%) with 57 false positives, enabling retrospective diagnostic verification.

articles.headline: RDT samples detect tick-borne borreliosis at mass gatherings
articles.title_original: [Identification of tick-borne borreliosis cases by qPCR from rapid malaria diagnostic tests during the Grand Magal of Touba in Senegal].
articles.details: Nucleic acid extraction and PCR screening from 2,381 malaria RDTs collected during Grand Magal of Touba in Senegal 2022-2023 for dengue virus, Borrelia spp., Bartonella spp., and Coxiella burnetti. Identified 13 cases (0.5%) of tick-borne relapsing fever borreliosis including 2 malaria-borreliosis co-infections. No cases detected for dengue, Bartonella, or C. burnetti.

articles.headline: Type 1 diabetes debut complicated by malaria and pancreatitis
articles.title_original: Convergence of diabetic ketoacidosis, acute pancreatitis, and malaria: A case report.
articles.details: Case report of 20-year-old female presenting with inaugural type 1 diabetes ketoacidosis (Glasgow score 9, glucose 6 g/dL), acute stage E pancreatitis confirmed on CT, and confirmed malaria. Severe DKA with hypertriglyceridemia, elevated pancreatic enzymes, and malaria parasitemia. Rapid multidisciplinary management with insulin, rehydration, antimalarial and analgesic therapy resulted in favorable outcome with glucose normalization after 10 days.

articles.headline: Benzo-ring modifications of MMV008138 show limited benefit
articles.title_original: Benzo-ring modification on Malaria Box hit MMV008138: effects on antimalarial potency and microsomal stability.
articles.details: Structure-activity relationship study of benzo-ring substitutions on MMV008138, a P. falciparum IspD inhibitor targeting the MEP pathway. Of 19 variants tested against multidrug-resistant Dd2 strain, only 3 maintained activity within 3-fold of parent compound. One analog (20c) achieved marked improvement in microsomal stability, but this did not translate to improved plasma exposure or oral efficacy in a mouse malaria model.

articles.headline: Imatinib repurposing for malaria: evidence and future directions
articles.title_original: The emerging role of Imatinib in malaria management: a review of evidence and future directions.
articles.details: Systematic review synthesizing studies through February 2025 on imatinib efficacy and safety in malaria across multiple databases. Nine studies with varying sample sizes and dosing reported parasite density decline, growth inhibition, and synergism with artesunate; no adverse drug-related events documented. Recommends future randomized controlled trials with larger samples and combination therapy exploration.

articles.headline: Artemisinin modulates pathogenic CD8+ T cells via JAK2-STAT3
articles.title_original: Single-cell profiling reveals immunoregulation of artemisinin on CD8(+)GZMB(+) T cells via JAK2-STAT3 in malaria-infected mice.
articles.details: Single-cell RNA sequencing of 241,837 cells from P. berghei ANKA-infected mice revealed artesunate modulates pathogenic CD8+GZMB+ T lymphocytes (which drive hepatic injury) through JAK2-STAT3 pathway, reducing frequency and inflammatory response. Provides mechanistic insight into immunoregulatory mechanisms beyond parasite killing.

articles.headline: Community engagement critical for successful larviciding programs
articles.title_original: Assessing the role of community involvement and capacity building in larviciding applications for malaria control in Africa: A scoping review.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Scoping review of 32 papers from 13 African countries through December 2024 evaluating larviciding delivery mechanisms. Findings: larviciding significantly reduced mosquito density and in some cases malaria incidence; community engagement strategies (workshops, participatory meetings) were critical for success. Challenges included logistical barriers, lack of awareness, and financial constraints.

articles.headline: Imported and non-travel malaria cases in Europe analyzed
articles.title_original: Imported and non-travel related cases of malaria in Europe - a ten-year analytic retrospective review.
articles.authors: Vanitha R, Abelein F, Stoyanova KP
articles.details: Retrospective analytic review of imported and non-travel related malaria cases in Europe over ten years examining epidemiological patterns and sources of infection.

articles.headline: Metabolic reprogramming and microbiota restrict P. vivax in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Metabolic reprogramming and gut microbiota ecology drive divergent Plasmodium vivax infection outcomes in Anopheles darlingi.
articles.details: Experimental infections of A. darlingi with field-derived P. vivax produced low and high oocyst phenotypes. Low-infection mosquitoes mounted sustained response with activation of detoxification, redox regulation, amino acid catabolism, purine depletion, and enrichment of anti-Plasmodium bacterial taxa. Physiological state dependent on gametocyte density in bloodmeal; identifies metabolic and microbial targets for transmission-blocking strategies.

articles.headline: New assay comprehensively measures artemisinin resistance phenotypes
articles.title_original: Measuring growth, resistance, and recovery after artemisinin treatment of Plasmodium falciparum in a single semi-high-throughput assay.
articles.details: Growth, Resistance, and Recovery assay (GRRA) using qPCR in 96-well format measures parasite growth (6-96h), resistance (120h), and recovery (120-192h). Validated with clinical isolates and genetic crosses. Results: growth accurately captured by qPCR, resistance at 120h most consistent phenotype, recovery identifies additional drug response. Dried blood spot samples equivalent to liquid samples.

articles.headline: Seasonal socioeconomic disparities drive malaria inequity rurally
articles.title_original: Inequity in the transmission of malaria infection among children and adolescents: a cohort study in rural Guinea.
articles.details: Cohort of 439 children aged 1–19 in rural Guinea followed monthly for nine months. Malaria infection varies seasonally and by household characteristics. Children in households with older heads (50–77 years) had higher dry-season odds (OR=3.44); adolescents more vulnerable in rainy season in middle-aged (OR=15.78) or single-parent households (OR=4.52). Concentration indices showed seasonal and socioeconomic disparities with distinct household-level equity drivers.

articles.headline: High community acceptability for seasonal malaria chemoprevention
articles.title_original: "We witnessed medicines help our children, then we became proud": experiences and acceptability of seasonal malaria chemoprevention in Aweil, South Sudan.
articles.details: Qualitative study of 97 participants (community leaders, caregivers, health workers) after five cycles of SMC with sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine in children aged 3–59 months in South Sudan. High acceptability reported; positive factors included perceived effectiveness, door-to-door delivery, and trusted health workers. Challenges: environmental barriers, inadequate health worker remuneration, difficulty reaching all children.

articles.headline: Systematic review confirms primaquine safety and transmission reduction
articles.title_original: Single low-dose primaquine for malaria control in Africa: a systematic review of safety, efficacy and implementation barriers.
articles.details: Systematic review of 41 studies (2012–2023) on SLDPQ (0.25 mg/kg) with ACTs in African P. falciparum treatment. SLDPQ found safe and well-tolerated in G6PD deficiency and children under 5. Eight RCTs confirmed significant gametocyte carriage reductions, validating transmission-blocking efficacy. Key barriers to implementation: toxicity misconceptions, lack of pediatric formulations, operational challenges. Supports SLDPQ scale-up in elimination strategies.

articles.headline: Household EIR relationship with individual malaria risk varies by site
articles.title_original: Relating household entomological measures to individual malaria risk.
articles.details: Cohort of 439 children aged 0.5–5 years in 239 households across three Ugandan sites (low-, intermediate-, high-EIR) from 2011–2016. Monthly household vector densities and sporozoite rates measured. Across-site trend showed increasing EIR–incidence relationship, but within-site associations varied: positive in Kanungu (IRR 1.09, 95% CI 1.04–1.14) but absent in Jinja (1.02, 0.774–1.26) or Tororo (1.02, 0.986–1.06).

articles.headline: Training significantly improves malaria guideline adherence rates
articles.title_original: Assessing Physicians' Adherence to the 2023 Sudan Malaria Case Management Protocol.
articles.details: Two-cycle audit at Sudan hospital: first cycle (N=42) showed baseline protocol adherence 30.95%, IM artesunate injection 30.95%, second-line MIP treatment 23.81%. After training and guideline dissemination, second cycle (N=39): protocol adherence increased to 89.74% (58.79% improvement), IM artesunate to 79.49%, MIP treatment to 64.10% (40.29% improvement), IPTp sulfadoxine/pyrimethamine to 92.31% (49.45% improvement).

articles.headline: Machine learning generalizes MDA trial findings to non-trial settings
articles.title_original: Using transportability methods to map the local effectiveness of mass drug administration for malaria in Senegal.
articles.details: Transportability study using machine learning to generalize a cluster-randomized trial finding (55% MDA-induced incidence reduction with SMC) to 116 non-trial Senegal communes. Accounting for weather, vegetation, and population density differences, estimated incidence reductions of 36%–65% in 74 communes, with larger reductions in high-precipitation, denser-vegetation, lower-temperature areas. MDA not effective post-intervention year, indicating short-lived effects.

articles.headline: P. falciparum malaria mimics thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura
articles.title_original: Severe Plasmodium Falciparum Malaria Mimicking Thrombotic Thrombocytopenic Purpura (TTP): A Case Report From China.
articles.details: Case report of 51-year-old with severe P. falciparum malaria presenting with markedly elevated von Willebrand factor and normal ADAMTS13 activity, mimicking thrombotic thrombocytopenic purpura (PLASMIC score 7). Clinical features overlapped with TTP including fever, thrombocytopenia, severe anemia, and intravascular coagulation. Patient improved with intravenous artesunate. Highlights importance of rapid differential diagnosis.

articles.headline: Malaria-anemia comorbidity affects 39% of pregnant women
articles.title_original: Malaria-anemia comorbidity and its determinants among pregnant women in high- and moderate-malaria-risk countries in Sub-Saharan Africa.
articles.details: Multilevel logistic regression of 50,545 pregnant women across 17 Sub-Saharan African countries (2016-2022). Pooled prevalence of malaria-anemia comorbidity 39% (95% CI 29–49%). Key risk factors: no education (OR=1.43), untreated bed nets (OR=1.23), poor wealth (OR=2.37), no indoor residual spraying (OR=2.15), rural residence (OR=2.73), South Sub-Saharan Africa (OR=18.76). Recommends targeted interventions in rural high-burden regions.

articles.headline: Malaria seasonality varies by district and region in Togo
articles.title_original: Identifying malaria epidemic periods in Togo by health district and target group: a generalised additive model approach.
articles.details: Analysis of 5.5 million confirmed malaria cases from 2013–2017 using generalized additive models. Time lag of ≥1 month between precipitation onset and epidemic start across age groups. Savanes region showed later onset with greater relative case increases; Maritime and Lome-commune regions had short or undetectable peaks. Results support district-level tailoring of seasonal chemoprevention timing.

articles.headline: CHW management in ATSB trial aligns with best practices
articles.title_original: Comparative analysis of the use of Community Health Workers while deploying the Attractive Targeted Sugar Bait (ATSB) for malaria control in Western Province, Zambia.
articles.details: Comparative analysis of Community Health Worker involvement in phase III Attractive Targeted Sugar Bait trial in Western Zambia (2021–2023). ATSB trial practices aligned with literature best practices in training and incentive structures (97.1% CHW fidelity, 97.4% community acceptability). Challenges: low gender balance (32% female), coordination gaps. Adequately-funded research achieved best practices despite universal cadre challenges.

articles.headline: Malaria prevention alone insufficient to improve child nutrition
articles.title_original: Malaria-malnutrition interaction: prevalence, risk factors, and the impact of intermittent preventive therapy for malaria on nutritional status of school-age children in Muheza, Tanga, Tanzania - A cross-sectional survey and a randomized controlled open-label trial.
articles.details: Randomized controlled open-label trial in Muheza, Tanzania with children aged 5–15. Baseline: 27% malaria, 21% stunted, 28% thin/severely thin. Children with malaria had 78% higher odds of stunting (AOR=1.78, 95% CI 1.36–2.33, p<0.001). Three treatment arms (DP, ASAQ, control) showed no significant difference in weight, height, BMI change over time. Integrated malaria-nutrition interventions essential.

articles.headline: Galectin-3 cannot predict insulin resistance in malaria
articles.title_original: Galectin-3 did not associate with malaria-related insulin resistance in diabetic and non-diabetic respondents at a Ghanaian General Hospital.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of diabetic and non-diabetic adults at Tema General Hospital, Ghana. Galectin-3 higher in malaria but could not predict HOMA-IR or HOMA-B in any group. Insulin resistance associated with glucose (B=0.603, p=0.001) and insulin (B=1.145, p<0.001) levels, model explaining 67.7–91% variation. Galectin-3 relationship to malaria-related insulin resistance more complex than linear.

articles.headline: Expanded CHW role accepted by 97% in Myanmar malaria setting
articles.title_original: Acceptability, feasibility and fidelity of an expanded role for community health workers for malaria elimination in Myanmar: A mixed-method study.
articles.details: Mixed-method study (qualitative: 6 leaders, 14 stakeholders, 36 FGDs, 69 supervision visits; quantitative: 643 community members). Expanded CHW model for malaria elimination acceptable to 97.4% (626/643) with 97.1% high fidelity (67/69) and 98.3% community reliance (632/643). 93% reported met health demand. Needs improvement: training, supervision, supply chain. Feasible and acceptable for wider implementation.

articles.headline: Capsicum Frutescens shows antiplasmodial activity in mice
articles.title_original: Evaluating the Oxidative Stress Reducing Potentials of Sub-Fractions of Capsicum Frutescens L. Aqueous Extract on Early Established Plasmodium in Mice.
articles.details: Experimental study of 204 P. berghei NK65-infected mice testing Capsicum Frutescens (CFL) leaf sub-fractions at doses 0.5–8.0 mg/kg or chloroquine 10 mg/kg. CFL-treated groups showed significantly reduced lipid peroxidation (malondialdehyde, p<0.05) with dose-dependent increases in antioxidant enzymes (days 4 and 8). Sub-fraction B most pronounced. Results suggest antiplasmodial and antioxidant activity in vivo.

articles.headline: Inhibitory and activating KIRs recognize RIFINs in malaria
articles.title_original: Both inhibitory and activating KIRs recognize RIFINs: a dual-edged mechanism of NK cell control in malaria.
articles.details: Title only; no abstract provided. Describes mechanism where both inhibitory and activating killer immunoglobulin-like receptors (KIRs) recognize RIFIN antigens, suggesting dual-edged natural killer cell control in malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Host sphingolipids essential for Plasmodium liver stage
articles.title_original: Host sphingolipids support Plasmodium berghei liver stage development.
articles.details: Experimental study showing sphingolipids critical for P. berghei liver stage invasion and development. Exogenous C16-ceramide enhanced parasite development and nuclear replication; sphingomyelin in host membrane essential for invasion. Host ceramide transporter CERT1 enriched at parasitophorous vacuole; CERT1 depletion significantly reduced P. berghei load. CERS3 and SPHK1 depletion affected infection rate and vacuole size.

articles.headline: Seven-day high-dose primaquine equals 14-day standard course
articles.title_original: Shortening the Journey to Cure: A Quasi-experimental Trial Comparing Seven-Day and 14-Day Primaquine Radical Therapy for Vivax Malaria.
articles.details: Quasi-experimental trial of 100 P. vivax patients (ages 18–76) in Peshawar comparing 7-day high-dose primaquine (1 mg/kg/day, n=50) vs standard 14-day low-dose (0.5 mg/kg/day, n=50). Recurrence at 90 days: 2% vs 26%, p=0.012. Adherence: 94% vs 76%, p=0.012. Both well-tolerated. Seven-day regimen offers equivalent efficacy with improved adherence.

articles.headline: Phytonanotechnology strategies for Chagas and malaria
articles.title_original: Harnessing Phytonanotechnology to Tackle Neglected Parasitic Diseases: Focus on Chagas Disease and Malaria.
articles.authors: García M, Magi MS, García MC
articles.details: Comprehensive review of phytochemical-based nanocarrier systems for neglected parasitic diseases. Discusses plant-derived compounds with antiparasitic activity against Trypanosoma cruzi and Plasmodium spp., addressing limitations of traditional natural products including poor solubility, low bioavailability, and chemical instability. Emphasizes nanotechnology-based drug delivery to improve therapeutic properties and clinical accessibility.

articles.headline: High malaria vaccine acceptance among caregivers in sub-Saharan Africa
articles.title_original: Community readiness and acceptance for the implementation of the malaria vaccine among caretakers of at-risk children in sub-Saharan Africa: a systematic review and meta-analysis.
articles.details: Systematic review of 34 studies (25,867 participants in meta-analysis) assessed acceptance and willingness to vaccinate children against malaria. Pooled acceptance rate was 82% (95% CI: 73%-90%) and willingness 80% (95% CI: 70%-90%), with lowest acceptance (53%) in the DRC. High heterogeneity (I² > 99%) and publication bias were observed. Authors conclude strong community readiness for vaccine rollout, though context-specific strategies are needed.

articles.headline: Correction to Artemisia extract antimalarial activity study
articles.title_original: Correction: Assessment of the in vitro activity and selectivity of Artemisia afra and Artemisia annua aqueous extracts against artemisinin-resistant Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: This is a correction notice to a previously published study on Artemisia afra and annua aqueous extracts tested against artemisinin-resistant P. falciparum. No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Environmental factors, not LLIN type, drive vector habitat changes
articles.title_original: Ecological drivers of malaria vector habitat and transmission over 1 year of long-lasting insecticidal net intervention in Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.details: Ecological niche modeling study in Côte d'Ivoire with 33 clusters examined effects of long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) and environmental factors on three Anopheles species and P. falciparum-infected vectors over 1 year. Vector habitat reductions ranged <1% to >60% across species; >50% reduction in infected vectors. While no significant intervention arm effect was detected, environmental variables (land cover, climate, topography, population density) strongly influenced species occurrence.

articles.headline: Community-based malaria care expansion to all ages acceptable
articles.title_original: An Anthropological Analysis of Acceptability and Feasibility of Expanding Community-Based Malaria Management to All Ages in Madagascar: Levers and Challenges for National Scale-Up.
articles.details: Qualitative analysis of a cluster randomized trial in Madagascar examined acceptability and feasibility of expanding community health worker-based malaria case management to all ages beyond the traditional <5 years. Through 87 interviews and 12 focus groups, researchers found age-expanded mCCM was acceptable to caregivers and CHWs and consistent with existing roles, increasing awareness of adult malaria susceptibility. However, structural barriers including cost of care remained unresolved.

articles.headline: Plasma protein signature identifies severe malaria at molecular level
articles.title_original: Systems analysis of clinical malaria reveals proteomic perturbation and innate-adaptive crosstalk linked to disease severity.
articles.details: Proteomic analysis of 1,463 plasma proteins in adult travelers with acute P. falciparum malaria revealed >250 perturbations and identified an 11-protein signature enriched in severe malaria. Integration with single-cell RNA sequencing linked systemic proteomic changes to innate-adaptive immune crosstalk and organ-specific responses. Protein profiles clustered participants by disease severity, opening new avenues for biomarker discovery and therapeutic target identification.
articles.notable_reason: Novel protein signature links immune pathways to severe malaria

articles.headline: Service members from malaria-endemic regions have 5-fold higher risk
articles.title_original: Malaria Incidence in US Military Families Is Related to Service Member's Birthplace.
articles.details: Retrospective cohort study of US military personnel and families (2012-2018) identified 281 malaria cases. Service members born in malaria-endemic countries had 5-fold higher malaria incidence (IRR 5.2, 95% CI 2.4-10.4) versus non-endemic birthplaces. Sub-Saharan Africa region accounted for 86.6% of endemic-origin cases with 42 per 100,000 annual incidence. Only 48% of all cases had sought pretravel medical counseling.

articles.headline: Historical malaria eradication campaign prevented half-million cases
articles.title_original: Costs and impact of the Malaria Eradication Campaign in Colombia, 1957-1969.
articles.details: Retrospective cost analysis of Colombia's Malaria Eradication Campaign (1959-1969) calculated total institutional costs of USD $41.8 million, with 74.1% from national sources. Attack phase accounted for 80.4% of expenditure; personnel for spraying comprised 40% of attack costs, insecticide 19%. Campaign prevented an estimated 568,439 malaria cases (average 41,965/year) but ultimately did not achieve eradication.

articles.headline: Emerging K13 variants without resistance detected in Gabon
articles.title_original: Clonal Transmission of Emerging Novel Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 Mutations and Increasing Complexity of Infection in Libreville, Gabon, 2021-2023.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance in Libreville, Gabon (2021-2023) of 468 P. falciparum samples identified 21 novel K13 mutations of unknown significance (E433D, Q613H, V520I, V637I), with E433D prevalence increasing from 0.7% to 5.5%. These mutations showed significant identity-by-descent indicating clonal transmission chains, though no validated artemisinin-resistance markers were detected. MDR1 Y184F increased from 52.6% to 68.8%; complexity of infection rose over time.

articles.headline: Artemisinin effective in Mozambique; SP resistance widespread
articles.title_original: Antimalarial drug resistance and population structure of Plasmodium falciparum in Mozambique using genomic surveillance at health facilities in 2021 and 2022.
articles.details: Genomic surveillance of 1,146 P. falciparum samples in Mozambique (2021-2022) detected no artemisinin-resistance (pfk13) or piperaquine-resistance (pfpm2) markers. DHFR/DHPS quintuple mutants conferring sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine (SP) resistance were highly prevalent (>87.8%), though pfdhps-A581G was rare. Mutation prevalence and infection complexity increased from south to north. Findings support continued artemisinin-based combination therapy use while recommending close monitoring of SP-based preventive efficacy.

articles.headline: Mathematical model confirms treatment and prevention reduce malaria
articles.title_original: Fractional-order model of malaria incorporating treatment and prevention strategies.
articles.details: Mathematical modeling study using fractional-order derivatives examined malaria transmission dynamics with treatment and prevention strategies. Researchers established conditions for solution existence, assessed endemic equilibrium stability using Lyapunov functions, and performed sensitivity analysis of the basic reproduction number. Simulations demonstrated that improved treatment and preventive measures including mosquito control and timely medication significantly reduced malaria cases, while increased mosquito contact and treatment ineffectiveness aggravated burden.

articles.headline: MicroRNA-92a binding conserved in parasite despite variation
articles.title_original: Exploring the Potential of miRNA-92a-3p as Lead for Sequence-Based Therapies for Malaria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional and case-control study examining 20 infected patient samples identified conserved miR-92a-3p binding motifs within the DBLα domain of P. falciparum erythrocyte membrane protein (PfEMP), despite notable sequence variability across isolates. Comparative miR-92a mutation analysis of cases and healthy controls identified single and multi-nucleotide variants suggesting genetic influences on miRNA-mediated regulation. Findings suggest potential for antisense miRNA-based therapeutic strategies targeting PfEMP variants.

articles.headline: Ferrocenyl hybrids show multistage antimalarial activity in vitro
articles.title_original: Ferrocenyl Quinoline-Benzimidazole Hybrids: A Multistage Strategy to Combat Drug-Resistant Malaria.
articles.details: In vitro evaluation of ferrocenyl-based quinoline-benzimidazole molecular hybrids demonstrated potent submicromolar activity against both drug-sensitive NF54 and multidrug-resistant K1 P. falciparum strains. Compounds exhibited multistage activity targeting both immature and mature gametocytes. Mechanistic analysis revealed hemozoin formation inhibition as primary mode of action, with reactive oxygen species generation contributing under oxidative conditions. Findings support further development of ferrocenyl-based hybrids as antimalarial candidates.

articles.headline: Anopheles seasonality and climate impacts in malaria-free Sri Lanka
articles.title_original: Seasonal abundance and climatic determinants of Anopheles vectors in Sri Lanka: its implications in the malaria post-elimination era.
articles.details: Two-year entomological surveillance across five Sri Lankan districts using cattle-baited traps, human landing catches, and hand collections identified 15 Anopheles species. An. culicifacies showed highest densities at intermediate-zone sites with peaks during inter-monsoon and southwest monsoon seasons. Vector abundance significantly influenced by climate factors (p<0.05), with An. culicifacies densities increasing with humidity and secondary vectors showing species-specific temperature and rainfall responses.

articles.headline: Pv47 genetic diversity suggests mosquito-driven parasite adaptation
articles.title_original: Worldwide genetic diversity of Plasmodium vivax Pv47 is consistent with natural selection by anopheline mosquitoes.
articles.details: Global analysis of P. vivax Pv47 (ortholog of P. falciparum Pfs47) identified predominantly non-synonymous polymorphisms with excess substitutions in domain 2, indicating positive selection. Greatest haplotype diversity found in East/Southeast Asia and Oceania. Pv47 K27E polymorphism associated with differential infectivity to phylogenetically distinct Anopheles species (A. albimanus and A. pseudopunctipennis), paralleling selection patterns observed in Pfs47.

articles.headline: Decreasing lumefantrine and DHA susceptibility in Uganda P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Changes in susceptibility of Plasmodium falciparum to antimalarial drugs in Uganda over time: 2019-2024.
articles.details: Ex vivo susceptibility testing of 1,114 P. falciparum isolates from Uganda (2019–2024) revealed declining susceptibilities to lumefantrine and dihydroartemisinin (DHA) over time. Lumefantrine resistance associated with PfCRT K76T, PfMDR1 N86Y, PfK13 C469Y/A675V, and PfCARL D611N mutations; DHA resistance with PfK13 C469Y/A675V and PfMDR1 Y500N. Chloroquine susceptibility improved, but decreasing artemisinin-combination therapy efficacy threatens leading malaria regimens.
articles.notable_reason: Declining DHA and lumefantrine susceptibility threatens first-line ACTs

articles.headline: Ferroportin inhibition exacerbates Plasmodium infection severity
articles.title_original: Pharmacologic Inhibition of Erythrocyte Ferroportin Expression Exacerbates Plasmodium Infection.
articles.details: P. yoelii-infected mice treated with oral ferroportin inhibitor VIT-2763 (Vamifeport) showed increased parasitemia accompanied by elevated pro-inflammatory cytokines, erythropoietin, and liver injury markers. VIT-2763 suppressed hepatic hepcidin expression while reducing RBC ferroportin protein and increasing hepatocyte ferroportin, revealing tissue-specific hepcidin-ferroportin regulation impacts parasite control.

articles.headline: Amodiaquine resistance haplotype rising in São Tomé
articles.title_original: Trajectory of Plasmodium falciparum Molecular Markers of Amodiaquine Resistance in São Tomé and Príncipe.
articles.details: Temporal analysis of P. falciparum amodiaquine-resistance markers in São Tomé and Príncipe (2020–2022) revealed successive SNP accretion leading to dominance of pfmdr1 86Y/Y184/1246Y haplotype in the context of near-fixation pfcrt 76T. Pattern suggests selection pressure from artesunate-amodiaquine use as first-line therapy since 2005, following decades of chloroquine.
articles.notable_reason: Rising resistance to first-line artesunate-amodiaquine therapy

articles.headline: Lightweight neural network for automated malaria detection
articles.title_original: Hybrid Capsule Network for precise and interpretable detection of malaria parasites in blood smear images.
articles.details: Hybrid Capsule Network (Hybrid CapNet) architecture for parasite identification and life-cycle stage classification from blood smear images. Lightweight design (1.35M parameters, 0.26 GFLOPs) achieves up to 100% accuracy on four benchmark datasets (MP-IDB, MP-IDB2, IML-Malaria, MD-2019) with improved cross-dataset generalization and interpretable Grad-CAM visualizations focusing on biologically relevant parasite regions.

articles.headline: Survey acceptance high but ivermectin MDA completion low
articles.title_original: Community acceptance of Ivermectin mass drug administration for malaria in Southern Thailand.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 391 household heads in Surat Thani Province, Thailand, regarding ivermectin mass drug administration (MDA). Although 96.4% reported acceptance in surveys, only 59.0% of 3,137 eligible individuals completed all three MDA rounds. Forest-related workers (aOR 4.2, 95% CI 1.1–16.1) and those believing elimination possible (aOR 9.1, 95% CI 2.8–29.9) showed higher acceptance, but village absence and drug reluctance were key completion barriers.

articles.headline: High malaria burden and artemisinin resistance in East Africa
articles.title_original: The burden of malaria in East Africa: prevalence, risk factors, and control strategies.
articles.details: Systematic review of malaria in 12 East African countries (2015–2024) reports highest incidence in Uganda, South Sudan, and Burundi (≥250 per 1,000). Artemisinin partial resistance exceeds 20% in multiple areas; pyrethroid vector resistance undermines control efforts. Climate change expands transmission to highland zones. Integrated strategies (LLINs, indoor residual spraying, vaccination, community engagement) reduce severe malaria by up to 47%.

articles.headline: Malaria-schistosomiasis co-infection linked to malnutrition
articles.title_original: Malaria, urogenital schistosomiasis and co-infection and nutritional status of school children in Ondo state.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 185 schoolchildren in Ondo State, Nigeria, examining malaria and urogenital schistosomiasis prevalence. Malaria prevalent in 57.1% (Oke Igbo) and 49.2% (Ipogun); schistosomiasis in 28.6% vs 4.1%; co-infection in 20.6% vs 3.3%. Males 2.19× more likely to have co-infections (p=0.02, 95% CI 1.13–4.28). Both single and co-infections were significantly associated with increased chronic malnutrition (>75% in both communities).

articles.headline: Annona muricata enhances artesunate and pyrimethamine activity
articles.title_original: Enhanced Antimalarial Efficacy of Annona muricata Leaf Extract Combined With Artesunate, Chloroquine, and Pyrimethamine in Plasmodium berghei-Infected ICR Mice.
articles.authors: Sarakul O, Boonhok R, Somsak V
articles.details: P. berghei-infected ICR mice treated with Annona muricata leaf extract (AME) combined with artesunate (ART) or pyrimethamine (PYR) showed synergistic effects, achieving >90% parasitemia inhibition and prolonged mean survival times (up to 30 days). AME alone exhibited significant dose-dependent antimalarial activity, while the AME-chloroquine combination demonstrated no synergistic effect.

articles.headline: Low-diversity P. vivax genomes in Duffy-negative hosts
articles.title_original: Highly conserved Plasmodium vivax genomes in Duffy-negative individuals from Sudan.
articles.details: Comparative genomic analysis of P. vivax from Duffy-positive and Duffy-negative Sudanese individuals revealed significantly lower parasitemia in Duffy-negatives (p=0.0000132). Duffy-positive parasite genomes showed substantially greater diversity across 14 chromosomes and 44 erythrocyte binding gene candidates, particularly merozoite surface protein genes, with multiple loci under positive and negative selection pressure.
articles.notable_reason: Novel mechanism constraining P. vivax adaptation in Duffy-negative hosts

articles.headline: Sequential RDT algorithm improves malaria diagnosis accuracy
articles.title_original: Refining malaria diagnosis in high-transmission areas: a dual-approach with rapid diagnostic tests (RDTs) and dbPCR-NALFIA.
articles.details: Evaluation of sequential two-step RDT algorithm detecting HRP2 and pLDH with confirmatory dbPCR-NALFIA in 438 febrile patients at rural Burkina Faso clinics. Sequential interpretation identified 87.2% conclusive results without confirmatory testing (sensitivity 98.8%, specificity 95.3%). Including dbPCR-NALFIA for inconclusive cases achieved sensitivity 97.9%, specificity 94.8%, PPV 97.2%, NPV 96.1%—substantially superior to single HRP2 RDT.

articles.headline: High P. falciparum genetic diversity among asymptomatic carriers in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Genetic polymorphism of msp2 in Plasmodium falciparum isolates among asymptomatic malaria infections from two ecological settings in Cameroon.
articles.details: Assessed msp2 polymorphism in P. falciparum isolates from 876 asymptomatic individuals in Mfou (59.2% malaria prevalence) and 808 in Tibati (63.4% prevalence) using nested PCR and capillary electrophoresis. Detected 36 and 42 msp2 alleles respectively, with >60% multiclonal infections (MOI 2.08 in Mfou vs 1.80 in Tibati). Negative correlation between MOI and age in Mfou, with males having higher MOI than females. High allelic diversity reflects high transmission intensity with distinct epidemiological patterns between sites.

articles.headline: Night activities and gardens increase malaria risk in Zimbabwe
articles.title_original: Risk factors for contracting malaria in six wards of Mudzi District, Zimbabwe: A case control-study.
articles.authors: Fambirai T, Chimbari MJ, Ndarukwa P
articles.details: Unmatched case-control study with 94 cases and 91 controls in Mudzi District. Night outdoor social/religious activities (AOR 8.13, 95% CI 1.74-37.90) and having a garden (AOR 4.51, 95% CI 1.55-13.12) were independent risk factors. Protective factors: full body clothing at night (AOR 0.13, 95% CI 0.03-0.53) and sleeping in sprayed rooms (AOR 0.04, 95% CI 0.01-0.31). Despite 96.74% of cases having good malaria knowledge, outdoor activities remained significantly associated with infection.

articles.headline: Global disparities in quality of care for malaria and NTDs
articles.title_original: Inequalities in disease burden and care quality of neglected tropical diseases and malaria, 1990-2021: Findings from the 2021 Global Burden of Disease Study.
articles.details: Analysis of Global Burden of Disease 2021 data assessing quality of care index (QCI) for neglected tropical diseases and malaria. QCI highest in Bahrain, Qatar, and UAE (91.9, 91.5, 91.5) and lowest in Uganda, Niger, Burkina Faso (78.5, 77.1, 75.2). QCI positively correlated with country economic development. Incidence increased 1990-2021 while age-standardized mortality rates and DALYs declined. Findings highlight need for improved care in low SDI areas and among young children.

articles.headline: Malaria: Reply
articles.title_original: Malaria. Reply.
articles.details: Brief reply communication regarding malaria (minimal detail in abstract)

articles.headline: Malaria
articles.title_original: Malaria.
articles.authors: Barkati S, Balerdi-Sarasola L, Yansouni CP
articles.details: Brief communication on malaria (insufficient abstract detail)

articles.headline: Host circadian phase minimally affects P. berghei liver establishment
articles.title_original: Time-of-day of infection: impact on liver stage malaria parasites in untreated and drug-treated hosts.
articles.details: Experimental study infecting mice with P. berghei-infected Anopheles stephensi during rest and active phases, in untreated and pyrimethamine-treated groups. Parasite burdens quantified by qPCR adjusted for sporozoite inocula. Pyrimethamine reduced parasite liver burdens but effects were not dependent on host time-of-day. Host time-of-day did not affect parasite burdens in untreated mice, indicating host circadian rhythms do not significantly influence vector-to-host transmission.

articles.headline: P. vivax risk factors and trends in Thailand-Myanmar border
articles.title_original: Slide positivity, trends, and risk factors of febrile Plasmodium vivax malaria along the Thailand-Myanmar border, 2018-2023.
articles.details: Community-based longitudinal surveillance (2018-2023) of 13,347 febrile malaria-suspected patients screened by microscopy; cumulative P. vivax slide positivity 11.0%. Risk factors: school-aged children (aOR 1.56), working-age adults (aOR 1.43), males (aOR 1.19), Myanmar nationals (aOR 2.37), non-Thai nationals (aOR 5.50), malaria history (aOR 1.59), recent travel (aOR 2.38), livestock occupations (aOR 2.49). Bed net use protective (aOR 0.39). Strengthening surveillance and targeting high-risk populations crucial for elimination.

articles.headline: Climate change increases malaria transmission suitability in Canada
articles.title_original: Current and future temperature suitability for autochthonous transmission of malaria in Canada.
