articles.details: Applied historical temperature and duration thresholds for Plasmodium transmission to gridded climate data (2000-2023 observed; future projections). Rising trends identified: surges reached 34% of Canadian population under suitable conditions for P. falciparum and 56% for P. vivax by 2023. Projected population percentages vary significantly by species, climate scenario, and model. Results underscore increasing risk of autochthonous malaria transmission in Canada due to climate change.

articles.headline: Altitude shapes anopheline species composition and malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Diversity of anopheline species and malaria transmission dynamics in high-altitude areas of western Cameroon.
articles.details: Entomological study of 17 villages across altitudes (700-1500 m) in western Cameroon using human landing catches. Identified 2,835 Anopheles: An. gambiae s.l. (82.88%), An. funestus s.l. (15.92%), An. nili (0.09%), An. ziemanni (1.11%). An. gambiae biting rates increased dramatically with altitude (45.25 bites/human/night at Penka Michel vs 0.41 at Dschang, p<0.001). Entomological inoculation rate 13-fold higher in Penka Michel (1.11 vs 0.08 infective bites/human/night). P. falciparum dominant (62-67%), with P. malariae and P. ovale also detected.

articles.headline: JAK-STAT pathway mediates cerebral malaria BBB pathogenesis in vitro
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum egress disrupts endothelial junctions and activates JAK-STAT signaling in a microvascular 3D blood-brain barrier model.
articles.details: Developed 3D blood-brain barrier model using primary brain endothelial cells, astrocytes, and pericytes. Exposure to P. falciparum parasite egress products increased microvascular permeability, downregulated junctional genes, increased ferroptosis and type I interferon markers, and upregulated JAK-STAT pathway. Cytoadherent P. falciparum-infected erythrocytes induced localized transcriptional changes and inter-endothelial gaps. JAK-STAT inhibitor Ruxolitinib prevented increased permeability, suggesting potential adjunctive therapeutic avenue for cerebral malaria pathogenesis.

articles.headline: Time series forecasting of malaria in pregnancy across Nigeria
articles.title_original: Time series analysis of malaria in pregnancy, using wavelet and SARIMAX models.
articles.details: Analyzed malaria in pregnancy (MIP) variability in Nigeria (January 2015-January 2025) using wavelet coherence and comparing ARIMA vs SARIMAX models. Demonstrated significant regional variability in MIP temporal patterns. Strong cross-correlations between MIP and general malaria transmission in some states (Kebbi, Niger, Yobe, Ondo), weaker in others (South-South, South-East). SARIMAX captured trends more effectively than ARIMA in most regions. Findings emphasize importance of climate-sensitive forecasting and targeted seasonal interventions.

articles.headline: Gene drive parameters for malaria control identified via modeling
articles.title_original: A model-informed target product profile for population modification gene drives for malaria control.
articles.authors: Mondal A, Sánchez C HM, Marshall JM
articles.details: Eco-epidemiological modeling of population modification gene drives in African settings (Burkina Faso, Kenya) at multiple transmission intensities. Identified parameter ranges satisfying target outcomes: 50% reduction in clinical malaria incidence for ≥3 years with time-to-impact <1 year. Most influential parameters: fitness cost of gene drive allele and effector gene efficacy. Low functional resistance allele generation preferred. Homing rates achieved need not be improved. Resistance parameters most influential for field efficacy but only measurable in field.

articles.headline: Screening identifies 12 compounds active against P. cynomolgi hypnozoites
articles.title_original: Identification of novel compounds with prophylactic activity against hypnozoites using a Plasmodium cynomolgi in vitro model.
articles.details: A high-throughput screen of 568 compounds from GSK's TCAMS library identified 12 with IC50 values below 2 µM against Plasmodium cynomolgi liver-stage hypnozoites, with the most active displaying nanomolar activity. These compounds grouped into six chemical clusters, with some potentially acting through cytochrome bc1 or kinase inhibition, though mechanisms for others remain unknown. The high hit rate suggests that preselecting compounds based on activity against gametocytes and liver stages in other Plasmodium species effectively identifies hypnozoite-active agents.

articles.headline: GABA receptor RDL controls malaria mosquito hearing and mating
articles.title_original: The GABA(A) receptor RDL modulates the auditory sensitivity of malaria mosquitoes.
articles.details: Researchers investigated how GABA modulates hearing in malaria mosquitoes through the GABAA receptor RDL, an insecticide resistance target. Using picrotoxin to antagonize RDL, they demonstrated multilevel auditory effects including anatomical changes to the ear and increased antennal nerve spontaneous firing, with effects partially dependent on octopamine signaling. These findings implicate RDL in controlling auditory sensitivity and mating in natural malaria mosquito populations, suggesting a potential mechanism for vector biology.

articles.headline: Potentially novel filarial species identified in Thai chickens with Plasmodium co-infection
articles.title_original: Discovery of potentially novel species of the Onchocercidae (Nematoda: Filarioidea) in Burmese fighting chickens (Gallus gallus): Genetic insights into avian filariasis and co-infection with Plasmodium juxtanucleare.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Molecular and microscopy screening of 12 Burmese fighting chickens in Nakhon Ratchasima Province, Thailand, revealed 2 carriers of onchocercid filarioids representing potentially novel species based on cox1 gene analysis and phylogenetic clustering. Plasmodium juxtanucleare was detected in 6 of 12 chickens, including one co-infected with filarioids. These findings highlight filarial diversity in avian hosts and identify emerging parasite hotspots in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Primaquine mass drug administration eliminates P. vivax in Myanmar trial
articles.title_original: Efficacy of Focal Primaquine Mass Administration for Eliminating Plasmodium vivax Malaria in Northern Myanmar: A Cluster-Randomized Trial.
articles.details: A cluster-randomized crossover trial in northern Myanmar evaluated focal primaquine mass drug administration (0.25 mg/kg/day for 14 days) in 541 and 667 participants across two groups. Clinical P. vivax incidence declined to zero within one month and remained absent for nearly a year. Subclinical P. vivax prevalence was significantly reduced, with adjusted odds ratios of 0.46 (95% CI: 0.25–0.83) in round 1 and 0.15 (95% CI: 0.03–0.65) in round 2. PQ-MDA was effective at reducing both clinical and subclinical P. vivax in a subtropical, low-endemicity setting.
articles.notable_reason: Shows primaquine mass drug administration efficacy for P. vivax elimination

articles.headline: Microhaplotype sequencing distinguishes P. vivax relapse from recrudescence
articles.title_original: Microhaplotype deep sequencing assays to capture Plasmodium vivax infection lineages.
articles.details: Researchers developed a high-throughput amplicon sequencing assay with 93 microhaplotype markers to determine identity-by-descent (IBD) between P. vivax isolates, enabling discrimination of recrudescence, reinfection, and relapse. Testing 745 global infections including 128 pairs from a randomized controlled trial showed low error (RMSE < 0.12) and strong geographic clustering. IBD analysis revealed lower frequencies of suspected relapses or recrudescence in patients treated with primaquine versus untreated, with greater impact when paired with chloroquine than artemether-lumefantrine.
articles.notable_reason: Enables genetic distinction of P. vivax relapse from other recurrence types

articles.headline: Tafenoquine disrupts parasite hemozoin synthesis via nonclassical pathways
articles.title_original: Tafenoquine Disrupts Intraerythrocytic Stages of Malaria Parasites by Inhibiting Nonclassical Pathways of β-Hematin Nucleation and Growth.
articles.details: This study elucidates how tafenoquine (FDA-approved 2018) inhibits malaria parasite survival by disrupting hemozoin crystal formation. Tafenoquine blocks a fraction of hemozoin growth sites and potently suppresses nonclassical crystal growth and nucleation by impairing formation of mesoscopic hematin-rich clusters. The drug exhibits stage-specific effects, with greater suppression of ring-stage parasites when hemozoin crystals nucleate than of trophozoites during crystal growth, in addition to disrupting mitochondrial function.

articles.headline: Penicillium compounds show strong in silico PfLDH inhibition potential
articles.title_original: Penicillium-Derived Inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum Lactate Dehydrogenase (PfLDH): A Computational Approach for Novel Antimalarial Therapy Development.
articles.details: Molecular docking and dynamics simulations identified three Penicillium-derived compounds as promising inhibitors of P. falciparum lactate dehydrogenase (PfLDH). Penicilactone B showed the strongest binding affinity (−8.71 kcal/mol) and lowest inhibitory constant (414.77 nM) with stable interactions over 200 ns of molecular dynamics. Off-target predictions suggested minimal interaction with human LDH, indicating potential selectivity. However, experimental validation, pharmacokinetic optimization, and efficacy testing against Plasmodium strains are needed.

articles.headline: Deep immune monitoring reveals compartment-specific dynamics in malaria ARDS
articles.title_original: Monitoring of deep lymphocyte phenotypes in the blood and bronchoalveolar lavage fluid of patients with severe malaria-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome.
articles.details: This case report describes longitudinal deep immune phenotyping in a severe malaria-associated acute respiratory distress syndrome patient supported by extracorporeal membrane oxygenation. Despite persistent lymphopenia, researchers observed dynamic shifts in T cells and rare innate lymphoid cell populations, with a type 2 immune profile predominating acutely. Blood and bronchoalveolar lavage fluid exhibited significantly different immune profiles, with decreased exhausted T-cell populations indicating recovery, demonstrating that multi-compartment immune monitoring can guide precision immune modulation.

articles.headline: Scoping review identifies social determinants of malaria in Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Malaria and determinants of health: a scoping review of malaria vulnerabilities in Southeast Asia.
articles.details: This scoping review synthesized 22 peer-reviewed studies from 2014–2024 examining malaria vulnerabilities in Southeast Asian populations, including cross-border and migrant groups. Using a social determinants of health lens, researchers identified five thematic domains influencing vulnerability: biological factors, demographic and socioeconomic parameters, built and lived environments, behavioral practices, and healthcare access. Findings underscore that SE Asian malaria vulnerability is multifaceted and requires region-specific, multi-sectoral interventions addressing these interrelated factors.

articles.headline: P. malariae SSU rRNA variant impairs nested PCR detection in Africa
articles.title_original: First report of a Plasmodium malariae SSU rRNA gene variant in Africa associated with reduced amplification by nested PCR.
articles.details: A 56-year-old Japanese man presented with fever two months after returning from Sierra Leone, with quartan malaria suspected on blood smear. Nested PCR confirmed P. malariae infection, but sequence analysis revealed a four-base deletion in the SSU rRNA gene within the species-specific reverse primer-binding site, causing fainter secondary PCR amplification. Cytochrome b sequencing confirmed P. malariae identity, highlighting that genetic variants may partially impair species identification by standard molecular diagnostic methods.

articles.headline: Machine learning identifies malaria hotspots and key social determinants globally
articles.title_original: Unraveling global malaria incidence and mortality using machine learning and artificial intelligence-driven spatial analysis.
articles.details: Analysis of 106 countries' malaria data from 2000–2022 using XGBoost machine learning and spatial analysis identified key geographical clusters. In 2022, 251.75 million malaria cases were recorded, with peak mortality of 99,554 deaths in 2020, concentrated in Nigeria (1.3 billion cases), Democratic Republic of Congo (623 million), and India (320 million). The XGBoost model achieved strong predictive performance (r² = 0.93, RMSE = 0.63) and identified sanitation access, electricity availability, population growth, and under-5 mortality as key determinants.

articles.headline: Pantothenate regulates mosquito reproduction with transgenerational effects
articles.title_original: Pantothenate regulates feeding and reproduction in the malaria vector Anopheles stephensi, with patterns dependent on supplementation scheme and parental nutrition.
articles.details: This study investigated pantothenate (vitamin B5) effects on the malaria vector Anopheles stephensi across multiple generations, using water or blood supplementation routes. Pantothenate regulated reproduction in concentration-, delivery-, and age-dependent ways, with the strongest effects on fecundity. Water-supplemented mothers' unsupplemented offspring showed increased fecundity, indicating transgenerational effects, whereas blood-supplemented mothers only affected their own and supplemented offspring. Findings suggest that targeting pantothenate-CoA biology in Anopheles could provide novel vector-control strategies.

articles.headline: P. vivax and P. falciparum gametocyte carriage risk factors identified
articles.title_original: Risk factor for gametocyte carriage and gametocytemia in Plasmodium vivax and Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: 2011-2020 microscopy study in Myanmar of 8,240 P. vivax patients (7,249 with gametocytes, 88%) and 510 P. falciparum cases (56 positive, 11%). For P. vivax, dry season, prior malaria history, fever, and parasite density associated with gametocyte carriage; density increased with asexual parasites (P<0.001). For P. falciparum, younger age and prior malaria associated with carriage. Polyclonal P. vivax infections had higher gametocyte densities than monoclonal (P<0.0001).

articles.headline: Vaccine models show 60-80% malaria incidence reduction with optimized regimens
articles.title_original: Performance characteristics and potential public health impact of improved pre-erythrocytic malaria vaccines targeting childhood burden.
articles.details: Individual-based transmission model assessed vaccine performance trade-offs using RTS,S/AS01 and R21/Matrix-M trial data. Vaccine with 90% initial efficacy against infection, 6-12 month half-life, co-administered with blood-stage drug and yearly boosters achieved 60-80% yearly incidence reduction in children, consistent with seasonal trial results. Sustained ≥35% incidence reduction for 12 months after vaccination cessation in low-transmission settings (PfPR2-10 <30%).

articles.headline: Correction to asymptomatic malaria detection study in Panama
articles.title_original: Correction: Detection through the use of RT-MqPCR of asymptomatic reservoirs of malaria in samples of patients from the indigenous Comarca of Guna Yala, Panama: Essential method to achieve the elimination of malaria.
articles.details: Correction to article DOI: 10.1371/journal.pone.0305558.

articles.headline: Review: GST inhibition proposed for artemisinin resistance prevention
articles.title_original: Targeting Plasmodium glutathione S-transferases to overcome antimalaria drug resistance in Southern Africa.
articles.details: Review examining glutathione S-transferases (GSTs), multifunctional detoxification enzymes contributing to antimalarial drug resistance. While artemisinin resistance not yet observed in Southern Africa, K13 mutations present in sub-Saharan Africa. Proposes GST inhibition as adjunct therapy and pre-emptive strategy to avert resistance emergence and consolidate malaria control.

articles.headline: Case: Hepatitis A with P. vivax triggers autoimmune hepatitis
articles.title_original: Hepatitis A-Induced Fulminant Hepatic Failure Linked to Autoimmune Hepatitis in a Child Amidst Diagnostic Challenges of Dengue and Malaria.
articles.authors: Bilal M, Ajmal MU, Ali A
articles.details: Five-year-old with HAV IgM-positive and P. vivax parasitemia, initially suspected dengue (serology negative), developed fulminant hepatic failure and hepatic encephalopathy. Managed conservatively with antibiotics, antimalarials, and supportive care. Persistent transaminitis prompted evaluation revealing ANA positivity (1:320, speckled) suggestive of type 1 autoimmune hepatitis, responsive to corticosteroids.

articles.headline: Case: Rickettsial infection triggered P. vivax relapse in traveler
articles.title_original: Acute Rickettsiosis Triggering Plasmodium vivax Relapse in a Returned Traveler: A Case Report and Clinical Review of Travel-Related Coinfections.
articles.details: Returned traveler from South America with confirmed concurrent rickettsial and P. vivax infection; rickettsial infection potentially triggered P. vivax relapse. Case highlights diagnostic pitfalls when rickettsial testing not performed following malaria confirmation, particularly with P. vivax or P. ovale where relapse risk is high. Emphasizes need for suspicion of tropical coinfections in returned febrile travelers.

articles.headline: Novel PX1 haplotype confers lumefantrine and artemisinin resistance in Uganda
articles.title_original: A novel locus associated with decreased susceptibility of Plasmodium falciparum to lumefantrine and dihydroartemisinin has emerged and spread in Uganda.
articles.details: Whole genome analysis identified novel phosphoinositide-binding protein gene (PX1) haplotype containing three SNPs (L1222P, M1701I, D1705N) and two indels (PIN) associated with decreased drug susceptibility. PIN-carrying parasites showed significantly decreased IC50s to lumefantrine, mefloquine, and dihydroartemisinin, with or without K13 mutations. Haplotype first detected in 2008, rapidly increased reaching >50% prevalence in northern Uganda by 2016 and eastern Uganda by 2023.
articles.notable_reason: Novel artemisinin/lumefantrine resistance locus emerging in Uganda

articles.headline: IPTp achieves high coverage, associated with lower malaria in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Intermittent preventive treatment, malaria, HIV, hepatitis and pregnancy outcomes in Nigerian women: a cross-sectional study in two healthcare facilities.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 134 pregnant women in southeastern Nigeria. IPTp use 73.9%. Malaria prevalence 5.97% (8/134), HIV 3.73%, hepatitis B 4.47%. Preeclampsia 3.73%, PROM 2.24%, preterm deliveries 5.2%. Lower malaria prevalence attributed to high IPTp implementation, supporting chemoprevention's role in reducing gestational malaria.

articles.headline: Avian Plasmodium and West Nile virus detected in Spanish wetland mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Vector surveillance of avian Plasmodium and West Nile virus in Culex mosquitoes from Doñana, a UNESCO World Heritage Site.
articles.authors: Martínez-de la Puente J, et al.
articles.details: Surveillance of 95 mosquito pools (1,376 females, 4 Culex species) from protected Spanish wetland. WNV detected in 5 Cx. perexiguus pools. Avian Plasmodium found in 28 pools (17 Cx. perexiguus, 11 Cx. pipiens) representing three lineages: P. vaughani (n=22), P. matutinum (n=4), unidentified Plasmodium sp. (n=1). No Dirofilaria detected. Expands knowledge of pathogen diversity in this protected habitat.

articles.headline: pfhrp2/3 gene deletions widespread in Ethiopia, threaten RDT sensitivity
articles.title_original: Deletion of target gene (histidine-rich protein 2/3) for Plasmodium falciparum rapid diagnostic tests in Amhara region, Ethiopia: a cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 1,232 microscopy-confirmed P. falciparum from 28 health facilities in Amhara, Ethiopia (2021-2022). Among 179 samples tested, 88.3% (158) carried pfhrp2 and/or pfhrp3 deletions: 51.4% dual deletion, 34.6% pfhrp3-only, 2.2% pfhrp2-only. Among 109 RDT false-negatives, 93.6% (102) were due to gene deletions. Regional deletion prevalence exceeds WHO 5% threshold; deletions found in all survey domains.

articles.headline: Community-led Bti achieves 96%+ Anopheles suppression in Gambia
articles.title_original: Empowering communities for malaria control: Effectiveness of community-led biolarviciding using Bacillus thuringiensis israelensis in The Gambia.
articles.details: Non-randomized controlled trial in Gambia (2023-2024) comparing community-led vs expert-supervised Bacillus thuringiensis var. israelensis (Bti) application to breeding sites. By round 10: community-led achieved 96.8% larval reduction, 97.4% pupal emergence reduction, 96.2% adult mosquito reduction; expert-supervised slightly higher (98.6%, 99.1%, 98.8%, p<0.001). Over 85% participant satisfaction; demonstrates scalability and sustainability of community-led approach.

articles.headline: Bayesian spatio-temporal analysis identifies emerging malaria hotspots in Cameroon
articles.title_original: Identifying hotspots of malaria incidence and mortality for tailored interventions in Cameroon using routine data from 2011 to 2021: A Bayesian space-time variability modeling.
articles.details: Bayesian space-time variability analysis of routine malaria data from 189 health districts in Cameroon (2011-2021). For uncomplicated malaria: 42.9% districts with decreasing trend, 44.9% increasing. For severe malaria and deaths: 37.6% and 32.8% with increasing trends, respectively. Identified 24.3% (46), 23.3% (44), and 21.7% (41) districts with high likelihood of becoming hotspots for uncomplicated, severe, and fatal malaria.

articles.headline: Lycopene attenuates malaria-induced oxidative stress in mice brain and lungs
articles.title_original: Lycopene Mitigates Malaria-Induced Reactive Oxygen and Nitrogen Species and Oxidative Damage in Mice Brain and Lungs.
articles.details: Mice infected with Plasmodium berghei received oral lycopene (3.11 mg/kg) or N-acetylcysteine (62 mg/kg) daily. Lycopene significantly reduced infection-induced increases in TBARS, uric acid, and nitric oxide (p<0.0001 vs untreated), normalizing levels to uninfected controls, suggesting therapeutic potential against malaria-related oxidative damage.

articles.headline: Biannual azithromycin reduces childhood malaria parasitemia in Niger
articles.title_original: Biannual Mass Azithromycin Distributions for Preschool Children and Malaria Parasitemia: A Secondary Analysis of the MORDOR Cluster Randomized Trial.
articles.details: Cluster RCT in Niger randomized 30 communities (1,695 children on azithromycin 20 mg/kg twice-yearly; 3,031 on placebo) with 5-year follow-up. At month 48, parasitemia was not significantly lower overall (-3.3 percentage points, p=0.05). Before seasonal malaria chemoprevention began at month 36, azithromycin showed significantly lower parasitemia (-4.8 percentage points, p=0.02), indicating diminishing effect over time.

articles.headline: Fluctuating temperature and humidity alter malaria vector immunity
articles.title_original: Fluctuating Warm and Humid Conditions Differentially Impact Immunity and Development in the Malaria Vector Anopheles stephensi.
articles.details: Anopheles stephensi exposed from egg to adult to elevated fluctuating conditions (+4°C and +10% relative humidity) showed enhanced basal immune responses and accelerated development. Post-bacterial challenge conditions led to immune suppression, potentially enhancing Plasmodium susceptibility. Elevated fluctuations also altered transcriptional activity of transgene promoters in genetically modified vectors.

articles.headline: Behavioral health interventions enhance community malaria prevention
articles.title_original: Behavioral health interventions in malaria control: Efficacy and implementation.
articles.authors: Obeagu EI, Obeagu GU, Iduh MU
articles.details: This review examines behavioral health interventions including community education, behavioral change communication, and community-based strategies for malaria control. Community education improved insecticide-treated net use and early treatment-seeking, while community-based approaches enhanced ownership and reduced malaria prevalence. Implementation challenges include cultural barriers, resource limitations, and inadequate monitoring frameworks.

articles.headline: Plastic pollution alters malaria vector gut microbiota composition
articles.title_original: The Effect of Larval Exposure to Plastic Pollution on the Gut Microbiota of the Major Malaria Vector Anopheles arabiensis Patton (Diptera: Culicidae).
articles.details: Anopheles arabiensis larvae exposed to degraded plastic and additives (phthalic acid, Bisphenol-A) showed altered beta-diversity and increased bacteria associated with plastic degradation and pesticide metabolism. Inflammation-associated bacterial genera increased in both insecticide-selected and unselected strains, suggesting immune modulation, though no change in Plasmodium-protective bacterial genera was observed.

articles.headline: Malaria prevalence 8% in Tanzanian children; sharp geographic disparities
articles.title_original: Prevalence and determinants of malaria among children aged 6-59 months in Tanzania: a nationwide cross-sectional study.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 2022 DHS/MIS data from 4,923 children aged 6-59 months found 8% malaria prevalence (95% CI 6.2%-9.5%). Risk factors included age 24-59 months (aOR=1.71), rural residence (aOR=6.92), lower economic status (aOR=3.08), and no insecticide-treated net use (aOR=2.73). Highest regional prevalence in Tabora (23%) with <1% in several southern regions.

articles.headline: Malaria risk in Tanzania declined 10-11% from 2016-2023
articles.title_original: Bayesian spatio-temporal modeling and prediction of malaria cases in Tanzania mainland (2016-2023): unveiling associations with climate and intervention factors.
articles.details: Bayesian spatio-temporal analysis of 2016-2023 monthly malaria data (23.4 million cases ≥5 years; 17.3 million <5 years) found malaria risk decreased 11% in children <5 years and 10% in those ≥5 years. Long-lasting insecticide nets reduced risk by 1.2% (<5 years) and 7% (≥5 years). Climate factors including minimum temperature, wind speed, and vegetation index were associated with increased malaria risk.

articles.headline: Asymptomatic malaria associated with impaired growth in infants
articles.title_original: Impact of asymptomatic malaria infection on children's growth in rural Malawi.
articles.details: Among 840 children aged 6-18 months in rural Malawi, those with asymptomatic P. falciparum infection had lower weight-for-age Z-scores (-1.03 vs -0.87, 95% CI -0.17 to -0.04) and weight-for-length Z-scores (-0.03 vs -0.13, 95% CI -0.22 to -0.06) than uninfected peers. Association remained significant after adjusting for confounders, suggesting asymptomatic malaria impairs childhood growth.

articles.headline: CYP2B6*6 polymorphism delays artemether-lumefantrine parasite clearance
articles.title_original: CYP2B6*6 single nucleotide polymorphism among patients with uncomplicated malaria in Adjumani district, Uganda: Implications on efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine.
articles.details: Among 100 Ugandan patients with P. falciparum malaria treated with artemether-lumefantrine, CYP2B6*6 variant allele frequency was 0.37 (43% GG, 40% GT, 17% TT). Heterozygous (GT) patients showed 10-fold slower parasite clearance than homozygous patients (p=0.019), with day 3 microscopy positivity 37.5% (GT) vs 14% (GG) and 17.6% (TT). Host genetic factors should be incorporated into therapeutic efficacy studies.

articles.headline: JAK inhibitor ruxolitinib rescues malaria-induced hypoglycemia in mice
articles.title_original: JAK/STAT inhibition protects glucocorticoid receptor knockout mice from lethal malaria-induced hypoglycemia and hyperinflammation.
articles.details: Glucocorticoid receptor knockout mice infected with Plasmodium chabaudi developed lethal hypoglycemia driven by hepatic and splenic metabolic dysfunction and JAK/STAT pathway overactivation. JAK1/2 inhibitor ruxolitinib significantly improved survival by preventing hypoglycemia and suppressing cytokine storm, revealing a link between glucocorticoid signaling, STAT3 activation, and glucose metabolism in severe malaria.

articles.headline: Apicoplast transporters represent novel targets for malaria drug development
articles.title_original: The apicoplast transportome of the malaria parasite.
articles.authors: Akkerman LJ, Kooij TWA, de Vries LE
articles.details: This review discusses the apicoplast transportome comprising 11 confirmed and 17 putative transporters that provide substrates and export products for isopentenyl pyrophosphate and coenzyme A biosynthesis in Plasmodium parasites. These transporters are proposed as excellent novel drug targets, with particular interest in identifying IPP and CoA exporters.

articles.headline: Fighting malaria as a patient and scientist
articles.title_original: Fighting malaria as a patient and scientist.
articles.details: No abstract provided.

articles.headline: Statistical methods for analyzing mosquito Plasmodium infections
articles.title_original: When a mean can be meaningless: evaluating mosquito infections with Plasmodium parasites.
articles.details: Methodological review advocating for generalized linear and mixed models to analyze parasite infection data in mosquitoes. Methods address non-normal distributions and datasets with zero or near-zero prevalence counts, particularly useful for evaluating transmission-blocking interventions. Approaches broadly applicable across parasitic infections.

articles.headline: ART-heme adduct identified as biomarker for artemisinin activity and resistance
articles.title_original: Pharmacokinetics and metabolism of artemisinin (ART) in Plasmodium yoelii: ART-heme adduct as a potential biomarker for its resistance.
articles.journal: Int J Parasitol Drugs Drug Resist
articles.details: Study in an ART-resistant P. yoelii strain (iPy) developed via prolonged ART pressure found 2.9-fold higher ED90 compared to wild-type. ART-heme adduct was the major metabolite. Resistant iPy parasites showed 61.1% of wild-type iRBC-normalized ART-heme exposure after oral dosing. ART-heme adduct proposed as biomarker for antimalarial activity and resistance.

articles.headline: Hematology analyzers detect infected RBCs for early malaria diagnosis in Korea
articles.title_original: [Pilot Project for Early Diagnosis of Malaria Infection Using a Hematology Analyzer].
articles.details: Pilot project across 47 medical institutions in Korea tested hematology analyzer detection of infected RBCs for early malaria diagnosis. Among 7.27 million hematologic analyses (April-October 2024), 1,359 iRBC cases were detected, 499 referred for confirmation, and 239 confirmed malaria-positive. Demonstrates feasibility of early detection using existing medical infrastructure.

articles.headline: Plasmodium brasilianum detected in wild primate in Brazil
articles.title_original: Molecular Surveillance of Plasmodium spp. Infection in Neotropical Primates from Bahia and Minas Gerais, Brazil.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of 298 blood and tissue samples from 10 primate species across 47 municipalities in Bahia and Minas Gerais. Only 1 sample (0.3%) tested positive for P. brasilianum/P. malariae using nested PCR of 18S SSU rRNA gene. This represents the first detection in a free-living Callithrix geoffroyi in Brazil, highlighting continued need for surveillance of zoonotic transmission risk.

articles.headline: Glucose-functionalized DHA nanoparticles enhance antimalarial drug targeting
articles.title_original: Glucose-functionalized redox-responsive dihydroartemisinin prodrug nanosystem for targeted malaria therapy.
articles.details: Researchers developed a glucose-functionalized redox-responsive dihydroartemisinin prodrug nanosystem (D@GLU-PMs-SS) enhancing erythrocyte uptake via GLUT-mediated transport and activated by intracellular glutathione to release free DHA. In vitro experiments demonstrated enhanced parasite uptake, reduced glutathione levels in Plasmodium, and growth inhibition while maintaining cell safety. Warrants further investigation in clinical trials.

articles.headline: Microfluidic sensor detects P. falciparum-induced RBC deformability at single-cell level
articles.title_original: Measuring Asymmetric Ionic Current Waveform Through Micropores for Detecting Reduced Red Blood Cell Deformability Due to Plasmodium falciparum Infection.
articles.details: Researchers developed a microfluidic device measuring ionic current waveforms through micropores to assess RBC deformability at single-cell level. Using in vitro P. falciparum culture, decreased RBC deformability produced asymmetric waveforms. Computer simulations suggest waveform parameters are largely independent of RBC size and represent a reliable indicator of malaria-associated deformability reduction.

articles.headline: PfSDP antigen elicits short-lived antibody response with modest invasion inhibition
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Subtilisin-like Domain-Containing Protein (PfSDP), a Cross-Stage Antigen, Elicits Short-Lived Antibody Response Following Natural Infection with Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: In silico screening identified Plasmodium falciparum subtilisin-like domain-containing protein (PfSDP), found conserved across continents and expressed in all parasite developmental stages. Anti-PfSDP peptide antibodies inhibited erythrocyte invasion by 20-60% in dose-dependent manner. The protein is immunogenic in children from endemic areas, with antibody levels peaking at day 7 post-treatment then gradually declining. Modest invasion inhibition suggests additional roles in parasite development.

articles.headline: Outcrossing confers transmission advantage in P. falciparum through faster maturation
articles.title_original: Advantages of outcrossing in Plasmodium falciparum: insights from genetic crosses using fluorescent labelled parasites.
articles.details: Researchers crossed fluorescently-labeled P. falciparum strains (NF54/mCherry and NHP4026/GFP) and scored genotypes of 8540 oocysts from 435 mosquitoes sampled 7-14 days post-infection. Outcrossed oocysts showed decreasing proportions and increasing inbreeding over time, indicating faster maturation and transmission advantage of outcrossed parasites. This likely permits removal of deleterious mutations accumulated during asexual replication, maximizing recombination in transmitted populations.

articles.headline: ENABLE 1.5 protocol: prospective cohort studying Lassa fever and malaria coinfection
articles.title_original: Prospective cohort study to evaluate Lassa fever incidence, symptoms and coinfection with malaria in West Africa: the Enable Lassa Research Programme ('ENABLE 1.5') - study protocol.
articles.details: ENABLE 1.5 is a prospective cohort study protocol across 5 West African sites (Liberia, Nigeria, Sierra Leone) recruiting 5000 participants stratified by age groups (0-5, 6-10, 11-17, 18-50, >50 years) with 12-month follow-up. The study will assess symptomatic RT-PCR-confirmed Lassa fever incidence, including coinfection with malaria, addressing gaps in disease burden understanding for future vaccine trials.

articles.headline: Abbott RDT shows high sensitivity for P. falciparum in Central Cameroon
articles.title_original: Malaria infection prevalence and diagnostic performance of the Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/Pan rapid test in rural populations of Central Cameroon.
articles.details: Cross-sectional surveys in Central Cameroon (March 2022, May 2024) assessed Abbott Bioline Malaria Ag P.f/Pan RDT performance in 64.9-71.7% malaria prevalence population with P. falciparum predominant (97.48%). Using microscopy as reference, RDT showed sensitivity 93.37%, specificity 68.50%, positive predictive value 84.43%, negative predictive value 85.71%, with moderate agreement (k=0.652). RDT demonstrated higher sensitivity and accuracy than microscopy compared to qPCR.

articles.headline: Diverse artemisinin and multidrug resistance markers detected in Zambia
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Genomic Surveillance Reveals a Diversity of Kelch 13 Mutations in Zambia.
articles.details: Genomic surveillance of 2486 P. falciparum samples from 61 health facilities across all 10 Zambian provinces (March-July 2023) identified artemisinin partial-resistance markers: kelch 13 P574L (0.66% adjusted national prevalence) and P441L (1.39%). Multidrug resistance protein 1 N86 found in all samples; sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance marker K540E at 93.26%. Distribution heterogeneous, with some facilities reporting >5% markers and up to 46% in certain instances.

articles.headline: Deep learning emulates agent-based malaria transmission model for calibration
articles.title_original: Multitask deep learning for the emulation and calibration of an agent-based malaria transmission model.
articles.authors: Mondal A, Anirudh R, Selvaraj P
articles.details: Researchers developed a multitask deep learning approach to emulate the EMOD agent-based malaria transmission model. A neural network trained on large simulation suites captured relationships between immunological parameters and epidemiological outcomes including age-stratified incidence and prevalence across 8 sub-Saharan African study sites. The trained emulator was used with parameter estimation techniques to calibrate the underlying model to reference data.

articles.headline: Egypt's malaria-free certification achieved through integrated elimination
articles.title_original: Comparative analysis of Egypt's malaria elimination strategies and implementation science: Pathways to achieve malaria-free status for other African countries.
articles.details: Implementation science study analyzing Egypt's malaria elimination program (2004-2024), which achieved WHO malaria-free certification in October 2024. Integrated interventions (ITNs, IRS, antimalarial treatment, education) in endemic regions targeting vulnerable groups achieved 96% malaria incidence reduction over 15 years, 94% reduction in Anopheles mosquito density, 98% case detection within 48 hours, and 91% treatment coverage. Community awareness reached 84%; cost per DALY averted was $24; $1.5 billion saved in healthcare costs and productivity losses.

articles.headline: mRNA-LNP vaccine protects mice from P. vivax sporozoite challenge
articles.title_original: mRNA-LNP vaccine encoding the Plasmodium vivax circumsporozoite protein is highly immunogenic and confers protection in mice.
articles.details: Nucleoside-modified mRNA-LNP vaccines encoding P. vivax circumsporozoite protein (CSP) were tested in mice. The construct with wild-type signal peptide and GPI anchor induced significantly higher anti-CSP antibodies than SP-only construct. Intradermal delivery produced superior dose-dependent responses; delayed third intramuscular dose 5 months after the second generated significantly enhanced anti-repeat region antibodies and T cell responses. This dosing strategy conferred strong protection against sporozoite challenge, with antibody magnitude and avidity linked to protection.

articles.headline: CD44 cross-linking enhances P. falciparum RBC invasion pathway
articles.title_original: CD44 cross-linking promotes Plasmodium falciparum invasion.
articles.details: Forward genetic screening identified CD44 as a host factor for P. falciparum RBC invasion. Anti-CD44 antibody BRIC 222 significantly promotes invasion through CD44 cross-linking, which alters RBC cytoskeletal phosphorylation and increases accessibility of surface proteins including Basigin (essential invasion receptor). The parasite ligand EBA-175 similarly enhances invasion via CD44 engagement. Both BRIC 222 and EBA-175 increase binding of the essential PfRH5/PCRCR invasion complex to Basigin.

articles.headline: No artemisinin-resistant Pfkelch13 mutations in Western Kenya
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum parasites harbouring chloroquine-resistant but not artemisinin-resistant alleles in Busia County, Western Kenya.
articles.details: Molecular epidemiological survey of 118 P. falciparum samples from Busia County, Western Kenya, using deep amplicon sequencing. Pfkelch13 mutations were found in 5.1% (6/118), all carrying only A578S variant, which does not confer artemisinin resistance. Pfcrt mutations occurred in 11% (62% with CVIET triple mutant); 98% carried dhfr mutations; all samples had dhps mutations (84% single A437G). Absence of validated Pfkelch13 markers associated with artemisinin resistance was confirmed, though sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine-sensitive parasites remain rare.

articles.headline: VLP vaccine targeting L9 epitope achieves sterilizing immunity in mice
articles.title_original: Virus-like particle vaccines targeting a key epitope in circumsporozoite protein provide sterilizing immunity against malaria.
articles.details: Virus-like particle (VLP) vaccines displaying the L9 epitope from P. falciparum circumsporozoite protein were engineered using Qβ and MS2 bacteriophage scaffolds. MS2 L9 VLPs with a novel adjuvant elicited strong, durable anti-CSP antibody responses providing protection from liver-stage infection in mice. A combination vaccine of MS2 L9 and Qβ L9 VLPs, each presenting the L9 epitope in distinct structural contexts, achieved sterilizing immunity in the highest percentage of mice compared to single-scaffold approaches and matched RTS,S/AS01E protection.

articles.headline: EaveTubes reduce malaria vectors 75% during peak season
articles.title_original: The In2Care® EaveTubes improved protection against malaria vectors in a small-scale field study at Kagera Sugar Ltd, Missenyi, Tanzania.
articles.details: Small-scale field study (October 2019–July 2020) installed 350 In2Care EaveTubes in 135 structures at a sugar plantation in Tanzania. EaveTubes significantly reduced indoor anopheline mosquito densities by 58% overall and 75% during peak season, with no insecticide degradation or loss of mosquitocidal efficacy over 6 months. Malaria incidence declined 25.6% in the intervention camp. Findings suggest EaveTubes may complement existing vector control tools in areas facing insecticide resistance.

articles.headline: Hair analysis detects lumefantrine weeks post-malaria treatment
articles.title_original: Can hair concentrations of artemether-lumefantrine be used as a tool to retrospectively determine drug exposure during malaria treatment?
articles.details: Proof-of-concept study collected hair samples from 6 P. falciparum malaria patients 4 weeks after artemether-lumefantrine (AL) treatment, with AL cure rates >95% and recrudescence occurring in 2–14% of cases (often linked to inadequate lumefantrine exposure). LC-MS/MS analysis detected lumefantrine and its metabolite desbutyl-lumefantrine in all samples (quantifiable in 5); artemether and dihydroartemisinin were undetectable. No participants developed recrudescence. Hair analysis may retrospectively assess lumefantrine exposure in recrudescent cases.

articles.headline: Pakistan emphasizes ITN and IRS in malaria vector control
articles.title_original: Malaria vector control strategies in Pakistan: a scoping review.
articles.details: Scoping review synthesized 46 articles on malaria vector control strategies in Pakistan through September 2023. WHO-recommended interventions included insecticide-treated nets (ITNs, 29.79%) and indoor residual spraying (IRS, 29.79%), with lower use of spatial spraying (12.77%), larval source management, spatial/airborne repellents, and house modifications. Strategies varied by province: Khyber Pakhtunkhwa primarily used ITNs (55%) while Punjab relied on IRS (50%). ITN use increased over time.

articles.headline: Diagnostic sensitivity reduces test-negative vaccine trial statistical power
articles.title_original: Power and sample size considerations for test-negative design with bias correction: a case study on the world first malaria vaccine.
articles.authors: Ko YK, Alfvén T, Yoneoka D
articles.details: Monte Carlo simulations evaluated how bias correction for imperfect diagnostic tests affects statistical power and sample size in test-negative design vaccine effectiveness studies using a malaria vaccine example. With six vaccination status categories and assumed VE <50%, bias-corrected VE estimates were unbiased but had wider confidence intervals. At 80% test sensitivity, only 3 of 6 vaccination groups achieved 80% power with n=10,000, whereas n=6,000 sufficed with perfect testing, demonstrating diagnostic imperfection substantially reduces trial power.

articles.headline: PfGrp170 ER chaperone exhibits nucleotide-sensitive protein folding
articles.title_original: Biochemical and biophysical characterization of Plasmodium falciparum glucose regulated protein 170.
articles.details: Study characterized PfGrp170 (ER Hsp110 homologue), a P. falciparum protein essential for parasite survival and likely important for exporting parasite proteins to host cytosol. Recombinant PfGrp170 displayed predominantly α-helical structure with moderate thermal stability (Tm >55°C) and basal ATPase activity. ATP binding perturbed structural stability and increased heat sensitivity. The protein exhibited nucleotide-independent ability to suppress heat-induced substrate aggregation, with molecular docking showing selective ATP/ADP binding and greater ATP-induced structural responsiveness.

articles.headline: Deep learning achieves 99.24% accuracy in malaria parasite classification
articles.title_original: MalNet-DAF: Dual-Attentive Fusion Deep Learning Model for Malaria Parasite Classification.
articles.details: MalNet-DAF, a deep learning architecture combining stacked convolutional neural networks and bidirectional LSTM with spatial and temporal attention modules, was trained and validated on a public NIH malaria microscopy dataset for parasite classification. The dual-attentive fusion model achieved 99.24% classification accuracy, outperforming traditional baseline approaches, and may support real-time clinical decision-making for malaria diagnosis.

articles.headline: Bangladesh achieves high LLIN coverage; gaps persist in refugee camps
articles.title_original: Assessing long-lasting insecticidal net coverage, access, and use: Insights from malaria-endemic regions and Rohingya camps in Bangladesh.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 1,575 households in five malaria-endemic Bangladeshi districts and 10 FDMN refugee camps (May–October 2023) assessed LLIN coverage, access, and use. Among Bangladeshi households, 97.6% owned ≥1 LLIN with 93.2% sufficient coverage; 96.4% used LLINs previous night, and 95% of pregnant women and 98.3% of children <5 used LLINs, surpassing WHO's 80% threshold. In FDMN camps, despite 98.2% ownership, only 44.3% had sufficient coverage and 65.7% used LLINs, primarily due to inadequate LLIN supply.

articles.headline: Severe P. falciparum malaria causes rapid multiorgan failure
articles.title_original: Severe Malaria Due to Plasmodium falciparum in an Immunocompetent Young Adult: Rapid Progression to Multiorgan Failure.
articles.authors: Sanclemente-Cardoza V, Payán-Salcedo HA, Estela-Zape JL
articles.details: Case report of 21-year-old immunocompetent male from Colombia with P. falciparum presenting with severe respiratory distress requiring intubation and developing pulseless electrical activity. Progressed to multiorgan failure including ARDS with pulmonary edema, refractory distributive shock, acute kidney injury with severe hyperkalemia, and consumptive thrombocytopenia despite aggressive management including mechanical ventilation, vasopressor support, and platelet transfusions. Patient died after second cardiac arrest.

articles.headline: NR2F6 knockout protects mice from experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: NR2F6 as a Disease Driver and Candidate Therapeutic Target in Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.details: Experimental cerebral malaria model using P. berghei ANKA-infected mice. Nr2f6-deficient mice showed significant survival benefit with markedly reduced endotheliopathy, blood-brain barrier damage, sequestered parasites, and brain-infiltrating cytotoxic T lymphocytes compared to wild-type. Results indicate NR2F6 is a non-redundant driver of cerebral malaria pathology, suggesting pharmacological NR2F6 inhibition could protect BBB integrity.

articles.headline: Three Anopheles species identified as deer malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Incriminating vectors of deer malaria (Plasmodium odocoilei) at a Florida deer farm.
articles.details: Mosquito survey at Florida deer farm with documented P. odocoilei transmission. Four mosquito species specialized in white-tailed deer feeding (≥75% blood meals). An. quadrimaculatus showed highest P. odocoilei infection rate at 4.1%, followed by An. punctipennis at 3.1%, and An. crucians at 0.47%. An. quadrimaculatus and An. punctipennis met vector incrimination criteria with high anthropophily (>83%) and detectable infection rates.

articles.headline: Mining identified as dominant risk factor for urban malaria
articles.title_original: Emergence of urban malaria and the associated risk factors: a case-control study in Mutare city, Zimbabwe.
articles.details: Case-control study in Mutare, Zimbabwe (2022–2023) of confirmed malaria cases (N=7,222). Artisanal mining showed strongest association (RR 22 [95% CI 10.7–44.1], p<0.001); household proximity to water/wells (RR 16 [3.8–67.5], p<0.001) and travel to endemic areas (RR 9 [5.2–14.4], p<0.001) also significant. Males exhibited 4-fold higher odds of malaria than females.

articles.headline: RTS,S malaria vaccine achieves 80% first-dose coverage
articles.title_original: Subnational introduction of the RTS,S/AS01(E) malaria vaccine into routine immunization: experience and lessons from the three pilot countries.
articles.details: Implementation of RTS,S/AS01E vaccine across Ghana, Kenya, and Malawi (2021–2023). First-dose coverage reached approximately 80% and third-dose approximately 75% by 2023 in initial pilot areas; fourth-dose coverage remained low at median 46% across three countries. Key lessons identified included importance of clear stakeholder roles, appropriate communication and demand generation, flexible scheduling, and improved data collection tools.

articles.headline: Larvicide reduces malaria vectors and transmission in Africa
articles.title_original: Heterogeneous impacts for malaria control from larviciding across villages and considerations for monitoring and evaluation.
articles.details: Individual-based transmission model simulating larvicide + ITN trials from Western Kenya and Côte d'Ivoire. Entomological monitoring showed 82–88% reductions in Anopheles density in Kenya and 82% in Côte d'Ivoire. Model predicted 48–72% malaria prevalence reduction in children aged 6 months–10 years in Kenya and 11–78% all-age clinical incidence reduction in Côte d'Ivoire, though impacts varied substantially between villages.

articles.headline: Plasmepsin gene copies modulate piperaquine resistance mechanism
articles.title_original: The plasmepsin-piperaquine paradox persists in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: CRISPR-generated single and double knockouts of plasmepsin II and III in Cambodian P. falciparum strains with varying plasmepsin copy numbers. Deletion of plasmepsin II and/or III increased parasite sensitivity to piperaquine. Increased plasmepsin copy numbers contribute to piperaquine resistance likely through digestive vacuole pH modulation rather than increased hemoglobin digestion or protease activity.

articles.headline: P. berghei infection reduces cutaneous leishmaniasis in mice
articles.title_original: Impact of co-infection with Plasmodium berghei ANKA in Leishmania major-parasitized mice on immune modulation and cutaneous leishmaniasis.
articles.details: C57BL/6 mice co-infected with P. berghei ANKA and L. major. P. berghei infection reduced L. major lesion size and dermal parasite burden, associated with elevated serum TNF and reduced infiltration of activated T cells and inflammatory monocytes at infection sites compared to L. major single infection. Findings suggest cross-regulation of immune responses in parasitic co-infections.

articles.headline: Malaria hotspot linked to infective mosquito anthropophily
articles.title_original: The spatial heterogeneity of malaria transmission: An entomological investigation in a highly endemic setting of Burkina Faso.
articles.journal: Curr Res Parasitol Vector Borne Dis
articles.details: Entomological survey in Goden village, Burkina Faso. An. coluzzii constituted 79% of An. gambiae s.l. (n=1,492). Human blood index was 45%, sporozoite rate 10%, and 43% of human blood meals contained parasites. Infective mosquitoes exhibited 1.8-fold higher anthropophily than non-infective. Spatial clustering analysis revealed hotspot in northwestern area with higher human-vector contact and parasite acquisition rates.

articles.headline: Polycyclitol compounds restore memory in cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Polycyclitol Derivatives Restore Long- Term Memory Via cdk5/p25 Activation of Tau Signaling in Experimental Cerebral Malaria.
articles.details: Experimental cerebral malaria treated with artemether plus four polycyclic derivatives (SR4-01–04). SR4-02 and SR4-04 showed significant improvements in Barnes maze long-term memory and short-term memory compared to artemether alone, with reduced hippocampal and cortical phospho-tau (Ser396) and enhanced neuronal arborization in CA1, CA3, and cortex. Western blotting confirmed reduced Cdk5-p25-mediated tau phosphorylation.

articles.headline: Physics-informed neural networks improve malaria transmission models
articles.title_original: Analysis of a mathematical model for malaria using data-driven approach.
articles.authors: Rajnarayanan A, Kumar M, Tridane A
articles.details: Novel SIR-SI compartmental model with temperature and altitude-dependent transmission functions. Compared ANNs, RNNs, and physics-informed neural networks (PINNs) for parameter estimation; PINNs outperformed purely data-driven approaches by embedding epidemiological dynamics into training. Implemented Dynamic Mode Decomposition to derive data-driven transmission risk index from infection trajectories for real-time risk assessment.

articles.headline: Bivalent vaccine protects against P. falciparum and vivax
articles.title_original: Malaria bivalent viral vectored vaccine protects against Plasmodium falciparum and vivax and blocks parasite transmission.
articles.details: Heterologous prime-boost with vaccinia virus LC16m8Δ and AAV1 vectors expressing Pfs25-PfCSP and Pvs25-PvCSP fusion proteins. Provided 70% protection against both P. falciparum and P. vivax transgenic sporozoites. Membrane feeding assay with P. vivax patient isolates from Brazilian Amazon showed 90% transmission-blocking efficacy. Immune responses maintained for over 7 months.

articles.headline: Mathematical model: asymptomatic infections drive 30% of malaria transmission
articles.title_original: Impact of asymptomatic infections on malaria transmission dynamics.
articles.authors: Haringo AT, Obsu LL, Bushu FK
articles.details: Deterministic mathematical model examining malaria spread with focus on asymptomatic infections. Analysis of basic reproduction number (R₀) using next-generation matrix method revealed that asymptomatic cases contribute approximately 30% to R₀ while symptomatic cases account for 70%, with backward bifurcation identified. Model emphasizes need for integrated control strategies targeting both symptomatic and asymptomatic malaria cases.

articles.headline: Zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi, cynomolgi, inui detected in Assamese macaques
articles.title_original: First molecular detection of zoonotic Plasmodium knowlesi, Plasmodium cynomolgi, and Plasmodium inui in Assamese macaques from northern Thailand.
articles.details: Molecular surveillance of 133 blood samples from Assamese macaques in Chiang Rai Province, Thailand using semi-nested PCR targeting 18S rRNA. Plasmodium spp. detected in 32 samples (24.06%, 95% CI 17.07-32.23): P. knowlesi 13.53% (18/133), P. cynomolgi 9.77% (13/133), P. inui 0.75% (1/133). First molecular evidence of these zoonotic species in this macaque population; findings highlight role as natural reservoirs and need for surveillance to prevent human spillover.

articles.headline: Tephrosia villosa root extract exhibits antimalarial activity in mice
articles.title_original: Antimalarial activity of root extract of Tephrosia villosa L. Pers. (Fabaceae) on Plasmodium berghei-infected mice.
articles.details: Dichloromethane/methanol extract of T. villosa roots tested in P. berghei-infected mice at 100, 200, 400 mg/kg doses. At 400 mg/kg: suppressed parasitemia 24.78% (suppressive test), 52.16% (curative), 49.09% (prophylactic test, p<0.001). Extract was non-toxic at 2 g/kg acute dose and prevented packed cell volume reduction and survival time loss. Results support in vivo efficacy complementing prior in vitro findings.

articles.headline: XGBoost outperforms machine learning models for malaria detection
articles.title_original: Comparative analysis of machine learning models for malaria detection using validated synthetic data: a cost-sensitive approach with clinical domain knowledge integration.
articles.details: Comparison of five machine learning algorithms (Naive Bayes, Logistic Regression, Random Forest, XGBoost, Enhanced Bayesian Logistic Regression) using validated synthetic dataset (87% representativeness against Sub-Saharan African clinical benchmarks). XGBoost achieved best AUC of 0.956 (95% CI 0.952-0.961) with 2.8% improvement over Random Forest; Enhanced Bayesian Logistic Regression achieved comparable AUC 0.954 (95% CI 0.950-0.959). McNemar test showed significant differences between XGBoost and Random Forest; authors emphasize real-world clinical validation remains essential.

articles.headline: Drug discovery platform for P. falciparum transmission-blocking compounds
articles.title_original: An all-in-one pipeline for the in vitro discovery and in vivo testing of Plasmodium falciparum malaria transmission blocking drugs.
articles.details: Integrated discovery and validation platform using engineered NF54/iGP1_RE9Hulg8 parasites expressing red-shifted firefly luciferase for stage V gametocyte viability assessment. Includes robust in vitro screening assay for gametocytocidal compounds and preclinical in vivo model using humanized NODscidIL2Rγnull mice inoculated with pure stage V gametocytes. Combines bioluminescence imaging with mosquito feeding assays to systematically evaluate transmission-blocking drug efficacy and pharmacodynamic profiles.

articles.headline: South Korea malaria vector surveillance shows 67% increase in mosquito density
articles.title_original: [Malaria Vector Surveillance in 2024].
articles.details: 2024 surveillance of 76 sites in malaria-risk areas of Republic of Korea from April-October. Cumulative trap index reached 120.6, representing 66.6% increase from 2023 and 59.3% increase from 2020-2022 average. Malaria-positive mosquitoes detected in 3 pools (MIR=0.15) at weeks 31, 32, 34 in Paju-si. Mosquito population peak preceded malaria case increases by 5-6 weeks. Authors note vector surveillance essential for malaria elimination.

articles.headline: DRAG2 nanopore sequencing assay for malaria parasite molecular surveillance
articles.title_original: An expanded method for malaria parasite genetic surveillance using targeted nanopore sequencing.
articles.details: Updated multiplex nanopore-based method (DRAG2) for P. falciparum molecular surveillance incorporating expanded drug resistance markers, msp2 polymorphic surface antigen, and 18S rRNA for species detection. Assessed performance across parasitemia ranges and sample types (venous blood, dried blood spots). Includes quality control using engineered synthetic plasmids with test SNPs (drug resistance markers) and control SNPs for contamination detection. Paper provides standard operating procedures for laboratory implementation.

articles.headline: Learning curves determine training sample size for malaria prediction models
articles.title_original: How to use learning curves to evaluate the sample size for malaria prediction models developed using machine learning algorithms.
articles.authors: Zaloumis SG, Rajasekhar M, Simpson JA
articles.details: Tutorial on using learning curves to assess sample size requirements for machine learning prediction models of malaria outcomes. Example using simulated P. falciparum gene expression data (1043 isolates, 29% slow clearing infections) to predict artemisinin resistance status. For sPLSDA+SVMs: balanced error rate improved from 50% (20 samples) to 14% (835 samples); for random forests: 50% to 22%. Authors conclude increasing training size beyond 835 samples unlikely to significantly reduce error further.

articles.headline: Ivermectin against malaria: emerging evidence and opportunities
articles.title_original: Ivermectin against Malaria - Good News in Bad Times.
articles.details: No abstract provided; based on title only. Commentary or editorial piece on ivermectin's potential role in malaria control.

articles.headline: Ivermectin reduces malaria incidence 26% versus albendazole control
articles.title_original: Ivermectin to Control Malaria - A Cluster-Randomized Trial.
articles.details: Cluster-randomized trial in Kwale County, Kenya among 28,932 participants across 84 clusters. Children aged 5-15 received mass administration of ivermectin 400 μg/kg or albendazole 400 mg (control) monthly for 3 consecutive months. At 6-month follow-up, malaria incidence was 2.20 per child-year (ivermectin) versus 2.66 (albendazole); adjusted incidence rate ratio 0.74 (95% CI 0.58-0.95, p=0.02). Serious adverse events did not differ significantly (IRR 0.63, 95% CI 0.21-1.91).

articles.headline: Emergency cesarean delivery in undiagnosed malaria: anesthetic management
articles.title_original: Emergent Cesarean Section Due to Malaria: A Perioperative Challenge.
articles.details: Case report of woman in early 30s at 39 weeks gestation with undiagnosed malaria presenting with fetal distress requiring emergency cesarean section. Patient developed malarial symptoms 5 months after returning from Haiti. Rapid sequence induction with etomidate and succinylcholine used to minimize hemodynamic instability and secure airway. Empirical antimalarial treatment initiated intraoperatively before diagnostic confirmation. Case highlights anesthetic challenges when malaria diagnosis is delayed.

articles.headline: Poor malaria treatment adherence in Rwanda drives artemisinin resistance risk
articles.title_original: Understanding Malaria Treatment Adherence in Rwanda: Implications for Artemisinin Resistance.
articles.details: Cross-sectional knowledge, attitudes, practices (KAP) survey at six health facilities in Rwanda (December 2023-February 2024) with 406 participants. Knowledge was high (81% recognized symptoms, 72% understood transmission), but adherence was poor: of 180 participants who received malaria treatment in prior 6 months, only 46% (83/180) completed full 3-day course; 37% (66/180) stopped within 2 days, 11% (19/180) stopped after >3 days. 27% (109/406) took antimalarials for fever, majority obtaining from drug outlets/pharmacies. Authors emphasize urgent need for interventions to improve adherence.

articles.headline: P. vivax emerges in Duffy-negative Cameroonians despite RDT failures
articles.title_original: Epidemiological Insights and Duffy Binding Protein Evolution of Plasmodium vivax in Duffy-Negative Cameroonians.
articles.details: Study detected P. vivax in 11% of febrile and 5.5% of asymptomatic Duffy-negative Cameroonians across three ecological zones. Standard RDTs produced false-negatives even at high parasitemia, while qPCR showed superior sensitivity. Genetic analysis identified prevalent PvDBP1 mutations (I379L 73%, E225K 61%) suggesting adaptive evolution; phylogenetic analysis clustered Cameroonian isolates with Botswana but distinct from Ethiopian and Sudanese strains.

articles.headline: Microglia reactivity and inflammatory response in experimental cerebral malaria
articles.title_original: Microglia exhibit a dynamic response, modulating inducible nitric oxide synthase expression and the production of pro-inflammatory cytokines during experimental cerebral malaria.
articles.details: Experimental cerebral malaria model (P. berghei ANKA in C57BL/6 mice) using flow cytometry documented increases in Ly6C+ T cells, inflammatory monocytes, resident macrophages, and microglia (CD45lowCD11b+TMEM119+CD206-). Ex vivo analysis showed increased numbers of these populations expressing iNOS, IL-1β, and TNF. Findings highlight pronounced microglia reactivity and leukocyte dynamics in cerebral malaria development.

articles.headline: Health facility vulnerability to climate-driven vector diseases in Western Kenya
articles.title_original: Public health facility vulnerabilities, preparedness, and health outcomes for Plasmodium falciparum and dengue virus-infected children under 5 years with acute febrile illnesses in Western Kenya.
articles.details: Prospective cohort study of 378 participants over three months assessed 21 public health facilities. Among facilities, 17 (81%) could diagnose and treat malaria while 4 (18%) could only diagnose and treat dengue. In Bunyala, 6 of 10 facilities (60%) and in Kisumu 5 facilities (45%) were located on floodplains or wetlands. Longest hospitalizations (>5 days) occurred in Kisumu; highest recovery rate (96.8%) was in Bunyala versus 91% in Kisumu.

articles.headline: Systematic review: malaria research underutilizes gender analysis beyond sex-disaggregation
articles.title_original: Conceptualization of gender in published malaria and gender research: a systematic descriptive review.
articles.details: Systematic review of 57 published articles (1995-2024) on malaria and gender found most papers were biomedical in nature, reducing gender analysis to sex-disaggregated data only. Majority employed quantitative methods with laboratory-based research focus in sub-Saharan Africa. Study calls for more social science research using qualitative and mixed-methods approaches to extend gender analysis beyond sex and gender-disaggregated data.

articles.headline: Self-medication for malaria in 62% of Ugandan households; inadequate dosing prevalent
articles.title_original: Self-medication for malaria and associated factors in Kakumiro District, Uganda, August 2023: implications for malaria management and mortality prevention.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 592 households in Kakumiro District found 62% (95% CI 58.2-65.9%) self-medicated for malaria. Risk factors: household head age ≥35 years (APR 1.77, 95% CI 1.04-3.01), distance ≥5 km to facility (APR 3.05, 95% CI 2.09-4.47), and antimalarial storage at home (APR 2.21, 95% CI 1.36-3.59). While 85% used recommended drugs, 66% gave inappropriate doses. Main driver: antimalarial stockouts at health facilities.

articles.headline: Low IPTp-SP uptake (31%) in Tanzania driven by individual and community factors
articles.title_original: Individual and community-level correlates of optimal doses of sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine for intermittent preventive treatment of malaria during pregnancy in Tanzania: a multilevel analysis of the 2022 national survey.
articles.details: Multilevel analysis of 4497 women from 2022 TDHS found optimal IPTp-SP uptake (≥3 doses) at 31.2% (95% CI 29.1-33.3). Individual correlates: women aged 25-34 years (AOR 1.52, 95% CI 1.21-1.90), primary education (AOR 1.34, 95% CI 1.07-1.68), and >3 ANC visits (AOR 1.77, 95% CI 1.47-2.12) increased uptake. Community-level: higher ANC rates (AOR 1.49) and Northern/Lake zones showed higher uptake; Zanzibar had very low uptake (AOR 0.01) as IPTp-SP no longer recommended there.

articles.headline: Vaccine effectiveness perception drives community engagement for malaria vaccine uptake
articles.title_original: Community engagement in promoting malaria vaccine uptake for children Under-Five.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 1,183 caregivers identified factors influencing community engagement in malaria vaccine promotion for children under five. Random forest analysis highlighted perception of vaccine effectiveness, educational level, and age as most relevant factors, with vaccine safety and acceptability as moderate contributors. Educational differences emerged: those with higher education prioritized vaccine effectiveness and safety, while lower education groups emphasized cost and cultural factors.

articles.headline: Transfusion-transmitted malaria prevalence 0.87-2.3% in blood donors; under-recognized threat
articles.title_original: Transfusion-transmitted Plasmodium spp. infections and safety challenges for malaria in the Indian subcontinent: a systematic review.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Sharma S, Sharma A
articles.details: Systematic review of 122 studies involving >6.5 million individuals found Plasmodium parasitemia prevalence in blood donors ranged from ~0% to 0.87% (light microscopy) and ~0% to 2.3% (rapid diagnostic tests), with post-transfusion malaria cases ranging 0.8-6.8% across studies. Clinical outcomes ranged from mild to fatal, including complications from P. vivax, P. falciparum, and P. malariae, plus drug-resistant and pfhrp2 gene-deleted P. falciparum strains.

articles.headline: Malaria PCR diagnosis overrelies on single 18S rRNA target for two decades
articles.title_original: Overreliance on Plasmodium 18S rRNA gene for malaria molecular diagnosis-inferences from systematic review.
articles.details: Systematic review found 93% of PCR studies used 18S rRNA gene; 68% employed nested PCR as the sole assay type. Among studies using 18S rRNA and nested PCR, 85% relied on methods developed in 1993. Study highlights concern about overreliance on a single molecular target and calls for research validating newer multi-copy targets for improved sensitivity, reproducibility, and cost-effectiveness.

articles.headline: EEG distinguishes retinopathy-negative cerebral malaria from nonmalarial coma
articles.title_original: Using Electroencephalography to Assess Coma Etiology in Children with Retinopathy-Negative Cerebral Malaria.
articles.details: EEG analysis of Malawian children with retinopathy-negative cerebral malaria, retinopathy-positive cerebral malaria, or nonmalarial coma found that quantitative EEG distinguished retinopathy-negative CM from nonmalarial coma with AUROC 0.83; combined qualitative and quantitative interpretation improved discrimination (AUROC 0.87). EEG patterns in retinopathy-negative CM were similar to retinopathy-positive CM and significantly different from nonmalarial coma.

articles.headline: Chaperonin complex CPN60 regulates apicoplast proteostasis; potential drug target
articles.title_original: A chaperonin complex regulates organelle proteostasis in malaria parasites.
articles.details: Identified a conserved apicoplast chaperonin complex (CPN60) regulating organelle proteostasis through interaction with the Clp proteolytic complex. CPN60 ablation caused parasite death immediately in the first replication cycle. Inhibitors of the bacterial CPN60 ortholog (GroEL) demonstrated anti-Plasmodium activity, including effects outside the apicoplast, suggesting potential as an antimalarial target.

articles.headline: P. vivax thrombocytopenia linked to anti-phosphatidylserine autoantibodies and cytokines
articles.title_original: Thrombocytopenia in patients with Plasmodium vivax in Colombia is associated with anti-phosphatidylserine autoantibodies and IL-6, IFNγ, IL-10 and TGF-β.
articles.details: Study of P. vivax-infected patients with and without thrombocytopenia found elevated IFNγ, IL-6, and IL-10 in thrombocytopenia group (P < 0.05), while TGF-β1 was elevated in non-thrombocytopenic malaria group (P < 0.0001). Anti-phosphatidylserine autoantibodies were higher in the thrombocytopenia group and negatively correlated with platelet counts, suggesting thrombocytopenia results from autoantibodies to phosphatidylserine on activated platelets.

articles.headline: Community knowledge shapes malaria prevention success globally
articles.title_original: A scoping review of community knowledge in malaria prevention and control programmes.
articles.details: Scoping review of 63 studies from 27 countries (2000-2024) examining community knowledge in malaria prevention. Higher awareness consistently linked with better preventive practices and earlier treatment-seeking behavior. Critical knowledge gaps persist regarding environmental risk factors and vector control.

articles.headline: Urban-rural disparities drive pregnant women's malaria prevention gaps
articles.title_original: Bridging urban-rural disparities in malaria care during pregnancy in Senegal: evidence from household and health facility surveys.
articles.details: Senegal DHS/SPA data 2012-2019 show pregnant women received average 1.66 IPTp-SP doses (95% CI 1.65-1.68), with higher urban uptake (1.73) vs rural (1.63). Service readiness increase of 1 point raised doses by 0.251; rural women benefited less (0.089 vs 0.612 dose increase, p<0.001). Differential strategies required for urban vs rural settings.

articles.headline: Correction to CRISPR-Cas9 screening in Plasmodium berghei
articles.title_original: Correction to 'A scalable CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing system facilitates CRISPR screens in the malaria parasite Plasmodium berghei'.
articles.details: Correction to previously published work describing CRISPR-Cas9 gene editing system for screening in P. berghei malaria parasite.

articles.headline: Novel UV light traps outperform standard surveillance methods in Kenya
articles.title_original: Assessment of BG-Pro (Biogent AG) and Silver Bullet 2.1 (Lumin8) UV-Light Traps Efficiency for Surveillance of Malaria Vectors in Western Kenya.
articles.details: Field comparison of light traps over 64 nights in western Kenya. Silver Bullet 2.1 UV trap most effective for An. gambiae s.l. (RR 4.3, 95% CI 2.5-7.3) and An. funestus s.l. (RR 7.1, 95% CI 3.9-13.1) versus CDC UV LED trap. BG-Pro also superior (An. gambiae RR 2.0, 95% CI 0.9-3.9; An. funestus RR 3.5, 95% CI 1.9-6.4). Both novel traps 3-6 times more efficient, promising for low-density settings.

articles.headline: COVID-19 history markedly elevates severe malaria and dengue risk
articles.title_original: COVID-19 infection and its association with severe malaria & dengue: an epidemiological study from Southern India.
articles.details: Case-control study of 293 patients in southern India (Nov 2022-Aug 2024): 70 malaria, 223 dengue. COVID-19 history strongly associated with severe dengue (aOR 55.93, 95% CI 7.17-435.92, p<0.001; 98.1% of COVID+ dengue patients) and severe malaria (aOR 263.7, 95% CI 34.9-1987.4, p<0.001; 85.7% of COVID+ malaria patients). Lower socioeconomic status also associated with severe dengue.

articles.headline: Malaria diagnostic test accuracy varies by transmission and demographics
articles.title_original: Varying Malaria Rapid Diagnostic Test Accuracy by Regional Transmission Level and Demographics in Tanzania.
articles.details: Tanzania 2021 cross-sectional study comparing 3 national standard mRDTs and 1 research mRDT against qPCR. Test accuracy differed significantly by age, gender, and regional transmission rate. Research test underperformed with poor sensitivity. Transmission intensity affects mRDT diagnostic agreement, with implications for accurate prevalence estimation.

articles.headline: Computational tool identifies broadly protective vaccine epitopes for malaria
articles.title_original: Computational Prioritization of T Cell Epitopes to Overcome HLA Restriction and Antigenic Diversity in Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: Computational analysis of 42 P. falciparum vaccine antigens using HLA data from 24 sub-Saharan populations. TEpiNom tool identified 2,265 MHC I and 1,992 MHC II epitopes with ≥95% sequence conservation and median rank <10% binding affinity. MHC I epitopes from pre-erythrocytic antigens achieved near 100% HLA-A/B/C population coverage. Strategy integrates epitope promiscuity and multistage antigens for broad, durable protection.

articles.headline: Genomic atlas maps insecticide resistance evolution in African malaria vectors
articles.title_original: An atlas of positive selection in the genomes of major malaria vectors.
articles.details: Whole-genome sequencing of 4,306 An. gambiae complex and An. funestus from 21 African countries identifies widespread positive selection at insecticide resistance loci. Confirms selection at known sites (Vgsc, Rdl, Ace1) and metabolic enzymes (Cyp6p, Cyp9k1, Gste); reveals novel signals at diacylglycerol kinase and Keap1. Parallel evolution between species with regional clustering, enabling real-time resistance monitoring.
articles.notable_reason: Comprehensive atlas of emerging resistance mechanisms in major vectors

articles.headline: Illegal gold mining fuels malaria resurgence in Yanomami Indigenous Territory
articles.title_original: Human mobility, garimpos and spatiotemporal malaria transmission in the Yanomami Indigenous Territory: a retrospective observational study.
articles.details: Retrospective spatial analysis of surveillance data 2007-2023 in Brazil's Yanomami Territory using Malaria Mobility Networks. Found 86% of malaria cases and 91% of population concentrated spatially; autochthonous transmission in indigenous localities reached 90% by 2023. Garimpo (illegal mining) involvement peaked ~27% in 2022, showing robust garimpo-urban transmission networks.

articles.headline: Healthcare provider adherence to malaria guidelines critically low in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Evaluation of anti-malarial treatment for elimination of malaria in South West Ethiopia: a concurrent triangulation design for prompt decision.
articles.details: Southwest Ethiopia facility-based study (March-July 2024) reviewed 1,684 patient cards (mean age 20.99 years). 95.96% underwent microscopy/RDT; 58.31% diagnosed P. falciparum, others P. vivax or mixed. Provider adherence to updated treatment guidelines only 36.99% (95% CI 33.93-38.52%). Main barriers: drug shortage, lack of training, staffing, and laboratory supply gaps.

articles.headline: Serum endocan elevates with malaria severity and parasite burden
articles.title_original: Preliminary assessment of serum endocan as a biomarker of disease severity in Plasmodium falciparum and Plasmodium vivax malaria.
articles.authors: Nantavisai K, Puttikamonkul S, Viriyavejakul P
articles.details: Preliminary study of 99 participants (20 controls, 36 P. vivax, 30 uncomplicated P. falciparum, 13 severe P. falciparum).
