All malaria groups had significantly higher serum endocan than controls (p<0.0001), progressively increasing with severity: severe P. falciparum 4.67 ng/ml (IQR 2.85-7.93), uncomplicated P. falciparum 3.27 (2.24-4.33), P. vivax 1.85 (1.44-3.23). Endocan correlated with parasite density and thrombocytopenia.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistant mutations emerging across Ethiopia despite elimination goals
articles.title_original: Rising prevalence of Plasmodium falciparum Artemisinin partial resistance mutations in Ethiopia.
articles.details: Molecular analysis of 1,199 P. falciparum from 12 sentinel sites across 5 Ethiopian regions (2019-2023). k13 R622I mutation present in 15.7% with marked regional variation. Three validated artemisinin resistance mutations detected in Ethiopia for first time: P441L, P574L, A675V. Polymorphisms in partner drug resistance genes (crt, mdr1, dhps, dhfr) nearly fixed; 87.2% monogenomic infections with clonal transmission.
articles.notable_reason: Emerging artemisinin resistance threatens ACT therapy and elimination targets

articles.headline: Improving malaria trial sample sizes using coefficient of variation
articles.title_original: Characterisation of between-cluster heterogeneity in malaria cluster randomised trials to inform future sample size calculations.
articles.details: Meta-analysis of 24 cluster randomised trials calculating the coefficient of variation (k), a measure of heterogeneity used in sample size calculations. Empirical k values often exceeded those originally used in trial designs, reducing statistical power. Elevated k was associated with incidence outcomes, lower endemicity settings, and uneven intervention coverage.

articles.headline: Sterile insect technique costs and benefits for Gambian malaria
articles.title_original: Evaluating the cost of malaria elimination by Anopheles gambiae precision guided SIT in the Upper River region, The Gambia.
articles.details: Cost-effectiveness analysis of precision guided sterile insect technique (pgSIT) using genetically sterile male Anopheles gambiae in Upper River region, The Gambia. Projected to prevent approximately 230 deaths and 48,000 sick days annually. Cost-effectiveness ranged 11–94 USD per disability-adjusted life year, with annual cost 0.36–3.03 USD per person.

articles.headline: Dual-target antimalarial from checkpoint kinase inhibitor repurposing
articles.title_original: The Human Chk1 Inhibitor CHIR-124 Shows Multistage Activity Against Plasmodium falciparum via Dual Inhibition of PfArk1 and Hemozoin Formation.
articles.details: CHIR-124, a human checkpoint kinase 1 inhibitor, demonstrated in vitro activity against drug-sensitive and drug-resistant P. falciparum parasites and moderate activity against liver and gametocyte forms. Its parasiticidal mechanism involves dual inhibition of P. falciparum aurora-related kinase-1 (PfArk1) and β-hematin (hemozoin) formation, potentially reducing resistance risk.

articles.headline: Blood count parameters predict malaria severity and prior exposure
articles.title_original: Comparative Analysis of Initial Full Blood Count Parameters in Adults Infected With Plasmodium falciparum for Classification of Disease Severity and Previous Exposure Across Endemic (Gabon) and Nonendemic (Germany) Settings.
articles.details: Analysis of full blood count parameters in 1,392 adults with P. falciparum infection across endemic (Gabon) and non-endemic (Germany) settings. Platelet counts decreased from asymptomatic (218 ×10³/µL) to uncomplicated (150 ×10³/µL) to severe malaria (40 ×10³/µL, P<0.0001). Machine learning integration outperformed single parameters (area under curve 0.94 and 0.84) in differentiating severity and prior exposure.

articles.headline: Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio diagnoses malaria in resource settings
articles.title_original: Diagnostic value of full blood count derived systemic inflammatory biomarkers in malaria infection.
articles.details: Case-control study (45 malaria patients, 50 healthy controls) evaluating full blood count-derived inflammatory biomarkers. Neutrophil-to-lymphocyte ratio (NLR) showed highest diagnostic value with area under curve 0.937, sensitivity 86.7%, and specificity 92.0%. NLR correlated with parasite count (ρ=0.5), and ≥2.12 cutoff identified malaria inflammation.

articles.headline: Splenic macrophage subsets have distinct roles in malaria immune response
articles.title_original: Red pulp macrophages clear parasites, while marginal metallophilic and marginal zone macrophages support CD4(+) T cell activation during Plasmodium yoelii infection.
articles.details: Study in P. yoelii-infected transgenic mice identifying compartmentalized roles of splenic macrophage subsets. Red pulp macrophages were essential for clearing infected erythrocytes; their depletion resulted in elevated parasitemia. Marginal zone macrophages contributed to CD4+ T cell activation and interferon-gamma production but did not modulate parasitemia progression.

articles.headline: Emerging molecular mechanisms and novel therapeutic approaches in malaria
articles.title_original: Emerging Molecular Mechanisms in Malaria Pathogenesis and Novel Therapeutic Approaches: A Focus on P. falciparum Malaria.
articles.details: Review examining P. falciparum pathogenesis mechanisms, immune evasion strategies, and drug resistance patterns. Discusses emerging approaches including vaccines RTS,S and R21 and antibody therapies. Identifies persistent challenges in extending vaccine-induced immunity duration and ensuring effectiveness across diverse parasite strains.

articles.headline: Temperature, rainfall, ENSO drive malaria risk in Brazilian Amazon
articles.title_original: The impact of weather and extreme events on malaria transmission in the Brazilian Amazon: a case-crossover and population-based study.
articles.details: Case-crossover analysis of 5,381,105 malaria cases (2003–2022) in Brazilian Amazon quantifying weather and El Niño-Southern Oscillation effects. Temperatures 25.64–30.85°C and rainfall >4.46cm in preceding week increased risk 9% (95% CI 8–10%) and 86% (95% CI 35–155%). Two-week lagged temperatures >25.64°C reduced risk 27% (95% CI 19–33%); La Niña associated with 37,258 fewer cases (95% CI 37,171–37,345).

articles.headline: Uganda 2022 malaria epidemic: 3.3 million excess cases in 64 districts
articles.title_original: Malaria epidemics and its drivers in Uganda in 2022.
articles.details: Epidemiological analysis of Uganda's 2022 malaria epidemic using district health data (2017–2022). Cases increased 31% in 2022, affecting 64 of 146 districts with 3,379,309 excess cases (95% CI 1,553,714–5,339,709) and 149,789 excess admissions (95% CI 66,029–235,743). Root causes: programmatic gaps in indoor residual spraying and community case management, vector composition changes, and reduced insecticide efficacy.

articles.headline: Malaria care cascade remains suboptimal in Guinea and Sierra Leone
articles.title_original: The malaria care cascade and factors associated with receiving a malaria test in children under 5 years of age and pregnant women with fever: a cross-sectional household survey from three regions in Guinea and three districts in Sierra Leone in 2022.
articles.details: Cross-sectional household survey of 1,377 children under 5 and 254 pregnant women with fever (May–August 2022). Malaria testing rates 42–96% (children) and 62–91% (pregnant women) by region. Treatment within 2 days ranged 54–81% (children) and 24–69% (pregnant women). Trust in healthcare systems, informative messaging, and antenatal care attendance were associated with receiving malaria tests.

articles.headline: Placental malaria and preeclampsia association remains inconclusive
articles.title_original: The Relationship between Placental Malaria and Preeclampsia in Blantyre, Malawi.
articles.details: Analysis of 751 pregnancies from intermittent preventive therapy trial in Malawi examining placental malaria and preeclampsia association. Placental malaria present in 125 (16.7%); preeclampsia occurred in 3.3% and eclampsia in 0.7%. Those with hemozoin (early pregnancy infection marker) had higher preeclampsia (6.7% vs 3.7%, P=0.16), though not statistically significant and study was underpowered.

articles.headline: Vaccination offers protection during climate-driven malaria disruptions
articles.title_original: Vaccination to mitigate climate-driven disruptions to malaria control in Madagascar.
articles.details: Commentary examining malaria infection rebound in Madagascar following tropical cyclones when interventions are interrupted. Recently available malaria vaccines with longer protection duration could address intervention gaps. Proposes quantification of symptomatic infection reduction under various vaccination scenarios to evaluate building climate resilience into malaria control programs.

articles.headline: Malaria, anemia linked in Ethiopian schoolchildren; ITN protective
articles.title_original: Prevalence of malaria, anemia and associated factors among school children in Hawassa city, Sidama, Ethiopia.
articles.authors: Gena A, Asnake S, Menjetta T
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 329 children in Hawassa, Ethiopia (April-June 2024) using microscopy and RDT for malaria diagnosis, hemoglobinometer for anemia. Malaria prevalence 8.5%, anemia 9.4%, with 29% of anemic children co-infected. Children with malaria were 13.3× more likely anemic (AOR=13.258, 95% CI: 3.188–55.139). ITN use significantly reduced malaria risk (AOR=0.024, 95% CI: 0.001–0.755).

articles.headline: Blood groups do not affect malaria risk in deployed soldiers
articles.title_original: Impact of the ABO and Rh Blood Groups on Malaria Incidence, Recurrence, and Severity Among a Moroccan Military Contingent in the Central African Republic.
articles.details: Eight-month study (October 2024–May 2025) of 749 Moroccan soldiers with chemoprophylaxis in Central African Republic. Malaria incidence 20.8% (156 cases), 89% P. falciparum, 1.1% severe (8 soldiers). No significant association between ABO/Rh blood groups and malaria incidence, recurrence, or severity, despite P. falciparum predominance. Robust prevention strategies appear to override genetic influences.

articles.headline: Noncoding RNAs enable coordinated var gene switching in malaria parasites
articles.title_original: The molecular basis of coordinated antigen switching enabling chronic infection by human malaria parasites.
articles.details: Reveals molecular mechanism by which var2csa functions as a transiently activated "switching hub" through interactions of noncoding RNAs, upstream open reading frames, and nonsense-mediated mRNA decay pathway. This coordination unifies the multi-copy var gene family into single network, enabling P. falciparum to perpetuate chronic infection through coordinated antigenic variation and antibody evasion.
articles.notable_reason: Novel mechanism enabling chronic parasite persistence; druggable pathway for immune evasion

articles.headline: Malaria vaccination and strategic action needed in Uganda
articles.title_original: Addressing the malaria crisis among children in Uganda: the urgent need for vaccination and strategic action.
articles.details: Title indicates discussion of malaria crisis among children in Uganda and need for vaccination and strategic interventions, but no abstract provided.

articles.headline: HRP2 RDTs miss non-falciparum species in Burkina Faso children
articles.title_original: Undetected Plasmodium malariae and P. ovale infections in HRP2 RDT-positive children with uncomplicated malaria in Nanoro, Burkina Faso.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 207 children <5 years with HRP2 RDT-positive uncomplicated malaria in Nanoro, Burkina Faso (July 2021–June 2022). Microscopy detected 4 patent non-falciparum cases (3 P. malariae, 1 P. ovale). PCR additionally detected 6 sub-patent P. ovale and 5 sub-patent P. malariae infections. Non-falciparum infections exhibited lower parasite densities than P. falciparum mono-infections.

articles.headline: Humans show IL-10 dominance; macaques IL-6 in P. knowlesi infection
articles.title_original: Comparative immunological study of Plasmodium knowlesi infections in humans and macaques: insights into cytokine dynamics.
articles.authors: Abd Rachman Isnadi MF, et al.
articles.details: Comparative study of 30 P. knowlesi-infected humans and 35 wild macaques in Sabah, Malaysia, measuring parasite gDNA, mtDNA, and six cytokines (TNF, IFNγ, IL-1β, IL-4, IL-6, IL-10). Macaques had significantly higher parasite gDNA and mtDNA than humans. Humans showed IL-10 dominance (positively associated with parasite burden), macaques showed IL-6 dominance. Despite lower parasite burden, humans exhibited higher IL-10/TNF ratio indicating greater disease severity.

articles.headline: G6PD-guided high-dose primaquine trial for P. vivax radical cure
articles.title_original: High daily dose Short COurse PrimaquinE after G6PD testing for the radical cure of Plasmodium vivax malaria in Indonesia and Papua New Guinea: the SCOPE implementation study protocol.
articles.details: Protocol for staged binational implementation study in Indonesia and Papua New Guinea testing G6PD-guided primaquine dosing for P. vivax radical cure. Stage 1: 800 patients at 4 clinics assessing safety. Stage 2 (if safe): ~11,410 patients at 10 clinics assessing feasibility and cost-effectiveness. Dosing: 7 mg/kg over 7 days for G6PD normal (≥70% activity), 14 days for intermediate (30–<70%), or lower-dose weekly over 8 weeks for deficient (<30%).

articles.headline: Deep learning framework achieves 96% accuracy for malaria diagnosis
articles.title_original: Automated multi-model framework for malaria detection using deep learning and feature fusion.
articles.details: Multi-model deep learning framework for automated malaria detection validated on 27,558 microscopic blood smear images. Combined ResNet 50, VGG16, and DenseNet-201 for feature extraction, principal component analysis for dimensionality reduction, and SVM-LSTM hybrid for classification with majority voting ensemble. Achieved 96.47% accuracy, 96.03% sensitivity, 96.90% specificity, 96.88% precision, 96.45% F1-score.

articles.headline: Congenital malaria rare in Nigeria; maternal parasitaemia key driver
articles.title_original: Prevalance and risk factors of congenital malaria in Osogbo, Southwestern Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 77 newborns within 24 hours of delivery at State Specialist Hospital, Osogbo, Nigeria, using microscopy and RDT. Congenital malaria prevalence 6.5%, with all infected newborns demonstrating cord blood parasitaemia. Maternal malarial parasitaemia emerged as significant predictor (p<0.05), while ITN use, chemoprophylaxis, and antimalarial therapy showed no significant association.

articles.headline: Anopheles aquasalis wings show higher nanomorphological complexity
articles.title_original: Unveiling the Nanomorphological Architecture of Malaria Vectors' Wings via Minkowski and Multifractal Formalisms.
articles.details: Atomic force microscopy and multifractal analysis of Anopheles darlingi and A. aquasalis wing surfaces revealed pronounced interspecific and dorso-ventral asymmetries. A. aquasalis displayed higher elevation variance and denser nanomorphological heterogeneity. Multifractal spectra showed broader width (Δα ≈ 1.073 vs 1.009) and stronger left-asymmetry (H ≈ -0.852 vs -0.532) in A. aquasalis, with generalized fractal dimensions D₀ ≈ 2.00 and D₂ range 1.951–1.982.

articles.headline: Corrigendum to WIMANET workshop meeting report
articles.title_original: Corrigendum to "The Wildlife Malaria Research network (WIMANET): Meeting report on the 1st WIMANET workshop" [Int. J. Parasitol: Parasites and Wildlife 25 (2024) 100989].
articles.details: Correction to Wildlife Malaria Research Network (WIMANET) 1st workshop meeting report published in International Journal of Parasitology: Parasites and Wildlife.

articles.headline: High malaria burden in rural Côte d'Ivoire before dam development
articles.title_original: Epidemiological Profile and Risk Factors for Malaria in Rural Communities Before the Operationalization of the Singrobo-Ahouaty Dam, Southern Côte d'Ivoire.
articles.details: Cross-sectional baseline survey of 915 individuals in rural communities before Singrobo-Ahouaty dam operationalization in Southern Côte d'Ivoire. Overall malaria prevalence 43.1% (394/915) with average parasite density 405.7 parasites/µL. School-age children (6–13 years) showed highest prevalence (74.3%), while children <5 years had highest parasite density (1218.0 parasites/µL). Risk factors: informal work (ORa=1.5), raw material sector work (ORa=1.4), frequent mosquito bites (OR=0.4).

articles.headline: Traditional medicine potential for malaria control in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Investing in traditional medicine: leveraging evidence and innovative research to strengthen the fight against malaria in Nigeria.
articles.details: Perspective paper examining traditional African medicine, particularly Nigerian herbal remedies, as a resource for malaria management. Drawing on historical success of Artemisia annua in modern drug development, the paper advocates for increased funding and scientific validation of traditional remedies, emphasizing growing reliance on herbal treatments in rural Nigeria and the need for pharmacological safety testing.

articles.headline: 22 malaria cases detected in northern Iran in 2023
articles.title_original: The resurgence of malaria in Northern Iran in 2023: a wake-up call.
articles.details: Surveillance study of 445 malaria-suspected individuals in Golestan Province, Iran (March–December 2023) identified 22 confirmed cases (4.94% positivity). P. vivax accounted for 77.27% (17/22), P. falciparum 18.18% (4/22), and co-infection 4.54% (1/22). Mean parasitemia: 16,029±5,060/µL for P. vivax, 105,460±102,146/µL for P. falciparum. All patients achieved parasite clearance with treatment. Authors note concern about gametocytes in specimens and potential for local transmission.

articles.headline: Severe malaria acute kidney injury long-term outcomes study protocol
articles.title_original: Malaria associated pathogenesis of chronic kidney disease (MAP-CKD): a prospective study of children hospitalized with severe malaria.
articles.details: Prospective multi-site cohort study protocol enrolling 750 children with severe malaria and 375 community controls at three Ugandan hospitals (ages 3 months–<16 years) with 2-year follow-up. Study investigates short- and long-term kidney recovery post-acute kidney injury, risk factors for chronic kidney disease, maladaptive repair mechanisms, and impacts on morbidity, mortality, and neurological outcomes including brain MRI assessment.

articles.headline: Large inter-individual variation in P. falciparum infection rates in Mali
articles.title_original: Marked heterogeneity in malaria infection rate in a Malian longitudinal cohort.
articles.details: Amplicon sequencing of >13,000 blood samples from 464 Malian participants (3 months–25 years) over 6-year longitudinal study characterized molecular force of infection (molFOI). molFOI varied extensively among individuals (0–55 in 2011) but was consistent within individuals across multiple seasons, independent of age. HbS allele associated with lower molFOI; CSP C-term and RH5 antibody responses correlated with low infection risk. Authors conclude inter-individual variability reflects heterogeneous exposure to infectious mosquito bites.

articles.headline: P. falciparum drug resistance markers in central Ethiopia 2023
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 Variants and Histidine-Rich Protein 2/3 Deletions in Central Ethiopia, 2023.
articles.details: Genotyping of 220 symptomatic malaria isolates from central Ethiopia assessed resistance markers: pfk13 R622I (artemisinin partial resistance) in 1.1% (2/174), P441L in 1.7% (3/174); pfhrp2 deletion in 24.0% (36/150), pfhrp3 deletion in 78.8% (118/150), dual deletion in 22.7% (34/150). P. falciparum prevalence 86.4%, P. vivax 12.3%, P. ovale 3.6%. Authors conclude high proportions of infections missed by HRP-based RDTs while artemisinin resistance markers remain rare.

articles.headline: Environmental factors shaping zoonotic malaria vector dynamics Southeast Asia
articles.title_original: Impact of environmental factors on the bionomics of Anopheles mosquito vectors of zoonotic malaria: A narrative review.
articles.details: Narrative review examining vectors of non-human primate malaria in Southeast Asia, particularly Plasmodium knowlesi and emerging species P. cynomolgi, P. inui, P. fieldi. Reviews environmental determinants—temperature, humidity, elevation, precipitation, seasonality, land use—that shape Anopheles vector abundance and survival affecting transmission intensity. Advocates One Health approach integrating vector surveillance, sustainable land management, and targeted public health interventions.

articles.headline: Three P. falciparum antigens identified for malaria pregnancy protection
articles.title_original: Exploring the P. falciparum antigens associated with reduced risk of malaria in pregnancy.
articles.details: Serological study assessed antibody responses to 698 P. falciparum recombinant antigens in Kenyan primigravida and multigravida women. Selected 3 statistically significant antigens in multigravida: PF3D7_1301800 (SURFIN 13.1), PF3D7_0424400 (SURFIN 4.2), PF3D7_1252100 (rhoptry neck antigen 3). All VAR2CSA domains showed immunoreactivity (seroprevalence 42–62%) and correlated with selected antigens but lacked statistical significance in gravidity association. Authors conclude selected antigens warrant further characterization for vaccine prioritization.

articles.headline: Tryptophan catabolism and Tregs increase in P. vivax malaria
articles.title_original: IDO1-AhR axis increases T regulatory cells in Plasmodium vivax malaria infection.
articles.details: In vitro and clinical study of Brazilian P. vivax patients investigated IDO1-mediated tryptophan catabolism promoting regulatory T cell (Treg) induction. P. vivax stimulation increased IDO-1, elevated kynurenine/tryptophan ratio, and enhanced Tregs; inhibition of IDO, MyD88, or AhR reduced Tregs and CD4+ proliferation. Acute P. vivax patients showed elevated KYN/TRP ratios and increased Tregs; prior infection was protective with lower Tregs, IFN-γ, and KYN/TRP ratios. Authors identify IDO-mediated TRP catabolism promoting tolerogenic phenotype.

articles.headline: Four artemisinin combinations retain efficacy in Mozambique 2022
articles.title_original: Efficacy of artemether-lumefantrine, artesunate-amodiaquine, dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-pyronaridine for the treatment of uncomplicated Plasmodium falciparum malaria in Mozambique, 2022.
articles.details: Therapeutic efficacy study of 828 children (6 months–11 years) with uncomplicated P. falciparum malaria (parasitemia 1000–200,000/µL) at 5 Mozambique sites with 28–42 day follow-up. Artemether-lumefantrine day-28 corrected efficacy: 100% (Massinga/Dondo/Moatize), 97.63% (Mopeia), 98.68% (Montepuez). Artesunate-amodiaquine: 100% (Dondo/Moatize). Dihydroartemisinin-piperaquine and artesunate-pyronaridine day-42 efficacy: 100% (Massinga), 97.75% (Mopeia). All drugs well tolerated (<2% adverse events). Authors conclude first-line treatments remain effective; DP and AS-PY are suitable alternatives.

articles.headline: Disability prevalence linked to age, gender, wealth in Tanzania
articles.title_original: Socioeconomic and geographical disparities in disability distribution among Tanzanian population: insights from the 2022 demographic and health survey and malaria indicator survey.
articles.details: Secondary analysis of 58,425 individuals from 2022 Tanzania Demographic and Health/Malaria Indicator Survey examined disability prevalence (11% overall). Older age (≥50 years) associated with higher disability (APR: 3.85; 95% CI: 3.33–4.52); females higher than males (APR: 2.67; 95% CI: 2.63–2.71). Secondary education, separation/widowhood, wealthiest households, and health insurance associated with higher reported disability. Regional disparities noted with Northern zone higher than Lake zone. Authors suggest counterintuitive wealth findings may reflect improved diagnosis access.

articles.headline: P. falciparum chloroquine and pyrimethamine resistance increased Ecuador
articles.title_original: Tracking Plasmodium falciparum antimalarial resistance markers during a malaria pre-elimination period in the Pacific coast of South America.
articles.details: SNP analysis of 67 P. falciparum samples from northwestern Ecuador (2019–2021) compared resistance markers to 2013–2015 period. Mutant haplotypes CVMET (Pfcrt, chloroquine resistance) and CICNI (Pfdhfr, pyrimethamine resistance) increased to 100% frequency in Esmeraldas. NEDFSDFY in Pfmdr-1 detected for first time; A16V in Pfdhfr (proguanil resistance) reported for first time in Ecuador. Wild-type SAKAA (Pfdhps, sulfadoxine) and MYRIC (Pfk13, artemisinin) remained at 100%. Authors conclude current first-line treatment (artemether-lumefantrine + primaquine) remains effective.

articles.headline: Artificial intelligence applications in malaria diagnosis treatment surveillance
articles.title_original: An insight into the role of artificial intelligence in combating malaria: recent developments.
articles.details: Narrative review examining AI applications across malaria control spanning diagnosis, surveillance, treatment, and vector control. Reviews AI-driven solutions for accelerating drug discovery, enabling personalized medicine, and enhancing diagnostics through machine learning and deep learning approaches. Emphasizes AI's role in epidemiological tracking and predicting transmission dynamics amid rising drug resistance. Authors advocate for sustained investment in AI-driven malaria research.

articles.headline: Cambodia 1-3-7 surveillance strategy shows 59-72% timely case notification
articles.title_original: Evaluating malaria reactive surveillance and response strategies in northeast Cambodia: a mixed-methods study.
articles.details: Mixed-methods study evaluated the 1-3-7 reactive surveillance and response (RASR) strategy in Ratanakiri and Stung Treng provinces involving day-of-diagnosis notification/investigation, 3-day reactive case detection, and 7-day active focus investigation. In 2020 and 2022, 72% and 59% of malaria cases were notified and investigated within one day; reactive case detection timeliness was 89% and 45% respectively. Despite challenges including poor mobile network coverage, heavy workload, and inadequate technical knowledge among health workers, the RASR strategy was deemed acceptable by all health personnel. The authors recommend allocating sufficient budget, capacity building, and improved community engagement to achieve 100% timely response.

articles.headline: LDH-based RDT achieves 78% sensitivity, addresses hrp2/3-deleted Pf challenge
articles.title_original: Performance and usability evaluation of three LDH-based malaria rapid diagnostic tests in Kédougou, Senegal.
articles.details: Cross-sectional diagnostic accuracy study of three LDH-based RDTs in 220 febrile patients in Senegal (154 [70%] Pf-positive by PCR). The BIOCREDIT Pf (pLDH/HRPII) test achieved 78% sensitivity (95% CI 70.9–84.5%) and 89% specificity (95% CI 79.4–95.6%), outperforming microscopy (53% sensitivity). Although the HRP2 line on the Pf (pLDH/HRPII) test was more sensitive than the SD BIOLINE HRP2-based test (71%), the pLDH line alone (61–64%) could not fully compensate. In populations with low hrp2/3 deletion rates, dual HRP2/LDH performance proved superior to LDH alone.

articles.headline: Delayed treatment and high parasitemia drive severe malaria hospitalizations
articles.title_original: Factors associated with hospitalizations due to severe malaria in the non-endemic Brazilian region: a case-control study in the extra-Amazon Region from 2011 to 2019.
articles.details: Case-control study of malaria notifications and hospitalizations in Brazil's extra-Amazon region (2011–2019) identified risk factors for hospitalization. Delayed treatment ≥8 days (OR 2.08, 95% CI 1.62–2.66), parasitemia >100,000/mm³ (OR 3.15, 95% CI 2.20–4.50), and low education (OR 2.21, 95% CI 1.78–2.75) significantly increased severe malaria risk. Infection acquired in the Amazon Region (OR 1.50) or abroad (OR 1.72) also increased risk; active case detection was protective (OR 0.65, 95% CI 0.54–0.78). The authors highlight that delayed treatment enabling high parasitemia reflects socioeconomic inequalities in healthcare access.

articles.headline: Forty ethnomedicinal plants used traditionally for malaria in Odisha
articles.title_original: A review on the ethnomedicinal plants used for the traditional treatment of malaria in the Mayurbhanj district of Odisha, India.
articles.details: PRISMA-based systematic review of 20 literature sources identified 40 plant species from 24 families used traditionally for anti-malarial treatment in Mayurbhanj district, Odisha, India. The majority belonged to Acanthaceae and Apocynaceae families. Formulations included decoctions, pastes, juices, and powders, predominantly administered orally; leaves and roots were the significant plant parts used. No adverse effects were reported in reviewed literature. The authors note that formal investigation of these ethnomedicinal remedies could provide better therapeutic understanding and preserve tribal knowledge.

articles.headline: Cameroon P. falciparum shows complex population structure with regional variation
articles.title_original: Genetic Diversity and Phylogenetic Relatedness of Cameroonian Plasmodium falciparum Isolates and Comparative Analysis with Global Populations.
articles.authors: Kojom Foko LP, Hawadak J, Singh V
articles.details: Genetic diversity and phylogeny analysis of Plasmodium falciparum from three epidemiological zones in Cameroon using merozoite surface protein 1 and 2 (msp1/2) markers. The K1 and MAD20 msp1 families showed high structural diversity due to 3-amino acid repeats; RO33 presented several mutation points. K1 and RO33 sequences were phylogenetically closer to parasites from China/Myanmar, while MAD20, IC/3D7, and FC27 sequences grouped with parasites from India, Panama, and Papua New Guinea. The complex phylogenetic patterns indicate a structurally diverse P. falciparum population in Cameroon.

articles.headline: Ookluc transgenic parasite enables high-throughput transmission-blocking compound screening
articles.title_original: Ookluc: A Plasmodium berghei Line for Identifying Transmission-blocking Compounds.
articles.details: Development of a novel high-throughput screening assay for transmission-blocking compounds targeting Plasmodium berghei sexual stages. The transgenic Ookluc parasite expresses nanoluciferase exclusively after zygote formation, with peak luminescence during ookinete development. The luminescence signal quantifies gamete-to-zygote and zygote-to-ookinete differentiation, enabling rapid assessment of compounds blocking sexual stage development in the mosquito vector. This assay addresses the challenge of limited robust in vitro tools for evaluating transmission-blocking interventions.

articles.headline: Mathematical model suggests treating detected cases better than deportation
articles.title_original: A meta-population model of malaria with asymptomatic cases, transmission blocking drugs, migration and screening.
articles.authors: Tchoumi SY, Banasiak J, Ouifki R
articles.details: Two-patch mathematical model of malaria transmission incorporating migration, screening, transmission-blocking drugs (TBDs offering full but waning immunity), and policy choices. One site has superior disease control resources; policy options were to refuse entry to detected cases or treat them in the better-resourced site. Qualitative analysis examined endemic equilibria and backward bifurcations; numerical simulations demonstrated that allowing detected infected individuals entry and immediate TBD treatment yielded better disease control than deportation, which risked illegal re-entry and uncontrolled transmission.

articles.headline: P. vivax dbp and ebp2 gene amplification varies by Amazon region
articles.title_original: The pattern of gene amplification of members of the Plasmodium vivax erythrocyte binding-like proteins family across the Amazon rainforest.
articles.details: Analysis of 192 Plasmodium vivax isolates from three Amazon regions for copy number variation (CNV) in dbp and ebp2 erythrocyte-binding protein genes. Western Amazon samples showed 25% dbp amplification (up to 8 copies) versus 2–9% in other regions (2–3 copies); ebp2 amplification was 15% in western and 1–4% in other regions. All study participants were DARC-positive; no association was found between gene amplifications and DARC genotypes. The authors suggest further investigation into the biological significance of regional variations in gene amplification.

articles.headline: Early parasitemia surge predicts cerebral malaria onset with 97% sensitivity
articles.title_original: Temporal Parasitemia Trends Predict Risk and Timing of Experimental Cerebral Malaria in Mice Infected by Plasmodium berghei ANKA.
articles.details: Machine learning analysis of 370 mice (153 C57BL/6, 164 CBA, 53 Swiss Webster) infected with Plasmodium berghei ANKA to predict experimental cerebral malaria (ECM) risk and onset timing. A logistic regression model estimated ECM risk using day 1–4 parasitemia data; a Random Forest model predicted symptom onset day. Overall, 64.5% developed ECM with onset between days 5–11. A parasitemia increase of ≥0.05 between days 1 and 3 predicted ECM development with 97% sensitivity; the Random Forest predicted onset day with mean absolute error 0.43 and R² 0.64.

articles.headline: Scoping review protocol on African perspectives of gene-drive mosquitoes
articles.title_original: Knowledge, Perspectives, and Risks Perceptions on Gene Drive and Genetically Modified Mosquitoes for Malaria Control among African Stakeholders: A Scoping Review Protocol.
articles.details: Protocol for a scoping review of African stakeholder knowledge and perspectives regarding gene-drive and genetically modified mosquitoes for malaria control. The review will search PubMed, Embase, Science Direct, Cochrane, and Google Scholar for scientific articles and grey literature. Documents will be screened in four stages by independent reviewers using Rayyan software; data extraction will include authorship, design, sample size, findings, and limitations with thematic analysis of extracted data. The review aims to improve community engagement with this emerging malaria control technology.

articles.headline: Plasma biomarkers IL-20, HO-1, FCGR2B associated with placental malaria
articles.title_original: Plasma protein biomarkers of Plasmodium falciparum infection in pregnant women: a high-throughput proteomics study.
articles.details: High-throughput proximity extension assay screening of plasma from Plasmodium falciparum-exposed pregnant women identified candidate biomarkers for pregnancy-associated malaria (PAM). Malaria-positive pregnancies showed elevated IgG Fc receptor IIb (FCGR2B) and heme oxygenase-1 (HO-1), while neurturin (NRTN) and IL-20 were downregulated. IL-20 emerged as the top candidate biomarker, as current diagnostic tools cannot detect placental sequestration; only postnatal placental histopathology confirms PAM. The authors identify IL-20 as warranting validation in large cohorts with placental histopathology.

articles.headline: Multiple antigens overcome genetic diversity in P. vivax serological tests
articles.title_original: Navigating parasite antigen genetic diversity in the design of Plasmodium vivax serological exposure markers for malaria.
articles.details: Global genetic analysis of Plasmodium vivax serological markers (DBPII, MSP5, RBP2a, S16, and others) revealed extensive antigen diversity (0.4–99% sequence similarity to reference strain Sal-1). Novel haplotype-based antigens reflecting global diversity were tested in well-characterized cohorts from Brazil and Thailand for accuracy in identifying infections within 9 months prior. Extensive regional immunogenicity differences were observed in some antigens (e.g., RBP2a), but for most proteins (5 of 8), these differences had minimal impact on accuracy; where performance was affected, adding multiple antigens into the classification model overcame regional variation.

articles.headline: R21/Matrix-M vaccine induces diverse antibody responses in semi-immune adults
articles.title_original: R21/Matrix-M malaria vaccine drives diverse immune responses in pre-exposed adults: insights from a phase IIb controlled human malaria infection trial.
articles.details: Phase IIb CHMI trial in coastal Kenya with n=19 vaccinated and n=17 challenged with P. falciparum PfSPZ NF54 sporozoites. Vaccination increased IgA, IgM, and IgG antibodies including IgG1 and IgG3 subclasses (but not IgG2/IgG4), with complement-fixing capabilities and maintained responses. Circulating T follicular helper cells (cTfh) increased with Th2/Th17 predominance. Results support R21/Matrix-M vaccination potential in semi-immune populations.

articles.headline: P. vivax malaria reveals underlying chronic lymphocytic leukemia
articles.title_original: Underlying Chronic Lymphocytic Leukemia in a Case of Vivax Malaria With Thrombocytopenia: A Case Report.
articles.details: A 64-year-old male presented with P. vivax infection complicated by severe thrombocytopenia (platelet count 15,000/μL), leukocytosis (TLC 29,100/μL), and coagulopathy (INR 4.2). Flow cytometry revealed underlying chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL). Authors note the unusual combination of leukocytosis with thrombocytopenia in malaria without sepsis should raise suspicion for underlying CLL.

articles.headline: P. yoelii detected in invasive black rats in Gabon
articles.title_original: Rodent malaria parasites detected in the invasive Rattus rattus in Gabon.
articles.details: Systematic molecular screening of 527 rodents in rural Gabon (2021-2022) identified P. yoelii in 2 invasive Rattus rattus individuals (prevalence 0.38%). Phylogenetic analysis confirmed these strains matched those from native rodents in the region. Findings challenge the traditional view that rodent malaria parasites are restricted to native hosts and raise questions about invasive rodents' capacity to integrate into local transmission cycles.

articles.headline: Rapid multiplex malaria diagnosis using LAMP-MS with simplified extraction
articles.title_original: Rapid and Multiplex Diagnosis of Malaria Using Chelex-100 Extraction and LAMP-MS Assay.
articles.authors: Lim MS, Choe YL, Jang WS
articles.details: A diagnostic platform combining Chelex-100 resin-based nucleic acid extraction with multiplex loop-mediated isothermal amplification microscanner (LAMP-MS) enables simultaneous detection of P. falciparum, P. vivax, pan-malaria, and internal control within 40 minutes. The method demonstrates sensitivity and specificity comparable to traditional PCR-based diagnostics, with low-cost, field-deployable design suitable for resource-constrained environments.

articles.headline: Alveolin proteins require specific domain interactions for cytoskeletal localization
articles.title_original: Malaria Cytoskeletal Proteins Require Alveolin-Alveolin Interactions for Differential Localization: Recruitment and Organization of Alveolin Proteins.
articles.details: Biochemical characterization of four P. falciparum alveolins revealed variable dependence on alveolin-alveolin interactions for recruitment to cytoskeletal substructures. PfIMC1e requires PfIMC1f for sequential basal complex recruitment, whereas PfIMC1c and PfIMC1g localize independently to the inner membrane complex. Alveolin domains contain unique signatures for specialized localization; regions outside alveolin domains also contribute to basal complex recruitment.

articles.headline: Malaria hemozoin dampens dendritic cell interferon response
articles.title_original: Malaria-derived hemozoin skews dendritic cell responses to bacterial infections by reducing interferon gene-transcription by SWI/SNF-NuRD.
articles.details: In vitro study of monocyte-derived dendritic cells exposed to hemozoin (malaria pigment) and lipopolysaccharide (bacterial component). HZ co-exposure suppressed transcription of interferon-controlled genes, HLA-DR, and PD-L1 despite LPS inducing inflammatory genes. The suppression was associated with recruitment of NuRD chromatin remodeling complex (replacing SWI/SNF variant PBAF) and decreased NF-κB RELA binding, without changes in chromatin accessibility.

articles.headline: Malaria antibody affinity develops differently across parasite antigens
articles.title_original: Development of high affinity antibodies to Plasmodium falciparum merozoite and sporozoite antigens during infancy and adulthood.
articles.details: Longitudinal immunological study in Uganda followed newborn infants and mothers for 9 months. Antibody affinity (dissociation rate constant, kd) against merozoite antigens AMA1 and MSP2 decreased birth to 6 months, then increased to 9 months but remained below maternal levels; CSP sporozoite antigen affinity remained unchanged. Mothers showed stable responses. Antibody magnitude and affinity evolved independently, with correlations observed between specific antibody affinities and atypical memory B cells.

articles.headline: ITN use reduces malaria risk in sub-Saharan African children
articles.title_original: Effects of vector control interventions on spatio-temporal changes of falciparum malaria risk in children aged 2-10 in sub-Saharan African regions during 2011-2020.
articles.details: Analysis of Malaria Atlas Project data covering 634 administrative areas in 45 sub-Saharan African countries (2011-2020) using spatiotemporal Bayesian regression. Among children aged 2-10, P. falciparum prevalence declined from 21.32% in 2011 to 16.75% in 2016 with slight resurgence in 2017. Each unit increase in individuals using insecticide-treated nets (ITN) correlated with 34.07% reduction in infection risk. Highest risk in West-central, Central, and select Eastern regions.

articles.headline: Malaria terminology and medical discourse contested in modern China
articles.title_original: Malaria in China: a discourse-historical perspective.
articles.details: Historical discourse analysis tracing malaria's linguistic and conceptual evolution in late Qing and Republican China through medical dictionaries, newspapers, and periodicals. Early missionary dictionaries (1822-1860s) prioritized symptom-based translations; 1870s-1920s witnessed terminological competition between traditional Chinese and western approaches; by 1930s-1940s, the term "nueji" became institutionally dominant. Western parasitological frameworks were selectively assimilated (Plasmodium as "nueyuanchong"), yet immune mechanisms remained unexplained in Chinese medical discourse.

articles.headline: First case of P. vivax malaria with hemophagocytic syndrome in pregnancy
articles.title_original: A Rare Presentation of Plasmodium vivax Malaria: Pancytopenia due to Hemophagocytic Syndrome in a Pregnant Woman.
articles.authors: Lema GD, Dagnaw AB, Gebeyaw ED
articles.details: Case report of a 22-year-old pregnant woman (9 weeks gestation) from Ethiopia presenting with high-grade fever, headache, and systemic symptoms after travel to an endemic region. Diagnosed with P. vivax infection (blood smear positive for trophozoites and gametocytes) complicated by hemophagocytic syndrome (HPS) and pancytopenia, with elevated triglycerides and ferritin. Patient achieved full recovery with antimalarial and supportive treatment.

articles.headline: Malaria relapse undermines elimination efforts in Indonesia
articles.title_original: Postmalaria elimination: Overview of recurrent malaria cases in Riau Province, Indonesia.
articles.details: Retrospective analysis of 4,622 malaria cases in Riau Province, Indonesia (2019-2022) identified 232 recurrent cases (5%), all occurring after 28 days. Among recurrent cases, 220 (94.83%) were P. vivax and 11 (4.74%) were P. falciparum; study unable to distinguish relapse from reinfection. Rapid diagnostic tests used in 69% of cases; while all received standard treatment, 73.45% did not receive complete post-treatment evaluation. Malaria relapse represents significant challenge to elimination efforts.

articles.headline: Malaria in children drives inflammation, anemia, and nutritional deficiency
articles.title_original: Analysis of the relationship between malaria and inflammatory biomarker levels in children in rural Iraq: Impact of nutritional deficiencies, anemia, and hematological disorders.
articles.details: Case-control study of 150 malaria-infected children and 50 controls (age 5-8) in rural Iraq (August 2023-February 2025). Malaria cases showed significantly elevated inflammatory markers [CRP 18.4 vs 5.2 mg/L (p<0.001), IL-6 10.2 vs 3.1 pg/mL (p<0.001), TNF-α 12.5 vs 4.0 pg/mL (p<0.001)], lower hemoglobin (9.8 vs 12.5 g/dL, p<0.001) and iron (45.3 vs 90.2 μg/dL, p<0.001), and reduced zinc and vitamin D (p<0.001). Malnutrition 65% vs 15%, anemia 70% vs 20%, blood disorders 40% vs 10% (all p<0.001).

articles.headline: No zoonotic Plasmodium transmission detected in Indonesian human-cattle study
articles.title_original: Prevalence of Plasmodium spp. in humans and cattle: Implications for zoonotic malaria transmission in Indonesia.
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 41 humans and 43 cattle in malaria-endemic Indonesia using PCR. All human samples tested negative for Plasmodium spp. (0%, 95% CI 0.0–8.5%), while 1 cattle sample was positive (2.33%, 95% CI 0.06–12.0%). Although Plasmodium DNA was detected in cattle, no zoonotic transmission to humans was observed, highlighting the need for continued surveillance under a One Health framework.

articles.headline: Late treatment failure with artemether-lumefantrine in African-acquired P. falciparum
articles.title_original: [Successful Treatment of Two Cases of Plasmodium falciparum Malaria that Developed Recrudescence after Artemether-Lumefantrine Therapy].
articles.details: Case report of two P. falciparum malaria cases imported from Africa (Gabon, Uganda) treated with artemether-lumefantrine (AL). Both patients showed initial clinical improvement but developed recrudescence with high fever and chills after discharge. One patient was cleared after a second course of AL, while the other required non-ACT treatment (quinine and doxycycline). Cases highlight the risk of treatment failure even with standard-dose AL.

articles.headline: Cerebral malaria: epidemiology, pathogenesis, and care gaps in children
articles.title_original: Cerebral malaria.
articles.details: Comprehensive review of cerebral malaria, defined as unexplained coma in someone with malaria parasitemia. Case fatality in children is approximately 17% (1 in 6), with many survivors experiencing neurologic, cognitive, or behavioral sequelae. Acute seizures are common and associated with worse outcomes. Review covers epidemiology, pathogenesis, diagnostic approaches, clinical care pathways, and identifies care gaps in managing neurological complications.

articles.headline: Plant-derived silver nanoparticles show larvicidal activity against malaria vectors
articles.title_original: Eco-Friendly Synthesis of Silver Nanoparticles and Its Larvicidal Property Against Malaria, Filariasis, and Dengue Vectors.
articles.details: Study of green-synthesized silver nanoparticles using Aerva lanata flower extract evaluated against larvae of Culex quinquefasciatus, Anopheles stephensi, and Aedes aegypti. LC50 values were 6.5 ppm (Cx. quinquefasciatus), 20.9 ppm (An. stephensi), and 22.6 ppm (Ae. aegypti), with oxidative stress as the primary mechanism. Biosafety tests showed minimal toxicity to Artemia salina, suggesting potential for sustainable mosquito control.

articles.headline: Immunoinformatics-designed multi-epitope P. falciparum vaccine shows predicted immune potential
articles.title_original: Development of a multi-epitope vaccine candidate targeting blood-stage of malaria through immunoinformatics approach.
articles.details: In silico design of multi-epitope vaccine targeting four P. falciparum proteins (PfPHB1, PfPHB2, PfHSP70, PfGARP) selected based on conserved blood-stage expression. Vaccine construct incorporated 12 B-cell, 11 CTL, and 7 helper T-cell epitopes with FliC adjuvant. Molecular docking predicted strong TLR5 interactions, and immunogenicity simulations predicted robust immune responses with 95.04% population coverage. Further experimental validation is needed.

articles.headline: P. simium originated from P. vivax with ongoing human-monkey transmission in Brazil
articles.title_original: Plasmodium simium: birth and evolution of a zoonotic malaria parasite species.
articles.details: Whole-genome analysis of 31 P. simium and 1086 P. vivax isolates revealed that P. simium is a discrete lineage derived from P. vivax introduced during European colonization, with closest similarity to Amazon Basin P. vivax. P. simium shows 50% the genetic diversity of P. vivax, consistent with recent origin. Sympatric human and monkey isolates were as closely related as meiotic half-siblings, demonstrating ongoing zoonotic transmission. P. simium currently causes most/possibly all infections attributed to P. vivax in Serra do Mar, Brazil.
articles.notable_reason: Clarifies P. simium origin; demonstrates ongoing zoonotic transmission

articles.headline: Pf8: 33,325 P. falciparum genomes, largest eukaryote variation dataset released
articles.title_original: Pf8: an open dataset of Plasmodium falciparum genome variation in 33,325 worldwide samples.
articles.details: MalariaGEN released Pf8, an open-access resource containing genome variation data from 33,325 P. falciparum samples across 99 studies from 122 locations over 50+ years. Includes genotypes at 12+ million genomic positions and, for the first time, copy-number variation (CNV) calls for drug-resistance genes gch1 and crt alongside mdr1, plasmepsin2/3, and hrp2/hrp3 deletions. Includes interactive web tools and Python packages for exploring drug-resistance and genetic variation patterns.
articles.notable_reason: Largest eukaryote genome variation dataset; foundational for resistance

articles.headline: Grassi's fundamental 19th-century contributions to malaria vector identification
articles.title_original: Giovanni Battista Grassi (1854-1925): a forgotten Italian scholar and his fundamental studies on malaria.
articles.details: Historical account of Giovanni Battista Grassi (1854–1925), an Italian physician and naturalist who contributed foundational research on malaria transmission. Article reviews the development of malaria science from Pasteur's germ theory through Laveran's 1889 identification of Plasmodium parasites. Grassi's 1898 studies were pivotal in identifying the vector of human malaria, establishing a cornerstone of modern malariology.

articles.headline: P. cynomolgi co-infection misdiagnosed as P. vivax in human case
articles.title_original: Molecular and Microscopic Challenges in Detecting Plasmodium cynomolgi Co-Infections with Plasmodium vivax: A Case Report.
articles.details: Case report of 32-year-old male with co-infection of P. vivax and P. cynomolgi initially misidentified as P. vivax alone by routine microscopy and qPCR due to high genetic similarity. Institute for Medical Research cross-examination and sequencing at MAPELAB confirmed mixed-species infection. Case illustrates diagnostic limitations in detecting P. cynomolgi, an emerging zoonotic malaria species in Southeast Asia.

articles.headline: Brucea javanica extract inhibits P. falciparum with additive antimalarial drug effects
articles.title_original: Growth Inhibition and Additive Effect to Antimalarial Drugs of Brucea javanica Extracts on Asexual Blood-Stage Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: In vitro study evaluated Brucea javanica root and fruit extracts against asexual blood-stage P. falciparum. Root extract showed IC50 of 0.41 µg/mL and fruit extract 0.26 µg/mL, both significantly inhibiting parasite development at the ring stage without hemolysis. Both extracts demonstrated additive effects when combined with artesunate and chloroquine, reducing IC90 levels. Findings suggest potential for incorporating B. javanica into novel antimalarial drug combinations.

articles.headline: Chlorfenapyr LLINs reduce malaria infection and vector density in Africa
articles.title_original: Effectiveness and Efficacy of Long-Lasting Insecticidal Nets for Malaria Control in Africa: Systematic Review and Meta-Analysis of Randomized Controlled Trials.
articles.journal: Int J Environ Res Public Health
articles.details: Systematic review and meta-analysis of 11 randomized controlled trials involving 21,916 households in Africa. Chlorfenapyr long-lasting insecticidal nets (LLINs) reduced malaria infection prevalence to 25.96 per 100 children versus 41.60 per 100 in pyrethroid-only controls (10% risk reduction). Chlorfenapyr also reduced mean indoor vector density by 23% and mean inoculation rates, with greater efficacy than piperonyl butoxide-treated nets. Findings support chlorfenapyr LLINs for malaria control in pyrethroid-resistant areas.
articles.notable_reason: RCT meta-analysis supports chlorfenapyr LLINs over pyrethroid nets

articles.headline: Immunoinformatics approaches accelerate multi-epitope malaria vaccine discovery
articles.title_original: Search continues: Exploring immunoinformatics platforms for designing an effective multiepitope malaria vaccine candidate.
articles.details: Review and case study of immunoinformatics-aided vaccine design for P. falciparum. Bioinformatics pipeline identified 30 vaccine-fit epitopes from 133 initial candidates, resulting in a 570 amino acid construct comprising 12 B-cell, 11 CTL, and 7 helper T-cell epitopes. Construct demonstrated favorable structural/antigenic properties, 95.04% global population coverage, and strong predicted molecular binding and simulated immune responses.

articles.headline: Seven P. vivax binding proteins interact with erythrocyte Basigin
articles.title_original: Majority of the erythrocyte binding proteins of the Pvfam "a" family of Plasmodium vivax interact with Basigin to assist parasite entry into the host cell.
articles.details: Study identified that 7 of 10 Pvfam "a" family erythrocyte binding proteins (PvTRAg, PvTRAg33.5, PvTRAg35.2, PvTRAg34, PvTRAg36, PvTRAg38, PvTRAg69.4) interact with Basigin receptor via solid-phase binding assay and surface plasmon resonance. Each protein inhibited parasite growth in culture; combinations showed higher growth inhibition rates, indicating multiple ligand-receptor interactions are important for parasite invasion.

articles.headline: CD8+ T cells drive tubular injury in malaria-induced kidney disease
articles.title_original: CD8(+) T cells promote tubule-interstitial damage in malaria-induced acute kidney injury.
articles.details: In mouse models of malaria-induced acute kidney injury (MAKI), adoptive transfer of infected donor T cells caused kidney tubule-interstitial damage with increased CD8+ accumulation. Selective CD8+ T cell depletion protected from tubular injury, and FYT720 (an immunomodulatory drug sequestering T cells) prevented proteinuria and reduced urinary γ-glutamyl transferase levels, a marker of tubular injury.

articles.headline: Machine learning tool automates malaria oocyst counting in mosquitoes
articles.title_original: OocystMeter, a machine-learning algorithm to count and measure Plasmodium oocysts, reveals clustering patterns in the Anopheles midgut.
articles.details: OocystMeter, a Mask R-CNN-based software trained on 11,178 P. falciparum oocysts from A. gambiae midguts, achieved Spearman correlation of 0.998 for oocyst counts and 0.978 for size measurements comparable to expert parasitologists. Application revealed non-random clustered spatial distribution of oocysts in infections with fewer than 75 oocysts/midgut, independent of anatomical regions.

articles.headline: mRNA vaccine confers complete protection against blood-stage malaria
articles.title_original: An mRNA Vaccine Expressing Blood-Stage Malaria Antigens Induces Complete Protection Against Lethal Plasmodium yoelii.
articles.authors: Ott AC, Loll PJ, Burns JM Jr
articles.details: An mRNA vaccine expressing PyMSP1/8 (fused blood-stage antigens) achieved 100% survival after lethal P. yoelii 17XL challenge in mice; 50% of vaccinated animals showed no detectable parasitemia (<0.01%), with peak parasitemia significantly lower than recombinant vaccine groups. Membrane-bound antigen formulation (PyMSP1/8-mem) provided superior protection to secreted antigen (PyMSP1/8-sec).
articles.notable_reason: First mRNA vaccine achieving complete protection against blood-stage malaria

articles.headline: Mathematical model supports combined vaccination and vector control
articles.title_original: Vaccination and combined optimal control measures for malaria prevention and spread mitigation.
articles.details: A deterministic mathematical model analyzing malaria transmission dynamics shows combined strategies (vaccination, mosquito nets, insecticide spraying) significantly reduce R₀ and transmission. Although current vaccines are not 100% effective, the model indicates vaccination meaningfully contributes to malaria control when combined with other prevention measures.

articles.headline: Ivermectin mass administration could avert substantial malaria cases
articles.title_original: Wonder drugs and where to use them: a forecast of ivermectin's impact on malaria in Africa.
articles.authors: Greenhalgh S, Roman A, Provencher A
articles.details: Mathematical model using transmission data from 41 sub-Saharan African countries estimated that mass drug administration of ivermectin to 12.5%, 25%, or 50% of population annually would avert 248.7, 261.4, or 288.7 incidences per thousand people and save 5.4, 5.7, or 6.3 disability-adjusted life years, respectively, with cost-effectiveness in 41, 18, or 6 countries.

articles.headline: P. vivax circumsporozoite protein shows repeat region diversity in India
articles.title_original: Evolution of tandem repeats in putative circumsporozoite protein to enhance its function: A recent and exclusive event in Plasmodium vivax in India.
articles.details: Sequencing of putative circumsporozoite protein (PvpuCSP) in 71 P. vivax isolates from endemic Indian regions revealed well-conserved N- and C-terminal regions (~80% of gene) but extensive diversity in the C-terminal repeat region (1.8–2.2 kb), identifying 37 different alleles. Episodic diversifying selection shapes repeat region diversity, potentially facilitating adaptation to different ubiquitination-related interactions.

articles.headline: Single-dose acridone antimalarial targets all Plasmodium lifecycle stages
articles.title_original: Potent Acridone Antimalarial against All Three Life Stages of Plasmodium.
articles.details: Acridone compound T111 demonstrates potent activity across all three Plasmodium lifecycle stages: oral single-dose cure of blood-stage asexual infection in rodent models, activity against relapsing parasites in primate liver cells, and prevention of parasite development in mosquitoes. T111 shows synergy with tafenoquine and inhibits mitochondrial electron transport chain with favorable therapeutic index in rats.
articles.notable_reason: Novel pan-stage antimalarial with single-dose cure and transmission-blocking potential

articles.headline: Machine learning reveals migration in Anopheles gambiae vector populations
articles.title_original: On the use of generative models for evolutionary inference of malaria vectors from genomic data.
articles.details: Using generative deep learning models, researchers inferred joint evolutionary history of Anopheles gambiae complex populations from Guinea and Burkina Faso. A model incorporating post-split migration fit the data better than no-migration model, with machine learning approach outperforming prior summary statistics methods in capturing population genetic differentiation.

articles.headline: Intensive control reduces P. falciparum diversity to single predominant genotype
articles.title_original: Impact of intensive control on malaria population genomics in Eastern Myanmar.
articles.details: Genomic analysis of 2270 P. falciparum infections from 283 malaria posts in Myanmar's Kayin State over 58 months (2015–2020) shows intensive control (rapid detection/treatment plus mass drug administration) achieved 97% transmission reduction. Parasite effective population size decreased; 1726 single-genotype infections comprised 166 unique genomes with 9 families accounting for 62.5% of parasites. By 2020, one predominant genotype carrying kelch13-R561H remained.

articles.headline: Massive subamniotic hemorrhage from severe malaria in pregnancy
articles.title_original: A rare presentation of massive subamniotic hemorrhage in a pregnant woman with severe malaria: A case report.
articles.details: Case report of 28-year-old pregnant woman with severe P. falciparum infection experiencing massive subamniotic bleeding from placental hematoma containing ~800 mL lysed blood, resulting in preterm delivery. Mother recovered after minimal post-delivery bleeding; neonate died in ICU from prematurity complications, illustrating severe maternal-fetal complications of malaria in pregnancy.

articles.headline: Malaria causes significant amino acid and micronutrient dysregulation
articles.title_original: Malaria-induced dysregulation of selected micronutrients (Ca, Mg, Na, K, Zn, Fe, Co) and plasma free amino acids; implications for pathogenesis and host immunity in a Nigerian population.
articles.authors: Ekeanyanwu CR, Chikezie PC, Ekeanyanwu CL
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 200 Nigerian participants comparing malarious patients to healthy controls found malaria significantly reduced (p<0.01) plasma levels of essential amino acids (including leucine and valine), immune-related amino acids (arginine, tyrosine, tryptophan, glutamine, methionine, cysteine), and micronutrients (calcium, sodium, zinc, iron), indicating metabolic disruptions contributing to disease severity.

articles.headline: TRAP glycosylation essential for P. falciparum salivary gland colonization
articles.title_original: Glycosylation of Plasmodium falciparum TRAP is essential for infection of mosquito salivary glands.
articles.details: P. falciparum TRAP protein contains thrombospondin type 1 repeat (TSR) domain with Thr 256 O-fucosylation and Trp 250 C-mannosylation. CRISPR/Cas9 glyco-null mutants (T256A, W250F) showed major fitness defects comparable to TRAP knockout: failed salivary gland colonization, severely impaired gliding motility, reduced hepatocyte invasion, and significantly reduced protein abundance despite normal transcripts. Demonstrates TSR glycosylation is critical for TRAP expression and function in mosquito-stage parasites.

articles.headline: Graphene biosensor design for stage-specific malaria detection
articles.title_original: Design and simulation of a graphene-integrated SPR biosensor for malaria detection.
articles.details: Surface plasmon resonance biosensor with graphene, silicon nitride, and thiol-tethered ssDNA designed to differentiate malaria stages via transfer matrix method. Two configurations (Sys3 and Sys4) tested on ring, trophozoite, schizont stages. Sys3 achieved sensitivities of 353.14°/RIU, 291.14°/RIU, and 263.26°/RIU respectively, exceeding comparable SPR platforms. Sys3 showed superior optical performance. Study limited to simulation; potential for label-free, stage-specific point-of-care diagnostics.

articles.headline: Novel K13 mutation and widespread SP resistance in Burundi
articles.title_original: A Novel Plasmodium falciparum Kelch13 A675T Mutation and High Levels of Chloroquine and Sulfadoxine-Pyrimethamine Resistance in Burundi.
articles.details: 157 P. falciparum isolates from Burundi sequenced for 10 resistance markers. No validated artemisinin-resistance kelch13 mutations detected, but novel pfkelch13 A675T identified. Sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine resistance widespread: 82.3% quintuple and 12.3% sextuple haplotypes. Background mutations pffd D193Y (45.9%), pfarps10 V127M (11.5%). pfcrt IET triple mutant 94.9% and pfmdr1 N-F-D 57.9% indicate sustained chloroquine resistance and artemether-lumefantrine efficacy. Substantial SP resistance poses major threat to IPTp and SMC strategies.

articles.headline: Artemisinin-resistant R622I and RDT failure rising in Ethiopia
articles.title_original: Artemisinin resistant kelch13 R622I and RDT negativity approaching predominance in northern Ethiopia and emerging C580Y of African origin threaten falciparum malaria control.
articles.details: 903 P. falciparum cases in northern Ethiopia: validated artemisinin-resistance kelch13 R622I at 44.3% prevalence (52% Gondar Zuria vs. 40% Tach Armachiho; p<0.001). RDT negativity significantly higher in R622I mutants: 48.3% vs. 30.7% wild-type (p<0.001). 98.2% of R622I parasites carried pfmdr1 NFD haplotype. Novel pfkelch13 C580Y detected in 2 patients (0.4%), first reported in Horn of Africa; whole-genome and identity-by-descent analysis (IBD=1) confirmed local de novo clonal emergence, distinct from Southeast Asian lineages.
articles.notable_reason: High R622I prevalence with RDT-negativity association threatening diagnosis

articles.headline: Differential DNA repair pathways maintain P. falciparum genome structure
articles.title_original: Plasmodium falciparum DNA repair dynamics reveal unique roles for TLS polymerases and PfRad51 in genome diversification.
articles.details: P. falciparum genome contains conserved core and hypervariable subtelomeric regions. CRISPR knockout lines created for PfRad51 and TLS polymerases (PfPolζ, PfRev1). TLS mutants showed cell-cycle-dependent irradiation hypersensitivity; PfRad51 mutants uniform sensitivity. PfRad51 essential for homologous-recombination-mediated double-strand break repair in core genome only. All three knockout lines retained Rad51-independent homology-directed repair in subtelomeric regions. Differential DNA damage response maintains distinct diversification patterns across chromosomal locations.

articles.headline: Low artemisinin resistance markers in Brazilian P. falciparum
articles.title_original: Tracking Drug Resistance in Plasmodium falciparum: Genetic Diversity of Key Resistance Markers in Brazilian Malaria Hotspots.
articles.details: 141 P. falciparum isolates from Brazil (2013-2023) sequenced for pfcrt, pfmdr1, pfk13. No validated artemisinin-resistance pfk13 mutations detected. pfcrt: 50% SVMNTMCGI, no C350R. pfmdr1: 70% NYCDY dominant, 4% N86Y, no Y184F. Strong intragenic linkage disequilibrium within pfcrt (C72S/K76T) and pfmdr1 (S1034C/N1042D) but not between genes. Absence of pfk13 mutations and low pfmdr1 markers support continued artemisinin-based combination therapy efficacy in Brazil.

articles.headline: Mathematical optimization of MDA and treatment strategies amid resistance
articles.title_original: Optimizing MDA and antimalarial treatment in the presence of drug resistance for effective malaria control.
articles.details: Compartmental mathematical model incorporating asymptomatic, paucisymptomatic, and clinical infection states to evaluate mass drug administration (MDA) combined with antimalarial treatment under drug resistance. Global sensitivity analysis identified critical factors: MDA coverage, treatment access, mutation probability, and initial resistance prevalence. Optimal control analysis: low mutation rates favor combined antimalarial drugs and MDA with gradual drug-use reduction; high mutation rates favor broader MDA with significantly reduced antimalarial use. Numerical simulations reinforce importance of strategic intervention.

articles.headline: Ungulate Plasmodium parasites form distinct evolutionary lineages
articles.title_original: Bringing to light unnoticed data on the genetic and host diversity of ungulate Plasmodium.
articles.details: Ungulate Plasmodium research largely neglected until 2016. Phylogenetic reanalysis of African forest and savanna ungulate parasites from Tragelaphus, Syncerus, and Cephalophus genera. Ungulate Plasmodium form monophyletic clade distinct from primate, rodent, and bat species, with two main subclades. Cephalophus parasites cluster with previously described sequences; Tragelaphus and Syncerus form distinct lineages likely representing novel species. Findings suggest substantial uncharacterized genetic diversity.

articles.headline: CD81-targeting siRNA prevents malaria infection at liver stage
articles.title_original: Protection from malaria infection using liver-targeted siRNA.
articles.details: CD81 tetraspanin on hepatocytes is critical entry receptor for P. falciparum sporozoites. N-acetylgalactosamine-conjugated siRNA targeting CD81 silenced mouse and human hepatic CD81 in vivo in dose-dependent manner. In mouse malaria model, CD81 siRNA blocked parasite liver infection and prevented onset of blood-stage infection. In human liver-chimeric mice, CD81 siRNA reduced P. falciparum infection. Results demonstrate siRNA-based host-targeted approach as novel strategy for malaria prevention.
articles.notable_reason: First demonstration of host-targeted siRNA preventing malaria infection

articles.headline: Indoor residual spraying reduced malaria transmission 10% in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Modelling the Effect of Indoor Residual Spraying on Malaria Transmission in Nassarawa State, North-central Nigeria.
articles.authors: Suleiman AG, Kareem L, Obi C
articles.details: SIRS mathematical model of indoor residual spraying (IRS) impact in Nassarawa State, Nigeria, 2012-2013, using surveillance data to estimate parameters. Transmission rate in IRS-intervention area (0.95) lower than non-intervention control area (1.05). Over two years, IRS reduced transmission rate by 10%. Authors attributed modest effect to implementation factors and surveillance data uncertainties. Findings support IRS efficacy but highlight need for optimized spray frequency and robust monitoring.

articles.headline: Ultra-sensitive RDT superior for malaria detection in pregnant women
articles.title_original: Comparison of diagnostic accuracy of ultra-sensitive and conventional rapid diagnostic tests for malaria in asymptomatic pregnant women at a tertiary hospital in North-Central Nigeria.
articles.authors: Anenga UM, Swende TZ, Hembah-Hilekaan SK
articles.details: Cross-sectional study of 107 asymptomatic pregnant women in Nigeria. P. falciparum prevalence: 14% microscopy, 12.1% ultra-sensitive RDT, 2.8% conventional RDT. Ultra-sensitive RDT: sensitivity 66.7% vs. 13.3%, positive predictive value 83.3% vs. 50%, negative predictive value 94.7% vs. 87.4%, accuracy 93.5% vs. 86% compared to conventional RDT. Specificity similar 97.8% for both (p=0.002). Ultra-sensitive RDTs significantly more effective for detecting infections in asymptomatic pregnant women.

articles.headline: Imported malaria diagnostic capacity varies widely by facility level
articles.title_original: [Epidemiological characteristics of imported malaria cases in Chengdu City from 2016 to 2023].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: 463 imported malaria cases in Chengdu City, 2016-2023: P. falciparum 71.27%, 94.17% from Africa, male:female 21.05:1, predominantly migrant workers age 20-50. Peak months May, June, December across 20 county districts. Among 456 initial healthcare visits, 74.78% (341) correctly diagnosed at first presentation. Diagnostic accuracy by facility: county-level 91.43%, city-level 82.45%, provincial 80%, but township centers 11.11%, village clinics 0%, individual doctors 0%. Substantial diagnostic capacity gaps in primary care.

articles.headline: Secondary school adolescents' willingness to accept malaria vaccine in Nigeria
articles.title_original: Willingness of secondary school adolescents to get vaccinated against Malaria: a cross-sectional study in Enugu, South-East Nigeria.
articles.details: Cross-sectional survey of 496 secondary school students in Enugu, Nigeria (June–September 2023). 68.1% expressed willingness to receive malaria vaccine; 32.1% had good knowledge. Willingness was independently associated with age <15 years (AOR=2.2, 95%CI 1.1–4.7), female sex (male AOR=0.6, 95%CI 0.4–0.9), and good vaccine knowledge (AOR=2.2, 95%CI 1.4–3.4). Authors conclude acceptance is influenced by age, sex, and knowledge.

articles.headline: Propolis phytochemicals show promise as P. falciparum lactate dehydrogenase inhibitors
articles.title_original: In vitro and in silico investigations of Propolis-derived phytochemicals as potential inhibitors of Plasmodium falciparum.
articles.details: In vitro and molecular docking study of propolis extracts from Iranian regions against P. falciparum NF54. Kermanshah propolis exhibited highest anti-P. falciparum activity (IC50=6.69±1.44 μg/mL). Two compounds, tectochrysin and galangin, showed strong binding affinities for PfLDH (−7.8 and −7.5 kcal/mol, respectively), exceeding standard antimalarials. Molecular dynamics simulations confirmed stable protein-ligand interactions, warranting further in vivo validation.

articles.headline: Genomic surveillance reveals parasite population dynamics during malaria elimination in Myanmar
articles.title_original: Malaria parasite population genomics during an elimination program in Eastern Myanmar.
articles.details: Population genomic analysis of 2270 P. falciparum genomes (from 5014 blood spots) collected over 58 months (November 2015–August 2020) across 413 malaria posts in Kayin State, Myanmar. Parasites showed genetic depletion (166 unique genomes from single-genotype infections); nine families comprised 62.5% of isolates. Kelch13 diversity was stable 2016–2019, but only one clonal genotype (kelch13-R561H) remained by 2020. Mass drug administration showed founder effects, providing genomic evidence of MDA efficacy.

articles.headline: Plasmodium yoelii infection alters skin odors and increases mosquito attraction in mice
articles.title_original: [Changes in murine skin odors following Plasmodium infections and their impact on mosquito attraction].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Experimental study in 20 C57BL/6 mice (10 infected with P. yoelii 1×10⁶, 10 controls) tracked parasitemia (rising to 51% by day 6) and measured Anopheles stephensi attraction. Infected mice showed significantly higher p-cresol, ethylbenzene, and nonanal, lower toluene, benzaldehyde, and indole. Mosquito attraction increased from 50% at baseline to 67% at peak parasitemia (χ²=9.54, P<0.05). Skin microbiota shifts included higher Streptococcus and Rothia, lower Lactococcus.

articles.headline: Faculty enrichment program in applied malaria modeling: implementation and participant outcomes
articles.title_original: Building contextually-relevant training programs for scientific development: process and lessons learned in implementing two iterations of a faculty enrichment program in applied malaria modeling.
articles.details: Report on a 4-month faculty enrichment program at Northwestern University (2022–2023) with two cohorts training in malaria modeling, communication, and epidemiology. Qualitative interviews showed participants valued growth in technical expertise and communication skills but faced challenges with cross-disciplinary learning. Instructors adapted teaching approaches based on feedback. Authors recommend training programs include continuous feedback mechanisms and knowledge-transfer pathways to maximize long-term impact.

articles.headline: Nanopore sequencing with recombinase-aided amplification for Plasmodium species identification
articles.title_original: [Establishment and preliminary evaluation of recombinase-aided isothermal nucleic acid amplification combined with nanopore sequencing for identification of Plasmodium species].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Diagnostic method development using 89 malaria patient blood spots. Recombinase-aided isothermal amplification combined with nanopore sequencing identified P. vivax, P. ovale, P. malariae, and P. falciparum. Among 49 samples, the assay achieved 92% sensitivity, 97.33% specificity, and 96% accuracy for species identification using nested PCR as gold standard, outperforming RT-qPCR (88.24% sensitivity, 97.32% specificity, 95% accuracy).

articles.headline: Malaria and malnutrition in tribal areas of India: epidemiology and integrated solutions
articles.title_original: Malaria and malnutrition in tribal areas of India: implications for paediatric health.
articles.authors: Verma AK, Shrivastava S, Saxena N
articles.details: Mini-review discussing the interrelated epidemiology of malaria and undernutrition in Indian tribal populations. Addresses poverty, inadequate diet, poor sanitation, and limited healthcare access creating a vicious cycle of infection and malnutrition. Malaria and intestinal infections alter gut flora, exacerbating malabsorption. Authors advocate for integrated approaches addressing both nutritional and malaria-specific needs for elimination from tribal areas.

articles.headline: Mechanisms of immune escape in Plasmodium parasites: a comprehensive review
articles.title_original: [Progress of researches on mechanisms underlying immune escape of Plasmodium].
articles.journal: Zhongguo Xue Xi Chong Bing Fang Zhi Za Zhi
articles.details: Review summarizing advances in parasite immune evasion mechanisms, including antigenic variation, epigenetic regulation, antagonism against IgM antibody, cGAS-STING signaling activation, suppression of splenic immune function, and molecular camouflage. Intended to provide insights for vaccine and drug development and formulation of malaria control strategies.

articles.headline: Seasonal malaria chemoprevention shows benefits beyond malaria: reduced childhood infections
articles.title_original: Indirect Benefits of Seasonal Malaria Chemoprevention for Non-Malarial Pediatric Infections and Routine Antibiotic Use in Real-World Programmatic Settings: A Pre-Post Study Using Positive and Negative Controls.
articles.details: Pre-post study in 51 Burkina Faso health facilities examining benefits of seasonal malaria chemoprevention (monthly sulfadoxine-pyrimethamine plus amodiaquine) beyond malaria prevention. Compared SMC administration weeks to post-intervention period. Modest reductions observed in pneumonia (0.7 per 1,000 person-weeks, 95%CI 0.3–1.0), diarrhea (0.2, 95%CI 0.1–0.4), acute malnutrition (0.05, 95%CI 0.001–0.09), and antibiotic prescriptions (0.90, 95%CI 0.4–1.4). Malaria diagnoses declined 3.7 per 1,000 person-weeks (95%CI 2.6–4.8).

articles.headline: First documented cerebral malaria from P. vivax in Oman: case report
articles.title_original: Cerebral Malaria and Multi-Organ Dysfunction Caused by Plasmodium vivax in an Adult: A case from Oman with a literature review.
articles.details: Case report of a 38-year-old Pakistani male presenting in 2025 with cerebral malaria and multi-organ dysfunction syndrome caused by P. vivax (confirmed by blood smear and PCR). Treated with intravenous artesunate and intensive supportive care, achieving full neurological recovery.
